Name: Eastern and Oriental Tea House and Coffee Parlour
Author: Kyle Danubio
Review:
The food here is generally very good! They have an excellent selection of cakes and baked goods and all breakfast items were very tasty!

I was a bit disappointed by the coffee options. All drinks were quite sweet which was something I would have expected more in HCMC… I ordered the “iced coffee” and was very surprised to find milk and cream both added in already.. I had just wanted a black iced coffee, but that’s partly my fault at most places in Vietnam will add sugar and milk by default.

Food aside, the restaurant is set in an older French style house. It’s quirky, but enjoyable. We sat on the second floor in what may have been a large bedroom at one point. It makes getting the servers attention a but challenging, but they are generally quite attentive. I think we’ll try the outdoor seating if we return in the future as it has a nice view if the lake and feels a bit more open.

Name: Eastern and Oriental Tea House and Coffee Parlour
Author: Gator of Wall Street
Review:
The restaurant was located within an old house facing the lake that gave diners some great views. As I walked into the restaurant, I was greeted and introduced to all the cakes on offer. The choices were simply too much. Being a cake person, naturally I selected a number of cakes to try. After ordering, I was guided to walked up to the higher levels to be seated. Naturally I walked up to the highest level to enjoy some great views. I ordered the eggs Benedict and egg coffee for brunch. I was given a choice to serve the cakes last. The egg Benedict was ok, but the egg coffee was really good. The cakes were also very tasty. The slices were huge and in the end, I couldn’t finish all of them.

Met a server (Nam Anh) who’s extremely friendly and hospitable. We ended up chatting with him while eating our food. He shared many aspects of the Vietnamese culture with us which was really interesting as a tourist. In the end, we spent about two hours there ordering more drinks.

Name: Eastern and Oriental Tea House and Coffee Parlour
Author: Anh NHQ (NoireGK-)
Review: A very nice place. The food is great and the staff members are very friendly. Even though the price of the food is a bit pricey to Vietnamese pricing, it is very worth it. The dessert is a definite must try too.

Name: Eastern and Oriental Tea House and Coffee Parlour
Author: Hailee
Review:
Has to be my favorite cafe place in the entire West Lake area! I'm blown away every time by their delicious pastries, and their tea is always superb, too. They're coming up with new recipes once in a while too, I wish I have enough chance to try all of their cakes LOL
I love sitting out on the balcony facing the lake, though some people might find it too hot during summer :)
Definitely in my top-rated list in Hanoi

Name: Eastern and Oriental Tea House and Coffee Parlour
Author: Amandeep Kaur
Review:
A good pit-stop on a solo walk around the neighborhood. The salmon bagel hit the spot, and the matcha crepe had a nice balance of sweetness. Portions were generous, as others have mentioned—I couldn't quite finish. The decor is kitschy and warm. It was peaceful and quiet - just the spot to unwind with a book or tackle some work.

The cake display area was a bit cramped and hectic, with freshly baked cakes casually stacked around, waiting for the final touches. You almost feel like you’re stepping on their toes.

While the staff were helpful enough, a welcoming smile would have elevated the experience. It's always the little touches.

Props for the clean restroom though!

Name: Marucha Teahouse
Address: 104 K3 ngõ 48 Tạ Quang Bửu, Bách Khoa Ngõ 50 Tạ Quang Bửu, Bách Khoa Hà Nội
Author: Mia Lee
Review: Accidentally entered the shop to find a place to sit and shelter from the rain, but found the shop to be ok, except that it was a bit empty, and the staff were also cute.

Name: Marucha Teahouse
Address: 104 K3 ngõ 48 Tạ Quang Bửu, Bách Khoa Ngõ 50 Tạ Quang Bửu, Bách Khoa Hà Nội
Author: Helliven
Review: Very chill, empty and quiet, there is music, tables and chairs are arranged properly, the consultant is ok, the drinks are ok

Name: Marucha Teahouse
Address: 104 K3 ngõ 48 Tạ Quang Bửu, Bách Khoa Ngõ 50 Tạ Quang Bửu, Bách Khoa Hà Nội
Author: Hà Mạnh Tuyến
Review: Delicious drinks, reasonable prices, beautiful open space! 10 points mmm

Name: Marucha Teahouse
Address: 104 K3 ngõ 48 Tạ Quang Bửu, Bách Khoa Ngõ 50 Tạ Quang Bửu, Bách Khoa Hà Nội
Author: Thu Nguyễn Minh
Review: Delicious drinks, nice, chill restaurant

Name: Marucha Teahouse
Address: 104 K3 ngõ 48 Tạ Quang Bửu, Bách Khoa Ngõ 50 Tạ Quang Bửu, Bách Khoa Hà Nội
Author: Khanh Vu Hong
Review: Enthusiastic service

Name: Minh Lam Teahouse
Address: Marina Arc, Khu đô thị Ecopark
Author: Nhung Bùi
Review: I love the peaceful vibe and the interior design of the Tea house shop. It features a series of specialty in clever way with multiple of the brand stories. It also has an amazing piano so people can play it. I ordered a milk tea and I like the flavor of the tea here, it’s fragrant slightly and sweet enough. The staffs are so gentle, elegant, well-trained and helpful. We came on a day they made a photo shooting for their advertising campaign, but they apologized us about the noisy they could make, we are impressed with the experience they created for customers. They also provide the space for Tea workshops on the 2nd floor if you wanna try one day. Highly recommended!

Name: Minh Lam Teahouse
Address: Marina Arc, Khu đô thị Ecopark
Author: Yen Pham
Review: It's likely showroom of tea culture than business place, I really enjoy the atmosphere here. Everything is perfect 🥰 and staff are so nice and friendly. I love to work or gather with friends and family here...highly recommended!!!

Name: Minh Lam Teahouse
Address: Marina Arc, Khu đô thị Ecopark
Author: Huy Nguyen
Review:
A must-go within Ecopark
My family cross by and really impressive with this house. Nice decoration, tranquillent space and tasty drinks
Next time I will try tea in second floor

Name: Minh Lam Teahouse
Address: Marina Arc, Khu đô thị Ecopark
Author: Teresa
Review: Highly recommend the salted coffee! It comes with a piece of rosemary, which is surprisingly enhancing the flavour of the coconut cream. All the staff are super friendly and speak English. They’ve truly put the satisfaction of their customers at heart and attend to all the details, like adjusting the richness of the latte for us, setting an extra fan, closing the curtain to reduce the heat, all without us asking. The tea house itself is very spacious. It has big tables for groups and quiet corners for individuals. The outdoor space has very comfortable cushioned chairs and shade. We’ve had a very contented time spent here. Thanks to all the staff!

Name: Minh Lam Teahouse
Address: Marina Arc, Khu đô thị Ecopark
Author: Ộp Vũ
Review:
Minh Lam might be one of the best tea house in Hanoi. There they have social teaspace & several private tearoom with different styles, cozy & elegant.
Many kind of special tea made in Vietnam, worth to try out as a tea-lover. Also their tea artist - tea master are knowledgeable about tea.

Will be back for more tea experience!

Name: NAGOCHA Matcha Specialty & Tea House Hanoi Westlake
Author: Chu Phuong Anh
Review: Pricey but superb Matcha shop in the Westlake. The location is just so chill. The quality of the drink is very fine because they serve imported Matcha from Japan. Mainly Kyoto and Nagoya. Very good taste. Good vibe. Good service. But yes, it comes with higher price than any other cafe in town. You can also shop for tea ceremony like matcha powder, chasen, mugs... there. I love it. I will definitely come back when my finance is better lol. Highly recommended spot for Matcha lovers!

Name: NAGOCHA Matcha Specialty & Tea House Hanoi Westlake
Author: Trịnh Minh
Review:
The shop specializes in Matcha, but the space and menu look really good.
+ The menu is divided into many types of matcha with different origins and flavor profiles, so the deliciousness will depend on whether you choose the type that suits your taste or not.
+ I don't know too much about tea, but I drank Matcha latte at the shop twice and found the matcha flavor to be very clear and delicate, not overwhelmed by the rich taste of milk like many other shops.
+ The price is quite high, from 150 - 200k/cup of matcha latte, but I find it suitable for the quality.
+ Spacious, elegant, quiet space. Some days the service is fine, some days it's a bit difficult.

Name: NAGOCHA Matcha Specialty & Tea House Hanoi Westlake
Author: Violet Medarico
Review: Best matcha I’ve had in Hanoi so far. Most of their matcha are rich in umami and Yame is my favorite. The place wasn’t as crowded as other Vietnamese places and location is really cool next to a lake. Oddly, I love their bathroom and sink in there. Even the soap was in greentea scent.

Name: NAGOCHA Matcha Specialty & Tea House Hanoi Westlake
Author: Anna
Review: By far my most favourite branch in Vietnam. I came here twice and I just love it. Such a huge selection of different matcha powders and desserts. The staff are really friendly. There are so many different places to sit. If you enjoy match I highly recommend you come here.

Name: NAGOCHA Matcha Specialty & Tea House Hanoi Westlake
Author: Eileen Lui
Review: More points for the zen atmosphere. However, alot of the menu items were sold out when I was there. Only tried the Sparkling Matcha Yuzu and the Charcoal Croffle with Houjicha Ice Cream. The sparkling Matcha Yuzu was nice but the croffle and ice cream was really quite bad - the croffle was literally like rubber. Anyway... an experience sure.

Name: Bách Trà Tea & Coffee
Author: Tran Hang
Review: Nice place for teamwork, peaceful. good drinks, service good, always here with friends when have free time

Name: Bách Trà Tea & Coffee
Author: D P
Review:
Good price, good quality and good service.
This is one of the very few places that you get all three.
And my Vietnamese girlfriend really likes when I take her here too.

Name: Bách Trà Tea & Coffee
Author: peter madden
Review: This is a lovely tea and coffee shop, stylishly decorated modern and sophisticated with a great ambiance and very helpful and attentive staff offering a wide range of drinks.  It's a great place to meet and relax away from the hustle and bustle of the city.

Name: Bách Trà Tea & Coffee
Author: Be Lan Anh
Review: Lovely space, fresh fruit for drink, high quality tea, special green Thai Nguyen tea

Name: Bách Trà Tea & Coffee
Author: Hsieh wen-jin
Review: Peaceful and service acceptable,tea taste good

Name: Bach Hac Tea
Address: Ng. 215 P. Tô Hiệu, Dịch Vọng
Author: lostinindo
Review: My fav tea house so far in Hanoi. As I don't really into sweet drinks, this place is perfect, as most of their tea is unsweetened. Perfect place to hang out during cold weather or rainy day. It's located on the not too busy street too, so it's great

Name: Bach Hac Tea
Address: Ng. 215 P. Tô Hiệu, Dịch Vọng
Author: Samuel Gutknecht
Review: Nice and cosy place with beautiful decor and friendly staff. A big variety of different drinks. The tea was a bit bitter for my taste but apparently the older generation of Vietnamese people prefer it that way. I'll definitely go back there and try different tea!

Name: Bach Hac Tea
Address: Ng. 215 P. Tô Hiệu, Dịch Vọng
Author: Guillaume
Review: Staff is nice and efficient. It is quiet and proper to relax. They have many sort of teas, which are, I guess, based on asian medicine. You can probably find a tea that cure you in case of you lack of sleeping of your muscles hurts. Ask the staff, they are helpful when it come to choose a tea, as there are dozens of them on the menu.

Name: Bach Hac Tea
Address: Ng. 215 P. Tô Hiệu, Dịch Vọng
Author: Long Han
Review: Very good place to met close friend, fantatic tasty tea, really for relax

Name: Bach Hac Tea
Address: Ng. 215 P. Tô Hiệu, Dịch Vọng
Author: Clack Nguyễn (Tùng Bách Aka Clack Ng)
Review: Good

Name: Matecha Hidden Tea House (Ajisai)
Address: 17/9 P. Đặng Thai Mai, Quảng An
Author: Cảnh Mai
Review: Cozy space, friendly people. This place has a friendly owner, soft drink and tea. I like to come here just sitting down, smoking and talking to him. Such a nice place for those who want to explore hidden places around the Hanoi city. I really the vibe here

Name: Matecha Hidden Tea House (Ajisai)
Address: 17/9 P. Đặng Thai Mai, Quảng An
Author: Mauro Leccese
Review: Everyone is super nice, the MATE is amazing

Name: Matecha Hidden Tea House (Ajisai)
Address: 17/9 P. Đặng Thai Mai, Quảng An
Author: Giáp Nguyễn Hữu
Review: Ok

Name: Matecha Hidden Tea House (Ajisai)
Address: 17/9 P. Đặng Thai Mai, Quảng An
Author: Banh My Choripana

Name: Matecha Hidden Tea House (Ajisai)
Address: 17/9 P. Đặng Thai Mai, Quảng An
Author: Marcus Håkansson

Name: Four Spring Tea House
Author: Peter Brinkhoff
Review:
Very cosy and relaxing place with nice tea and calm music. Enough distance between the tables make it a good place to work or having private conversations. Seating is on 2 floors and each seat has a power plug at arms length. WiFi is at top speed, too.
The only downside is the noisy street in front of the tea shop, that is slightly affecting the setting.

Name: Four Spring Tea House
Author: Jefferson Liew
Review:
There is another listing for this cafe which provides a more accurate direction to the place!

This cafe is along the main road! With it’s Vietnamese name on the banner so it’s difficult to recognise this shop for non-locals…

Personally like the cafe interior a lot and the convenience provided such as charging ports!

The drinks are not to my preference but it’s still quite crowded! It’s a great cafe as it somehow separates the different levels for different activities.

Level 1 is quite noisy, while the upper levels are really nice to do your own work!

Name: Four Spring Tea House
Author: Anh Thư
Review: One of the best places to chill and enjoy a tasty cup of iced tea. The location is quaint and nicely designed, spacious and very quiet the day I visited it. The design of the balcony provides a barrier between the chaotic street outside and the peaceful space.

Name: Four Spring Tea House
Author: Dave Anderli
Review:
Nice tea place… good vibe…
Bring your laptop.

Name: Four Spring Tea House
Author: Ngan Nguyen
Review: Tea for the tea enthusiasts, brewed very nicely. Service was friendly too.

Name: The Lanes Café Tea Room
Address: 05 P. Hồ Xuân Hương, Nguyễn Du
Author: Jasmine Smith
Review: This was such a treat, it's literally like walking into a tearoom at home, cute British tearoom styling and proper British cream tea set. Excellent quality food that literally tastes like you're in an English tearoom, the scones were cooked perfectly and to have smoked salmon was such a treat! This was everything I need after having been travelling for 4 months. I'm an absolute British teapot and It's not just 'good for foreign cream tea', If I'd eaten this in the UK I would be equally happy with the food and experience! Thank you and totally recommend!

Name: The Lanes Café Tea Room
Address: 05 P. Hồ Xuân Hương, Nguyễn Du
Author: Gilyn Diampa
Review: The scones and pastries are delicious and the Mayfair tea is really good! Would love to come back for more scones and tea!

Name: The Lanes Café Tea Room
Address: 05 P. Hồ Xuân Hương, Nguyễn Du
Author: kathy Lau
Review: We had the afternoon tea set filled with delicious toast, scones and cakes. Great balance in flavour. Aromatic milk olong tea. The coffee sticky pudding was a good choice to add on. Love the deco, great to chill for hours.

Name: The Lanes Café Tea Room
Address: 05 P. Hồ Xuân Hương, Nguyễn Du
Author: Ngan Nguyen
Review:
It really felt like I walked through Doraemon’s magic door and went back to a British tearoom at The Lanes. Brought back so many beautiful memories when I was living in Brighton 7 years ago. The Lanes tearoom was very cosy and nostalgic.
I tried their cream tea which was authentic and delicious. Highly recommend their milky oolong with its warm vanilla scent but still very light. Also tried their eggnog (my favourite Christmas drink!) and it was amazing too.
The bill came to 400k VND for 2 and we were very full.

Name: The Lanes Café Tea Room
Address: 05 P. Hồ Xuân Hương, Nguyễn Du
Author: May Luong
Review: Will definitely come back next time!

Name: Teahouse.vn - Tea & Gifts
Author: N/A
Review: N/A

Name: BeMine Teahouse
Address: 3 P. Phạm Tuấn Tài, Dịch Vọng Hậu
Author: Laour Med
Review: A fresh, cosy, and chilling atmosphere at Bemine.

Name: BeMine Teahouse
Address: 3 P. Phạm Tuấn Tài, Dịch Vọng Hậu
Author: FAN Cuong
Review: A nice Cafe ! Recommended 👍

Name: BeMine Teahouse
Address: 3 P. Phạm Tuấn Tài, Dịch Vọng Hậu
Author: Wu Mark
Review: The layout of my bakery counter is not reasonable. The smell of frying affects customers sitting and drinking.

Name: BeMine Teahouse
Address: 3 P. Phạm Tuấn Tài, Dịch Vọng Hậu
Author: Lê Công Kiên
Review: Cool space, small restaurant but kind and quick service

Name: BeMine Teahouse
Address: 3 P. Phạm Tuấn Tài, Dịch Vọng Hậu
Author: Tú Bá
Review: The drinks are super delicious but the staff's attitude (a guy named Quan) is very poor

Name: Link's Tea Room
Address: Nhà B2, B2 P. Trần Huy Liệu, Giảng Võ
Author: Thanhhang Vu
Review: There are tables and chairs both outside and inside the restaurant. There is air conditioning, wifi, fan, anti-mosquito lamp. The toilet is clean and dry. The decor shop has a tea and cake corner for virtual living. The menu is not too diverse, but I ordered orange and pineapple juice, which was fine, 45k. The shop is located in a dormitory area so it is not crowded and quite quiet

Name: Chà Trà Tea Room
Address: 5 Ngõ 73 Giang Văn Minh, Đội Cấn
Author: ismadina ismail
Review: Love this place, the place is so warm, and we get lot of experience and knowledge about vietnamese tea. After drinking the tea, my body feels so warm and more refreshed.

Name: Chà Trà Tea Room
Address: 5 Ngõ 73 Giang Văn Minh, Đội Cấn
Author: Sen Nguyen
Review:
I recently went to a cozy tea place, and it was really nice. The space is warm and comfy, and the owner is super friendly and kind. They have a bunch of really cool and pretty teapots.

They've got a lot of different kinds of old Vietnamese teas, which is pretty cool if you're into that. It's like a trip back in time with each sip. The variety is great, and you can tell they love their tea.

But what I liked the most was the people there. Everyone was nice, and it felt like a place where you can just chill and chat. It's a cool spot to meet people who love tea just like you. If you want a simple, warm place with good tea and friendly faces, this is it.

Name: Chà Trà Tea Room
Address: 5 Ngõ 73 Giang Văn Minh, Đội Cấn
Author: Oliver Schusdziarra
Review:
So nice place
Fre tea and culture to try learn and feel
Super friendly host and beautiful family
Thank you so much
I can not recommended it more for foreigners

Your friends from Germany and Korea

Name: Chà Trà Tea Room
Address: 5 Ngõ 73 Giang Văn Minh, Đội Cấn
Author: Klára Trsková
Review: This is literally my best moment in Hanoi! I was very well welcomed, I ate wonderful rice sweets, chatted with the other guests about tea and life during a tea ceremony, was invited for dinner and drank various delicious teas, my favourite came from a 450 years old tea tree. Can't express how wonderful this place is!

Name: Chà Trà Tea Room
Address: 5 Ngõ 73 Giang Văn Minh, Đội Cấn
Author: Toby Wade
Review:
An amazing experience. If you love tea, this place is a must-visit.

This is different to any other tea house you will ever visit. This is simply a sharing of generous hospitality, Vietnamese culture and a love of tea.

My favourite experience in Hanoi.

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

Name: Royal Tea Láng Hạ
Author: LixiL Thiết bị vệ sinh Inax
Review: 300m from home but still order online now. The delivery guy was friendly, but it was reported that the store staff lacked enthusiasm and was very chaotic. Because one of the items I ordered was out of stock, the delivery guy asked the employee (at my request) if there was any other item at the same price, but the employee turned away and did not answer. When I asked the shipping guy to see if there was any matcha left and order it for me, but the matcha was also out of stock. Because phone exchanges were limited and the store staff were uncooperative, I had to order bt pearl milk tea and the difference was not deducted. Even though I received a cup of milk tea, it was still labeled as fresh milk with black sugar pearls and still cost 55k. . Quality: bland milk tea. It's not light because there's no sugar, it's light because the amount of all the main ingredients (tea flavor, milk flavor) is small. Drink like water mixed with sugar. Not as good as the franchised royaltea stores far from the center like Kim Dong or Tran Quoc Hoan. I also like the light taste like Gorky's is also light but this bland milk tea makes me really surprised and disappointed. . The shop's signature dish is red fruit tea, which is nothing special except that it's twice the size of a cup of bt tea and unusually bland. . . Shipper reported that waiting for the shop staff to make the items was unbearable. I also felt the same way because I was only 300m away from home but more than 20 minutes later I saw the shipper telling me to pick up the goods while there were 2 simple cups of tea. . . In short, even shippers are bored with the royaltea franchise's way of working at this location. I don't want to say more.

Name: Royal Tea Láng Hạ
Author: Phong vu Lai
Review: Worked for a long time

Name: Royal Tea Láng Hạ
Author: SungChul Jung
Review: Toppings can be added to sweet tea

Name: Royal Tea Láng Hạ
Author: Thao Nv
Review: Ok laah

Name: Royal Tea Láng Hạ
Author: Hùng Nguyễn
Review: Lam cham

Name: Daisy Tea Lounge (Tiệm Trà Hoa Cúc)
Author: Phạm Huy
Review: Delicious milk tea, beautiful shop

Name: ArtiShan Tea & Coffee
Author: Phuong Nguyen
Review:
The best brewed coffee shop in the area and Vietnamese tea are also delicious. You must try Yen Phu coffee, red tea set and lemon ice tea (for hot day)
Highly recommend

Name: ArtiShan Tea & Coffee
Author: Stefan Koch
Review: Very nice folks who run the place! Try the Red Tea for its unique flavor.

Name: ArtiShan Tea & Coffee
Author: Søren Madsen
Review: Let me be the first to confirm here that the hot cappuccino here is unbelievably good.

Name: ArtiShan Tea & Coffee
Author: Nguyễn Quỳnh Phương Anh
Review: Delicious matcha, reasonable price, comfortable seating, not overpriced like some matcha shops today

Name: Bụt Tea & Coffee
Author: Wray Buntine
Review: Wonderful place to chill out, read, study

Name: Bụt Tea & Coffee
Author: Nguyen Hoang Ha
Review: Very nice and quiet place, nice view and decoration, located in a new and modem infrastructure place

Name: Bụt Tea & Coffee
Author: Trần Quốc Tuấn
Review: Normal place :)

Name: Bụt Tea & Coffee
Author: Wernen Sandamal
Review: I love this place

Name: Bụt Tea & Coffee
Author: The Royal Spa
Review:
Buddha Tea Shop really brings me a wonderful experience with a quiet and peaceful space. From the moment I entered, I felt the quiet atmosphere, far different from the noisy life outside. The shop is delicately designed with rustic wooden architecture, combined with the gentle scent of tea and flowers, creating a relaxing space full of meditation.

In particular, But Tra Quan has many beautiful little corners, from the sitting area on the wooden floor to small tables, surrounded by the green color of bonsai trees and lush green climbing plants. This makes me feel connected to nature in the closest and most natural way.

Regarding tea, the shop has many types of herbal tea and traditional tea, with elegant flavors, helping to relax the mind. I especially like the lotus tea here.
The service staff is also very gentle and delicate, making the tea time a truly fulfilling moment. This is the ideal place to find tranquility and balance amidst the hustle and bustle of life.

Name: Andy Coffee And Tea House
Address: 110 Đ. Trần Nhật Duật, Hàng Buồm
Author: K
Review: Was looking for breakfast places in the wee hours (6am~) and this store was beside the bus meeting point. Was ran by two lovely ladies. They even man the banh mi store outside (Banh Mi Sai Gon). The bac xue was great, abit of coconut taste and very creamy. There is only pork options for Banh mi, the bread was crushed and toasted. Absolutely delicious.

Name: Andy Coffee And Tea House
Address: 110 Đ. Trần Nhật Duật, Hàng Buồm
Author: Lee Lee
Review:
Delicious coffee and great service.

@lena__stumpf

Name: Andy Coffee And Tea House
Address: 110 Đ. Trần Nhật Duật, Hàng Buồm
Author: Kai Allison
Review: The best coffee I had while in Vietnam and very friendly service.

Name: Andy Coffee And Tea House
Address: 110 Đ. Trần Nhật Duật, Hàng Buồm
Author: Anh Han
Review: So good ! I like the bread.

Name: Andy Coffee And Tea House
Address: 110 Đ. Trần Nhật Duật, Hàng Buồm
Author: Snow Ball
Review: very nice

Name: Cửu Thuỷ Liêm Fengshui & Teahouse - Trà Thất Phong Vị Á Đông
Author: Ộp Vũ
Review: Elegant Teahouse inspired with Indo-chine interior, must visit!

Name: Cửu Thuỷ Liêm Fengshui & Teahouse - Trà Thất Phong Vị Á Đông
Author: Minh Ngô
Review: Great choice for foreigners who want to enjoy traditional Vietnamese tea around the West Lake

Name: Cửu Thuỷ Liêm Fengshui & Teahouse - Trà Thất Phong Vị Á Đông
Author: Nest Asia Travel
Review: Good location for tea room. Just next to the West Lake

Name: Cửu Thuỷ Liêm Fengshui & Teahouse - Trà Thất Phong Vị Á Đông
Author: Duy Nguyen
Review: Good Tea & Peaceful Spaces

Name: Cửu Thuỷ Liêm Fengshui & Teahouse - Trà Thất Phong Vị Á Đông
Author: Rồi Em Chừa
Review: The shop is beautiful, clean, delicious tea. The owner is very enthusiastic. If there is a chance I will come back

Name: Ổ Mèo Tea House
Address: 108 C1 P. Hoàng Ngọc Phách, Láng Hạ
Author: Yuvaraj Subramani
Review: Peace✌️

Name: Ổ Mèo Tea House
Address: 108 C1 P. Hoàng Ngọc Phách, Láng Hạ
Author: Chizz Pách
Review: ok

Name: Ổ Mèo Tea House
Address: 108 C1 P. Hoàng Ngọc Phách, Láng Hạ
Author: Minh Lê
Review:
A true cat nest. Entering the shop, there were two "4-legged" security guards lying on their backs ready to rub their stomachs. The "4-legged" staff members come from many different countries, but they are a bit arrogant. They have to be fed to be touched, but that's fun.

Bought a drink combo + entrance ticket for 55k/ticket (including 1 cat food bar) but the drinks were so boring, the lemon tea looked like it was made from a nestea tea packet, the milk tea was too sweet. The main thing that comes here is to play with cat friends, the restaurant should improve the water.

Name: Ổ Mèo Tea House
Address: 108 C1 P. Hoàng Ngọc Phách, Láng Hạ
Author: Bích Ngọc Nguyễn
Review:
I arrived at the restaurant quite early, just in time to feed the children. Every baby here is big and round 😂 Before entering the shop, there are 2 cute puppies welcoming you enthusiastically and friendly at the door.

I just came to play, I didn't drink water. Dill combo is 55k including water + playing with cats and ticket to play is 30k. So I can't rate the quality of the drinks here.

The children are very friendly. Almost every baby is allowed to stroke and scratch their neck and belly comfortably. There is one baby who is the most adorable, which is the Egyptian cat. Sitting on you the whole time and not leaving at all. When I picked it up to go home, the baby bit me once :)) The last photo has a cat sleeping on my bag. Every baby is cute

Compared to the price, I think it's cheap here. The way to the restaurant is easy to find. Park your car right in front of the shop, remember to lock it

Name: Ổ Mèo Tea House
Address: 108 C1 P. Hoàng Ngọc Phách, Láng Hạ
Author: Linh Đinh
Review: The cats here are diverse and friendly, only one employee has an attitude of wanting to punch customers. Delicious food, clean space. 10/10

Name: (Tra Hoa) Tea House Shop
Address: 132 P. Bùi Thị Xuân, Street, Ward
Author: Hoàng Hòa
Review: Exactly the space needed for a tea shop: Airy, clean, quiet; Sometimes there are guests who talk a bit loudly. What I like best is that the shop is non-smoking :)

Name: (Tra Hoa) Tea House Shop
Address: 132 P. Bùi Thị Xuân, Street, Ward
Author: なごみん
Review:
This is a tea shop where you take off your shoes and sit on the tatami floor.
We have jasmine tea and oolong tea, but we recommend chrysanthemum tea!
The sweetness and bitterness are just right.
Pour hot water from the bot yourself, put the lid on, let it steep for a few minutes, then remove the lid and drink.
It seemed like a drink that had to be refilled over and over again, so I was able to enjoy the tea culture in its own way.

Name: Yu Tang Tea House 玉堂

Review:
Unique combo of a bubble tea cafe and a noodle shop.
Upstairs area was mainly used by people studying, working or socializing so we stayed on the main floor for dining.
Bubble tea was big, not too sweet with 50% sugar. Beef noodles was great with tasty noodles and soft, tender beef. Nice portion too. Mixed noodles teriyaki portion looked a bit smaller, but eventually it was just right.
Price wise a bit higher than most.

Review: The bubble tea here is amazing and not too sweet like at other places. The decor is very simple and modern. There is a good amount of comfortable seating along with a porch area that looks out on the street below. I love working on my laptop for hours at a time, sipping on my boba. Staff is very professional and they keep the  place clean.  Highly recommend getting tea here.

Name: Hien Minh Tea House
Address: No. 13 P. Ngô Tất Tố, Văn Miếu
Author: Emilia Nieminen
Review:
On our first visit I gave the place a glowing 5 star review. Coming back we encountered a hostile and rude staff member taking a break. She took one of the kittens feeding off the mother cats breast, hit it twice and then sat next to us watching loud videos. We asked her to please stop as we came to enjoy the tranquility of tea ceremony, but she continued even after another staff member asked her not to. It was like she was being purposefully obnoxious: showing us the soles of her feet ( I read this gesture is very rude in Vietnam) and cracking her bare toes loudly. Completely ruined our experience. I wanted to come back every day next week, but now I don't want to come again. Just horribly rude and who hits a kitten?!

My original review:
"Beautiful and tranquil haven. Exquisite selection of tea , elegant service and super cute family of cats. If you are in Hanoi you cannot miss this treasure! It should have a Michelin star. I'm only here for two weeks but I think I need to come back many times to try more tea. Today we tried Full Moon white tea and Puer vintage 1994. Both were lovely, different and could be refilled 8-10 times (they will give you a thermos and fill it regularly). Perfect place for journaling, working on a laptop or just enjoying the tranquil atmosphere and music. Perfection!"

Name: Hien Minh Tea House
Address: No. 13 P. Ngô Tất Tố, Văn Miếu
Author: Farid Hamka
Review: An oasis of a teahouse. The interior and the donation directly makes anyone who enter quickly shift to a zen mode. The kind shopkeepers were helpful in choosing teas based on the season and our preferences. Recommending this place for a nice respite, perhaps around the time you visit the Van Mieu Temple of Literature.

Name: Hien Minh Tea House
Address: No. 13 P. Ngô Tất Tố, Văn Miếu
Author: Canan Üstündağ Ayçiçek
Review: I feel so lucky to find such a heavenly place and have the opportunity to taste their special tea selection. We visited this tea house in the morning after visiting the temple of literature in an extremely hot weather. We came from sa pa with night train in that morning with our backpacks and needed a relaxation moment so badly. Indoor area was so cosy with floor cushion seats and low tables, it was cool with fans. We had special lotus green tea, it has a dreamy delicate taste. And black aged tea has a earthy full bodied taste. She prepared tea with care and explained so well, she was so kind. Garden is so peaceful with all plants, decorations, pond with acuarium fishes and a lovely cat. There was no one except us and we had the opportunity to chill on our own enjoy the teas with more pour over for a long time. We bought some lotus tea while we leave and I can’t wait to prepare tea ceremonies at home too. It was such an exquisite experience, I recommend for the ones looking for a runaway from chaotic city center.

Name: Hien Minh Tea House
Address: No. 13 P. Ngô Tất Tố, Văn Miếu
Author: Lan-Elise Hoang
Review:
As a tea enthusiast, I love to see places like this tea house which are very rare. The selection of tea and teawares is great and the people there seem to be very passionate.
We only went there for a break in the morning and it was very peaceful. If I get the chance, I'd like to return there and buy some tea or teaware!

Name: Hien Minh Tea House
Address: No. 13 P. Ngô Tất Tố, Văn Miếu
Author: Johnathan MacDonald
Review: Without a doubt one of the best things I did. To experience this tea ceremony and get history and all the information was outstanding. My host had very good conversational english. You must do this.

Name: Piraha Vietnamese Tea & Coffee
Author: Parisara Chancharoen
Review:
Nice coffee with the train view. I reccommend this place if you're around this area
There're both traditional and Italian coffee menus to choose. I ordered iced latte which tastes nice and smell good. Staff can speak English and nice. The decor is great, but a bit hot inside. I visited there in early Sep. I sat outdoor seat and fortunately that there's train passing by.

Name: Piraha Vietnamese Tea & Coffee
Author: Aaron Filler
Review: Lovely place, lovely staff, lovely coffee. It's got lots of charm, quaint and quiet, which creates such a nice atmosphere. The staff are so friendly, kind and warm. The coffee tasted great (latte, 50Kđ), and they've got a great selection of teas too. Right near the mural Street and many hostels. Honestly it's a perfect little coffee shop, really recommend you support them if you're visiting.

Name: Piraha Vietnamese Tea & Coffee
Author: V T
Review:
Wonderful Café in the Heart of the City
Visited on October 12th and 13th, 2024, around 3:30 PM

A lovely café with a very pleasant atmosphere and charming decor. The soothing and well-chosen music creates a peaceful ambiance, making it the perfect spot to escape the hustle and bustle of the city. Despite its central location, it’s not as crowded as other nearby places – ideal for those seeking a quiet moment.

The staff are exceptionally friendly and talented, especially when it comes to tea preparation. I enjoyed both white and green tea, along with a warm slice of banana bread and a freshly made iced tea. Their recommendations were detailed and passionate, even in English – making the café a great option for international visitors.

The banana bread was served warm and perfectly baked. The iced tea was refreshing and not overly sweet, which is rare for this area. In addition to excellent teas, the café also offers various coffee and tea specialties, both hot and cold.

All in all, this has become my favorite place in the city to relax and enjoy some peace. I can highly recommended :)

Name: Piraha Vietnamese Tea & Coffee
Author: Canan Üstündağ Ayçiçek
Review: We wanted to cool down in a hot afternoon in Hanoi and visited this cafe. It is a cool area with aircon. Two ladies helped us, they are eager to communicate when you ask some questions but with limited English. We had black tea in teapot and americano. They both were very good. You can add hot water to the teapot and enjoy 5-6 more time. Banana cake was very good too.

Name: Piraha Vietnamese Tea & Coffee
Author: Janko Kyselica
Review: This little coffee is hidden gem. We have ordered different varieties of coffee and each one was delicious and also ordered the special vietnamese tea. The ladies took care of us and explained the right way of serving so we can enjoy it the right way. We have felt very welcomed here 🙏 thank you

Name: Eastern and Oriental Tea House and Coffee Parlour
Author: Kyle Danubio
Review:
The food here is generally very good! They have an excellent selection of cakes and baked goods and all breakfast items were very tasty!

I was a bit disappointed by the coffee options. All drinks were quite sweet which was something I would have expected more in HCMC… I ordered the “iced coffee” and was very surprised to find milk and cream both added in already.. I had just wanted a black iced coffee, but that’s partly my fault at most places in Vietnam will add sugar and milk by default.

Food aside, the restaurant is set in an older French style house. It’s quirky, but enjoyable. We sat on the second floor in what may have been a large bedroom at one point. It makes getting the servers attention a but challenging, but they are generally quite attentive. I think we’ll try the outdoor seating if we return in the future as it has a nice view if the lake and feels a bit more open.

Name: Eastern and Oriental Tea House and Coffee Parlour
Author: Gator of Wall Street
Review:
The restaurant was located within an old house facing the lake that gave diners some great views. As I walked into the restaurant, I was greeted and introduced to all the cakes on offer. The choices were simply too much. Being a cake person, naturally I selected a number of cakes to try. After ordering, I was guided to walked up to the higher levels to be seated. Naturally I walked up to the highest level to enjoy some great views. I ordered the eggs Benedict and egg coffee for brunch. I was given a choice to serve the cakes last. The egg Benedict was ok, but the egg coffee was really good. The cakes were also very tasty. The slices were huge and in the end, I couldn’t finish all of them.

Met a server (Nam Anh) who’s extremely friendly and hospitable. We ended up chatting with him while eating our food. He shared many aspects of the Vietnamese culture with us which was really interesting as a tourist. In the end, we spent about two hours there ordering more drinks.

Name: Eastern and Oriental Tea House and Coffee Parlour
Author: Anh NHQ (NoireGK-)
Review: A very nice place. The food is great and the staff members are very friendly. Even though the price of the food is a bit pricey to Vietnamese pricing, it is very worth it. The dessert is a definite must try too.

Name: Eastern and Oriental Tea House and Coffee Parlour
Author: Hailee
Review:
Has to be my favorite cafe place in the entire West Lake area! I'm blown away every time by their delicious pastries, and their tea is always superb, too. They're coming up with new recipes once in a while too, I wish I have enough chance to try all of their cakes LOL
I love sitting out on the balcony facing the lake, though some people might find it too hot during summer :)
Definitely in my top-rated list in Hanoi

Name: Eastern and Oriental Tea House and Coffee Parlour
Author: Amandeep Kaur
Review:
A good pit-stop on a solo walk around the neighborhood. The salmon bagel hit the spot, and the matcha crepe had a nice balance of sweetness. Portions were generous, as others have mentioned—I couldn't quite finish. The decor is kitschy and warm. It was peaceful and quiet - just the spot to unwind with a book or tackle some work.

The cake display area was a bit cramped and hectic, with freshly baked cakes casually stacked around, waiting for the final touches. You almost feel like you’re stepping on their toes.

While the staff were helpful enough, a welcoming smile would have elevated the experience. It's always the little touches.

Props for the clean restroom though!

Name: Thưởng Trà Quán
Address: nhà tập thể, 2E P. Tông Đản, Lý Thái Tổ
Author: Michael Marousek
Review: You know that the place is worth the effort you need to put into finding it…IT REALLY IS worth the effort though. Such a peaceful oasis in the heart of the bustling Hanoi. Knowledgeable and kind staff, phenomenal selection of teas, individualized approach! One of my best memories from Hanoi!!

Name: Thưởng Trà Quán
Address: nhà tập thể, 2E P. Tông Đản, Lý Thái Tổ
Author: Nguyen Nhi
Review: 4 stars for the staff quality and atmosphere. Minus 1 because the tea isn’t my type but it’s a decent change. Finished 3 pots of tea and the staff started to ask if i’m okay so i’m quite impressed. I might take a friend here next time if i want to hang out but i don’t think i’ll visit here if i just want tea

Name: Thưởng Trà Quán
Address: nhà tập thể, 2E P. Tông Đản, Lý Thái Tổ
Author: Desireé L.
Review: An incredible hidden gem in Hà Nội if you like tea. A lovely beautiful well curated space. Very hidden with no sign posting. A retreat from the hustle and bustle of the city. Can’t wait to be back. Be mindful smoking is allowed inside which may disturb some patrons.

Name: Thưởng Trà Quán
Address: nhà tập thể, 2E P. Tông Đản, Lý Thái Tổ
Author: Thuha Ng
Review: Feeling disappointed to visit that Vietnamese tea shop although I have already seen many positive reviews about this place. Tea taste is not notable or flavorful at all, just like other normal tea shops somewhere. They don’t provide any menu and price is really expensive, roughly vnd 300k for two pots of tea. White Crane tea is considered one of the best kind of tea there but it just like a dried Northern tea on Thai Nguyen hills. The Com cake is quite bad since it is not fine or smooth. The positive points are a friendly staff and a quite cozy place.

Name: Thưởng Trà Quán
Address: nhà tập thể, 2E P. Tông Đản, Lý Thái Tổ
Author: Tanya Matyushenko
Review: This tea house is fantastic! They have a wide variety of Vietnamese, Chinese, and Japanese teas. The teaware is beautiful and high quality. The music and atmosphere are perfect for relaxing. Highly recommend!

Name: Tiệm Khầy Coffee & Tea
Address: 9. B7 Ngõ 199 Trần Quốc Hoàn, Khu tập thể Đại học Sư phạm

This delicious restaurant is having a promotion

Review: I like the Egg coffee and the tea

Name: Moonie Tea & Coffee

Name: Rosier Fresh Tea & Coffee - Trần Quốc Vượng
Address: 10 P. Trần Quốc Vượng, Dịch Vọng Hậu
Author: Chan
Review: The store is cute and the price is average. A very clean and simple style of decoration. I got a chocolate drink with boba and the boba was very soft and chewy.

Name: Rosier Fresh Tea & Coffee - Trần Quốc Vượng
Address: 10 P. Trần Quốc Vượng, Dịch Vọng Hậu
Author: Hoa Hoa
Review:
The shop space is a bit small, but in return the virtual living corner is great
The shop's lights are bright so you don't have to worry about your skin getting dark when taking photos. Going around 4-6 a.m. is extremely quiet, so you can freely take photos without worrying about people getting stuck
In my opinion, the drinks are okay but not delicious. Price ranges from 35-60k

Name: Rosier Fresh Tea & Coffee - Trần Quốc Vượng
Address: 10 P. Trần Quốc Vượng, Dịch Vọng Hậu
Author: Đức Quang
Review: The shop has a parking garage, small space, and a modern style. The drinks here are quite good.

Name: Rosier Fresh Tea & Coffee - Trần Quốc Vượng
Address: 10 P. Trần Quốc Vượng, Dịch Vọng Hậu
Author: Luân
Review:
Friendly staff and delicious drinks
The space is small but quite cozy

Name: Rosier Fresh Tea & Coffee - Trần Quốc Vượng
Address: 10 P. Trần Quốc Vượng, Dịch Vọng Hậu
Author: Linh Lương
Review: The shop has spacious space, modern decor style, pleasing to the eye and super delicious drinks

Name: NAGOCHA Matcha Specialty & Tea House Hanoi Westlake
Author: Chu Phuong Anh
Review: Pricey but superb Matcha shop in the Westlake. The location is just so chill. The quality of the drink is very fine because they serve imported Matcha from Japan. Mainly Kyoto and Nagoya. Very good taste. Good vibe. Good service. But yes, it comes with higher price than any other cafe in town. You can also shop for tea ceremony like matcha powder, chasen, mugs... there. I love it. I will definitely come back when my finance is better lol. Highly recommended spot for Matcha lovers!

Name: NAGOCHA Matcha Specialty & Tea House Hanoi Westlake
Author: Trịnh Minh
Review:
The shop specializes in Matcha, but the space and menu look really good.
+ The menu is divided into many types of matcha with different origins and flavor profiles, so the deliciousness will depend on whether you choose the type that suits your taste or not.
+ I don't know too much about tea, but I drank Matcha latte at the shop twice and found the matcha flavor to be very clear and delicate, not overwhelmed by the rich taste of milk like many other shops.
+ The price is quite high, from 150 - 200k/cup of matcha latte, but I find it suitable for the quality.
+ Spacious, elegant, quiet space. Some days the service is fine, some days it's a bit difficult.

Name: NAGOCHA Matcha Specialty & Tea House Hanoi Westlake
Author: Violet Medarico
Review: Best matcha I’ve had in Hanoi so far. Most of their matcha are rich in umami and Yame is my favorite. The place wasn’t as crowded as other Vietnamese places and location is really cool next to a lake. Oddly, I love their bathroom and sink in there. Even the soap was in greentea scent.

Name: NAGOCHA Matcha Specialty & Tea House Hanoi Westlake
Author: Anna
Review: By far my most favourite branch in Vietnam. I came here twice and I just love it. Such a huge selection of different matcha powders and desserts. The staff are really friendly. There are so many different places to sit. If you enjoy match I highly recommend you come here.

Name: NAGOCHA Matcha Specialty & Tea House Hanoi Westlake
Author: Eileen Lui
Review: More points for the zen atmosphere. However, alot of the menu items were sold out when I was there. Only tried the Sparkling Matcha Yuzu and the Charcoal Croffle with Houjicha Ice Cream. The sparkling Matcha Yuzu was nice but the croffle and ice cream was really quite bad - the croffle was literally like rubber. Anyway... an experience sure.

Name: IF YOU - Juice, Tea & Coffee
Author: Duc Anh Le Cong
Review: Nice decoration. Tasty and refreshing drinks. Good price also. Have nice and clear view if you want to stay and enjoy. Fast ready-to-go. Vietnam Football Federation’s favorite place!

Name: IF YOU - Juice, Tea & Coffee
Author: Bùi Phương Giang
Review: Beautiful shop, friendly staff, reasonably priced drinks.♥️

Name: IF YOU - Juice, Tea & Coffee

Review: Highly recommend the Soursop Tea, everyone!! The drinks are delicious!!

Name: IF YOU - Juice, Tea & Coffee

Review: Fruit tea is very delicious and refreshing, drink 1 cup and then want to drink another cup. Very delicious, everyone ❤️

Address: R3-B2 Đ. Nguyễn Trãi, Khu đô thị Royal City

Name: Là Mây Coffee & Tea
Author: K W
Review: This shop and its tea was the thing that I missed the most when I went back to my country.  I'm really happy to come back here!

Name: Là Mây Coffee & Tea
Author: Hoàng Hà Lê
Review:
Very nice designed Coffee shop, provided both cozy inhouse and airy space outside experience for coffee lover.
They are also selling handmade pottery originated from Bat Trang village.
5 stars for the drinks, location, and the sourvenirs.

Name: Là Mây Coffee & Tea
Author: U E
Review: Very friendly and helpful staff would crash here again 100%

Name: Là Mây Coffee & Tea
Author: Toan Nguyen
Review: A nice & unique coffee shop

Name: Là Mây Coffee & Tea

Review: Extremely friendly staff, beautiful girls. The drinks are ok. You should visit this shop with many beautiful corners

Address: 2 P. Trần Quý Kiên, Dịch Vọng

Review: If you're a fan of Manga they have tons of them, plus cool anime collector's item.

Address: 2 P. Trần Quý Kiên, Dịch Vọng
Author: Leer Mr
Review: Quiet shop, lots of entertainment like broadgame comics and games. There is a view on the 4th floor overlooking Cau Giay street. Drinks are quite cheap but delicious is not enough.

Name: Stellar Tea & Coffee
Address: 2 P. Trần Quý Kiên, Dịch Vọng
Author: Hoàng Anh Nguyễn D. (Kro)
Review: The space is beautiful and clean. Drinks are relatively good. There is a view on the 4th floor overlooking Cau Giay street which is quite airy. The 2nd floor has many interesting stories and items.

Name: Star Up Coffee & Tea
Author: Long Nguyen
Review:
Solid cafe served by really friendly staff! The place is also clean and cozy. I ordered an iced Americano here expecting an average cup, but their drink delivered a real kick. Will be back for more!
Edit: "salted coffee" (cà phê muối) is good too!

Name: Star Up Coffee & Tea
Author: Amy
Review: Lovely little cafe, the gentleman who served us was very friendly. We had coconut coffees and a butterfly pea lemonade, all of which were delicious!

Name: Star Up Coffee & Tea
Author: Dan Cerf
Review: Outstanding coffee with a nice outside table/chairs (at regular height!) and great service to boot!

Name: Star Up Coffee & Tea

Review: Coffee was so good I finished it before taking the pic. Highly recommended!!

Name: Star Up Coffee & Tea

Review: Cool drinks, clean and nice environment

Author: Tưởng Vương

Name: Andy Coffee And Tea House
Address: 110 Đ. Trần Nhật Duật, Hàng Buồm

Review: Was looking for breakfast places in the wee hours (6am~) and this store was beside the bus meeting point. Was ran by two lovely ladies. They even man the banh mi store outside (Banh Mi Sai Gon). The bac xue was great, abit of coconut taste and very creamy. There is only pork options for Banh mi, the bread was crushed and toasted. Absolutely delicious.

Name: Andy Coffee And Tea House
Address: 110 Đ. Trần Nhật Duật, Hàng Buồm
Author: Lee Lee
Review:
Delicious coffee and great service.

@lena__stumpf

Name: Hoth Coffee. and Tea
Author: Lucille Rodgers
Review: Fresh, tasty Vietnamese coffee and a lovely lady who served us. We had the coconut coffee and a white coffee - both delish. Cute little chairs for taking a breather while walking around the old quarter 😊

Name: Hoth Coffee. and Tea
Author: Andreea-Corina Rus
Review:
Delicious coffee made by a very cute and skilled lady.

In my opinion best salted coffee in Hanoi. ✨

Name: Hoth Coffee. and Tea
Author: Arlene Lum
Review: Great little cafe for morning coffee, yogurt, etc.  They have delicious drinks, reasonable prices, and quality offerings.  I absolutely love their coconut coffee, its the best!  And their passion fruit yogurt is delicious.  Their almond curved cookies are also a treat, so crispy, thin and delicious.  I walk extra blocks to come here!

Name: Hoth Coffee. and Tea
Author: Jan van Buul
Review: Good coconut coffee, salted coffee and the steamed bun with quail eggs was a nice snack to refuel. Also did a really quick breakfast here on our day to Ninh Binh. Good service, quick service and a lovely lady that will help you.

Name: Hoth Coffee. and Tea
Author: Chalee N.
Review: Egg coffee is yummy, sweet yet perfect bitterness. Cute decoration, friendly service.

Name: Ding Tea Kham Thien
Author: jordan camp
Review: Good

Name: Ding Tea Kham Thien
Author: Sơn Bình Tô
Review: Good

Name: Ding Tea Kham Thien
Author: Nguyễn Xuân Mai
Review: The restaurant is not an ideal place to sit in place. That's why I usually buy it to take home. The shop's milk tea is super delicious and I really like the way the shop makes it without too much ice like other shops. Will continue to support the restaurant.

Name: Ding Tea Kham Thien
Author: Việt Vũ
Review:
Just bought it tonight, asked a friend to accumulate points on the card, not sure if he accidentally entered 11 wrong numbers and couldn't get the name out, and didn't get a response.
I went home and looked at the new invoice but I couldn't earn points and discounts

Name: Ding Tea Kham Thien
Author: Anh Le
Review:
The shop is in an easy to find location. Diverse menu. Staff good attitude. The restaurant prepares dishes quickly.
Drinks taste right.

Name: Feeling Tea Minh Khai
Author: Nhi Nguyen
Review: I've always liked Feeling Tea's boba. Most common order for me is mint milk tea with black boba and jelly.

Name: Feeling Tea Minh Khai
Author: Luu Hoang Gia
Review: So bad

Name: Feeling Tea Minh Khai
Author: Hoàng Thu
Review: Not bad

Name: Feeling Tea Minh Khai
Author: Tân Khánh Phụ Tùng phụ kiện xe ô tô
Review: Ok

Name: Feeling Tea Minh Khai
Author: Quỳnh Hoàng
Review: I have been a regular customer of Feeling Tea Minh Khai since the shop first opened. I am very satisfied with the quality of service and drinks of the restaurant. Reasonable prices, enthusiastic and friendly staff, diverse menu of drinks from milk tea, hot tea, chocolate to cakes. The drink is still as delicious as the first day, not bland or too sweet. The shop also offers home delivery via ShopeeFood and GrabFood, so it's very convenient when I'm busy. I often drink chocolate milk tea and vanilla milk tea from the shop because I find it very suitable for my taste. I also like the spacious and clean restaurant space. I recommend you try Feeling Tea Minh Khai if you want to drink delicious food at a reasonable price.

Name: Royal Tea Láng Hạ
Author: LixiL Thiết bị vệ sinh Inax
Review: 300m from home but still order online now. The delivery guy was friendly, but it was reported that the store staff lacked enthusiasm and was very chaotic. Because one of the items I ordered was out of stock, the delivery guy asked the employee (at my request) if there was any other item at the same price, but the employee turned away and did not answer. When I asked the shipping guy to see if there was any matcha left and order it for me, but the matcha was also out of stock. Because phone exchanges were limited and the store staff were uncooperative, I had to order bt pearl milk tea and the difference was not deducted. Even though I received a cup of milk tea, it was still labeled as fresh milk with black sugar pearls and still cost 55k. . Quality: bland milk tea. It's not light because there's no sugar, it's light because the amount of all the main ingredients (tea flavor, milk flavor) is small. Drink like water mixed with sugar. Not as good as the franchised royaltea stores far from the center like Kim Dong or Tran Quoc Hoan. I also like the light taste like Gorky's is also light but this bland milk tea makes me really surprised and disappointed. . The shop's signature dish is red fruit tea, which is nothing special except that it's twice the size of a cup of bt tea and unusually bland. . . Shipper reported that waiting for the shop staff to make the items was unbearable. I also felt the same way because I was only 300m away from home but more than 20 minutes later I saw the shipper telling me to pick up the goods while there were 2 simple cups of tea. . . In short, even shippers are bored with the royaltea franchise's way of working at this location. I don't want to say more.

Name: Royal Tea Láng Hạ
Author: Phong vu Lai
Review: Worked for a long time

Name: Royal Tea Láng Hạ
Author: SungChul Jung
Review: Toppings can be added to sweet tea

Name: Royal Tea Láng Hạ
Author: Thao Nv
Review: Ok laah

Name: Royal Tea Láng Hạ
Author: Hùng Nguyễn
Review: Lam cham

Name: Vườn Nắng Coffee & Tea
Address: 65 P. Nguyễn Hy Quang, Chợ Dừa
Author: long quang
Review: Enjoy the very Vietnamese coffee espresso with the rooftop view of Hanoi. Lovely atmosphere, warm and peaceful at any time of the day.

Name: Vườn Nắng Coffee & Tea
Address: 65 P. Nguyễn Hy Quang, Chợ Dừa
Author: Thảo Nguyễn
Review: Nice and peaceful place. At 9th floor with good city and sky view

Name: Vườn Nắng Coffee & Tea
Address: 65 P. Nguyễn Hy Quang, Chợ Dừa
Author: Linh Bui
Review: Ohhh, I love beverage menu here.. All is delicious, specially the creamy coffee.. Enjoying coffee while working is the most comfortable experience..

Name: Vườn Nắng Coffee & Tea
Address: 65 P. Nguyễn Hy Quang, Chợ Dừa
Author: TRỊNH THỊ THÔNG
Review: The first time we visited, the shop was so beautiful, the owner was enthusiastic and friendly. Even though the shop was closed, the owner still opened the shop for us to come in, the drinks were beautiful and delicious, I promise to come back many times 🥰 the space is very chill. Anyway, bth doesn't rate any restaurant, but this restaurant has to be rated 🤣

Name: Vườn Nắng Coffee & Tea
Address: 65 P. Nguyễn Hy Quang, Chợ Dừa
Author: HN02 Lô Thị Ánh Tuyết
Review: The shop is located on the 9th floor of a building, lovely and clean, with gentle and pleasant music. We arrived at an empty hour so we really liked it because it was quiet. I drank coconut water with pandan leaves or something, it was delicious and refreshing. Overall, I am very satisfied with this experience. Prefer.

Name: HẺM Garden Cafe & Tea
Address: Ng. 1 Phố Nghĩa Tân, Khu tập thể Nghĩa Tân
Author: John Dean
Review: Garden café in populair area. Nice drinks for a very reasonable price. Juices were pure and well made.

Name: HẺM Garden Cafe & Tea
Address: Ng. 1 Phố Nghĩa Tân, Khu tập thể Nghĩa Tân
Author: Jo Ko
Review: Bright team in a stunning place to enjoy local coffees ☕. Lovely 😍

Name: HẺM Garden Cafe & Tea
Address: Ng. 1 Phố Nghĩa Tân, Khu tập thể Nghĩa Tân
Author: Phuong Nguyen Minh
Review: A best place to relax and enjoy coffee.

Name: HẺM Garden Cafe & Tea
Address: Ng. 1 Phố Nghĩa Tân, Khu tập thể Nghĩa Tân
Author: Ngoc Ngoc
Review: Quite place and drink is cheap

Name: HẺM Garden Cafe & Tea
Address: Ng. 1 Phố Nghĩa Tân, Khu tập thể Nghĩa Tân
Author: Nga Le
Review: Nice & quiet place, delicious drinks

Name: Beeyan Tea Vietnam - 5 Trần Thái Tông
Address: 5 P.Trần Thái Tông, Dịch Vọng Hậu
Author: 29.Trần Thu Hiền
Review: Super fast shop with enthusiastic staff. The drinks are nothing to criticize

Name: Beeyan Tea Vietnam - 5 Trần Thái Tông
Address: 5 P.Trần Thái Tông, Dịch Vọng Hậu
Author: Thanh Trần
Review: The restaurant serves enthusiastically and attentively, delicious drinks at reasonable prices. Lsau will continue to support the shop

Name: Beeyan Tea Vietnam - 5 Trần Thái Tông
Address: 5 P.Trần Thái Tông, Dịch Vọng Hậu
Author: Quỳnh Anh Viên
Review: Delicious food, friendly enthusiastic staff, chill view

Name: Beeyan Tea Vietnam - 5 Trần Thái Tông
Address: 5 P.Trần Thái Tông, Dịch Vọng Hậu
Author: Hạnh Phạm
Review: Good quality, delicious drinks. The staff are very enthusiastic and happy with guests.

Name: Beeyan Tea Vietnam - 5 Trần Thái Tông
Address: 5 P.Trần Thái Tông, Dịch Vọng Hậu
Author: mtrangg
Review: The shop chooses good music, ogi drinks, and enthusiastic staff.

Name: Phuc Long Coffee & Tea Royal City
Address: 72A Đ. Nguyễn Trãi, Khu đô thị Royal City
Author: Valéry Gibson
Review: Latte was not good :/ maybe the teas options are better.

Name: Phuc Long Coffee & Tea Royal City
Address: 72A Đ. Nguyễn Trãi, Khu đô thị Royal City
Author: Tran Nghia
Review: Ok.

Name: Phuc Long Coffee & Tea Royal City
Address: 72A Đ. Nguyễn Trãi, Khu đô thị Royal City
Author: Thế Hiển Hoàng
Review: Ok

Name: Phuc Long Coffee & Tea Royal City
