Author: Jonas K
Review: Tasty coffee with a good money for value

Name: Phuc Long Coffee & Tea Royal City
Address: 72A Đ. Nguyễn Trãi, Khu đô thị Royal City
Author: Long Pham
Review: Very nice coffee and tea

Name: The Hope Coffee-Tea-Milktea
Author: vo hoang giang
Review: Small nicely cafeteria.

Name: The Hope Coffee-Tea-Milktea
Author: BodoiCuHo
Review: Chill chill!

Name: The Hope Coffee-Tea-Milktea
Author: Phương Đỗ Đức
Review: Good coffee

Name: The Hope Coffee-Tea-Milktea
Author: Trịnh Duy Anh
Review: Normal coffee is said to be brewed for a while, but the shop is extremely crowded with people jostling, the crowd is incomprehensible, maybe it's the fate of the shop owner, if you accidentally sit in a cafe at 7am, you won't be able to get a car at 8am, even though it's a few meters away. The right hand has Tora, the right hand has salt coffee, sitting inside the air conditioning is extremely cold, the right hand can hold a plastic chair and fly to the surrounding houses to sit more comfortably. Next to it there is a row of sticky rice for breakfast coffee. Sitting 1 Twice to feel it is fine, but sitting for a long time is quite tiring

Name: An House - Tea & Coffee
Author: Quynh Nguyen Thi Nhu
Review: The restaurant is so good❤️❤️ the drinks are also delicious and delicious❤️ I recommend everyone to visit An House to try it outaaaaaa

Name: An House - Tea & Coffee
Author: Nguyen Van Vuong
Review: Spacious, airy space, delicious drinks at affordable prices.

Name: An House - Tea & Coffee
Author: Luyến Phạm
Review: Delicious drinks and nice, cheerful staff

Name: An House - Tea & Coffee
Author: Nga Nguyễn
Review: I really like the restaurant space, delicious drinks, and especially like the enthusiastic and thoughtful service.

Name: An House - Tea & Coffee
Author: Lê Việt Dương
Review: The employees are so beautiful 🥰🥰

Name: Feeling Tea - Milk Tea
Author: Daniel Kurth
Review: Best tea I had in Vietnam. Such a big variety and very kind staff!

Name: Feeling Tea - Milk Tea
Author: Eva Thai
Review: Diversity of drinks with cheaper prices in comparison with other milk tea shops. Place was small with limited seats. It could smell somtimes. No parking lot.

Name: Feeling Tea - Milk Tea
Author: Gerald Kusche
Review: Worst burger I have ever eaten, from the microwave. Stay away. A few metres further down is Lotteria Burgeria!

Name: Feeling Tea - Milk Tea
Author: SufferingRin
Review: Not really the cheapest place to get milk tea. The quality is OK, which isn't great.

Name: Feeling Tea - Milk Tea
Author: Hồng Quân Trần
Review: Cheap prices, good place to find, normal products quality and service

Name: Minh Lam Teahouse
Address: Marina Arc, Khu đô thị Ecopark
Author: Nhung Bùi
Review: I love the peaceful vibe and the interior design of the Tea house shop. It features a series of specialty in clever way with multiple of the brand stories. It also has an amazing piano so people can play it. I ordered a milk tea and I like the flavor of the tea here, it’s fragrant slightly and sweet enough. The staffs are so gentle, elegant, well-trained and helpful. We came on a day they made a photo shooting for their advertising campaign, but they apologized us about the noisy they could make, we are impressed with the experience they created for customers. They also provide the space for Tea workshops on the 2nd floor if you wanna try one day. Highly recommended!

Name: Minh Lam Teahouse
Address: Marina Arc, Khu đô thị Ecopark
Author: Yen Pham
Review: It's likely showroom of tea culture than business place, I really enjoy the atmosphere here. Everything is perfect 🥰 and staff are so nice and friendly. I love to work or gather with friends and family here...highly recommended!!!

Name: Minh Lam Teahouse
Address: Marina Arc, Khu đô thị Ecopark
Author: Huy Nguyen
Review:
A must-go within Ecopark
My family cross by and really impressive with this house. Nice decoration, tranquillent space and tasty drinks
Next time I will try tea in second floor

Name: Minh Lam Teahouse
Address: Marina Arc, Khu đô thị Ecopark
Author: Teresa
Review: Highly recommend the salted coffee! It comes with a piece of rosemary, which is surprisingly enhancing the flavour of the coconut cream. All the staff are super friendly and speak English. They’ve truly put the satisfaction of their customers at heart and attend to all the details, like adjusting the richness of the latte for us, setting an extra fan, closing the curtain to reduce the heat, all without us asking. The tea house itself is very spacious. It has big tables for groups and quiet corners for individuals. The outdoor space has very comfortable cushioned chairs and shade. We’ve had a very contented time spent here. Thanks to all the staff!

Name: Minh Lam Teahouse
Address: Marina Arc, Khu đô thị Ecopark
Author: Ộp Vũ
Review:
Minh Lam might be one of the best tea house in Hanoi. There they have social teaspace & several private tearoom with different styles, cozy & elegant.
Many kind of special tea made in Vietnam, worth to try out as a tea-lover. Also their tea artist - tea master are knowledgeable about tea.

Will be back for more tea experience!

Name: Longing Cafe Tea Room
Address: ngõ 36 P. Vạn Bảo, Liễu Giai
Author: Woanly Gurl
Review:
The drink and cake is average but we love the atmosphere and the set up of the shop. Been here 2 times already and will come again.

The cakes look beautiful but probably do not taste as expected. We tried the vanilla coconut.

The staff are helpful and friendly.

Name: Longing Cafe Tea Room
Address: ngõ 36 P. Vạn Bảo, Liễu Giai
Author: Milo Tran
Review:
We got here on Friday night, but not crowded. The place is quite chilling. I got the teapot and my friend got the macchiato. The teapot comes out with some store-made ginger cookies. Those cookies have a light ginger smell & not that sweet. Their variation is limited, but I believe it's good enough cuz I was hard to choose.
We ordered a mousse cake as well n it is very impressive, not so sweet as a regular mousse. The inside of the mousse came from non expectation, but it was goood for sure

Name: Longing Cafe Tea Room
Address: ngõ 36 P. Vạn Bảo, Liễu Giai
Author: John Dean
Review:
Cute little cafe with afresh made pastries and a nice selection of tea and coffee.
The hot and cold tea menu are refreshing to see. The Pandan Green Tea was flavorful and well presented.
The Espresso Chocolate was creamy on the outer circle, and more packed in the middle. Chocolate flavor was excellent.
Parking nearby on the playground.

Name: Longing Cafe Tea Room
Address: ngõ 36 P. Vạn Bảo, Liễu Giai
Author: Nguyen Tuan My
Review: Small tea shop but i think it is cute. Tea and pastry are nice. Must try Almond coconut, strawberry short cake is ok too. Tea is not so special to me but still better than some other shops 🤗. Located in quiet place and i like the environment.

Name: Longing Cafe Tea Room
Address: ngõ 36 P. Vạn Bảo, Liễu Giai
Author: Lucka Pokorná
Review:
Lovely place 🌸 nice and friendly staff 😊 I like the atmosphere here.
Try Banana bread! Delicious and Homemade! This bakery is so tasty and fresh.

Name: Ding Tea
Author: Jaideep Thombare
Review:
Quiet at the time when we visited. Highly under rated Bubble Tea shop.

The matcha tea with black pearls was brilliant. We could customise the quantity of ice, sugar and types of pearls.  The guys made the drink to perfection.

They were fluent in english and could help us with the order as well.

Name: Ding Tea
Author: Emma Jackson
Review: Very expensive, watery chocolate flavoured water. They didnt give us what we ordered and what they did give us was expensive, and cheap tasting.

Name: Ding Tea
Author: Mike VA
Review: The milk teas are delicious though a little expensive. All of the staff are very friendly and they all speak great English.

Name: Ding Tea
Author: Ralph Lauren
Review: Someone damaged my bike at this spot while I was getting some cups inside, and they wouldn't even be of good service to a 1st time customer. Now a last time customer! Bye

Name: Ding Tea
Author: Ahmad Aufa Bil Jihadi
Review: Suitable place, with indoor place, which fit for hanging out, and make a conversation with friends

Name: U-Tea Quan Hoa
Author: Hiệp Nguyễn
Review: One of the best tea shop I have ever tried. The staff is so helpful and polite. The decorations is absolutely wonderful and fashionable - such a great environment for innovative ideas.

Name: U-Tea Quan Hoa
Author: Kiyomi Maeda
Review:
Cute cafe. The young owner seems to be particular about tea leaves, and the tea menu is very delicious.
However, the menu is only in Vietnamese, so ordering is a little difficult for foreigners.
The staff can speak English.
I told the owner that I would like them to create an English menu and a menu with photos.
It just opened in the summer, so I look forward to future improvements.
We recommend the Aulong Chunchai, which is the third from the top left on the menu and costs VND45,000.
A cream brûlée-like custard cream is placed on top of the fragrant black tea.
Additionally, black and pearl tapioca are served separately. The black tea is moderately sweet, but you can choose to have it unsweetened or half-sweet.
This is a very satisfying item.

This is the kind of shop I would like to take home so I can drink this tea in Japan.

Name: U-Tea Quan Hoa
Author: Rhythm Love
Review: The space is spacious, the drinks are good. But the staff didn't really pay attention, I told them to heat up a dish, the staff confirmed it, but when I brought it up they still gave me ice. The shop also uses disposable utensils to store water. :

Name: U-Tea Quan Hoa
Author: hiệp phí minh
Review: The drinks are quite good! Relax and make a glass of water and have more energy to make money

Name: U-Tea Quan Hoa
Author: THUY TRAN
Review: Tried this shop on my way to work and was addicted. Try all of the restaurant's menu and the food is very good. Every 4th day, I lured the entire office to 29k already

Name: 薡茶 Ding Tea 109A Thụy Khuê
Author: Ninh Tran
Review:
A cosy place to hang out with friends
The price are reasonable for adults
Friendly staff
I study near this place so I visit it quite often
I would say this is a quite milk tea shop where you can sit alone or doing your homework

Name: 薡茶 Ding Tea 109A Thụy Khuê
Author: Ngoc Le Phuong Bao
Review: Good place to chill. The store is near Chu Van An Secondary School so it's usually not very crowded. Best suited for somebody looking for a place to do some writing or complete assignments.

Name: 薡茶 Ding Tea 109A Thụy Khuê
Author: Mang Tran
Review: Very poor drink quality. Not recommended

Name: 薡茶 Ding Tea 109A Thụy Khuê
Author: Binh Nguyen
Review: Dont like

Name: 薡茶 Ding Tea 109A Thụy Khuê
Author: Đức Mạnh Nguyễn
Review: No.1

Name: Phuc Long Coffee & Tea
Address: 283 P. Trần Quốc Hoàn, Khu tập thể Đại học Ngoại ngữ
Author: Phương Mai
Review: The shop is clean, beautiful, spacious and airy. Most importantly, the attitudes of all the staff and security guards are very lovely. The cool autumn weather is really wonderful to sit and watch the streets 😍 If any of the shop staff reads this review, I hope you will thank the security guards for their gentle and kind attitude. from you guys 🫂

Name: Phuc Long Coffee & Tea
Address: 283 P. Trần Quốc Hoàn, Khu tập thể Đại học Ngoại ngữ
Author: Hồng Quân Trần
Review: Nice restaurant, spacious space

Name: Phuc Long Coffee & Tea
Address: 283 P. Trần Quốc Hoàn, Khu tập thể Đại học Ngoại ngữ
Author: Triệu Kim Trường
Review: This place has beautiful autumn road views. Good drinks don't have to go far

Name: Phuc Long Coffee & Tea
Address: 283 P. Trần Quốc Hoàn, Khu tập thể Đại học Ngoại ngữ
Author: Có Gì Vui

Name: Phuc Long Coffee & Tea
Author: Ade Pinontoan
Review: Number 1 fans when I arrived at Vietnam. Reasonable price when you buy their product like tea leaves and ground/roasted beans coffee, room in full air conditioner, friendly staff and well known with english. Extremely reccomended here.

Name: Phuc Long Coffee & Tea
Author: Ding Mark
Review:
This location is quite good and I like how they setup the coffee shop here.

There are not so many people in the early morning around 9 am. It’s good to have a coffee while chilling here and try to got awaken by the coffee.

However, didn’t find the specialty from the iced latte, maybe I should give a try again for hot. (Vietnam is so hot in day time that you want to avoid ordering something hot)

They provide places for using laptops that you can work here and doing group studies or you might even gathering here with your friends.

Name: Phuc Long Coffee & Tea
Author: Ian
Review:
My experience was nothing compared to the 1-star review. The staff was very helpful, they spoke basic English and understood my order. The waiting time was normal, although only 6 people were waiting before me.

I really like the tea and I had the best seller, Phuc Long Milk Tea. It was properly boiled, and I could taste the bitterness of the tea, and the caffeine as well.

I bought the tea bags for souvenirs. They have various selections of flavors, lotus, lychee, black, oolong, jasmine, and peach. I'd recommend you to go here if you love tea. The price is standard like any other coffee shop, Phuc Long Milk Tea regular is 45k VND.

Name: Phuc Long Coffee & Tea
Author: Sunny Bhuiya
Review: Food isn't bad. Good atmosphere more importantly. Had the Phuc Long Milk Tea Large (50000 VND) and a Caramel Mousse slice (38000 VND).

Name: Phuc Long Coffee & Tea
Author: Dương Thuỳ
Review: So bad experience. Their drinks are 90% of ices. It costs me 70k for the drink. Their interior is getting so old and damaged and the smell of the space is like in a toilet. I would never come back again :((

Name: feeling tea khương hạ
Author: Anh Phan
Review: The first time I drank it, I thought it was okay, but I encountered an unenthusiastic staff with an unpleasant attitude. The staff was so empty and curt that I felt like I was an employee, not a customer who came to pay to receive service. Won't come back

Name: feeling tea khương hạ
Author: Tuyết Anh Mai
Review: Fast service. Personally, the drinks here are 100% sugar, so I often order 50% sugar or less. Recommend oolong tea rather than milk tea.

Name: feeling tea khương hạ
Author: Tuyền Nguyễn Thị
Review: Delicious drinks, enthusiastic and friendly staff.  I really like the taste of green tea here, it's very fragrant and delicious.

Name: feeling tea khương hạ
Author: Dinh Thuy Dung
Review: Drink it like a jasmine milk drink :)) not as delicious as the one at the temple. Really a waste of money

Name: feeling tea khương hạ
Author: Tuấn Kiệt
Review:
Feeling Tea Milk Tea - Khuong Ha

Location: Khuong Dinh bridge section, right at Khuong Ha - Vu Tong Phan intersection.

Space: Been here twice, once for takeout and once to sit at the restaurant on the first floor, found it a bit small.

Staff: It's late, but your child still asked to come over to buy milk tea for him. It's almost closing time, but the staff is still very friendly and enthusiastic with customers. I quite like the service attitude of the staff at this facility.

Drinks: Feeling Tea is the cheapest milk tea brand, so the quality of the drinks cannot be too high. It's only milk tea, so friends invite me in. I don't really like milk tea, I like it. More fruit tea. The taste and quality here are just like any other establishment, there is no difference.

Name: Canteen tea & coffee
Address: 23 P. Cao Bá Quát, Điện Biên
Author: M. P.
Review: Great coffee, very cozy and the baristas are super friendly. A bit hidden but worth checking out :)

Name: Canteen tea & coffee
Address: 23 P. Cao Bá Quát, Điện Biên
Author: quang tran
Review: lovely coffee, cool spot for coffee lovers

Name: Canteen tea & coffee
Address: 23 P. Cao Bá Quát, Điện Biên
Author: Trí Dũng
Review: very nice coffee shop. must try!

Name: Canteen tea & coffee
Address: 23 P. Cao Bá Quát, Điện Biên
Author: William Hayes
Review:
Nice coffee and environment. The people there are friendly and stylish.
I recommend.

Name: Canteen tea & coffee
Address: 23 P. Cao Bá Quát, Điện Biên
Author: Vlad Nosachev
Review: Hidden gem with cozy and peaceful patio! Great coffee too!

Name: CARAT COFFEE & TEA
Author: Cleve
Review: Nice atmosphere

Name: May Coffee and Tea
Address: 54 P. Lý Quốc Sư, Hàng Trống
Author: Benjamin Kraus
Review: Located next to the cathedral, this small store offers various drinks, including coffee and smoothies. My mango smoothie consisted mainly of ice, resulting in a bland experience, but was priced a bit below what I was expecting given the location.

Name: May Coffee and Tea
Address: 54 P. Lý Quốc Sư, Hàng Trống
Author: XIN LI
Review: Close to the cathedral, the drinks are good,suitable for sitting down and resting.

Name: ArtiShan Tea & Coffee
Author: Phuong Nguyen
Review:
The best brewed coffee shop in the area and Vietnamese tea are also delicious. You must try Yen Phu coffee, red tea set and lemon ice tea (for hot day)
Highly recommend

Name: ArtiShan Tea & Coffee
Author: Stefan Koch
Review: Very nice folks who run the place! Try the Red Tea for its unique flavor.

Name: ArtiShan Tea & Coffee
Author: Søren Madsen
Review: Let me be the first to confirm here that the hot cappuccino here is unbelievably good.

Name: ArtiShan Tea & Coffee
Author: Nguyễn Quỳnh Phương Anh
Review: Delicious matcha, reasonable price, comfortable seating, not overpriced like some matcha shops today

Name: Savanee Tea n Coffee - Linh Đàm
Address: 56BT2, Khu đô thị Bắc Linh Đàm
Author: Đỗ Việt Dũng
Review: Liked

Name: Savanee Tea n Coffee - Linh Đàm
Address: 56BT2, Khu đô thị Bắc Linh Đàm
Author: Phương Thảo Nguyễn
Review: I drank it a few times and found the drink to be delicious, with just the right amount of ice and suitable for my taste. The air-conditioned space is cool, clean, and the staff is friendly. The shop has a card allowing customers to pick up their own food, but the tray is a bit slippery, the young men also have to be a little careful when carrying it, there was a time when the glass was slippery and almost fell over, and the older ladies and gentlemen carrying it was a bit difficult, the shop fixed it into a tray. It's worth 10 points to keep the clothes from running rough

Name: Savanee Tea n Coffee - Linh Đàm
Address: 56BT2, Khu đô thị Bắc Linh Đàm
Author: Na Cà
Review: The water is okay, not delicious! The view here is beautiful and the water price is also good! 8/10 points

Name: Savanee Tea n Coffee - Linh Đàm
Address: 56BT2, Khu đô thị Bắc Linh Đàm
Author: Châu Nguyễn
Review:
Drinks are fine, 2 cups of matcha as shown in the picture, thawed and drank better. The cakes here are delicious and reasonably priced. Cool air conditioner. The restaurant is spacious, there are sockets almost everywhere, and it's clean. 10/10

matcha latte - 50k/cup without any matcha flavor. When asked, I was told that the matcha here is whipped into cream, but the concept of matcha latte is using powder mixed with water. The shop should change it to matcha cream powder milk, not matcha latte, why would customers misunderstand the name? Haven't tried other products yet, but this cup alone is not worth the money

Name: Savanee Tea n Coffee - Linh Đàm
Address: 56BT2, Khu đô thị Bắc Linh Đàm
Author: Thuy Duong
Review:
I went to Linh Dam facility, a glass of water was discounted for 44K. The staff asked me if I could give you 55K. And in the end, she gave me back 10K without telling me anything and asked me to give her 1K.
I don't regret the 1K, but every time I come in, I automatically default to cutting the customer's 1K without asking permission, which makes me feel quite disappointed. Just ask and I'll be fine.
Probably this will be the last time I come here even though the shop looks beautiful

Name: Rosier Fresh Tea & Coffee - Hàm Nghi
Address: VN Hà Nội Nam Từ Liêm Số 17, Lô TT02, HD Mon City, Ng. 2 P. Hàm Nghi
Author: Anh Nguyen
Review: This shop offers delicious milk tea and provides a relaxed ambiance, perfect for sitting and chatting with friends. I tried the golden bubble milk tea, and its flavor was uniquely delightful.

Name: Rosier Fresh Tea & Coffee - Hàm Nghi
Address: VN Hà Nội Nam Từ Liêm Số 17, Lô TT02, HD Mon City, Ng. 2 P. Hàm Nghi
Author: MELVINA NGUYEN (sheisverymoon)
Review:
🌱 About tea in general: Rich and delicious. The mixed recipes do not lose the scent and flavor of the tea, even when I add pearls/egg cream.

Highly recommend that anyone who likes to drink strong milk tea should try: Kim Tuyen/Chi Tu Olong Milk Tea.
• 25% sugar if golden pearls are added (because sweet pearls are cooked with sugar)
• 50% for medium sweetness.

✨About space: The shop has 2 seats inside and outside. Sitting outside in the evening with friends was very peaceful. What I don't like when sitting inside is that the staff often closes the cabinet door very forcefully. One day I was startled so much that I had a headache ;(

Name: Rosier Fresh Tea & Coffee - Hàm Nghi
Address: VN Hà Nội Nam Từ Liêm Số 17, Lô TT02, HD Mon City, Ng. 2 P. Hàm Nghi
Author: Sơn Hoài
Review:
The shop is located in a villa area and stands out with its pure white color, which is very Korean. The space is not large but very airy, mainly outdoor seating. The downside is that in the summer, sitting outside will be a bit hot.

👉The menu is quite diverse such as milk tea, fruit tea, machine-brewed coffee... Pink guava tea I find quite delicious, fragrant with guava scent, easy to drink, very suitable for summer drinking. The fresh milk and egg cream are not too sweet. The egg cream doesn't taste very clear to me, but the pearls are especially soft and fragrant. In addition, we also ordered croissants 🥐. The cake is a bit dry and nothing special.

The staff are quite cute and friendly. Another plus point is that takeaway drinks are wrapped in cold bags.

Name: Rosier Fresh Tea & Coffee - Hàm Nghi
Address: VN Hà Nội Nam Từ Liêm Số 17, Lô TT02, HD Mon City, Ng. 2 P. Hàm Nghi
Author: 유광우
Review:
It's a beverage cafe.
The price is about 100,000 dong for 2 drinks.
The size is medium large, and the large is served in a large can.

If you purchase 4 cans, you will receive a discount of about 200,000 dong compared to the basic price.

Personally, raspberry tea is delicious.

This place only accepts cash.

Name: Rosier Fresh Tea & Coffee - Hàm Nghi
Address: VN Hà Nội Nam Từ Liêm Số 17, Lô TT02, HD Mon City, Ng. 2 P. Hàm Nghi
Author: 近藤辰弥
Review: Even after ordering, the staff continues to play noisily like children. Hygiene is poor, with employees throwing the cups they serve at each other and hitting each other on the head with cooking utensils. I understand that there are many stores like that, but this is the first time I've felt this much disgust. Very disappointing because the drinks were delicious.

Name: Feeling Tea Đội Cấn
Author: Md. Gulzar Hussain
Review: I took only the Mango yogurt tea and it was great and tasty.

Name: Feeling Tea Đội Cấn
Author: quynh nguyen
Review: Decent tea! They didn’t have all bobas available but regarding the late time (≈ 8pm) we were there, it was fine. The bobas we got were sticking together as chunks a bit, so not that easy to drink.

Name: Feeling Tea Đội Cấn
Author: Tiddfull Talks
Review: It's a small outlet but definitely worth trying for amazing tea with so many variety of great flavors. I tried the Rose tea which had a good taste could feel the essence of rose and was very refreshing. The staff was nice and jolly. Served in disposables with proper lid.

Name: Feeling Tea Đội Cấn
Author: Thành Trung Lê (Lê Bin)
Review: ok

Name: Feeling Tea Đội Cấn
Author: Nga DCKT12.10.1 Nguyễn Thị Quỳnh
Review: I don't know how to do business, does milk tea have centipedes? Called forever without explanation

Name: Tiger Sugar Tea
Author: Anh Duong Nguyen
Review: Avocado ice cream is amazingly delicious, Mr. Sun ☺️☺️☺️

Name: Tiger Sugar Tea
Author: Huy Phong Trịnh
Review: Delicious milk tea, especially black sugar buffalo pearls

Name: Tiger Sugar Tea
Author: Hiếu Trần
Review: TASTY

Name: 8Hours Coffee and Tea
Address: 2A P. Đặng Thái Thân, Phan Chu Trinh
Author: Trung Nguyen
Review:
Nice view to a beautiful street in the heart of Hanoi.
Quite a variety of drinks and most of them have good quality, with a reasonable price.
Have good atmostphere for working.
I often stay there some hours for work and the staff are still very cheerful and lovely. I will come back here many more times.

Name: 8Hours Coffee and Tea
Address: 2A P. Đặng Thái Thân, Phan Chu Trinh
Author: Steven Venton
Review: 8HOURS Coffee & Tea is a cozy haven for coffee lovers and people-watchers alike. With its rustic charm, bathed in warm sunlight streaming through large windows, it’s the perfect spot to lose track of time. The comfy sofas, captivating paintings, and bustling street view create an ambiance that’s both inviting and inspiring. ☕🏙️

Name: 8Hours Coffee and Tea
Address: 2A P. Đặng Thái Thân, Phan Chu Trinh
Author: Maryam Aliyeva
Review:
This is your place if you love classical art.
What a vibe! Such paintings everywhere! Super aesthetic and elegant. Nice place for small and big companies. Comfortable chairs and sofas. Good lighting and location. Cool staff.

Small area, which reminds you of Harry Potter. I tried to escape the wall and take the train, nah, it didn't work out. Try, maybe, and in the end, you will find yourself in Hogwards!)

Recommended! Mona Lisa is waiting for you👋

Name: 8Hours Coffee and Tea
Address: 2A P. Đặng Thái Thân, Phan Chu Trinh
Author: Trung Dao Tuan
Review:
The drink is good, also the theme is influenced by Harry Potter vibe which is extremely cozy. Had a great time here, definitely will come back.
Also, the employees are extremely nice👌

Name: 8Hours Coffee and Tea
Address: 2A P. Đặng Thái Thân, Phan Chu Trinh
Author: Hoang Jan
Review:
worst coffee experience in vietnam. the area is super small, there’s no table for us, we asked the people there but they ignored and let us stand waiting.

the drinks is mid, nothing special, too expensive comparing to other places.

the empolyee attitude is just the worst. they are ignorance, careless. i think the manager should seriously recheck the quality. disappointing!

Name: Premier Coffee and Tea
Author: Judith Wiedmann
Review: Very nice Café, exzellent drinks and friendly staff!

Name: Premier Coffee and Tea
Author: These Vagabond Shoes
Review: Friendly staff and quality drinks. Very calm and chill atmosphere. Premier is a great Cafe worth visiting.

Name: Premier Coffee and Tea
Author: son tung dinh
Review: Nice interior, friendly staff

Name: Premier Coffee and Tea
Author: Bonjour Mai
Review: nice

Name: Premier Coffee and Tea
Author: Onimation
Review: Delicious drinks, reasonable prices, friendly staff :D The restaurant has few customers so if you want a private space, you should come

Name: Ding Tea
Address: XQCH+78W, P. Trần Hưng Đạo, P. Nguyễn Trãi
Author: Cavin Le
Review: On second floor, have a nice background with a big angel wing. I always like taking a photo of it. Great service!

Name: Ding Tea
Address: XQCH+78W, P. Trần Hưng Đạo, P. Nguyễn Trãi
Author: Bùi Thị Nga
Review: Beautiful, cool space, with angel wings to check in, very satisfying😍

Name: Ding Tea
Address: XQCH+78W, P. Trần Hưng Đạo, P. Nguyễn Trãi
Author: Chuyen Nguyen
Review: When 8 people were shocked by milk tea, they had a stomach ache after drinking it

Name: Ding Tea
Address: XQCH+78W, P. Trần Hưng Đạo, P. Nguyễn Trãi
Author: Hương Kim Cương
Review: Not excellent but acceptable.

Name: Ding Tea
Address: XQCH+78W, P. Trần Hưng Đạo, P. Nguyễn Trãi
Author: Xuân Thông Phạm
Review: There are no dingtea shops here.

Name: Feeling Tea
Address: 4 P.Trần Thái Tông, Dịch Vọng Hậu
Author: Van Thi Lan Huong
Review: This place has been renovated with a new, better and more beautiful design. However, it was noisy and quite bothersome to me, because of a noise behind counter. It sounded like the staff are grinding ice, but it took 20 minutes and I felt like I were sitting in the center of a construction.

Name: Feeling Tea
Address: 4 P.Trần Thái Tông, Dịch Vọng Hậu
Author: Luu Hoang Gia
Review: So bad

Name: Feeling Tea
Address: 4 P.Trần Thái Tông, Dịch Vọng Hậu
Author: chris day
Review: You can tell that this place has been open for a long time on the stairs the tiles are chipped the walls are dirty they don't maintain the building like they should for a business

Name: Feeling Tea
Address: 4 P.Trần Thái Tông, Dịch Vọng Hậu
Author: Dinh Nguyen Truong
Review: Taste is acceptable but it has a rumor the quality met problem.

Name: Feeling Tea
Address: 4 P.Trần Thái Tông, Dịch Vọng Hậu
Author: N4 Tran (novaxp4)
Review: Wow the place has changed alot since my last time going there!

Name: Milk's Coffee & Tea
Address: 25 P. Hàng Vải, Phố cổ Hà Nội
Author: CA Joel Saralkar
Review:
Staff tried their best by making me understand what they have to offer. I visited around 10 pm, they informed that now they are serving only milk tea. Hot milk tea will take lot of time so ordered cold milk tea. Staff could speak broken English. 5/5 for the try though. Since I don't mind being adventurous I went for cold milk tea.
Great choice.
Atmosphere might not be everyone's cup of tea. It's always crowded when i passed it 3-4 days. People sit almost on streets. Finally decided to give a try.

Name: Milk's Coffee & Tea
Address: 25 P. Hàng Vải, Phố cổ Hà Nội
Author: Hanna Sanchez
Review: Cool tea set-up, milk tea was sooo good, and nice place to people watch. This place is something you shouldn't miss and should be part of everyone’s Hanoi list!

Name: Milk's Coffee & Tea
Address: 25 P. Hàng Vải, Phố cổ Hà Nội
Author: Michael Rasmussen
Review: If you can get in your order it’s amazing - super busy and very traditional the locals love it and live up for it.

Name: Milk's Coffee & Tea
Address: 25 P. Hàng Vải, Phố cổ Hà Nội
Author: Jacobina de Rivera
Review: This is a revelation! I passed by many times and was always crowded by locals. Best milk tea I have ever had!

Name: Milk's Coffee & Tea
Address: 25 P. Hàng Vải, Phố cổ Hà Nội
Author: Pu Noi
Review:
Had a surprise boba here, which turned out just right.
Staff tried their best by handing me their phone with a live-translated picture of the menu, which was full of broken English. 5/5 for the try though. Since I don't mind being adventurous I went for "error mistake coal".
Great choice, got milk tea with some cream cheese and tapioca pearls for a great price.
Atmosphere might not be everyone's cup of tea. I went there around night, they had loud music blasting. I personally enjoyed that.

One of the best bobas I've had in a while!

Name: Forli Coffee & Tea
Address: Số 4 BT1, Khu đô thị Bắc Linh Đàm
Author: Lê Thanh Bảo
Review: Ok

Name: Forli Coffee & Tea

Review: Ok

Name: Forli Coffee & Tea
Address: Số 4 BT1, Khu đô thị Bắc Linh Đàm
Author: Monalisa Mona
Review: Great place

Name: Forli Coffee & Tea
Address: Số 4 BT1, Khu đô thị Bắc Linh Đàm
Author: Chau Giang Dang
Review: The shop is clean, airy, and has convenient parking. Rcm mango hibiscus tea or milk tea🤤

Name: Forli Coffee & Tea
Address: Số 4 BT1, Khu đô thị Bắc Linh Đàm
Author: Trọng Hùng Hà

Review:
The shop has delicious salty ice cream oolong.
When I bought it, I also received cheese pearls as a gift, which were also very delicious.

Name: Tea Break - Trà Ô Long đậm vị Tạ Quang Bửu
Address: 104 Ng. 1 P. Tạ Quang Bửu, Bách Khoa
Author: Uyên Hoàng Tú

Name: Tea Break - Trà Ô Long đậm vị Tạ Quang Bửu

Name: Tea Break - Trà Ô Long đậm vị Tạ Quang Bửu

Author: Hong Le

Review: The drink is quite good but there isnt enough room to sit...and it's so noisy when the waitress and waiter make the drink.....aww

Author: Ngọc Huy Nguyễn
Review: Good

Name: Link's Tea Room
Address: Nhà B2, B2 P. Trần Huy Liệu, Giảng Võ
Author: Thanhhang Vu
Review: There are tables and chairs both outside and inside the restaurant. There is air conditioning, wifi, fan, anti-mosquito lamp. The toilet is clean and dry. The decor shop has a tea and cake corner for virtual living. The menu is not too diverse, but I ordered orange and pineapple juice, which was fine, 45k. The shop is located in a dormitory area so it is not crowded and quite quiet

Name: Ding Tea
Author: Vu Phong
Review: The place decorate is fine and the drinks are okay. Nevertheless, it is a good place to go with your friends or alone to study or working. A bit crowded in the noon and in the evening. The prices here are acceptable. Overall, it is okay, I guess...

Name: Ding Tea
Author: Nguyen Hoang Duy
Review: Location is good while the decor is just acceptable. Can be hot if it is crowd. Have a quite good variety of bubble tea.

Name: Ding Tea
Author: Phuong Anh Nguyen
Review:
Nice tea
Nice service
Highly recommend black sugar bubble tea ;)

Name: Ding Tea
Author: Linh Ha
Review: Good quality but slow to serve customer

Name: Ding Tea
Author: Nhu-Uyen Nguyen
Review: Very nice!

Name: Révi Coffee & Tea
Address: Capital Place, 29 P. Liễu Giai, Ngọc Khánh
Author: Hailee Nguyen
Review: Got my drink in just 1 minute. Love the service! The drink is of high quality as well. Very affordable price. I am impressed.

Name: Révi Coffee & Tea
Address: Capital Place, 29 P. Liễu Giai, Ngọc Khánh
Author: Anh Huỳnh
Review: The coffee is okay, the breakfast cake is okay. Cute and enthusiastic staff,

Name: Révi Coffee & Tea
Address: Capital Place, 29 P. Liễu Giai, Ngọc Khánh
Author: SUHYUN BAEK
Review: This is a take-out specialty coffee shop located on the first basement level of Capital Place, across from Ba Dinh Lotte Center. You can enjoy iced Americano served right away for the low price of 25000vnd.

Name: Révi Coffee & Tea
Address: Capital Place, 29 P. Liễu Giai, Ngọc Khánh
Author: Duong Van
Review: Révi tea is very delicious, salt coffee has a moderate taste, service is fast and enthusiastic. Will definitely stop by Révi again and recommend it to friends😍😍

Name: Révi Coffee & Tea
Address: Capital Place, 29 P. Liễu Giai, Ngọc Khánh
Author: Nguyen Duy
Review: The drinks are delicious and the prices are even better. I sit on the 15th floor and check in at least 3 cups every week.. thank you shop!

Name: Ding Tea
Author: santosu kaori
Review: Ok

Name: Ding Tea
Author: huan phung
Review: Media

Name: Ding Tea
Author: Đại Lý Nissan Hà Nội
Review: delicious drinks. Enthusiastic and friendly staff

Name: Ding Tea
Author: Huy Nam (Thở bento)
Review: Waited a long time. The order was taken home and sat down for a nap, but it still wasn't finished. I ordered in advance but didn't return the order.... When I asked about my order, I was told it was out of tea and I didn't notify the customer to cancel or exchange for another product. I've drank a lot of tea and dinged a lot of tea, but I didn't expect to do so much business.

Name: Ding Tea
Author: Hieu Lang
Review:
A milk tea shop of the Ding Tea brand - a popular milk tea brand from Taiwan that is very popular with young people.
Ding Tea Kieu Mai restaurant is not too large but the restaurant space is very eye-catching and modern. Enthusiastic service staff.
In addition to milk tea, Ding Tea also offers many drinks, snacks...

Name: Trà Sữa Feeling Tea
Address: 112 Đ. Hồ Tùng Mậu, Mai Dịch
Author: Alf Prøysen
Review: Was delicious

Name: Trà Sữa Feeling Tea
Address: 112 Đ. Hồ Tùng Mậu, Mai Dịch
Author: Quyến Trịnh
Review: The drinks are good but quite expensive, the seats have a strong smell, the tables and chairs are not clean, the bathroom is super smelly, narrow and in general, the experience is extremely bad.

Name: Trà Sữa Feeling Tea
Address: 112 Đ. Hồ Tùng Mậu, Mai Dịch
Author: Linh Nguyễn
Review: Too bad, out of items but still opened the order for customers to order, then called back to change the order, chocolate cookies were out of cookies. Very disappointed with the restaurant.

Name: Trà Sữa Feeling Tea
Address: 112 Đ. Hồ Tùng Mậu, Mai Dịch
Author: Vinh Quang Đỗ
Review: The restaurant staff is impolite, the female staff speaks without respect for visitors at all, I recommend that the shop owner fire this shop employee, seeing that it is a new employee.

Name: Trà Sữa Feeling Tea
Address: 112 Đ. Hồ Tùng Mậu, Mai Dịch
Author: Cao Dũng Official
Review: Here I really like the working attitude of the staff. Although the space is cramped, it is also very clean. 5 Stars

Name: Trà Đài Loan Ding Tea
Author: HÙNG NGUYỄN VĂN
Review: Good

Name: Trà Đài Loan Ding Tea
Author: Vy Nguyen Quynh
Review:
The service here is very bad. On September 6, I bought it at around 9:30.
The first time, I miscalculated the price of size L even though I bought size M for myself.
There is a combo of jasmine milk and matcha for 79k but the staff charges the original price and does not support customers. When I noticed it, they said I had already ordered it so I couldn't get any more discount even though the promotion didn't end until September 9. (One employee said they have all types of matcha, another employee said they don't use the type of matcha they buy?)
I'll wait until next time to apply it.
It takes a long time to make even though the restaurant has no customers and I only ordered 2 cups.
There will be no coming back next time.
Close!

Name: Trà Đài Loan Ding Tea
Author: Phương Thủy Phạm
Review: The shop takes so long to make things =)) there are 4 cups, there are no other customers but it takes nearly 1 hour to finish =)) the staff has 2-3 people but 4 cups can be made in 1 hour =))

Name: Trà Đài Loan Ding Tea
Author: Huyền Nguyễn
Review:
First time buying ts without pearls
1 cup size 49k is bland

Name: Trà Đài Loan Ding Tea
Author: Sàn gỗ Việt An
Review: Delicious drinks, diverse, good service, enthusiastic staff

Name: SHAN MOCHA - Fresh Tea & Coffee
Address: 86 P. Lê Trọng Tấn, Khương Mai
Author: Ron Rozen
Review: Very tasty drinks and great service!

Name: SHAN MOCHA - Fresh Tea & Coffee
Address: 86 P. Lê Trọng Tấn, Khương Mai

Review: The restaurant space is very good. Impressed with longan in jasmine lotus tea, fresh. Thanh tea is not too sweet but not too special. 9/10

Name: SHAN MOCHA - Fresh Tea & Coffee
Address: 86 P. Lê Trọng Tấn, Khương Mai
Author: Dũng Nguyễn Hữu
Review: Nice decor, good drinks 9.5/10

Review: Made a mistake. Bad. Say you'll have something in 5 minutes but you're still doing it in 10 minutes.

Address: 86 P. Lê Trọng Tấn, Khương Mai
Author: Anh Trần
Review: drinks ok

Name: Trà sữa Wait Tea
Author: Long Hoang
Review: This is very nice shop, Tea is very good smell.

Name: Trà sữa Wait Tea
Author: Văn Vững Nguyễn
Review:
The shop is very clean, quiet, and airy for students to study.
The drinks are delicious, the menu has many dishes, there are catchers

Name: Trà sữa Wait Tea
Author: Mai Trần
Review: Delicious and nutritious milk tea, cheap, quiet and suitable for studying, the owner is easy-going

Name: Trà sữa Wait Tea
Author: Nguyễn Nguyễn (Handsome)
Review: The restaurant space is beautiful, affordable prices, seating on both sides, those who like to sit at tables and chairs have tables and chairs, those who like to sit on the floor have a wooden counter to sit on the floor, all the electricity and refrigeration equipment are available, the bathroom is large and clean, ...

Name: Trà sữa Wait Tea
Author: Khoa Phan
Review: Tropical pineapple tea and custard apple tea are delicious and cheap. The owner is cute and will support you for a long time.

Name: Chẩn Nấm Coffee & Tea
Address: 1 Đ. Đại Cồ Việt, Bách Khoa
Author: Tân Trần Công
Review: The restaurant is located inside the campus of Hanoi University of Science and Technology. Food and drinks are very good for everyone, easily accessible

Name: Chẩn Nấm Coffee & Tea
Address: 1 Đ. Đại Cồ Việt, Bách Khoa
Author: Lam Bui

Name: RAAW Coffee Roastery
Address: B7 P. Hoàng Đạo Thúy, Nhân Chính
Author: Taylor Main
Review:
Absolutely gorgeous little coffee shop.
Service was amazing the staff helped me to
Choose a coffee and offered some alternatives to suit my food intolerances. They have raw fresh cashew nut milk which is a  lovely alternative to milk!
I had the Chan troi ( coffee with coconut cream & cashew nut milk) and the chocolate pistachio brownie- it cost me £3.88/$4/€5!!
Atmosphere is the best it’s so quiet with relaxing jazz music , I’ll be back!

Name: RAAW Coffee Roastery
Address: B7 P. Hoàng Đạo Thúy, Nhân Chính
Author: Maryam Aliyeva
Review:
L u x u r y ....
Place is minimalistic. If your goal is to work, this place might suit you. Don't be surprised that prices are a bit higher than in other coffee shops , however taste of coffee and quality are very good. The price of coconut coffee here is 70vnd, and it tastes good. And you can see in the picture that it looks a bit different from coconut coffee that we all know. And the way it serves also different. Appreciated!)

Even if you have a big company you can go there cause they have big tables and chairs. And also they places to sit outside.

Name: RAAW Coffee Roastery
Address: B7 P. Hoàng Đạo Thúy, Nhân Chính
Author: Bee BeYourCyber
Review:
Staff are well polite and well care.
Caphe is good but not too strong enough.

Name: RAAW Coffee Roastery
Address: B7 P. Hoàng Đạo Thúy, Nhân Chính
Author: M
Review:
Good place for Vietnamese coffee beans.

Only light snacks available.

Barista is attentive and patient.
I had a cortado and canele. Will try something else the next time.

Great industrial vibe with lots of light and soothing jazz music playing in the background.

You get 10% for giving a review 😉🤐

Name: RAAW Coffee Roastery
Address: B7 P. Hoàng Đạo Thúy, Nhân Chính
Author: Somi Sirichantaropas
Review:
RAAW has a lot of variety, options & choices for specialty coffee - and as a person who has picky taste, my personal two favourites are: Natural Catimor (Son La) & Honey Typica (Dalat) 🌸

I also purchase their blend (the cute little cat pink packaging) to drink at home ☕️

Besides coffee, I am a big fan of their banana bread & brownie to pair with

For those who likes genuine coffee & want to learn and explore more about what Vietnam’s beans has to offer - here is the place to try it 🤌🏻

Name: The Note Coffee
Address: 64 P. Lương Văn Can, Hàng Trống
Author: Bhavna T
Review: This is such a nice place to have your morning coffee, especially on a rainy Sunday morning. It has a great view of Hoàn Kiếm Lake. The decor is made up entirely of notes that their customers have written over the past and stuck on the walls so you can spend hours reading these. The coffee is wonderful - strong with that nice condensed milk kick. The servers are sooooo warm and welcoming. One of them even came up to us an offered to take a photo for us. Must visit (multiple times)!

Name: The Note Coffee
Address: 64 P. Lương Văn Can, Hàng Trống
Author: Sophie Lam
Review:
Definitely more of a one and done kind of place. The drinks we ordered was okay nothing majorly special in my opinion but it’s worth it to write a cute little note that will live there forever!
Worth popping in even for a small drink to experience the atmosphere! It’s about 4 floors high but the higher you go the better especially if you want to look out the window and see the streets of Hanoi.

Name: The Note Coffee
Address: 64 P. Lương Văn Can, Hàng Trống
Author: Alvin Chan
Review:
Wonderful place to people watch!
Nice beverages served as well..
There are uncountable pieces of little notes which people from all over the world pasted on the walls of the cafe!
Must visit if you are around the area but it could get a little warm during the hot weather..

Name: The Note Coffee
Address: 64 P. Lương Văn Can, Hàng Trống
Author: Ruturaj Patil
Review: The cafe occupies entire 4-5 floors and it is a bit compact to navigate however it is an amazing concept to keep a note there and write whatever u want. The entire cafe is laced with notes which really looks  funky. We tried coconut coffee which was great it had perfect blend of coffee and an aftertaste of coconut. The egg coffeewas equally great..

Name: The Note Coffee
Address: 64 P. Lương Văn Can, Hàng Trống
Author: divya girish
Review:
Trendy place . Very famous among tourists. There are 4 storeys in the narrow building. Fun reading messages from people all around the world . There are stickers everywhere roof,stairs, chairs, ceilings ….
Lot of seating available and it’s moving crowd. We had cappuccino, hazelnut coffee and the famous egg coffee.
Egg coffee was good , you don’t get the taste of egg while drinking it ( i assumed we may get that raw egg taste)
Had heard a lot about this place. Worth the hype.

Name: Hidden Gem Cafe Hanoi
Address: 1 Hàng Mắm, Phố cổ Hà Nội
Author: Andrew “Andy” Davis
Review: Interesting little cafe. Love the entrance and stairs to get up to this and the rooftop with open air and fans was a delight on a nice afternoon. Decor is good with some lovely paint songs and a relaxed feeling. Coffee is good but the papaya salad we ordered was dry and rather disappointing by Vietnam standards lacking flavor. Can recommend coming for drinks but I’d skip the food next time. Salt coffee was a highlight though.

Name: Hidden Gem Cafe Hanoi
Address: 1 Hàng Mắm, Phố cổ Hà Nội
Author: Anita Rosaleh
Review: Cool not so little cafe with beautiful murals. Spanning two floors with a lot of fans dotting around for airy feeling even at summer. Salt coffee is nice and not too sweet. Vietnamese coffee with condensed milk could really use more condensed milk. However the papaya salad is rather dissatisfying and very dry.  Flavor is definitely more suited for westerners. Good pricing.

Name: Hidden Gem Cafe Hanoi
Address: 1 Hàng Mắm, Phố cổ Hà Nội
Author: Nurtac Somunkiran
Review: Great, cozy place, staffs so kind and friendly. The interior design is authentic and creative. The food tastes delicious and the prices are quite normal. Additionally, its ambiance makes you feel very comfortable. Their music fits well with the environment. This place definitely deserves its name.

Name: Hidden Gem Cafe Hanoi
Address: 1 Hàng Mắm, Phố cổ Hà Nội
Author: Harry Fraser
Review: Lives up to its name - it is an absolute gem, beautiful and unique set up. Could sit here all day! So many nooks you could hide away in. Great food, and enjoyed my first ever egg coffee. Staff are lovely. Would definitely recommend visiting!

Name: Hidden Gem Cafe Hanoi
Address: 1 Hàng Mắm, Phố cổ Hà Nội
Author: Daisy Lau
Review: Based on many good reviews on this hidden gem cafe, I decided to come here this morning for breakfast. The ambiance is great and unique, I love it so much. I left with 1 hour in Hanoi, so I need to try the combo set to taste every dish as possible.  I was quite surprised the color noodles bowl is actually quite a big portio for me. The 1st bite of the noodles I knew I was going to come again to this cafe in the future if I come to Hanoi again. I ordered the set in vegan. They all tasted so fresh and good. As the noodles are quite a big bowl, I decided to bring the bread to the airport for my snacks.   I also ordered salted coffee, and it was so creamy, I love it too, and I finished it within a few minutes. The bread still tasted good even after 2 hours when I had it after clearing the immigration. Highly recommended to come here, price is affordable, and you won't regret it.

Name: Blackbird Coffee
Author: Tek Ong
Review: Great place for a good Vietnamese coffee. You can feel really comfortable in the shop while enjoying your drink. Staff are friendly and speak perfect English.

Name: Blackbird Coffee
Author: Annalisa Esposito
Review: As a coffee lover, I was so pleased! The iced latte was so good! Also had the ham & cheese croissant and it was very delicious as well. The place is super hipster and you can sit in their patio, which is very beautiful and you can have a break from the bustle of the city. Definitely a must go if you love good coffee!

Name: Blackbird Coffee
Author: Iain King
Review: A great place - get the catimor as flat white or latte, sit back, and enjoy. A seriously good coffee. A very popular place, so be prepared to not immediately get a seat - though we got the last two seats both times we went. Lives up to the hype, definitely.

Name: Blackbird Coffee
Author: Karni Galantsan
Review:
The best specialty coffee that we had in Hanoi, and we are super picky with our coffee!
The coffee beans are Vietnamese, yet they make also cappuccino amazingly! I really recommend this nice atmosphere for also to work from.

Name: Blackbird Coffee
Author: T- Lam
Review: With so many great coffee spots in the French Quarter you’re spoilt for choice but this is one you shouldn’t miss. It just feels right & the coffee is superb. Friendly staff.

Name: Dream Beans Coffee Roastery
Address: 79 P. Lý Nam Đế, Cửa Đông
Author: Chris CZK
Review:
Had been anticipating to come this place for awhile. The owner was friendly and got to chat about the coffee industry here in Vietnam.

Coffee was good but maybe Vietnamese single origin coffee is not for me. Cannot taste much of the tasting notes and was a bit flat in terms of taste compared to the African beans. However I respect and appreciate the passion and coffee served here. One of the better ones in Hanoi.

Name: Dream Beans Coffee Roastery
Address: 79 P. Lý Nam Đế, Cửa Đông
Author: Jeremy Ow
Review:
A good cozy🛋️ coffee corner if you want to slow down over a good book.

The egg coffee☕️ has 3 layers: Egg foam, espresso shot and condensed milk. You may want to talk to their friendly barista to see how you can best enjoy the coffee. 😄

💵/💳: This place accepts cash or card payment.

Name: Dream Beans Coffee Roastery
Address: 79 P. Lý Nam Đế, Cửa Đông
Author: Joyce Lim
Review: Finally after running to 3 other cafes supposedly to be open at 730am but is closed when I was there. I found this gem after 3 dissapointments and it truly amazes me. Mocha was superb, nice comfortable cafe with a great service too.

Name: Dream Beans Coffee Roastery
Address: 79 P. Lý Nam Đế, Cửa Đông
Author: yy
Review: The coffee tasted very nice and robust and the server was very friendly and explained to us each item on the menu before ordering as well as our coffees when serving them. the place was well decorated and could really tell the effort put into making the atmospehere nice and educating customers about the art of coffee!

Name: Dream Beans Coffee Roastery
Address: 79 P. Lý Nam Đế, Cửa Đông
Author: Đạt Nguyễn
Review:
The coffee shop is amazing. The staffs here are very active and enthusiastic.
The atmosphere is so cozy.
V60 - exciting drink!
You can also order vegan drinks (oat milk).

Name: Eggyolk Coffee
Author: sang nguyen
Review: I stumbled upon this place while I was riding around the city exploring hidden gems, and everything about this coffee shop is amazing! From the atmosphere to how friendly the staff are. The egg coffee is definitely a must try if you want to break away from the regular Vietnamese iced coffee. I’ve been coming here for weeks, every morning, and the quality has been as consistent as my time being here. Definitely give it a try (you may fall in love). If you like vintage Vespas, you’d see them pass by every so often as well (say hi if you see a white Vespa) :)

Name: Eggyolk Coffee
Author: Shella Gonzales
Review: This was my first cafe visit in Hanoi. I did not expect much at first, since this was not in my list of “Good Coffee Shops” around the city, but luckily…I wasn’t disappointed. Their egg coffee was, by far, the best I’ve ever had. I’m really looking forward to going back here. I also tried their pastries, and I can say that their croffles and banana cake are a must!!!

Name: Eggyolk Coffee
Author: Farhan
Review:
Got all 3 iced egg drinks; my ranking from favourite to least — coffee, cocoa, matcha.

Pastries were just okay, a little pricier than other cafes and bakeries. Croissants are good and flaky, but the donuts and banana dessert didn’t have great texture.

Great view on the higher floors. Had trouble with air-conditioning on the third floor but we relocated and it was good.

Name: Eggyolk Coffee
Author: Stephanie Song
Review: Instagrammable café with delicious drinks and croissants. They do offer other pastries too but we did not order them. The croissants are flaky and crispy, but not buttery enough. The egg drink series were delightful (we ordered the matcha, coffee and cocoa), but would be better with less ice. Service was a tad slow (we waited half an hour before our drinks arrived). Other than that, it’s a place I’d return to if I had the chance!

Name: Eggyolk Coffee
Author: Mateusz Braman
Review: Delicious egg matcha and egg coffee. We also tried brownie, cheesecake and red velvet croissant and it all was really good. Beautiful pottery and very kind people. Recommend!

Name: Ca Bop (Egg Coffee)
Author: Chris Carciller
Review: The best egg coffee in town! I literally let out an audible gasp when I first tasted it. You cannot go to Hanoi and not taste their egg coffee. Love the vibes of their place too. It has an interesting stairway entrance, from there on, I know I was going to be a fan. 5 stars!

Name: Ca Bop (Egg Coffee)
Author: Geordie boy lost in Asia
Review: Be careful, I had Ca Bop bookmarked for their egg coffee, but the whole of the upstairs is closed (Oct 2024). I was lucky to get the last small seat to enjoy their delicious egg coffee.

Name: Ca Bop (Egg Coffee)
Author: Nor Ardila Binti Che Ayob
Review: Nice cafe with classic design. Serve egg coffee (7/10). Everyone should try once you come to hanoi. Suggested to order other drinks such as peach ice or something refreshing to refresh your mouth after trying egg coffee

Name: Ca Bop (Egg Coffee)
Author: Sarah C
Review: We were going to skip this spot due to already going to the most famous OG egg coffee shop in Old Quarter but we are so glad we didn’t. This place for its atmosphere alone is fantastic and unique. It has around 5-6 floors with winding wooden staircases to lead you to different floors with different vibes. Almost like an intricate tree house. You take your shoes off and you head on up. There is a covered rooftop, a floor with more of a lay down vibe, a more traditional floor with seating and a balcony just to describe a few. We opted to sit on the 3rd floor on the balcony to people watch right above the street. The egg coffee itself is more egg than coffee compared to the OG one in old quarter so it was perfect for me as I prefer sweeter drinks but for my partner he prefers a heavier coffee presence. The only small issue was the washroom on the ground floor was a bit precarious in terms of the wooden slatted door that wasn’t the best for privacy and it appeared to only be one washroom for the entire establishment. However, definitely worth a visit especially for the atmosphere!

Name: Ca Bop (Egg Coffee)
Author: Julia T.
Review: Great place to try the famous egg coffee! Very tiny stairs, you can climb up to the 3-4th floor to enjoy the street view. The owners are very nice and friendly. The coffee itself is yummy, highly recommended!

Name: Hanoi Coffee Station
Author: Eilidh Brettell
Review: LOVED this place. I was in Hanoi for one week and I discovered this place day one and came back every single morning. Tried a lot of the food available and it was all fantastic, but the yellow bowl was the most delicious bowl of goodness I have ever put in my mouth. On top of that they are really serious about coffee and clearly know what they’re doing. I just wish I’d had time to come and do a coffee class, but hopefully I’ll return to Hanoi one day! Overall an amazing experience and the staff are all so so welcoming, kind and friendly. Amazing place, 10/10

Name: Hanoi Coffee Station
Author: Eliza Wright
Review: Me and my boyfriend love this coffee shop! We come here to draw as it’s a nice quite spot in Hanoi. The food and drinks are super tasty and the staff are so sweet, especially Aki! Ask him about his cats 🐈

Name: Hanoi Coffee Station
Author: Elis Rosana
Review:
One of my favorite breakfast spots in Hanoi!
Hanoi coffee station is a cozy spot with great local vibes, serving delicious Vietnamese coffee, especially their famous egg coffee. Friendly staff specially Dat, and the atmosphere is so relaxed which makes it a perfect place to unwind and enjoy authentic flavors. Definitely worth a stop!

Name: Hanoi Coffee Station
Author: J K
Review: Coffee is fairly average in flavors. Not a lot of complex notes. Very friendly staff. Cozy little coffee shop. Prices are fairly average. Not hard to find but going up the stairs is a bit hassle. If you are in the area it is not a bad choice 😄

Name: Hanoi Coffee Station
Author: kateřina molnárová
Review: Beautiful place, however, the coffee is not very strong in flavour and the food was just okay. I think the toast dough could do with some salt in it or something as it tasted extremely plain, there was also a very little amount of peanut butter. Excellent service though.

Name: 1984 Coffee & Drinks
Author: Zia Mellican
Review: Coffee was super yummy! The girl that works here spoke English beautifully and gave great explanations. Must come for your coffee ventures in Hanoi

Name: 1984 Coffee & Drinks
Author: Budi Geisler
Review: After a 6,5 hour bus trip from Sapa we chose to finally have some good coffee. Very nice place with great coffee and atmosphere!

Name: 1984 Coffee & Drinks
Author: Andrew Fram
Review:
Lovely little rooftop bar

Prices very reasonable, and nice cocktails. Not sure why, but we appeared to be the only people coming up here despite being here for several hours (although this theme was consistent with the rest of our trip in Hanoi).
We ordered some bar snacks which were yummy and enjoyed our time up here.
As a plus, the WiFi is very good!

Name: 1984 Coffee & Drinks
Author: stevan
Review: Nice place for cup of coffe latte and matcha yogurt..  Im from Indonesia and i love hanoi . See you hanoi next time i will back here..

Name: 1984 Coffee & Drinks
Author: Nikita Bijoux
Review: Lady serving us was super nice. Answered my questions on where to buy razors and d showed me where to go. Iced latte was fine. I asked for nut milk and got regular but I’m used to that 😂 really liked the giant sphere ice cube and they got a super soft velvety thick layer of foam on top almost like a cold foam. My boyfriends coconut coffee was delicious

Name: GAD8
Address: 50 P. Đào Duy Từ, Hàng Buồm
Author: Stefanie Green
Review: We tried several drinks and all were delicious! Favorites were the salted coffee and the egg coffee, both which were very rich and creamy. Be mindful, people come here to work so use respectful voice level or you may be asked to be quiet 😁 it is nice that they have extra seating upstairs, so plenty of room to sit and enjoy your drinks.

Name: GAD8
Address: 50 P. Đào Duy Từ, Hàng Buồm
Author: tramngnh
Review:
The cafe located in a small alley right in the center of the old town, it has a unique vibe of Hanoi - cozy and lovely.

Cafe was so so, I personally don’t like the coffee beans here, but they did offer cookies to have together with cafe, which was great😍

P/s: Do not sit near the door, as people keep getting in and out and it got really annoying for us. Even the staff, they do not care about the door being too noisy.

Name: GAD8
Address: 50 P. Đào Duy Từ, Hàng Buồm
Author: Denis Chernenko
Review:
One of the most beautifully designed coffee shop, that I have ever visited 💥💚⭐️ I was offered various beans and guided upon the best way to get them brewed for the best taste of it.

Interior of the place deserves separate compliments 🔮Just like the music selection and the sound quality👌And the whole place seems to be carefully maintained, including the bathroom, which tells a lot about the whole attitude of the owner☝️

Name: GAD8
Address: 50 P. Đào Duy Từ, Hàng Buồm
Author: Esther Phung
Review: Excellent coffee - We had the salted coffee and egg coffee. Both drinks were well balanced. Very impressed particularly with the salted coffee, it had a silky mouthfeel and an almost floral finish which was so pleasant. Croissant was average but a nice complement to the drinks.

Name: GAD8
Address: 50 P. Đào Duy Từ, Hàng Buồm
Author: Renato Garcia
Review: Best coffee shop in Hanoi. Very cozy and minimalist deco. The staff are super friendly and coffee geeks. We were so lucky that we got to try a new recipe they were working on. I can’t recommend this place enough. If you are a coffee lover, you must visit this place. Good on ya, GAD8, keep up with the good work!!

Name: ikigai coffee
Address: 43 Ng. 38 P. Phương Mai, Kim Liên
Author: jerry veve
Review: Beautiful japanese-vibe cafe next to a small lake.Perfect co-working/working place with good wifi, AC, toilet, and amazing coffee(I had iced ca phe den dor 35 000dongs which is a black coffee with condensed milk. It had a rich aroma and taste, and the presentation was beautiful too!). One of their displays has inspired me a lot-Ikigai it is! Thank you.

Name: ikigai coffee
Address: 43 Ng. 38 P. Phương Mai, Kim Liên
Author: Maaike M
Review: We had a great time chilling here and were fortunate enough to be the only 2 customers at the time. The staff was committed to great service and super helpful especially the tall waiter with glasses on. The cafe itself has plenty of space, different sets of tables and chairs. The atmosphere was awesome, drinks were delicious. Happy to come back some time soon!

Name: ikigai coffee
Address: 43 Ng. 38 P. Phương Mai, Kim Liên
Author: Huan Dong
Review:
My new favorite coffee spot in Hanoi.

Excellent coffee, many brew options to your liking. Cute location and excellent views, environment is calm and good for working / reading.

My absolute favorite drink is the Iki coconut over ice 🤤 both sweet and fragrant

Name: ikigai coffee
Address: 43 Ng. 38 P. Phương Mai, Kim Liên
Author: John Dean
Review:
Ikigai all the way. At the time of visiting it was not too busy, so we were able to enjoy the calming atmosphere of the cafe.
Different seating options on the first and second floor. Good for socializing or working too.
Bac Xiu was leaning towards a more coffee bean taste. It also had a more distinctive flavor compared to other Bac Xiu-drinks, which I liked.

Name: ikigai coffee
Address: 43 Ng. 38 P. Phương Mai, Kim Liên
Author: Merve D
Review: Delicious specialty coffee with oat milk / almond milk and other vegan nut milk options. Right at the lake.

Name: Sociō Coffee
Author: Charleston
Review:
Ordered the anti-bitter coffee (though I told the kind lady to make it as bitter as possible) and the grape-something coffee blend (as an Americano). We also had a croffle (not pictured).

I enjoyed the anti-bitter coffee. The cream has a bit of sweetness (just right for me) and a bit of saltiness. When you get the cream with the coffee, the balance of flavours is perfect. So enjoyable that I would want to get another cup.

I didn't enjoy the grape blend as much because the grape scent was too overpowering. Nonetheless, the blend lives up to its name.

Croffle was good. it was served warm and with some cream (which was also a little salty).

Name: Sociō Coffee
Author: Nguyen Xuan Ha Anh (Milu)
Review:
Not much to say since this might be one of the best cafe I have ever been so far. This cafe has been on my list forever and it did not disappoint me at all. The cafe has plenty of spaces to sit for different purposes, from studying to hanging out. The only minor thing is the shop closes at 7 pm, which is quite early for Hanoian like me 🥹
.
Matcha Latte is a MUST TRY as it was delightful and not bery sweet. If you love bitter matcha with a tiny uint of sweetness and creaminess from cream, this drink is for you!
.
Colombia Hand Brew is one of the best Hand Brew I have ever drunk in my coffee review journey. It smells and tastes elegantly like a fruit and flower farm with lemon flowers, pears and grapefruits.

Name: Sociō Coffee
Author: An Hoe
Review: Clean, easy vibes. Outdoor and indoor seatings are both available. They have tables suppose for work but the lighting isn't really proper for working or writing. Staffs are helpful and nice. Thoughtful of them when bring to the table a warm water bottle during a cold winter day. Drinks are cute but quality is average, nothing special, also for the croffle. But the overall experience is okay.

Name: Sociō Coffee
Author: Val G
Review: A bit hard to find because the cafe is located inside a narrow lane. But... our struggles were worth it. Their drinks and waffles are to die for. The atmosphere is quite pleasant, even though it was very crowded during our visit. We got a bottle of really hot water, the first time we saw something like this in a cafe. Normally they would offer a glass of barely warm water, but not in this place. Anyway, we will come back for those waffles, and some coffee too.

Name: Sociō Coffee
Author: Ann L
Review: Pay and order first, then sit. Very good coffee, a bit too pricey but they made it unique. I like how they serve water with our order. The seating area felt crammed in some areas and the bigger room was more like an office space. Good amount of plugs for people with laptops. The waffle croissant was clever and good. Not the place I would go to sit and relax, maybe to work.

Name: Phiêu Me Coffee
Author: leeroded
Review: Very nice staff, cute quiet place to sit in the back of the caffe. The coconut coffee was good!!! 10/10 would come back

Name: Phiêu Me Coffee
Author: Thành Nguyễn
Review: Located in the heart of the city. Nice decoration. Friendly staff. Very good services. Best quality.

Name: Phiêu Me Coffee
Author: Anh Nguyen
Review: Such a hidden gem in the heart of Ha Noi’s Old Quarter! This new coffee shop blends perfectly with the charm of the area, offering a cozy and relaxing atmosphere amidst the hustle and bustle of the city. The coffee is top-notch—rich, smooth, and freshly brewed. They also have a great selection of pastries to pair with your drink. The staff is super friendly, and the interior design has a perfect mix of modern and traditional vibes. Definitely a must-visit spot for anyone looking to escape the busy streets for a quiet coffee break!

Name: Phiêu Me Coffee
Author: Nguyen Phuc
Review: A great place to experience the Hanoi atmosphere with a modern twist!! Amazing drinks also

Name: Phiêu Me Coffee
Author: Ayendra Silva
Review: Charming spot that’s perfect for coffee and tea lovers. Genuinely friendly vibe made me feel right at home. Highly recommended!!!

Name: Blackbird Coffee
Author: Thanh Lam Tran
Review:
Their special mocha was absolutely delightful, with the perfect balance of rich chocolate and smooth espresso. The cozy space was inviting and perfect for relaxing with a good book or catching up with friends. I also had the pleasure of trying their summer menu, which included a refreshing cider fruit drink that was both unique and delicious.

To top it off, the shop had great music playing in the background, creating a wonderful ambiance.

Name: Blackbird Coffee
Author: Serith
Review:
Cascara tea: was ok, light taste, not overpowering, recommended for non-caffeine drinkers.
Cocobina: can only taste Pennywort juice, coconut water and strawberry extract tasteless. Not recommended.
Cold-pressed juice: fresh, sweet, cold. Recommended.
Service was good, very attentive.
Too little tables in my opinion, worth checking for space on the 2nd floor first before ordering, as the 1st floor is too crowded.

Name: Blackbird Coffee
Author: Giang Trần
Review: Great atmosphere for studying at the cafe. Delicious banana cakes, various options from caffeine to non-caf.

Name: Blackbird Coffee
Author: Val G
Review: The coffee cup size is rather small. The taste was good though. The pastry was somewhat good but far from excellent. They don't allow talking on the phone with the loud speaker which is a plus. I guess they want to keep the area rather quiet. The staff was nice. But I am not sure I will return. The interior isn't special enough to make me want come back again.

Name: Blackbird Coffee
Author: Hoang-HIEP NGUYEN
Review: Coldbrew is strong and bitter. Air-con is a bit weak and very hot on the 2nd floor.

Name: RAAW Coffee hàng buồm
Author: Chris CZK
Review:
A quaint place with a lot of Vietnamese single origin beans. The staff was friendly but couldn’t speak English so we translated using Google.
Coffee here for v60 was decent although couldn’t taste much of the tasting notes. Vietnamese beans aren’t for me. I respect and appreciate the passion of the baristas.

Name: RAAW Coffee hàng buồm
Author: Iain King
Review: A quiet oasis away from the hustle and bustle of Hanoi. A well-needed break! We went here three times during our time in Hanoi, because it was just so good. They were also really caring when my partner dropped a glass, and even replaced the drink for free. We tried the coffee tasting board, the latte, flat white, the salted egg, and the coconut. All amazing. I’d highly recommend it.

Name: RAAW Coffee hàng buồm
Author: Ven
Review:
My friends and I visited Raaw last week for a specialty coffee tasting class with the owner, Ms. Linh, who is both friendly and highly skilled in her craft. As it was my first time attending a coffee class, I wasn’t sure what to expect, but Ms. Linh made the experience exciting and interactive. She encouraged us to grind and brew coffee ourselves, which I thoroughly enjoyed. We learned so much about different types of coffee beans, such as Arabica, Robusta, and Liberica. Ms. Linh also demonstrated various brewing methods, each fascinating in its own way—especially the siphon technique.

I truly appreciate that Raaw only offers locally sourced coffee beans from Vietnam, supporting local farmers and highlighting the region’s rich coffee culture.

After the class, Ms. Linh treated us to two of their specialties: salted egg coffee and Avococo (avocado-coconut coffee). Both were absolutely delicious—highly recommended!

It was an amazing experience overall. I’ll definitely return whenever I’m in Hanoi. Big thanks to Ms. Linh and the team for the unforgettable coffee journey! ❤️

Name: RAAW Coffee hàng buồm
Author: M *
Review: Nice small cafe, the menu is limited but matcha and coffee are quite good. The downside the cafe smells smoke from smokers and the smell sip into the non smoking space .  But a good coffee, good place to try in the area

Name: RAAW Coffee hàng buồm
Author: Jennifer Tan
Review:
Recently visited Raaw Cafe for their Coffee class it was  fun and fascinating learning different types of local coffee  in vietnam. They even showcase  different method of coffee maker from the usual Vietnamese style drip, french press, aero press to  siphon. It was a class to remember.

The Menu offered  wide variety of delicious coffee and tea option. We tried the Salted egg coffee and the Avocado coffee. It was Amazing!

Will surely comeback and try different flavors.
A must visit and class to attend when in Hanoi.

Name: Drop In Cafe
Author: Judy Campardo
Review: What a great find. Their egg coffee and pastries are really yummy.  We tried many others here in Vietnam and I’d rate this one as the best. Nice and thick topping and it tastes like a tiramisu dessert. Mmmmm

Name: Drop In Cafe
Author: Fabio Biocchetti
Review:
I found the egg coffee to be a bit too bitter, but coconut coffee was the best. Tasty and refreshing.

Staff is kind, they speak good English and accept payment by card. They also offer complementary tap water with ice, which is a nice touch.

Name: Drop In Cafe
Author: kapsell Heinoski
Review:
I tried the Egg Coffee, Coconut Coffee, Saigon Coffee and the Jasmin Orange tea. All of them were very good, especially the Egg Coffee and the Jasmin Orange tea!
The staff is very friendly and speaks good english, if you need help with your decision they can support you with your choice.
They have self-service for water and an air con in the shop, that's nice to cool down. Free Wifi is available as well and a clean toilet.
You can even see the train passing by.

Name: Drop In Cafe
Author: Aniket Ankam
Review:
So my Vietnamese friend took me here to have coffee when it was raining in Hanoi and the coffee experience turned out to the amazing.
I had Vietnamese hot milk coffee and he had hot chocolate and both of them were amazing and as demanded by the weather. The rates are pretty pocket-friendly here as I paid 30k for the coffee.

Name: Drop In Cafe
Author: Myriam Ward
Review:
Best egg coffee and best coconut coffee in Hanoi.

We tried few other place including café Giang and their coffees are better.

The coconut one is special not only it’s only coconut ice-cream but also they put some crunchy toasted coconut on top, that extra touch makes a big difference to the whole experience.

For the egg coffee, it is just succulent perfect balance with cacao on top.

Also we tried the Marguerita one. It also a must try.

The 3 coffees were amazing and if you have to go to only one place in Hanoi to have a coffee, go to Drop in, we won’t regret it!

Name: Dawnbird Coffee
Address: 31B Trần Quốc Toản Hoàn Kiếm Hanoi City
Author: Ryan
Review:
While strolling around, I accidentally stumbled upon Dawnbird Coffee and was impressed by its simple design and outdoor seating on the sidewalk. I decided to give it a try. The staff was friendly and courteous, and the drinks were decent. An iced Americano cost 45,000 VND.
The interior may not be spacious, but there are seats by the window overlooking the street, and outdoor seating, which is definitely a plus. It's a suitable place to read a book or have a chat with friends.

Name: Dawnbird Coffee
Address: 31B Trần Quốc Toản Hoàn Kiếm Hanoi City
Author: Lana Tran
Review: Small cosy cafe in Hanoi. You will feel the warmth right after you step into the cafe. Lots of small little details that will cheer you up and inspire you!

Name: Dawnbird Coffee
Address: 31B Trần Quốc Toản Hoàn Kiếm Hanoi City
Author: Thuy Tien Phung
Review: Delightful coffee in the middle of old town. Cozy space, has both indoor and outdoor seatings

Name: Dawnbird Coffee
Address: 31B Trần Quốc Toản Hoàn Kiếm Hanoi City
Author: S. R.
Review: Certainly recommend. Great thoughtful coffee selection by the owner.

Name: Dawnbird Coffee
Address: 31B Trần Quốc Toản Hoàn Kiếm Hanoi City
Author: Kateřina Merleová
Review: Loved the space. Coffee is fine 😊. Very nice staff. Recommend to visit!

Name: Gau Coffee & Bakery
Author: Fergus MacDermot
Review: Great little coffee shop with tasty croissants, well made coffee and decent smoothies. Only one person working on a Sunday afternoon and so took some time for the order.

Name: Gau Coffee & Bakery
Author: pooja dubey
Review:
I recently visited Gau Coffee, and I was blown away by their amazing bakery collection. The variety of fancy food items was impressive, and each one was deliciously tempting. From decadent pastries to savory treats, there was something for every palate. I especially appreciated the unique touch of offering the option to 'go your own fish,' ensuring a fresh and personalized experience. Overall, Gay Coffee and Cross is a must-visit for anyone craving exceptional baked goods and culinary delights. I'll definitely be returning to indulge in more of their mouthwatering offerings

Although there is one drawback …sadly they don’t accept card payment :(

Name: Gau Coffee & Bakery
Author: Ella Morante
Review:
Lovely cafe that should satisfy your flaky pastry needs! The menu is really easy to read and it makes ordering a breeze! 🍃

Unable to comment if it’s laptop friendly but there are plug sockets around the seating area 💻!!

They warm your pastries for you 🥐 which makes them so so perfect with an iced Bac Xiu 😋

Note: Cash Only 💸

Name: Gau Coffee & Bakery
Author: Diana Medeisa
Review:
Coconut coffee not good.
Egg coffee very nice!
Croissant – just ok.

Not nice employee.
No tables (just chairs).
Only accept cash.

Would only return for egg coffee.

Name: Gau Coffee & Bakery
Author: Joel Louzado
Review: Probably one of the best egg coffees I've had in Hanoi. Skip note cafe's egg coffee, this is a hundred times better.

Name: Hatchin Coffee
Address: 8 P. Hai Bà Trưng, Tràng Tiền
Author: Stefan Brenner
Review: Wonderful hidden gem close to Hanoi‘s opera house. I had the cold brew hibiscus which offered a unique fruity flavor.

Name: Hatchin Coffee
Address: 8 P. Hai Bà Trưng, Tràng Tiền
Author: Linh Phi
Review:
A nice place, but it’s not comfortable enough for me to sit here and working.
Good to meet up with your friends.

Name: Hatchin Coffee
Address: 8 P. Hai Bà Trưng, Tràng Tiền
Author: Hà Trang Nguyễn
Review: Quite a comfortable and cozy location for both coffee talks and coffee work; has both outdoor and indoor spaces; matcha is good for me, but the latte is more like creamy cafe

Name: Hatchin Coffee
Address: 8 P. Hai Bà Trưng, Tràng Tiền
Author: Елизавета Романова
Review: Good place, definitely can recommend.  Interesting tea option. Good arabica and sweets.

Name: Hatchin Coffee
Address: 8 P. Hai Bà Trưng, Tràng Tiền
Author: vo hoang giang
Review:
Next to my old school. Love that.
Pretty. Nice service. Quiet and cozy.
Good coffee.
But non smoking.

Name: Tenement Coffee and Wine Bar
Address: 1B Lê Phụng Hiểu Hà Nội Hanoi City, 1B P. Lê Phụng Hiểu, Tràng Tiền
Author: James Murnane
Review: ☕️ 🍵 📕 Good coffee & matcha in a minimalist setting. They have the classic coffee drinks (both Viet & Western) as well as many mixed coffee drinks. I got a coffee with orange. They added more seating indoors now so there’s a good amount of space. Park at the end of the alley, usually a parking lady there. And don’t forget the buy 10 get 1 free card at the register if you plan to come again!

Name: Tenement Coffee and Wine Bar
Address: 1B Lê Phụng Hiểu Hà Nội Hanoi City, 1B P. Lê Phụng Hiểu, Tràng Tiền
Author: Dương Ttttran
Review: Nước ngong và service tuỵt nhắm. But its just another coffee shop that has a bookshelf for the sake of aesthetics. Its okay but i prefer something with more substance

Name: Tenement Coffee and Wine Bar
Address: 1B Lê Phụng Hiểu Hà Nội Hanoi City, 1B P. Lê Phụng Hiểu, Tràng Tiền
Author: tung the
Review: i love this place. its good to be here everyday! Friendly staffs, good drinks, fine wines. You should try it when u go to Hanoi

Name: Tenement Coffee and Wine Bar
Address: 1B Lê Phụng Hiểu Hà Nội Hanoi City, 1B P. Lê Phụng Hiểu, Tràng Tiền
Author: Nat
Review: Tenement was the first cafe that I visited during my first trip to Hanoi and Vietnam overall. The cafe was chic and clean. ✨Both indoor and outdoor seating were available, although we settled for the indoor seating during our visit. The baristas were friendly and happy to make recommendations. My friends and I had You and Me, Red Door (fruity tea), and Morocco (sweet, creamy coffee). The Red Door and Morocco were great!

Name: Tenement Coffee and Wine Bar
Address: 1B Lê Phụng Hiểu Hà Nội Hanoi City, 1B P. Lê Phụng Hiểu, Tràng Tiền
Author: Huyền Trần
Review:
Imressive service, nice and friendly owners and staff.
Nice ambience, nice decoration, relaxing outdoor sit place (a beautiful mirror and space to make selfies :)))
Coffee and wine are OK, good to drink everyday.
Suitable for work.

Name: Level Café & Coworking
Address: 104 - D5D Đ. Võ Chí Công, Vườn Đào, Khu biệt thự
Author: Erik Rybár
Review:
This is my go-to coffee shop, hands down! The vibe is super cozy, and the smell of fresh coffee hits you as soon as you walk in—instant mood boost. The staff is always friendly and somehow remembers my order, even when I switch it up!

Their coffees are spot-on every time, and the pastries are dangerously good. It's the perfect place to chill, catch up on some work, or just relax for a bit. Highly recommend if you're looking for a great cup of coffee and a welcoming atmosphere.

Name: Level Café & Coworking
Address: 104 - D5D Đ. Võ Chí Công, Vườn Đào, Khu biệt thự
Author: Katherin Cueller
