Author: N/A
Review: N/A

Name: Pinewood Cafe n' Restaurant
Author: Md Tanvir Hossain
Review:
No parking space. Road side parking only.

The place is a bit noisy. Good for gathering with friends or colleagues. Not so good for couple dine in experience.

Food price is not so cheep not so high. I like their decoration but not so fan of their sitting  arrangement. I tried their set menu 4 and 5. Costs me around 1.2k. Liked their food except the thai veg.

Name: Pinewood Cafe n' Restaurant
Author: Aysa Akter Akhi
Review:
I Went to Pinewood after a long time. Glad to see that they still maintained their food quality. We had the fried calamari, and Oreo cheesecake.
Liked all of the items. The fried calamari could use a bit more seasoning.
Overall, the food was loved by me.
I Didn’t have high expectations from the oreo cheesecake but to my surprise it was good.
I also like their clear seafood food and use to order it a lot before.

Their ambience is beautiful.
The price is little bit costly.
But the food taste is satisfactory.
The Waiters were humble enough too.

Good luck!
Cheers!!

Name: Pinewood Cafe n' Restaurant
Author: Abir Mahmud
Review:
Pinewood Cafe n' Restaurant has three branches in Dhaka City, two in Dhanmondi and one in Banani, I went to their branches and was satisfied.
Their foods are so delicious, their services are very first class and the atmosphere also so wonderful.
I like most their Fried Clamari, Oreo Cheesecake, and Cream of Mushroom Soup.
Their cheese's quality is good that make the food tasty.
The price is a bit high but worthy. Going there once a month is better than going daily to the mid level restaurant.

Name: Pinewood Cafe n' Restaurant
Author: Sirajum Munira
Review: We ordered fried calamari, mint lemonade and alfredo baked pasta. I liked the Calamari. It was crunchy also flavour of the pasta was good.

Name: Pinewood Cafe n' Restaurant
Author: Rahul Das
Review: This café is nicely decorated and cozy, with a relaxing and visually pleasing atmosphere. It's a great spot for photos. We tried their blueberry cheesecake, mushroom pasta and chicken burger. The burger tasted good. The pasta wasn't good at all and felt like a waste of money. However, the cheesecake was delicious. Although the café is a bit expensive, the friendly service and nice environment make it worth it. Overall, it was a good experience.

Name: Coffeeholic cafe
Address: Front of arribs international school, Sheer E Bangla Rd
Author: S. M. Zakaria
Review: The best coffee in the town. Their service is very good and their behaviour is also very pleasant. The food quality is awesome. Try any one item there and surely you will come back again. They have two branch. One in Octo Office and another is in Khanpur Hospital Road. I prefer the Octo Office branch. The environment is little congested but it's very cozy. A must try Café while visiting the city. It's popular specially to spend a quality time with your friends or partner. Try once and thank me later.

Author: hasan nahim
Review:

Unfortunately, I was quite disappointed with the calamari—both the portion size and the actual size of the pieces didn’t justify the price at all. However, their pizza definitely made up for it. It was loaded with cheese and toppings, making it really satisfying.

The Thai soup was another highlight—it was hearty and full of flavorful toppings. Despite the hiccup with the calamari, Barcode Café remains a good spot for hangouts and casual meet-ups.

we tried the sausage-pepperoni mafia.

Name: Bhalogari Cafe
Author: MD. Munim Bin Salim
Review:
This is a coffee place inside SKS Tower, Mohakhali. They also have a good collection of various types of foods.

We went there for their breakfast package. The food was fresh and tasty, but didn't feel anything extraordinary. I won't consider it value for money.

The interior and the seating arrangements were good. The service wasn't as good as I expected. At the early morning, we were the only customers there, but it took a good while before someone attended us after calling.

The coffee was good.

They have an outside seating available, if you want to have your cigarettes while sipping your coffee.

This restaurant has a lot of scope to improve. I hope they'll do.

I entered Bhalogari Cafe and got wowed by their decorative cafeteria environment. They have all sorts of  coffee, deserts and even pastry cake that you will love.

If you are nearby Mohakhali and looking for a premium cafe near Mohakhali then do check out Bhalogari Cafe.

Review:

Name: Capawcino Cat Cafe
Address: Navana Mashira, 1st Floor Plot 275k, Road 16 (New), 27 (Old Dhanmondi Secondary Entry:, Block #C, Dhaka
Author: Fatima Adreeta (Ads)
Review:
Was really excited about going to this one. Because I believed it was a great initiative to introduct cats in a positive light to regular people, plus it would also rehabilitate strays. And it does achieve that.  But we noticed some stuff which meeds a little bit attention. But first let me talk about the positive things we experienced:

1. The food was really amazing. The latte was fresh with great amount of strength and milkiness. The latte arts were also beautiful. The seafood pesto pasta was spot on with generous and well-cooked chunks of crabs, squids and prawns. It also had a pronounced basil flavour which we really really loved. The calamaris were the highlight of the dinner - with amazing lemony peppery flavour and beautiful crunch. The sauce served with calamari was beautifully creamy, zesty, peppery with a hint of sweetness.
2. The cats were really friendly and looked healthy. Their fur was shiny and soft. Most of them had an amazing attitude towards humans.
3. I loved the synergy that was present between the humans and cats in the cat playground.
4. The location was easy to find.
5. The decor was minimalistic but aesthetic.

Now, let me talk about the side that needs more attention:
1. The food portion, especially the pasta was not justified. It was a medium portion for 480 taka.
2. The section were people play with the cats or keep the cats had a horrible stink. It came from not vaccuming or cleaning the spot frequently. I saw some litter boxes and water dispensers - but they were not enough for 10+ cats. Cats are clean animals who need adequate quietness and space. It did feel quite crammed. I definitely recommend the spot being cleaned properly - at least once a week. And the amount of profit the restaurant makes, I do believe its possible.
3. Most of the staffs did not feel cat-literate. One of them tried to poke a torti cat with hand sanitizer. The hand sanitizers humans use are extremely alcoholic for cats. It can cause severe irritaiton to their skin and possibly burn them.
4. The cats did not have enough toys. And because the space was small, some cats felt extremely insecure. Especially a Torti cat was slightly aggressive towards humans, but very aggressive towards other cats. Definitely recommend proper health checkup for the all cats, or training the cats to feel at home or at ease with that place. Cats are an important part of that business. So, cats should be an equal priority along with customers.
5. The staffs should be adequately trained to handle and love cats. No staffs were aggressive, but they were not particularly affectionate either. Having affectionate staffs will be good for the cats.
6. Barely have complain about the food. But the pasta would have been more amazing with either a little bit of cheese or a little squeeze of lemon.

Overall, really a nice experience. Its hard to find a cat cafe in Bangladesh. Love the initiative, but the owners should educate themselves more in how to create+maintain a proper cat cafe. Cats are not toys or ornaments. In fact, they are integral part of the business and that should be kept in mind before every business decision.
As of for the customers, if you are looking for a good time with good food and good feline company, definitely pay a visit. It is a slightly overpriced place. But I do believe its totally worth it. Recommended.

Name: Capawcino Cat Cafe
Address: Navana Mashira, 1st Floor Plot 275k, Road 16 (New), 27 (Old Dhanmondi Secondary Entry:, Block #C, Dhaka
Author: Tahsina Naz Khan
Review:
This is an excellent cafe having a separate glass room for pet cats. Visitors can enjoy meal in the cafe as well as spend quality time in the cat room with a fee of only Tk. 200 per hour. The fee is used for maintenance of the cats. Visitors entering the cat room are well-behaved and sensitive to cats, as I found.

If I talk about food, it's tasty and holds a reasonable price.

Service is excellent in both the cafe and the cat room.

I found the place comfy, relaxing, soothing, and much more! Every cat lover in Dhaka must visit this cafe and have a pleasant time with the feline friends.

Address: Navana Mashira, 1st Floor Plot 275k, Road 16 (New), 27 (Old Dhanmondi Secondary Entry:, Block #C, Dhaka
Author: Zaki Imtiaz
Review: So I went there this Friday (24th May 2024), their service was wonderful. The environment was open and inviting. The food quality was nice and the drinks were extra special coming in unique glasses. I had a really fun time there. They maintain a superb level of hygiene which truly impressed me. I spoke to the PR manager and the person in charge and they were truly remarkable folks. 10/10 would visit again and would recommend others to go there. This is a restaurant with a unique twist and Bangladesh deserves more places like this for family visits 👍

Name: Capawcino Cat Cafe
Address: Navana Mashira, 1st Floor Plot 275k, Road 16 (New), 27 (Old Dhanmondi Secondary Entry:, Block #C, Dhaka
Author: Faerie Kanon
Review:
I went here today. Everyone was friendly. The food was good and even made an adjustment as I don't eat meat or cheese. The cats were cute too! The restaurant was very clean and the prices reasonable (for me as a German).
Just three things about the cat zone:
1. Get them more litter boxes. Two aren't enough. Maybe put them somewhere where the guests can't go. (Hole in the wall to another room) That way you can also create an area for the cats when they're too stressed out. This will make them less aggressive.
2. Get them scratching posts! Please! There was nothing for them to scratch on except the sofa.
3. Make a guide for guests on how to handle the cats. E.g. stop petting when the cat is hissing or trying to hit, no picking up, be careful when ears are in airplane mode or tail is moving fast.
...
We have cat cafes in Germany which follow the above points and the cats are rarely aggressive.

Review:
Pashawari Tea is one of the best tea stalls in the Banani area. It's located beside the Banani Central Jame Mosque.

First of all , I am going to talk about their services and the quality of their food. Specially, pashawari tea is an amazing taste, and they serve food  the way of traditional system. People can connect to bangali heritage and can feel that you live in an ancient place. Because of their interior design and how way they have served.

I can't talk about others' dishes because i didn't have these items.

Author: Mamunur Rashid
Review:
Peshwar Kitchen, previously known as Peshwar Tea, is a delightful spot located near the Banani Mosque. This charming eatery offers a wide range of teas, making it an ideal place for tea enthusiasts. In addition to their impressive tea selection, they serve a variety of lunch and snacks primarily focused on Bengali cuisine.

The food is consistently tasty, showcasing authentic flavors that truly represent the local culinary heritage. However, one drawback is the portion sizes, which can feel somewhat on the smaller side, leaving diners wanting a bit more.

The ambience of Peshwar Kitchen is another highlight. The serene and beautifully designed interior creates a peaceful dining experience, perfect for enjoying a leisurely meal or a quiet cup of tea. Overall, Peshwar Kitchen is a lovely place to unwind, savor good food, and indulge in excellent teas.

Name: Peshawari Tea
Author: Rashique Chowdhury
Review:
I had their Malai cream chaa and it was really good. The portion of malai was quite decent. And the taste was also good. However it's overpriced to me. This tea is not worth 100 even if you're sitting in a nice environment. They also have food items such as nehari, khichuri etc.

They offer indoor and outdoor seating areas. The outdoor area was very vibrant but too noisy as it is right beside the road. The indoor area is neat and clean.

I would recommend this place for their tea!

Review:
One of the best tea place in Banani. Quite suitable for tourists / foreigner like me which place and comfort is essential. Had the Peshawari Milk Tea and it was nice, not too sweet. Price is also quite reasonable.

Only issue will be their limited space.

Name: Peshawari Tea
Author: Ashiq Billah Monjil
Review:
Restaurant’s Name: পেশোয়ারী চা

I was seeing reviews of this place & since i am a diehard tea fan, i had to try this. I visited this place twice & 1st time i ordered their regular tea, chicken pakora & chicken momo & the 2nd day i tried their malai cha. Now the review:

Malai cha- 100tk
Seeing the pics made me feel this was one of the best tea but i was dishearten. The consistency of the tea was light & there was not much malai in it. They have topped it with maltova. Overall i won’t have this again rather i’d go to either Cha & Chill in Banani or Star Kabab for tea.

Regular Tea- 50tk
The story of this tea is also the same. A very average quality tea & i won’t try this again in future.

Chicken Pakora- 180tk
Its made with minced chicken & you can barely taste the chicken. I wasn’t impressed sadly.

Chicken Momo- 180tk
This was just a basic chicken momo which you can get everywhere. Nothing special but it wasn’t bad either.

Need to mention they have 2 sitting arrangements & one outside & the other inside. I found both the places to be dirty having tissues & dust everywhere. You can check this in my last picture in you notice properly. Overall i wan’t happy with their tea, food & the place & won’t revisit.

Name: Best Exotic Bookstore & Teahouse/বেস্ট এক্সোটিক বুকস্টোর এন্ড টিহাউস
Author: Aria Ahmed Mome (mome)
Review: Had an absolute blissful experience in the possibly only book cafe in Uttara. The serene and peaceful ambience, intriguing collection of fictions and non fictions,along with  perfect cup of flavoured tea as well as mouth watering savories. The amicable approach of the stuffs was cherry on the top. Would definitely recommend to those who are looking for a place to indulge in thoughts and spending chaos with solace without being interrupted by chaos like a regular hectic place.

Name: Best Exotic Bookstore & Teahouse/বেস্ট এক্সোটিক বুকস্টোর এন্ড টিহাউস
Author: noname
Review:
I tried the latte, average taste and less dense. Both white and brown sugars were served.

The books in the display are for sale only. There are some books inside only for reading but this collection is small.

The environment is cozy, the staff were so cordial. Recommended 💯

Name: Best Exotic Bookstore & Teahouse/বেস্ট এক্সোটিক বুকস্টোর এন্ড টিহাউস
Author: Abidur Rahman
Review:
It's a bookstore and a coffee place. The place looks beautiful but I'd be glad if those books are available to read. Those books are only for sale. Though they've got a great collection of books there. This combination of a bookstore and a cafe looks great. So the ambience was perfect.
About their items, I tried their Delicious Dumpling Soup, Iced Peach Tea and Watermelon Juice.
The Dumpling Soup was great. I don't like soup but maybe that was the first time I really liked one. They also provided a bun with it which I didn't expect as the price wasn't that high.
I'd rate the Dumpling Soup a 9/10.
Iced Peach Tea looked great and tasted perfect. The watermelon juice was fresh.
So I'd rate both of them 9.2/10.
Their services were nice too.
So judging by their prices and everything I think it's a budget friendly place with a nice ambience to have some coffee.
NB- I didn't buy any of their books neither look at their prices so can't review their bookstores but it looked great.

Name: Best Exotic Bookstore & Teahouse/বেস্ট এক্সোটিক বুকস্টোর এন্ড টিহাউস

Review: Best place for small chitchats and pass leisure time in Uttara. We ordered Chicken Chowmin and Chicken Yakitori. The food was delicious and the prices were reasonable too.

Name: Best Exotic Bookstore & Teahouse/বেস্ট এক্সোটিক বুকস্টোর এন্ড টিহাউস

Review:
Beautiful and cozy place. The food is really top notch. Dumpling soup is highly recommended. Tea was good enough. The service staff were happy and cordial, despite us being there at the closing hours.
Books they claim to be all original, but there might be a bit of a mix there. Prices are reasonable, both books and food.
Shall be visiting again hopefully.

Name: Halda Valley Tea Lounge
Author: Radi Shafiq
Review:
Fantastic atmosphere. A hidden gem in Dhaka. I spent maybe 4 hours here yesterday, immersed in a book and some tea. The butter milk tea was great.

However the food was just okay. The service, even though very welcoming, was unevenly paced. The music was good but suddenly a youtube advert was playing at full volume. The eyebrow black tea (a name something along this line) was disappointing. So I am not sure how the other special teas are that you can buy here.

Attention to a few small details can do wonders for the place. I'd recommend this for a lazy afternoon hangout.

Name: Halda Valley Tea Lounge
Author: Ziaul Haque
Review: The ambiance is really nice but the experience wasn't. They didn't have any Iced Tea, according to them the vending machine wasn't functioning. They've agreed to do tea testing for us but 20 minutes later one of them came up and said it's unavailable. Whatever tea we have ordered, it came almost 20-25 minutes later. Previously they used to serve cookies 🍪 with tea, I don't why they stopped that as well. Not sure what's going on with the place.

Name: Halda Valley Tea Lounge
Author: Hasin Hayder
Review:
Wonderful place, excellent ambience. Great for adda, meetings and work.

Foods are expensive here, nothing extra ordinary. But the environment is excellent, gives you a very positive vibe. Also they have dedicated tea rooms available for rent at 800 taka per hour. Nice idea. Overall I like this restaurant. Will visit again.

Name: Halda Valley Tea Lounge
Author: Asif Mahmud
Review: The ambiance was cozy and inviting, making it the perfect spot to relax and unwind. The cookies were absolutely delicious, and they paired perfectly with my tea. The staff was friendly and attentive, adding to the overall pleasant atmosphere. I'll definitely be returning for more cookies and relaxation in the future!

Review: went here back in winter early in the morning so it was very peaceful and nice. the coffee was average, the sandwich was really disappointing, it was cold and the bread was hard but the cheese inside it was good. however the place is really cute and comfy!

Name: Monno Tea Room
Author: 繁光丸山
Review:
I visited the cafe on a Friday evening.
I like the atmosphere and have been there a few times, but this is my first post.
It was hot, so I ordered a brownie with ice cream and an iced cafe latte.
The brownie and ice cream were delicious, but the iced cafe latte didn't taste like coffee.
The coffee didn't taste good, probably because they serve mainly tea.
The male staff was nice, but the female staff were not very friendly.
Maybe they didn't like their jobs, but they weren't very friendly.
I like the atmosphere of the cafe, so I think it would be better if they had more friendly staff.
I also hope the coffee tastes better.

Name: Monno Tea Room
Author: Sumaiya Ismail
Review:
Went here 3 days back. The food is quite expensive. The waiters are not well trained. Welcome manners or while taking order they are not smiley at all. If extra person comes they are giving broken chair. Then we had to change it again. We wanted to shift to another room but when we asked “ekhane bosha jabe “ she answered “na oita bondho” please train up the waiters with etiquettes.

About food we took pasta which costed 450 to but amount was very less. Momo was fine.  Cream of mushroom was also very good.

Name: Monno Tea Room
Author: Sabbir Hosen Emon
Review:
This is basically an experience store of Monno Ceramics. The ground floor is dedicated to the ceramics showroom, and the upper floor is actually the restaurant.

It's not that big but it's aesthetically organized and minimal. You can have coffee, tea, brunch, and snacks.

They offer snacks type starter food. I tried chicken momo and Chicken francaise. Chicken francaise was really good - buttery and juicy with great fragrance.

Next, I will try their coffee and tea.

You can come here for snacks, brunch, or some small coffee adda.

Name: Monno Tea Room

Review:
If I have to tell you about this place I must admit their atmosphere is exemplary! so neat and clean.
The food tastes top notch and the price is also budget-friendly.
The only cons I have to tell that the food portion isn't sufficient as per the price.
I would recommend this place for hanging out with friends and family. They take cards for payment.

Name: RB Tea House
Author: Rudra Sharadindu
Review:
Delicious Naga Singara and Perfect Milk Tea!

I had the pleasure of visiting this place, and it was such a treat! The Naga Singara here is absolutely delicious—crispy on the outside, with a spicy and flavorful filling that is just perfect. It's unlike any other singara I've had. To top it off, the milk tea is a must-try. Rich, creamy, and brewed to perfection, it pairs so well with the singara. The ambiance is cozy, and the staff is friendly, making it a great spot to unwind and enjoy some snacks. Highly recommended if you're craving authentic Naga flavors and a refreshing cup of tea!

Name: Rio Coffee Rajarbagh
Author: Fakrul Islam
Review:
History of Coffee

The earliest credible evidence of coffee drinking appears in modern-day Yemen in southern Arabia in the middle of the 15th century in Sufi shrines. It was here in Arabia that coffee seeds were first roasted and brewed in a manner similar to how it is now prepared. But the coffee seeds had to be first exported from East Africa to Yemen, as Coffea arabica is thought to have been indigenous to the former. By the 16th century, the drink had reached Persia, Turkey, and North Africa and eventually in Europe.

Coffee has a long history in Italy. Venice was one of the first European ports to import coffee beans in the 16th century, and in the 19th century, men in bowler hats met in Turin's coffee shops to plan for the country's unification. Italy truly emerged as the global leader in coffee thanks to Milanese inventor Luigi Bezzera.

You can find many kind of coffees and delicious deserts there. The Cookie was very perfect for the coffee. Easily it can take a place in top 15 coffee shop in Dhaka city.

You can take a time there with a nice coffee. Around 15-20 people can seated here at a time.

Name: NORTH END coffee roasters Shahabuddin Ahmed Park
Author: shahajahan khandakar
Review: This coffee shop is a hidden gem with a warm, inviting atmosphere perfect for both work and relaxation. The coffee is consistently rich and flavorful, with a smooth, well-balanced taste that never disappoints. The staff is friendly and attentive, always ensuring you feel welcome and taken care of. Their menu offers a great variety of drinks and freshly made pastries, all reasonably priced. Overall, it's the ideal spot for a quick pick-me-up or a peaceful afternoon.

Name: NORTH END coffee roasters Shahabuddin Ahmed Park
Author: Akib Jabed
Review: Absolutely loved their ambience. The baristas are super friendly and I would recommend visiting during the day time as it gets really crowded in the evening. Quite smaller than other branches but they have outdoor seating space too.

Name: NORTH END coffee roasters Shahabuddin Ahmed Park
Author: Sajjad Hossain
Review:
The litter cafe inside the park is a must visit for coffee lovers.
They have both indoor and outdoor seating space. With indoor space you will have air conditioning but in outer space you will have ceiling fans and you'll be able to enjoy your smoking.
The services as always self-service you have to place your orders at the computer and pay ahead of Time. After food is ready, they'll serve that to you.
The ambience is very nice with glass view of outside both of the park and the road.

Name: NORTH END coffee roasters Shahabuddin Ahmed Park
Author: NJ Lily
Review: It's inside the justice shahabuddin park. The place is cozy and nice. And m9st importantly they use paper plates. Which is amazing. The food is as usual good. And the coffee smells and tastes amazing. If you want to have a morning or evening walk you can go and take a coffee to enjoy.

Name: NORTH END coffee roasters Shahabuddin Ahmed Park
Author: Maisha Maliha Rahman
Review: Hands down, this is my most favorite North End branch right now. If you want to hangout with your friends or get some work done in a coffee shop, but also love being close to nature, this will be the ideal place for you. As it is inside a park, the ambiance is different than a super busy loud cafe. The food is always consistent and delicious. The servers are one of the most humble and friendly out there. I love to do my weekend works over there in the morning. All of their sandwiches are amazing and my personal favorite hot beverages are: caramel latte and hot chocolate. Highly recommended place!

Overall, it is an ultimate go-to place if you are a coffee-lover.

Review: As usual they serve the best coffee in BD. Though this branch is comparatively small, the interior is great. Tried the Frozen Salted Caramel Latte and Brownie with Hazelnut cream cheese. Both were top notch.

Name: NORTH END coffee roasters LK Tower 2
Author: saamiah jardine
Review: This place was such a gem to find in Dhaka. Their coffee tasted like home and the quiet atmosphere is perfect for early morning coffee's

Name: NORTH END coffee roasters LK Tower 2
Author: Shahriar Mamun
Review:
Hands down, this is the best cafe in town. They are the pioneer in grooming the cafe culture here. I love the taste of their coffee. In terms of coffee, I mostly take Americano, and I tried that at various places, but the coffee at North End is unparalleled.

Other than coffee, I also like their brownie, croissant and roasted beef sandwich.

Service is nice. Seating arrangement is good. They have both smoking and non-smoking zones.

Overall, it is an ultimate go-to place if you are a coffee-lover.

Name: NORTH END Coffee Roasters Shahjadpur
Author: Sadat KM
Review: The best coffee shop in the town.. there was a mix of lighting and darkness and classic vibes..the place was crowded yet felt quiet. provided great service and coffee was good.. you should try cappuccino using the French blend coffee, better than regular blend.. salted ice caramel coffee was good too. Espresso and americano were good as usual compared other branches

Name: NORTH END Coffee Roasters Shahjadpur
Author: Maisha Maliha Rahman
Review: One of the best places to get good coffee and sandwiches in that area. The seating arrangement is nice, cozy and properly spaced. Their service is friendly and welcoming. My favorite there to order is: Roast beef croissant and Hot chocolate/Hazelnut latte. The only thing they can improve is keeping up the washroom clean. Would highly recommend for going there just by yourself, your friends and loved ones.

Name: NORTH END Coffee Roasters Shahjadpur
Author: Md. Al- mamun
Review: The atmosphere here is great for having coffee as well as having a good time.

Name: NORTH END Coffee Roasters Shahjadpur
Author: S R official
Review:
"Northend is a delightful local coffee shop chain nestled within Dhaka, boasting over 18 branches scattered across the vibrant neighborhoods of Dhanmondi, Gulshan, and Banani. My personal haven is the Northend main branch in Baridhara, conveniently located near the U.S. Embassy in Dhaka.

During my recent visit, I indulged in a comforting Americano paired with a bagel generously slathered in hazelnut cream - a personal favorite from North End's menu. What sets this branch apart is its enchanting ambiance; the moment I stepped in, the aromatic embrace of freshly roasted coffee beans enveloped me. The interior exudes a warm, homely vibe, making it a haven for coffee enthusiasts like me.

In essence, the Northend flagship branch in Baridhara is more than just a coffee shop; it's an inviting sanctuary where the rich aroma of coffee and cozy surroundings create an unparalleled experience."

Address: Block# I, Plot# 77 Rd No. 3, Dhaka

Address: Block# I, Plot# 77 Rd No. 3, Dhaka
Author: Mashfia Rahman
Review:
Ordered butterscotch frozotino and was quite disappointed. It is just crushed ice with no flavour, asked them to fix it they just added little more butterscotch flavour but that’s it. It was still very bland. Their brownie is more like a cake. :/ not happy with their food quality.
Their service is alright, quite slow and forgetful. Need to keep reminding them or asking for things again and again.

My late night best place for coffee and light snack is this one. Their coffee and their cool drinks are recommended along with their croissants. However this time we had their brownie and carrot cake; carrot cake stood out among these two.

Their interior will give you a vibe that this place is for long adda place. They have smoking zone too but that looks bit shady to me.

Road side parking with adequate guards keeps me worry free.

I gotta try their food items, but so far the staff practice good behaviorism. I would recommend the place for a warm coffee date actually, will add some more details once I check in again to try out more. Stay tuned!

Highly recommend!

Review:
I go to the Banani branch in Kollen Kopi and eat sometimes. Because I like their service and quality everything, so when I go out to eat, I mostly go to Kolen Coffee, my home is in Mirpur, but I didn't know that there is an outlet of Kolen Coffee in Mirpur too, after knowing that, I went one day and saw this branch. It seemed small, maybe the branch is in the foot court of a shopping mall. So I thought to myself that the food will be good or how the service will be?
But when I dined I saw the service and the place was also very nice to me according to Chhota, still Kolen Kopi has managed to maintain its name with its food service and beauty. So I once again got caught up in the illusion of cologne coffee.

Name: Triangle Coffee Hut
Address: Dakshinkhan, Uttar Khan Mazar Rd, Dhaka
Author: DAVEL RAIDER
Review: Triangle Coffee Hut is a hidden gem nestled in the heart of the city. With its cozy atmosphere and aromatic brews, it's the perfect spot to unwind or catch up with friends. The coffee selection is diverse, catering to both traditionalists and adventurous palates alike. Each cup is brewed to perfection, delivering a rich and satisfying flavor profile. The menu also boasts delicious pastries and light bites to complement your coffee experience. Add in the friendly staff and reasonable prices, and Triangle Coffee Hut easily earns its spot as a go-to destination for coffee enthusiasts.

Author: Afreen Moon
Review: The place is cozy. Not very crowded like other branches. Croissant items were good. Loved the dessert. The service was excellent.

Name: Arabika Coffee
Author: Yusuf Zubayer
Review: This place is in the lobby of a hotel. Small and efficient. Shares the lobby sitting area with the hotel. Coffee is, like their other outlets, good and reasonably priced. Good to have them in my office neighborhood!

Author: Shadman Samin
Review: Cosy place compared to the other branches of Arabika but it's nice. Slightly overpriced due to location but still a great place to grab some good coffee other than North End

Author: Rahat Chow
Review: Nice cozy place for a smalltime chill. It's basically a small space beside the reception of a hotel. Tasted their Oreo Cheesecake. It was vv tasty. Although the cheese portion could’ve been better.

Name: Ruya Coffee Shop (Military Museum Coffee Shop)
Author: Atanu Shome
Review: The place is top notch, but food quality not so good. I had cold coffee and orange juice. Cold coffee was okay but orange juice was disappointing. Service was quick and good. However, it’s the decorations that is out of the box. Fabulous architecture. Neat and clean. It’s quite pricy. Cold coffee was 180 and orange juice 190. It’s cash only as of Aug-22. Another negative thing is that the basin is quite far from the sitting area.

Name: Ruya Coffee Shop (Military Museum Coffee Shop)
Author: Mirza Khalid
Review: I ordered Ruya Cafe Special Sandwich (300tk each) and Orea Crumble (230tk each). To my utter surprise the sandwich was nothing other than an egg sandwich with no chicken or salad inside. And Orea shake was made from ovaltine with breadcrumbs not Oreo biscuits. I regret ordering food from them. Total waste of money. The kitchen owners are simply robbers and authority should look into it.

Name: Ruya Coffee Shop (Military Museum Coffee Shop)
Author: Shakib Reza
Review:
Nice place, really liked sitting here. Peaceful. Only trouble is the food. We tried the Sandwiches and the Coffee along with fresh juice. Each had its issues.

I expected better based on the price.

Name: Ruya Coffee Shop (Military Museum Coffee Shop)
Author: susmita banik priya
Review:
Worse.
Don't go there.
Overpriced with out quality.
Selling flat bread by the sake of shwarma.
Not even using shwarma dough.
Bread was too rubbery to chew. Waste of money

Name: Ruya Coffee Shop (Military Museum Coffee Shop)
Author: MD. RABIUL ISLAM
Review: The restaurant is located behind the museum exit gate. Beautiful place but food is not up to the mark and expensive.

Name: Crimson Cup Coffee
Address: Plot# 191/B, Regnum Resource Limited, Rahman’s Regnum Centre, Bir Uttam Mir Shawkat Sarak, Dhaka
Author: Lamiah Israt
Review: A beautifully decorated crimson outlet in a cozy corner. The outlet is very nice just as the staff. They were quite friendly and dedicated. I have always had their hazelnut latte but lately it feels different than before. It tastes more watered down, lighter than my taste. Mocha is still alright. While the coffee prices are fine, I honestly don't understand the idea of selling thin layered sandwiches for 350+, which is actually quite common in these coffee shops. I'd actually refrain from having sandwiches at places like this. Other than that, it was a good place to wait around and sip your coffee calmly.

Name: Crimson Cup Coffee
Address: Plot# 191/B, Regnum Resource Limited, Rahman’s Regnum Centre, Bir Uttam Mir Shawkat Sarak, Dhaka
Author: Mehedi Hasan
Review: Wowww.... This crimson cup branch is a piece art to me. Beautifully decorated. Much better than many fine dining restaurants. Highly recommended.  Such a beautiful Photogenic place. I liked the chocolate cake.much better than the one from tabaq in terms of pricing (personal opinion).  I really really liked this branch..anyone would love this place.

Name: Crimson Cup Coffee
Address: Plot# 191/B, Regnum Resource Limited, Rahman’s Regnum Centre, Bir Uttam Mir Shawkat Sarak, Dhaka
Author: Tanha Islam
Review: Fantastic interior, this is probably the best outlet CC has opened in Dhaka. This vibes more elegant vibe and the place is super cozy! They have both outdoor and indoor space along with arrangements for both big and small groups. Food was good as usual! The desserts were fantastic!

Name: Crimson Cup Coffee
Address: Plot# 191/B, Regnum Resource Limited, Rahman’s Regnum Centre, Bir Uttam Mir Shawkat Sarak, Dhaka
Author: Mahinul Islam Meem

Name: Crimson Cup Coffee
Address: Plot# 191/B, Regnum Resource Limited, Rahman’s Regnum Centre, Bir Uttam Mir Shawkat Sarak, Dhaka
Author: monirul Islam

Name: Tabaq Coffee
Address: House 22, 1st Floor, Crystal Palace, Road No 140, Gulshan Ave, Dhaka
Author: Jesan Tanvir
Review:
Great staff, great atmosphere, great coffee, great location. What else do you need.

+1 for the staff behaviour. They were courteous and attended to our every need. Must try the dark mocha and their regular cappuccino. Maybe skip the salted caramel stuff

Name: Tabaq Coffee
Address: House 22, 1st Floor, Crystal Palace, Road No 140, Gulshan Ave, Dhaka
Author: Sadia Afrin
Review: We tried their mocha and decaf latte. The decaf latte had a similar taste and flavor to the regular one ( according to their description only the caffeine portion had been extracted ). It is recommended if you are having coffee at late hours ( for example in the afternoon). We also got a free fudge brownie for the loyalty card. The ambiance and service of the cafe were nice as usual.

Name: Tabaq Coffee
Address: House 22, 1st Floor, Crystal Palace, Road No 140, Gulshan Ave, Dhaka
Author: Md. Moniruzzaman Tarafder Papon (Mo)
Review: My recent visit to Tabaq Coffee in Gulshan 1 was an absolute delight. Having heard great things about this branch, I decided to try it out with a couple of friends, and I must say, it exceeded my expectations in every way.We ordered two Affogatos and an Oreo Cheesecake Bar, and everything was perfect from start to finish. The Affogato, a delightful blend of hot espresso poured over creamy vanilla ice cream, was simply exquisite. The balance between the robust espresso and the smooth, sweet ice cream created a harmonious flavor that was both refreshing and indulgent. It’s clear that Tabaq Coffee takes great pride in their coffee, as the quality and taste were top-notch.The Oreo Cheesecake Bar was another highlight of our visit. It was rich, creamy, and had the perfect amount of sweetness. The Oreo crust added a delightful crunch, complementing the smooth texture of the cheesecake beautifully. It was a treat that satisfied our sweet cravings without being overly heavy.The ambiance at Tabaq Coffee is inviting and cozy, making it an ideal spot for catching up with friends, getting some work done, or simply enjoying a quiet moment with a great cup of coffee. The decor is modern yet comfortable, with plenty of seating options and a warm, welcoming vibe. The staff were friendly and attentive, ensuring that we had everything we needed without being intrusive.Additionally, the cleanliness and organization of the space were commendable. It’s evident that the management pays attention to detail, creating a pleasant environment for their patrons.In terms of pricing, Tabaq Coffee offers good value for the quality of food and beverages they provide. While it may not be the cheapest option in town, the quality and experience justify the price. For coffee aficionados and dessert lovers, it’s well worth the visit.Overall, my experience at Tabaq Coffee in Gulshan 1 was nothing short of excellent. From the delicious Affogato and Oreo Cheesecake Bar to the inviting ambiance and attentive service, everything was spot on. I highly recommend this branch to anyone looking for a perfect coffee experience. I’ll definitely be returning soon to explore more of their menu.

Name: Tabaq Coffee
Address: House 22, 1st Floor, Crystal Palace, Road No 140, Gulshan Ave, Dhaka
Author: Sheikh Fardin Oyon
Review: Tabaq is a incore trusted coffee shop in Dhaka...The Interior and ambience was awesome...Roasted beef sandwich was tasty enough...Oreo cheese cake was also tasty...Mocha was fine...Price was okay...Service was also fine..

Name: Tabaq Coffee
Address: House 22, 1st Floor, Crystal Palace, Road No 140, Gulshan Ave, Dhaka
Author: CR7LM10NJ11
Review: Food is good but not mind blowing. Their tandoori chicken sandwich and mango strawberry smoothie are awesome.

Name: Crimson Cup Uttara
Address: 18, 2nd Floor, Quantum Properties Ltd. Quantum Mostafa Tower, Towers Gausul Azam Ave, Dhaka
Author: SSK Tonmoy
Review:
Located at the second floor of Quantum Mustafa Tower, Sector-13, Uttara.
We went there on Friday evening, when the sizzling heatwave was punishing the entire country.
During our last visit, we had to wait for a bit to use their seating arrangement. This outlet of Crimson Cup also has a smoking zone, which was occupied too. It has a library. If you like reading books, you can seat there. There was a projector screen, where they were streaming IPL.
We had Crimson Cup Mocha & Snickers Mocha. Both of these drinks were so refreshing. I would say it's a recommendable hangout place for having iced drinks or coffee.

Name: Crimson Cup Uttara
Address: 18, 2nd Floor, Quantum Properties Ltd. Quantum Mostafa Tower, Towers Gausul Azam Ave, Dhaka
Author: All Season
Review:
Today I went to crimson specifically to test waffles but I was disappointed by the rest.
We tried
1. Sunrisers omelette 🍳🥪 which contains fried eggs 🍳 in cheese 🧀 which test worst among all of the food 🤮 it test like some mashed boiled eggs. Chicken sausage and mushrooms 🍄 was good. Lastly on this meal grilled bread 🥪 which came with peanut butter 🧈 🥜. I was hardly able to bite on this because it was bitter 🤮 when I complained they said the new chef made a mistake 😕 although they served another bread 🥪 as a compliment and said this time the original chef made this. But it was also tested bitter but better than before. I think the problem was that peanut butter 🧈.

2. Chocolate 🍫 waffles: not worthy either. 😒
In drinks we tried:
Flavoured cream 🍦 with extra wiped cream. This one was quite good ☺️

Customer service was satisfactory.

Name: Crimson Cup Uttara
Address: 18, 2nd Floor, Quantum Properties Ltd. Quantum Mostafa Tower, Towers Gausul Azam Ave, Dhaka
Author: Noor
Review:
The Coffee We Ordered Was On Time &  Taste Was Pretty Decent too!  But The Pricing Might Not Be Everyone's Cup Of Coffee ;)

We Also Had Chicken Club Sandwich Which Was Good Too, Then Again a Bit Overpriced!  The Atmosphere Was Good!  Nothing Really Worth According To The Price, But Still, a very Good Place to Hangout Or Coffee date Nonetheless!

Name: Crimson Cup Uttara
Address: 18, 2nd Floor, Quantum Properties Ltd. Quantum Mostafa Tower, Towers Gausul Azam Ave, Dhaka
Author: Aniqa Tasnim
Review:
Last Day I visited the crimsoncup and had a mere time but I've had some Issues with their coffee Taste
They added much sugar which made the wholesome coffee sugary. 😑😑

Name: Crimson Cup Uttara
Address: 18, 2nd Floor, Quantum Properties Ltd. Quantum Mostafa Tower, Towers Gausul Azam Ave, Dhaka
Author: Mohammad Humayun Kabir Hasan
Review: I recently had the pleasure of trying crimson cup coffee. The aroma was simply divine, filling the air with a rich and inviting scent.  Each sip was smooth and flavorful, with a perfect balance of bitterness and sweetness.  It was truly an exceptional coffee experience. I would highly recommend it to any coffee enthusiast.

Name: Coffee Express Fast Food & Juice Bar
Author: Dark Man
Review:
It is a small coffee shop with an air conditioned facility. Besides coffee you can get various fruit juices, cold drinks and fast food items. The taste is average compared to the price.

This place gets overcrowded as seats are limited. Not a spacious shop. The staff are well behaved.

Name: Crimson Cup Coffee
Author: Ashik Udoy
Review:
*NEW DISH ALERT at OG Coffee place*
CRIMSON CUP is very famous among the youth since 2015. Recently went there and got the opportunity to taste their 2 new items along with my 2 forever favourite drinks.
I had :
White mocha - 345 tk
Frozen Cafe Mocha - 430 tk
Chicken Choux Bun - 120 tk
Chocolate Eclairs - 160 tk

The Chicken Choux bun and the Chocolate Eclairs are newly added in the menu and my experience with them was quite pleasant.

The Choux bun was very flavourful and fulfilling. It was soft, yet crunchy and buttery. Overall, a very good item that will go with your coffee perfectly.

The Eclairs was a delightful treat. The pastry was flaky, buttery and soft which was generously filled with smooth, creamy custard and topped with rich chocolate ganache.
Overall it is a  very amazing item for any sweet lover.

Coming to the drinks, both the Hot White Mocha and Frozen Cafe Mocha were amazing as always. The White Mocha had a velvety texture and had a rich flavour of white chocolate and coffee.
The Frozen Cafe Mocha, on the other hand, was very refreshing with its blended ice and smooth chocolate taste.
The thing i loved most is that, both the drinks had a very subtle sweetness in them which is perfect for people like me who doesn't have a sweet tooth.

Name: Crimson Cup Coffee
Author: Sheikh Fardin Oyon
Review: Crimson cup is a famous coffee destination for many coffee lovers...Interior and environment is really good...Peri peri chicken sandwich was good, hot mocha was also good but not satisfying...Price was okay...

Name: Crimson Cup Coffee
Author: Sumit Razib
Review:
A solid coffee shop with good food in Gulshan-01, Dhaka. It's an ideal spot for enjoying a peaceful moment.
Their coffee was good, and so was the sandwich.
Place feels a bit cramped. Having more space would make it better.

Name: Crimson Cup Coffee
Author: Mr. khan
Review: Good place for varieties of items specually coffee. But little bit expensive.

Name: Crimson Cup Coffee
Author: Biksam
Review: One of the finest coffee makers that is located at banani 11. First of all the space inside is not so big but as the quality and taste goes, the coffee was really good. Apart from coffee I ordered an Italian chicken sandwich that was filled with egg and chicken inside and to be honest with you it was like what you would have at any other cafe’s, so it was just ok though the edge portion of that sandwich was a bit hard. I had a cookie too and that was sweet and gave a good taste to my tastebud as i took that with the coffee. You can park your car by roadside if you can find out a space available as there isn’t any specific parking zone.

Name: Gloria Jean's Coffees Uttara
Address: Plot-16 & 18, Navana Centroid, Gareeb-e-Nawaz Ave, Dhaka
Author: Abeda Sultana
Review: We ordered their chicken strips with French fries, Momos, and their chicken kebabs. Chicken pieces were very soft and melted in the mouth. Their chillers are very refreshing. Good hangout space for family and friends. People running the space were very friendly. While we were there it was time for prayers and they accommodated people who wanted to pray. The volume for the music/songs played needs to be toned down a bit.

Name: Gloria Jean's Coffees Uttara
Address: Plot-16 & 18, Navana Centroid, Gareeb-e-Nawaz Ave, Dhaka
Author: TM Iftesum Zami
Review: Finally Gloria Jean’s coffee is in uttara. I went there on the opening day (2 September, 2024). The ambience is really good. They have total 3 different sections and every section gives different vibe. Services and stuff behavior are top notch as well

Name: Gloria Jean's Coffees Uttara
Address: Plot-16 & 18, Navana Centroid, Gareeb-e-Nawaz Ave, Dhaka
Author: susmita banik priya
Review:
Finally a good cafe at Uttara.
I was eagerly waiting for Uttara brunch. Service n stuff behavior is top notch. Food is good aswell.
Gulshan serves pretty better ambience tho.

Name: Gloria Jean's Coffees Uttara
Address: Plot-16 & 18, Navana Centroid, Gareeb-e-Nawaz Ave, Dhaka
Author: Mohi Uddin
Review:
We have tried -

Calamari - 7/10
Kabab platter - 10/10
Chicken momo - 6.5/10
Cappachino - 9.5/10

Overall good. But a bit pricey. However sometimes it feels too crowdy.

Name: Gloria Jean's Coffees Uttara
Address: Plot-16 & 18, Navana Centroid, Gareeb-e-Nawaz Ave, Dhaka
Author: The Harmony
Review: A delightful experience 😊 at Gloria Jean’s Coffees ! From the moment I walked in I was greeted with warm smiles 😊🤗and a cozy atmosphere🏠. The coffee was absolutely divine rich, flavorful, and perfectly brewed. I tried the caramel latte  🥤🍮and it was hands down the best I’ve had in a long time. The selection of pastry and snacks was impressive offering something for everyone. The ambiance was perfect for catching up with friends or getting some work done in a relaxed setting. The staff 🙋‍♂️🙋‍♀️was attentive and made sure my visit was nothing short of excellent. I’ll definitely be coming back  for more😊!"

Name: The Coffee Lounge Bangladesh
Author: Laiza Habib
Review:
Amazing place for having a good time with friends and acquaintances. A perfect place for a hangout. While there is nothing negative about their coffee collection, I would like to emphasize their pizza.

I had their seafood pizza and tandoori chicken pizza. Both were unique in terms of taste and flavour. This is the first time I found squids and baby octopuses in a seafood pizza. The flavour was wholesome.

Name: The Coffee Lounge Bangladesh
Author: Shankharupa Ashish Damle
Review:
Recently visited this place. Checked out photos on google and went there for an official one-to-one meeting.
It has a good location and quite spacious. Road facing and the cafe is on the first floor of a self contained building.
Service quite poor and the waiters had to be called more than twice to place orders and again to get the bill they had to be called thrice.
However the place was apt to have a meeting.

Name: The Coffee Lounge Bangladesh
Author: tahiya tabassum
Review:
The Coffee Lounge has become one of my favourite cafe in Gulshan. Excellent services, wonderful environment, and amazing food.

I had their chicken tandoori wrap, Chicken Pizza, creamy chicken and mushroom pasta. The taste was up to the mark and it didn’t disappoint me. ❤️

Name: The Coffee Lounge Bangladesh
Author: Zubair Mustafa
Review:
If you're in search of a great coffee spot in Gulshan, look no further than The Coffee Lounge. My go-to order is their Americano—a perfectly brewed coffee with exceptional roasted beans that never fails to impress.

The other items on their menu also maintain excellent quality and flavor. The Coffee Lounge comes highly recommended. ❤️

Name: The Coffee Lounge Bangladesh
Author: Russel Rasi
Review: The service was courteous, and the cappuccino was decent. However, the chocolate croissant was unexpectedly too oily for my liking—it seemed more fried than baked.

Name: Coffee Buzz (কফি বাজ)
Author: Biswajit Chakraborty
Review: Quality of their coffee not bad. Service customer friendly. There is seating for 10 to 12 people at a time.

Name: Coffee Buzz (কফি বাজ)
Author: Khaled Mahamod
Review:
Every time I go to a cafe or a hotel or a restaurant I check Google reviews first 😑.
Because I really have some trust issues....
1. I ordered a cappuccino. And it tastes average I will give it 4.7
2. But I feel very awkward. At first they served my coffee at my table... So I thought it was a table service.
But unfortunately when it was time to pay the bill they told me to play the bill at the counter.  I feel very awkward... 🙃 ( আমি উনাদেরকে লিটারেলি বিলের জন্য ডাক দিচ্ছিলাম )

Name: Coffee Buzz (কফি বাজ)
Author: Zubaid Rahman
Review: Place was neat and clean but sitting arrangement was not sufficient. Food was really fresh and good. Price was slightly higher. Behavior of their stuff was nice..helpful..and also their service was quick. Really recommend their food .

Name: Coffee Buzz (কফি বাজ)
Author: Sarnojjal Chakma
Review: Everything was perfect except the pricing of potato wedges ! I think it's too much for 200 gm.

Name: Coffee Buzz (কফি বাজ)
Author: Arafath Munna
Review:
Nice and tidy place. Sitting arrangement is limited. But great for quick coffee or if you need a place to have a short meeting.
Tried their Cappuccino & Americano. Felt okay with the taste.

Name: Nerdy Bean Coffee Haus
Author: Biksam
Review: This coffee house is located in Ahmed & Kazi tower on first basement floor. It's a well decorated coffee house that offers quality coffee and breakfast meal. I had one Hazelnut Latte that was so good and an English Breakfast meal that was just outstanding. But one thing that they need to improve on is their service because they usually take so much time to serve but considering the lively atmosphere it can be overlooked. However there is no specific place to park  so street parking is the only option but even this might be difficult sometimes as the road remains busy with traffic most of the times.

Name: Nerdy Bean Coffee Haus
Author: Z M Sabbir Sabab
Review:
It was a great place to sit with your loved one and enjoy your alone time. There was little to no noise, the atmosphere very soothing. I spent over 5 hours there and they didn't even ask to leave or asked to order more. Their waiters are very well mannered. They have a great collection of books. You can enjoy a peaceful time there comfortably.

I would recommend their English breakfast. It was super tasty and great to start your day. They can definitely work on their cappuccino as it was a little less dense than I would have liked. Overall I fully enjoyed my time there.

Name: Nerdy Bean Coffee Haus
Author: sifat sipu
Review:
The place is more attractive for their vintage style decorating over their coffee items. It's located in the basement of the building.A separate smoking zone is available but fully close doors place that is. Less waiting time is required after ordering food. Cold & hot both types of coffee are presented into their menu. A desert is also available.Price is in the medium range. The behavior of the waiter is good enough.

I got a bbq smoked sandwich and a cafe lette.

SMOKED CHICKEN SANDWICH (Definitely Recommended)Two pieces of sandwich stuffed with loads of smoked chicken and cheese. The melted cheese was dripping out of the sandwich. The smocked chicken was also perfectly cooked and full of flavour. They had the option to choose white or brown bread. I went with brown bread because it is healthier.

Love this place overall.

Name: Nerdy Bean Coffee Haus
Author: Ashiq Billah Monjil
Review:
.
Classic minimalistic interior decor
Reasonably priced food
Loads of great books to read for free
Bigger and better smoking zone
Cozy comfortable vibe
.
FULL ENGLISH BREAKFAST- (Recommended)
The English breakfast is a scrumptious meal that has a sunny-side-up egg, 3 sausages, slices of beef bacon, baked beans, grilled mushroom, half a grilled tomato, lettuce, loads of butter and 2 pieces of toasted brown bread. It made for a wholesome and filling breakfast/brunch.
.
SMOKED CHICKEN SANDWICH (Definitely Recommended)
Two pieces of sandwich stuffed with loads of smoked chicken and cheese. The melted cheese was dripping out of the sandwich. The smocked chicken was also perfectly cooked and full of flavour. They had the option to choose white or brown bread. I went with brown bread because it is healthier.
.
SAN SEBASTIAN CHEESECAKE -   (Definitely Recommended)
A slice of heavenly soft cheesecake dripped entirely in a chocolate syrup. The cheesecake had a very subtle sweet flavour that was perfectly balanced by the sweetness of the chocolate.
.
RED VELVET CAKE - (Recommended)
Basic moist red velvet cake.

WHITE MOCHA FRAPPE - (Recommended)
.
SALTED CARAMEL MOCHA -
The salted caramel mocha unfortunately had a slight bitter taste so I could not drink it. They asked to replace it but I was already full from all the food so opted against it.

Name: Nerdy Bean Coffee Haus
Author: Shobnum Alam
Review:
Nerdy Bean has been a long-lasting favourite of mine for several years. Savory food, cozy atmosphere, combined with good music makes a good place to hang out or have some discussions for 2 hours easily.
While the atmosphere is still my favourite thing about this place, the food quality seems to deteriorate a bit compared to my previous experiences.

Name: Crimson Cup Coffee
Address: Rangs Nasim Square, House no. 275/D, Ground Floor, 27 রোড নং:- ২, Dhaka
Author: sky Forhad
Review:
This is Crimson Cup Coffee shop
is always my favorite place for coffee. Their service, food, atmosphere, and behavior are all very friendly. I like spending time there.
Well if I have to describe this place, in few words it is just kind of a heaven for coffee  lovers. Most of the time this place is filled with people who like to try different authenticated flavors of coffee. The place has a vintage yet modern kind of vibe. They also have numerous types of dessert. I liked their pastry cake also . It was so soft yet creamy and sweetness level was also appropriate.

Address: Rangs Nasim Square, House no. 275/D, Ground Floor, 27 রোড নং:- ২, Dhaka

Review: Crimson Cup is a famous coffee chain of Dhaka.. The interior and environment is quite energetic...Their coffees ,frappes are really good ik taste...Specially the chicken sandwich is also tasty enough...Price is a bit high...Staffs are also friendly...

Address: Rangs Nasim Square, House no. 275/D, Ground Floor, 27 রোড নং:- ২, Dhaka

We ordered desserts which were below average. Cheesecake tasted really bad. The hot & cold coffee was okish. Overall, I'll visit this place again if i don't get vacant sits in other cafe.

The food is not so good either not so bad.. I got many varieties of platter in lunch. Their mocktails and grilled chicken were super delicious.
Amazing food and service.. Purely loved it..
