Name: NORTH END coffee roasters LK Tower 2
Author: FY’s SpAcE
Review: The place has a warm and inviting atmosphere, perfect for enjoying a cup of coffee with friends or for some quiet time alone. The coffee is also delicious, with a variety of options to choose from. Whether you're looking for a cozy place to catch up with loved ones or a peaceful spot to relax and recharge, this café has it all.

Name: NORTH END coffee roasters LK Tower 2
Author: Nawar Akter Bushra
Review:
Hands down, this is the best cafe in town. They are the pioneer in grooming the cafe culture here. I love the taste of their coffee.

Service is nice. Seating arrangement is good. They have both smoking and non-smoking zones.

Overall, it is an ultimate go-to place if you are a coffee-lover.

Name: NORTH END coffee roasters LK Tower 2
Author: Subangkar Karmaker
Review: As usual they serve the best coffee in BD. Though this branch is comparatively small, the interior is great. Tried the Frozen Salted Caramel Latte and Brownie with Hazelnut cream cheese. Both were top notch.

Name: NORTH END coffee roasters LK Tower 2
Author: saamiah jardine
Review: This place was such a gem to find in Dhaka. Their coffee tasted like home and the quiet atmosphere is perfect for early morning coffee's

Name: NORTH END coffee roasters LK Tower 2
Author: Shahriar Mamun
Review:
Hands down, this is the best cafe in town. They are the pioneer in grooming the cafe culture here. I love the taste of their coffee. In terms of coffee, I mostly take Americano, and I tried that at various places, but the coffee at North End is unparalleled.

Other than coffee, I also like their brownie, croissant and roasted beef sandwich.

Service is nice. Seating arrangement is good. They have both smoking and non-smoking zones.

Overall, it is an ultimate go-to place if you are a coffee-lover.

Name: Arabika Coffee
Address: Block# I, Plot# 77 Rd No. 3, Dhaka
Author: Sheikh Fardin Oyon
Review: Arabika is a good name to many coffee lovers...The interior and ambience was fresh and posh...The hot mocha was really good and the Tiramisu tart was also fantastic...Price was okay...Staffs were also friendly enough...Had a good experience here...

Name: Arabika Coffee
Address: Block# I, Plot# 77 Rd No. 3, Dhaka
Author: N A I L A Z
Review:
I enjoyed their ice-matcha with a chicken croissant. The atmosphere was too loud and crowded, but they're pro-palestine. It's in a beautiful green place. It's a nice, calm place to hang out with friends.

Matcha: 4/5
Chicken croissant: 3/5
Service: they took their time.

Name: Arabika Coffee
Address: Block# I, Plot# 77 Rd No. 3, Dhaka
Author: tondra cs
Review: One of the best thing I had this year is this "Tiramisu'. Taste is very unique and goes well with a cup of coffee. I like Latte, So I always order latte. I recommend this  'Tiramisu'. One of the must try item for coffee lovers.

Name: Arabika Coffee
Address: Block# I, Plot# 77 Rd No. 3, Dhaka
Author: Sumit Razib
Review: This is one of the best coffee shop in Basundhara.I absolutely love this. The coffee tastes good compared to others, but it's a crowded place. This place is not for those who want to spend some quiet time while having coffee.

Name: Arabika Coffee
Address: Block# I, Plot# 77 Rd No. 3, Dhaka
Author: Mashfia Rahman
Review:
Ordered butterscotch frozotino and was quite disappointed. It is just crushed ice with no flavour, asked them to fix it they just added little more butterscotch flavour but that’s it. It was still very bland. Their brownie is more like a cake. :/ not happy with their food quality.
Their service is alright, quite slow and forgetful. Need to keep reminding them or asking for things again and again.

Name: NORTH END coffee roasters Shahabuddin Ahmed Park
Author: shahajahan khandakar
Review: This coffee shop is a hidden gem with a warm, inviting atmosphere perfect for both work and relaxation. The coffee is consistently rich and flavorful, with a smooth, well-balanced taste that never disappoints. The staff is friendly and attentive, always ensuring you feel welcome and taken care of. Their menu offers a great variety of drinks and freshly made pastries, all reasonably priced. Overall, it's the ideal spot for a quick pick-me-up or a peaceful afternoon.

Name: NORTH END coffee roasters Shahabuddin Ahmed Park
Author: Akib Jabed
Review: Absolutely loved their ambience. The baristas are super friendly and I would recommend visiting during the day time as it gets really crowded in the evening. Quite smaller than other branches but they have outdoor seating space too.

Name: NORTH END coffee roasters Shahabuddin Ahmed Park
Author: Sajjad Hossain
Review:
The litter cafe inside the park is a must visit for coffee lovers.
They have both indoor and outdoor seating space. With indoor space you will have air conditioning but in outer space you will have ceiling fans and you'll be able to enjoy your smoking.
The services as always self-service you have to place your orders at the computer and pay ahead of Time. After food is ready, they'll serve that to you.
The ambience is very nice with glass view of outside both of the park and the road.

Name: NORTH END coffee roasters Shahabuddin Ahmed Park
Author: NJ Lily
Review: It's inside the justice shahabuddin park. The place is cozy and nice. And m9st importantly they use paper plates. Which is amazing. The food is as usual good. And the coffee smells and tastes amazing. If you want to have a morning or evening walk you can go and take a coffee to enjoy.

Name: NORTH END coffee roasters Shahabuddin Ahmed Park
Author: Maisha Maliha Rahman
Review: Hands down, this is my most favorite North End branch right now. If you want to hangout with your friends or get some work done in a coffee shop, but also love being close to nature, this will be the ideal place for you. As it is inside a park, the ambiance is different than a super busy loud cafe. The food is always consistent and delicious. The servers are one of the most humble and friendly out there. I love to do my weekend works over there in the morning. All of their sandwiches are amazing and my personal favorite hot beverages are: caramel latte and hot chocolate. Highly recommended place!

Name: NORTH END coffee roasters Borak Mehnur
Author: Shankharupa Ashish Damle
Review:
A very vibrant coffee house bustling with crowd most of the time. A number of options in various kinds of bread and cookies and a unique salted caramel coffee that’s my favourite.
Staff is very friendly and jovial.
One short of 5 stars is because the place tends to be very noisy at times and becomes difficult to have a candid discussion.

Name: NORTH END coffee roasters Borak Mehnur
Author: Sharif Farzana
Review: The salted caramel has lost it’s flavor and taste. Halfway through my drink it started tasing like milk flavored water. The quality of the coffee has also plummeted since the beginning of this year. Not the best coffee place anymore

Name: NORTH END coffee roasters Borak Mehnur
Author: Sheikh Fardin Oyon
Review: North end is a famous name in the coffee section of Bangladesh...The interior and ambience was relaxing... Tried their croissant with roasted beef, dark choco coffee and the taste was quite impressive..  Staffs were friendly enough...Price is a bit high but okay...

Name: NORTH END coffee roasters Borak Mehnur
Author: MOON MONIRA
Review: North end is nothing but home to us. We've spent our many days here. This place hold so much memories! & thanks for this beautiful gesture for us..😇😇

Name: NORTH END coffee roasters Borak Mehnur
Author: Susmita Hossain
Review: This is my go to place on my way back home from office. The mornings here are pleasant too. The servers are quick and polite and coffee,  excellent as always.  I found the crowd that comes here regularly are more into coffee and talks and work and less into taking snaps, that is a relief!

Author: CR7LM10NJ11
Review: Just too awesome. Amazing calm cozy relaxing vibe. Servicers are quite friendly. Food is fabulous.

Author: Sadat KM
Review: The best coffee shop in the town.. there was a mix of lighting and darkness and classic vibes..the place was crowded yet felt quiet. provided great service and coffee was good.. you should try cappuccino using the French blend coffee, better than regular blend.. salted ice caramel coffee was good too. Espresso and americano were good as usual compared other branches

Author: Maisha Maliha Rahman
Review: One of the best places to get good coffee and sandwiches in that area. The seating arrangement is nice, cozy and properly spaced. Their service is friendly and welcoming. My favorite there to order is: Roast beef croissant and Hot chocolate/Hazelnut latte. The only thing they can improve is keeping up the washroom clean. Would highly recommend for going there just by yourself, your friends and loved ones.

Author: Md. Al- mamun
Review: The atmosphere here is great for having coffee as well as having a good time.

Name: NORTH END Coffee Roasters Shahjadpur
Author: S R official
Review:
"Northend is a delightful local coffee shop chain nestled within Dhaka, boasting over 18 branches scattered across the vibrant neighborhoods of Dhanmondi, Gulshan, and Banani. My personal haven is the Northend main branch in Baridhara, conveniently located near the U.S. Embassy in Dhaka.

During my recent visit, I indulged in a comforting Americano paired with a bagel generously slathered in hazelnut cream - a personal favorite from North End's menu. What sets this branch apart is its enchanting ambiance; the moment I stepped in, the aromatic embrace of freshly roasted coffee beans enveloped me. The interior exudes a warm, homely vibe, making it a haven for coffee enthusiasts like me.

In essence, the Northend flagship branch in Baridhara is more than just a coffee shop; it's an inviting sanctuary where the rich aroma of coffee and cozy surroundings create an unparalleled experience."

Name: Rio Coffee Rajarbagh
Author: Fakrul Islam
Review:
History of Coffee

The earliest credible evidence of coffee drinking appears in modern-day Yemen in southern Arabia in the middle of the 15th century in Sufi shrines. It was here in Arabia that coffee seeds were first roasted and brewed in a manner similar to how it is now prepared. But the coffee seeds had to be first exported from East Africa to Yemen, as Coffea arabica is thought to have been indigenous to the former. By the 16th century, the drink had reached Persia, Turkey, and North Africa and eventually in Europe.

Coffee has a long history in Italy. Venice was one of the first European ports to import coffee beans in the 16th century, and in the 19th century, men in bowler hats met in Turin's coffee shops to plan for the country's unification. Italy truly emerged as the global leader in coffee thanks to Milanese inventor Luigi Bezzera.

Review: Triangle Coffee Hut is a hidden gem nestled in the heart of the city. With its cozy atmosphere and aromatic brews, it's the perfect spot to unwind or catch up with friends. Author: Lindsay Rimpel
Review:
Currently at the the cafe as i write this. I love this place. Amazing coffee. The service is top notice and understanding. The atmosphere is cozy and cute. And if you need more privacy, there is the upstairs area where someone can get work and studying done.

The best part is that it stays open pretty late or later than the cafe that is around. My go-to remote place to work at.

Name: Caffe Trieste
Author: Deniz Gunduz
Review: I visited one of the oldest cafes in an Italian Village Caffe Trieste next to Chinatown in San Francisco, and it was an amazing experience. The atmosphere was incredible, giving off a strong sense of Italian culture that feels authentic and unchanged over the years. I enjoyed a delicious latte and a croissant while observing that most of the clientele were older locals, adding to the charm. It truly felt like stepping into a local gem that has stood the test of time. Highly recommended!

Name: Caffe Trieste
Author: Attosa Tavallai
Review: 4.5 stars! I really enjoy coming to this little gem of a place after I eat across the way at El Farolito. If they had better coffee, they'd get a 5 from me. The service is hit or miss. Either you'll get some super lovely guy in glasses who likes to speak in a fake Italian accent (in an adorable way) or you get a super grumpy hipster kid who doesn't want to be there, pained to muster up a "have a nice day." The cheesecake is TO DIE FOR and the seating and table space is good for a little San Francisco shop. Love to play backgammon and make art here. They've got that much table room!

Name: Caffe Trieste
Author: PPanayotov
Review: The place is one of the iconic places in San Francisco, and the staff, food, and drinks are worth it. It was a joy to visit. P.S.: They only accept cash, but there's an ATM inside the cafe so you can get some cash out.

Name: Caffe Trieste
Author: Chef Tony
Review:
So this place was recommended and I went there. It started off bad, but after I relaxed with a cappuccino and replayed everything,  I went and apologized to the 2 workers.
Now the cappuccino was great, and the cookie was good, too. I was disappointed that they didn't have sandwiches, but they do have some amazing looking pastries. I will go back in a few weeks.
This is just a reminder to those who do read this it's cash only here.

Name: Caffe Trieste
Author: P
Review: Great place to have a real cappuccino! The pastries are delicious without being too sweet. The establishment has lots of indoor and outdoor seating, you will always find locals chatting and having a good time. In addition, they have a bathroom and a little store to get your own coffee beans or mocha pot.

Name: Mercury Cafe
Author: Isabella Grandic
Review: Super cute cafe, good wifi, was not super busy on a Monday midday. However the coffee took 20-30 mins to come and the avocado toast was a bit stale. I’m not sure that the food was really worthwhile. The coffee was nice, $6.50 for a vanilla latte. Staff were nice but not super friendly :(

Name: Mercury Cafe
Author: Matt Hickman
Review:
Food took over half an hour to arrive. Love the location but don’t order anything more complicated than a coffee or you’re SOL.

Edit in response to owner: we ordered two unmodified egg dishes, staff never said it would take a while. Food seemed to be taking a long time for all guests.

Name: Mercury Cafe
Author: Bobby G
Review:
Local independent cafe! Everything is good. - I usually get their cold brew or drip coffee. And they also serve alcohol.

I live in the neighborhood and love the sporadic concerts they have - I would welcome more of that :)

Name: Mercury Cafe
Author: Greg Polakoff
Review: Whenever I come to San Francisco, this is my go-to-cafe for excellent coffee, tasty food, and a comfortable workspace. Mercury brews modern tasting third-wave coffee, makes solid pour overs, and serves egg dishes, avocado toasts, great sandwiches and  scones. However, it also feels like a traditional 80s-90s San Francisco cafe with informal service, a worn in look, and used books lying around. It's pretty charming. I also like the employees, who seem to be artists and musicians. The music is usually quite interesting. If you are looking for cutting edge coffee and contemporary cafe food with an old-school San Francisco feel, look no further!

Name: Mercury Cafe
Author: Katie Ahmann
Review: So cute and cozy. Lots of books scattered throughout the place to read and great art. They have a piano for the occasional jazz night. Delicious pour over. Really tasty sandwiches with fresh bread. Overall, a really tasteful and tasty place to visit!

Name: Mellis Cafe
Author: Bianka Hain
Review: We found ourselves returning to this spot repeatedly. The breakfast bagels are fantastic, and the sandwiches are even better. Everything is incredibly fresh, and the staff is both friendly and efficient. Conveniently located just down the street from Union Square and Parc 55.

Name: Mellis Cafe
Author: Macy M
Review: Breakfast bagel sandwich was really good, chai latte is a skip. Walk-up counter with a handful of tables and chairs on the sidewalk. Quick service, maybe a five minute wait for both items.

Name: Mellis Cafe
Author: Feryal Alayont
Review: I was in San Francisco for a conference in January and Mellis Cafe happened to be near where the conference was. I decided to try it out and I'm so glad I did. I got sandwiches and the egg and cheese bagel two different days. I also tried their desserts. Their Turkish delight was probably the best I've eaten and I'm originally from Turkey. Their baklava was the best baklava I ate in the US only to be beaten by homemade baklava in my home country. I also tried two other desserts that I don't remember the names of and they were delicious as well. They get top scores on desserts. The egg bagel and sandwiches were good but I can't say they were great. The food came out pretty quickly every time though and that's an important plus for me. The price was also super reasonable. The only downside is they don't have much seating available. That wasn't an issue thankfully since not many people seem to be interested in sitting down. Most people just grabbed their food to go when I sat down one time to eat. Overall, I was satisfied with my experience and recommend them, especially if you want good baklava or desserts.

Name: Mellis Cafe
Author: Chris Hillmann
Review:
This place was great! The staff was very friendly and the food and drinks were delicious. I ordered breakfast bagels and a breakfast croissant as well as a couple coffee drinks.

The only reason I took away from the atmosphere was because of an unpleasant person screaming obscenely on the sidewalk nearby. Otherwise, the atmosphere is decent. It's a walk-up restaurant very close to our hotel.

Name: Mellis Cafe
Author: Madira Kee
Review: Delicious real coffee. They have organic coffee beans and deliciously roasted. Hard to find a god cup of coffee but Mellis' coffee is really good. All the pastries, bagels and sandwiches are good. They also have a selection of pastries and cakes. Excellent cafe! Keep it going guys!

Name: South Beach Cafe
Author: Jackie DeJesse
Review: Cute, cozy traditional Italian cafe. Very old school with classic items like tiramisu, thin crust pizza, fresh pasta and more. Highly recommend for folks looking for Italian food in the south beach area!

Name: South Beach Cafe
Author: Poorvi Goradia
Review: Sunny corner cafe with outdoor seating and a view of the water! We ordered the vegetarian egg and cheese sandwich that had the most fluffy eggs. The owner very kindly cut it in half and brought it on two separate plates for us even though we didn't ask. Thank you, we will be frequenting this place more often!

Name: South Beach Cafe
Author: Eddie Valadez
Review: South Beach Cafe has been my 5 star place for over 20 years. Everything on the food menu has been sampled by me. The Cafe is clean, comfortable and spacious. The owner and his wife are very friendly and understand the SOMA culture. The location is on point for people watching, nature, and relaxing for a drink. I recommend the pizza of any style. The sandwiches are made to order and the bread is a type of ciabatta. For a quick breakfast, I usually order the scrambled eggs.

Name: South Beach Cafe
Author: Michael McGregor
Review: So full of promise but so disappointing. Some new blood needed. Displaying Costco bought cookies etc in the plastic container is not inspiring. And maybe put away the boxes and folding tables in the back, instead of where your customer sit? The one plus is that the coffee is very tasty.

Name: South Beach Cafe
Author: Jan Vlachy
Review:
Lovely owners. Great views. A place with a character. The food items seem like a standard breakfast / sandwich (and pizza!) fare, but are made with love - not your usual, generic sandwich. I got a tuna sandwich, served with salad - very delicious.

Fast wifi available, though this is not a place to sit and work all day (but good for an hour or two when waiting).

Name: MLK Cafe
Address: 3860 Martin Luther King Jr Way, Oakland
Author: Christopher Chung
Review:
East African and American classics served in a dark sport bar.

Kelwa Beef, spicy tomato sauce and carmelized onions — flavorful and comforting.

Name: MLK Cafe
Address: 3860 Martin Luther King Jr Way, Oakland
Author: zelma lewis
Review:
I've had lunch at MLK Cafe in the past and thoroughly enjoyed the food. My friend and I ordered our dishes by menu number so the young server would not get confused. However, it didn't help, because she brought us 1/2  wrong main dishes. About 15 min. later she brought the spinach dish my friend wanted and we liked. The fried potatoes and shrimp appetizer were edible. The potato and cabbage dish was very bland. The injera under our food was dry, and we were both disappointed that neither of the main dishes were saturated with juices from the food so that we could truly "sop" up the liquid with injera as we have done in other Ethiopian restaurants. The music was very loud (we  are Seniors and it was hard to talk).
Despite my above comments, I am impressed that the restaurant is still standing strong, I hope, in the community despite the pandemic. Kudos to the owner!

Name: MLK Cafe
Address: 3860 Martin Luther King Jr Way, Oakland
Author: Nick Patterson
Review: Excellent food with an attentive staff and friendly clientele. Lamb and rice with French Fries were delicious.

Name: MLK Cafe
Address: 3860 Martin Luther King Jr Way, Oakland
Author: medhane mesfun
Review:
A well know place for Eritrean community.
But it lack hospitality and internal decorations. It must introduce new things.

Name: MLK Cafe
Address: 3860 Martin Luther King Jr Way, Oakland
Author: Bereket Feday
Review:
MLK cafe created such a warm and inviting atmosphere here, it's truly a haven for coffee in the morning and beer in the evening lovers.
Here ,the Foul and Kicha Fitfit, which are traditional Eritrean breakfast food are a culinary delight! Each bite is a symphony of flavors that leaves us craving more.
Bereket Feday

Name: Twisted St. Cafe
Author: Tom Heilmann
Review:
Twisted St. Café is a great spot! I had the cappuccino, and it was absolutely spot on—rich, smooth, and perfectly brewed. But the real stars of my visit were the green smoothie and the açaí bowl. The smoothie was fantastic, just the right balance of sweetness, making it incredibly refreshing without being overwhelming. As for the açaí bowl, it was the perfect way to start the day—fresh, flavorful, and beautifully presented. The combination of textures and flavors really hit the mark.

The atmosphere in the café is cozy and welcoming, making it a great place to relax and enjoy a healthy, delicious meal. I can’t recommend Twisted St. Café enough—everything from the drinks to the food is on point!

Name: Twisted St. Cafe
Author: Luciana Ricart
Review: This coffee place is just amazing! The best coffee place I have been too in the US. The coffee is excellent and all the food is prepared with fresh ingredients and made to order. I have recently visited Sydney and Australians pride on their coffee and avocado on toast. This place in San Francisco has coffee and avocado on toast of similar quality as the best places in Sydney. Don’t go anywhere else if you are in the area. The staff is really lovely and service is great even when they are super busy which is often the case.

Name: Twisted St. Cafe
Author: Dan Mark
Review:
This place is a gourmet place.
They have a great variety of items: coffees, smoothies, sandwiches, molettes, yoghurt bowl with fruit, fruit bowl etc. many very delicious choices.
We had the yoghurt bowl, molettes, cafe latte and a smoothie.
Only small minus is that a lot of people order coffee and not food - so if you order both you’ll get the food first and about 10-15 minutes later you’ll get your coffee.
I would like to suggest that drink orders combined with food are prioritised, so one can enjoy the coffee along side the food.

There are indoor and outdoor seating.

It is gourmet so expect a pricy meal.

Name: Twisted St. Cafe
Author: Jen Deal
Review: Was a nice place to chill with a beverage and snack.  Staff was friendly and not overly chatty. Knowledgeable about their products, making an excellent drink. Good people watching through windows. I'd frequent if I was in the hood.

Name: Twisted St. Cafe
Author: Marina Ferrari Vobroucek
Review: It was one of the best coffees I had in SF. Great avocado toast, as well as the croissant with bacon egg and cheddar. I also loved the prosciutto and fig toast. The morning bun tasted not too fresh . Always very busy but they manage to be corteous and  efficient

Name: Bean Bag Cafe
Author: Michelle Lu
Review:
Love this place, staple of the neighborhood. Good cafe food: my favorites are the beet salad with chicken and the shrimp pasta, but also great breakfast, tacos, etc. Cozy and casual environment, nice people, good for working or meeting friends or just grabbing food/coffee.

Always features local artists with works for purchase which is a nice touch.

Name: Bean Bag Cafe
Author: Steven Beverly
Review:
I have been coming in here randomly for years to sit and have a nice cup of coffee. The vibes are always good and it’s always and warm inside, lots of sun coming in the windows.

There is even more sun coming from behind the counter and I always leave this place with a smile on my face.

I tried the cheesesteak sandwich today, first time ordering food, and it was stellar. Highly recommend this lovely little spot.

Name: Bean Bag Cafe
Author: Alle Esor
Review:
Delicious, reasonable pricing (for SF), peaceful, clean and cozy environment.

They accommodated my dietary restrictions which shows a level of care rare to find in today’s world!

They don’t make spinach salads, but custom made a spinach and orange vine grate side salad to go with my portobello sandwich, since I despise lettuce and spring mix. Additionally, I asked to sub bell peppers for cucumber and the cashier ran back to the kitchen to notify them! No extra charge for the substitutions, by the way. The service is phenomenal.

To find a delicious sandwich AND side salad for $15 in a clean, cozy shop in a great location is incredible. We had a blast people watching and enjoying the city sounds and sites through the windows too :)

Name: Bean Bag Cafe
Author: Snigdha Agarwal
Review:
Pros:
- lots of vegetarian options
- very polite and friendly staff
- everything was flavorful. I got the iced oat milk latte, bean bag sweet crepe and Vienna savory crepe. Liked it all!
Cons:
- messed up my mocha order to a latte
- brought out the sweet dish along with the mains which made the crepe stretchy and chewy by the time we got to it.

Name: Bean Bag Cafe
Author: Richard Shulaw
Review: Poor service and food was adequate at best. Honestly I ate better in Army dining halls. Asked specifically for hash browns and they brought some horrible pan fried potatoes with no condiments. When I went inside and asked for salt, pepper and anything to add some taste to the sorry mess they brought ketchup! Then the manager had the nerve to ask me if anything was wrong. One piece of toast with a single small pat of butter. Absolutely no refills of a cup of brewed coffee? We can bring you one but you have to pay for it before you leave?🙄#wontbeback

Name: Café de Olla
Author: C S
Review: Café de Olla offers an authentic Mexican experience that's hard to beat. Their tamales are a must-try—packed with great flavors and just the right amount of spice. At about $6 each, they’re worth every penny. The texture is perfect, and the filling is always generous. If you're looking for a place that delivers on both taste and value, this is it. Highly recommended!

Name: Café de Olla
Author: mitch
Review: I really wanted to like this place. The tamale was dry and bland and the Tlayuda was crispy but I have no idea what was inside. The meat tasted processed and it all had that really hot inside thing which seemed like they microwaved the food. Wasn’t too happy with it unfortunately.

Name: Café de Olla
Author: Jeanette G
Review: I am Oaxacan and I go to this spot because the food reminds me of my grandmother’s cooking. You will find a variety of dishes and drinks, perfect for any meal of the day.

Name: Café de Olla
Author: Hannah Meiseles
Review: Absolutely a hidden gem! I got a chileajo tamale and a Oaxacan hot chocolate. The tamale came with a sauce that was smoky, a little spicy, and the perfect compliment to the tamale. And it was only $6! Hot chocolate was also delicious and made by hand at our table. So happy to have found this place and would recommend to anyone visiting the Mission.

Name: Café de Olla
Author: Alan Cuevas
Review: This place is great! A great alternative to the common taqueria style Mexican dishes you’ll find throughout the mission. In my top 3 of Mexican places in SF. I predict it’s only a matter of time before this places blows up and has overwhelming lines! Which would be a good thing! 👍

Name: 1428 HAIGHT Patio Cafe & Crepery
Author: Jerica Saddler Bragner
Review: Stumbled into this dimension and found  crepes that taste like they’ve been kissed by the flavor gods. The savory crepe hit me like a savory freight train, hearty enough to satisfy my soul. The patio? A secret oasis where time warps, and the Bloody Mary? Perfectly balanced like a ninja—half spice, half strength, all magic. 9/10 would time travel here again.

Name: 1428 HAIGHT Patio Cafe & Crepery
Author: Karli Jaenike
Review: So soooooo good! Just picked this place out on Google due to the good reviews and it didn’t disappoint. Everything we ordered was so delicious and everything seemed very fresh and expertly made to order. Crabcake Florentine crêpe was amazing. The burrito was surprisingly, really really really good. Croissant sandwich also got great reviews. Check it out!

Name: 1428 HAIGHT Patio Cafe & Crepery
Author: Vaishali Sabhahit
Review: Cozy covered patio. We loved the vegetarian crepes and the Nutella one. We ordered two because one wasn't enough! Bloody Mary was bloody delicious :) service is warm

Name: 1428 HAIGHT Patio Cafe & Crepery
Author: Nikol Ortiz
Review: Food is excellent!!! Perfectly cooked and surprisingly not too heavy. Service was excellent and really quick. Mimosa was nice and stiff and coffee wasn’t bitter. Vibe was awesome. We will be coming back!

Name: 1428 HAIGHT Patio Cafe & Crepery
Author: Vikram Haer
Review:
Came again and the Benedict eggs were perfect this time! Great portions and a lovely atmosphere.

Old review:  Eggs for the Benedict were over cooked, but the potatoes are the best part, perfect texture and flavor

Name: Sweetheart Cafe
Author: Donna Fong
Review: I had a ginger milk which is served hot. It took a lot of time to make but there were 4 to 5 people ahead of me. The day was hot so there was a line. My ginger milk also takes a while to solidify and boy was it worth the wait. Spicy, sweet and warm. The shop can make food, or serve you ice cream, candies, bingsu,  boba and more. Considering how small the footprint is, it's pretty amazing. However there is no place to sit. You have to type in your own order. And it has no sense of decor or style. If you are okay with all of that, this place is for you. I thought it fit the bill for me.

Name: Sweetheart Cafe
Author: Alejandra Garcia
Review:
I ventured into this place with hopes of savoring authentic Asian desserts, and while my overall experience was normal, I want to share some additional details. This sweet haven is tucked away in a small shop nestled in Chinatown. Upon arrival, I realized that the service process here is quite peculiar; you take your order yourself and then simply wait for it to be delivered to you.

We opted for the Oreo Bingsu and the Nutella crepe, two choices that promised to satisfy my sweet cravings. However, the wait to receive our order was longer than expected, almost 20 minutes, despite the place not being particularly crowded. This aspect could be an inconvenience for those in a hurry, although personally, I found the delay to be an opportunity to enjoy the diversity of Asian treats adorning the cozy space.

What makes this place interesting is that, despite its small size and unique service approach, the desserts showcase an authenticity and quality worth highlighting. Additionally, I appreciate the attentive attitude of the staff, even though most of them are older individuals. In summary, this is a charming corner in Chinatown where you can take your own order, immerse yourself in the atmosphere of Asian sweets, and enjoy authentic flavors in a peculiar setting.

Name: Sweetheart Cafe
Author: Kim Chan
Review:
This place is ok, I've come twice so far.  Decent place for egg waffles...but I can't recall if egg waffles usually cool down as fast as they do here.  I came to post this review to remind myself and everyone that plans to go here, don't get the strawberry milk tea.  I pray that you don't have to experience this drink like I had to.  The color was crazy artificial as well as the taste.  I had a flashback of the liquid medicine we had to take as children except it was milky.  Please save yourself by getting something else unless this is what you're after.

The previous visit I had a grapefruit green tea and their sign says that they use organic grapefruit juice and they put in a grapefruit slice in their drink.  It was bitter for me but maybe cuz I ordered it with less sugar.

Order with low expectations.  You can pay with cash, go up to the register and call for someone to take your order.  Prices are reasonable here.  I think flavored drinks are $5.50 and the boba is included. The grapefruit green tea was $5 with boba included.  Other toppings are an additional $0.75.

If you think they are taking a long time with your order and you used the kiosk outside, you should check in with them.  Sometimes the orders don't go through due to their wifi (happened to me the first time I went).  Make sure to print your receipt.

Name: Sweetheart Cafe
Author: Stacy Moore
Review: What a fun snack and dessert shop!  I enjoyed the hot grass jelly with all the condiments (red beans, small and large bobas) and grapefruits tea with bob.  Staff is friendly and no wait during weekdays. Will try the home made sesame ice cream next time.  Supposedly sesame has antioxidant, anti inflammatory, heart healthy, and improve hair effect

Name: Sweetheart Cafe
Author: Mrimon Guha
Review: Stumbled upon this cute cafe in the middle of Chinatown. They have a good number of options for tea, boba and snacks. My friends loved the cookies and creme smoothie with boba. It was decent and quite affordable.

Name: Garage Cafe
Author: Fiamma Giger
Review: Absolutely amazing... This place is a hidden gem.  The inside is not at all pretty but omg the tacos were so good! The manager or owner happed to be there and explained that the tortillas were non GMO the fish was wildcaught and the beef was grass fed.. besides those facts it tasted amazing and the portions were generous! Will definitely be coming back.

Name: Garage Cafe
Author: C S
Review:
Not open for the past couple weekends and weeks in between - everyone that populates 11th street miss our guy so much!!  He is a pillar of stability that supports the action taking place on the “Club Corridor”.

We’re all hoping he returns soon and in good health.

Joe is a treasure on 11th.  Every day I look to see if he’s opened back up - it’s been a rare day to see this place not taking business.  Truly, you never know watcha got til it’s gone 😥

We all hope to see you soon, and are sending the best wishes and loves and positive vibes. 🥰💜💝

Name: Garage Cafe
Author: 1John 2:27
Review: This place isn’t open though Google maps says it is on a Saturday at 3pm.

Name: Garage Cafe
Author: Pat Chesser
Review: Bagel pizza was great!  Prices on menus great too. Did I mention Military Discount

Name: Garage Cafe
Author: Emily Giglio
Review: AMAZING tacos for only $3.50! They give you generous portions of high quality meat and toppings. I highly recommend the fish tacos!

Name: Rose's Cafe
Author: OB1
Review: Yes great lunch experience.   Food was delicious and well balanced flavors.   Loved the pizza crust, very light and crispy.    We had the Salmon Cozy sandwich and the Mushroom Pizza.   Salmon sandwich was amazing and also with a very light bread.   Flavors abound. Recommended by the locals and they are right.

Name: Rose's Cafe
Author: Christine Mamuad
Review: Came here on a Sunday morning and ordered the smoked salmon, crème fraîche, and scrambled egg breakfast pizza and an arugula peach salad with ricotta salata. Food came out quickly, which was good. The rest of the experience... far from good. The very expensive pizza was offensively bland and boring, with a crust akin to a soft cracker, skimpy on the crème fraîche, and awkwardly arranged with a tiny pile of flavorless scrambled eggs in the middle. The arugula salad was uneven and, despite its well-meaning combination of ingredients, utterly unremarkable. We ordered a side of toast to put the smoked salmon and eggs on instead of the pizza crust, and it was marginally better, though the toast was partly burnt. Service was great, so it's too bad that what they serve their customers isn't. SF has so much great brunch food that I really can't recommend this place. Maybe it's better for dinner... I'm not going to find out.

Name: Rose's Cafe
Author: Tara Scherger
Review:
After plenty of research, we trekked across San Fran to dine here! The food was fabulous, outstanding to be fair, unfortunately the service was a disappoint. :(

Sad to say, the wait staff weren’t friendly at all.
We left feeling like we wouldn’t return. 🥺
Maybe others will have a better experience…

Name: Rose's Cafe
Author: jill abelson
Review: I love this place and go again and again. Great service, atmosphere and food is delicious. After a long day stopped in for a late lunch + do some work last week and got (1) my favorite waitress/server, so professional, attentive and efficient (2) favorite entree, the salmon cozy (3) delicious simple green salad, excellent vinaigrette (4) a warm frothy cafe au lait served in a bowl a la francaise. Next time will have (5) the hot apple cobbler the folks next to us ordered with a big dollop of vanilla ice cream on top. Roses is a gem.  Comfortable and never rushed. We locals love you.

Name: Rose's Cafe
Author: Rose Y
Review: What a great place to enjoy a delicious cup of coffee and freshly baked goods. From their oven baked pizza to their great verity of dishes, you’ll definitely find a new favorite. Although you may not see beautiful lighting and decoration, their great service and delicious food compensates for that. Our waitress was so attentive and pleasant. We will return to this restaurant in the near future.

Name: Cafe Uccello
Author: Drisana Moss
Review: I love the vibe! Great place for kids and adults. You can write on the walls and even watch TV. There’s a large room in back with tons of seating and even books. I liked my croissant with bacon egg and cheese. The berries were fresh. My iced egg coffee was really good too, definitely can’t taste the egg. It’s creamy. My husband had the mushroom burger he gave it a 6/10.

Name: Cafe Uccello
Author: Alexander Boulgakov
Review:
I love their lox bagel. It's reliably good every time. And, you see that little condiment thimble? That's really tasty hot sauce I think they make themselves.

Points off on atmosphere because the second, big room inside has a really boring and bland interior. UPDATE: They've decorated their larger room much better now since my original review.

Name: Cafe Uccello
Author: Taran K
Review:
Cafe Uccello is my little haven away from the chaos, where productivity meets pleasure. The vibe here is like a burst of energy, perfect for digging into work or just soaking in the moment.

Today, I indulged in the egg, cheese, and spinach croissant, and it was an absolute delight for my taste buds. The menu boasts a tempting array of options, ensuring there's something for every palate.

In essence, Cafe Uccello isn't just a coffee shop; it's a haven where the ambiance, service, and views converge to create a truly delightful escape. It's become more than a place to enjoy a meal—it's a comforting embrace on the busiest of days.

Name: Cafe Uccello
Author: Anuja Puranik
Review: Nice cute cafe in Oakland. They have cute coffees and bagel sandwiches. They also have omlettes, croissants and bakery items. Ambience is very cute and staff is friendly. I ordered rose cardamom latte and it was out of the world. They also give a nice red hot sauce with bagels. Definitely try this place if you are in town.

Name: Cafe Uccello
Author: Nayeli Davalos
Review:
Wow! This place is the GO TO place for breakfast and lunch. I usually come for their breakfast croissants and bagels which are super bomb and super filling. They don’t cheap out on their ingredients and fill it up. Their croissants and bagels are large not the tiny ones.

Today I had a steak sandwich and chipotle sauce and it was one of the best steak sandwiches I’ve tried in the Bay Area. I also had their iced latte. So Carmely and PERFECT to the taste buds.

Above anything, the ladies and gentleman that work here are extraordinarily nice. They go above and beyond to ensure their quality and kindness shows even during their busy times. Thank you Cafe Uccello for being open on time everyday for us Oaklanders. Definite worth the 5 stars.

Name: Cafe La Flore Irving
Author: Hawra
Review: Best Breakfast I ever had during my entire vacation to SF! The chef was very kind and welcoming. coffee was good, but the food was simply amazing and the fruit was incredibly fresh!

Name: Cafe La Flore Irving
Author: Arianna Greselin
Review: The sandwiches are always so good, fresh ingredients and really amazing combination of flavors. This time I tried the chicken salami and it was delicious, the sun dried pesto with avocado goes so well together, and the special aioli sauce is always great. The chicken breast and the fresh bread, everything so good!

Name: Cafe La Flore Irving
Author: Josue Pineda
Review: Really good. Staff were excellent, great service and food to bring friends and family. Very welcoming, and the food is delicious and impressive care for detail. Definitely recommend and I’m coming here daily before work. Perks: Charging outlets to work while enjoying a macchiato. There’s a lot of space and even has some tables on the upper level.

Name: Cafe La Flore Irving
Author: Will Chan
Review: Amazing! More than I expected. The portion is larger. The taste is fresh and tasty! We ordered 1 beef sandwich with salad. The host kindly offered to serve it on 2 plates for us. Did I mention the portion is large? We enjoyed it very much. Would come again!

Name: Cafe La Flore Irving
Author: Adam Boender
Review:
Really great service! We thought they had outdoor seating and brought our dog. Turns out they don’t, but they offered to move a table outside without us even asking. Really nice.
The food was great too. I think the breakfast wrap and the fruit platter were the most notable things we ordered.
The open faced breakfast sandwiches come with cream cheese which is not mentioned on the menu, now you know!
We are looking forward to going back.

Name: EA Cafe
Author: Hanna Kang
Review: We ordered the unagi bowl and beef teriyaki bowl and thoroughly enjoyed both. The portions were generous, and the prices were reasonable (about $18 each), especially considering the location near Oracle Park. The only downside is that they only offer takeout, but the food was delicious. For dessert, we shared a small cookie-n-cream ice cream, which was the perfect size for two and very tasty. We’re looking forward to coming back soon to try their poke bowls.

Name: EA Cafe
Author: Zu Zu
Review:
Small but cute. The batista made by far the best tasting Green Tea Latte I have ever had.

Mind you not a sit down place, but great for pick up!

The food looks delicious.

Name: EA Cafe
Author: Candice Curtin
Review: This is a take out only place that you need to try! Fantastic rice bowls and their ice cream sundaes are phenomenal. I got the strawberry which is filled with fresh strawberries and strawberry sauce. It looks like a tiny hole in the wall type place but their food is fresh and great quality. You won't regret eating here! Chicken teriyaki bowl with sushi rice and salad was fantastic. We've gone here twice now and both times were great.

Name: EA Cafe
Author: Zonnia Hernandez
Review: They sell amazing poke bowls to taste. After eating one you're already planning your next return,  which has been the same day for me. I ordered thesame thing, 6 hours apart, yet the bowls were soooo different.  The second bowl was lacking ingredients and overall disappointing compared to the phenomenal bowl they made earlier.   If the first one wasn't so good I wouldn't be so disappointed with the second one. I really do recommend them. I  just would not eat there twice in same day so you don't ruin it for yourself like I did. Picture is the first bowl they made. I didn't bother taking a picture of the second because it doesn't do them justice.

Name: EA Cafe
Author: Akil Iyer
Review: It's easy to miss from the street, but it's a pleasant, well-decorated space as soon as you walk in, and you'll be welcomed enthusiastically and genuinely! They've also got a great assortment of canned and bottled beverages beyond their own offerings (:

Name: Cafe Réveille
Author: Emily Chen
Review:
One of my favorite spots in Duboce! The food is pretty good - my favorite is the kale / mushroom bowl with chicken for lunch and their chicken pasta for dinner.

Great outdoors space. Friendly staff. WiFi only available during weekdays. It’s usually hard to find a spot to sit so I don’t usually plan to work or eat there unless I get there super early

Name: Cafe Réveille
Author: Bobby Miklaušić
Review: Wanted a healthy protein filled bowl. Got a healthy protein filled bowl. Great produce. Nice sharp dressing on the veggies. Delicious slightly spicy sauce on the poached eggs. Solid. And it's on really pretty, quiet street with a lovely outdoor dining situation.

Name: Cafe Réveille
Author: Gemma Boothroyd
Review:
Cute, buzzy spot with plenty of patio area to enjoy your coffee in the sun! Super friendly service, and high-quality espresso too.

Plenty of folks on their laptops - but no outlets here, as a forewarning!

Name: Cafe Réveille
Author: Michele Losse
Review: We went there twice for breakfast, as we were staying in the area. The first time, we ordered a white coffee and a latte. The girl taking the orders asked us what kind of coffee we wanted, they had a choice of two, I ended up with 2 coffees! When I queried it and asked for a refund for the extra, they couldn't do it, possibly because it's a UK card. The girl in charge couldn't do it either and as she was busy, said she would do it later. It would have been nice if she'd asked for some personal details to confirm this, I still don't know if the refund went through or if they even tried! The croissants were OK, not the best I ever had but not bad. The next day I had the coconut bowl which was weird, it was mostly frozen coconut, with a very light sprinkle of granola, a very light sprinkle of cocoa nibs and fresh fruit! Never had ice cream for breakfast before! At 15 dollars I thought it was well overpriced for what I got. My husband enjoyed his eggs and potatoes.

Name: Cafe Réveille
Author: Emma Dolcini
Review: We got an iced americano and a oat milk macha latte. The thing I love most about this cafe is that it’s open until 7:30pm. It’s busy in the morning and in the evening. Perfect place to come and do some work or relax after dinner.

Name: Enter The Cafe
Author: ADLbh
Review:
Great coffee shop in Chinatown. They have a lot of options for drinks and snacks.
Amazing avocado egg toast!

Name: Enter The Cafe
Author: P R
Review: Got a great breakfast here. I ordered the avocado toast with egg and fruits. Looked amazing and tasted very delicious. Perfect place to start the day! Very lovely and nice service, too.

Name: Enter The Cafe
Author: carol velasco
Review: I've found my new Favorite place to eat lunch, relax after lunch, patiently wait for friend, etc etc. Can order thru electronic kiosk or nice, helpful, young lady. ..So far I've tried 2 egg sandwiches & Purple Rice Yogurt-AWESOME-hear it Boosts Energy. I love that it's a relaxing corner café w wide windows to hear the world, but not be part of it.

Name: Enter The Cafe
Author: HaNg
Review: Great service. Really nice people and great food to match.

Name: Enter The Cafe
Author: Nico Gorbal
Review: The boba milk tea was alright, not the best not the worst. It’s basically a black tea+milk+boba. The stuff seemed pretty busy, took 15 min to get my tea.

Name: The Turn Cafe
Author: David Yang
Review: Ordered the chicken sando, watermelon/strawberry slush, and creme brulee. A little overpriced but overall pretty good, especially the watermelon/strawberry slush. The food arrived very quickly. Also, service was exceptional. The staff member at the register, Jorge, was very helpful and went beyond expectations to help me resolve issues with a food voucher. He was very helpful and very accomodating.

Name: The Turn Cafe
Author: Mercedees Edwards
Review: Very overpriced, but they have an area open to anyone with very comfortable seating. We had 3 hours to kill before we had to catch a bus home so we waited here and used the free wifi, and charging stations. The bathroom is downstairs and you have to ask the cafe employees for the key.

Name: The Turn Cafe
Author: Ari Delghetto (Goldii)
Review: Pinky and Christine are phenomenal bar tenders! They are charming and witty and deliver seamless service with the added bonus of the elegance of the building. Also Sephora is next door and is great to pitstop in for drinks. The happy hour menu is decent and they have mimosas in the morning. The Turnt chicken is so good. Go see the gals and treat them right. They're damn fine company

Name: The Turn Cafe
Author: Giacomo Luca
Review: Very nice atmosphere with a wonderful staff. However, many of the menu items here are way overpriced, especially the beverages.

Name: The Turn Cafe
Author: Iiona Scully
Review: I liked the chai latte, but everything else was awful. Very overpriced. Lasagna was the size of my palm and burger ordered medium came out burnt. Two stars for latte.

Name: The Crepe Cafe
Author: Joshua Brathwaite
Review: This place has amazing funnel fries and crepes! The Dulce De Leche crepe was so delicious and filling but presentation was really plain and basic. Prices are reasonable considering being at a tourist attraction! Good size portions and a variety of delicious food on the menu. Service was great and the facility was clean and welcoming.

Name: The Crepe Cafe
Author: Harry Canary
Review:
Found this great place first thing in the morning. No crowds, service was great, came out quick.

Went the savory route with a breakfast crepe of scrambled eggs and cheese. Very pleasantly surprised by how fresh and tasty it was. Also, hot sauce and sirracha was readily available for self-service.

This is a great alternative in the morning when Boudin Bakery is already crowded.

Name: The Crepe Cafe
Author: molloni molloni
Review:
While on family vacation, we wanted a quick meal when we arrived at the Bay.  The Crepe Cafe had no wait and it smelled good.
We each got dinner crepes.  The crab crepe was particularly delicious.
Then, before we headed out a few days later, we desired a fast breakfast... And crepes.  So we returned!
They use lots of strawberries in their crepes and plenty of fresh Nutella!
My only complaint was that i did ask for my dinner crepe to not have avocados in it, but they must have forgotten... If you like avocados, you'll have no problem here (it's not that I don't like them, i just would have preferred without).

Name: The Crepe Cafe
Author: Jerry Cortes
Review: Even though prices will sneak up on you, the food will be worth it. It is always fresh and made to order. Seating is a bit tight and even though there is outdoor seating, tables are not always set up

Name: The Crepe Cafe
Author: Sam Kazanci
Review:
We found this crepe place on our family trip to San Francisco, and it was amazing! They’ve got tons of different crepes and they all taste great. You could eat here for every meal, but the dessert crepes are the real stars.

The spot has indoor and outdoor seating, which was a nice touch. And it’s right in the wharf, so you can stop by while you’re sightseeing. Can’t recommend this place enough for anyone who loves good food. We’ll definitely be back next time we’re in town!

Name: Mudrakers Café
Author: j jones
Review: Had a craving for something sweet-carrot cake should due the trick. I asked the proprietor if it was today's and he said yes. It was the driest, stalest piece of cake I've ever had. On top of that, IT WAS $6!!!  When I mentioned this to the owner, he ignored me. When I left I was more specific, you told me that cake was from today but it was really stale. No, he said, it's only two days old-they brought it in 2 days ago. (Forgetting he told me it was from today AND he was claiming it was delivered on Sunday. ) Did I mention it was $6???

Name: Mudrakers Café
Author: C Abz
Review: Second time I've been here. Second time the matcha latte tasted like absolute sewer water. I order it the same everywhere I go and can tell when the batista didn't follow directions. Not worth the money.

Name: Mudrakers Café
Author: susan battle
Review: I was having my car repaired around the corner from the Cafe and asked the owner Amine, if I could camp out there and wait for my car to be finished. He could not have been more hospitable and kind. He prepared a cheese sandwich for me that wasn’t even on the menu. The coffee was great too. In addition, the entire care is clean as a whistle 😊. ThankYOU from the bottom of my heart, Amine.

Name: Mudrakers Café
Author: Anish Thakkar (Doc Thakkar)
Review: Great local place. Doesn’t pretend to be anything it isn’t. Owner / manager Hazem (or Hazim) is very kind and thoughtful. He is a worldly man with many interests and a big heart. I enjoyed my large latte, and my partner enjoyed her hot chocolate (and is a regular here). Would definitely recommend!

Name: Mudrakers Café
Author: Meggie Cansler
Review: I came from NYC needing a perfect place to have a coffee and stare at the streets. Kind owner and they had epic amounts of the New Yorker. It made my heart smile :) Thank you for making me feel at home with a wonderful espresso  and time with my husband.

Name: Café del Soul
Author: C S (CS)
Review:
Cafe del Soul: A Post-Hike Delight

After a day of hiking, my stomach was rumbling for something special. Luckily, I stumbled upon Cafe del Soul. The place was buzzing with a great vibe.

I dove into their famous Sunrise Wrap with tofu – and wow, it was a game-changer! The tofu was incredibly fresh, and I can only imagine how good it would be with chicken. To satisfy the little ones, we tried the Yummy Tummy Wrap, which was a hit!

The portions were surprisingly generous, so I couldn’t fit in anything else, but the rest of the menu looked equally tempting. If you’re in the area, do yourself a favor and check out Cafe del Soul. Your taste buds will thank you!

Name: Café del Soul
Author: Mackenzie Warnick
Review: Food was excellent, clean, and tasty. The service was nice and decently friendly. The organic options were very good! I especially loved the smoothie options.

Name: Café del Soul
Author: Lyssa Lou
Review: Nice little stop with great fresh food options. Customer service was also awesome!

Name: Café del Soul
Author: Emilie Kollock
Review:
Just really good clean food! not crazy busy like the rest of Mill Valley.
I always love coming here, no alcohol.
More of a cafe vibe.
I had the yogi tea iced, super yummy
and no caffeine, the nachos de Maria  were healthy and super delicious!
Ask for the hot sauce!
Just a simple and delicious! Also, great to take a wrap to the beach!

Name: Café del Soul
Author: Na To
Review: Great little place. The staff were very nice and the kids nachos and the smoothie we got were delicious. I got a huge migraine coming down from Muir beach on the winding road and they had really good yogi tea and black tea that helped me out (along with my Tylenol-Advil combo). If we’re visiting Muir beach again I would come back to eat here.

Name: Brioche Bakery & Cafe
Author: Paulo Lee
Review: What a cute little cafe! The smooth, lounge music really sets the mood and the food was excellent! I HIGHLY RECOMMEND the grilled turkey sandwich - it comes on a french roll that was soft on the inside with a slight crisp on the outside, the cubed turkey was super moist and flavorful, and it came with an awesome chipotle aioli that tied everything together. The accompanying salad was fresh, light, and not over-dressed. My partner's fine herbs omelet with apple cider sausage came with potatoes and fresh fruit. What a treat! Our waitress was extremely nice and attentive despite it being a surprisingly busy Monday morning. Definitely check it out if you are in the neighborhood!

Name: Brioche Bakery & Cafe
Author: Rafaela D
Review: The highlight of the place is the cute patio. Their oatmeal cookie is delicious too. Food wise it was just fine. Portions are a bit small compared to other cafes but make sense for the price. Service can be messy on busy days (like weekends)

Name: Brioche Bakery & Cafe
Author: Kimberly Abbott
Review: Excellent coffee, tomato soup, and pear salad. The lasagna was dry and flavorless, tasted like it was bought in the frozen section of the grocery store.

Name: Brioche Bakery & Cafe
Author: Alvin Shiu
Review: The backyard space is really nice but loud. Food is delicious and not too pricey. Large lattes

Name: Brioche Bakery & Cafe
Author: Bruna Oliveira
Review: Please please, the tomato soup is a whole something! Amazing atmosphere, staff very friendly. Not much quick to take your order, but nice!

Name: Heirloom Café
Author: Lauren Feldman
Review: This is the perfect neighborhood restaurant, but also so much more than a neighborhood restaurant. The vibe is incredible - perfectly lit for a cozy, romantic evening. The food is incredible and the wine list is unmatched in SF. Not to be missed!

Name: Heirloom Café
Author: Maxine Hamilton
Review: Just discovered this gem and was so impressed I almost don’t want to tell anyone. Every single thing we ordered was delicious and seemed prepared with such thought and care. Can’t wait to go back.

Name: Heirloom Café
Author: Alex Lambert
Review: It’s been 4 years since we last ate at Heirloom and it’s just as good if not better than before. This little charming place with its tall ceilings and old timey aesthetics reminds me of New Orleans. The menu is constantly changing to reflect the season and has something for everyone. The wine menu also has many wines by the glass and are generous pours. The presentation and preparation of the food was a delight and delicious. Service was also great and was quick to offer tastes of wines when I wasn’t sure about which one to order. The 3 course tasting was $100 with excellent wine pairing and could be ordered even if others were ordering a la cart. I’m always surprised Heirloom doesn’t receive more accolades, but sometimes it’s nice to have a gem like this keep its neighborhood feel.

Name: Heirloom Café
Author: Adriana Fabbro
Review: We came here for my birthday dinner last week. We felt well taken care of in a friendly, not overly fussy way. Even though we traveled from the east bay, it felt like our neighborhood spot. Stellar wine list with fair mark ups (rare these days). Seasonal menu. All the food was good, but the cod and the corn soup really stood out.

Name: Heirloom Café
Author: C E White
Review: Excellent service and food. Portions seemed larger than normal high end places. Neighborhood vibe. Noise level quite high. Great wine selection. Will make room for dessert next time.

Name: Crepe Cafe
Author: Craig J
Review:
Cool place and you can eat in the shade. But make sure you have a seat because they were hard to come by!

The crepes were perfect for our tastes. It is reasonable in price and the crepes are filling and worth it. Super tasty!

I ordered the ham and cheese and my wife ordered the turkey pesto. We enjoyed them both and ate them all.

I didn’t notice the seating until we had already ordered so many were taken. We sat on a bench in the shade and ordered. So, that being said, just grab you a table before you order or while you wait and you will be fine.

Great place to eat and we are glad we did!

P.S. Just outside on the dock we saw a few seals.

Name: Crepe Cafe
Author: Yulia Herasimovich
Review: The crepes are so delicious, with a great variety of choices. You can have coffee with any type of milk. The crepes are large, and the cooking is fast with self-service. If you sit outside, be ready for the birds.

Name: Crepe Cafe
Author: Laura Gottschalk
Review: We tried 4 different crepes: lemon, mixed berry, s’mores, and chicken ranch. They were ready within 10 minutes. All were tasty. Seating is all outdoors, in the shade, but somewhat limited.

Name: Crepe Cafe
Author: Kristy Rix
Review:
To be fair, I had some of the best funnel cake fries I've possibly ever had here. However the system is so confusing when ordering, and the staff aren't really helpful.

Anyway, super tasty, crispy, sugary - a must. Their crepes looked amazing but I was in the mood for these! Think they were $9 tax inclusive.

Name: Crepe Cafe
Author: Baffour Anim
Review:
Great place to grab a crepe with the family when you are in San Francisco while walking down the pier.

The staff was super friendly and helpful even though their menu is clear and descriptive my wife had to be sure of her choice of crepe.

It was extremely busy and even though the line was long it took less than 10 minutes to get my order.

Get the banana and strawberry crepe Nutella crepe. It’s the best. I highly recommend this place and I will be coming back for years.

Name: Rudy's Can't Fail Cafe
Author: Rashidi
Review:
Rudy's has never  failed me. Ever since my first visit at the old downtown Oakland location, to today.

I haven't been in a couple of years, decided to take my kids out to breakfast. Food was on point, eggs perfect, pancakes smacking, everybody's plate looked immaculate.

Definitely refreshing to get food and service on par with the price.

Name: Rudy's Can't Fail Cafe
Author: Mal Pinlac
Review: This place will satisfy your taste buds and your tummy. Lots of options offered on the menu and will definitely be back to try more items. Just the right vibe for a diner and staff are friendly and attentive. Street parking is so so depending what time you go but food is worth the experience for sure!

Name: Rudy's Can't Fail Cafe
Author: Fan Zhao
Review: Pretty good brunch/family restaurant. The fries are so good, they’re crispy outside and soft inside, and have a special food flavor. I guess it has to do with the oil the restaurant uses.

Name: Rudy's Can't Fail Cafe
Author: TIFFANY LEWIS
Review: First I came here ordered lunch was great this time I ordered breakfast not to good but overall nice cafe

Name: Rudy's Can't Fail Cafe
Author: Kristen Holness
Review: Great food, quick service and a cute atmosphere. I got the corned beef hash and eggs, pancakes, French toast, Popeye omelet. The only thing we could’ve passed on were the pancakes, they’re pretty average. Also, the fresh squeezed orange and grapefruit juices were delicious

Name: Cafe Bakery Restaurant
Author: Vanessa Cheung
Review:
I only discovered this restaurant because my mom brought a to-go order home. It was a Baked Chicken with Portuguese Sauce that left such an impression that I had to bookmark and visit in person.

We managed to stop in on a Saturday night, just before closing time at 8:00PM. The place was packed but we were able to get seated with 30 minutes to spare.

It's a no frills restaurant with traditional Hong Kong cafe options. Most meals come with soup, veggies, rice, AND dessert! Each dish is not 10/10 crazy good but it will surely leave you satisfied.

Name: Cafe Bakery Restaurant
Author: John
Review: Food is decent for a hefty price.  Service is bland.  One of the Asian women saw me and ignored me when I needed a tea refill.  Luckily for me, another lady assisted  me with the tea.

Name: Cafe Bakery Restaurant
Author: Fiona Seah
Review:
Decent food, value for money kind of place.  We had the Baked Chicken w. Portuguese sauce and then find it it's actually curry flavor - it's not too spicy, just didn't know the connection between the name and the item. :)

Revisited Sept 2024
Food is still as expected.  Great value for money.  This time we had the Sunday special - baked pork & fish cutlets+fried.  I surely wasn't expecting that amount of food!  Comes with soup, a hot drink (coffee or milk tea or lemon tea) and jello.

Name: Cafe Bakery Restaurant
Author: Shane Li
Review:
They serve traditional and authentic HK food here! I had ox tongue in tomato source with spaghetti noodle, which was exactly how I’d like it!  Super delish and really fix my craving for HK style food!

The service was very prompt, attentive and friendly! They brought my milk tea and soup within seconds and my entree was followed minutes later!

The restaurant is very clean, although the decor is bit dated (but expected)!

Name: Cafe Bakery Restaurant
Author: Bill Lai
Review: Great customer service and price to portion ratio is reasonable (for 3 adults sharing two dishes and came out satisfied).  Definitely remain a good place to satisfy our HK Style Cafe cravings.  Will be back for more!

Name: Ocean Beach Cafe
Author: Cédric Josz
Review: This place is a real gem. I come on a daily basis to sip on nonalcoholic chardonnay while enjoying homemade hummus after strolling on the beach. Simply exquisite. The staff is super passionate and friendly, which explains why every item on the menu tastes delicious, with high quality ingredients (avocado toast, vegan wrap, turkey sandwich with sourdough bread from local bakery, etc). Come let Josh or Teddy educate you on the huge variety of nonalcoholic drinks.. I'm still blown away by the rich flavors of their wine and beer as someone who no longer tolerates alcohol. I usually like to sit outside and indulge in a second glass of wine. I encourage you to do the same!

Name: Ocean Beach Cafe
Author: Chef Tony
Review:
I did not eat, but my cappuccino was great. Also, I love that they have real cups if you want to stay there and enjoy your coffee. What I saw of the food definitely looked g
reat. This place makes cocktails all non-alcoholic. I do want to go back and try one, seeing I've been sober for 10 1/2 years. So if your interested in a great vibe and want to have a non-alcoholic cocktail go and try them out. Plus they are super friendly.

Name: Ocean Beach Cafe
Author: Hae-Eun P.
Review: Beautiful little cafe with delicious menu options. Our group tried the turkey, beef, chicken sandwich, avocado toast and the Ocean Beach Salad.

Name: Ocean Beach Cafe
Author: H Reilly
Review: Avocado toast is a must!! They have a variety coffee, cocktails, and mocktails. Right near the beach which is nice if you’re looking to grab a warm drink for your walk.

Name: Ocean Beach Cafe
Author: Kristen Bear
Review: I love this cafe so much!! Really good sandwiches, cute bathroom, and delicious mocktails. Although these drinks are more than mocktails. Think adult, non-alcoholic beverages, adaptogenic ingredients, complex flavors, and interesting options. Such a cozy vibe, will definitely be back next time I’m in San Francisco.

Name: The Golden Bear Café
Author: Angel Torres
Review: They gave me a microwaved Philly cheesesteak like 17 dollar isn’t worth a warm slab of bread with meat nd cheese 🤦‍♂️

Name: The Golden Bear Café
Author: Jacob
Review:
Incredible hash browns! As a British person I was extremely impressed with the hash browns here.
The staff member that I spoken to was really helpful.
Unfortunately not a lot of vegan options.

Name: The Golden Bear Café
Author: unpriced potatoes
Review: GBC has really good cheeseburgers, but the customer service is terrible. Most of the employees here are unnecessarily rude to students, and I feel like it might be racially charged. I am berated unprovoked by the employees for one reason or another every time I go to gbc.

Name: The Golden Bear Café
Author: lillian jiang
Review:
I got the egg burrito which was good (8.5/10)
My friend got the ancient grains salmon bowl. The salmon from there was solid, not bad but could be better with some seasoning IMO. They rated the ancient grains bowl a 8/10.

The line wasn’t too long in the morning (10am) and it’s a convenient spot to grab breakfast or lunch on campus

Name: The Golden Bear Café
Author: Sid Shah
Review: Pretty good place in a convenient campus location, and it takes flex dollars and swipes. It's very full normally, but the drinks are pretty good.

Name: Kuma Cafe
Author: Mar R.
Review:
I love having Kuma in the neighborhood! They have a great variety of drinks, and everything I have tried has been delicious (and so cute-- they put a smile on my face on the sweltering hot days this summer). Before Kuma, I never really had fruit drinks at boba places but now I have done a total 180 on them. The fruit flavored popping and fresh juices are so refreshing!
I'm also glad that they have poke bowls and other snacks.
Bonus: Their market up the street is a great thing to have in town for getting more Japanese drinks and snacks. So glad to have them in Marin county!

Name: Kuma Cafe
Author: Eve Sanchez
Review: Cute store but it took 25 minutes for my drink to be made. Several people who ordered after me were served first. When it was made no name was called it just sat on the inner counter until I asked for it. Left in a hurry because I hadn’t expected getting boba to be a half hour endeavor. Sat down to finally drink my post-work treat and no boba which costs extra.

Name: Kuma Cafe
Author: Dumnoi
Review:
Kuma cafe, the new boba tea 🧋 place on 4th street, San Rafael. I have tried the Thai tea with boba, the brown sugar milk tea, the lychee, dragon fruit tea, the hibiscus tea with aloe vera jelly and everything was great! Oh, and my favorite is mango and strawberry kakigori (Japanese shaved ice dessert). The ice is so light and goes well with mango and strawberry. They also  have warm drinks if you don’t want to have cold stuff.

And just last week, I stopped by and they started to have poke bowl. I got some for me and ma family and they tasted fresh and yummy!

Kuma cafe is a great addition to downtown San Rafael.

Name: Kuma Cafe
Author: Tara Raine
Review: This place is incredible. All the drinks are made to perfection. The presentation of the drinks is also amazing, the perfect aesthetic drink. An incredible amount of variety, everything from shaved ice to coffee drinks. The staff is very nice and welcoming, making sure every customer is taken care of. They offer a good range of food as well, poke bowls, sweets, spam musubi (personal fav), and other small bites. They even heat up your small bites for you. Atmosphere is relaxing with small tables for two, relaxing music, and clean environment. Highly reccomend!

Name: Kuma Cafe
Author: Dennis Chavez
Review:
sooo i was just walking past here to get to another shop and then i see a boba shop that isn’t sharetea??? and that is really good ???? what a splendid surprise :,D
i got the matcha latte and it automatically has boba so you don’t have to tell them that you want boba >:) it tastes very fresh , i like i like

Name: Café Zeb
Address: University of California, Berkeley, 2745 Bancroft Way, Berkeley
Author: Tushant Mittal
Review: The perfect Mediterranean salad with fresh, quality ingredients. Large portions so that’s a plus.

Name: Café Zeb
Address: University of California, Berkeley, 2745 Bancroft Way, Berkeley
Author: Tt Onigiri
Review: So bad. They deleted my past review about the fact that they don't measure their chai flavoring, resulting in the most awful drink and coffee. Wish the law school would put in an independent cafe with edible food/beverages.

Name: Café Zeb
Address: University of California, Berkeley, 2745 Bancroft Way, Berkeley
Author: Nikhil Vohra
Review: I do my morning coffee runs here. Bring your own cup or thermos to save some money!

Name: Café Zeb
Address: University of California, Berkeley, 2745 Bancroft Way, Berkeley
Author: Wei Cui
Review:
A despicable loser of a restaurant.

We tried several Michelin reviewed restaurants in Italy during our recent trip. While this restaurant had some interesting-looking offerings that one cannot find in some other places that tourists stumble into, it falls far short of fine dining in terms of the quality of the food. Our dishes came out like microwaved buffet food, and we were surprised that it was what the owners seemed to take unusual pride in.

In terms of service, the hosts were simply obnoxious. When telling us about the dining options, the waiter insisted that he can answer all questions in English, and therefore the lack of a menu was not a problem. But when we subsequently asked him questions, he started pretending that he couldn't understand us.

I won't go into the remaining nasty details of our encounter. Suffice it to say that you've got plenty of other excellent options in Firenze (and the rest of Italy), so the restaurant's pretension that you have to have it their "Italian" way is a sorry farce.

Name: Café Zeb
Address: University of California, Berkeley, 2745 Bancroft Way, Berkeley
Author: Vamshi Balanaga
Review:
They don't have traditional coffees, they have coffee similar to Starbucks, if that's your cup of tea ;). Wouldn't recommend it if you want a really good coffee. There are better traditional Cafes not very far away.

Nonetheless, it's not bad.

Name: Moonlight Cafe
Author: bijay Gurung
Review:
1. Moonlight Cafe offers a delightful dining experience with its cozy ambiance and friendly service.
2. The delicious American cuisine at Moonlight Cafe is a true culinary delight for the taste buds.
3. From classic burgers to flavorful BBQ dishes, the menu at Moonlight Cafe showcases the best of American comfort food.
4. Overall, Moonlight Cafe is a must-visit for those seeking a mouthwatering meal in a welcoming setting.

Name: Moonlight Cafe
Author: Margie Mersky
Review: Incredibly fresh. Layers of flavors. Great little cafe with a wide selection of thoughtfully prepared food.

Name: Moonlight Cafe
Author: Sagar Patwardhan
Review:
Ate here twice in 3 days, food is delicious but best word to describe it is actually "balanced".  I ordered falafel plate and nepali chow mein w tofu.  Both dishes were perfect portion and good mix of carb + vegetable so when you finish eating you actually feel quite balanced and refreshed, it was unexpected.  Normally with noodle dish you might feel very heavy for example but this had perfect combo of noodles and variety of vegetables.

Owner Chandra is very kind, and vibe of place is completely chill.  Loved it and thank you Chandra :)

Name: Moonlight Cafe
Author: Cecelia. Gallagher
Review:
My husband I were greeted by a very friendly host. So far, so good.
We both  ordered blintzes.
When our order came out, there was one 4 inch blitz on each plate.
The crepe itself was undercooked and soggy.
There MAY have been a tablespoon of ricotta.
The crepe had an abundance of strawberry jelly INSIDE the blintz, not outside.
Not jam. Not preserves. It was kiddie style cheap jelly, 50's style.
There was a container of sour cream and another of absolutely tasteless apple sauce.
All yours for the better part of 13 dollars.
So far, so bad.
We will not be returning.

Name: Moonlight Cafe
Author: James Toy
Review: A friend referred for a quick and a great spot for a brunch.  Got there Friday around 10. Check the menu board and made my choice.  Guy at counter made a few suggestions to add to my breakfast vege burrito and great choice in coffees.  Super 👍.  Friendly and helpful. Will return..  Parking not too bad.

Name: Good Luck Cafe and Deli
Author: Pascual Carrasco
Review:
BACON EGG & CHEESE BAGEL.

From amazing bagel sandwiches to an endless variety of bubble tea and coffee to even coconut pudding, this cafe located in Chinatown SF is a must try when visiting. You can’t go wrong with Good Luck Cafe!

Name: Good Luck Cafe and Deli
Author: AG
Review: I got the Turkey sandwich (add bacon and avocado) on Dutch crunch and it was great. Tasted fresh and was prepared quickly. The people working (man up front and woman cooking) were friendly and helpful. I also got a strawberry boba latte; they were able to substitute the milk for almond milk which was fantastic. Super small inside with just a few places to sit but I’ve always loved places like that. Stop by and show them some love!

Name: Good Luck Cafe and Deli
Author: Ping
Review:
Hole in the wall small business with limited seating. Amazing sandwiches. Hands down the best tropical fruit tea I have ever had, it even had real passion fruit inside. Every item on the menu so far is amazing. The owners are very sweet and hardworking, but their English isn't great. They accept cash and Venmo. The full menu is only available in person but they are also available on DoorDash.

Eel Sandwich - Good quality eel with unagi sauce, tomatoes, pickles, lettuce, mayo, mustard, and red onion. I added on provolone cheese ($1.50) and avocado ($2) the total was $15. (which is very cheap since eel, especially sushi grade eel, is very expensive)

Smoked salmon sandwich - My go-to sandwich. The smoked salmon is not very salty and has an amazing texture. Much cheaper than your average smoked salmon bagel or sandwich for the quality and quantity.

BLT - Tried it with Dutch crunch bread, amazing. Lots and lots of bacon (I think around 8-10 pieces) compared to most sandwich places that put in 3-6 pieces (no picture because I forgot and ate it too fast)

Creme Brûlée Milk Tea - Sweet and really nice looking, tastes like the name suggests.

Red Bean Ice - Very cheap ($4 for a large size $3 for a small) just tastes like milk with condensed milk and red beans.

Red Guava soda - Like an upgraded version of a sparkling red guava drink.

Strawberry Yogurt - Strawberry flavored yogurt probiotic drink, can't go wrong with it.

Avocado smoothie - Just tastes like an avocado smoothie. It’s fresh and creamy.

Green Milk Tea - You expect regular green tea with milk from the name but it's actually Thai green milk tea which is the more floral and fruity version of regular green tea. A very niche flavor of tea that is pretty hard to find.

Brown sugar milk tea - Surprisingly unique taste, brown sugar milk tea is everywhere nowadays but this one tastes different, very good and a classic.

Tropical Fruit Tea - Very strong tea, you can tell they use good quality tea and brew it for a long time. It's so strong it feels drying if you get 50% sweetness or lower but the sweetness and fruit balance out the strong tea very well. Real fruit with a good amount (2-3 slices of apples, 2-3 orange slices, 2 strawberries and 1-2 tablespoons of passion fruit) definitely the best menu item from this place.

Brown Sugar Boba Milk Tea - Tastes more like Hokkaido milk tea with brown sugar. (win for me since I prefer Hokkaido milk tea over the classic brown sugar milk tea) Boba is warm and slightly more on the soft side.
