I recommend mixing the boba into your drink to cool it off if you like it more chewy.

Mango Soda - An upgraded version of mango sparkling water. It’s fruity and refreshing, can’t go wrong with it.

Champagne Milk Tea - I got it iced like everything else but I think it was meant to be a hot drink since the flavors were more mellow and fragrant. Still really good but will definitely be trying this hot and updating this review.
Update: The hot version was definitely the way to go.

Strawberry Milk Tea - Real strawberries inside. The tea taste isn’t as strong but the flavor is wonderful anyway. One of the best strawberry milk teas I’ve had.

Mango Milk Tea - Includes mango chunks in the drink. The mango complements the tea flavor well and the taste is very balanced. The best mango milk tea I’ve had so far.

Honey Citron Tea - Got it iced, very nostalgic. Preserved sweet citron pieces at the bottom. Very fragrant and nostalgic.

Thai Tea - A little above the average Thai tea but it’s not amazing. Good for someone looking for a solid milk tea at a low price point.

Jasmine Green Tea Cheezo - The foam topping is rich and creamy and the tea is strong and fragrant.

Most drink sweetness can be adjusted,  ranging from 0%, 25%, 50%, 75% and 100%. Their default sweetness is 100%.

Looking forward to trying their pudding and other sandwiches soon as well!

Name: Good Luck Cafe and Deli
Author: jasmine
Review: Best iced tea I ever had. The Tropical fruit punch tea was so amazing. The owner added real fruits. It was just amazing! Great value for money.

Name: Good Luck Cafe and Deli
Author: Joe G.
Review: Every time I’m in San Francisco I make it a point to get my custom Dutch crunch sandwich from Good Luck Cafe and it is SO DAMN DELICIOUS! They don’t have Dutch crunch in Seattle, where I live, so this sandwich always makes me happy, and they make it the best! Turkey, salami, bacon, cheddar, with lettuce, tomatoes, onions, pickles, mayo and mustard. It’s HEAVEN!

Name: Duboce Park Cafe
Author: Agustin Farias
Review: I pass by this cafe often while commuting by bike and finally decided to stop by and try it. Love the atmosphere. Sit outside and just watch all the pups with their humans playing by the park across the street. Food was nice!

Name: Duboce Park Cafe
Author: Christopher Chung
Review:
Solid neighborhood cafe, with approachable American breakfast-lunch menu — complete with espresso machine.

Sidewalk seating on tree-lined corner makes for a sense very similar to somewhere Northern Europe. Just across the street from Duboce Park, perfect for caffeinating before or after the dog park.

Breakfast Burrito, fillingly savory and a hearty start to the day.

Name: Duboce Park Cafe
Author: Emma Dolcini
Review: This cafe is right across the street from duboce park (hence the name). Service was quick and the drinks were delicious. The smoothie was super refreshing and tasty.

Name: Duboce Park Cafe
Author: Ana Sofía Vázquez
Review:
Would you look at that French toast? It tastes even better than it looks if you can believe it.

The place is nice, spacious, great for chatting with friends outside by the park or inside and maybe working a little.

The coffee is great too, strong. Only thing is that service might be a bit slow to get your order. However, once that happens the kitchen is really fast so it might just be a logistic thing.

Name: Duboce Park Cafe
Author: Steve Murdock
Review:
Duboce Park Cafe in San Francisco offers a refreshing blend of warmth, charm, and excellence in culinary delight that make it an enchanting oasis for families, especially for those with young children. The atmosphere brims with a sense of homely comfort, deftly pairing a quiet sophistication with a relaxed vibe that is perfect for a leisurely breakfast or lunch with your spouse and little one. Their coffee, undeniably a star attraction, is a masterful symphony of taste, always brewed to perfection, providing a soothing start or pick-me-up to any day.

Just across the way, the beautiful expanse of Duboce Park provides a wonderful backdrop, adding a natural grace to the overall ambiance of the cafe. The park is a fantastic place to enjoy a stroll after a satisfying meal or watch your little one enjoy their first sights of nature’s vibrant palette. In essence, Duboce Park Cafe has become more than just a culinary destination; it’s a place where family memories are brewed, and moments are savored just like the cafe’s unforgettable coffee. A visit here promises a well-rounded, satisfying experience, merging the joys of food, family, and nature into one delightful outing.

Name: Caffe Central
Author: Jemellee Manaoat
Review:
There’s a variety of breakfast items and snacks. They also have different types of sandwiches. When we came in we just wanted coffee and snacks. So we picked up a couple different cookies. I got the Sweet Sydney’s chocolate chip and Ginger Snap and cafe centrals own chocolate chip cookie. They were soft and chewy like how I like it. The coffee was nice and strong.

Service was fast and friendly. You could sit in and enjoy your treats or you can just order to go.

Ambiance, it’s a cafe: I like their light fixtures. So, there’s a counter where you order and a few tables. Nothing fancy. If you’re in the city and need a quick grab and go come here.

Name: Caffe Central
Author: Playmaker88x
Review: This place rocks! My wife and I came in here for breakfast around 9:30am on a Wednesday and it was very busy. We waited in line about 5-6 minutes to order at the counter then paid and took our seat. The coffees came to our table first, and then our food. Very efficient and timely service. We had a breakfast croissant, iced mocha, central latte, and 2 well shot shots, called “gut check”. All items were fresh and absolutely delicious! Great place in San Fran

Name: Caffe Central
Author: J T
Review:
This small but mighty cafe has many terrific breakfast options with lots of types of hot and cold coffees. I highly recommend the Lox Bagel. It's big, crunchy, and loaded with smoked salmon, avocado, and more. It also comes with a side of fresh fruit. The Gibraltar coffee was nice and strong. And the chocolate hazelnut croissant and cheese Danish were delicious.

There's a restroom at the end of the seating area. You can sit indoor or outdoor, but it was packed when I got there. I ordered to-go, and the coffee and food came out in just a few minutes.

It's not far from Union Square, so having this good breakfast with the beautiful weather outside made it a very enjoyable morning.

Name: Caffe Central
Author: Ivan Baradi
Review:
I decided to come here to eat since I’m hungry. I’m quite surprised that they have a good location despite the one at Westfield Valley Fair Mall since that was just a food vendor at a food court. I like how colorful the menu is.

I honestly thought that this is just a regular cafe, but it’s more than that. It’s the way that they prepared the food as if it’s a five-star restaurant. Food is really good and also like how they display the food from the display bars.

It’s definitely one of the go to places if you want to stop by and eat if you’re hungry. Highly recommended to try out this place!

Name: Caffe Central
Author: Isaiah Jackson
Review:
Really great, hearty breakfast and healthy choices. Omelettes, sandwiches, toasts, pancakes, etc. - there is something for everyone. They have juices and smoothies too. Or if you need baked goods, they have a great selection as well.

The line is long but service is incredible. They have a great thing going here.

Name: Lord Fairfix Cafe & Tap
Author: Jennifer Lutman
Review: Stopped by for lunch and the service was great. The Jerry Garcia sandwich had good flavor, crunchy yet soft bread, and a generous amount of avocado.

Name: Lord Fairfix Cafe & Tap
Author: Erw Business
Review: Thought I’d stop by this cute little café to grab a quick bite to eat. I ordered a pastrami sandwich, ham, sandwich, clam chowder, water, and Coke.  Glistening rainbow like the customer service.

Name: Lord Fairfix Cafe & Tap
Author: David Ghiringhelli
Review: Great cafe ! Nice salads and sandwiches. Mario is a great owner !

Name: Lord Fairfix Cafe & Tap
Author: Summer Lan Franco
Review: I love coming here because it’s spacious, they have friendly service, good baristas, and the atmosphere is relaxed and welcoming. Mario, who has been working here for 11 years, is a staple of the cafe. I love supporting the business and visit weekly.

Name: Lord Fairfix Cafe & Tap
Author: Tony Robinson
Review: Awesome place to get a bite to eat. Right in the heart of Fairfax.

Name: THE PLANT cafe organic
Author: Leigh and Ben Williams
Review: Great place for some pre-game drinks and food. Lots of Gluten Free options and vegan and other dietary needs. Covering GF items that normally wouldn’t be GF so great to indulge! Highly recommend the meatballs!

Name: THE PLANT cafe organic
Author: David Barwick
Review:
ORGANIC food menu!!

On a business trip from Portland and we always 2 a difficult time finding genuine organic restaurants and when we do, they aren’t usually open for dinner. THE PLANT has everything you need!

We had the unicorn tots and BLT avocado (bacon was tremendously delicious!!) and a green juice. The staff was helpful and genuinely very nice people.  Lots of space inside but their indoor courtyard was such a pleasant place to enjoy our food.

Highly recommend you ear here. We are coming back before we leave.

Name: THE PLANT cafe organic
Author: CJ Smith
Review: Very delicious and healthy food that’s served gourmet style at a very good price point. The dinning experience from start to finish was quite welcoming and everyone was super friendly and attentive. I ordered a tasty cafe mocha and their beautifully presented avocado toast (A work of Art) probably the best avocado toast I’ve ever had in my life. Well worth the visit and I’ll be back for more delicious healthy food that’s not over priced. Thanks for a great dinning experience. I love how you can buy any of the plants displayed inside. Reminds me of my home since I have plants everywhere.

Name: THE PLANT cafe organic
Author: Darryl Diptee
Review: Wonderful staff and ambiance. Great lattes. Loved the Latin music 🎶. Indoor and outdoor seating available. Wi-Fi available. Mimosas available for Sunday brunch as well ❤️ Food was delicious!

Name: THE PLANT cafe organic
Author: Maria Lee
Review: Lovely place for healthy, organic food. Great vibe here, and friendly staff. Met the owner Matt and he was very kind. I loved my chicken empanadas which were homemade and super yum and not dry at all. Highly recommend! Can’t wait to go back and try some of their other options. 😋

Name: Lou's Cafe
Author: Jonny Steven (JSTV)
Review: Great first time! Honestly really good quality and price. The chicken pesto was okay but the hot and spicy was my favorite 😍

Name: Lou's Cafe
Author: Dutty
Review: Guy at the counter was super friendly patient and helpful sandwhich was fresh the menu was broad many items were good will be back I just wish there were tables outside and inside but other than that good experience

Name: Lou's Cafe
Author: CS Desales
Review:
Sandwiches that cost less than $15 in overpriced San Francisco felt like a winning lotto ticket. The sandwiches was quite a pleasant surprise. Sweet tangy spicy secret sauce with roast beef and extra goodies inside the crunchy Dutch crunch.

Yummy!

The wait time….. that’s another story.

I wished them merry Christmas and a happy new year once I got my order.

Name: Lou's Cafe
Author: Ursula Blyth
Review: Stumbled upon this place right before exploring Golden Gate Park. So happy we did. Seriously the best sandwich of my life. Small establishment with amazing efficiency. Workers were getting killed but they kept smiling and making delicious sandwiches. We all got different sandwiches. Pesto chicken was amazing. My son devoured the buffalo chicken. I got a cranberry turkey that was mind blowing. It made the rose garden an even more special experience. SUPER affordable for the product received. Only regret is I don’t know when I’ll have this again. Colorado loves great sandwiches ;) Definitely recommend!!!

Name: Lou's Cafe
Author: David Huey
Review:
One of my go to spots for a wonderful sandwich.
From salads, to sandwiches, chips and soup… this spot has it all.
I lean towards the tuna salad or veggie-licious and it is consistently tasty, typically large and can easily be shared and at the end of the day, filling and satisfying.
You haven’t been here, make it a definite spot to visit. Enjoy!

Name: Copenhagen Bakery & Cafe
Author: Aryana Imani
Review: We come here every time we go to/from the airport. They have the best pastries! I love all their cakes, the banana surprise, cookies, and brownies. Sitting outside is so lovely, great people watching, and great service! Best grilled chicken sandwich I’ve ever had! And love their Denver omelette. 10/10

Name: Copenhagen Bakery & Cafe
Author: Elizabeth Ignizio
Review: yummy all the way very delicious everything . Today I got the very tasty  swiss cheese and mushroom omelet.It came with heavenly delicious breakfast potatos.and toast I also ordered a diet coke and to take home a slice of lemon cake and raspberry danish . I highly recommend this place

Name: Copenhagen Bakery & Cafe
Author: Praveen Rattan
Review:
Guadalupe was amazing and awesome!!! She helped me and my daughter in law order a wedding cake. We wanted the Princess Cake, and the sample was delicious. I'll write an update after the wedding. The entire process so far has been very smooth and easy.

As an update, they did an amazing job with the princess cake. The pictures don't do it justice. We had many reviews from all the guests.

Name: Copenhagen Bakery & Cafe
Author: Ahmad Lashgar
Review: Tried a couple pastries with tea. Very good. There is plenty of seating, including French tables outside.

Name: Copenhagen Bakery & Cafe
Author: Sarah R
Review: We had sweets here for my BDay. The customer service was fantastic! I didn't expect any kind of polite gesture, but it was given to me and my husband anyway. The cakes was so good. I love the pound cakes. Also, I love the buttercream they used on the cupcake.

Name: Mezzo
Author: Hanna Kang
Review:
The quality of the sandwich and salad was excellent, as always. I usually go for the half sandwich and half salad combo, and the portions are generous—I’ve never been able to finish it in one sitting. The ingredients are fresh, and there's a great selection of bread. My usual choice is honey wheat, but this time I tried the ciabatta. Unfortunately, I found it a bit dry and hard, so I'll likely stick to honey wheat next time.

I did have to take out a star due to the customer service. Some staff members were unnecessarily rude. One of them didn’t even acknowledge me and kept chitchatting with another staff member while I was talking to her. There was no "thank you" or "goodbye," which left a poor impression.

I'll probably return because the sandwiches are that good. However, there's definitely room for improvement in their customer service.

Name: Mezzo
Author: Steviejr
Review: A really nice place to have Breakfest the back side of the restaurant has a really nice area to sit down with a nice open canopy area to sit and eat. I ordered the Breakfest burrito and you can put a ton of stuff on it for 9 bucks. Overall it was pretty tasty the green sauce was really rich and all the ingredients were pretty flavorful and I liked the sausage and bacon. Highly recommend this place for brunch or breakfast.

Name: Mezzo
Author: Emily X
Review: Had a good brunch with my mom here. I ordered the Veggie Delight with homemade Greek yogurt green goddess dressing and the BLTA sandwich with beef chili. The portions are huge! The kombucha and pineapple agua fresca were refreshing. The outdoor patio was comfortable, which made up for the slightly lacking service.

Name: Mezzo
Author: Tara Tuchyner
Review: my dad introduced this place to me as it was a relatively frequent destination of his while in college and we’ve stopped here ever since i was little :) the veggie delight with extra poppyseed dressing is both of our go-to’s and it’s absolutely delicious. i’ve yet to been able to actually finish the entire thing in one sitting as the portion sizes are huge for the price. highly recommend mezzo, and just wish i lived in the area so i could stop by more often!

Name: Mezzo
Author: Sho Yoshimura
Review: Half sandwich was still very filling for me. Went with the chilli con carne soup combo. Had the BLT but should have added some sauce to the sandwich since it felt a bit bland.

Name: Cafecito
Author: Taeko Ichioka
Review: Tiny little hole in the wall cafe run by the sweetest staff. The tamales are INCREDIBLE. Right amount of spice, tender meat and so moist. I also had their breakfast sandwiches. MASSIVE! For SF, best bang for your buck. I stayed in the neighbourhood for 4 nights, went there twice - if I didn’t have to catch an early flight, probably would’ve been 3 times. Neighbourhood is sketch but…downtown. Everywhere is sketch

Name: Cafecito
Author: Sascha S.
Review:
We had breakfast in this small neighborhood place. My girlfriend likes her coffee strong, and we were definitely in the right place for that.
We both had the veggie quesadilla which was very delicious.
Don't sleep on the salsa!
Friendly people, good coffee, and reasonable prices made this a 5* experience for us

Name: Cafecito
Author: Nočnica Fee
Review: A great meal from the sweetest people. My breakfast burrito was hot, crispy and deliciously flavored. My latte smooth and rich. And again: kind personable staff. A little oasis.

Name: Cafecito
Author: Marco Gomes
Review: excellent sandwich and mango smoothie. i'm definitely coming back for more food.

Name: Cafecito
Author: Atasha Jordan
Review: I loved my breakfast from this restaurant. I got the breakfast burrito and asked for it spicy. The portion was huge and there was so much flavor in every bite. I also enjoyed the Mexican chocolate. The nice spiced hot chocolate was a refreshing yet indulgent drink.

Name: Cafe Encore: Coffee, Breakfast, and Lunch Delights
Author: Janna Glaze
Review: This tiny cafe is an absolute delight! The small bites, including fresh salads and tasty sandwiches, are perfect for a light meal. Their coffee is fantastic, especially the lattes, which are smooth and rich. The real treat, though, is their amazing pecan pie—it’s a must-try! Whether you’re stopping in for a snack or just grabbing a coffee, this cozy spot is sure to impress. Highly recommend!

Name: Cafe Encore: Coffee, Breakfast, and Lunch Delights
Author: Bobby Kariyatty
Review:
I walked in to grab a quick breakie and a cup of coffee before my San Francisco city tour (yes, im a tourist) only to see to witness two women tirelessly delivering on their coffees and sandwiches to patrons based on their orders.

It was just incredible to watch these two handling the cafe like pros. Smiling & welcoming everyone with a smile and also exchanging casual pleasantries with anyone who walks into the store.

As for the food I ordered, A club sandwich and a Ghirardelli milk, both were top notch!

Overall, a perfect place for your on-the-go breakfast for the day.

Name: Cafe Encore: Coffee, Breakfast, and Lunch Delights
Author: Rachel Simms
Review: I loved this cafe! I took my daughters and husband for breakfast and plan to return. The smoothies were light and delicious! The breakfast and bakery delights were amazing! I just wish it was bigger or had a staff member to clean tables and empty trash. I could have changed her trash bag for her as she was alone and handled everything on her own.

Name: Cafe Encore: Coffee, Breakfast, and Lunch Delights
Author: David Perrett
Review: Coffee ok, food also not bad at all. Quiet this morning but small and comfortable. Seems like a popular spot!

Name: Cafe Encore: Coffee, Breakfast, and Lunch Delights
Author: Melody Murphey
Review: Amazing place, delicious coffee and a killer breakfast sandwich. I had the salmon which was fairly standard with the notable inclusion of some delicious cucumbers that elevated it to the next level. I'll definitely be coming back 🤩

Name: The Social Study
Author: Adroit
Review:
I wanted to like this place but especially for a venue only open from 1-5pm for laptop use, the experience as a whole just fell below my expectations.

When I placed my order I waited over 15 minutes for something that was likely just scooped out from a pan, heated up, and came with a side of cream. This Strawberry Bread pudding tasted of mostly bread, lacked butter, possibly salt, made a barely noticeable custard, and the whipped topping served adjacent to it made it palatable considering I gassed $10 for it. Its more so as if they just forgot about me I dunno. The way the entire place is decked out in "Laptops prohibited after 5pm", the fact that all users were being shuttled out, the entire experience souring my liking of the joint instead of discouraging laptop use or better giving people a more relaxed cutoff, it felt extremely strict. Almost as a bad as having a strict parent taking away your device to go to bed.

My appetite spoiled at the price/quality of my bread pudding and the atmosphere so I took off at that 5pm cutoff.

Name: The Social Study
Author: Andrew H.
Review:
I was so disappointed because this place has been on my list for so long. :( Thought this would be a great place to study because it’s quite literally in the name but they don’t allow laptops after 5pm.

Pros
- super cute environment
- honey latte was so yummy
- fair pricing, latte was $5.75

Cons
- they couldn’t do hot beverages even though they’re on the menu?
- not super friendly service, I had to ask to pay and they didn’t say thank you ( I did though :))
- no sign on the door that laptops aren’t allowed after 5pm. There was a tiny sign on the table, but was blocked until some people moved. The place was probably about 20% full so who cares?
- it smelled like cigarettes inside🚬
- ceiling had tiles and dust falling off of it

Name: The Social Study
Author: felicia
Review: I wanted to like this place for various reasons: location, drinks, bites, music vibe, aesthetics and, of course, Prince on the wall! 💜 After today’s visit, I don’t know. The overall vibe today was, “why is this Black lady in here?” All while playing the music of mostly Black entertainers. I don’t understand. Yes, I was the only Black patron. 🤷🏾‍♀️

Name: The Social Study
Author: Brian Osweiler
Review: We stopped in for a drink on a Sunday afternoon.  Great spot, nice atmosphere and very reasonable drink prices.  The food looked good, but we were short on time.  Definitely worth a stop if you’re in the area.

Name: The Social Study
Author: Аlisa Paludeti
Review: So I was strolling around Japantown and stumbled upon this cafe. Since I needed a caffeine fix, I decided to give it a try and ordered a cappuccino. The vibe of the place was awesome and the ambiance was on point. Although the cafe was pretty packed on a Saturday afternoon, I managed to grab a cute little table for myself. They also have bigger tables for groups if you need them. I loved the vintage decor on the walls - books, skateboards, and vinyls - it was a feast for the eyes! It was so chill just hanging out there and people-watching. Definitely planning to come back with a friend next time.

Name: Shooting Star Cafe
Author: Hannah
Review: Came in at ~11pm and was seated immediately. The menu is huge and has so many meal, boba, and dessert options. Mango pudding was lukewarm and you have to pay $1.50 for the hot Taiwanese tea to be cold… But for the overall price, variety and speed I do recommend!

Name: Shooting Star Cafe
Author: Jacqueline Montiel
Review: Impressively fast service. Food got to us so fast even though they were fairly busy. Very reasonably priced for the large portion you are getting. Beautiful inside. Food was delicious and so many options to choose from. Definitely would come again and recommend it to anyone. Although 15% dine in charge is added, it's worth the fast service, portion, and atmosphere. Beef stew noodle soup was 10/10.

Name: Shooting Star Cafe
Author: Cup O' Noodles
Review: Honestly 10/10 place. It's open late at night, they have an EXTREMELY big menu so there's something for anyone. I had the wonton soup which was just kind of okay, improved by chili oil. But the Chinese sausage fried rice, literally the best in Oakland. The milk teas are served in these reusable cups that you can keep, and they were amazing. Prices are fair, hours are great, staff was nice, and the food came out quick. My favorite place in Chinatown right now!

Name: Shooting Star Cafe
Author: Suyapa Lainez
Review:
We arrived before 8 PM and were puzzled by the long wait for our orders. After getting tired of waiting, a waiter finally approached us and mentioned a minimum consumption of ten dollars. I agreed, but later realized it was ten dollars per person. We only wanted a hot drink after dinner, so it felt forced to order things we didn't really want.

The desserts were disappointing; my son disliked them. We requested condensed milk to improve the taste and showed the staff how unappetizing the desserts looked, but they seemed indifferent.

Moreover service was really bad, no even offered water, and tips/ gratuity is forced after 8pm. No coming bad.

Name: Shooting Star Cafe
Author: Jim Tan
Review: Great selection of Hong Kong styled breakfast and lunch. And the prices for most is half that of SF city. Take a Bart here and enjoy authentic HK food!

Name: Cafe Francisco
Author: Juan Carlos Rivera
Review:
I enjoyed my brief stay in this lovely Cafe.  The Owner is very friendly and it's nice to see him engage with locals and tourists. Really hard working and I liked the sandwich I had , "The San Fran", you must try it! It's about 6-8 minutes walk from Pier 39, I chose to come here to be away from the busy and touristy area. Definitely worth it, easy to locate, just by the corner, few seats outside, you won't miss it! I'll definitely come back
-JC from Italy/UK

Name: Cafe Francisco
Author: Shannon Lynch Dickinson
Review: I've been a fan of Cafe Francisco for over a decade and have never once been disappointed! Due to a number of factors (primarily the pandemic), I hadn't been back since summer 2019. Imagine my delight when the vibe, food, and friendly staff were just as fabulous as ever. Just slightly off the beaten path, between North Beach and Fisherman's Wharf, locals and tourists alike are treated like family here. The Francisco bagel with an iced latte is and always will be my fave, but I'm going to try the avocado toast next time!

Name: Cafe Francisco
Author: Logan Rosselli
Review: Simply delicious. Great coffee and the Francisco Bagel is the reason for my annual trip to SF. I can’t recommend this spot enough.

Name: Cafe Francisco
Author: Rachel Wilcox
Review:
Absolutely love Cafe Francisco! What a North Beach gem, simple food with fresh ingredients!

I always get an iced coffee and the francisco bagel that has schmear, tomatoes, red onion, fresh basil, and what really makes it great is the olive oil and capers. Owner is very friendly and the cafe is a staple to the North Beach community! Dog friendly spot too :)

Name: Cafe Francisco
Author: Collin Street
Review: I don't usually leave reviews, or take pictures of my food, but I had to capture the essence of my experience. I really wanted to like this place. I paid $32 for a frittata and an iced latte, including tip. The frittata came recommended by the owner himself, and was bland, included 3 slices of avocado, a pathetic strand of red onion, 1 slice of tomato, packaged butter and smuckers jelly and a pack of tapatio (???), served on a paper plate on wax paper which ripped, with plastic utensils - for $19. I left it as shown and had oatmeal at home. 2 stars only for the atmosphere.

Name: Dolores Park Cafe
Author: Luis Capobianco
Review:
The smell when you walk in front invites you to go in and order.
I had the Avocado Toast and added the bacon because the smell of bacon was so inviting…
The best bacon I had in a long time.
I also tasted the Red Eye smoothie, and is delicious.
Great place, good food, friendly staff, and not pretentious.

Name: Dolores Park Cafe
Author: Krishin Asnani
Review: Outstanding design : feels like we are in the living room of a friendly and spacious house ; views of the park and courts gives it a sense of a community; refreshing homemade lemonade. Server and barista are nice . I will have to order sandwich or salad here.

Name: Dolores Park Cafe
Author: Joshua Brathwaite
Review: Great service and a great place to get a quick bite to eat. Avocado toast was light and delicious with a Chai Crush smoothie. Menu is full of good eats so I will be returning to try other items on the menu. The place is clean and giant windows open which is perfect on a nice sunny day at Dolores Park.

Name: Dolores Park Cafe
Author: dreamynation
Review: Typical cafe. I got the breakfast burrito it was really good came with fruits. My friend had a hot chicken chiptole sandwich (don't remember the actual name), she didn't like it and it wasn't hot or tasted like chipotle to her. The lavender lemonade green tea kombucha had a super interesting taste. If you don't like kombucha I do not recommend trying it at all you won't enjoy it.

Name: Dolores Park Cafe
Author: Mitchell Hanson
Review: Ordered a DBL espresso (which wasn’t amazing or special) but the gluten free nutter butter was great! Creamy butter cream between two soft crumbly cookies. Impressed by the taste and price of spring $3. Nice place to sit inside/outside at near Dolores Park. Service was quick too. I’d say go here out of convenience, not necessarily as a destination while visiting.

Name: Cafe New Honolulu
Author: Jonathon Paul
Review:
Good porridge, ok Hong Kong milk tea. Excellent fried rice varieties.
Few to none healthy or vegan options. Service was painfully slow.

Name: Cafe New Honolulu
Author: Wilson Tsang
Review:
TLDR: solid food at reasonable price

I ordered a fish and gravy rice and a HK milk tea and the bill came out to just over $15. The HK milk tea was strong and flavorful and fragrant. The fish and gravy rice came out quickly as well and while tasty, would be nice to have some vegetables with it as it only had fish and tofu. Adding some broccoli or something else would help bring some freshness.

Name: Cafe New Honolulu
Author: Junhyun Kim
Review: It was an authentic Hong Kong restaurant. Clean, quiet, and most importantly, delicious. I just took rice with steamed pork, and this simple cuisine tasted amazing. The rice was cooked perfectly, and the amount of rice they gave is more than enough to fill one's stomach. Price was also reasonable. Highly recommend.

Name: Cafe New Honolulu
Author: Derek Wong
Review:
September 27, 2024- Friday.

I came here on a Friday afternoon for lunch and got the Beef Chow Fun in Dry Style. Unfortunately, it wasn't really good- one of the worst Beef Chow Fun I've ever had. Usually this dish comes oily which makes up for the flavor, however it was super dry. Service was okay and food did come up in a timely manner.

Name: Cafe New Honolulu
Author: Vicky Vuong
Review:
Tasty food for affordable price that hits the spot when your looking for fried rice and noodles. Your basic no frills Hong Kong style cafe that serves stir fry noodles, congee, cheung fun, coffee and milk tea etc.

Only one waitress for the entire restaurant, she was nice albeit, poor lady running around the whole time.

Food came out in reasonable amount of time.

Name: Réveille Coffee Co.
Author: Darshan Gajara
Review:
Delicious coffee & breakfast. The avocado toast was a little hard to cut, but it tasted good nevertheless.

The only inconvenient part was the designer chairs. They looked good but didn't have a backrest and were quite uncomfortable.

Name: Réveille Coffee Co.
Author: Kristin P Ho
Review: I can’t speak to the coffee but the food is good - really enjoyed the breakfast bowl and crunchy bowl (lunch) with added eggs. More flavorful than it looks.

Name: Réveille Coffee Co.
Author: Charmie Kapoor
Review:
I've tried avocado toast in about 20 different places in SF and this would be in the top 3.

Good coffee, good toast.

They gave us a take out even though we mentioned dining in.

Name: Réveille Coffee Co.
Author: Marina
Review: You have to try their Acai bowl, which has GF granola and fruits. Their matcha chia pudding is really good too. Their coffee is a no-brainer.

Name: Réveille Coffee Co.
Author: Ieva Dekaminavičiūtė
Review:
There is one guy cooking in the kitchen and kudos to him! I have been here a few times and in food and coffee we can trust.

Perfectly poached egg, well seasoned, elevated simple perfection!

Name: Sutter Street Cafe
Author: Marvin Ford
Review: This is literally a hole in a wall cafe. Delicious, quick, and warm! Best of all, it’s affordable! The entrance is a garage ramp, and is easy to miss. I posted pictures of the ramp so that it can be better spotted! Will def come back!

Name: Sutter Street Cafe
Author: Tanyaporn Tamrongterakul
Review: Small little deli inside of 450 Sutter building. You wouldn't spot them from the street side. Entrance is through the building's front door or the garage entrance. Since it's inside, it's a little quieter than street-side cafes. A couple of seats for dine-in. Great service. Awesome sandwich. Fresh, not soggy with sauces. Bread is soft and crispy toasted, which is the type that I like. Good strong coffee. Great place to meet up for coffee or grab a quick lunch.

Name: Sutter Street Cafe
Author: Allie Cauthorn
Review: The reviews did not disappoint! I got the Spicy S.W. Chicken on a sourdough roll, no pictures because I ate it too fast! Customer service is 10/10 and even though the deli is tiny, it feels very cozy and I would have been happy to eat there. Lots of snacks/candies available for purchase, too!

Name: Sutter Street Cafe
Author: Casey Swain
Review: A "hole in the wall" but the food was great. It is also known as the Sutter Street Deli! That is good information to know when asking around for it. Check out my pictures. I had the sourdough breakfast sandwich and the people there were very friendly. I would go there again for any meal and even snacks!

Name: Sutter Street Cafe
Author: Natasha G
Review: Delicious and an absolute gem! I had an incredible breakfast and we went back to get sandwiches to go. This is by far the best place we've eaten at so far in San Fran. It's a hidden gem, you need to enter a large building and follow signs for the deli. Service was awesome.

Name: Le Cafe du Soleil
Author: Dmytro Buldakov
Review:
The cafe offer a gorgeous vibe of a French bistro combined with picturesque view to San Francisco Victorian homes and accompanied by absolutely delicious coffee and food.

We had some pastry which name I fail to remember, it was buttery, raspberry, and fluffy; salmon quiche (I would add some feta to it, btw), lemon eclair, and late. And I do recommended every single item of that list.

Name: Le Cafe du Soleil
Author: Terrell Marshall
Review: Cafe du Soleil was a great spot for breakfast! We tried the vanilla matcha latte and the French toast, and both were fantastic! The French toast was well presented, topped with fresh strawberries and bananas, and surrounded by a delicious berry sauce. It was perfectly fluffy and not too sweet. The vanilla matcha latte was creamy and refreshing, a great complement to the meal. The café had a cozy, laid-back vibe, perfect for relaxing with a book or catching up with friends. Highly recommend if you're looking for a great start your day

Name: Le Cafe du Soleil
Author: Emily Chen
Review:
My favorite cafe in Duboce! Clean, friendly staff, the food is delicious, the coffee is fresh, and the ambience feels local / friendly. Lots of natural light comes into the cafe

I got the bacon cheese toast with avocado and it was so good! Side of easy over eggs were cooked perfectly. WiFi available

Name: Le Cafe du Soleil
Author: George Montgomery
Review: The owner is absolutely amazing the culinary art qualities that he has is top-notch. They are Really down to earth friendly people. I plan on taking my dates there for now on. breakfast lunch or dinner they got it going on like donkey Kong.LOL.

Name: Le Cafe du Soleil
Author: Darla D
Review: Absolutely delicious. I had the bacon quiche with greens and my gf had the French toast. The latte was so fresh and one of the best coffees I’ve had in a while.

Name: BeanStalk Cafe
Author: Max Davis
Review: Nice and casual place with delicious sandwiches, bagels and coffee and...! We went there every day for breakfast during our stay to avoid the bad and overpriced food in the hotel. If there was a place like that near us, I would go there regularly. Great! The interesting sandwich combinations are worth mentioning, e.g. Funky Town or Cranberry Turkey. 😋

Name: BeanStalk Cafe
Author: Barbara C
Review: They have the BEST sandwiches! Amazing fresh bread. My fav sandwich is egg salad-see pic YUMMY!

Name: BeanStalk Cafe
Author: William Dawson
Review: The best ciabatta egg sandwich I have had ever. Large selection of coffees. My Latte was perfect.

Name: BeanStalk Cafe
Author: Kristin E Anthony
Review: What an enjoyable breakfast and coffee spot. So quaint and the best part was that they keep everything so simple yet so delicious. The person working the counter (woman) was absolutely a joy. So kind and she really made my morning! It's the type of place you dream about sitting in on a cold morning, enjoying hot coffee and reading the news. Also, get the lox bagel, trust me!

Name: BeanStalk Cafe
Author: Maria Fernanda Wetzel
Review: Great place for breakfast. We got two items from their specials, funky town and veggie bagel. Both really tasty and big, consider sharing. My daughter got the cragel sando and loved it. Cappucino, hot chocolate and pour coffee were excellent. Stop by if you are hungry!

Name: Simple One Cafe
Author: Ellen Hung
Review: Really good baked meat sauce spaghetti and chicken a la king baked rice. Their set means come with a small milk tea boba, which you can upgrade to coconut mango sago for $3.75. The food is really good and drinks delicious. We got there a little after 6 on Saturday and it got super busy after with so many to go orders.

Name: Simple One Cafe
Author: Dina Harris
Review: Although the food has good flavor, I was SO disappointed with my last purchase. I got the fried chicken w mushrooms to go. When I got home, I had gotten a big piece of skin w only 3 small pieces of meat attached. I was too hungry to go back. Bad form Simple One!

Name: Simple One Cafe
Author: Lynn Eglan
Review:
It's been a while since I was last here, but my has their menu grown since they first opened.

The place still looks the same, but the difference is that you place the order at the register now instead of them taking your order. It wasn't super busy, but the person who works there didn't seem too happy to help me either.

I went here because I was craving the fried buns with condensed milk, and it was just like I remembered. Crispy on the outside with a balanced fluffy and doughiness on the inside.

Peanut butter toast with condensed milk did not taste the same. It had a hint of coconut taste to it that I wasn't a fan of and lacked a creaminess to it.

Waffles (hk style)- i got it because I was curious. I mean, you really can't go wrong with waffles. I was wrong.  This had the same filling as my toast, and the filling was mainly on one side. They made it into a sandwich, which left me with wanting something more, and this fell short.

French fries - surprisingly tasty and crunchy.

Popcorn chicken - crunchy,  flavorful,  and moist on the inside

Mango matcha latte - This was good, but I was expecting like fresh mango chunks or something.  Instead, it was a puree smeared around the cup. I was also a little disappointed they don't have the cups anymore that have their logo on it.

Name: Simple One Cafe
Author: Lee Felder
Review:
Nice place to enjoy a simple meal with milk tea with or without boba.   The combo meals are tasty, decently price in  just the right amount.

Baked pork chop over rice was good, though the rice was not hot enough.  Fried chicken wings are nicely crunchy on the outside and juicy and tender inside.

I would skip the tofu red bean dessert though. The tofu tasted of the plastic container it was stored in.

Boba was just the right texture and flavor. Tea was nice and strong. It was a little too sweet and a bit too much ice so I’ll ask for less of both next time.

The staff were all very friendly and patient with our questions.

I would definitely come back again.

Name: Simple One Cafe
Author: Ron Lui
Review: Stopped in to try the bingsu.  Ordered mixed fruit chocolate bingsu, strawberry sage and lychee sago drinks.  They top the drinks with fresh fruit, nice touch.  Both drinks were good and refreshing.  The bingsu was a healthy portion size.  Smooth chocolate shaved ice and fresh fruit to top.  I think my son ate least a quarter of it himself, it was smooth and not Icy.  Good refreshing dessert.

Name: Paradise Park Cafe
Author: Mina Khan
Review: Super cute cafe, got the chipotle sandwich and a cold brew coffee. There’s a wall full of stickers which I find so cute, gives you something interesting to look at. The staff is very friendly & the music is always good vibes ✨

Name: Paradise Park Cafe
Author: Cynthia J. Roman Cabrera
Review: Cute little spot with absolutely delicious breakfast options. I wish I’d live here to have this be my regular weekend treat. I absolutely bodied my burrito. It was perfectly cooked and the tomatoes were a delight. Also don’t sleep on the baked goods! Excellent when toasted =) overall VERY good spot

Name: Paradise Park Cafe
Author: Petiri Munyikwa
Review: Went to Paradise Park Cafe this morning hoping to have some brunch fare n neighborhood vibes. The service was great. Vibes were off…mostly just laptopers and door dashers. I got the French toast and my partner got the breakfast burrito. French toast was phenomenal! The breakfast burrito not at all. It was dripping wet, and yet still barely together. It felt as if it was frozen the night before, slapped on the grill to hear and put on a plate. I am currently listening to my partner throw it up in the bathroom. I bet the salads and sandwiches are great BUT STAY AWAY FROM THE BREAKFAST BURRITO!!! Overpriced but what isn’t these days.

Name: Paradise Park Cafe
Author: Jean Villabona
Review:
Went to get an avocado toast today and came out with this …11$+tax for this….what happen with the
quality paradise park cafe? I honestly want to take it back but I am far now…wish I would’ve taken a look before leaving….

Name: Paradise Park Cafe
Author: Chloe Martins
Review:
Huge Meh!

We were disappointed by an establishment near by and wanted to take shelter at a place that had vegan options.

The menu was confusing because there would be a vegan/veg symbol but had meat on it? Like what?

The ban mi had the wrong type of bread.

Avocado toast, more like avocado basic bread.

The breakfast burrito average.

The tot dish, greasy and mediocre.

Our coffee drinks were meh.

Staff was friendly.

They only had one bathroom for customers and someone was taking a long poo. He didn't even buy anything from the place so just a free loader bathroom user.

Overall, meh. Will not be going back.

Name: Café La Bohème
Author: Chelle Culp-Vargas
Review:
Bohemian place with chill vibe. Blended Mochas and baklava!
Extensive menu with salad, bagels, Sandwiches, soups, coffees, tea, and alcoholic beverages.

Name: Café La Bohème
Author: Matt Savage
Review: Cafe La Boheme is a Mission District institution .  It long predates the Mission's  gentrification and was serving "gourmet" coffee before it was a thing.  It continues to have a great vibe, still serves great coffee and food, and  is a great place to sit and think or work on laptop.   The hummus plate is my favorite dish, but there are many other great choices. I am a decades-long customer, and I hope it never closes!

Name: Café La Bohème
Author: David Samuelson
Review:
Weirdly small portions for how expensive it is. Guy who runs it seems like he is reslly bitter and burned by the world. Pretty empty yet has one hour long WiFi pass.

If is cute and there is sort of a fun old-timey alcoholic vibe that I was into. But the prices and the guys’ energy. No bueno.

Name: Café La Bohème
Author: Zaykeese Riley
Review: I have never had better tea in my life. His secret lavender blend is legendary. As someone who has served in the armed forces this is TRULY Authentic!!!! Must go!!!!!

Name: Café La Bohème
Author: Russ Lewis
Review: Had the egg cheese bacon croissant, and the locks bagel with a fruit smoothie and just a cold carbonated water. Interesting place. The food was very good. Worth visiting.

Name: Cafe Zoetrope
Author: R Dohe
Review: Excellent meal in a historic building. My friend had the spaghetti and meatballs and said it was delicious. I had a Caesar salad and a caprese sandwich. The salad especially was amazing. Excellent service from a lovely server. Cool atmosphere. Overall, a fun find!

Name: Cafe Zoetrope
Author: Petya Dimitrova
Review: I loved my little lunch there so very much!!! I totally trusted the waiter with his suggestions for wine and pizza and didn't regret it at all. Had a glass of Primitivo and Prosciutto pizza. Delicious!!!!!! The pizza crust was the best I ever had. The staff is super friendly. They charged my phone and as locals, suggested places to visit through my stay in SF. I liked the vibes of the place. From the pictures and posters on the walls, you can see many memories were made there. Love the place and would definitely visit again when back in San Francisco!!!

Name: Cafe Zoetrope
Author: Joshua Brathwaite
Review: Overall experience was good. The amount of pasta didn’t reflect the price but the linguine with clams was delicious and the meatballs made from veal were delicious as well. We sat outside to enjoy the nice weather and service was also great as our waiter scooped spoons of Parmesan cheese to our liking on our plate. The Italian soda and complimentary rolls were good also.

Name: Cafe Zoetrope
Author: Konrad Juengling
Review:
Cafe Zoetrope is a cute Italian restaurant in Little Italy in a gorgeous building on the ground floor. The big draw for us to make reservations was the short story vending machine, which is free.

For the vending machine, you can select a story in 1 minute, 3 minute, or 5 minute read times. It prints on thick receipt-sized paper (think a thick extra-long Walgreens receipt). You get a randomized short story and it’s a cool experience.

The food isn’t anything exciting. It was decent, but nothing memorable. I had the Linguine Alle Vongole, which was fresh clams, white wine, parsley, and red pepper flakes. I added more pepper, red pepper flakes, and salt because it wasn’t very flavorful. For dessert, I had the chocolate mousse, which was more firm than I’m used to, but it wasn’t anything I’d write home about.

Our server was attentive, friendly, and knew the menu well. After the short story vending machine, the service was the best part of the experience with the food coming in last.

Name: Cafe Zoetrope
Author: Keri Ferguson
Review:
Tired Midwesterner from a full day of travel. We.. just..  need.. pasta! Google Maps + Francis Ford Coppola ("Godfather") + his wines + unique old building = love. It was "dead" at 1830 local time, so perfect for us to get the best seat in the house!

Waitress recommended the wine tasting ($23) and really, all 3 were good.

Fennel salad was fresh ingredients and refreshing, but needed a smidge of salt.

The pasta.... the pasta! Puttanesca! The pasta was chewy al dente with a hearty tomato sauce (more like a jam) filled with whole roasted garlic gloves (easy to see if you want to eat them first! or remove - boo), calamata olives, and capers!

So good: food, ambiance, wine, and how they treated us (gave us bday champagne and let us linger a little).

Name: Fiddle Fig Cafe & Coffee
Author: Kristina Femal
Review: Went here on a beautiful Saturday afternoon and ordered an iced oat milk latte and veggie sandwich without cheese. I asked the staff and they confirmed that it was vegan when ordered that way, which is always appreciated! The latte was very smokey, which I personally don't like, and the sandwich was on the dry side. The mixture of roasted vegetables was yummy so if I went here again I would order the sandwich with extra pesto to make up for the cheese removal. Almost every table was taken by someone on their laptop and the table I did choose was unstable and kept moving while eating.

Name: Fiddle Fig Cafe & Coffee
Author: Amanda Dalton
Review: Found this place on a whim all thanks to the beautiful plants that captivated me and my husband! A cute spot for a fast bite and coffee, or nice to just chill here. If you’re a plant lover, you’ll appreciate this spot! It’s also nice they have local SF artists items for sale. The workers were all a delight and very helpful!

Name: Fiddle Fig Cafe & Coffee
Author: Ganisha De Silva
Review:
It was a very nice little cafe with a nice atmosphere. But the food we ordered were too dry. We ordered an avocado toast and a tuna sandwich. Both the bread were dry and less tasty. Paid $2.7 extra for the eggs on the avocado toast but the eggs felt old or the mixture was pre made and tasted below average. Expected the sandwiches to be warm but both were cold by the time they were served.
Coffee tasted like made out of overly roasted coffee beans which I didn’t like personally.
Overall I wouldn’t visit there again, comparing the prices with the surrounding cafes it’s a bit expensive and the quality is not very great.

Name: Fiddle Fig Cafe & Coffee
Author: Toni Sterling
Review: Walked in and was able to place my order right away. Our food and drink came out pretty fast. The breakfast sandwich was good. Plenty of indoor seating.

Name: Fiddle Fig Cafe & Coffee
Author: Charmaine A
Review:
We were there on a Monday morning before 9am. Dined in and left just before 10am where the cafe started to get crowded. Gotta love the self order tablet but the staff can get your order if you’re not tech savvy.

I had an avo toast + add on smoked salmon and it was yummy! Latte was decent but I’d on another shot the next time.

Would def recommend for a no frills breakfast!

Name: Pho Cafe
Author: Amanda C. Sun
Review: Came here on a Saturday night for a pho fix with my husband around 6:30-7pm. It wasn’t full or empty, so we got seated right away. There’s a tv in our nook to watch football so my husband appreciated it. We had cha goi (fried egg rolls), pho, and garlic noodles with chicken. The fried rolls were good as it came with noodles and veggies to wrap with. The pho was also solid, but the garlic noodles disappointed me a lot. The noodles were all stuck together and not in separate strands, and overcooked as it was very soft and mushy. Chicken lacked flavor as well :( better to stick to other dishes next time but service was friendly, though. Around $20 per person for the three dishes.

Name: Pho Cafe
Author: Rosita Lesmana
Review:
Ambience was nice and the employees were all really nice! However, the dishes took a really long time to come.
Crab meat & tomato soup: the soup was absolutely bland. Have you ever tried putting tomatoes on boiled water? Yeah, it tasted exactly like that. The tomato wasn't even boiled/cooked. It looks like it's fresh from the garden/market and dumped into the bowl after they pour the hot water/"broth".
Grilled pork chop noodles soup: opposite of the tomato soup, this is too salty/sweet not in a good way. I think tasted more like instant pho seasoning that you can get in asian stores. The pork chop on the other hand, is more in the bland side but the seasoning also tasted off.

I'll give them points for the portion though. For small bowls and their price, they are huge and have a lot of noodles.

Name: Pho Cafe
Author: Max G
Review: Delicious! We were staying in Pacifica for vacation and ended up getting take-out from here twice. The Five Spice Chicken pho is a tasty broth served with fresh cooked chicken. The egg rolls are some of the best we’ve ever had! The staff was very friendly and welcoming too. Highly recommend.

Name: Pho Cafe
Author: L Chew
Review: Interesting place and a little bit surprise finding Laksa on a Vietnamese menu. Every thing was satisfying but nothing really stood out.  Like this chilly evening I would not hesitate getting warm up with a bowl of Pho.

Name: Pho Cafe
Author: Nicole Leary
Review: Best banh mi I’ve ever had! Must stop, will be returning immediately. The meat was excellently marinated and grilled. The bread was fresh and soft yet still maintained a little crisp on top. Filled with the perfect ratio and combo of cilantro, pickled carrots and daikon radish. This banh mi created a symphony in my mouth. Was beyond thrilled with every bit and moment of eating, enjoying, and loving this banh mi!

Name: Alamo Square Cafe
Author: Cassandra Stokholm
Review:
Great place to stop for a delicious sandwich and coffee if you’re visiting the painted ladies!

My fiancé and I were visiting for an event and stayed nearby within walking distance. I got a salmon avocado sandwich on a whole wheat roll with a small cappuccino and my fiancé got a smoked turkey sandwich on French bread with a small iced black tea. Everything was great! The whole wheat roll was SO soft and all parts of my sandwich were seasoned, including the lettuce. I was pleasantly surprised and finished the whole sandwich. My fiancé also enjoyed his sandwich (finished it!) and his black tea was so good! My coffee was dark and foamed well, like a cappuccino should be.

Service was great. There was only one gentleman working the shop and he was able to get ours and others’ orders out quickly.

There are 6 small tables in the seating area, on either side of the front entrance and each space has access to an outlet with multiple plugs. Every table was clean and the space was well kept so we were able to choose any of the tables that weren’t already taken! Very much appreciated that.

There was a medium flow of traffic on a Monday, late afternoon and a couple of people using the space with their lap tops when we were there. It was a chill space with a lively radio in the background, Drake, Lizzie, Taylor swift, etc.

There’s local art work on the walls with pricing and a QR code for purchase too!

I really liked this space and wish I lived nearby so that I could stop by more often! For now, I’d recommend to friends and family.

TLDR: Unpretentious cafe with a chill vibe, delicious sandwiches, and lots of outlets.

Name: Alamo Square Cafe
Author: Linda
Review: Got a cappuccino and bagel breakfast here. Nice ambience and medium level of business  on a Sunday morning. I really enjoyed the ambience, the kind barista, and the ability to do work in this cafe. 6 tables and lots of power outlets! However the coffee is not my favorite at all. The coffee tasted like dark roast and reminded me of Starbucks coffee. Maybe that would fit the palette of someone else, but I would come back here to do work or grab a bite, and not for the coffee.

Name: Alamo Square Cafe
Author: Doug Maner
Review:
This is a nice, unpretentious small cafe just around the corner from Alamo square.
The couple who served us were super friendly and attentive.
It's clean and calm inside with limited outside seating.
Prices are reasonable for the neighborhood, three dollar coffee, $13.50 sandwiches.
Nice place

Name: Alamo Square Cafe
Author: Michael Breach
Review: All and all a decent spot! Perfect place to grab a sandwich and have yourself a picnic in front of the Painted Ladies houses. Very substantial sandwich and put together very well.

Name: Alamo Square Cafe
Author: Ronny Dote
Review: i got their bacon turkey avo club and for $17 it was very worth every penny. portion was huge and was very fresh and tasty. if you miss nyc bodega style sandwiches, go here!!

Name: Florio Bar & Cafe
Author: Helen Qin
Review:
5 stars for everything. We booked a reservation online for a group of 7 night before Saturday dinner. We were pleasantly surprised to be seated in a private dining area, which made the dinner special.

The food tasted like it was made with care. The service was wonderful too.

Name: Florio Bar & Cafe
Author: Philip Gissen
Review: We stopped in for drinks at Florio and were charmed by the neighborhood friendliness.  Everyone was nice and down to earth, not like the usual San Fran smugness.  The menu looked wonderful, and I wished we dined here rather than the fancy place up the street where our reservation was. I wish Florio was opened for lunch, but we’ll go back for apps tonight before we meet friends for dinner.

Name: Florio Bar & Cafe
Author: Laura Peters
Review: My friend and I felt immediately comfortable in Florio Bar & Cafe. This welcoming restaurant is nicely appointed. The Brussel Sprouts were exceptional. The Fresh Roasted Eggplant and Goat Cheese Ravioli boasted complex flavors that scintillated the palate. The service and presentation of each dish was perfect. Oh, and the dessert we shared, an Olive Oil cake, (not pictured) was delicious. I look forward to returning to this restaurant.  Update from recent visit on a Saturday night: The food and service is still top notch, but wow, the restaurant ambient noise sure is loud on a Saturday night!

Name: Florio Bar & Cafe
Author: Morgana “MoNa” Q
Review: I have visited Florio Bar and Cafe in San Francisco many times. The cozy atmosphere is inviting, and the food is delicious, even when they're out of brussels sprout. The service is attentive and friendly, though the noise level can be high on busy nights. Prices are a bit steep, and occasional discrepancies on the final bill can occur. Despite this, the overall quality makes it worthwhile. While many reviews mention issues with the service and the bartender, I have never had any problems.

Name: Florio Bar & Cafe
Author: Nick Parham
Review: My husband and I dine out alot in the city, and frequently at Florio and we find the restaurant to be one of our favorites. First, the food, be it steak, pasta or fish is always superb. The wait staff are experienced and very friendly. The bartender is skilled with traditional drinks and the exotic. It is a great venue for celebrations. Nick

Name: Awaken Cafe & Roasting
Author: Karl Peterman
Review: Just in town for a couple days for some meetings nearby and met a friend here for coffee before. Really like their cafe design and the ambience. Met the guy who does the roasting and the staff are all friendly. I like lighter fruitier coffees but they don’t do pour overs but they had a medium roast coffee on drip so I tried that and liked it. Good tasting notes and balanced. Honestly I’m a bit overwhelmed by so many coffee options in the area but this one seems to be a good one so I certainly recommend!

Name: Awaken Cafe & Roasting
Author: Michael Johns
Review: This coffee spot seems to be an awesome shill spot to grab a quick cup of coffee or even stay awhile and get some work done. The space is beautiful and the Ube Latte (only thing I have ordered from there - so far) is delicious. Unfortunately I did not know that it did not have coffee inside of it but the barista asked me if I knew that after I already ordered and added in an espresso shot for free 👌🏽👌🏽♥️♥️. Great customer service!! Check this place out. I'm sure everything is fantastic including the delicious looking food! The menu is pretty extensive!

Name: Awaken Cafe & Roasting
Author: Paul Nelson
Review: A very friendly staff and a wonderful coffee menu. The location is a wonderful quiet haven from the noise of the big city outside.

Name: Awaken Cafe & Roasting
Author: Eli Wallis
Review: Im from out of town and went here because it came up when I searched for sensory friendly coffee shops. Love the dimmer lighting and quiet music. Also, because they don't have wifi there was plenty of seating available for the coffee+chat crowd

Name: Awaken Cafe & Roasting
Author: Kapil Chandra
Review: I surprised my wife with a Barista class at Awaken Coffee, Cortt (owner) and Dustin were just fantastic right from communication to the whole class. Dustin went into such great detail and I wasn't expecting a session with that level of info. He made sure we both understood each aspect and above all made us enjoy the experience. I would go as far as saying Dustin is a connoisseur on anything coffee, he has plethora of information that he was willing to share and was super patient overall. The coffee was fantastic, we did get to taste multiple roasts that they had and cortt even gifted us a bag to take home. It was a wonderful experience overall.

Name: Tea On Piedmont
Author: Heidi Liou
Review:
If you order tea (and you stay in the store), you can get one refill. Highly recommend their Jasmine!

The staff are all so incredibly nice and helpful. They also have some merchandise/chocolate that make great gifts.

Love the big windows and natural lighting in the front nook area. Perfect vibe all around for a zen afternoon.

They allow you to bring in sushi from the store across the street!

Name: Tea On Piedmont
Author: Olivia Ma
Review:
I bet you won’t find any tea brand comparable to Tea On Piedmont on the West Coast! The Hojicha latte was a 1000 out of 10—absolutely amazing! And the iced matcha latte= A dream come true!!

I feel so disappointed how bad taste any tea shop store in Seattle🥹but in California! SooRad😘🤌🤌🤌 I will definitely back to check out any chance back here 🔥Tea On Piedmont🔥

Name: Tea On Piedmont
Author: V Tancredi
Review:
I'm not normally a tea drinker but I had the Hojicha latte and I have become addicted. It's amazing!

They also have nice pastries with a French influence as well as a number of fancy teas for sale. My daughter said the strawberry milk with boba is delicious.

Central location on Piedmont avenue with Wi-Fi and power if you need to do a little work on your laptop.

Highly recommend.

Name: Tea On Piedmont
Author: Sonia Garcia
Review: I ordered an unsweetened matcha with almond milk. The color of the matcha looked so green, it was beautiful, and as it turns out, super tasty.

Name: Tea On Piedmont
Author: Sophia D
Review:
Delicious fresh boba and great texture even later in the day
There are also local artists with pins, stickers, tea, etc for sale

Name: Wu Wei Tea Temple
Author: Chris Britt
Review: Wu Wei is a special place. It's very friendly; both the staff and the the people who frequent the place. It's a great place to bring a book, to journal, work on your laptop, play a table game, and meet with friends. It's super comfortable -- table top, couches, floor seating with cushions. There's a wonderful patio in the back. They have many kinds of drinks, hot and cold. I like the sweet drinks and Nora's desserts. Highly recommended.

Name: Wu Wei Tea Temple
Author: Sneha H S
Review: An intriguing spot with so much to take in! The colorful interior and decor, the comfortable Morocco looking lounge seating and carpets, the mystic music, and the variety of teas - were all a treat to the senses. The seating felt a little dusty, yet adds character to the place. Art for purchase on the walls. Parking was free for a couple of hours nearby over the weekend.

Name: Wu Wei Tea Temple
Author: Martin Bishop
Review: Great for tea, food (try the hummus) and atmosphere. Also a good place to work. An outside patio too

Name: Wu Wei Tea Temple
Author: Valerie Meyers
Review:
Wish I could give this company a 0. An ignorant woman refused service to a disabled woman with a service dog. I am contacting the ADA and reporting you. The woman claimed she didn’t “look disabled “ and rudely kicked her and the service dog out. Do you have ANY CLUE how many disabling conditions are invisible? I will not say what illness the young girl who got kicked out has, but let me give u some examples. One major one - CANCER. Cancer is invisible and a silent Growth! How can you tell if a woman has a very good wig on, hiding the fact that she gets chemotherapy?! (Again, this is an EXAMPLE, the woman who got kicked out doesn’t have cancer, but what she does have is serious, as I know her personally). Severe epilepsy is another example, it’s invisible and would warrant a service dog.  Autoimmune diseases for the most part are INVISIBLE 🫥 and can be DISABLING. I have one of the above mentioned conditions. You might want to stop judging people!! A lot of times, you don’t know someone has cancer unless they tell you! Some good makeup and a high quality wig, you’d never know! Shame on the woman who did this. Karma. One day you might be the sick one, and I hope the world treats you the exact same way you just treated this customer. You just pissed off an entire community. Remember, cancer grows silently, you might not know you have it until it’s too late, and NO ONE deserves poor treatment. (Again, the customer didn’t have cancer, I’m just making an example here, as it’s not my place to say what disability she has).  How nice it must be to be healthy enough to work,  something the sick people WISH we can do. We wish we didn’t have to suffer. Shame on you. God sees all, and he doesn’t like ugly. Have the day you deserve. And remember, karma. Also, I see you bark back on negative reviews- it shows you can’t take criticism. What a self centered egotistical Brat you are. I’m sure you’ll bark back to this so you get the final say, but don’t worry, enough people already know what happened here, you pissed of an entire community honey. Instead of being so rude, try being grateful that you aren’t sick yourself. Disgusting. Your true colors are showing and it’s not cute.

Edit-  it was not ME! I am her friend. But wow you have a bad attitude. You need mental help. Your business is blasted on TikTok in case you’re late to the game. It went viral.  The chronic illness community is pissed. Again, reporting you to the ADA. Bye 👋

Name: Wu Wei Tea Temple
Author: Andrea Perelson
Review: I was horrified and still reeling from my encounter at this establishment. I am young but disabled with an eventually terminal illness. I have a dog that my doctor wrote a letter for and then was trained to go in public with me everywhere. When I approached the door a very curt older women said " No animals in here ever!" Making a shooing motion at me. I calmly tried to explain I may not look it but am in deed disabled and need him. She said " no excuses no animals in here ever." I started to educate her on the law as I was leaving and she raised her voice and demanded " I wasn't getting my way and had the audacity to question my disability. I know people don't love dogs especially big ones but I was more than willing to make concessions such as sitting in the back patio where no one is. I left humiliated by the scene and cried. Feeling helpless, hurt and attacked. Believing my need for a dog or not no one should be speaking to me like that and raising there voice. Very unfortunate situation. They need to brush up on the ADA laws.( I saw your comment to the other persons review and would love to come in with my documentation to show you I am not “ lying”. I noticed the other negative reviews you have also recount unpleasant experiences when having service dogs. Do better)

Name: Village Tea House
Author: Alice Lee
Review:
The Dan Dan Noodles here are exceptional. I ordered both the Dan Dan Noodles and the chili oil wontons. The sauce for the Dan Dan Noodles is rich and flavorful, and the portion size is perfect—not too much, not too little. The noodles come with a generous amount of minced meat.

The wontons are generously filled, with a noticeable amount of shrimp in each bite. They are not very spicy but still quite delicious.

The service staff is very courteous, and the interior decor is clean and well-maintained. The food is not overly salty, making for a very enjoyable dining experience. The seating by the window offers a particularly pleasant atmosphere.

Name: Village Tea House
Author: Gustavo Monaco
Review:
This was a good experience from the moment we walked in the restaurant. The staff made us feel like old friends being hosted by extraordinary culinary experts. Loved every bite. In descending order of wonder for me:
WOW: Dan Dan noodles
1. Chili Oil Wonton
2. Shrimps. Woodear. Chives dumpling
3. BBQ Pork buns
4. Pork Sheng Jian bao
5. Pork Xiao Long bao.
For dessert, the Coconut Pudding. It was a mystical experience that brought me back to eating fresh tender coconut from Cayo Sombrero in Venezuela.
My heart left full as well as my stomach.

Name: Village Tea House
Author: Nick Chambers
Review: This was the unexpected highlight of  great trip. Vincent took great care of us all night with one fantastic recommendation after another. The chef surpassed expectations on every order.

Name: Village Tea House
Author: Michael Chang
Review:
Came here with some co-workers for lunch.  The restaurant was crowded. Fortunately, we had made a reservation in advance so we were seated almost immediately.

Half of us ordered the Dan Dan Noodles while the other half ordered the beef noodle soup. My Dan Dan Noodles were decent, but the numb-inducing spice that normally accompanies Dan Dan Noodles was missing. So too was the nutty flavor. I was a bit disappointed. My co-workers seemed to enjoy the beef noodle soup so maybe that's the better dish to order.

Service was friendly, but the food took a long time to be served. Also, the Dan Dan Noodles came out about 10 minutes before the beef noodle soup so half of us sat there waiting as our food got cold.

The restaurant is extremely noisy so it's difficult to have a conversation. I won't be back.

Name: Village Tea House
Author: Lauretta Lu
Review:
We probably ordered half the menu, and everything was delicious. The only underwhelming dish was the boiled shrimp dumplings as they didn't have much filling.

The atmosphere and service were good, although if you sit in the back near the side door you may need to ask them to close it as the breeze can bring in whiffs of pee/etc from the street.

Name: Societea Tea House & Eatery
Author: Laura Michaelis
Review:
I was a little nervous at first because I am not very open when it comes to my diet. Let me tell you, I was extremely happy.

We had a party of six with two separate orders, I had ordered. The Pork Belly sandwich with the Furi-Furi Sprouts. My husband had the Thai Chicken over rice. The dishes were amazing!! I am craving the sandwich now.

The Thai Milk Tea was spot on and the Honeydew Tea was like they blended the melon and stuck a straw in it, it was that good.

The waffle fries were on point, the popcorn chicken was okay, The burgers were good, but the jalapenos were not spicy (according to my friend).

As we walked out, we discovered that you could actually check out a board game and play a game in the cafe while enjoying your meal. Might keep the kids off their phones.

Name: Societea Tea House & Eatery
Author: B Na
Review: Happy to have found a place close to the airport to have lunch with a friend while waiting out a layover. We ordered the lemongrass crispy tofu sandwich and I had a matcha brown sugar bubble tea. Both were great and we played some board games to pass the time. There is a large seating area in the back for group gatherings.

Name: Societea Tea House & Eatery
Author: Jennifer Ho
Review: Cute little place.  Craved banh mi and found them through Yelp.  Spacious room in the back to work and eat.  Can’t take work calls here 😉 background noise.  Plenty of parking in the back with easy QR code to scan and pay $1 for 2 hours.

Name: Societea Tea House & Eatery
Author: Esther C.
Review:
It is a casual cafe with enough seating space to sit and play games with friends or study on your own (which was the primary reason I came here and appreciated their set up) but unfortunately within the last month as I’ve started to come in more often and have brought friends, we’ve encountered coming into the store to music that is so loud you cannot hear one another speak, and 4-6 employees gathered at the register laughing loudly, which wouldn’t be a problem had my friends and I been greeted upon our entry.

As for the food, their drinks are fun and some are tasty but the boba is too stiff and not chewy at all. I find that their Jasmine tea is consistently over steeped but black tea based drinks are solid. Their food is okay.

If you do not mind a loud atmosphere, this can definitely be the place for you but if you’re seeking a place to study or get work done, this might not be the spot for you.

Name: Societea Tea House & Eatery
Author: Cindy L
Review:
Fav spot in the peninsula for popcorn chicken and milk tea! The workers are super friendly and drinks come out fast.

My go to is the strawberry matcha latte and Thai green tea. It’s the perfect balance of creamy yet refreshing matcha flavor and the honey boba is perfect lightly chewy texture and not too sweet.

Name: Feng Cha Teahouse - Berkeley
Author: J L
Review:
I was excited to try a new snack place, and we odes a few things to try. Ordered for delivery popcorn chicken,  fried tofu,  curly fries,  summer melon drink, Thai iced tea, dirty Boba, regular classic Boba drink.

Let's start with the drinks.

1. Summer melon was okay but wasn't sweet enough. There were bits of watermelon floating in the drink and maybe some bitsof lemon.  Unfortunately, the watermelon wasn't ripe, and it tasted like they used the white part of the watermelon rind. 😕 The Boba tasted just okay,  it had enough chew but didn't have that honey sweet taste to it like other places make it.

2. Dirty Boba was mediocre with hardly any brown sugar taste. It tastes like just a regular milk tea drink.

3. Thai ice tea was okay.

4. Classic Boba was just okay... nothing really to write about other than it was a bit bland.

Now let's talk about the food...

1. Popcorn chicken was beyond disappointing... it would have made the chicken that it came from very ashamed and disgusted. On the outside it looks good,  but once you pop it in your mouth it feels like you are eating a wad of cardboard.  Not only was it dry, but it was hardly seasoned. I had to put garlic salt on it to make it taste ok, but then I had to dip it in a sauce to hide the dryness of the meat.  The other disappointing thing was that the majority of the pieces of chicken were tiny.  It looked like someone used the tiny dregs of meat and balls of flour from the bottom of the flour bin to cook  and sell.

2. Deep fried tofu was another disappointing snack that tasted as bland as a white piece of paper.  Someone must have had a bag day or was too busy to pick up the seasoning shaker to flavor the tofu.  Also, they must be saving cooking gas because the oil they used was not up to temp to properly fry the tofu.  You can tell because the outside coating of the tofu absorbed all the oil and didn't properly crisp the outside.  Yes I know it was delivered and it couldn't be crisp by the time it got to me but I've had experience buying fresh deep fried tofu from Oakland Chinatown and driving it all the way home five cities away and the outside of the tofu was still hard.

3. Curly fries... I don't normally order this but my daughter likes them.  The curly fries were okay.. seasoning was fine.  I think the fries were the best thing in my whole order with the drinks coming in second.

Overall,  I wouldn't go back unless I totally forgot about this place and accidentally visited or ordered from them again.  😅

Name: Feng Cha Teahouse - Berkeley
Author: Rachel Kim
Review: Please close your hot drinks or tape them bc they spilled all over me and burnt my hands. Customer service was not friendly either. I asked her for a cup holder because I had three drinks and she threw the plastic bag on the counter and I had to put down all my drinks to put it in. Restrooms aren’t working either.

Name: Feng Cha Teahouse - Berkeley
Author: Brendan Sullivan
Review: Lighting fast service at lunch time, and the boba and tea were delicious. Place is spacious and good for a lot of people so they can handle crowds pretty well it seems. Staff was pleasant and upbeat.

Name: Feng Cha Teahouse - Berkeley
Author: Leo Lee
Review: The chicken is really good! I've been craving this for so many years and I've finally got the chance to try it. The sweet Chilli flavor for the chicken is really good and crunchy.

Name: Feng Cha Teahouse - Berkeley
Author: Faye
Review: Great location, but hard to find parking space nearby, so be prepared for looking for parking. otherwise, dessert and drinks are all great to try! This new location comes with a totally modern interior design and clean style, which I really do like it. The cake I would like to recommend matcha and strawberry.  All fruit drinks are awesome fresh,  also dirty milk tea is the signature in here too.

Name: Far Leaves Tea
Author: Carlos Apodaca
Review: An absolute serene and pleasant tea house. Wonderful atmosphere, friendly tea master service. Exquisite and aromatic tea drinking experience. I will definitely be back to this Wonderful place next time I visit the Bay area.

Name: Far Leaves Tea
Author: Jiahui Zhou
Review: A wonderful place to meet up with friends or just slow down and find the peace of mind again. A wide selection of teas to be served in appropriate manners (teapot, temperature, brewing time etc). Will come back next time visiting Berkeley.

Name: Far Leaves Tea
Author: Bryce McLean
Review: A wonderfully unique experience with extremely knowledgeable staff and an extensive tea range. Relaxed setting and reasonable prices.

Name: Far Leaves Tea
Author: Ever Apollo
Review: Wow. Very kind and attentive service. These are tea experts who are knowledgeable of different brewing methods depending on the tea. They introduced us to puer tea which is absolutely delicious and unique. We were in a rush so only bought tea to brew at home but hoping to stay a bit longer and do a flight of their rare teas some time. Also has a variety of decaf herbal options, including rose!

Name: Far Leaves Tea
Author: S D
Review: Such a calm place! Feels like a inside zen garden. The lady running the store was super friendly and made sure to check few times that our large group orders were correct. Got the blood orange tea and mochi. Both were really good! Great way to relax or just chat with fiends. Berkeley surprises me with how many cool places there are!

Name: Teaspoon
Author: felicie diaz
Review:
ABSOLUTE BANGER SO SO GOOD!!
Got the rose milk tea and the caramel cream boba with Oreo and it was divine so good.
Will be coming back
New obsession

Name: Teaspoon
Author: Julie Taeko
Review: I tried the watermelon juice that was absolutely delicious, and customized to less sweet. And it had delicious little bits of crunchy seeds at the bottom. My friend got one of the snow ice desserts which was also quite delicious. And the service was fantastic!

Name: Teaspoon
Author: Sher Zhang
Review:
Favorite bubble tea in the area!
I’ve visited 5 times already in the last month… my boba cravings are adequately satisfied here :)

Creamy Jasmine and and grasshopper are my two top drinks so far, depending on if I’m in a milk tea or fruity drink mood.

Liquid gold is a solid drink if you like the roasted flavour, and caramel cream is decadent and sweet (although I wish there was a milk tea version, as this one only contains milk)

Name: Teaspoon
Author: Mitchell Robinson
