Review: Everything is good, drinks, baked goods, teas, service, everything. The music could be cranked down just a little bit though. It’s quite loud in my opinion. But good place good stuff, nonetheless.

Name: Teaspoon
Author: Edison Huang
Review:
The kiosk may need to be updated. We ordered the lychee on lychee drink which has lychee jelly included.. the kiosk also mentioned that... but the next option is to add topping so we chose no topping because we don't want to add any additional topping.. well the drink ended up having no topping whatsoever. Pretty much paid for a full drink only to lose out on the topping.

The pear-adise drink isn't bad though. I recommend not going 0% sweet on that one.

Name: Jasmine Tea House
Author: Fengjiao Peng
Review: The wok-fried food is amazing and I love their house noodle soup. The lady is super kind and this is my favorite Chinese restaurant within 1 mile. Price is also decent

Name: Jasmine Tea House
Author: xXcashXx3
Review: Food is great best Wonton soup I ever had and the walnut shrimp is good to not over covered in sauce like how most places do chow mein and and fried rice is good not greasey like other places only thing if you dine in its hot inside and you have to share the hot sauce and soy sauce with other people

Name: Jasmine Tea House
Author: Ethan C
Review: This place saved my life when I had influenza. The wonton soup was the only thing I could keep down. Its hole in the wall Americanized Chinese food, it’s not fancy, but it’s delicious.

Name: Jasmine Tea House
Author: Chad Lahue
Review: If I could give the food, 100 stars I would. For what it lacks in charm it makes up in the food. My shrimp dish had real tremilla mushrooms in it. How rare is that! Sauce was excellent. Service was good. Will definitely come back.

Name: Jasmine Tea House
Author: Oscar Nova
Review: Our food was ready in no time, and it was hot and tasty.

Name: Asha Tea House
Author: James
Review:
One of my favorite milk tea places in the Bay Area! They serve high-quality teas and accept reusable boba containers! However, their straws and containers can be industrially-composted as well.

They have a set menu but also have seasonal flavors. You can order at the counter or at the tablet.

It is often filled with college students as the campus is nearby.

Name: Asha Tea House
Author: Alice L.
Review: My go-to for traditional non-milk teas; I favor hojicha & matcha. The seasonal flavors such as yuzu & asian pear add another level of variety & complexity & you can get it unsweetened as well. There are local baked goods but they disappear quickly, but I was able to snag a black sesame cookie. The matcha sparkler hits the spot on a warm day too.

Name: Asha Tea House
Author: Natenapa Serdynski
Review: I love the architecture and atmosphere of this place. Green tea is excellent! We didn't think this is the best place for Boba. (My husband and my daughter are the Boba freak!) I LOVE the menu. Very different and well done. I thought this place could be more cleaner. Flies are just everywhere and floor are very messy. But overall, come and try their many choices of hot tea.

Name: Asha Tea House
Author: Aayushi Trivedi
Review: This spot is aesthetically unique with it’s minimalistic modern architecture! The drinks hit the spot just as equally (pictured iced chai latte and matcha latte). While super flavorful and aromatic, the infused spices in the chai latte are quite strong and would suggest getting a hot version of the drink without boba.

Name: Asha Tea House
Author: Ushko Mi
Review: We didn't even finish our drinks, that's how unpleasant they were (blueberry tea and strawberry cream soda, the blueberry tea just tasted like dirty water and the soda was like a badly flavored milk). Not much customer service interaction, because we got it to go and you order from a tablet, so nothing to review on that either way. It was surprisingly extremely crowded (maybe there's something better than what we ordered?), so no tables were available (it does seem like the kind of place where people order 1 drink and sit for quite some time). The prices are fine, if the product was good (unfortunately, it was not). Definitely will not return, very disappointing.

Name: Brew Cha
Author: Alice Garner
Review: Really great experience here! Super fast. Ordered the mango passion fruit slushy, love that you can mix and match flavors! They have games and a nice couch and it’s in a super convenient location! Nice friendly staff. REALLY YUMMY BOBA! Would 100% go back again.

Name: Brew Cha
Author: cravesinthebay
Review: Brewcha SF 💌 one of the best freshly brewed boba tea spot in SF 😍! Their drinks were super fresh & tasty. I recommend getting the Black Sugar Creme Brulee Latte ❣️ which I felt in love with!

Name: Brew Cha
Author: Vee Dinh
Review:
Discovered a milk tea spot with light flavored drinks. Follow me!

Highlights of the menu:
1. Tiramisu milk tea
2. Lavender matcha latte
3. Panda milk tea
4. Roasted oolong tea with cheese cream top

Name: Brew Cha
Author: Flor Fernandez
Review: FRESH, organic, has quite a few milk options (even lactose free) and a large selection of drinks!  I loved that they used real taro for their drink. Friendly staff. Worth it!

Name: Brew Cha
Author: xo_julienna
Review: It’s my first time going there. My brother in law frequently goes to Brew Cha for boba drinks. See photos I took that gives you a full picture. My favorite drink is hot black sesame latte and it is so delicious!

Name: Palette Tea House
Author: Denise Lani Pascual
Review:
This place was delicious, according to my family. Unfortunately, most of the dishes contain MSG and/or yeast extract. BUT ask and they can make a few things without. I thoroughly enjoyed the dishes I could have, and my family raved about the rest.

The duck, dumplings, pot stickers, green beans, won ton soup had MSG, but my family loved it.

I had the eggplant and tofu dish and the mushroom & truffle chow fun. They were delicious. The custard dumplings for dessert were amazing.

I appreciated that they could tell me right away which dishes were safe for me to eat. Having food sensitivities makes going out to eat challenging. But they made it easy so I could spend the time with my family having a wonderful meal.

The prices were expected for a tourist attraction in SF. I only gave 4 stars for service because we didn't get our drinks (tea for my dad and auntie and fountain drinks for my son and niece) until we were almost done with lunch and had to ask for water refills.  No one came to check on us to see if we needed refills.

Name: Palette Tea House
Author: Scott B
Review: This place rocked! I thought the special of the day, the morel mushroom and shrimp dumpling, was probably the best tasting single bite of food I've had in the past year. The other standard menu items were great too, like the vegetable dumplings in chili oil or the pan fried black pepper beef bun. We came right when it opened and service was initially lighting fast. As it filled up, the service got slower and we ended up waiting 20 minutes for the dessert buns. Anyway, it was still great and the atmosphere was awesome. Would come again given the pricing.

Name: Palette Tea House
Author: michelle pender
Review: This is literally the BEST dim sum experience in the city!! Phenomenal food (the walnut prawns were amazing—soup dumplings were so flavorful—lobster dumplings were decadent. . . the list goes on!) , elegant ambiance and fantastic customer service. Perfect for large groups as well!

Name: Palette Tea House
Author: Manana Hakobyan
Review:
I think it’s a solid spot, I would definitely advise my friends to visit it. I was expecting the dumplings to be more mind-blowing since I have heard good things about it and the restaurant was in Michelin guide in 2023. However, the dumplings were ok.

The atmosphere was great though. My boyfriend and I were seated at this big shared countertop and there was a centerpiece aquarium with a fish in it, which was really cute.

Name: Palette Tea House
Author: 1John 2:27
Review: Went for happy hour, the chicken shu mai wasn’t good, the seafood dumplings were. The spring rolls were fabulous. I also had an opportunity to go to a featured dinner that I will post too. Everyone was welcoming and the restaurant has a great vibe.

Name: Quarters Teahouse
Author: Calvin Tong
Review: Amazing service for a boba shop, the staff was very attentive and offered to adjust sugar level etc even after we tasted our drinks, she seemed to be proud of her creations which is important! And honestly, we were very happy with our drinks, they used fresh fruits which makes a huge difference, and the drinks were so flavorful and refreshing. I haven't had a boba place make this impression in a long time, who knew it was hidden away in outer sunset in a tiny shop! We will definitely be coming back!

Name: Quarters Teahouse
Author: Jana Rodriguez
Review: We tried it once and already fall in love with the milk teas. Def coming back to try more flavors!

Name: Quarters Teahouse
Author: Corina Lange
Review:
This place is a hidden gem. I visited this place on a weekday afternoon for a little break from working. The passionfruit green tea I ordered was delicious and even had real passion fruit in the drink.

Only 1 other person was ordering for pick-up when I was at the store, and they have a kiosk for efficiency, so it didn't take long for my drink to be prepared. This is one of the better boba spots I've tried in the city, and I recommend checking them out.

Name: Quarters Teahouse
Author: Jacky Liu
Review:
Quarters has hands down the BEST fruit teas in the city. I've tried their longan lime, lychee, guava, pear drinks and more, and they've only ever used fresh fruit purees and pulps. The texture, sweetness level, and flavors are always very well-balanced.

The lady who runs the shop is also very sweet, and always offers to adjust my drink after I try it. I have been here over 7 times, but I'll definitely be coming back soon!
Food: 5/5  |  Service: 5/5  |  Atmosphere: 5/5

Name: Quarters Teahouse
Author: Mona Huang
Review:
Great boba and dessert! Passed by this place randomly but was very impressed with my lychee passion tea drink and the egg puffs!

They do not use artificial syrups for fruit teas and use real fruit puree instead. You can definitely taste the difference! The staff was also friendly and attentive.

Name: San San Tea House
Author: Darío Arzaba
Review: Great service, they were super helpful ❤️

Name: Mr & Mrs Tea House
Author: Valerie Pugs
Review: As soon as I arrived, an employee walked past me and it left a strong smell of poop. After smelling this, we still decided to order and we got our drinks, they did not taste as described. Customer service was bad and the person at the register was rude and wasn’t helpful with the menu. She seemed like she was in a bad mood. The drinks didn’t taste good, it seemed like they were from instant packets.

Name: Mr & Mrs Tea House
Author: Y Xu
Review:
Our experience here was extremely disappointing. We arrived and ordered 2 High Mountain Oolong Tea Creama drinks without being informed that all their beverages come with default ice. Surprisingly, we weren't given the choice of having ice or not. When our drinks arrived, we politely requested one without ice, as we weren't aware that all their drinks were served cold.

The staff member at the front counter complained about the default cold drinks and questioned how we could have known. We requested the removal of the ice and asked for room temperature drinks. And we have to pay extra $0.50 to remove the ice. She proceeded to the kitchen to fulfill our request.

However, when a man emerged from the kitchen with a stern expression and asked us, "What the hell do you want?" in an intimidating tone, it left us feeling genuinely frightened. All we wanted was a room-temperature drink, and amidst the tension, we managed to explain our preference to him. He went back to the kitchen and eventually prepared a room-temperature drink.

I can't adequately convey how scared I felt; it was a truly unpleasant experience. I have no intention of returning, and while the drink was acceptable, it was rather bland, almost like water.

Name: Mr & Mrs Tea House
Author: Calvin Tong
Review: Honestly our go to neighborhood boba spot! We think it slightly edges over Little Sweet but not by much. Our go to drinks are winter melon lemon juice, high mountain oolong milk tea, berry berry, and mango drinks. Service is fine, they'll recognize you if you're a regular, and there are just four stools inside to wait for or drink your drinks.

Name: Mr & Mrs Tea House
Author: Scott Wong
Review: Family wanted boba and we checked this place out. Long line outside but was hoping a quick service. Unfortunately, we waited 30 minutes for maybe 14 orders ahead of us. Taro and Oreo boba was really tasty. Just be ready to wait. I’d give 5 starts if the wait wasn’t so long.

Name: Mr & Mrs Tea House
Author: Fumiko Hoeft
Review: Kids love their boba but the service is so so slow. Especially when you order through DoorDash. Took over 45min after asking status. In person orders seem ti be somewhat better. Note of caution!

Name: Hang Ah Tea Room
Author: M W
Review:
For about 100 years or so this restaurant has been serving dim sum, quite possibly the first in the United States. We found this little restaurant like many online and we're also a little skeptical.

The food and service were outstanding. The dim sum menu was very comprehensive and featured some rare treats. We visited on a mid-afternoon and were one of three tables in the restaurant.

For an authentic and signature dim sum experience in Dan Francisco, I would give this place a try.

Name: Hang Ah Tea Room
Author: Annette Goh
Review: I recently visited this dim sum restaurant, but found the experience just okay. The food was decent, but nothing stood out. The place itself felt old and dated, lacking the ambiance you'd expect. Overall, it was an average meal in a setting that could use some updating.

Name: Hang Ah Tea Room
Author: Alison M C
Review: Our dim sum set B plus shenjian bao was basic, but satisfied our craving. No frills but decent. Friendly service. Delicious iced lemon tea and also incredible cold milk tea--strong tea flavor and not too much milk or sugar. I'd go back for tea even at $6, xiao long bao, and good scallion pancake. Other stuff like spring roll and jiaozi kind of average bland. I liked the chile oil but it was so gunky in the dish it was hard to serve. Some fruit flies. The restaurant and condiments and tables truly need a good cleaning.

Name: Hang Ah Tea Room
Author: Sherry
Review:
Like people have said, not necessarily the best tasting restaurant for dim sum, but it is nice to support a small business that's been around and get to say you're been to the first one in the US since the 1920s. :)

It was empty when we went in midday Thursday. We got the fried stuff eggplant, curry fishballs and lotus wrapsticky rice. The eggplant had fried shrimp inside and was the favorite. The fishballs tasted different than the ones I usually eat (it tasted artificial :/), and the curry was spicier than expected.

Name: Hang Ah Tea Room
Author: jack
Review: Social media took me here….Everything very dated and cluttered. Food very average and felt like it was frozen. Pork bun was disgusting. Found so many better places nearby

Name: MandRo Teahouse
Author: Sarah Bakhtary
Review: This new teahouse in Alameda is definitely worth trying! Just near the corner of Park and Encinal, a small cafe serves bingsoo and a variety of teas. I decided to get their Oolong Litchi Green Tea with Coconut Jelly and it was great! The tea was fragrant and the fruit was sweet. I also got their Strawberry Bingsoo along with Mango Bingsoo which was great, but not by itself because you NEED to get a side of condensed milk to make it creamy. The portions are HUGE so I recommend coming here with a friend. Overall the facility was calm which I liked, but one thing is that the front of their store is not that clean. The window area with pillows is quite dusty, which may impact other customer's experience. I would suggest they wash the pillows and clean the windows, besides that their food and service is amazing. Overall, the experience was great and I'll definitely keep coming here. Come check this place out!

Name: MandRo Teahouse
Author: Erik Foster
Review:
We tried this dessert and boba tea place yesterday, and it was absolutely fantastic! My wife and I shared the Mango Bingsoo and it was delicious and plentiful! We added an extra condensed milk for .50 (the dessert includes 1 serving of condensed milk, but I was happy we had 2 servings). This was is such a unique dessert experience. It consists of a milk-based shaved ice, with a generous layer of fresh mango slices and mango syrup on the top, and some mango pieces syrup and possibly condensed milk inside as well. It is kind of pricey as desserts go ($18 incl tip), but it was large enough for 2-3 ppl, and the price seemed fair once we received/ devoured the dish! If you have 3 adults sharing you might spring for the large size, which cost a few bucks more as I recall.

Truly an excellent experience and we’ll definitely be back to try the other dessert flavors and drinks too.

I have to say that it felt much healthier than many other desserts I could name, but I don’t actually know the calorie count. I don’t want to know to be fair, because I like assuming it’s very healthy, so we can return often!

Name: MandRo Teahouse
Author: Cz juarez
Review:
Delicious SNOW Ice.
Small drinks and shaved ice shop. They do have a wide range of options. From cold drinks, hot tea drinks, to smoothies. But the signature item has to be the variety of shaved ice options.
Relax environment, a few tables available for sitting down.

Name: MandRo Teahouse
Author: Michael Chang
Review:
MandRo's bingsu was average at best. We ordered the matcha and red bean bingsu. Their shaved ice wasn't very creamy so the matcha made it bitter. They were also skimpy on the red bean so it was basically plain shaved iced with matcha powder. Also, they do not include condensed milk as a topping so be sure to order a side. It is definitely needed. I should have ordered more than one side of condensed milk because they were skimpy with that too.

Service and dining area were fine. Overall, this is a pass in my book.

Name: MandRo Teahouse
Author: Rayna Lomker
Review: The place smelled very strongly of mold... The tea was good, but I just got it and left. I ordered on the touch screen so I can't speak for the service. Hopefully they can take care of that as it was a huge turn off for guests who might want to sit inside.

Name: Feng Cha Teahouse - San Francisco
Author: Joshua Brathwaite
Review: Crème Brûlée dirty boba was delicious! Black milk tea with bits of brown sugar. The staff here were very nice and the facility was very cozy and clean. A little higher up in price for what I'm used to paying for a bona but overall great.

Name: Feng Cha Teahouse - San Francisco
Author: Matt B
Review: Some decent oat milk tea with boba, though their default milk is dairy-free. You order from an ipad here, and the drinks arrive fairly quickly. It’s a pretty small store, but it’s located across the street from Metreon.

Name: Feng Cha Teahouse - San Francisco
Author: Janusz Kudełka
Review:
I would avoid it for now and give it a try in a few months when they get their act together.

The wait time is unreasonable, we ordered on the tablet and people who came and ordered after us were served first. The place was only medium busy at the time. If I were to guess the problem it seemed that staff is untrained and requires a lot of supervision. We had to wait over 20 min.

Then when the order was finally ready we learned they are out of boba. Which took another 2 minutes to substitute.

The place itself looks nice and clean.

The bubble tea itself is quite decent.

Name: Feng Cha Teahouse - San Francisco
Author: Brenda Gonzalez
Review: I was staying at a hotel nearby, this place was busy as expected (I ordered online, made it easier.) The drinks were really good though, would recommend. 😌

Name: Feng Cha Teahouse - San Francisco
Author: Barbara Yang
Review:
the tapioca is very chewy and the brewed tea flavor is good. I’m really happy that they opened here!

I would avoid the Matcha latte — it’s like 2 cups of milk and 2 tablespoons of Matcha concentrate. The Matcha color itself also doesn’t look high-quality, and has little taste.

Name: Sophie's Cuppa Tea
Author: Travis Pillon
Review: I don't know how to put this any other way, but this is one of the most special places I have ever been too.  This might seem a little extreme for a tea shop; however, Sophie's Cuppa Tea is a sanctuary of peace and serenity.  The owners, Bei and John, are two of the most incredible people I have ever met and they also serve the best tea I have ever had.  I can promise everyone that they will not be disappointed if they come to this shop.   In addition to the shop, if you are looking for your next vacation, ask them about their yearly China Tea Trip.  It is a once in a life time opportunity (although I plan on going every year!).  See photos below :). BTW: They have amazing snacks and salads (fresh and organic).

Name: Sophie's Cuppa Tea
Author: Vivian Pim
Review: The best tea I’ve ever had in my life. Everything about this place is made with a lot of love. The owners are very passionate about what they do and that is clearly reflected into their business. The shop is very cozy, peaceful and cute. Love it!

Name: Sophie's Cuppa Tea
Author: Ken Ingram
Review: A spectacular inventory of tea selections with friendly and knowlegable proprietors. Best place to sample authentic tea.

Name: Sophie's Cuppa Tea
Author: Mychal Nitta
Review: Tea lover’s paradise. The people here are friendly and welcoming which is great because the amount of tea to choose from can be overwhelming. Highly recommended.

Name: Sophie's Cuppa Tea
Author: MEJ
Review: Great selection of tea and even better people. Always a pleasure coming here.

Name: Blue Willow Teaspot
Author: Valerie Hall
Review: Beautiful, lovely atmosphere. While in town I went three times. They were very accommodating despite our lack of reservations on the weekend.  Sometimes service felt slow despite it being uncrowded, but it’s the pace that is a part of the place.

Name: Blue Willow Teaspot
Author: Talia Karasinski
Review: I’m excited to come back and try a different beverage. The chai latte was pretty flavorless. This place takes tea seriously and would make a great date spot. The ambiance is warm and cozy.

Name: Blue Willow Teaspot
Author: Michelle Wang
Review:
The pastry was decent but the tea really makes up for it!
I really enjoyed being able to have a flight of tea to taste.
Definitely a one of a kind place as I don't think this kind of shop exists anywhere else.
I definitely would love to return to try out their different seasonal teas :)

Name: Blue Willow Teaspot
Author: Abby Shen
Review: The peaceful atmosphere and beautiful interior design make this place special, in addition to the unique and extensive selection of teas. I spent a few hours here with my pot of Ancient Sun tea, reading and writing. A very aesthetically pleasing experience—both the tea itself and its presentation. There are multiple electric kettles to boil more water for your tea, which I appreciated. I need to come back!

Name: Blue Willow Teaspot
Author: Bijoux Beauregard
Review: Perhaps it's worth noting that this is one of the few tea houses I've encountered that offers both a traditional tea ceremony and iced/milk teas. Blue Willow offers an impressive selection of pu'erh, as well as layered black and green teas. The white and herbal menu is nothing to scoff at, either. The array of glass and porcelain delivered to the tables might seem a tad intimidating to customers that aren't super familiar with them, but I promise the staff are courteous, friendly, and more than happy to teach patrons about steep times and pour techniques.

Name: RareTea Berkeley
Author: Alisa B.
Review:
2.5 stars! This is a boba shop on the UC Berkeley campus, so parking is a pain. The ordering system is through SnackPass and the service is very quick. The staff are friendly and give free water too if you ask! There's lots of seating inside for students to mingle and study too.

I ordered a Grapefruit Green Tea, since I wanted something refreshing to drink while walking around campus. To my surprise, it was very honeyed and tasted like syrup - even though I ordered 50% sweet. It wasn't fresh grapefruit unfortunately. The green tea taste was a bit watered down too.

Name: RareTea Berkeley
Author: Sabreen Sarah Yaqubi
Review: Boba was great, I got this slushee thing? I did it when there was a buy one get one free deal. However, it would normally be ~$8 bucks which is simply insane. They also have inaccurate  times on Snackpass, and instead of simply jacking up their times they let the notification go off that my order was ready while I waited inside. I almost missed a class T-T

Name: RareTea Berkeley
Author: Charis Zhang
Review: Beautiful store, could be cleaner. Tea was fast, ice and sweetness options are to be expected. Staff was overworked and over appreciated. Tea was watered down. I now fully support automation.

Name: RareTea Berkeley
Author: ashley
Review: honestly I liked this place better than the one in San Rafael it tasted good and the tapioca balls were softer also great friendly environment and they play korean music

Name: RareTea Berkeley
Author: Can See No
Review: I'm so glad this location let me use my own boba cup and straw. I'm always trying to reduce my waste and plastic consumption.

Name: Golden Tea Shop
Author: Jasmine Yang
Review:
I have been to pretty much every boba shop in this area and have drank close to 1000 boba drinks in my life… and this is BY FAR the best place!!! They have such an extensive menu and everything tastes AMAZING! Their boba is sweet and chewy, their crema is fresh and perfectly salted, and all their teas are brewed perfectly. They also have the best prices I have ever seen. Since my first golden tea shop drink, I have been hooked. I literally refuse to go anywhere else now. Also, they are mom and pop shop which I love!! If you’re in the area, and even if you’re not, you should definitely come. This is the place I would walk through a hurricane to get to 😍

Pictured: Cake Crème Brûlée milk tea

Name: Golden Tea Shop
Author: Mina Park
Review: Truly excellent tea and boba here. I got the Jasmine green tea with boba and Hong Kong milk tea with boba and I have to say their tea based drinks are spectacular! One of the best in the Bay Area honestly, and I’ve gone to a lot of boba and tea shops. I wish there was a Golden Tea Shop closer to where I live, it definitely would be a place I would frequent. I will come again next time I’m in Oakland and this is a spot I would recommend to my tea and boba connoisseur friends! Also the price of the boba drinks here is unbeatable for the size and quality you get! Love this spot.

Name: Golden Tea Shop
Author: Monique Vincent
Review: GO HERE. They have an absolutely astounding array of options, including dozens of things I've never seen at other bubble tea places. (E.g red bean toppings or durian sago tofu pudding). Everything is customizable and they have over a dozen toppings to choose from. They are also very dairy-free/vegan friendly (they have almond, soy, oat, and coconut milk!).

Name: Golden Tea Shop
Author: Kirat Sun
Review: Love this place! The service is really great and I really enjoyed my rose milk tea with boba. Pricing is super reasonable and they have Taiyaki!

Name: Golden Tea Shop
Author: Alex Fong
Review:
If you care about the “tea” part of milk tea, go here. Store is two parts: boba shop and loose leaf tea shop.

Boba shop: huge selection of many different teas as well as flavors/cremas etc. my favorite part is that you can ask what tea they use in the drink and they will tell you what it is. This place is a dream. Prices are reasonable (~$5-6). It’s my favorite way to try new teas.

Their loose leaf selection is enormous, particularly for tieguanyin and puer. Not to mention they do sell teapots and other tea accessories. My favorite tea is their Strong Aroma Tieguanyin, which is also the tea used in their Tieguanyin Crema in the boba shop.

Name: Foam Tea House
Author: Scott Wong
Review: Awesome boba. Must try to popping boba with your drink. We had the tarp milk tea and strawberry fruit drinks. Easy self ordering. Great service. Able to use wifi to work. Parking isn’t easy in this area.

Name: Foam Tea House
Author: Wendy Mena
Review: Love this place, I always go here, best Milk Tea in the Sunset and their spicy marinara is the best💕

Name: Foam Tea House
Author: 杨宇辰
Review: This is a very very unique milk tea shop. It can print the picture on top of your milk tea as a milk cover. The quality of the picture is very good! You should be expecting that you can see that clearly. The shop said that the material they used is FDA approved so I trust them. The process is very simple, you connect to their wifi, scan the QR code, upload the picture from your local phone, then it is done. Quick and easy.

Name: Foam Tea House
Author: chris giang
Review:
wow!!! this place does edible ink printing on foam drinks!!!! i’ve never seen anything like this.

really gimmicky but i LOVE it lol i will definitely pay more for this. going to come back and print 100 photos of my dog.

wintermelon milk foam drink - 10/10!!!
i love this drink at gongcha but it’s always so sweet and you can’t adjust sweetness level there. i got light sweetness here and it was perfect. also they give you a huge amount of foam and it’s sooo tasty!!

i’ll definitely be back and bring all my friends here
💜

Name: Foam Tea House
Author: Oscar Wong
Review: the roasted oolong and the fresh mango sago drinks were lovely

Name: Leland Tea Company - Burlingame
Author: Colleen Gordon
Review: This was a great tea place. They offer tea service of varying levels for one or more and a variety of sandwiches, salads, and pastries. They have a great selection and a wonderful staff that is happy to make recommendations. We had the blue eyes tea which was delicious and complex. I also got a blend of two of their teas to bring home, can't wait to try it. Will definitely be back.

Name: Leland Tea Company - Burlingame
Author: Lily Mirsepassi
Review: Great service and fantastic atmosphere! I loved the tea selection, and it felt like a very fun spot to grab tea and light food. It has already been mentioned in other reviews but the portion sizes are quite small, so my friend and I went somewhere else for more food after eating here. I’d still come back though, overall it was a great experience.

Name: Leland Tea Company - Burlingame
Author: Adam Whipple
Review: We had a wonderful experience at Leland Tea Co. The food was so good: very simple but excellent flavor and quality. The teas were lovely. The service was friendly. We had a party of 6 so we did 2 orders of “Tea for 3”. Everyone left full and happy. This little gem is a definite do over.

Name: Leland Tea Company - Burlingame
Author: Betty Chin
Review: My first time here and I definitely will be coming back again.  Will, the owner is such a wonderful and friendly man.  He made my friends and I feel so welcomed.   The food was wonderful and the tea was great. Thank you for such a lovely experience Will.

Name: Leland Tea Company - Burlingame
Author: Omar Ghani
Review: Not quite sure what the hype behind this place here is but my wife and I were fairly disappointed. We were hoping for a nice tea and snack experience. The China is super simple it’s not anything special. The cookies and scones feel like they from Costco. The salad also was not anything special. Very plain. The only thing I enjoyed was the cucumber cheese spread and egg salad sandwich but these are dishes you can make well at home. If you are assuming to get a British tea shop experience, this is not the place.

Name: Dartealing Lounge
Author: Karen Keegan
Review: I brought my 13 year old granddaughter here for her first proper Tea. We had wonderful service, a vegan meal was prepared for me and a typical meal was made for her. Everything was very tasty. My granddaughter loved the food so much she ate it all in a matter of minutes! She loved the hole experience, an so did I. We recommend this tea room for your future tea event!

Name: Dartealing Lounge
Author: Yan Z
Review:
Came here with a friend Saturday 1pm. The place was very full. Reservation was recommended but they let us walk-in. (Thank you!

Great atmosphere. Service is good. Attentive but not too much.

Food is good. Savory bites were somewhat creative. Sweet items were good too. My favorite was the matcha tart.

The tea was average though.

Overall good for its price. I could see this place perfect for a team building lunch/afternoon tea.

Name: Dartealing Lounge
Author: Victoria A
Review: Amazing experience! I'm a big fan of tea like this. I don't live in the SF area so this was my first time here. This is an independent tea house, not a tea in a luxury hotel like I was used to. The quality of the tea and snacks was comparable with the big fancy hotel I usually go to in my area, but at a fraction of the price. There was a huge selection of teas and they give you a lot. The tower in this pic is for 4 people and it was plenty, we even had leftovers. The pots are one per person. It was street parking only and in an area with a sports stadium so keep in mind when making a reservation the area could be crowded due to a game. We did find parking a few blocks away.

Name: Dartealing Lounge
Author: Rocio G
Review:
Visited 4/28 before a Giants game. This place made the whole street corner smell good as we waited for the doors to open. Their reservation process sounds more complicated than it was. Once I filled out the form on their website, the rest was done via text and my reservation was confirmed within minutes. Service was awesome! It's a prix fixe menu so you just need to tell them the tea that you'd like. I opted to pay an extra $5.25 to have their memories of Saigon blooming tea. It comes in a glass tea pot and opens as it sits in the water. Tea was good but the blooming part (really slow bloom) sounded cooler than it actually was so I'll stick to one of the cheaper teas next time. Warm scones and cream were my favorite part!

They don't have a parking lot but metered street parking on Stillman St was super easy to find. It's limited to an hour though. Our tea time visit was an hour and 15 min.

Name: Dartealing Lounge
Author: Ur Brt
Review: The Owners and staff are very knowledgeable about tea. They easily crafted an experience that you will remember for a life time with their approach, sandwiches, snacks, and tea selections. I'm living in Arizona and plan to order tea to support and be sure to return when I'm in the area. Thanks Keith and everyone at Dartealing. You're all awesome.

Name: Malaya Tea Room
Author: Nicole Paulk
Review: Absolutely beautiful experience at Malaya Tea Room! We were a party of 6 and everyone enjoyed their tea and food! They have very unique flavors that are so fun! All of the sandwiches were delicious!!

Name: Malaya Tea Room
Author: Alyssa Siddiqui
Review:
Afternoon tea at Malaya was a fun and different experience. Asian meets western in this fusion afternoon tea experience.

I loved all of the flavorful tea sandwiches which you wouldn’t be able to try at your traditional tea services.

They had an array of sides and desserts and the tea was delicious.

You have to make reservations which is standard for most afternoon teas and I would recommend going here.
Parking is a bit difficult as it is all street parking and there is no private parking lot.

Name: Malaya Tea Room
Author: Jodessa Capri
Review: What a fun little find! Offers a different spin on traditional tea service. The tea selection is delicious,  although be aware if you get the Malaysian Pulled Tea, you get ONE glass (although,  it's so caffeinated you wouldn't want more). The Malaysian menu was fabulous with a lot of sweet flavors. You MUST make a reservation and the staff is very hands off...you ring a bell on the table for service (which is perfectly fine). I will definitely come back.

Name: Malaya Tea Room
Author: Priskilletz
Review: This was a nice experience! I loved the selection of  food and tea available, and the service was top-notch. It's a great place to go if you've never experienced a tea hour before and it's not too expensive like other places. Very kid friendly, but I do recommend 7 and up as it is a very small and quiet shop. Each person gets their own individual tea pot! (PICTURED: Sandwich flavors- Curry Chicken, Cucumber, Turkey and Fig, Smoked Salmon. Tea- Butterfly Pea Flower)

Name: Malaya Tea Room
Author: Tiffany Phu
Review:
Absolutely lovely experience! I’ve been to many tea houses and this one was unique and especially the service was attentive and quick.

They specialize in Malaysian style but also have the traditional tea treats. We enjoyed the chicken curry sandwich, salmon. Favorite tea was the caramel infused as well as the peach mango.

Loved that we got to choose a tea each person as some houses only a few to share with the table!

The place didn’t seem to have private rooms that we saw but our group of 5 sat next to the window and felt it was private enough while still part of the wider party. Will be back!

Name: Pink Pink Tea Shoppe
Author: Austin Pachano
Review:
My drink was super good. They had it ready in just a few minutes after I ordered. I ordered in person but they also have a self-order kiosk.

The location is in the mall with limited seating but this was great to carry around. Most stores at the mall don't allow drinks in, however they have tables near the front to set the drink down while you browse.

Name: Pink Pink Tea Shoppe
Author: Lorcan O'Regan
Review: Really nice food and amazing chefs. The wait staff throughout looked after our table of 12. Had the steak & prawns. Everything delicious.

Name: Pink Pink Tea Shoppe
Author: Ivan Martinez
Review: Stopped by Japan Town for a limited edition Pink Peach Milk treat. The boba pearls were an added plus. 😋

Name: Pink Pink Tea Shoppe
Author: Chasity Norton
Review: Refreshing lychee grape, half sweet, no cheese foam, with lychee and honey boba. Ordered on kiosk and ready in a couple minutes. The half sweet was perfect.

Name: Pink Pink Tea Shoppe
Author: JP G
Review: We ordered the mango pudding, they call it pinko-nut. It comes in a glass jar with a lid that is really nice and reusable. A really good portion and delicious dessert.

Name: Lovey's Tea Shoppe
Author: Maya Osweiler
Review: Literally the cutest little place ever!!! I’m enjoying tea and sandwiches and scones with my sister right now and it’s amazing! Such sweet and attentive workers and delicious tea and food. So much variety, even chocolate tea which I rarely find a truly good version of! Love it here so much and can’t wait to come back!!

Name: Lovey's Tea Shoppe
Author: Grace Nicolas -Kim
Review: I loved everything about this tea place. Me and my few girl friends came on Friday for high tea and everything was perfect . We each got different types of tea . I got white peach which was amazing with unlimited refill . The sandwiches were delicious and so as scones.  Service was great , the ladies were attentive and friendly .  We loved the little shop inside where they sell cutest tea pots, cups , teas , scones etc . I ended up getting lychee rose green tea to enjoy at home .

Name: Lovey's Tea Shoppe
Author: Amy Higgins
Review: Lovely lil tea shop that’s in an unexpected location. They have an outdoor seating space as well as a small inside spot. The sandwiches were nice but nothing special. However, the scones are to die for!!! We ordered more considering they were so good!!!

Name: Lovey's Tea Shoppe
Author: lorissa kelkar
Review: I’ve been to Lovey’s many times for their lovely scones and amazing chocolate truffles and petit fours…this was the first time having their afternoon tea. It was wonderful with attentive, friendly service, ensuring my pot of tea was kept hot and providing a considerate pace of the courses offered in a relaxed atmosphere. I had to purchase some chocolate truffles to go, of course!

Name: Lovey's Tea Shoppe
Author: Osbaldo Martinez
Review: Lovey's Tea Shoppe is a great tea house. I loved the atmosphere, it's so charming and lovely. The tea is fantastic, the scones were so delicious, the sandwiches were also really good. I came here with my friends and we had an amazing time. Everyone is so friendly and welcoming!!

Name: Lovejoy's Tea Room
Author: Katie Booth
Review: Wow! High tea has been a on my bucket list for awhile and Lovejoy was beyond my wildest dreams!! The atmosphere so unique and quirky. The food was so yummy with really fun sandwich options. We had the best time ever.

Name: Lovejoy's Tea Room
Author: Miranda Sequoia
Review:
A fun and whimsical place for a high tea. Dress up encouraged, but not required. A great option for a kids birthday or baby shower. Service and food were excellent.

Conveniently located at Church and Clipper stop on the J metro line.

Name: Lovejoy's Tea Room
Author: Alex Weis
Review:
This tea room is super cute and the atmosphere is amazing.  The servers are very friendly and helpful.  We got the light tea service, and the tea + snacks were yummy.  I learned that I'm not a huge fan of tea sandwiches, but that's not their fault.  They have heavier meal options as well if you're more hungry.  I would definitely bring a friend here for a birthday or a daytime celebration.
If they're fully booked, get on their notify waitlist--we were on their list and we ended up getting 2 notifications for tables that opened up last minute!

Name: Lovejoy's Tea Room
Author: Eri G
Review: Amazing !! Definitely recommend.  Very grounded, fast, and relaxed (yet busy) atmosphere.  Great way to spend teatime to zen or w/ a small group of friends to catch up. Beautiful antiques to take in and multiple seating options

Name: Lovejoy's Tea Room
Author: Osbaldo Martinez
Review: Lovejoy's Tea Room was an amazing experience. I enjoyed the sandwiches, the scones, and the tea. I had so much fun talking with my friends while enjoying tea. The place is decorated so well that it fits with the whole aesthetic of high tea. Although it's a bit on the pricey end, it was a wonderful experience. The staff are so helpful and friendly.

Name: Asha Tea House
Author: Alastor Marsh
Review: I enjoy the sparkling strawberry drink, its a great place to hang out, make friends, i see couples talking, honestly overall a Japanese Cafe like experience! I recommend those that want matcha, milk, or sparkling drink! You wont regret it!

Name: Asha Tea House
Author: Victor Wang
Review: Really enjoyed their ceremonial grade organic matcha latte with oat milk. Super creamy and delicious. Quick to order and fast to come out as well. Limited seating but we were able to find 3 seats on Tuesday afternoon without too much issue.

Name: Asha Tea House
Author: Jonathan Luu
Review:
I think this is one of the few times that a boba shop says “lightly” sweeten and it’s actually not that sweet. I got 100% sweetness and this might be 50% sweetness at other places. This lets the tea really shine through which is something i really like. They have more traditional teas as well so would recommend. I got a matcha cookie and this was excellent as well.

There are a few chairs and table here so it’s good spot to work.

Name: Asha Tea House
Author: S Sum
Review: Came at noon on a monday and there was no line, both of the staff working greeted me immediately. I got the strawberry matcha latte and it was delicious. I could distinctly taste the matcha and the taste wasn't covered by the milk, the strawberry jam added a nice sweetness. I also got the guava danish and matcha white chocolate cookie.

Name: Asha Tea House
Author: Lydia Cheng
Review: Was in the area and needed a caffeine hit and stumbled across Asha Tea House. There are a lot of tea focused options here and less coffee options. The interior was well decorated - cozy and inviting vibes. I got the matcha latte and added blueberry to it (seasonal fruit option) and it was delicious. I like my drinks on the less sweet side and this was perfect. Will definitely be back to try their other options!

Name: Honey B
Author: Stephanie Ngui
Review: Nice snacks and tea. The herbal jelly drink is great and the popcorn chicken is nice. The portions are big. There are no bathrooms. They have limited indoor seating. Food and drinks are prepared promptly but gets busy after school.

Name: Honey B
Author: Jeff Ho
Review: We stopped by Honey B this afternoon to get a quick snack. It took about 5 minutes for them to prepare my popcorn chicken. It tasted great without the need for chili powder, hot sauce or ranch sauce. Some items like the fried squid advertised on the front window were not available. The cashier said all the items on the menu at the front table are available. Next time, I'll come back and try some of their boba drinks.

Name: Honey B
Author: Julie Taeko
Review: Very cute little store with great safety measures for employees. And the staff are very helpful with providing directions and ideas for their drinks. I tried the grapefruit honey hot tea which was great because it was super cold that day. I asked for 25% sweetness so it was less flavorful but still very refreshing! It was also nice to see you can Venmo the store to pay as well.

Name: Honey B
Author: Jerry Liu
Review: Very good boba, and the popcorn chicken here is definitely a highlight. Well worth the money.

Name: Honey B
Author: Sapph Nor
Review:
In late 2020, I first visited this place and my friend had a diarrhea after drinking their boba tea. I have since returned to this location a couple more times with friends who really like Honey B. The teas remain my least favorite--hardly any tea flavor and boba always undercooked. I also dislike that their regular-size cups (for cold tea) don't fit into the cup holders in the car.

My rating went from 1-star to 2-star, because some of their snacks are pretty good (waffle fries and fried tofu highly recommended). The popcorn chicken is also good, but almost always without a trace of basil in it. I never ordered their eggettes again after trying it once, when it was folded a couple of times and squeezed into a tiny bag (it was all soggy when I took it out of the bag to eat it a few minutes later).

In my most recent visit, I noticed that they don't give out receipts. So I requested one, only to find that they put a $0.25 bag charge although we didn't ask for a bag. Nowhere on the menu indicates that there would be a $0.50 extra charge if you ask for "no ice" on the tea. When we placed the order, the cashier didn't tell us about the extra charge either. How tricky!

If you are a tea lover, avoid this place.

Name: Vital Tea Leaf
Author: Kaitlin V
Review: I had an absolutely amazing experience at this location! The staff were incredible, especially the girl who helped me—she was extremely sweet, friendly, and knowledgeable. She did a fantastic job recommending different teas based on what I was looking for, making the entire experience both welcoming and educational! The service was phenomenal, and the tea selection was extensive. The free tea sampling really enhanced the experience, allowing me to choose the perfect teas. I will definitely be coming back! I bought the Blue People Ginseng Oolong, and it was absolutely delicious!

Name: Vital Tea Leaf
Author: B. H.
Review: Amazing shop! Wonderful owner...great tasting! We were able to try four (4) different kinds of teas and received a thorough explanation of the beneficial properties of each of them as well as directions for how to make a proper cup. Friendly, helpful and enjoyable experience!

Name: Vital Tea Leaf
Author: Mazal Shavratov
Review: One of the most wonderful experiences I had in San Francisco. We came in at the quite early time in the day on Saturday. The owner was doing a presentation about different teas. He spent a whole hour showcasing and explaining black, green, white tea and the importance of them. I learned a lot of interesting facts and he is very entertaining. I very much recommend going there if you are in Chinatown. After an hour of this presentation I was able to buy some of the teas we tried and the owner was kind enough to show us a wonderful lunch spot to try close by. Overall very highly recommend. Will go again!

Name: Vital Tea Leaf
Author: Morgan Jenkins
Review: Free tea tasting is an absolute blast! The main employee is very funny but informative as well. We enjoyed our ~20 minute tasting and all walked away with a bag or two of newly purchased loose leaf tea to enjoy.

Name: Vital Tea Leaf
Author: Alyssa McGowan
Review: This place is every tea 🍵 fans dream! They offer free tea tasting paired with information about each tea and its benefits. The young woman conducting the tea tasting is super knowledgeable of all the tea’s properties and how they help all different kinds of ailments. My fiancé has high blood pressure and trouble sleeping. When asked what teas would help these issues, she jumped into action and gave recommendations followed up by a taste of those suggested teas. Did you know you’re supposed to wash your tea leaves before steeping to get rid of dust and grime? Me either! But now we do and it will become an unconscious practice when returning home. Very friendly staff and wide variety of tea. A must try if you’re ever on Grant in SF. 10 stars!

Name: Plentea
Author: Nina Timofeyeva
Review: Cool "plant" milk tea offerings. Variety of flavors of drinks made with quality ingredients. Teas have deep flavor and boba is fresh. They offer drinks in glass containers which you can re-use when you bring them back to the shop.

Name: Plentea
Author: LeiLani Bertram
Review: Fantastic! The service was wonderful these ladies are so nice and polite.  Drinks are fresh and hit the spot.  There is room to sit and sip or of course to just take on the go as well.

Name: Plentea
Author: Edison Huang
Review:
One of my go to's whenever I'm in the area! Highly recommend their grape fruit tea with sea salt crema (blueberry is my second choice)! The tea flavors here are better than the other boba shops. I still opt for 0% sugar, but y'all may want to go a little bit higher.

Sustainability is one of their core values. They offer glass bottles at the same price. You can get 10% off your drink if you reuse it!

Name: Plentea
Author: James
Review:
Hands-down, one of the best places to get milk tea in San Frnacisco, especailly for their sustainability.

All their teas come in reusable glass bottles that you can keep and compared to other ilk tea places, the prices are about the same if not sometimes cheaper.

You can order at the small tablet/kiosk or with one of the workers.

There is also a lovely dining space upstairs and a few chairs downstairs.

Only downside is that they use plastic straws.

Name: Plentea
Author: ayush kalani
Review:
Ideally, I'd give it a 2.5/5.

Plentea has a charming atmosphere. The shop itself is small but inviting, with terrariums lining the walls, adding a touch of nature. They offer a wide variety of drinks, from classic milk teas to fresh fruit options and even smoothies. You can also choose between getting your drink in a reusable glass bottle or a standard plastic cup, which is a nice eco-friendly touch.

Unfortunately, my experience with the actual drink fell a bit short. I opted for the Thai tea, a classic boba flavor. While the presentation was nice, the taste was a little disappointing. The tea itself was quite bitter, and there was a slightly chalky aftertaste.

Overall, Plentea is a cute spot with good intentions.  However, based on my experience with Thai tea, the flavor execution needs some work. If you're looking for a trendy boba shop with a wide variety of options, Plentea might be worth a try. But if you're a boba purist seeking the perfect cup, you might want to keep exploring.

Name: The Fate Tea House
Author: Anna
Review: Super cute boba shop! The interior is spacious with plenty of seating and beautiful floral decor. They have so many choices of drinks and also serve hot snacks and desserts! Great spot to hang out with friends or catch up on work. Also a great photo spot for taking Instagram photos! There's also a cool press-on nail vending machine by the entrance with a lot of cute designs. Worth taking a look at!

Name: The Fate Tea House
Author: Jeff Ho
Review: Fancy and beautifully decorated. Not your average boba/desert restaurant. This used to be another boba shop, but that one went defunct. The weird thing is, Fate Tea uses the same cups as the defunct boba shop. Popcorn chicken was not available as I walked in right when they were closing at 7pm. I usually get a popcorn chicken when I get a milk tea. Their iced milk tea $6 can be described as lacking good tea flavor with too much milk. I couldn't tell if it was black tea or jasmine tea. The redeeming factor was, it did hit the spot and quite refreshing. Lady cashier/barista was fast and cool. Good job.

Name: The Fate Tea House
Author: Eli
Review: The Fate Tea House is my go-to little treat spot! the service is always top notch, and i'm always pleasantly suprised how delicious and light their sweets are. my go to is the rose crepe cake but literary everything I've tried is lightly sweet with delecate flavors. their bubble tea is always spot-on, too. the cups are sturdy and the tea is always strong, never bitter.

Name: The Fate Tea House
Author: ashley
Review: Such a cute place doesn’t look huge from outside but the space is beautifully decorated and gives a warm atmosphere, the staff was nice and quick with the drink, they had such unique drinks and they looked good as well but I stuck with a mango jasmine milk tea since it’s my favorite,, 10/10 so cute!💕

Name: The Fate Tea House
Author: Michael Lee DS
Review: Pleasant atmosphere.  Very cute.  Delicious snacks & drinks!

Name: Plentea
Author: Sid Shah
Review: Great service, the employee I met was super chill and the matcha latte I had was pretty good. The glass bottles make an amazing presentation and have the reusable quality to them. Pretty well priced as well.

Name: Plentea
Author: Jared Strand
Review: Such a fun an unique boba spot! Their drinks are fantastic and the food is very well prepared. Bonus Tip: Mix the Sriracha and Mayo together for a delicious tempura dip with a kick!

Name: Plentea
Author: Ed Shelden
Review:
Great Ice Tea with Boba. Very friendly staff that are trying hard to serve you. You can get your tea in a plastic cup or a glass bottle. The glass bottle is great for home use. There is parking in the garage above (free 1 hr.)
Pleantea accepts credit cards and CASH!
Please give them a little break on service as this is a small business in a restart mode.

Name: Plentea
Author: Wesley
Review: The tea here was super good! We also love the glass bottles and use them at home! The staff were really nice and quick. The space for customer to wait and order seems a little crowded but that’s totally fine with the quick service. :)

Name: Plentea
Author: David Yang
Review: Sharp, broken glass chip in boba cut my mouth and now I am bleeding. I don't know how this even happens - I should really not be having to even think about glass chips in my drink. I used the plastic straw they have in store, and this chip seems to be from *within* the bottle. The servers are super nice, and I love this place, but this kind of scarred me.

Name: Sip Tea Room
Author: Jasmin Holguin
Review: The Sip Tea Room is an absolutely stunning/delightful place to spend time with family, friends, acquaintances, or even just yourself. Such a great atmosphere! Highly recommended!

Name: Sip Tea Room
Author: Katelyn Cohen
Review: So, TikTok kept showing me videos of the tea lady reviewing all her afternoon tea places and I suddenly NEEDED a tea time. After a quick search I found this place right near the Bnb where I was staying. This was perfect. The food was excellent and SO fresh- sandwiches, scones, shortbread. Sometimes at tea shops you can tell the sandwiches have sat around for a few hours, but not here. The tea options were unique and delicious, I tried the rose. Lovely atmosphere and waitstaff. This was just perfect after a walk in the park. Wish I lived closer!

Name: Sip Tea Room
Author: Manny
Review: Such a wonderful experience. We came here for my birthday brunch and I was blown away. The menu was delicious I was so happy they had such amazing gluten free options. The service and staff were incredibly wonderful and friendly. I would definitely recommend visiting.

Name: Sip Tea Room
Author: Stephanie
Review:
A cute and quirky place for afternoon tea right next to Golden Gate Park.

We had their full Traditional Afternoon Tea Experience. Their sausage rolls were my favourite - little morsels of deliciousness. The sandwiches were alright. They were a bit too thin and slightly on the dry side. I loved their lemon curd though.

For my tea selection, I had the Blue Malva Flower Earl Grey. A nice Earl Grey that’s floral forward.

The parking situation can be a problem, especially on the weekends so give yourself extra time. We ended up parking at the parking garage inside of Golden Gate Park, which has its own traffic issues but that’s another tale. It’s a great day out to have afternoon tea and visit the museums though!

Name: Sip Tea Room
Author: Michelle Barnes
Review:
Wonderful afternoon tea experience right next to Golden Gate park! The only downside is parking, which is nonexistent on weekends. I had reserved a 1pm seating on a Saturday. You do have to pay in full ahead of time, including tip, which I'm generally not a fan of tipping prior to receiving any service. We arrived 30 minutes late as we spent 25 mins looking for parking and 15 mins walking over. Luckily the staff were accommodating and allowed us to stay until closing rather than rushing us out the door after our 1.5 hour seating time was up.

After selecting our teas, china rose petal and green jasmine, they set out a plate of delicious baked sausage rolls. We were then served the 3 tier tray, which includes 4 sandwiches, small salad, a traditional and coconut scone with lemon curd, clotted cream, and jam, and a dessert tray 3 small treats and fresh fruit.

The scones were the highlight! Light, flaky, and perfect with the lemon curd and clotted cream. Their Hawaiian sandwich was a surprise as well along with the parfait dessert. We unfortunately weren't fans of the cookie, which was quite dry.

Name: Imperial Tea Court
Author: Megan Y
Review: Our best dining experience in SF so far. We had 3 different teas and had a lovely tea ceremony experience with the manager explaining each tea, how long to steep it and what sort of taste to expect. We also had a sampler of spring rolls and shu mai plus a yummy pork bun and sticky rice. Very enjoyable.

Name: Imperial Tea Court
Author: Kelly Burton
Review:
This place is THE place for dumplings and, tea, and (my favorite) hand pulled noodles.
The hand pulled noodles, which we got with pork, were so fresh and chewy and umami. I am a chef at an Italian restaurant and I know my way around fresh pasta. If you're a noodle fan, you are in the right place.
Liked being able to, as a table of three, get a sampler of dumplings so we could all try some of each for a very reasonable price.
The atmosphere is super nice and very relaxing and service was great. It's a must if you are going to the Ferry building.

Name: Imperial Tea Court
Author: Sana Lakhani
Review: The employee that always takes care of us is really nice and attentive. We always come here for tea and they’re all so delicious! It’s a really cool and unique spot with lots of seating and space. Highly recommend!

Name: Imperial Tea Court
Author: Matthew Ecclestone
Review: Outstanding food and tea here, surprisingly as good or better than what we've been sampling in Chinatown proper. It was the best green tea we've had, even 3 steeps in with the table service, and the hand-pulled noodles with vegetables could not have been more perfect.

Name: Imperial Tea Court
Author: Ken Wong
Review:
12-4-21:
Came here for lunch on a Saturday. Place was crowded. Happy that they checked my vaccination card 👍.

Ordered an Anxi Tie Guan Yie oolong tea to start and was treated with a show by the server as she rinsed and prepared my tea. I really liked my tea and took some dry leaves to go!

Ate a noodle soup dish, char sui bow, and sticky rice wrap!

Everything was really good. I will definitely be back again for more delicious food!

Name: Aroma Tea Shop
Author: Neal
Review:
Aromatic teas and great service make this place worth visiting.

The tea here is expensive, but it smells amazing. It's rarely busy and you can buy tea gift boxes, teacup sets, and t-shirts in addition to loose leaf teas. If you're very serious about tea, you can get fancy teapots and teacups here. If you're unsure of what you want, just ask whoever's working. I've never felt rushed and service has always been pleasant. The lychee black tea smelled incredible and tasted good too.

Name: Aroma Tea Shop
Author: Alissa Nolan
Review: A great tea shop find in San Francisco! We stumbled across Aroma Tea Shop while looking for a nearby bakery and what a find. There are a huge number of loose leave tea choices, sold by the ounce. The owner was super knowlegeable and happy to brew us a few samples. The Lychee Strawberry is a great cold brew Ice Tea and the Vanilla Black tea is my current favorite. The real find of the day was the Earl Grey Chocolate bar, I can't wait to go back and get that again.

Name: Aroma Tea Shop
Author: Christian Lee
Review: Aroma Tea shop has a great selection of teas, and they also have multiple locations. However, they are sadly not doing tea tastings at the moment.

Name: Aroma Tea Shop
Author: Lilla Sz
Review: One of the best tea shop experiences in my life. Huge plus to Homer (I hope I got that right) who gave us tasting samples and was very knowledgeable but also very welcoming.

Name: Aroma Tea Shop
Author: Kenny Bourgon
Review: Great tea selection. Owner is a good dude and knows his stuff. Very welcoming to beginners.

Name: Hi Tea
Author: Ziling Mai
Review: i actually ordered drinks as well but completely forgot to take pics of that but here chickens…lol. the chicken wings came with a decent amount. good flavor and delicious i would get it again. now as for drinks i got the “Fruit Party” and a “Lychee Slush” now surprisingly THEY ACTUALLY USE FRESH FRUITS. for the fruit party it was nice and refreshing, the drink was some type of green tea and you can eat the fruits after your drink. ok next one ik super surprised, they actually used lychee in the slush. not just syrup which i’m sure they also added, but i saw a cup with whole lychees in it. they r W for that

Name: Hi Tea
Author: Amina Choudhery
Review: The staff was very rude at the time of order and charged me extra. I ordered  Nutella strawberry milk tea. I can't lie it's the worst Boba drink I have ever tried. The chocolate was fake no Nutella and the strawberry was not fresh or artificial flavor. It tasted like sour milk with cheap chocolate on sides.  I won't recommend it.

Name: Hi Tea
Author: Ella Hou
Review: Very good experience but you can taste quality

Name: Hi Tea
Author: LHZ
Review: They’re open - good stuff! The mango pomelo is a bit pricy, but hard to find these types of drinks around San Bruno.

Name: Hi Tea
Author: C.V de leon
Review: Delicious appetizers, and of course bobas, nice and quiet place, we going back again !!!!!

Name: TOMO Tea House
Author: Kathleen Valerio
Review: Stopped in for a sweet treat and tea after dinner at Yo Sushi.  The decor is super fun. Self serve order kiosks feel very trendy.  Very easy to customize orders and pay by card. We also saw people walk in and order at the counter, presumably they accept cash as well.  Donuts are fun and tasty.  Background music is very eclectic,  several different genres and decades covered while we were there. Lots of pink and positive messaging.  Staff was willing to help us take a family photo by the round swing chair.

Name: TOMO Tea House
Author: Julia Gidwani
Review: Tomo is a sweet spot! Their mochi donuts are absolutely delicious—crispy on the outside, soft and chewy on the inside, with a variety of fun flavors. The drinks are equally yummy, with lots of options to choose from. What really sets them apart is their free kids' play area, which is a nice bonus if you have little ones. You just need to sign a waiver, and they can burn off some energy while you enjoy your treats. Overall, a fantastic place for a snack, drink, and some family-friendly fun!

Name: TOMO Tea House
Author: Edison Huang
Review: Seems like this used to be a Bober in the past cuz everything is hella pink. Pretty clean establishment. The drinks are alright. Slightly disappointed they don't offer no ice option for their classic milk tea options. Oh, they also have mochi donuts.

Name: TOMO Tea House
Author: Alison Enthoven
Review: I love getting milk tea and mochi donuts at Bober Tea. The tea is flavorful and high-quality, the stir-fried boba pearls have the perfect texture, and the mochi donuts are dangerous. They have a deal on Tuesdays when the donuts are $1 each when you purchase a drink, and they have an indoor play area for $5 per kid. The vibe is really fun though it does feel a bit gimmicky... but that just makes it more fun for the kids.

Name: TOMO Tea House
Author: Karen Liu
Review: Great atmosphere for instagram worthy pics. Drinks and mochi donuts are really good 😋wish we could adjust the sweetness of our drinks though

Name: Japanese Tea House
Address: 75 Hagiwara Tea Garden Dr, San Francisco
Author: Matt B
Review:
Lovely little tea house located in the heart of the Japanese Tea Garden. While they don’t have the most extensive menu, and are a bit pricier, I really enjoyed my lunch here!

The udon noodles, chicken karaage, and jasmine tea were all perfect to enjoy in the peaceful park-based patio.

Name: Japanese Tea House
Address: 75 Hagiwara Tea Garden Dr, San Francisco
Author: Bao Nguyen
Review: The ambiance is tranquil, with traditional tatami mats, low tables, and sliding paper doors. You can enjoy authentic Japanese tea ceremonies, where matcha is prepared with precision and care. The setting is often complemented by beautiful gardens, adding to the peaceful atmosphere.

Name: Japanese Tea House
Address: 75 Hagiwara Tea Garden Dr, San Francisco
Author: Jordan Asuncion
Review:
Loved the ambiance and scenery, however the food was not it. Disappointed in the udon and karrage chicken. Both tasted like they were heated up and were not fresh.

The miso soup and tea was good though. Will come back for the scenery but not the food.

Name: Japanese Tea House
Address: 75 Hagiwara Tea Garden Dr, San Francisco
Author: Trupti S
Review: Besutiful and serene place. Perfect to just go and relax amongst nature….

Name: Japanese Tea House
Address: 75 Hagiwara Tea Garden Dr, San Francisco
Author: Priyanka Navgire
Review: This tea house was a highlight of our visit to Japanese tea garden. We indulged in some delicious chicken nuggets and mochi ice cream that was simply divine. We tried Genmaicha and Hojicha in tea, enhancing our overall experience with their exquisite flavors. The tea house is a perfect spot to relax and savor the tastes of the garden after strolling through its beautiful landscapes

Name: Pink Cloud Tea & More
Author: Alyssa
Review: Food was super tasty and the egg toast sandwich was super full. The Super Lemon Tea is very good too. The individuals working there made everything fresh and were nice and friendly the entire time. I will definitely be back.

Name: Pink Cloud Tea & More
Author: Claudia Barrera
Review: I went for the first time recently because I love trying new boba spots, and it was really good! I didn’t get a chance to try the food, but I got the passion fruit green tea with brown sugar boba and lychee jelly, and it was super refreshing! The aesthetic of the place is really cute as well! Definitely coming back to try more drinks!

Name: Pink Cloud Tea & More
Author: Max Mendel
Review: My favorite boba place in Berkeley! Love the drinks and Jianbing! The workers are also always super friendly and nice. Highly recommend!

Name: Pink Cloud Tea & More
Author: Michelle Park
Review:
You must try the main chicks! The toast itself is crusty and good on top of overall deliciousness:) reminds me of Issac Toast in Korea

There is a diverse choice of menu and toppings like strawberry popping boba to choose from:) I usually try both milky and fruity drinks at boba shops and I really enjoyed both at pink cloud! The BEST boba at Berkeley for sure. The staffs are very kind too

Name: Pink Cloud Tea & More
Author: paper towel
Review:
The 'main chicks' are soooo good! i tried the cheesy ham and the royal king sandwiches and both do not disappoint. the bread is sooo flavorful and garlicky and the egg is so fluffy. be warned that the sandwich can be very messy to eat like a normal sandwich - there is so much egg in the sandwich it can fall out, so it comes with a fork. it's a big sandwich too.

the boba is OK, not the best but still up there, and you get a bigger size for the same price of some worse places in Berkeley. the tea tastes a little watered down and i wish the boba was sweeter, but it still goes great with the sandwich. the 'special drinks' are the main highlight.

staff is very nice, decor is cute and pink.

i hope this place never stops making sandwiches!!

Update 5/3/24 I don't know if i've changed or this place has changed their sourcing but the boba is good and sweeter now. :) I prefer the milk teas.

Name: Red Blossom Tea Company
Author: Yoomi
Review: What an incredible tea showroom! I first discovered this brand during a tasting at Opus One winery. They’ve partnered with renowned Michelin-starred restaurants in the Bay Area, including Benu, Atelier Crenn, and The French Laundry.

Name: Red Blossom Tea Company
Author: ayush kalani
Review:
Red Blossom Tea in San Francisco's Chinatown is a must-visit for any tea lover. This charming little shop isn't just a place to buy loose leaf tea and teaware; it's a gateway to a world of flavors and aromas. Red Tea Blossom ships their teas to restaurants as well as corporate offices, and after speaking with the owner, I can also confidently say that this place is all about sharing their passion for this ancient beverage.

We walked in on a Saturday afternoon and were surprised by the tranquility. Unlike the usual Chinatown bustle, the shop offered a calm, inviting atmosphere. The display shelves were lined with an impressive selection of teas, each with a label highlighting its origin, flavor profile, and price. A color-coded system made navigating easy: the red labels indicated their premium collection, while white labels marked the more budget-friendly options.

Our exploration began even before we reached the shelves. We were welcomed with a complimentary cup of refreshing cold-brewed osmanthus tea, a delightful introduction to what awaited us.

The in-store tea tasting is a fantastic feature, and while reservations aren't accepted, there was no wait on our visit. We learned that tastings are done standing-room only and conclude by 4:30 pm, accommodating up to four guests at a time. Though advertised as 5-10 minutes, our session turned into a delightful hour and twenty minutes, thanks to our incredible host.

Their tasting fee is very reasonable, with Red Label teas priced at $18 and others at $12 per sample. Our tasting began with three our three selections: a Dragonwell green tea, an Ao Fu Hou phoenix oolong, and a wild leaf Menghai Sheng Pu-erh from 2003.

The Dragonwell, crafted in Pan'an County, China, was a revelation. As described, it was smooth, buttery, and lingered pleasantly on the palate with a subtle corn aftertaste, likely due to the pan-frying process.

The Ao Fu Hou, on the other hand, was a symphony of apricot, honey, and warm spices. This oolong was a true showcase of quality, with a long, layered finish.

The wild leaf Menghai Sheng Pu-erh, hailing from Menghai County, Yunnan, offered a distinct contrast. Its earthy and camphor notes developed richness with longer steeping, culminating in a smooth, almost stone-fruity character.

But the journey didn't end there. After trying 3, we decided to try one more - Fu Shou Shan oolong from Taiwan. This delightful tea unfolded with each steep, revealing an initial sweetness of sugarcane and cream, followed by complex floral and tropical notes. The finish was rich and lingered pleasantly.

Throughout the tasting, the aroma of the teas was as captivating as their flavors. Each steep, from hot to lukewarm to even cold, offered a unique flavor experience. It was a testament to the quality of the teas and the expertise of our host in guiding us through the process.

Our host's passion and knowledge truly elevated the experience. She wasn't just pouring water; she was sharing a story about each tea's origin, how it's grown, and how geography influences the harvest. Her enthusiasm was infectious, and her willingness to share a personal favorite tea with us (free of  charge) was a heartwarming touch.

While we admittedly drank quite a bit of tea that afternoon, the Ao Fu Hou oolong emerged as the clear crowd favorite. The wild leaf Menghai Sheng Pu-erh was undeniably strong, best suited for seasoned tea drinkers.

I highly recommend this place and especially their in person tea tasting.

Name: Red Blossom Tea Company
Author: Matee Mana
Review:
Wonderful family-owned tea shop in historic San Francisco China Town. I’ve been a happy customer for over a decade now! They’ve been one of my local go-to vendors for high-quality tea and artisanal tea ware, though the latter tends to be priced on the higher side. Not a knock by any means, as the wares I purchased are some of the best pieces I own.

I’ve enjoyed their excellent Tieguanyin, Dragon Peral Jasmine, several Formosa Oolongs, and Three Cultivar Red, all teas I like to keep on hand as my tea budget allows.

Their in store environment (and the neighborhood) has changed a lot since 2020, by necessity, of course. I do miss lifting the lids of the tea jars and sampling the scents of the different teas, but I agree with the decision to keep everything pre-packed. Their online shop makes things very convenient, but I do recommend visiting the physical shop if you find yourself in the area. Looking forward to my next tea restock visit!

Name: Red Blossom Tea Company
Author: Claire Johnson
Review: Excellent selection of teas and I appreciated being able to sample some before purchasing. Friendly customer service.

Name: Red Blossom Tea Company
Author: Eli W. Hunter
Review:
Very friendly and knowledgeable staff with a good selection of Chinese teas. The tasting is a little expensive but they give you so much tea and tell you so much about brewing methods, tasting notes, and how & where they select their teas that it's well worth it. A must visit for tea lovers.

Also there's absolutely no language barrier which is very comforting for someone whose Chinese is as bad as mine.

Name: RareTea Oakland
Author: My Hoa N.
Review: Checked out this little spot in Rockridge. Decent place for boba tea. Popcornb chicken wasn't my favorite, the batter was thicker than what I was used to. Friendly staff and quick service.

Name: RareTea Oakland
Author: Annalise Aldin
Review: Delicious fruit tea, you can tell they use real fruit and juice not artificial flavor. The musubi are made to order not prepacked too.

Name: RareTea Oakland
Author: Rica Carlos
Review: The boba milk tea is delicious! Lots of choices in drinks and food. My son loves their popcorn chicken.

Name: RareTea Oakland
Author: Mychal Nitta
Review: We order from here frequently on UberEats.  One of our favorites to treat ourselves on a tough day or surprise for my wife.  We have tried an assortment of drinks from here, but always add lychee jelly.  The musubi are usually solid too

Name: RareTea Oakland
Author: Heidi Schimpf
Review: Stopped here on a whim one day and have been craving it and coming back ever since! They put real blended fruit into many of their bubble teas and drinks which is refreshing and delicious and makes it stand out from the other cafes nearby.

Name: Five Degree Tea House
Author: NORCAL OFFROAD
Review:
Nice boba and snack/food place! We passed 3 of them on the street and only stopped here because there was parking. But this place is authentic couldn't read half the menu cause it's not in English but that's fine just enough to get what we wanted.

Some nice booths and tables friendly quick service prices are great and drinks tasted very organic got the line passion tea, coconut, fresh fruit mix and boba and Taro $1.99 special milk tea which was awesome. Fruit drinks came with fresh fruit slices and coconut drink tasted like a pina collada. Lots of food options also. Definitely recommended wish we had this one in Sacramento:

Name: Five Degree Tea House
Author: D P
Review:
Not sure of the young girl working today, but I've had great customer service prior to today.

The cashier was multitasking as I was giving her my order. I had a question about a specific boba drink and someone else walked in so I offer that person the space to order since the cashier looked past me to the next person.

After that customer left, I began ordering and another customer came around and started ordering and the young girl took the second customers' order.

I'm still at the front of the line, in front of the cash register, and wondering if anyone sees me. Finally she asks if I was ready and I told yes and that I had been ready.

Ordered two milk boba tea and two avocado rolls. One had a hair in it (lower left corner) and barely avocado.

I showed them and they seemed to be upset that I was complaining... Yes, I am complaining about hair being in the food... Yes, I am confused as to what I am paying for if it doesn't look like the photos, but more importantly, the hair is in the rice!!

They made me a new one. But the vibe of this place today has been a disappointment.

I have the photos. Hope to add them to this post.

Name: Five Degree Tea House
Author: Tarrea Tolbert
Review: It’s a city hole in the wall spot so I don’t expect the service to be amazing but the food was in my opinion. Ordered a spam egg burger with strawberry lemonade that’s on the sweet side only 10 minute wait on a Sunday afternoon. My new go-to spot, I plan to come back for the waffle next time!

Name: Five Degree Tea House
Author: Ella Hou
Review: I mean some drinks are very cheap so it is worth it. But the drinks don't really taste as good if you pay an extra 2 bucks at a more reputable boba shop. The food is really a bit pricey.

Name: Five Degree Tea House
Author: P
Review: I won't say that this place is the best, but I will definitely say that you can't beat the price. You can find a lot of different options, from dessert to udon. This is great when you go with more than one person. The drinks were tasty and the boba was chewy as I like(plus they put a lot of it). Overall is great and, I would recommend you coming here if you want to pay less.

Name: Inner Sunset - Little Sweet
Author: Simon Tan
