Review: The mango / strawberry sago was just okay - kind of too sweet (ironically), with no option to customize using the digital ordering kiosk. The bubble waffle was also underwhelming.

Name: Inner Sunset - Little Sweet
Author: Matt B
Review: Very quick service bubble tea place, with some overly chewy boba and decent milk tea.

Name: Inner Sunset - Little Sweet
Author: Mel Nguyen
Review: We walked to Little Sweet from Golden Gate Park. This place has the best Winter Melon Gourd Milk Tea in SF. I would come back just to have it again. If you order a split cup they give you the drink in two half-filled cups. The drink was a good amount of creamy and tea. There's merch available if you want to represent. There's a broken glass area near the pick up that I hope they fix if it's legitimately broken. You'll be called/texted when your drink is available but until then they ask that you wait outside until your order is ready.

Name: Inner Sunset - Little Sweet
Author: Lyndon Zamora
Review: This place is great! Open during the pandemic and you can mobile order.  I had the Iron Buddha and it was strong. I felt this was a great value of a boba drink. The only thing I didn't like was the paper straws. Paper changes the taste of the drink.

Name: Inner Sunset - Little Sweet
Author: LLL
Review: Fabulous place with very friendly customer service. First time here today and found out they have a lots of drink I like. The lady named Cici helped me with my order. Super friendly customer service and helped me figure out what dink I would like the best. Got a orea shack and mango drink, and some other milk tea drinks, they all really fresh and delicious. Personal I recommend their fruit tea and chocolate egg puff. Highly recommended this location and will definitely come back again!

Name: Tea Plus Noodles House
Author: Judy Margaret Chen
Review:
First, the staff are nice and friendly. That’s probably the only plus point.

When it comes to Vietnamese Pho, I often judge by looking at the sauces on the table first. If you have chili sauce and hoisin sauce present, you probably nail it 50% I tell you. So, when I see they don’t have these, I know my expectations shouldn’t be high.

If you look at a different perspective, just a general noodle house, the quality is okay, not too bad but not good either. But, if you say this is Pho, no, I say it’s not. This is not Pho.

Name: Tea Plus Noodles House
Author: Gabby Lara
Review:
I was craving Phō. It’s cold in the Bay Area for this AZ girl. Came across this place. It was quiet and quaint. Really, no one was here, dining in, yet, constantly people coming in and out for take out. I ordered
the Tri Color Noodle Soup, $11. Server was very friendly and kind. Even with all the orders coming in, service didn’t take long. My soup was good…I actually liked the egg in it. Chicken and beef was good, as well. Wasn’t impressed with the flavor but I added a few spices on my own. To be honest, I’d come back just to give it another try only Bc the service was fantastic.

Name: Tea Plus Noodles House
Author: Ilyssa Summer
Review:
I ordered Sate beef, so don’t waste your money on this.
Bad quality beef. Broth very oily. Frozen veggies and flavorless. Trying to eat because I’m hungry, but the pho is not appetizing. They garnish with cilantro and green onions to try and make the food appear appetizing or edible.

Name: Tea Plus Noodles House
Author: Patrick Bane
Review: I had the pad thai. Very good.

Name: Tea Plus Noodles House
Author: Ivanna Ivanitska
Review:
If you have been to this place, you are definitely missing out. Their warm and welcoming atmosphere,  absolutely delicious food and boba tea will make your day. It's the perfect place to enjoy some delicious noodles with your favorite pearl drinks.

On the photo is a Singaporean style fried noodles, #80 on the Menu (Absolute MUST to try!)

Enjoy and can't wait to be back!!!

Name: Mr. T Cafe
Author: Jia Hong Situ
Review:
Rather disappointing.

I didn't taste the rose in the boba and the popcorn chicken was flavorless hardened oil, no chicken.
Cashier was energetic, attentive, and patient.
Place was relatively clean.
Very convenient location.

Name: Mr. T Cafe
Author: Yumiko Akaku
Review: The guy's friendly, the girls are not. I heard trashtalking about customers a couple of times. Not to mention, drinks are only good when the guy makes them, the difference is real. They also had another new worker i assume? and she couldn't even get my order right which is weird because i've been going here since. They need more training i guess.

Name: Mr. T Cafe
Author: Vincent Keeffe
Review: Almond milt tea and slushes are really good, my girlfriend really like the lavender black milk tea and the lavender crema. Their breakfast foods are good too!

Name: Mr. T Cafe
Author: Melissa Alexander
Review: My favourite is the plain strawberry smoothie!!  And the spiralized, deep fried potatoes are beyond amazing!!  This place is always busy!

Name: Mr. T Cafe
Author: Ella Hou
Review: Pretty good but expensive from the year before and don't reach the standard of the price

Name: Tea Hut
Author: chilito 456
Review:
I ordered my tea at 7 pm; I don't know who was on shift, but honestly, they are giving the place a bad reputation. I paid more than 30 dollars for a tasteless tea, with no flavor at all.

Edit: Well, the order cost $24.87 exactly (I thought it cost more due to another issue) and it was between 6 and 7 PM that it was ordered. I ordered two Oreo Milk Tea and one Osmanthus Rice Milk Tea. I had tried the teas before but this time the ingredients were not mixed and overall they didn't seem like the ones I had tried and liked.

Name: Tea Hut
Author: Laura Qi
Review: I used to think this was one of the better bubble teas in San Francisco, but it has let me down more and more. First, the jasmine milk tea no longer has the fragrance of tea and has become increasingly watery. Second, the Kyoho grape flavor is not Kyoho grape at all; in fact, it's not even grape. I've ordered four or five times, giving it chance after chance, and I can confidently say it always has an off taste. I'm deeply disappointed.

Name: Tea Hut
Author: Richard Shulaw
Review: Beautiful National forest lots of activities available for the family

Name: Tea Hut
Author: Faye
Review: New products are awesome! The pink lychee drink you must order once in here, and you will love the flavor. Dragon fruit drink also is a new one, since they use fresh dragon fruit, the flavor is incredible​.

Name: Tea Hut
Author: Kalen Gutierrez
Review:
If I'm being honest, I found out about this place on TikTok, and from looking at the drink with mochi, and the dragon fruit, we decided to try it out, and what more perfect timing that we spend our last day in California in San Francisco!

At first, I thought that we would be waiting forever in line to order our drinks, but it was a relief to see that the line was actually for the bakery right next door. It was still quite busy at Tea Hut, but that didn't stop anything from not being amazing! I was not expecting the drinks to be made from real, all natural fruits! It's actually pretty hard to find good places that are innovative AND use real, fresh fruit. It's even hard to find a good place that just uses real, fresh fruit!

I feel like everyone that was working had really good chemistry, and they all seemed experienced because I kept seeing them whipping out drink after drink! It was honestly pretty busy and more and more people kept coming and coming, but they still whipped out the drinks quickly and perfectly! They were also pretty nice as well.

I was happy to see that the seven drinks which we ordered were not only perfectly made, but also tasted delicious and were aesthetically pleasing! By the way, all the drinks weren't for me, but I did get two drinks, and one of them was the Mango Dreamer (with Boba), and the other was Cheese Dragonfruit (with Boba as well)! From my family I heard the drinks were super tasty, and one of my cousins finished it quickly and they NEVER finish any of their drinks, so I thought it must've been good. I personally liked the mango drink more than the dragonfruit one because it was more refreshing and I'm a big fan of mango drinks! The dragonfruit drink was tasty, but I wish it was colder, but overall it was really good and tasted just like dragonfruit! Same with the mango, it tasted just like mango and you could taste the tea that was added. The boba was also very tasty and very soft and lucious!

Overall, it was a pretty fun experience and opportunity to try this place out. It was awesome spending our final day of vacation in the bay area and San Francisco. We started in Vegas and stayed for a month, went to San Diego for a few days, then Sacramento, and now it's coming to an end, and I feel like I ended it on a high note by trying Tea Hut!

Name: Raretea Alameda
Author: Shani Laureta
Review: Normally Id rate 4 stars, but for some reason, the chicken was EXTREMELY salty. Dumped 2 boxes of chicken straight down the trash because it was literally inedible. The takoyaki and drinks in general were delicious otherwise.

Name: Raretea Alameda
Author: bunbun
Review: i’m sorry but it was so gross and the boba was soggy? i ordered regular sweetness but it legit tasted like milk (yes i did mix/shake cup before drinking) they r usually good when i go so idk what happened

Name: Raretea Alameda
Author: BAD TUMMY
Review: So good best place ever if you need a good drink the prices are good and the service are a 10/10 will come back again for a another!!

Name: Raretea Alameda
Author: Hui Aut
Review: We ordered rose milk tea. It almost all milk of the whole cup and only little rose. It tastes less.

Name: Raretea Alameda
Author: Mariela Testa
Review: The thai tea latte that I bought today tasted so bad and sour. The drink has these weird looking white dots that make me guess that the milk went bad. Why no one is checking the expiration dates of these things?!

Name: Tea Plus
Author: Dustin Decker
Review: Real tea, real fruit, and fresh milk. Even the $2 boba milk tea specials are high quality. The mango for my mango sago was blended to order. Support this family owned shop, it's a total gem. The two neighboring businesses use powdered non-dairy milk substitute which is not healthy and not as good.

Name: Tea Plus
Author: Perris Smith
Review: Food is great! Drinks are great! They even let us try the drinks in a small sample cup as they were making them. Julian made everything to perfection. Prices are really good to.

Name: Tea Plus
Author: James T
Review:
Delicious boba tea at very reasonable prices.  I really enjoyed my oolong milk tea boba!

The Thai iced tea had a bit of a strange smoky flavor, didn't love it as much as the oolong.

Name: Tea Plus
Author: Ping
Review: Mozzarella sticks are so good, seasoned well and very tasty. Spam musubi is sauced up well and not too salty. The popcorn chicken is tender and marinated, but it wasn’t seasoned enough for my personal taste. For most it would probably be perfect though. Thai tea is solid, it’s less milky and a very dark orange which I prefer. Shaved ice is much too sweet for my taste but the taste is good otherwise. THE CREPES AND HONEYDEW MILK TEA ARE SO GOOD JESUS. IT TASTES LIKE A MELONA POPSICLE WITH TEA!!! Curry fish balls slap and there is a lot of sauce which I appreciate. Everything here is top tier, just make sure to get the 25% sweet or else you might get diabetes since everything is much sweeter than other places.

Name: Tea Plus
Author: Alec Foster
Review: Delicious teas with tons of toppings, amazing food (served late) with unique Hong Kong style seasonings and ingredients.

Name: Urban Ritual Cafe
Author: Hilda Liu
Review: Nice unique tea place in the Hayes! The drinks are not overly sweet. They deliver the drinks fast. Some people were working there and I can tell it’s gonna be cozy for them :)!

Name: Urban Ritual Cafe
Author: Grace Lin
Review: Interesting menu. Ordered the oolong tea but was a bit astringent 🥲

Name: Urban Ritual Cafe
Author: Ray
Review: OMG OMG OMG Urban Ritual is Amaazzziiiinnnggg! I am a huge fan of the food blogger Feed MeiMei and had to try her signature collab drink with the cafe. It’s called ‘Feed Me Ube’. I ordered it with the honey boba topping and my word it was decadent! The Ube topping was rich and creamy and provided a warm nutty taste. The honey boba was a great addition and overall the drink was so worth it!

Name: Urban Ritual Cafe
Author: Samyukta Rao
Review: Great atmosphere. Tried the feed me ube and was a little disappointed. It had a very grainy over powering coconut instead of creamy coconut milk.

Name: Urban Ritual Cafe
Author: Kristina Setka
Review:
Large menu of teas, boba and more. Appreciate the option to customize: milk, level of sweetness & ice amount.  -2 stars for ambience: the tables are often sticky, floor looks dirty, and the pillows in the benches/alcoves should be removed or cleaned regularly. (It’s looking worn & dirty)

Otherwise highly recommend

Name: Kiss of Matcha
Author: Liann Amagan
Review: Was recommended to check this place out after a wedding nearby. Came here on a Friday afternoon and it’s a small little store very Japanese style and traditional. You can order via kiosk and there’s only about 1 or 2 people working. I was reading the reviews and had a hard time deciding on what to get. It was between the Espresso Matcha Latte (recommended on their physical menu) and the hojicha tea. I wish I tried the hojicha tea just because it’s different. I’m don’t know too much about matcha but it wasn’t bad! There’s street parking but it’s also in a pretty busy area. There’s some seating inside and a bench outside.

Name: Kiss of Matcha
Author: Daniel Lee
Review:
🎤🎶 *Oh baby, Kiss of Matcha’s got me feelin’ some kinda way!* 🎶🎤

From the first step inside, it was like *I Want It Matcha Way!* The vibes are smooth, the beats are sweet, and the matcha—*oh, the matcha*—it’s got all the flavor you’ve been dreamin’ of.

When I tasted that matcha soft serve, it was like a love song in my mouth—so creamy, so dreamy, just the right mix of sweet and matcha heat. And then, *bam*, the matcha lava cake hit me like a *Teenage Dream*—rich, warm, and melt-in-your-mouth good. It’s like they knew exactly what I needed.

The crew here? They’re like the perfect harmony, always there to help you find your matcha match. They’ve got the moves, the knowledge, and they’re all about making sure you’re vibin’ with every bite.

So if you’re lookin’ for a dessert spot that’s got it all—flavor, style, and that *special something*—Kiss of Matcha is *the one.* You know I’m comin’ back for more, baby!

Name: Kiss of Matcha
Author: Simi Odette
Review: Delicious matcha, 10/10! However, the inside is quite small with limited seating. If you’re looking to hang out or need more space, I’d recommend going to the other location. This spot is more suited for a quick grab-and-go.

Name: Kiss of Matcha
Author: Wilson Tsang
Review:
TLDR: gotta get it to go cause there’s really no place to stand, ice cream is decent but not sweet enough

We got the swirl ice cream from a worker who really looked like he wanted to close early. The cone it comes with is definitely nicer than a sugar cone. The soft serve had a nice consistency but I wanted a bit more sweetness from it, it didn’t taste very flavorful though it’s ice cream

Name: Kiss of Matcha
Author: Linh C.
Review:
My new favorite matcha shop. I had the parfait and the strawberry sundae.

Ice cream has excellent matcha flavor and slight sweetness. Mochi is so soft and chewy. Red bean was a good consistency and sweetness. Croffle was buttery too. The only thing I didn't like was the small piece of cake embedded inside (too dry/dense, would have been better just omitting it). The flavor of their strawberry sundae is also good. The strawberry sauce tastes homemade.

Looking forward to trying their hojicha and matcha latte next time :)

Name: TS cafe
Author: fruitypeaches22
Review:
The boba is decent. Never had any of the food.

What made me stop supporting them is that in a matter of a few months I was charged 4 different prices for the SAME ORDER and was never given any valid explanation as to why the price was never consistent.

There’s a price on the SCREEN MENU (that can be changed) and when I got charged something different than what was on the menu, I got a “that’s an old menu”… fast forward a month that old menu is still up.

I don’t know about y’all but I’m mindful of what businesses I support. I personally do no my like supporting businesses that have no consistent pricing and give no explanation why.

Also, question their tax rate.

7/30/24

Reporting again to report the SAME ISSUE- inconsistent pricing. These people are a joke that have no explanation as to why they have sudden surges of prices one day but not the next. They are not even worth getting mad over. Just question WHO you support, cause these guys aren’t worthy of your hard earned money.

Name: TS cafe
Author: Jeff Ho
Review: The food is not bad here. I wouldn't order the okonomiyaki $8 if you were in a hurry though. I called ahead and they said it would be ready in 25 minutes. It wasn't ready until after 35 minutes. If you were on your work lunch break, there would be no time to eat it. I also had the popcorn chicken $6. That also tasted OK. It is nice that there's a restaurant that serves okonomiyaki in this neighborhood. Thanks TS Cafe.

Name: TS cafe
Author: Michael Lee DS
Review: I usually order from here using doordash.  I decided to take a walk and visit this place.  It is very clean and bright even at night.  I like the decorations and atmosphere.  To me it seems romantic to my eyes.  I ordered the crab sandwich, japanese pancake, milk tea, and thai tea.  I didn't share the food pics cause it was to go.  I wanted share my view of this cute shop.

Name: TS cafe
Author: Raven Moon Child
Review: I want to say I've lived in my neighborhood my whole life. I live right upstairs from this Cafe. The other day, my daughter and her boyfriend went downstairs to order food. We are Latinos. I noticed my daughter was gone for a very long time, at least about forty-five minutes. I got concerned, so I called her, and she said they were still waiting.  I knew what time it was..I want to begin to say,I am forty five years old and probably a little jaded with life.my 15 year old kid is an innocent kid. And doesn't see life the way I do. But that day, she got a taste of what it really is. She noticed that they had my daughter and her boyfriend waiting, people came in way after them and were getting their orders. I would like to say that my daughter and her boyfriend didn't even order that much food. It was a drink for each and some corn dogs. And they were attending other people and they were getting their food a lot quicker than my daughter and her boyfriend did. I hate to be that person. But I guess it is what it is. My daughter did notice, and her boyfriend noticed as well that every person that came in after them were asian. I am boycotting this cafe and making sure anybody I know does not ever walk in there again. I guess they forgot what Mission Street/ Excelsior District they walked into.🤷🏻‍♀️

Name: TS cafe
Author: Dr D
Review: I initially ordered one drink and was informed that an extra 25 cents will be charged for credit card purchases under $6. Therefore, I decided to order two drinks instead to avoid the credit card fee. However, there was an extra charge that was not disclosed to me. They charged a “togo” fee. I failed to check my receipt until I got home. I do not tolerate places with hidden charges. The drinks taste standard which is comparable to Quickly's or Mr. T's in this neighborhood. I do not recommend TS Cafe. I do recommend Purple Kow if you’re looking for quality drinks at honest prices.

Name: T4 Oakland
Author: Abraham Abundis
Review: This place has some of the best prices for boba around. They have seats inside allowing you to spend some time with friends. The staff was friendly and quick with my order. I only wish that their payment system was a bit more updated to easily tip.

Name: T4 Oakland
Author: Helene
Review: Best popcorn chicken ever! My go to order is popcorn chicken and jasmine milk tea, half sweet, light ice. Service is alright. I like going here because the prices are still relatively affordable and almost half of all the other boba places. You can also order ahead online on their website.

Name: T4 Oakland
Author: James Brooks
Review: The fried tofu was great and I loved the taro milk tea. Standard sweetness is very sweet which is what I wanted!

Name: T4 Oakland
Author: Kristy Aki Oshiro
Review: Elegant Rose Aloe was so good! It's a lovely caffeine free rose-flavored iced drink that has coconut and aloe jellies in it. The words used to describe it in the advertisement on the TV menu were "romantic, fancy, and floral" and I would say that description was accurate. The lowest sweetness option was still a little too sweet for me but I still enjoyed it very much. I felt very romantic, fancy and floral. : )

Name: T4 Oakland
Author: Emily
Review: I heard great things from this boba tea shop but when I went inside and started ordering the person on the other side didn't even know what they had on the menu.And the speaker made it hard for me to understand. (They would also speak over me which already made it difficult)after like 20 minutes they finally figured out what I wanted which was Caramel boba, Twin tea boba, and spring rolls. Now as I was waiting for my food I would see other people With much Larger orders get their foods and drinks first and I have already been sitting there on the sofas for over 47 minutes. Next thing I know is they put my drinks and food on the side of their table without calling my number.I approach them and ask them why they didn't say my number But they insisted that they called my number(They never did) keep in mind This order took over 47 minutes and right when I saw the drinks I realized that they didn't give me the twin tea boba but Instead Gave me milk tea boba!And I waited over 47 minutes for a "little mistake" as said by the staff. When I asked for a refund they said they can't give me back my money Which was surprising to me. ALSO MY caramel boba didn't even have that much caramel Which made me even more Upset And the final thing was The spring rolls was really dry And didn't even have enough flavor, Actually more like it didn't have any flavor. Overall my experience here was HORRIBLE. Will I be coming back? HELL NO I don't know what I did to deserve the treatment I got.

Name: Teaspoon Corte Madera
Address: 132 Corte Madera Town Center, Corte Madera
Author: Jean Tsou
Review: Drink tastes way too sweet. I will suggest 0 sugar level if you order any fruite puree related drinks. Nice decor inside and friendly service.

Name: Teaspoon Corte Madera
Address: 132 Corte Madera Town Center, Corte Madera
Author: Millie Obregon
Review:
If you’re looking to get some yummy fresh boba tea then choose Teaspoon! I was impressed for my first time here.

Drinks: The boba teas were fresh and light. They both tasted just right, not too sweet, and with great flavors. We tried the taro milk tea and the dirty ube and we would highly recommend both!

Atmosphere: It was a charming little place with indoor and outdoor seating available. The place wasn’t too crowded as well and staff were friendly and welcoming.

Price: Normal price for these quality drinks! You get what you pay for.

Parking: Parking is found in the parking lot area outside of the many shops/restaurants. You don’t have to pay for it either.

Name: Teaspoon Corte Madera
Address: 132 Corte Madera Town Center, Corte Madera
Author: Jeremy Yee
Review: We catered Teaspoon for our wedding at StoneTree Estate in Novato and it was a hit! All of our guests loved the drinks and they were gone in a matter of minutes! Jennifer was a delight to work with, she answered all of our questions, and she provided great guidance in terms of how many and which drinks she recommends. Her communication was also very prompt. We would definitely recommend Teaspoon as a boba vendor for your event! They are also one of our regular go-to spots for boba!

Name: Teaspoon Corte Madera
Address: 132 Corte Madera Town Center, Corte Madera
Author: Johnny & Mora
Review:
We got catering through Teaspoon for a party of 150 people and everything from the initial request to the email exchanges to the pick up went so smoothly and everything was professionally and excellently executed.

We ordered 4 tea travelers of Liquid Gold, 3 Creamy Jasmine, and 3 Assam Black tea - all 25% sweet. Each traveller came with a topping so we chose 5 honey boba, 3 egg pudding, and 2 strawberry heart jellies. We got our own ice on the side and they gave us everything else including straws, cups, and lids.

The owner even gave us tips on how to serve the items so that it would be easy for guests and the proper way of storing everything (no putting boba in the fridge!). We ended up getting drink dispensers and pouring it all into them but the travelers were so high quality, that I would suggest skipping that step (it really was only for the aesthetic but the travelers are cute too). We also put some cute stickers on the cups to let people differentiate their drinks and to go along with the theme ($5 for like 100 stickers on Amazon).

We even had some extra tea and toppings and we served then the next day at church and it allowed for another 30 people to be happily satisfied. The whole thing was a hit and we will definitely be doing this again when we have a special occasion to celebrate. Or even for some low-key get-togethers since it really is a good deal!! It probably came up to $1.50 a guest since, with ice, people had like 2 rounds each. They all had a blast. Thank you so much!!

Name: Teaspoon Corte Madera
Address: 132 Corte Madera Town Center, Corte Madera
Author: Kim Stephens
Review: The boba was great as per usual. However, I had a bad experience with a server. I struggled to take my card out of my wallet and it took a minute or two and the girl working the register, Sofi, aggressively asked, “So are you gonna pay?!” Not only that but she gave me and my colleague dirty looks. I was baffled by the disrespect and grabbed my drinks and left.

Name: Boba Guys Fillmore
Author: Rose Garcia
Review: So good! I always drop by boba guys when I'm nearby. They have the best strawberry matcha. It does get busy here, though (but the wait is worth it).

Name: Boba Guys Fillmore
Author: S T
Review: They must have the worst cups. The lid simply doesn't stay on and we have had 2 incidents of lid popping out. It also seems like the stuff don't read special instructions like no ice, less sweet and extra topping. We have bought 10+ drinks from here and half of the drinks were made wrong. Last time we were here the bobas were undercooked and inedible, very disappointing.

Name: Boba Guys Fillmore
Author: Hannah R
Review: We had a great experience at this Boba Guys location! We arrived right when they opened and had to wait a little bit to order boba because it wasn’t ready, but the employees were so nice. Iris was especially helpful in making us feel welcome. Thanks guys for a great experience!

Name: Boba Guys Fillmore
Author: Christine
Review: This review is for the Boba Guys Catering.  We hired Boba Guys to provide a "boba bar" for two hours during a wedding celebration, and the experience was very positive.  The communication with the catering team was prompt; the delivery and presentation of the drinks were also excellent.  The drinks themselves were, of course, delicious, as always.  The guests were delighted to have an afternoon refreshment with the boba.  We would definitely hire them again in the future if the need arises.  Highly recommend!

Name: Boba Guys Fillmore
Author: Young Kim
Review: Small boba place, ordered lychee olong tea and was refreshing but little too sweet for my taste. The taste of sesame cookie was unique and actually it was delicious! Nice quiet atmosphere and friendly staff. Quick service!

Name: T4
Author: Adriana Perez
Review: Tried the lychee beverage, but it was just ice and syrup mixed, not very satisfying. The boba pearls were soft and chewy, but I think I'll opt for a different drink next time.

Name: T4
Author: Denise Pratt
Review: We got milk tea and an egg puff and both were excellent! The egg puff was the perfect balance of soft and crispy--I'd hugely recommend you get one. The tea was very reasonably priced compared to....pretty much every boba place I've ever been to. Love it!

Name: T4
Author: Sisi Chen
Review: Legit classic pearl milk tea and original egg waffles. The tea blend here tastes strong and it has a hint of jasmine green taste but not 100% certain. The pearls are QQ and sweet after you chew them. The waffle was very big and it was crunchy on the outside and soft on the inside. It took a little while to make but so worth it! If we are ever in the area, we will definitely drop by again.

Name: T4
Author: T K
Review: Order ahead with Muncho app. The store is very fast preparing your order. You can place the app order about 3 minutes before arrival and it will be ready for pickup.

Name: T4
Author: Myron Magcauas
Review: Tea is good. A little sweet but you can adjust it. Great selection of tea and good selection of snacks. Egg puffs were good but took a long time. One of the better bubble tea places in the area.

Name: Tea Plus Tea Bar
Author: Jeff Ho
Review: Yet another boba shop. Service was welcoming. I had the popcorn chicken, curly fries w/coke combo $10 and egg rolls $7. The food tasted fine. I would have preferred more breading on the chicken. They have different sauces to make the food less plain.

Name: Tea Plus Tea Bar
Author: Norman Antiporda
Review: I live in the area and come here pretty often. I usually order just a plain black lemon Iced tea with extra lemon and sometimes the calamari rings. Each time I come here, the staff is so nice and welcoming. I don’t know their names but all of the staff are so kind. Would definitely come again (I live only a block away) and recommend this place to friends or anyone who loves boba drinks and great snacks!

Name: Tea Plus Tea Bar
Author: Angelica Mae Tan
Review: Worst popcorn chicken ever. No breading mix, dry and a waste of money. The Boba was good but the one in San Bruno Avenue has much better food/ drink quality

Name: Tea Plus Tea Bar
Author: nikki esguerra
Review: I wouldn’t give any star at all! The photos on the menu looks great so I gave it a try and thought it would be worth it but the food tasted awful! Worst mango slush I had plus the boba has some weird texture when you chew it. Some of the fried snacks I ordered was soaking in moisture and oil combined. Takoyaki was undercooked and was soaking in sauce too so all you can taste was just the sauce. Will definitely not recommended this place. Very disappointing! Do not waste your money in this place!

Name: Tea Plus Tea Bar
Author: Edward Bennett
Review: Staff is fast & always gives the BEST customer service 👏

Name: Sharetea
Author: Tommy Nguyen
Review: Not my cup of tea. Drink looks boring. Drink are too sweet even though “No Sugar” was chosen. No fruit in the drink even though it’s call fruit tea.

Name: Sharetea
Author: Matt B
Review:
Half decent boba tea spot in the lobby of the Metreon building. A fine place to grab a drink before or after a movie, but certainly nothing to write home about.

½ sweetness was the right amount of sweetness.

Name: Sharetea
Author: Sean Nguyen
Review:
I love going to the city to watch Wrestling and I have been seen @shareteametreon on my Instagram feed and I wanted to check it out.  I got iced blended coffee with creama and ice cream. It was so sweet and amazing. The ice cream on top is so elevating. I love how the fresh fruit tasted in the Peach tea with kiwi fruit. I love a good mojito and the lime Mojito did not disappoint. It was sweet and tangy, which was perfect for me. The last drink I got was the Matcha red bean milk tea with mini pearls and it was so good. I usually enjoy red beans in Vietnamese Che but I been getting it in Boba and it so good. I can understand why this drink is so popular! All in all everything was great and I would be coming back to try more drinks from  @shareteametreon .

📍ShareTea in the Metreon in San Francisco, CA.

Name: Sharetea
Author: A A
Review: This is a 3.5 star rating. More sweet has the same sweetness as extremely sweet, and less sweet. The Happy Family Large, came to $6.00 with tax, tip, and mandate, and was a sour combination of big pearls, small pearls, hard lychee cubes, soggy red beans, and.....the kitchen sink. The actual milk tea was as bland, and forgettable as the local Evening News. Yet, there was, and always seems to be a line, due to the perfect location of being on the way to AMC movie theater, at the food court. There's so many better bubble tea stores out there, I would skip Sharetea. $$$ out of $$$$$. No dedicated bathroom. Garage parking nearby. I would not recommend Sharetea.

Name: Sharetea
Author: Benny Avalos
Review:
I wish that pricess were cheaper since half the labor is automated. I hope the operation cost trickle down to higher hourly rates for workers.

They were really lovely and nice

Name: Brew Cha
Author: Alice Garner
Review: Really great experience here! Super fast. Ordered the mango passion fruit slushy, love that you can mix and match flavors! They have games and a nice couch and it’s in a super convenient location! Nice friendly staff. REALLY YUMMY BOBA! Would 100% go back again.

Name: Brew Cha
Author: cravesinthebay
Review: Brewcha SF 💌 one of the best freshly brewed boba tea spot in SF 😍! Their drinks were super fresh & tasty. I recommend getting the Black Sugar Creme Brulee Latte ❣️ which I felt in love with!

Name: Brew Cha
Author: Vee Dinh
Review:
Discovered a milk tea spot with light flavored drinks. Follow me!

Highlights of the menu:
1. Tiramisu milk tea
2. Lavender matcha latte
3. Panda milk tea
4. Roasted oolong tea with cheese cream top

Name: Brew Cha
Author: Flor Fernandez
Review: FRESH, organic, has quite a few milk options (even lactose free) and a large selection of drinks!  I loved that they used real taro for their drink. Friendly staff. Worth it!

Name: Brew Cha
Author: xo_julienna
Review: It’s my first time going there. My brother in law frequently goes to Brew Cha for boba drinks. See photos I took that gives you a full picture. My favorite drink is hot black sesame latte and it is so delicious!

Name: WanPo Tea Shop
Author: Julie Taeko
Review: Excellent drinks but slightly pricier. Great ambiance and fun little store with tons of anime and tea aroma. Small and no place to sit but cute. I had the brown sugar boba milk.

Name: WanPo Tea Shop
Author: Johnny Chan
Review: One of my top go to spots for milk tea, sugar cane, mango pomelo, and their jelly toppings in the Bay. Quality tea and owner provided great service. The drinks are consistent and similar to what I had in Taiwan. I’d recommend to anyone looking for Taiwanese quality tea

Name: WanPo Tea Shop
Author: miketea
Review: Only Wanpo around. Come here many many many times. Always good quality! I usually get tea with 30% sugar level, because it's the best taste and favor compared to others. Once a while I will drink the milk tea.

Name: WanPo Tea Shop
Author: Nico De Osso
Review: it was so delicious, cute little pop in shop with authentic flavors! I got the brown sugar and matcha tea! Would love to try something else next time!

Name: WanPo Tea Shop
Author: Steph Wong
Review:
Recently discovered WanPo was in my area. I was randomly browsing online for a new boba spot that I haven't tried and this one popped up.  I was pretty excited to see that it was close to me and decided to head over.

There is a kiosk that you can order from on the side, but there's also the register where you can order from as well. The menu is pretty extensive so I would definitely recommend looking up what you want to order before you get there. Ordering is quick and painless you just use the kiosk and pay by card.

The decor inside is super cute. There's tons of figurines and posters of different anime. Inside is not the biggest, so if they get a bunch of orders you are able to wait outside. My drink was ready in less than 5 minutes.

I got matcha with boba and definitely liked the flavor.  The milk was not too overpowering and you could definitely taste the matcha flavor. The boba itself was not too chewy.

There is a parking lot, so you should have no trouble finding spaces.

Name: Teaspoon
Author: felicie diaz
Review:
ABSOLUTE BANGER SO SO GOOD!!
Got the rose milk tea and the caramel cream boba with Oreo and it was divine so good.
Will be coming back
New obsession

Name: Teaspoon
Author: Julie Taeko
Review: I tried the watermelon juice that was absolutely delicious, and customized to less sweet. And it had delicious little bits of crunchy seeds at the bottom. My friend got one of the snow ice desserts which was also quite delicious. And the service was fantastic!

Name: Teaspoon
Author: Sher Zhang
Review:
Favorite bubble tea in the area!
I’ve visited 5 times already in the last month… my boba cravings are adequately satisfied here :)

Creamy Jasmine and and grasshopper are my two top drinks so far, depending on if I’m in a milk tea or fruity drink mood.

Liquid gold is a solid drink if you like the roasted flavour, and caramel cream is decadent and sweet (although I wish there was a milk tea version, as this one only contains milk)

Name: Teaspoon
Author: Mitchell Robinson
Review: Everything is good, drinks, baked goods, teas, service, everything. The music could be cranked down just a little bit though. It’s quite loud in my opinion. But good place good stuff, nonetheless.

Name: Teaspoon
Author: Edison Huang
Review:
The kiosk may need to be updated. We ordered the lychee on lychee drink which has lychee jelly included.. the kiosk also mentioned that... but the next option is to add topping so we chose no topping because we don't want to add any additional topping.. well the drink ended up having no topping whatsoever. Pretty much paid for a full drink only to lose out on the topping.

The pear-adise drink isn't bad though. I recommend not going 0% sweet on that one.

Name: Teaspoon San Mateo
Author: Sharadha Ramaswamy
Review: Our friends in Bay area suggested this place and it’s absolutely worth visiting. This place has the best mango bubble tea. It was so refreshing and felt like summer on a tall cup. The caramel bubble tea was average when compared to mango flavour. The service was quick and the ambience was decent. I would recommend the silky mango bubble tea here.

Name: Teaspoon San Mateo
Author: Murillo Pontual
Review: Great boba tea at San Mateo. Staff is very polite and the store is clean. I highly recommend it.

Name: Teaspoon San Mateo
Author: Ahmad Lutfi
Review:
Tried their mango on fire smoothie:

First you've got the sweetness of mango, until you find the kick of red spice, but it's yet finished until you find salt, that's the last kicker !, it made my tongue dance from excitement!

Name: Teaspoon San Mateo
Author: Fire Hazard
Review: This is an ok spot to grab a quick boba. The drinks are a lot sweeter than what I normally would expect from other boba places so you might want to ask them to reduce the sugar. The biggest draw back was the straw. It is not plastic and I understand why they would want to help the environment by finding an alternative. What they are currently using is not the answer though. Both my wife and I went through more than one straw while finishing our drinks and if you aren’t really careful they will crack. If you are going to use a plastic cup with a plastic lid you might as well use a plastic straw. Also they no longer serve shaved ice at this location.

Name: Teaspoon San Mateo
Author: Venu Javarappa
Review:
Superb bakery. Sublime cakes. Fresh bread. Fresh hong kong milk tea.

Sublime egg tarts. Ahh, the pork buns.

Wonderful service. Clean store. Brightly lit. Great to sit and enjoy a cafe atmosphere with fresh smells and a trendy vibe.

Right in downtown san mateo with pretty easy parking. Great addition to downtown san mateo.

Name: Societea Tea House & Eatery
Author: Laura Michaelis
Review:
I was a little nervous at first because I am not very open when it comes to my diet. Let me tell you, I was extremely happy.

We had a party of six with two separate orders, I had ordered. The Pork Belly sandwich with the Furi-Furi Sprouts. My husband had the Thai Chicken over rice. The dishes were amazing!! I am craving the sandwich now.

The Thai Milk Tea was spot on and the Honeydew Tea was like they blended the melon and stuck a straw in it, it was that good.

The waffle fries were on point, the popcorn chicken was okay, The burgers were good, but the jalapenos were not spicy (according to my friend).

As we walked out, we discovered that you could actually check out a board game and play a game in the cafe while enjoying your meal. Might keep the kids off their phones.

Name: Societea Tea House & Eatery
Author: B Na
Review: Happy to have found a place close to the airport to have lunch with a friend while waiting out a layover. We ordered the lemongrass crispy tofu sandwich and I had a matcha brown sugar bubble tea. Both were great and we played some board games to pass the time. There is a large seating area in the back for group gatherings.

Name: Societea Tea House & Eatery
Author: Jennifer Ho
Review: Cute little place.  Craved banh mi and found them through Yelp.  Spacious room in the back to work and eat.  Can’t take work calls here 😉 background noise.  Plenty of parking in the back with easy QR code to scan and pay $1 for 2 hours.

Name: Societea Tea House & Eatery
Author: Esther C.
Review:
It is a casual cafe with enough seating space to sit and play games with friends or study on your own (which was the primary reason I came here and appreciated their set up) but unfortunately within the last month as I’ve started to come in more often and have brought friends, we’ve encountered coming into the store to music that is so loud you cannot hear one another speak, and 4-6 employees gathered at the register laughing loudly, which wouldn’t be a problem had my friends and I been greeted upon our entry.

As for the food, their drinks are fun and some are tasty but the boba is too stiff and not chewy at all. I find that their Jasmine tea is consistently over steeped but black tea based drinks are solid. Their food is okay.

If you do not mind a loud atmosphere, this can definitely be the place for you but if you’re seeking a place to study or get work done, this might not be the spot for you.

Name: Societea Tea House & Eatery
Author: Cindy L
Review:
Fav spot in the peninsula for popcorn chicken and milk tea! The workers are super friendly and drinks come out fast.

My go to is the strawberry matcha latte and Thai green tea. It’s the perfect balance of creamy yet refreshing matcha flavor and the honey boba is perfect lightly chewy texture and not too sweet.

Name: Tea Hut
Author: chilito 456
Review:
I ordered my tea at 7 pm; I don't know who was on shift, but honestly, they are giving the place a bad reputation. I paid more than 30 dollars for a tasteless tea, with no flavor at all.

Edit: Well, the order cost $24.87 exactly (I thought it cost more due to another issue) and it was between 6 and 7 PM that it was ordered. I ordered two Oreo Milk Tea and one Osmanthus Rice Milk Tea. I had tried the teas before but this time the ingredients were not mixed and overall they didn't seem like the ones I had tried and liked.

Name: Tea Hut
Author: Laura Qi
Review: I used to think this was one of the better bubble teas in San Francisco, but it has let me down more and more. First, the jasmine milk tea no longer has the fragrance of tea and has become increasingly watery. Second, the Kyoho grape flavor is not Kyoho grape at all; in fact, it's not even grape. I've ordered four or five times, giving it chance after chance, and I can confidently say it always has an off taste. I'm deeply disappointed.

Name: Tea Hut
Author: Richard Shulaw
Review: Beautiful National forest lots of activities available for the family

Name: Tea Hut
Author: Faye
Review: New products are awesome! The pink lychee drink you must order once in here, and you will love the flavor. Dragon fruit drink also is a new one, since they use fresh dragon fruit, the flavor is incredible​.

Name: Tea Hut
Author: Kalen Gutierrez
Review:
If I'm being honest, I found out about this place on TikTok, and from looking at the drink with mochi, and the dragon fruit, we decided to try it out, and what more perfect timing that we spend our last day in California in San Francisco!

At first, I thought that we would be waiting forever in line to order our drinks, but it was a relief to see that the line was actually for the bakery right next door. It was still quite busy at Tea Hut, but that didn't stop anything from not being amazing! I was not expecting the drinks to be made from real, all natural fruits! It's actually pretty hard to find good places that are innovative AND use real, fresh fruit. It's even hard to find a good place that just uses real, fresh fruit!

I feel like everyone that was working had really good chemistry, and they all seemed experienced because I kept seeing them whipping out drink after drink! It was honestly pretty busy and more and more people kept coming and coming, but they still whipped out the drinks quickly and perfectly! They were also pretty nice as well.

I was happy to see that the seven drinks which we ordered were not only perfectly made, but also tasted delicious and were aesthetically pleasing! By the way, all the drinks weren't for me, but I did get two drinks, and one of them was the Mango Dreamer (with Boba), and the other was Cheese Dragonfruit (with Boba as well)! From my family I heard the drinks were super tasty, and one of my cousins finished it quickly and they NEVER finish any of their drinks, so I thought it must've been good. I personally liked the mango drink more than the dragonfruit one because it was more refreshing and I'm a big fan of mango drinks! The dragonfruit drink was tasty, but I wish it was colder, but overall it was really good and tasted just like dragonfruit! Same with the mango, it tasted just like mango and you could taste the tea that was added. The boba was also very tasty and very soft and lucious!

Overall, it was a pretty fun experience and opportunity to try this place out. It was awesome spending our final day of vacation in the bay area and San Francisco. We started in Vegas and stayed for a month, went to San Diego for a few days, then Sacramento, and now it's coming to an end, and I feel like I ended it on a high note by trying Tea Hut!

Name: TP TEA San Mateo
Author: Vivian Lee
Review:
Oh they use real tea in this boba for sure. And probably a lot of it. Or something highly caffeinated. When I drink this stuff I can’t sleep for 3 days. But it’s also my coworkers favorite tea on the peninsula. It’s one of the better tea spots despite so many boba shops in San Mateo.

Maybe someday I can just drink this like a normal person….

Name: TP TEA San Mateo
Author: Gloria Ha
Review:
After hearing stories of TP Tea from friends, we ended up dropping the San Mateo location before heading to the airport.

We got the Tie Guan Yin milk tea with boba. It was good and reminded me of Taiwan. It had a strong tea flavor, and the caffeine kept us awake on the plane.

There are so many other options for boba in the area, so I'm not sure that I would return based on my experience. I would get a less caffeinated drink next time for sure!

Name: TP TEA San Mateo
Author: Aodrey Max
Review:
My friend recommended me TP tea in Cupertino but I didn’t get a chance to try it so I ended with the San Meto one. I ordered tie guan yin milk tea with 30% and less ice. It has no tea taste only milk and sugar. Very disappointed.
But their boba is chewy.
My friend ordered a fantastic fruit tea I tried it also. I have to say if you like fresh fruit tea then I don’t recommend. Same problem: no tea taste but strong passion fruit syrup.

Name: TP TEA San Mateo
Author: Katie
Review:
This is the worst TP Tea location I’ve been to. I ordered through snack pass which showed maybe 7+ items sold out including all the orange, peach, and watermelon items. This was weekday at night time.

I went with my usual order of a tea drink and tried one of their bubble waffles. The ice tea tasted super diluted and had such a weak tea flavor compared to other locations. The bubble waffle was okay, nothing amazing. Some of the workers did not seem friendly when I asked a question, but I understand they were busy.

Name: TP TEA San Mateo
Author: Pat Arcangel
Review:
ATMOSPHERE IS INTENSE ONLY BECAUSE IT'S REALLY REALLY BUSY.   STAFF IS VERY EFFICIENT IN TIME MANAGEMENT AND HUNDREDS OF ORDERS PER HOUR.

All the Tea choices I have tried so far have been amazing.  Lucky I get to come here once per week hopefully I will have a favorite within a month or two.

Name: Tiger Tea & Juice
Author: Julie Taeko
Review: Very good quality drinks! I’ve tried at least four different drinks now, and absolutely loved every single one. The service is excellent. They are incredibly friendly and nice. It’s also a very clean store with a very clean bathroom. Love it. New favorite place in Burlingame/Millbrae.

Name: Tiger Tea & Juice
Author: JP G
Review: The boba brown sugar with cheese foam was pretty good. I was quite thirsty so I downed it pretty fast, but the flavor was pretty good, though I think I was expecting more nuance of flavour in the brown sugar syrup. The cheese foam topping did have that nice nuance though. The default amount of boba isn't all that much be advised to order extra boba if you really like boba. The ordering is done through a tablet which allows you to see the pictures of the drinks if you need that in order to decide. I think you can probably also order from a person. The interior was nice and minimalist with a fair amount of small tables and one large table tucked into an alcove. It looked like there were plenty of electrical outlets if you want to do work there. I do kind of find it sad that so many businesses have very little interaction with people anymore. When your order is done you will get a text that it is done. I guess that this is helpful for efficiency and that is really nice for some people. I would say that the wait was pretty long though, so I don't know if this is actually making anything more efficient. Overall I enjoyed it and recommend this business though I think if there was more of a human touch I'd love it more.

Name: Tiger Tea & Juice
Author: L C.
Review:
The peach oolong milk tea was very disappointing. There was no taste and the tea tasted like water. The worst part is that I ordered regular ice, and there was literally only 2 pieces of ice and the rest was lukewarm light artificial peach flavored water. Made me doubt my taste buds.
The strawberry matcha in comparison was drinkable. But the matcha flavor was lacking.
The actual boba balls were fine, did not wow us but was at least edible.
Overall do not recommend. For the price, 0& is a much better choice and actually uses real fruit instead of powders and jam.

Name: Tiger Tea & Juice
Author: Andrew Jaru
Review: Hands down one of my top 3 favorite milk tea places in the Bay Area. Their potted plant option sounds gimmicky, but it adds a nice dimension of flavor to whatever you get it on. The tea itself is clean tasting and very smooth. The jellies are soft and have a nice texture. Inside is clean and well designed. Overall, a great place for drinks.

Name: Tiger Tea & Juice
Author: Wendel Tse
Review: The teas are great, but even better, the chill crew won't bother you if you decide to relax in the clean dining room with a friend, chat, and sip your beverages slowly.

Name: Milk Tea Lab
Address: 630 El Camino Real, South San Francisco
Author: Baby Panda
Review:
I wish I could give an even higher rating! I 100% recommend going here and only here for your boba! Not only are the drinks super delicious but also high quality, amazing choices and great prices! We refuse to go anywhere else for our boba and come at least once a week! 💜 My kids and family love this location because the owner and the employees are so sweet and friendly! They are the BEST!!

Remember to tip them, everyone works hard to make your drinks and it's well deserved! 🥹💖

PS, the drinks here are Thai tea with regular boba (tapioca pearls), mango slushies with mango popping boba and the big with mango stars and leachie jelly and a Green tea with lychee jelly and Crystal boba (aka cool boba) and I'm not sure what else but its my new favorite!
Strawberry raspberry is okay ..
Honeydew is good but get it 50% sweet only 😅
I'll add more pictures!

I also love their turo milk tea, Thai tea, honey roasted tea and matcha green tea milk with tiny boba! Matcha with the tiny boba is my #1 favorite!!

Name: Milk Tea Lab
Address: 630 El Camino Real, South San Francisco
Author: Emy Suhaimi
Review:
This is my absolute favorite boba place in all of the Bay. While I tend to stick to my personal favorites, (Wintermelon Milk Tea & Jasmine Milk Tea) I truly believe that their selection of drinks will always be of high quality. The menu options aren’t as widespread as other milk tea lab locations, so that is something to keep note of but still a very good selection to choose from. It is rare that I take time to rate a restaurant/business but I can’t not share my opinion on this store!!! It is my favorite boba place and I’m always looking forward to my next boba drink from here

+ the owner of this location is always so sweet, personable, friendly and attentive.

Name: Milk Tea Lab
Address: 630 El Camino Real, South San Francisco
Author: M Antonio C
Review:
This location is nice with pleasant staff.  We were in the area, and so this place is our  go-to location when south of the City.

We ordered 4 drinks and had no issues with quality or service.   We like it here.

Name: Milk Tea Lab
Address: 630 El Camino Real, South San Francisco
Author: 00 SVN
Review: Love this place. Honestly have worked my way through the menu! Mango lovers frozen drink is a go to! They are generous with their toppings & the prices are super fair for these huge boba teas. Smoothies to tea to tonics, all very satisfying. The green tea isn’t too strong on the tea flavor. Go here at least twice a week. Amazing efficient, fast, and friendly staff! Super clean place. Joanne is great! (I don’t recommend the Thai tea is my only negative.)  More flavors in store than the online menu too. Definitely recommend!

Name: Milk Tea Lab
Address: 630 El Camino Real, South San Francisco
Author: Tasnim Ara Islam
Review: They have a variety of choices for drinks. I ordered a regular mango milk tea. The drink was perfectly made and tasted rellay great. Another interesting thing of this shop is the their interior design. Very unique. And the price is absolutely reasonable.

Name: BB Tea
Author: P
Review: I had really high hopes when I came here and I was disappointed.I would give this place a 2.5 starts. There were 2 girls in the place and they were kind of rude. One of them was using her phone all the time that I was there. After I ordered one of them was doing everything. I ordered soufflé pancakes and it was supposed to take 12-15 minutes but i waited for at least 25 minutes! in addition the girl that was making my pancakes wasn't wearing gloves(that was the same girl in the cash register). Finally, I got the pancake and it was completely overcooked. With the cream on top, you wouldn't notice and it looked beautiful but I wanted to taste the correct way. It wasn't horrible but I didn't get what I paid for. I don't know if the place and service is like this all the time or if I was lucky but I will be coming back because I want to try the drinks.

Name: BB Tea
Author: Brandy Baker
Review: This is almost 5 stars for me, but not quite. I love the self order kiosk and the text when ready ordering system - it makes the process a lot smoother than other boba shops in the area. We ordered four drinks but the flavor wasn’t quite there on all of them. The drinks were good, and we enjoyed them! We will be back and hopefully we just had drinks on a bit of an off day.

Name: BB Tea
Author: Kiya J
Review: Tried BB Tea's Ube Mont Blanc cake at FoodieLand and I had to get it again because it's one of the best desserts i've had in years ! Too yummy !! Also got a Creme Brulee and Lavender Milk Tea, also super delicious.

Name: BB Tea
Author: Sisi Chen
Review: We got brown sugar milk tea with boba and salted egg cookies. Milk tea was a 3, not enough tea taste. 25% sweet is the least amount of sugar and it was definitely still on the sweeter side. The boba is a 5, perfectly chewy and sweet. The cookies were the highlight for me. So nutty, creamy and love the salty sweet taste. Will come back for more sweet treats!

Name: BB Tea
Author: Viveat T
Review:
Creme brulee milk tea is above average. My boyfriend tried the creme brulee crepe and it was so good, filled with creamy brulee, fresh strawberry, ice cream, and a super crunchy sugar top, definitely worth $9, don't be set back by the price!
They accidentally forgot to add boba to my drink but gave me a cup of boba on the side. Glad I caught that before walking out! It's really no big deal but they were very nice about it.

Name: Tea On Piedmont
Author: Heidi Liou
Review:
If you order tea (and you stay in the store), you can get one refill. Highly recommend their Jasmine!

The staff are all so incredibly nice and helpful. They also have some merchandise/chocolate that make great gifts.

Love the big windows and natural lighting in the front nook area. Perfect vibe all around for a zen afternoon.

They allow you to bring in sushi from the store across the street!

Name: Tea On Piedmont
Author: Olivia Ma
Review:
I bet you won’t find any tea brand comparable to Tea On Piedmont on the West Coast! The Hojicha latte was a 1000 out of 10—absolutely amazing! And the iced matcha latte= A dream come true!!

I feel so disappointed how bad taste any tea shop store in Seattle🥹but in California! SooRad😘🤌🤌🤌 I will definitely back to check out any chance back here 🔥Tea On Piedmont🔥

Name: Tea On Piedmont
Author: V Tancredi
Review:
I'm not normally a tea drinker but I had the Hojicha latte and I have become addicted. It's amazing!

They also have nice pastries with a French influence as well as a number of fancy teas for sale. My daughter said the strawberry milk with boba is delicious.

Central location on Piedmont avenue with Wi-Fi and power if you need to do a little work on your laptop.

Highly recommend.

Name: Tea On Piedmont
Author: Sonia Garcia
Review: I ordered an unsweetened matcha with almond milk. The color of the matcha looked so green, it was beautiful, and as it turns out, super tasty.

Name: Tea On Piedmont
Author: Sophia D
Review:
Delicious fresh boba and great texture even later in the day
There are also local artists with pins, stickers, tea, etc for sale

Name: Tea Hut
Author: seibun i
Review: TeaHut in Millbrae is great for fresh, real fruit tea. The Mango Pomelo Sago is a must-try—perfectly sweet with real ingredients. The Full Cup of Strawberries & Black Grapes is refreshing, with whole, in-season grapes. Cozy spot, and parking is convenient too!

Name: Tea Hut
Author: Jennifer L.
Review: Was eating in the area and came across this establishment. I am from Miami and so boba is what I thought I was ordering. Whatever I ordered was amazing. The associate even let me try the watercress boba since I was clearly new to this. It's a small little hole in the wall with great charm and atmosphere. If I am in the area again this is a for sure revisit.

Name: Tea Hut
Author: Vanessa Cheung
Review: Originally I came to try their Taro 2.0 drink but it seems to always be out of stock.....My second choice was the Oreo Matcha which I really liked. Boba was nice and chewy. Alterations to non-dairy milk is included, which I appreciate since most places charge extra. However I think ~$8 is a bit pricy, especially for the smaller sized cup. If they ever replenish their Taro drink I shall return.

Name: Tea Hut
Author: Julie Taeko
Review: Excellent decor and delicious drinks. The owners took over the old Yifang spot and did very well. I tried a non-caffeinated red bean drink that was very tasty. Very cute seating area and clean bathroom too.

Name: Tea Hut
Author: Pranit Ravuri
Review:
Recommendations: Don’t hesitate to speak up if your drink is not as desired. Try a few sips and tell the staff right away, and the staff will remake it. Just remember to be polite and explain why it is not to your liking. Best bet is to bring friends, get a variety off the menu and try them all.

Service is fast and efficient. It is an okay place to relax and chill, but more of an order and go kind of place.

Quality over quantity is big here.

Red Bean Milkshake:
I do not reccomend the red bean milk shake, far too icy.

If you do get it, ask for no ice and more milk.

Taro milk tea 2.0:

Only smalls are available.

Pro Tips - Get it in a bigger cup, and add some milk, much better sipping consistency. Don’t get extra toppings.

Purple yam is Thicc

Taro mud is dense(THICCC), fresh AF, 100% real, no powder bs here

However this is more like a pudding than a drink. It’s great for those who love thick and rich desserts. Very decadent for an all natural drink with little to no sweetener Best to get as is with no toppings.

———

Coming back for the dragon fruit with cheese and the standard black milk tea!

Name: Inner Sunset - Little Sweet
Author: Simon Tan
Review: The mango / strawberry sago was just okay - kind of too sweet (ironically), with no option to customize using the digital ordering kiosk. The bubble waffle was also underwhelming.

Name: Inner Sunset - Little Sweet
Author: Matt B
Review: Very quick service bubble tea place, with some overly chewy boba and decent milk tea.

Name: Inner Sunset - Little Sweet
Author: Mel Nguyen
Review: We walked to Little Sweet from Golden Gate Park. This place has the best Winter Melon Gourd Milk Tea in SF. I would come back just to have it again. If you order a split cup they give you the drink in two half-filled cups. The drink was a good amount of creamy and tea. There's merch available if you want to represent. There's a broken glass area near the pick up that I hope they fix if it's legitimately broken. You'll be called/texted when your drink is available but until then they ask that you wait outside until your order is ready.

Name: Inner Sunset - Little Sweet
Author: Lyndon Zamora
Review: This place is great! Open during the pandemic and you can mobile order.  I had the Iron Buddha and it was strong. I felt this was a great value of a boba drink. The only thing I didn't like was the paper straws. Paper changes the taste of the drink.

Name: Inner Sunset - Little Sweet
Author: LLL
Review: Fabulous place with very friendly customer service. First time here today and found out they have a lots of drink I like. The lady named Cici helped me with my order. Super friendly customer service and helped me figure out what dink I would like the best. Got a orea shack and mango drink, and some other milk tea drinks, they all really fresh and delicious. Personal I recommend their fruit tea and chocolate egg puff. Highly recommended this location and will definitely come back again!

Name: My Cup of Tea
Author: Gregg Tavares
Review: My Cup of Tea is like the unofficial community center for the local youth. Lots of groups playing boardgames, studying, working. I've stumbled into in store events like a k-pop fan goods event among other things. Also, the garlic noodles here are what made me fall in love with garlic noodles and the standard by which I judge the rest. The garlic noodles at My Cup of Tea have a very comfort food vibe. Soft, thick, and chewy and the sweet pickled shredded carrots are the perfect condiment. Free wifi and right next to an M line stop or 4 mins from a K line stop it's a great spot

Name: My Cup of Tea
Author: Elsie Militar
Review: I'm glad to have found a boba place that was open late. It was full of college students (I assume) at 10pm on a Monday. The fresh spring rolls were just okay but our drinks were good. I would come back and try other food. It was pretty loud in there from people playing board games. It also looked like a popular spot for people with laptops.

Name: My Cup of Tea
Author: Jeff Ho
Review:
Opened until 12am, My Cup of Tea is convenient for those of us that takes naps and wake up late. The ladies that work here are fast. I only had to wait 15 minutes for my food after ordering at the counter. The chicken garlic noodles $13 are buttery and tasted good. I would have liked more garlic. This was still good because it wasn't salty. It could have benefited from chopped pickled green onions. The grilled pork vermicelli noodles $13 was fine. Both dishes are served in small take out containers.

Minor stuff:
- They don't give you a bag or napkins.
- There are dorky, loud, male SFSU students playing their board games.
-  Homeless man sitting with his big plastic of clothes in the dining room. It kind of felt like a homeless shelter at 10:30pm.

Tip: wifi password is the restaurant's name without the spaces.

Name: My Cup of Tea
Author: Mei Fu Lee
Review:
UPDATE: I've been going here for a long time now and have noticed how much it has changed and how popular this boba shop has gotten now. I still appreciate the amount of space this place has and how clean it is too. Their food is really good! I was honestly quite skeptical of how their noodle soup would taste, but it's actually really tasty! I also typically order their pork banh mi. Usually the sandwiches are very stuffed, but I have gotten a few skimped out sandwiches before. For drinks, their oolong milk tea with boba is my go to drink. Their mango green tea is also really good. Their drink sizes are pretty big and the large should definitely be shared. Their boba quality is consistent and has really improved over time. Their prices have gone a bit higher, but I think it's worth it for the quantity and quality.

I highly recommend ordering by phone because it can get quite busy!

I've come here a few times. The sign is a little misleading, as it isn't really My Cup of Tea, but actually Cool Tea Bar. I liked that this place is open late, until midnight. It's really big inside, with lots of tables and free wifi, so I thought it would be an ideal place to study. I came around 9PM on a Tuesday and it was SO CROWDED. People were really loud and it was pretty hard to study. I assume a lot of students were here hanging out here since it's summer. Even after ordering, I noticed a lot of people still came inside.

I ordered the pork banh mi and a mango slush. For $6, the banh mi was pretty cheap (definitely cheaper than dinosaurs up the street). However, it took 20 minutes to make. Thankfully, they didn't make the drink until the food was done, so the drink wasn't all melted. I got the drink 50% sweet, but it still seemed pretty sweet. The regular size is actually really big and the large size is definitely meant to be shared. The sandwich was pretty tasty and decently sized. The other banh mi sandwiches they have are $8  though. I stayed for about an hour, before I just left since it was just so loud.

While not ideal to study, as it was really crowded and loud, but I can appreciate the drinks are really big and taste pretty good. This place can be a great place to hang out, especially since they also have board games available. I would definitely get the pork banh mi again. They also have other food like popcorn chicken and fries.

Name: My Cup of Tea
Author: Kevin L
Review:
We had popcorn chicken, pho, and mango 101 drink. Everything tasted good and would order again.

Place had a few games on hand and there is free wifi.

Name: Hi Tea
Author: Ziling Mai
Review: i actually ordered drinks as well but completely forgot to take pics of that but here chickens…lol. the chicken wings came with a decent amount. good flavor and delicious i would get it again. now as for drinks i got the “Fruit Party” and a “Lychee Slush” now surprisingly THEY ACTUALLY USE FRESH FRUITS. for the fruit party it was nice and refreshing, the drink was some type of green tea and you can eat the fruits after your drink. ok next one ik super surprised, they actually used lychee in the slush. not just syrup which i’m sure they also added, but i saw a cup with whole lychees in it. they r W for that

Name: Hi Tea
Author: Amina Choudhery
Review: The staff was very rude at the time of order and charged me extra. I ordered  Nutella strawberry milk tea. I can't lie it's the worst Boba drink I have ever tried. The chocolate was fake no Nutella and the strawberry was not fresh or artificial flavor. It tasted like sour milk with cheap chocolate on sides.  I won't recommend it.

Name: Hi Tea
Author: Ella Hou
Review: Very good experience but you can taste quality

Name: Hi Tea
Author: LHZ
Review: They’re open - good stuff! The mango pomelo is a bit pricy, but hard to find these types of drinks around San Bruno.

Name: Hi Tea
Author: C.V de leon
Review: Delicious appetizers, and of course bobas, nice and quiet place, we going back again !!!!!

Name: Gong Cha
Author: Kerwin McKenzie
Review: They were good. But the kiosk takes away from customer interaction. There's no talking between the server and the customers, except to call your number. Atmosphere was much better when they didn't have the kiosk.

Name: Gong Cha
Author: Daning Lu
Review: ignored my take out order and let three orders cut in before me. Wont come back anymore. I cannot believe I even tipped 20%.

Name: Gong Cha
Author: Natasha Kanhai
Review: Pretty cute place, bright and clean.  Big beautiful kiosks to oder from, but I assume you can order at the counter as well.  The employees making the drinks had their hands full though.   Lots of variety from Boba,  fruit mixes and tea with other additions.  My first time here but other family members have been. We had matcha with pudding, 1 in a cute bottle.   We came for the bottle!  Isn't s/he cute!

Name: Gong Cha
Author: David Brotman
Review: Most of the other milk tea restaurants close to Union Square had already closed for the evening and this place just happened to be across the street from the hotel I was staying at. The space inside Gong Cha is really small so some people were waiting outside and some inside. I ordered a simple milk tea with boba from one of the touchscreen kiosks and waited next to a staircase where I had a good view of the staff preparing the orders. I waited, and waited and waited some more as the staff called out the orders ahead of me. I watched as the female staff poured out the milk mixtures from nearly empty jugs. After waiting nearly a half hour, she finally got to my order. The milk tea tasted ok but not worth waiting a half hour for.

Name: Gong Cha
Author: FrauAM M
Review: Try pumpkin milk tea for first time. Nice!

Name: Tpumps
Address: 985 E Hillsdale Boulevard, Metro Center Blvd, Foster City
Author: Meng Pang
Review: Very good milk tea and tea. Love it and there is free sample to try. Nice service. But no seat inside.

Name: Tpumps
Address: 985 E Hillsdale Boulevard, Metro Center Blvd, Foster City
Author: James
Review:
If you're here for a premium milk tea experience, you've come to the wrong place. But if you're looking for a cheap, sweet drink, consider Tpumps as they're known for using syrups and flavored teas.

What I like about them is that they have affordable drinks ($6 for a 32 oz) and the boba doesn't cost extra.

They also have premade monthly specials and are quite fast.

Name: Tpumps
Address: 985 E Hillsdale Boulevard, Metro Center Blvd, Foster City
Author: Kiran
Review: This Boba place offers a satisfactory experience. The service is friendly, and they boast a wide range of flavors, both fruit-based and milk-based. They even provide options to combine two or three flavors in one Boba tea. However, it's worth noting that they use formula powder instead of original milk. I opted for almond milk, so I can't speak for the regular milk powder, but my overall experience was decent.

Name: Tpumps
Address: 985 E Hillsdale Boulevard, Metro Center Blvd, Foster City
Author: Theo Wiggin
Review: Very small interior, no seating. The staff was super nice and helpful, the service was very quick even though there was a big line. The boba was delicious and pretty inexpensive. Overall would recommend!

Name: Tpumps
Address: 985 E Hillsdale Boulevard, Metro Center Blvd, Foster City
Author: Janaki V
Review: Seems they have artificial flavors which is not fun. Trying the regular oolong tea, with honey boba and fresh ginger.

Name: Tea Ever -Daly City
Author: Jasmin Kirvin
Review: My sister and I got the fresh milk strawberry boba and we both thought it was amazing. My brother-in-law got the strawberry smoothie with cheese creama and it was also fire. I used mobile order and it was ready when I got there. Great experience!

Name: Tea Ever -Daly City
Author: Stefanie S
Review: Really really old and outdated boba tea place, more of a cafe with snacks than boba tea places. The milk tea are all hand shook with powders. Not recommended at all. 鸡蛋仔 is so hard.

Name: Tea Ever -Daly City
Author: Matthew N.
Review: amazing place, nice atmosphere, would’ve been better if there were more staff there at the time but i won’t blame them for that

Name: Tea Ever -Daly City
Author: Jennifer Z.
Review:
The drinks are good,  but they have a standard that even though you don't choose any toppings, they add the clear ones for you and i hate them. This is my 2nd time here and they did the same thing.  Please stop. It's ruining the drink. If customers decide to not have toppings, please don't add that! It's gross to me. I don't even want my drink anymore.

Went back on another day, their purple rice drink is too sweet. I think the milk was half and half. I threw away the drink.

Name: Tea Ever -Daly City
Author: Pyae Swam Htet Myat
Review: The ramen and tea combo is such a good deal. 9/10 for the ramen.

Name: 1011 Sip TEA SF
Author: Matt B
Review: Fairly innocuous storefront, and a snoopy themed interior. They make a pretty good boba milk tea, and use non-dairy milk as their default.

Name: 1011 Sip TEA SF
Author: Jem Le
Review: They have an interesting selection of flavored teas and many can be served hot. Their drink is on the sweeter side even though I had mine at 25%. The purple yam milk tea I had came with boba included already. Will try their cold drinks next time I visit.

Name: 1011 Sip TEA SF
Author: LLL
Review: I love their gum 🍑 peach!!!!!!! I tried both drinks from the beauty section and it was really good for the Apple one, cucumber not my type! But for some store willing to use some high food cost ingredients, I would definitely come back again. Love the decorations too! Highly recommended this location and will definitely come back again!

Name: 1011 Sip TEA SF
Author: Ketty Nakamura
Review: The cutest Snoopy themed tea shop ever!  Compared to Oahu we don’t have very much Chinese flavors for tea and toppings so it was refreshing to try all these different drinks.  My favorites were the Apple Red Dates and Lavender Goji Berry.  The Grapefruit Lychee was yummy too!  The Cucumber Kumquat is very strong in its cucumber flavor so unless you absolutely love cucumbers in combination with kumquat I don’t recommend it.  Otherwise, I’ll definitely be back when I’m in SF!

Name: 1011 Sip TEA SF
Author: Sisi Chen
Review: Got their most popular Taiwanese milk tea with tapioca. They cannot do 0% sweet on this drink so got 25%. The drink was tasty with a bit of coffee flavor to it. The boba was chewy and sweet. Will try their oolong next. Hopefully they can do 0% sweetness for other drinks.

Name: Tea Hut
Author: Mekah Kaur
Review: This is a cute place, the atmosphere is chill. There is a screen where you can self-order which makes my life easier since I have modifications and take time. I got jasmine milk tea with honey boba with 50% less sweetness, it was delicious! I definitely giving this place a try.

Name: Tea Hut
Author: Milton Chui
Review:
Jasmine Milk Tea, regular sweet, crystal and regular boba...Crystal boba was clumped together.
Order was very fast. They have a self-serve kiosk with tap pay.

Name: Tea Hut
Author: Catarina Zhang He
Review:
Used to be my go-to place when I’d be in the area.
The past month or two I’ve gone in 3 times and every visit has been worse than the previous one.
My most recent visit I ordered a Mango Coco drink (my go-to) and this time it came with grapefruit (!!!) inside, and a few pieces of my non-chosen topping. I called and the employee said it has always come with grapefruit. There’s a reason I ordered Mango Coco and not other mango drinks, I didn’t want any citrus & I’ve ordered a Mango Coco before.
The previous visit I saw two employees on their phone in plain sight by the back, while there was an ordering line, I wanted to order at the counter and no one came to assist me. A third employee exited from the back and made drinks.

Name: Tea Hut
Author: Mei Fu Lee
Review: I go to the post office next to tea hut pretty often. I was waiting for teaspoon to open here for such a long time, but it finally opened as tea hut. There aren't that boba shops in this area, other than up towards 19th and ocean.  Usually there's a long line and I heard that service can get slow here. The one time there was no line, I decided to try it. Inside is pretty small with no sitting space. They have a pretty extensive menu with a lot of variety of teas. I ordered the lemon lime green for my friend. She likes her drinks to be not as sweet and said that the drink was perfect. They put in pretty big slices of lemon and lime in the drink. I also tried their oolong milk tea with boba. Their boba was really well made and the milk tea tasted great. They also have other types of oolong teas that seem fun to try. I would love to come again, but it does get busy quickly.

Name: Tea Hut
Author: Jasmine Lo
Review: the worst boba shop ever. I paid for extra boba for osmanthus rice milk tea and they didn't even give me a single taro ball or boba. do not buy at this place. the milk tea also tasted bad.

Name: TP TEA Berkeley
Author: Safira CA
Review: This is one of the best boba place in Berkeley, it’s very close to the Southgate/sather gate of UC Berkeley. This place offer great variety of boba, some of them are nice, but some of them have weird taste lol. Whenever I visit this place, I always try the new variant, that’s why I ended up with the weird taste ones 😅 but yeah overall I like the boba, if you want a safe choice, just pick the original one. The price is a bit higher than other boba places. And yeah, They have 3-4 tables for seating area.

Name: TP TEA Berkeley
Author: Sid Shah
Review: I love the milk teas from here, they're very strong, giving the boba a little more depth than simply sugar milk. This is a personal preference though, as many of my friends dislike the strength. I do also think that 100% sweet isn't too sweet, and I normally do 75%-80%.

Name: TP TEA Berkeley
Author: Andrew Parker
