Review: All of the drinks we got were delicious! The fries were good too! The boba texture was perfect. Friendly and knowledgeable staff as well. Some people with us had questions about ingredients due to allergies, and the staff made some good suggestions on what they might like.

Name: TP TEA Berkeley
Author: Mahik Kaur
Review: I never really liked boba, but TP TEA DELIVERED. I usually try new flavours every time I go, and they haven’t disappointed. Also, I feel like the amount of sugar you request can really make or break the taste of the drink, and service quality can vary depending on demand. Overall, really enjoy this place

Name: TP TEA Berkeley
Author: Amish Gupta
Review: Having tried all the boba spots near campus, TP Tea is objectively the best boba in terms of price, quality of tea, and preparation. They make sure all their drinks follow a strict standard and it reflects in the taste

Name: E Tea
Author: Anthony
Review:
2/5.

Was in the area with few friends and we all had a craving for some boba. E Tea was close by, so why not give this spot a try.

The Food: I went with a Mango Sago with Lychee Jelly and my friend had a Black Milk Tea with boba. The mango sago wasn't that great. The mango flavor was incredibly overwhelming and sweet. The Black Milk Tea was bitter and had a little burnt after taste. Not sure how that happened, but we ended up just trashing it. Boba pearls and lychee jelly tasted good though.

The Service: Service was good! Staff was friendly when we walked in and browsed the menu.

The Atmosphere: Nice ad small boba shop. It looks like they serve food as well inside, and from the dishes we saw some people eat. It looked very good. Maybe the food is just better than the drinks.

Overall, a 2/5. The milk tea's we had were not good. It could've just been that particular day, but I don't think I'd recommend this place.

Name: E Tea
Author: Eating with Crystal
Review: The guy that checked us out was awesome, nice and made us feel very welcomed even with our service dog Samson. He definitely deserves to be promoted. He even came outside to hand us our food to go. Remembered me when I came back in to check and had our drinks ready and helped us. Amazing food and drinks and excellent customer service.

Name: E Tea
Author: T L
Review: Generous of good quality beef, will definitely come back again, $16 for the hot pot

Name: E Tea
Author: Annie Bennet
Review:
The one star is not a joke. In the end of my drink, I saw a mosquito floating on the bottom of lemons.

Typical Chinese way of business...

Name: E Tea
Author: K D
Review:
Ordered from here because I work nearby and didn't want to pay/wait for 2 separate orders of food and milk tea.

I love the .coconut paradise drink I ordered with the salty cheese foam!

The baked chicken Ramen was fine but really lacked flavor.  Plus side is that it was all packed separately and easy to store when not ready to eat!

Name: Tiger Tea & Juice
Author: Steph Wong
Review:
Since truedan closed, and I saw that Tiger Tea was opening up, I was excited to try since I've been to the Burlingame location.

You order off a kiosk which is pretty simple. You can either sit and wait for your drink or go outside. They can notify you by text if you'd like to know when your order is ready.

I got the matcha latte with boba and a watermelon slush. I really liked the boba as it was soft but still chewy. The watermelon slush was fresh blended watermelon with ice and I thought it was quite refreshing.

Staff is friendly and service is good.

Name: Tiger Tea & Juice
Author: Teason Miao
Review:
The wife was craving for some nighttime boba so we came to Tiger Tea & Juice. We got their oolong milk tea, coconut pudding, and brown rice tea. Glad they're so close to us!

We ordered online and it was ready within 10 minutes. Very quick and efficient.

We got there late at night so there were less people but it's always nice to see the giant black tiger logo. Reminds me of me . They had music playing and it was chill. Definitely one of our go to boba spots.

- BlackTigerEats

Name: Tiger Tea & Juice
Author: Josh Schoen
Review: I ordered the specialty drink, Da Hong Pao 大紅袍 milk tea. Honestly one of the best milk teas I have ever had in the bay area. The tea quality was superior and you could taste the full depth of the flavor, even with the addition of milk & sugar. The tea was very strong, but not at all bitter. I added boba as well, which was perfectly soft and chewy, and had an amazing black sugar taste. I will be making the 45 minute trip for this again!

Name: Tiger Tea & Juice
Author: Excel Ang
Review: Delicious fresh drinks. I love how strong and fragrant the tea is. Fast attentive service. Clean modern decor.

Name: Tiger Tea & Juice
Author: Tina R
Review: This is my favorite boba place in Daly City! They give you a discount on milk tea on Mondays from 11 am to 7 pm. The black sugar milk tea with boba is always my go to drink!

Name: Tiger Tea & Juice San Mateo
Author: Edison Huang
Review: They're really expanding here. Thought they're only in Burlingame but they have a few locations now. Tried their orchid milk tea and strawberry jasmine with salted cheese. Didn't really get any orchid flavors, but I honestly don't even know what to expect lol. The tea was good though! The strawberry drink was fine despite 0% sweet. Overall 4/5 for us. This is a good  addition to the neighborhood!

Name: Tiger Tea & Juice San Mateo
Author: Dharma M
Review:
New tiger tea shop in San Mateo.  I tried the one at Burlingame before. It is known for their black sugar boba molk tea. It is a small store. This place, as I remember, used to be a boba shop, too.  The shop is pretty neat. You ordered via the touch scree kiosk. It is good it advise what sugar level to be chosen. We ordered grape fruit, and it suggested 50% sugar and mentioned less than that would be bitter, and it was correct. Pricewise, it is considered cheaper than other boba shops. The drink doesn't look like the one on the menu. It is supposed to be red ish, but it turns out to be a different color, and the worker said it was green tea. The grape fruit is very light taste on the grapefruit. I wish it was stronger.
The good thing about this shop is you can substitute the milk.

Name: Tiger Tea & Juice San Mateo
Author: Son Lee
Review: Wonderful boba! In fact the best tasting boba balls I tasted. If you're not into a lightly sweetened boba balls, then you might not like it.

Name: Tiger Tea & Juice San Mateo
Author: Vivian Lee
Review:
I have a major sweet tooth and this tea was incredibly SWEET. May want to get a lower % of sweetness for the Hojicha.

I still wasn’t able to sleep that night, so at least you know they use tea.

Name: Tiger Tea & Juice San Mateo
Author: Flora Tsui
Review: I got the avomango shake and my friend got the brown sugar boba milk tea. Both good. Reasonable wait time. Cute ambience. Would do it again. Happy customers. This place might not be for you if you like an extensive menu as their selection is quite small.

Name: District Tea
Author: Claudia K M
Review:
District Tea in SF's Mission District is a hidden gem. Stumbling upon this quaint spot, I was pleasantly surprised by the plethora of drink options it offered.

The Mangochada was a tropical dream, beautifully balanced and incredibly refreshing, while the Spam Masubi was a savory delight that left me craving more.

District Tea boasts an impressive array of innovative tea blends, catering to diverse preferences. The creative flair extends beyond the drinks to the charming ambiance, providing a cozy retreat in the midst of the bustling Mission District.

However, it's important to note that District Tea primarily operates as a takeout spot, lacking seating for dine-in. Nevertheless, for a unique tea adventure in the heart of SF, District Tea is a must-visit.

Name: District Tea
Author: Mitchell Hanson
Review:
Got the assam float which is black tea base and vanilla ice cream (one of their more popular drinks) and added tapioca boba. The subtle bitterness of the black tea went very well with the sweet ice cream and Boba. $8.25 for a large and add-ons were very reasonably priced.

Service was fast and very nice asking questions. The store itself is located in a slightly awkward location and there is only standing room to order. It is a place to grab a drink but not necessarily to stay at.

Name: District Tea
Author: Sage Lee
Review: we got the picnic box and took it to the park. it was a great deal, convenient, and delicious. we chose the katsu sandos, mix onigiri (1 salmon 1 tuna), and takoyaki for our box, with jasmine milk tea & oolong milk tea. the food came out pretty quickly and it was really good. highly recommend as a little brunch activity with friends!

Name: District Tea
Author: Viennelyn Maier
Review:
District Tea is a hidden gem nestled in the hustle and bustle of the Mission District in San Francisco. My husband and I ordered the chicken katsu sandwich, katsu sando, spam musubi, tonkatsu musubi, and a Taro Latte with oat milk topped with crema. Everything was incredibly delicious, and I especially loved the Taro Latte. It was made with real taro chunks and was the first Taro Latte I've had that didn't taste like it was loaded with tons of sugar. One thing that's really great about District Tea is that they use real ingredients and no artificial flavorings or sweeteners. The prices are also very reasonable.

Overall, we'd love to visit District Tea again for the delicious food and drinks and friendly service. The only caveat is that I wish parking were easier in the area.

Name: District Tea
Author: Angelica SF
Review:
District Tea is in Mission - in between 17th and 18th street. Easy to get to using Bart by getting off 16th street. It’s not the safest neighborhood, but it is home to so many amazing food places.

District Tea’s a bit small, but the minimalist style & high ceiling kind of makes the place big. You can order in person or via the self-ordering machine. I think they prefer self ordering since they get so busy like today. Anyway, it took a while for me to order because no one’s really in FOH, most of them are in the back prepping food and drinks.

They have a diverse menu from drinks to snacks. I got matcha latte and corn dogs (2 pcs, half cheese & half sausage). The matcha latte is good but not how I preferred it. It’s more on the bitter side which is what some people prefer. I like mine to be sweeter, aha. The corn dogs are great - very delicious. I think they use the Berkshire sausage. If not, it tastes similar to it.

Service is fast. The lady packing the order is very courteous and accommodating. Will definitely come back to try other drinks when we’re in the area.

Name: Cool Tea Bar
Author: Julie Taeko
Review: Excellent! Absolutely loved visiting this shop after our Barbie lookalike contest in Chinatown for autumn moon festival. They have a really cute “Made in San Francisco” neon sign. We got 3 drinks, all of which were delicious!

Name: Cool Tea Bar
Author: Martin Aki Tsui
Review:
Updated on May 27, 2024. Despite a slow day with only 2 customers, it took this person more than 15 minutes to make my roasted milk tea, 30% sweet, less ice, NO toppings. Previously it took 30 minutes during New Year Festival when then place was overwhelmed with customers yet understaffed (they should not accept orders both via the kiosk and cashier when they were not delivering the orders in a remotely similar pace. Hence dropping the review to 3 stars, but specifically the service to 1 star.

Original: A little pricey but for a large milk tea thr volume is pretty impressive. Didn't get to try their oolong milk tea, but roasted milk tea isn't bad. Will come back to try more.

Name: Cool Tea Bar
Author: 龔振勝
Review: Was nice that I was told the thing I wanted would take an extra 10 minutes, because they just ran out. I had no problem with that, and it was nice of them to let me know.  Wish I was given a spoon though, because I totally forgot I had 紅豆/azuki beans sitting at the bottom, and it was a waste of throwing them away at the end because I couldn't scoop them out.  But no big deal.  Tea is actually fresh here, and you get your choice of sugar level.  No spot to sit down and enjoy, just some chairs to wait to grab your drink.

Name: Cool Tea Bar
Author: Diana Gonzalez
Review: Very good! Got the Matcha Milk tea with Lychee pop on 75% sugar. Only $6! Unheard of in this city. The yeah is very good and they loaded up the jelly. Only thing I’d with they offered pop as well but that’s neither here nor there. Really like that they offer sugar percentages that way you can get your preferred sweetness.

Name: Cool Tea Bar
Author: ANNE YIM
Review: Small boba store. There was only one person working at the time we came in. I ordered the grass jelly roasted milk tea, pearl milk tea, jasmine milk tea and oolong milk tea all with boba. They have regular and large.  Their regular is my normal large where I live. Different levels of sweetness to adjust to tasting.  Had to wait awhile because the pearls had 5 minutes before it was ready and there was only one person to order and make drinks. Overall,  the drink was good.

Name: Mr & Mrs Tea House
Author: Valerie Pugs
Review: As soon as I arrived, an employee walked past me and it left a strong smell of poop. After smelling this, we still decided to order and we got our drinks, they did not taste as described. Customer service was bad and the person at the register was rude and wasn’t helpful with the menu. She seemed like she was in a bad mood. The drinks didn’t taste good, it seemed like they were from instant packets.

Name: Mr & Mrs Tea House
Author: Y Xu
Review:
Our experience here was extremely disappointing. We arrived and ordered 2 High Mountain Oolong Tea Creama drinks without being informed that all their beverages come with default ice. Surprisingly, we weren't given the choice of having ice or not. When our drinks arrived, we politely requested one without ice, as we weren't aware that all their drinks were served cold.

The staff member at the front counter complained about the default cold drinks and questioned how we could have known. We requested the removal of the ice and asked for room temperature drinks. And we have to pay extra $0.50 to remove the ice. She proceeded to the kitchen to fulfill our request.

However, when a man emerged from the kitchen with a stern expression and asked us, "What the hell do you want?" in an intimidating tone, it left us feeling genuinely frightened. All we wanted was a room-temperature drink, and amidst the tension, we managed to explain our preference to him. He went back to the kitchen and eventually prepared a room-temperature drink.

I can't adequately convey how scared I felt; it was a truly unpleasant experience. I have no intention of returning, and while the drink was acceptable, it was rather bland, almost like water.

Name: Mr & Mrs Tea House
Author: Calvin Tong
Review: Honestly our go to neighborhood boba spot! We think it slightly edges over Little Sweet but not by much. Our go to drinks are winter melon lemon juice, high mountain oolong milk tea, berry berry, and mango drinks. Service is fine, they'll recognize you if you're a regular, and there are just four stools inside to wait for or drink your drinks.

Name: Mr & Mrs Tea House
Author: Scott Wong
Review: Family wanted boba and we checked this place out. Long line outside but was hoping a quick service. Unfortunately, we waited 30 minutes for maybe 14 orders ahead of us. Taro and Oreo boba was really tasty. Just be ready to wait. I’d give 5 starts if the wait wasn’t so long.

Name: Mr & Mrs Tea House
Author: Fumiko Hoeft
Review: Kids love their boba but the service is so so slow. Especially when you order through DoorDash. Took over 45min after asking status. In person orders seem ti be somewhat better. Note of caution!

Name: Asha Tea House
Author: James
Review:
One of my favorite milk tea places in the Bay Area! They serve high-quality teas and accept reusable boba containers! However, their straws and containers can be industrially-composted as well.

They have a set menu but also have seasonal flavors. You can order at the counter or at the tablet.

It is often filled with college students as the campus is nearby.

Name: Asha Tea House
Author: Alice L.
Review: My go-to for traditional non-milk teas; I favor hojicha & matcha. The seasonal flavors such as yuzu & asian pear add another level of variety & complexity & you can get it unsweetened as well. There are local baked goods but they disappear quickly, but I was able to snag a black sesame cookie. The matcha sparkler hits the spot on a warm day too.

Name: Asha Tea House
Author: Natenapa Serdynski
Review: I love the architecture and atmosphere of this place. Green tea is excellent! We didn't think this is the best place for Boba. (My husband and my daughter are the Boba freak!) I LOVE the menu. Very different and well done. I thought this place could be more cleaner. Flies are just everywhere and floor are very messy. But overall, come and try their many choices of hot tea.

Name: Asha Tea House
Author: Aayushi Trivedi
Review: This spot is aesthetically unique with it’s minimalistic modern architecture! The drinks hit the spot just as equally (pictured iced chai latte and matcha latte). While super flavorful and aromatic, the infused spices in the chai latte are quite strong and would suggest getting a hot version of the drink without boba.

Name: Asha Tea House
Author: Ushko Mi
Review: We didn't even finish our drinks, that's how unpleasant they were (blueberry tea and strawberry cream soda, the blueberry tea just tasted like dirty water and the soda was like a badly flavored milk). Not much customer service interaction, because we got it to go and you order from a tablet, so nothing to review on that either way. It was surprisingly extremely crowded (maybe there's something better than what we ordered?), so no tables were available (it does seem like the kind of place where people order 1 drink and sit for quite some time). The prices are fine, if the product was good (unfortunately, it was not). Definitely will not return, very disappointing.

Name: Cha Thai Tea
Author: Kristina
Review: I enjoyed visiting Cha Thai tea! The employees are always very welcoming and helpful with their recommendations. I really enjoyed this drink (I think it was a coconut avocado special? I forgot to look at the exact name of the drink) and pandan custard toast. I also enjoyed the mango coco the previous time I came here. They have a smaller, simpler menu that's not too overwhelming but the ingredients seem very fresh and tasty! I'll definitely be back!

Name: Cha Thai Tea
Author: Shayna Montero
Review: I paid a visit with some good friends of mine, & it was delicious! I got the Thai tea frappe & smoked salmon toast, I’m a big fan of both now. My friends got the ham & cheese toast, the mango berry toast with hazelnut spread, & classic Thai tea’s with boba. They said everything they got was really good as well! Highly highly recommend stopping by to try them out, I’ve been telling everyone I know it’s my new go to spot when in Berkeley :)

Name: Cha Thai Tea
Author: miko mimiko
Review:
"Thai Milk Tea with Boba": The best Thai milk tea in Berkeley uses quality tea. It's very authentic and the best tea I have ever had. It reminds me of my visit to Thailand.

"Pang Cha": So good! I like the bread topping on top; it's perfect with Thai tea blend.

"Mango sticky rice" with fresh mango and homemade coconut sauce sounds absolutely delicious! The combination of sweet mango, sticky rice, and creamy coconut sauce.

"Pandan Custard Toast": Not too sweet; their pandan is so fresh and goes very well with bread toast.

Name: Cha Thai Tea
Author: Chananan Pieamkong
Review: I drove from Sacramento to get real taste of thai tea and it’s worth it! today I ordered Pang Cha, Mango coco, Lime black tea and pandan toast every thing is so good I really enjoyed it and want to try other item in the menu also the vibe of cafe is so nice and service is great too friendly staff !

Name: Cha Thai Tea
Author: Peiheng Zhang
Review: The oat milk Thai tea is very unique in a good way, esp if you’re in the mood for something not super sweet as the traditional Thai tea. The pandan toast is a little disappointing tbh. I think it’s mainly because 1. The toast is not crispy and hot as it should be 2. The pandan sauce is too dense and doesn’t taste super SEA:( feels like too much gelatin in it…

Name: Mr. Green Bubble | Oakland
Author: James
Review:
This Mr. Green Bubble location offers exquisite service and has a lovely interior with plenty of dining tables. You can order up front or at one of the Snackpass kiosks.

They offer specialty drinks as well as good milk tea (although I don't recommend their Earl Grey, which is one of two decaf options).

Prices are fair and quality is superb.

Name: Mr. Green Bubble | Oakland
Author: Miles Rowley
Review: Mr. Green Bubble is my go-to boba place! The milk teas and food are delicious. I will definitely be coming here again very soon!!

Name: Mr. Green Bubble | Oakland
Author: J L
Review:
Ordered for delivery.  We wanted to celebrate the end of the school week and the beginning of the Presidents' holiday week with a nice treat for the kids.  We ordered from this new place (to us) and truly enjoyed the meal.  We got some popcorn chicken,  deep fried tofu,  egg Puff pancake,  Thai iced tea with boba, popping boba, and lychee bits, and lastly an oreo milk tea drink.  The tofu was really good and well seasoned and fried.  The popcorn chicken was well seasoned and fried too and the size of the nuggets were good for a nice mouthful of flavorful chicken.  The Thai iced tea with all the add-ons was tasty but too sweet, but my son loved it.  The oreo milk tea was a bit bland and not sweet at all. The egg Puff pancake was tasty  and had heart shaped puffs. I didn't get a chance to take a photo of the egg Puff cuz everyone ate it all before I could take a shot at it.  🥲😒😆😅

Overall, this place is pretty good and next time I'm in the neighborhood I may just stop by and try out the food fresh and not delivered.

Name: Mr. Green Bubble | Oakland
Author: Andres M
Review:
At first glance, you might believe it's a typical boba drink pastry shop. Small space, simple design, green letter hinting of a potential vegan-esque feel and approach.

I took a look at the menu. And it was quite surprising at the variety of snacks and drinks they offered . I was in the mood for popcorn chicken , and to my surprise they had some of the best popcorn chicken within walking distance of where I reside.

There a popcorn chicken was exceptional and they have a variety of options in terms of the way you want it cooked and things you might want to add or avoid. Chicken fully cooked.Do stress if you want your popcorn chicken extra crispy as that is the only thing I would have improved

I also ordered the lemonade and strawberry lemonade and the packaging they come in ( meaning the cup) is environmentally friendly and also makes it easy to share amongst each other in your group. The drink could use less sugar as it was a bit over sweet.

The online ordering process was quick and simple, and how's your order track live with updates and approximate time for you to pick up your order.  Parking is limited to the main street. Indoor lounge holds approximately 3 to 4 tables. No matter your budget there's always a great value for you with great food waiting on the other end.

The place was clean will it was warm and inviting, the customer service was quick, friendly and helpful.

I overlooked this place all the time while walking down Piedmont. It just became one of my go-to places

Name: Mr. Green Bubble | Oakland
Author: Meichi Chan
Review:
I love their tofu and mix mushroom.the best ever.
Dark roast oolong tea, pink lady, grapefruit paradise and fruity tea are my go to drinks.

Seating area nice and cozy, good to hangout with friends.

Name: Inner Richmond- Little Sweet
Author: Ian Kidder
Review: Got the lemon with green tea, aloe, and agar and really liked it. 25% sugar was as expected and not too sweet. I wish the green tea had a slightly stronger flavor, but otherwise enjoyed it and the price was decent. Favorite spot near me so far

Name: Inner Richmond- Little Sweet
Author: Pratik Naik
Review:
About the Boba Tea Place:
This boba tea place is situated in the lively Inner Richmond area and boasts ample parking options, making it a convenient destination. The only quirk I noticed was their choice of songs, which didn't quite match my personal taste but seemed to appeal to others.

About the Drinks:
During my visit, I ordered a matcha milk tea, and it turned out to be a good choice. I opted for boba and brown sugar agar, both of which complemented the drink well. The brown sugar boba was a particularly enjoyable addition. One notable feature is that the drinks are non-dairy by default, but customization is available to suit individual preferences.

Overall, the boba tea place offers a pleasing experience, with a variety of options and customization available. For its quality beverages and convenient location, a four-star rating seems fitting.

Name: Inner Richmond- Little Sweet
Author: RJ
Review:
Absolutely loved this place for milk teas with boba and agar 🧋 We tried the creamy thai green milk and brown sugar milk tea. I tried Agar for the first time and loved it. Both the drinks were perfect as customized although a little pricey. The drinks are made with non dairy products by default but you can customize it. Definitely recommend this place for anyone in the area.

You have to place an order through their system/monitor in store or online and the drinks are made for you. There is little to no interaction with the staff. Service can be quick based on how crowded it is.

Name: Inner Richmond- Little Sweet
Author: Lingling
Review: Randomly picked this boba shop, and I really liked the watermelon strawberry w/ salt cream here—it tasted good, especially with the fresh fruits. The mix of salty and sweet was just right. I chose 25% sweetness, and it was a bit sweet for me. If you're not into sweet stuff, maybe go for a lower sweetness level or you might find it too sugary. But overall, it's a tasty treat with options for different tastes.

Name: Inner Richmond- Little Sweet
Author: Lilizhu Lee
Review:
I have always loved communication in any kind of way! And I think this is one of them :)) I usually ordered Iced Coffee from Little Sweet on my day off but I scrolled through the menu list today and I could not find it, so I left the note! And the staff kindly took her time to give me some response! So, I just want to shout out for this small little service that created my smile this morning!!! Thank you so much for making me good coffee every time and communicating with me. Your great service means so much :))

Updated my review here!!!

Anna and Angelina are the best!!! They both make a very good coffee every single time!!!! I love the old recipe for iced coffee and I feel the same way until now :) Shout out to both of you!!!! I am working at Philz Coffee but I love coming here on my off day because it is also nearby and love having sight glass coffee taking a little break from Philz a little!! And they both have made me impressed every time!!! Thank you

Name: Tea World
Author: Катерина Савкова
Review: Super tasty 😍 i got tiger milk tea and it was perfect! Very soft bobas, as i like

Name: Tea World
Author: Jon Horvath
Review: Came in with my daughter first time tonight  for ice cream - ordered a toffee crunch double scoop. They gave me an espresso toffee crunch, the "espresso" was written on the line above the toffee crunch and Kim did not correct me. I told Kim i didn't like espresso and asked for something else. They hummed and hawed and after a few minutes reluctantly gave me one scoop. I will never go back - Kim's weak service cost you a new customer (or 2) over one scoop of ice cream.

Name: Tea World
Author: Ya-Ping Chen
Review:
The ice cream is too soft.  This is an insult to ice cream, and when we got the ice cream,it was dripping like someone put it in the microwave.

-too soft
- too sweet
- mixed everything together and looks not good

+ the server is nice.

Name: Tea World
Author: Bear Dog
Review: Five stars because they require masks and the employees wear masks which is important because we’re still dealing with a pandemic despite some thinking otherwise, they have a good variety of flavors but it’s not an overwhelming menu like other some bubble tea menus, and the tea was great. Had a good flavor without tasting too sweet and there was a good balance of ice and jelly. Overall very pleased and would definitely recommend.

Name: Tea World
Author: Sabrina S.
Review: This place is great if you like to customize your drink. They have many flavors and toppings to choose from. I got rose oolong milt tea with crystal and regular boba. They have Bamboo straws as well.If Boba is not your thing they serve Mitchels ice cream! We had Kalil as our barista today and he was very sweet! He made our milk teas quickly. There was lots of people in line, and it looked like he was working by himself. He did a great job holding it down!

Name: Vital Tea Leaf
Author: Kaitlin V
Review: I had an absolutely amazing experience at this location! The staff were incredible, especially the girl who helped me—she was extremely sweet, friendly, and knowledgeable. She did a fantastic job recommending different teas based on what I was looking for, making the entire experience both welcoming and educational! The service was phenomenal, and the tea selection was extensive. The free tea sampling really enhanced the experience, allowing me to choose the perfect teas. I will definitely be coming back! I bought the Blue People Ginseng Oolong, and it was absolutely delicious!

Name: Vital Tea Leaf
Author: B. H.
Review: Amazing shop! Wonderful owner...great tasting! We were able to try four (4) different kinds of teas and received a thorough explanation of the beneficial properties of each of them as well as directions for how to make a proper cup. Friendly, helpful and enjoyable experience!

Name: Vital Tea Leaf
Author: Mazal Shavratov
Review: One of the most wonderful experiences I had in San Francisco. We came in at the quite early time in the day on Saturday. The owner was doing a presentation about different teas. He spent a whole hour showcasing and explaining black, green, white tea and the importance of them. I learned a lot of interesting facts and he is very entertaining. I very much recommend going there if you are in Chinatown. After an hour of this presentation I was able to buy some of the teas we tried and the owner was kind enough to show us a wonderful lunch spot to try close by. Overall very highly recommend. Will go again!

Name: Vital Tea Leaf
Author: Morgan Jenkins
Review: Free tea tasting is an absolute blast! The main employee is very funny but informative as well. We enjoyed our ~20 minute tasting and all walked away with a bag or two of newly purchased loose leaf tea to enjoy.

Name: Vital Tea Leaf
Author: Alyssa McGowan
Review: This place is every tea 🍵 fans dream! They offer free tea tasting paired with information about each tea and its benefits. The young woman conducting the tea tasting is super knowledgeable of all the tea’s properties and how they help all different kinds of ailments. My fiancé has high blood pressure and trouble sleeping. When asked what teas would help these issues, she jumped into action and gave recommendations followed up by a taste of those suggested teas. Did you know you’re supposed to wash your tea leaves before steeping to get rid of dust and grime? Me either! But now we do and it will become an unconscious practice when returning home. Very friendly staff and wide variety of tea. A must try if you’re ever on Grant in SF. 10 stars!

Name: Top Up Tea
Author: JR Sanchez
Review: This is a cute area my wife and I often pass on our way to the beach. Normally we go to HappyLemon but we wanted to try something new and so we chose TopUp. The cashier left a bad taste in our mouth with her rude attitude to the couple before us as well as being rude to us. She seemed to be drooling and kept her right hand to her mouth as she took our order. She had a very condescending tone and a demeaning attitude when telling us they could not substitute. My wife saw the cashiers eyes scanning her up and down making the environment feel very hostile. I ordered 75% sweetness but my drink didn’t even taste like it was 50%. The drinks are also overpriced. $13 for two mediocre drinks and bad service. We’ll be taking our business elsewhere from now on.

Name: Top Up Tea
Author: Nancy Guadalupe Vasquez
Review: I love this place! They have very tasty drinks, so many options, and it's easy to order (no human interaction necessary if you like that). Quick service as well and reasonable prices. My favorites are the fries, pink lemonade, and the classic.

Name: Top Up Tea
Author: Jessica T
Review:
Went here with my Cousin and it was legit tasty Boba Tea. They had a TON of different options and combinations! The outside is unassuming, but it's much nicer on the inside.
If you like Boba, then you gotta try it here!

Name: Top Up Tea
Author: Paul-Elie Nauleau
Review: Great place with a variety of different combos you don’t find everywhere. They also have Popcorn chicken and Gyzoa! You can order ahead online or at the kiosk in store. Parking is easy

Name: Top Up Tea
Author: Karen Pacheco
Review: Located in Alameda, This is an awesome place with good boba and tasty treats like bacon fries and popcorn chicken. It’s a small shop that gets your order out to you fairly quickly. They have a kiosk where you order so you don’t really interact with anyone. They also sell some pretty cool looking glass bottles if that’s something you’re interested in.

Name: Urban Ritual Cafe
Author: Hilda Liu
Review: Nice unique tea place in the Hayes! The drinks are not overly sweet. They deliver the drinks fast. Some people were working there and I can tell it’s gonna be cozy for them :)!

Name: Urban Ritual Cafe
Author: Grace Lin
Review: Interesting menu. Ordered the oolong tea but was a bit astringent 🥲

Name: Urban Ritual Cafe
Author: Ray
Review: OMG OMG OMG Urban Ritual is Amaazzziiiinnnggg! I am a huge fan of the food blogger Feed MeiMei and had to try her signature collab drink with the cafe. It’s called ‘Feed Me Ube’. I ordered it with the honey boba topping and my word it was decadent! The Ube topping was rich and creamy and provided a warm nutty taste. The honey boba was a great addition and overall the drink was so worth it!

Name: Urban Ritual Cafe
Author: Samyukta Rao
Review: Great atmosphere. Tried the feed me ube and was a little disappointed. It had a very grainy over powering coconut instead of creamy coconut milk.

Name: Urban Ritual Cafe
Author: Kristina Setka
Review:
Large menu of teas, boba and more. Appreciate the option to customize: milk, level of sweetness & ice amount.  -2 stars for ambience: the tables are often sticky, floor looks dirty, and the pillows in the benches/alcoves should be removed or cleaned regularly. (It’s looking worn & dirty)

Otherwise highly recommend

Name: YY Coffee & Milk Tea
Author: J L
Review: Yy has been our go-to spot after the 1st visit. Love their super fruit tea, salty plum lemon soda, oolong milk tea with mochi and crema, Jasmin green milk tea and four seasons milk tea. We usually get 50% sugar no ice for the milk tea, with their unique barley popping boba.

Name: YY Coffee & Milk Tea
Author: Ruofei Du
Review:
The quality control needs to be more consistent.. we had very different taste of our favorite cherry tea recently :(

The summer peach fruit tea, cherry green tea, and Hong Kong milk tea are absolute must-tries. Ever since Yifang departed this region, YY has become my go-to spot for the best fruit tea in town. What sets them apart is not only the delicious flavors but also their flexibility with sweetness levels - opting for no sugar is always an option. Additionally, their range of toppings allows for a personalized touch to each drink. If you're a milk tea enthusiast like myself, YY Coffee & Milk Tea is the place to be!

Name: YY Coffee & Milk Tea
Author: Kenia H.G.
Review: The Oreo milk tea boba is delicious with oat milk. My cup didn’t have the logo on it though I saw it on my friends cup a couple of days ago.

Name: YY Coffee & Milk Tea
Author: Karan Gurung
Review: Hidden Gem in SF! Love the drinks. Try OolongMilk tea with Mochi and cheese cream!!!

Name: YY Coffee & Milk Tea
Author: P
Review: The place is small and clean it has 2 tables inside. You can order boba or yogurt. There is only one person working so be patient. You can either order from the tablet or ask them for help. The process is very quick. Boba is good to satisfy the craving.

Name: Far Leaves Tea
Author: Carlos Apodaca
Review: An absolute serene and pleasant tea house. Wonderful atmosphere, friendly tea master service. Exquisite and aromatic tea drinking experience. I will definitely be back to this Wonderful place next time I visit the Bay area.

Name: Far Leaves Tea
Author: Jiahui Zhou
Review: A wonderful place to meet up with friends or just slow down and find the peace of mind again. A wide selection of teas to be served in appropriate manners (teapot, temperature, brewing time etc). Will come back next time visiting Berkeley.

Name: Far Leaves Tea
Author: Bryce McLean
Review: A wonderfully unique experience with extremely knowledgeable staff and an extensive tea range. Relaxed setting and reasonable prices.

Name: Far Leaves Tea
Author: Ever Apollo
Review: Wow. Very kind and attentive service. These are tea experts who are knowledgeable of different brewing methods depending on the tea. They introduced us to puer tea which is absolutely delicious and unique. We were in a rush so only bought tea to brew at home but hoping to stay a bit longer and do a flight of their rare teas some time. Also has a variety of decaf herbal options, including rose!

Name: Far Leaves Tea
Author: S D
Review: Such a calm place! Feels like a inside zen garden. The lady running the store was super friendly and made sure to check few times that our large group orders were correct. Got the blood orange tea and mochi. Both were really good! Great way to relax or just chat with fiends. Berkeley surprises me with how many cool places there are!

Name: Honey B
Author: Stephanie Ngui
Review: Nice snacks and tea. The herbal jelly drink is great and the popcorn chicken is nice. The portions are big. There are no bathrooms. They have limited indoor seating. Food and drinks are prepared promptly but gets busy after school.

Name: Honey B
Author: Jeff Ho
Review: We stopped by Honey B this afternoon to get a quick snack. It took about 5 minutes for them to prepare my popcorn chicken. It tasted great without the need for chili powder, hot sauce or ranch sauce. Some items like the fried squid advertised on the front window were not available. The cashier said all the items on the menu at the front table are available. Next time, I'll come back and try some of their boba drinks.

Name: Honey B
Author: Julie Taeko
Review: Very cute little store with great safety measures for employees. And the staff are very helpful with providing directions and ideas for their drinks. I tried the grapefruit honey hot tea which was great because it was super cold that day. I asked for 25% sweetness so it was less flavorful but still very refreshing! It was also nice to see you can Venmo the store to pay as well.

Name: Honey B
Author: Jerry Liu
Review: Very good boba, and the popcorn chicken here is definitely a highlight. Well worth the money.

Name: Honey B
Author: Sapph Nor
Review:
In late 2020, I first visited this place and my friend had a diarrhea after drinking their boba tea. I have since returned to this location a couple more times with friends who really like Honey B. The teas remain my least favorite--hardly any tea flavor and boba always undercooked. I also dislike that their regular-size cups (for cold tea) don't fit into the cup holders in the car.

My rating went from 1-star to 2-star, because some of their snacks are pretty good (waffle fries and fried tofu highly recommended). The popcorn chicken is also good, but almost always without a trace of basil in it. I never ordered their eggettes again after trying it once, when it was folded a couple of times and squeezed into a tiny bag (it was all soggy when I took it out of the bag to eat it a few minutes later).

In my most recent visit, I noticed that they don't give out receipts. So I requested one, only to find that they put a $0.25 bag charge although we didn't ask for a bag. Nowhere on the menu indicates that there would be a $0.50 extra charge if you ask for "no ice" on the tea. When we placed the order, the cashier didn't tell us about the extra charge either. How tricky!

If you are a tea lover, avoid this place.

Name: iTea
Author: Kim Chan
Review:
I can't recall any other boba places in SF having tofuhua as a topping so I usually get a drink from here when I'm in the area.

Writing this review to remind myself to get my drinks at 25% sweetness.

FYI any card payments for orders under $15 get charged $0.50 extra

Name: iTea
Author: Adriana Perez
Review:
After finishing a delightful dinner at a nearby restaurant, we decided to satisfy our sweet tooth with some fun drinks for dessert and it was an excellent decision!

The variety of flavors and toppings was impressive, catering to both traditional and adventurous palates. I opted for 100% sweetness, to my watermelon, sago, and it was refreshing.

There was no seating, it was definitely a grab and go Location .

Name: iTea
Author: Michael
Review: Straight-forward no frills bubble tea shop with a lot of variety and good taste. They don’t even have any seating, just a small space to order when you walk in. They seem well staffed, so the orders come out pretty fast. They have good prices, lower than some of the other bbt shops around but they do have a credit card transaction fee though!

Name: iTea
Author: Mithun Manivannan
Review: The boba was good, we went on a really hot day so it was packed. They had many people in line and got orders confused a couple times. I think they were under staffed but they handled the situation fine. My classic boba was good, and their tapioca pearls were soft.

Name: iTea
Author: A Fashionable Condition
Review: The only reason I am shorting this place a star is because they did not have the Thai tea I ordered but I think that's because we came in so late so probably not their fault. otherwise one of the many, many holes in the wall in the sunset District that you should stick your head into if you have the chance and are looking for some boba, milk tea or Shave Ice. Very friendly service  and  one of the places  open  late  on  Irving past 19th Avenue. Another one of the places I always suspect are laundering operations  because  they "prefer* cash  and the machines are down that take cards  a little too often  but I can assure you they are not the only place  where this  is  on the Reg. And it always looks like it's chronically staffed by someone's cousin's friend. get there early if you want the Thai iced tea! ♥️♥️♥️♥️

Name: TOMO Tea House
Author: Kathleen Valerio
Review: Stopped in for a sweet treat and tea after dinner at Yo Sushi.  The decor is super fun. Self serve order kiosks feel very trendy.  Very easy to customize orders and pay by card. We also saw people walk in and order at the counter, presumably they accept cash as well.  Donuts are fun and tasty.  Background music is very eclectic,  several different genres and decades covered while we were there. Lots of pink and positive messaging.  Staff was willing to help us take a family photo by the round swing chair.

Name: TOMO Tea House
Author: Julia Gidwani
Review: Tomo is a sweet spot! Their mochi donuts are absolutely delicious—crispy on the outside, soft and chewy on the inside, with a variety of fun flavors. The drinks are equally yummy, with lots of options to choose from. What really sets them apart is their free kids' play area, which is a nice bonus if you have little ones. You just need to sign a waiver, and they can burn off some energy while you enjoy your treats. Overall, a fantastic place for a snack, drink, and some family-friendly fun!

Name: TOMO Tea House
Author: Edison Huang
Review: Seems like this used to be a Bober in the past cuz everything is hella pink. Pretty clean establishment. The drinks are alright. Slightly disappointed they don't offer no ice option for their classic milk tea options. Oh, they also have mochi donuts.

Name: TOMO Tea House
Author: Alison Enthoven
Review: I love getting milk tea and mochi donuts at Bober Tea. The tea is flavorful and high-quality, the stir-fried boba pearls have the perfect texture, and the mochi donuts are dangerous. They have a deal on Tuesdays when the donuts are $1 each when you purchase a drink, and they have an indoor play area for $5 per kid. The vibe is really fun though it does feel a bit gimmicky... but that just makes it more fun for the kids.

Name: TOMO Tea House
Author: Karen Liu
Review: Great atmosphere for instagram worthy pics. Drinks and mochi donuts are really good 😋wish we could adjust the sweetness of our drinks though

Name: Asha Tea House
Author: Alastor Marsh
Review: I enjoy the sparkling strawberry drink, its a great place to hang out, make friends, i see couples talking, honestly overall a Japanese Cafe like experience! I recommend those that want matcha, milk, or sparkling drink! You wont regret it!

Name: Asha Tea House
Author: Victor Wang
Review: Really enjoyed their ceremonial grade organic matcha latte with oat milk. Super creamy and delicious. Quick to order and fast to come out as well. Limited seating but we were able to find 3 seats on Tuesday afternoon without too much issue.

Name: Asha Tea House
Author: Jonathan Luu
Review:
I think this is one of the few times that a boba shop says “lightly” sweeten and it’s actually not that sweet. I got 100% sweetness and this might be 50% sweetness at other places. This lets the tea really shine through which is something i really like. They have more traditional teas as well so would recommend. I got a matcha cookie and this was excellent as well.

There are a few chairs and table here so it’s good spot to work.

Name: Asha Tea House
Author: S Sum
Review: Came at noon on a monday and there was no line, both of the staff working greeted me immediately. I got the strawberry matcha latte and it was delicious. I could distinctly taste the matcha and the taste wasn't covered by the milk, the strawberry jam added a nice sweetness. I also got the guava danish and matcha white chocolate cookie.

Name: Asha Tea House
Author: Lydia Cheng
Review: Was in the area and needed a caffeine hit and stumbled across Asha Tea House. There are a lot of tea focused options here and less coffee options. The interior was well decorated - cozy and inviting vibes. I got the matcha latte and added blueberry to it (seasonal fruit option) and it was delicious. I like my drinks on the less sweet side and this was perfect. Will definitely be back to try their other options!

Name: Golden Tea Shop
Author: Jasmine Yang
Review:
I have been to pretty much every boba shop in this area and have drank close to 1000 boba drinks in my life… and this is BY FAR the best place!!! They have such an extensive menu and everything tastes AMAZING! Their boba is sweet and chewy, their crema is fresh and perfectly salted, and all their teas are brewed perfectly. They also have the best prices I have ever seen. Since my first golden tea shop drink, I have been hooked. I literally refuse to go anywhere else now. Also, they are mom and pop shop which I love!! If you’re in the area, and even if you’re not, you should definitely come. This is the place I would walk through a hurricane to get to 😍

Pictured: Cake Crème Brûlée milk tea

Name: Golden Tea Shop
Author: Mina Park
Review: Truly excellent tea and boba here. I got the Jasmine green tea with boba and Hong Kong milk tea with boba and I have to say their tea based drinks are spectacular! One of the best in the Bay Area honestly, and I’ve gone to a lot of boba and tea shops. I wish there was a Golden Tea Shop closer to where I live, it definitely would be a place I would frequent. I will come again next time I’m in Oakland and this is a spot I would recommend to my tea and boba connoisseur friends! Also the price of the boba drinks here is unbeatable for the size and quality you get! Love this spot.

Name: Golden Tea Shop
Author: Monique Vincent
Review: GO HERE. They have an absolutely astounding array of options, including dozens of things I've never seen at other bubble tea places. (E.g red bean toppings or durian sago tofu pudding). Everything is customizable and they have over a dozen toppings to choose from. They are also very dairy-free/vegan friendly (they have almond, soy, oat, and coconut milk!).

Name: Golden Tea Shop
Author: Kirat Sun
Review: Love this place! The service is really great and I really enjoyed my rose milk tea with boba. Pricing is super reasonable and they have Taiyaki!

Name: Golden Tea Shop
Author: Alex Fong
Review:
If you care about the “tea” part of milk tea, go here. Store is two parts: boba shop and loose leaf tea shop.

Boba shop: huge selection of many different teas as well as flavors/cremas etc. my favorite part is that you can ask what tea they use in the drink and they will tell you what it is. This place is a dream. Prices are reasonable (~$5-6). It’s my favorite way to try new teas.

Their loose leaf selection is enormous, particularly for tieguanyin and puer. Not to mention they do sell teapots and other tea accessories. My favorite tea is their Strong Aroma Tieguanyin, which is also the tea used in their Tieguanyin Crema in the boba shop.

Name: Tea & Others
Author: Lady Venom
Review: When I visited, the store had no seating and had closed their restrooms to customers, not sure if this is a usual thing. I ordered from an iPad, I liked that it had photos & an in-depth description of all the different tea options. My friend got the Oreo Matcha & I got the Lil’ Giraffe. It was pretty good, I was surprise they didn’t already come with the traditional boba ($0.75 additional charge). My friend’s was better, I didn’t taste much of the cake batter maybe due to my 75% sweetness instead of 100%? I wouldn’t recommend the Lil’ Giraffe, I’m sure there other options are better.

Name: Tea & Others
Author: egc
Review: Got a basic matcha latte and it was delicious. I like that you can customize the level of sweetness.

Name: Tea & Others
Author: Gary Ogasawara
Review: Thai iced tea was good with strong tea flavors. Mango frappe is not recommended as it had too many chunks of ice in it, not blended well. Good quality boba. Fast and friendly service. Short hours, hopefully they stay open longer in the future.

Name: Tea & Others
Author: Fae LeDawson
Review: Delicious, fresh tea quickly prepared!  Like most shops, drinks run pretty sweet so you can ask for 50% sweetness if you want more tea and fruit flavor instead.  It's too bad that all the seating was removed, even outdoors.

Name: Tea & Others
Author: Scarlett Humphries
Review:
Man this was good tea! I got the lil giraffe and my sister got the Thai tea. I was unsure of what mine would be like since cake is an ingredient. It’s essentially just cake flavored tea. No weird consistency at all! The cups are bigger than they look so the price isn’t bad either. The straws they used did have a strange taste (they’re a paper variety) so I’d opt for one of their other straw options next time. An older review on Google says they don’t have dairy free options but that isn’t true anymore. Some of the items on the menu do say they can’t be substituted. They have soy, almond, and oat milk though.

I wish I was able to get more aesthetic pictures for the review but with COVID they only allow 1 party in at a time so I wanted to grab the tea and get out. Will definitely be coming back next time I’m in SF.

Name: Aroma Tea Shop
Author: Neal
Review:
Aromatic teas and great service make this place worth visiting.

The tea here is expensive, but it smells amazing. It's rarely busy and you can buy tea gift boxes, teacup sets, and t-shirts in addition to loose leaf teas. If you're very serious about tea, you can get fancy teapots and teacups here. If you're unsure of what you want, just ask whoever's working. I've never felt rushed and service has always been pleasant. The lychee black tea smelled incredible and tasted good too.

Name: Aroma Tea Shop
Author: Alissa Nolan
Review: A great tea shop find in San Francisco! We stumbled across Aroma Tea Shop while looking for a nearby bakery and what a find. There are a huge number of loose leave tea choices, sold by the ounce. The owner was super knowlegeable and happy to brew us a few samples. The Lychee Strawberry is a great cold brew Ice Tea and the Vanilla Black tea is my current favorite. The real find of the day was the Earl Grey Chocolate bar, I can't wait to go back and get that again.

Name: Aroma Tea Shop
Author: Christian Lee
Review: Aroma Tea shop has a great selection of teas, and they also have multiple locations. However, they are sadly not doing tea tastings at the moment.

Name: Aroma Tea Shop
Author: Lilla Sz
Review: One of the best tea shop experiences in my life. Huge plus to Homer (I hope I got that right) who gave us tasting samples and was very knowledgeable but also very welcoming.

Name: Aroma Tea Shop
Author: Kenny Bourgon
Review: Great tea selection. Owner is a good dude and knows his stuff. Very welcoming to beginners.

Name: Blue Willow Teaspot
Author: Valerie Hall
Review: Beautiful, lovely atmosphere. While in town I went three times. They were very accommodating despite our lack of reservations on the weekend.  Sometimes service felt slow despite it being uncrowded, but it’s the pace that is a part of the place.

Name: Blue Willow Teaspot
Author: Talia Karasinski
Review: I’m excited to come back and try a different beverage. The chai latte was pretty flavorless. This place takes tea seriously and would make a great date spot. The ambiance is warm and cozy.

Name: Blue Willow Teaspot
Author: Michelle Wang
Review:
The pastry was decent but the tea really makes up for it!
I really enjoyed being able to have a flight of tea to taste.
Definitely a one of a kind place as I don't think this kind of shop exists anywhere else.
I definitely would love to return to try out their different seasonal teas :)

Name: Blue Willow Teaspot
Author: Abby Shen
Review: The peaceful atmosphere and beautiful interior design make this place special, in addition to the unique and extensive selection of teas. I spent a few hours here with my pot of Ancient Sun tea, reading and writing. A very aesthetically pleasing experience—both the tea itself and its presentation. There are multiple electric kettles to boil more water for your tea, which I appreciated. I need to come back!

Name: Blue Willow Teaspot
Author: Bijoux Beauregard
Review: Perhaps it's worth noting that this is one of the few tea houses I've encountered that offers both a traditional tea ceremony and iced/milk teas. Blue Willow offers an impressive selection of pu'erh, as well as layered black and green teas. The white and herbal menu is nothing to scoff at, either. The array of glass and porcelain delivered to the tables might seem a tad intimidating to customers that aren't super familiar with them, but I promise the staff are courteous, friendly, and more than happy to teach patrons about steep times and pour techniques.

Name: Tea Plus Tea Bar
Author: Jeff Ho
Review: Yet another boba shop. Service was welcoming. I had the popcorn chicken, curly fries w/coke combo $10 and egg rolls $7. The food tasted fine. I would have preferred more breading on the chicken. They have different sauces to make the food less plain.

Name: Tea Plus Tea Bar
Author: Norman Antiporda
Review: I live in the area and come here pretty often. I usually order just a plain black lemon Iced tea with extra lemon and sometimes the calamari rings. Each time I come here, the staff is so nice and welcoming. I don’t know their names but all of the staff are so kind. Would definitely come again (I live only a block away) and recommend this place to friends or anyone who loves boba drinks and great snacks!

Name: Tea Plus Tea Bar
Author: Angelica Mae Tan
Review: Worst popcorn chicken ever. No breading mix, dry and a waste of money. The Boba was good but the one in San Bruno Avenue has much better food/ drink quality

Name: Tea Plus Tea Bar
Author: nikki esguerra
Review: I wouldn’t give any star at all! The photos on the menu looks great so I gave it a try and thought it would be worth it but the food tasted awful! Worst mango slush I had plus the boba has some weird texture when you chew it. Some of the fried snacks I ordered was soaking in moisture and oil combined. Takoyaki was undercooked and was soaking in sauce too so all you can taste was just the sauce. Will definitely not recommended this place. Very disappointing! Do not waste your money in this place!

Name: Tea Plus Tea Bar
Author: Edward Bennett
Review: Staff is fast & always gives the BEST customer service 👏

Name: New Love Tea
Author: Helen M
Review:
The drink is just sweet. The babo were hard and don’t taste fresh.

It doesn’t help that the worker mess up my order.

Name: New Love Tea
Author: Malia
Review:
On a hot summer day I walked in, withering away from the heat wave outdoors. Before me I saw standing four other patrons, also escaping from the heat to obtain some iced boba tea… Alas, I thought, I will be waiting forever… But I stood for barely five minutes, before my savior in the form of rose milk tea came to my aid!

If you prefer it less dramatized: Hot day, walked in to get an iced milk tea. The place was pretty busy, I thought there’d be a long wait—but the lady behind the counter was so quick, she took care of all four people ahead of me and then completed my order in under five minutes. Respect!!!

There are a ton of flavors and items on the menu so I’ll have to come back and try more.

This place is also pretty clean and though I have not used their restroom, it seems like there is a restroom in the back.

Name: New Love Tea
Author: Angelica SF
Review: The tea place is cute and clean. We only get to try one drink - mango fresh milk. It’s nice that they have sago as an add-on so we got that. The drink is delicious. Service is fast too, not too long of a wait.

Name: New Love Tea
Author: Sapph Nor
Review: Nice ambiance, convenient location, competitive prices, and the store was clean. I really liked the snacks, especially the popcorn chicken. Teas were average, with tapioca on the chewier side. The selection of toppings was pretty good. Customer service was courteous, but it would have been better if the staff wore a mask.

Name: New Love Tea
Author: Rachel Windsor
Review:
Their drink is good! Relatively fast service!

They have not opened for dine-in! After ordering, you would need to wait outside to be called till your drink is ready……

But it looks like a great environment place! Pretty umbrellas decorations under the ceiling!

Name: Dartealing Lounge
Author: Karen Keegan
Review: I brought my 13 year old granddaughter here for her first proper Tea. We had wonderful service, a vegan meal was prepared for me and a typical meal was made for her. Everything was very tasty. My granddaughter loved the food so much she ate it all in a matter of minutes! She loved the hole experience, an so did I. We recommend this tea room for your future tea event!

Name: Dartealing Lounge
Author: Yan Z
Review:
Came here with a friend Saturday 1pm. The place was very full. Reservation was recommended but they let us walk-in. (Thank you!

Great atmosphere. Service is good. Attentive but not too much.

Food is good. Savory bites were somewhat creative. Sweet items were good too. My favorite was the matcha tart.

The tea was average though.

Overall good for its price. I could see this place perfect for a team building lunch/afternoon tea.

Name: Dartealing Lounge
Author: Victoria A
Review: Amazing experience! I'm a big fan of tea like this. I don't live in the SF area so this was my first time here. This is an independent tea house, not a tea in a luxury hotel like I was used to. The quality of the tea and snacks was comparable with the big fancy hotel I usually go to in my area, but at a fraction of the price. There was a huge selection of teas and they give you a lot. The tower in this pic is for 4 people and it was plenty, we even had leftovers. The pots are one per person. It was street parking only and in an area with a sports stadium so keep in mind when making a reservation the area could be crowded due to a game. We did find parking a few blocks away.

Name: Dartealing Lounge
Author: Rocio G
Review:
Visited 4/28 before a Giants game. This place made the whole street corner smell good as we waited for the doors to open. Their reservation process sounds more complicated than it was. Once I filled out the form on their website, the rest was done via text and my reservation was confirmed within minutes. Service was awesome! It's a prix fixe menu so you just need to tell them the tea that you'd like. I opted to pay an extra $5.25 to have their memories of Saigon blooming tea. It comes in a glass tea pot and opens as it sits in the water. Tea was good but the blooming part (really slow bloom) sounded cooler than it actually was so I'll stick to one of the cheaper teas next time. Warm scones and cream were my favorite part!

They don't have a parking lot but metered street parking on Stillman St was super easy to find. It's limited to an hour though. Our tea time visit was an hour and 15 min.

Name: Dartealing Lounge
Author: Ur Brt
Review: The Owners and staff are very knowledgeable about tea. They easily crafted an experience that you will remember for a life time with their approach, sandwiches, snacks, and tea selections. I'm living in Arizona and plan to order tea to support and be sure to return when I'm in the area. Thanks Keith and everyone at Dartealing. You're all awesome.

Name: Sophie's Cuppa Tea
Author: Travis Pillon
Review: I don't know how to put this any other way, but this is one of the most special places I have ever been too.  This might seem a little extreme for a tea shop; however, Sophie's Cuppa Tea is a sanctuary of peace and serenity.  The owners, Bei and John, are two of the most incredible people I have ever met and they also serve the best tea I have ever had.  I can promise everyone that they will not be disappointed if they come to this shop.   In addition to the shop, if you are looking for your next vacation, ask them about their yearly China Tea Trip.  It is a once in a life time opportunity (although I plan on going every year!).  See photos below :). BTW: They have amazing snacks and salads (fresh and organic).

Name: Sophie's Cuppa Tea
Author: Vivian Pim
Review: The best tea I’ve ever had in my life. Everything about this place is made with a lot of love. The owners are very passionate about what they do and that is clearly reflected into their business. The shop is very cozy, peaceful and cute. Love it!

Name: Sophie's Cuppa Tea
Author: Ken Ingram
Review: A spectacular inventory of tea selections with friendly and knowlegable proprietors. Best place to sample authentic tea.

Name: Sophie's Cuppa Tea
Author: Mychal Nitta
Review: Tea lover’s paradise. The people here are friendly and welcoming which is great because the amount of tea to choose from can be overwhelming. Highly recommended.

Name: Sophie's Cuppa Tea
Author: MEJ
Review: Great selection of tea and even better people. Always a pleasure coming here.

Name: Pink Pink Tea Shoppe
Author: Austin Pachano
Review:
My drink was super good. They had it ready in just a few minutes after I ordered. I ordered in person but they also have a self-order kiosk.

The location is in the mall with limited seating but this was great to carry around. Most stores at the mall don't allow drinks in, however they have tables near the front to set the drink down while you browse.

Name: Pink Pink Tea Shoppe
Author: Lorcan O'Regan
Review: Really nice food and amazing chefs. The wait staff throughout looked after our table of 12. Had the steak & prawns. Everything delicious.

Name: Pink Pink Tea Shoppe
Author: Ivan Martinez
Review: Stopped by Japan Town for a limited edition Pink Peach Milk treat. The boba pearls were an added plus. 😋

Name: Pink Pink Tea Shoppe
Author: Chasity Norton
Review: Refreshing lychee grape, half sweet, no cheese foam, with lychee and honey boba. Ordered on kiosk and ready in a couple minutes. The half sweet was perfect.

Name: Pink Pink Tea Shoppe
Author: JP G
Review: We ordered the mango pudding, they call it pinko-nut. It comes in a glass jar with a lid that is really nice and reusable. A really good portion and delicious dessert.

Name: Red Blossom Tea Company
Author: Yoomi
Review: What an incredible tea showroom! I first discovered this brand during a tasting at Opus One winery. They’ve partnered with renowned Michelin-starred restaurants in the Bay Area, including Benu, Atelier Crenn, and The French Laundry.

Name: Red Blossom Tea Company
Author: ayush kalani
Review:
Red Blossom Tea in San Francisco's Chinatown is a must-visit for any tea lover. This charming little shop isn't just a place to buy loose leaf tea and teaware; it's a gateway to a world of flavors and aromas. Red Tea Blossom ships their teas to restaurants as well as corporate offices, and after speaking with the owner, I can also confidently say that this place is all about sharing their passion for this ancient beverage.

We walked in on a Saturday afternoon and were surprised by the tranquility. Unlike the usual Chinatown bustle, the shop offered a calm, inviting atmosphere. The display shelves were lined with an impressive selection of teas, each with a label highlighting its origin, flavor profile, and price. A color-coded system made navigating easy: the red labels indicated their premium collection, while white labels marked the more budget-friendly options.

Our exploration began even before we reached the shelves. We were welcomed with a complimentary cup of refreshing cold-brewed osmanthus tea, a delightful introduction to what awaited us.

The in-store tea tasting is a fantastic feature, and while reservations aren't accepted, there was no wait on our visit. We learned that tastings are done standing-room only and conclude by 4:30 pm, accommodating up to four guests at a time. Though advertised as 5-10 minutes, our session turned into a delightful hour and twenty minutes, thanks to our incredible host.

Their tasting fee is very reasonable, with Red Label teas priced at $18 and others at $12 per sample. Our tasting began with three our three selections: a Dragonwell green tea, an Ao Fu Hou phoenix oolong, and a wild leaf Menghai Sheng Pu-erh from 2003.

The Dragonwell, crafted in Pan'an County, China, was a revelation. As described, it was smooth, buttery, and lingered pleasantly on the palate with a subtle corn aftertaste, likely due to the pan-frying process.

The Ao Fu Hou, on the other hand, was a symphony of apricot, honey, and warm spices. This oolong was a true showcase of quality, with a long, layered finish.

The wild leaf Menghai Sheng Pu-erh, hailing from Menghai County, Yunnan, offered a distinct contrast. Its earthy and camphor notes developed richness with longer steeping, culminating in a smooth, almost stone-fruity character.

But the journey didn't end there. After trying 3, we decided to try one more - Fu Shou Shan oolong from Taiwan. This delightful tea unfolded with each steep, revealing an initial sweetness of sugarcane and cream, followed by complex floral and tropical notes. The finish was rich and lingered pleasantly.

Throughout the tasting, the aroma of the teas was as captivating as their flavors. Each steep, from hot to lukewarm to even cold, offered a unique flavor experience. It was a testament to the quality of the teas and the expertise of our host in guiding us through the process.

Our host's passion and knowledge truly elevated the experience. She wasn't just pouring water; she was sharing a story about each tea's origin, how it's grown, and how geography influences the harvest. Her enthusiasm was infectious, and her willingness to share a personal favorite tea with us (free of  charge) was a heartwarming touch.

While we admittedly drank quite a bit of tea that afternoon, the Ao Fu Hou oolong emerged as the clear crowd favorite. The wild leaf Menghai Sheng Pu-erh was undeniably strong, best suited for seasoned tea drinkers.

I highly recommend this place and especially their in person tea tasting.

Name: Red Blossom Tea Company
Author: Matee Mana
Review:
Wonderful family-owned tea shop in historic San Francisco China Town. I’ve been a happy customer for over a decade now! They’ve been one of my local go-to vendors for high-quality tea and artisanal tea ware, though the latter tends to be priced on the higher side. Not a knock by any means, as the wares I purchased are some of the best pieces I own.

I’ve enjoyed their excellent Tieguanyin, Dragon Peral Jasmine, several Formosa Oolongs, and Three Cultivar Red, all teas I like to keep on hand as my tea budget allows.

Their in store environment (and the neighborhood) has changed a lot since 2020, by necessity, of course. I do miss lifting the lids of the tea jars and sampling the scents of the different teas, but I agree with the decision to keep everything pre-packed. Their online shop makes things very convenient, but I do recommend visiting the physical shop if you find yourself in the area. Looking forward to my next tea restock visit!

Name: Red Blossom Tea Company
Author: Claire Johnson
Review: Excellent selection of teas and I appreciated being able to sample some before purchasing. Friendly customer service.

Name: Red Blossom Tea Company
Author: Eli W. Hunter
Review:
Very friendly and knowledgeable staff with a good selection of Chinese teas. The tasting is a little expensive but they give you so much tea and tell you so much about brewing methods, tasting notes, and how & where they select their teas that it's well worth it. A must visit for tea lovers.

Also there's absolutely no language barrier which is very comforting for someone whose Chinese is as bad as mine.

Name: Lovey's Tea Shoppe
Author: Maya Osweiler
Review: Literally the cutest little place ever!!! I’m enjoying tea and sandwiches and scones with my sister right now and it’s amazing! Such sweet and attentive workers and delicious tea and food. So much variety, even chocolate tea which I rarely find a truly good version of! Love it here so much and can’t wait to come back!!

Name: Lovey's Tea Shoppe
Author: Grace Nicolas -Kim
Review: I loved everything about this tea place. Me and my few girl friends came on Friday for high tea and everything was perfect . We each got different types of tea . I got white peach which was amazing with unlimited refill . The sandwiches were delicious and so as scones.  Service was great , the ladies were attentive and friendly .  We loved the little shop inside where they sell cutest tea pots, cups , teas , scones etc . I ended up getting lychee rose green tea to enjoy at home .

Name: Lovey's Tea Shoppe
Author: Amy Higgins
Review: Lovely lil tea shop that’s in an unexpected location. They have an outdoor seating space as well as a small inside spot. The sandwiches were nice but nothing special. However, the scones are to die for!!! We ordered more considering they were so good!!!

Name: Lovey's Tea Shoppe
Author: lorissa kelkar
Review: I’ve been to Lovey’s many times for their lovely scones and amazing chocolate truffles and petit fours…this was the first time having their afternoon tea. It was wonderful with attentive, friendly service, ensuring my pot of tea was kept hot and providing a considerate pace of the courses offered in a relaxed atmosphere. I had to purchase some chocolate truffles to go, of course!

Name: Lovey's Tea Shoppe
Author: Osbaldo Martinez
Review: Lovey's Tea Shoppe is a great tea house. I loved the atmosphere, it's so charming and lovely. The tea is fantastic, the scones were so delicious, the sandwiches were also really good. I came here with my friends and we had an amazing time. Everyone is so friendly and welcoming!!

Name: Song Tea & Ceramics
Author: Danielle Veluz
Review: A unique shop that specializes in all things about tea - from imported tea leaves to beautifully made ceramics. If you ask them they'll offer you a taste of whatever tea they brewed for the day. My husband and I got to sample their Big Leaf Shuixian, an oolong tea from Taiwan. Relaxing vibe here and helpful staff. Just wish they were open more days of the week!

Name: Song Tea & Ceramics
Author: Grace Lee
Review: so glad we met Amanda. She’s super welcoming and shared a ton about tea. Her detailed explanations helped us choose the perfect tea flavor to start with. I highly recommend this place for tea lovers especially those into Taiwanese or Fujian teas. Different spectrum and harvest to explore, along with a selection of cool teaware. Great for gifting or treating yourself with some good tea.

Name: Song Tea & Ceramics
Author: Karen Gao
Review: Song Tea and Ceramic was really cute and had a great variety of teas and ceramics. Upon arrival to the shop, we were greeted with samples of oolong tea. I really enjoyed smelling all the teas and we ended up getting the Hello set to try out a red, green, oolong and white tea. The teas make a great gift and definitely recommend this shop for anyone who wants to the try out authentic teas.

Name: Song Tea & Ceramics
Author: nicolo sari
Review:
Amazing ambiance with amazingly kind staff. I would like to highlight Cinndy, who gave recommendations of the area while I was on vacation.
The tea is well varied and will have one for any person of taste, the books are a fun read with insightful tips on the ceramics and the designs are all beautiful.
Thank you, song tea & ceramics, for the short lived but amazing impression and experience, best wishes.

Name: Song Tea & Ceramics
Author: Alejandra
Review: Very small and personal shop. We got free tea samples to taste and there's a table to enjoy the tea samples. The people working in there made us feel welcomed and I got help when I was ready to purchase.

Name: Sip Tea Room
Author: Jasmin Holguin
Review: The Sip Tea Room is an absolutely stunning/delightful place to spend time with family, friends, acquaintances, or even just yourself. Such a great atmosphere! Highly recommended!

Name: Sip Tea Room
Author: Katelyn Cohen
Review: So, TikTok kept showing me videos of the tea lady reviewing all her afternoon tea places and I suddenly NEEDED a tea time. After a quick search I found this place right near the Bnb where I was staying. This was perfect. The food was excellent and SO fresh- sandwiches, scones, shortbread. Sometimes at tea shops you can tell the sandwiches have sat around for a few hours, but not here. The tea options were unique and delicious, I tried the rose. Lovely atmosphere and waitstaff. This was just perfect after a walk in the park. Wish I lived closer!

Name: Sip Tea Room
Author: Manny
Review: Such a wonderful experience. We came here for my birthday brunch and I was blown away. The menu was delicious I was so happy they had such amazing gluten free options. The service and staff were incredibly wonderful and friendly. I would definitely recommend visiting.

Name: Sip Tea Room
Author: Stephanie
Review:
A cute and quirky place for afternoon tea right next to Golden Gate Park.

We had their full Traditional Afternoon Tea Experience. Their sausage rolls were my favourite - little morsels of deliciousness. The sandwiches were alright. They were a bit too thin and slightly on the dry side. I loved their lemon curd though.

For my tea selection, I had the Blue Malva Flower Earl Grey. A nice Earl Grey that’s floral forward.

The parking situation can be a problem, especially on the weekends so give yourself extra time. We ended up parking at the parking garage inside of Golden Gate Park, which has its own traffic issues but that’s another tale. It’s a great day out to have afternoon tea and visit the museums though!

Name: Sip Tea Room
Author: Michelle Barnes
Review:
Wonderful afternoon tea experience right next to Golden Gate park! The only downside is parking, which is nonexistent on weekends. I had reserved a 1pm seating on a Saturday. You do have to pay in full ahead of time, including tip, which I'm generally not a fan of tipping prior to receiving any service. We arrived 30 minutes late as we spent 25 mins looking for parking and 15 mins walking over. Luckily the staff were accommodating and allowed us to stay until closing rather than rushing us out the door after our 1.5 hour seating time was up.

After selecting our teas, china rose petal and green jasmine, they set out a plate of delicious baked sausage rolls. We were then served the 3 tier tray, which includes 4 sandwiches, small salad, a traditional and coconut scone with lemon curd, clotted cream, and jam, and a dessert tray 3 small treats and fresh fruit.

The scones were the highlight! Light, flaky, and perfect with the lemon curd and clotted cream. Their Hawaiian sandwich was a surprise as well along with the parfait dessert. We unfortunately weren't fans of the cookie, which was quite dry.

Name: Sweetheart Café & Tea
Author: Lily L.
Review: Very honest and clean mom and pop shop. One of the best popcorn chickens that I've had in my life and I've had a lot of different ones in Taiwan. Love how they always include the fried basil as that's the soul of this street dish. Also love their Barley tea creama, so perfect for the hot weather. Generous amount and fairly priced. Will continue to support the business! (Regular sized rose/oolong/barley tea creama is on sale now for $3.45! Not sure how long the sale would last)

Name: Sweetheart Café & Tea
Author: Justin Xiao
Review:
We ordered drinks on our walk around Chinatown. They have a great selection of drinks that you order at the entrance through touch Street. Not only do they have the classic teas they also have smoothie and I got the avocado shake. The building which the store is located in is architecturally significant.

They have seating inside but no bathrooms.
They also sell asian snacks.

Name: Sweetheart Café & Tea
Author: Melissa
Review:
I've been coming here since I was in Chinese school (a very long time. )

Today, I ordered spicy popcorn chicken, curly fries and blacktea Boba.
The spicy popcorn chicken was delicious as always.
However, the curly fries tasted like it came out of a freezer bag. It just has that nasty aftertaste. That never happened before by the way, so I was surprised when I tasted these curly fries, it was not edible for me.
As for the Boba, I've always ordered the black tea with pearls, but the pearls this time around seems less chewy than before. The middle of it was still hard.

Suffice to say, the quality seems to have gone down and I'm quite disappointed.

Name: Sweetheart Café & Tea
Author: Alice Hou
Review:
