I recently visited and was blown away by their incredible offerings. The black milk tea with boba was an exquisite blend of flavors that left me craving for more. The Hong Kong style milk tea was a delightful balance of sweetness, and the boba pearls were cooked to perfection – soft and chewy, enhancing the overall experience.

What's even more remarkable is the affordability. Shockingly, Sweetheart Cafe offers their delectable treats at half the price compared to other places, like the one in Berkeley. So if you're in the mood for some top-tier milk tea without breaking the bank, this is the place to be!

*THERE'S an exciting bonus for all Pokemon Go enthusiasts out there. Sweetheart Cafe offers a unique Pokemon Go Level Discount that truly sets them apart. As a long-time Pokemon Go Player, I never imagined I would get tangible benefits from my gaming passion. Here's how it works:

Level 1-28 = 5% Discount
Level 29-38 = 10% Discount
Level 39-46 = 15% Discount
Level 47-50 = 20% Discount

Name: Sweetheart Café & Tea
Author: Beryl V.
Review: Grew up going here and even with all the boba shops that have opened in Chinatown since, Sweetheart is still my favorite place for Taiwanese popcorn chicken in Chinatown! They are one of the few that include lots of fried basil with their chicken and it's always well seasoned and fresh. Their drinks may not be the fanciest in town nowadays, but everything is consistently good and the tapioca is always a nice texture (something that is trickier to find than one would think). Thank you Sweetheart Cafe for your many years(since 1996) of providing Oakland with delicious drinks and snacks!

Name: Sharetea
Author: Tommy Nguyen
Review: Not my cup of tea. Drink looks boring. Drink are too sweet even though “No Sugar” was chosen. No fruit in the drink even though it’s call fruit tea.

Name: Sharetea
Author: Matt B
Review:
Half decent boba tea spot in the lobby of the Metreon building. A fine place to grab a drink before or after a movie, but certainly nothing to write home about.

½ sweetness was the right amount of sweetness.

Name: Sharetea
Author: Sean Nguyen
Review:
I love going to the city to watch Wrestling and I have been seen @shareteametreon on my Instagram feed and I wanted to check it out.  I got iced blended coffee with creama and ice cream. It was so sweet and amazing. The ice cream on top is so elevating. I love how the fresh fruit tasted in the Peach tea with kiwi fruit. I love a good mojito and the lime Mojito did not disappoint. It was sweet and tangy, which was perfect for me. The last drink I got was the Matcha red bean milk tea with mini pearls and it was so good. I usually enjoy red beans in Vietnamese Che but I been getting it in Boba and it so good. I can understand why this drink is so popular! All in all everything was great and I would be coming back to try more drinks from  @shareteametreon .

📍ShareTea in the Metreon in San Francisco, CA.

Name: Sharetea
Author: A A
Review: This is a 3.5 star rating. More sweet has the same sweetness as extremely sweet, and less sweet. The Happy Family Large, came to $6.00 with tax, tip, and mandate, and was a sour combination of big pearls, small pearls, hard lychee cubes, soggy red beans, and.....the kitchen sink. The actual milk tea was as bland, and forgettable as the local Evening News. Yet, there was, and always seems to be a line, due to the perfect location of being on the way to AMC movie theater, at the food court. There's so many better bubble tea stores out there, I would skip Sharetea. $$$ out of $$$$$. No dedicated bathroom. Garage parking nearby. I would not recommend Sharetea.

Name: Sharetea
Author: Benny Avalos
Review:
I wish that pricess were cheaper since half the labor is automated. I hope the operation cost trickle down to higher hourly rates for workers.

They were really lovely and nice

Name: Lovejoy's Tea Room
Author: Katie Booth
Review: Wow! High tea has been a on my bucket list for awhile and Lovejoy was beyond my wildest dreams!! The atmosphere so unique and quirky. The food was so yummy with really fun sandwich options. We had the best time ever.

Name: Lovejoy's Tea Room
Author: Miranda Sequoia
Review:
A fun and whimsical place for a high tea. Dress up encouraged, but not required. A great option for a kids birthday or baby shower. Service and food were excellent.

Conveniently located at Church and Clipper stop on the J metro line.

Name: Lovejoy's Tea Room
Author: Alex Weis
Review:
This tea room is super cute and the atmosphere is amazing.  The servers are very friendly and helpful.  We got the light tea service, and the tea + snacks were yummy.  I learned that I'm not a huge fan of tea sandwiches, but that's not their fault.  They have heavier meal options as well if you're more hungry.  I would definitely bring a friend here for a birthday or a daytime celebration.
If they're fully booked, get on their notify waitlist--we were on their list and we ended up getting 2 notifications for tables that opened up last minute!

Name: Lovejoy's Tea Room
Author: Eri G
Review: Amazing !! Definitely recommend.  Very grounded, fast, and relaxed (yet busy) atmosphere.  Great way to spend teatime to zen or w/ a small group of friends to catch up. Beautiful antiques to take in and multiple seating options

Name: Lovejoy's Tea Room
Author: Osbaldo Martinez
Review: Lovejoy's Tea Room was an amazing experience. I enjoyed the sandwiches, the scones, and the tea. I had so much fun talking with my friends while enjoying tea. The place is decorated so well that it fits with the whole aesthetic of high tea. Although it's a bit on the pricey end, it was a wonderful experience. The staff are so helpful and friendly.

Name: Five Degree Tea House
Author: NORCAL OFFROAD
Review:
Nice boba and snack/food place! We passed 3 of them on the street and only stopped here because there was parking. But this place is authentic couldn't read half the menu cause it's not in English but that's fine just enough to get what we wanted.

Some nice booths and tables friendly quick service prices are great and drinks tasted very organic got the line passion tea, coconut, fresh fruit mix and boba and Taro $1.99 special milk tea which was awesome. Fruit drinks came with fresh fruit slices and coconut drink tasted like a pina collada. Lots of food options also. Definitely recommended wish we had this one in Sacramento:

Name: Five Degree Tea House
Author: D P
Review:
Not sure of the young girl working today, but I've had great customer service prior to today.

The cashier was multitasking as I was giving her my order. I had a question about a specific boba drink and someone else walked in so I offer that person the space to order since the cashier looked past me to the next person.

After that customer left, I began ordering and another customer came around and started ordering and the young girl took the second customers' order.

I'm still at the front of the line, in front of the cash register, and wondering if anyone sees me. Finally she asks if I was ready and I told yes and that I had been ready.

Ordered two milk boba tea and two avocado rolls. One had a hair in it (lower left corner) and barely avocado.

I showed them and they seemed to be upset that I was complaining... Yes, I am complaining about hair being in the food... Yes, I am confused as to what I am paying for if it doesn't look like the photos, but more importantly, the hair is in the rice!!

They made me a new one. But the vibe of this place today has been a disappointment.

I have the photos. Hope to add them to this post.

Name: Five Degree Tea House
Author: Tarrea Tolbert
Review: It’s a city hole in the wall spot so I don’t expect the service to be amazing but the food was in my opinion. Ordered a spam egg burger with strawberry lemonade that’s on the sweet side only 10 minute wait on a Sunday afternoon. My new go-to spot, I plan to come back for the waffle next time!

Name: Five Degree Tea House
Author: Ella Hou
Review: I mean some drinks are very cheap so it is worth it. But the drinks don't really taste as good if you pay an extra 2 bucks at a more reputable boba shop. The food is really a bit pricey.

Name: Five Degree Tea House
Author: P
Review: I won't say that this place is the best, but I will definitely say that you can't beat the price. You can find a lot of different options, from dessert to udon. This is great when you go with more than one person. The drinks were tasty and the boba was chewy as I like(plus they put a lot of it). Overall is great and, I would recommend you coming here if you want to pay less.

Name: Tea Plus
Author: Dustin Decker
Review: Real tea, real fruit, and fresh milk. Even the $2 boba milk tea specials are high quality. The mango for my mango sago was blended to order. Support this family owned shop, it's a total gem. The two neighboring businesses use powdered non-dairy milk substitute which is not healthy and not as good.

Name: Tea Plus
Author: Perris Smith
Review: Food is great! Drinks are great! They even let us try the drinks in a small sample cup as they were making them. Julian made everything to perfection. Prices are really good to.

Name: Tea Plus
Author: James T
Review:
Delicious boba tea at very reasonable prices.  I really enjoyed my oolong milk tea boba!

The Thai iced tea had a bit of a strange smoky flavor, didn't love it as much as the oolong.

Name: Tea Plus
Author: Ping
Review: Mozzarella sticks are so good, seasoned well and very tasty. Spam musubi is sauced up well and not too salty. The popcorn chicken is tender and marinated, but it wasn’t seasoned enough for my personal taste. For most it would probably be perfect though. Thai tea is solid, it’s less milky and a very dark orange which I prefer. Shaved ice is much too sweet for my taste but the taste is good otherwise. THE CREPES AND HONEYDEW MILK TEA ARE SO GOOD JESUS. IT TASTES LIKE A MELONA POPSICLE WITH TEA!!! Curry fish balls slap and there is a lot of sauce which I appreciate. Everything here is top tier, just make sure to get the 25% sweet or else you might get diabetes since everything is much sweeter than other places.

Name: Tea Plus
Author: Alec Foster
Review: Delicious teas with tons of toppings, amazing food (served late) with unique Hong Kong style seasonings and ingredients.

Name: The Spice & Tea Exchange
Author: Stephanie Bernardino
Review: What a beautiful store. They offer all kinds of spices such as salt, peppers, tea, honey, and much more. They specialize in blends, and they have these jars filled with the spices, and this is the only place they will encourage you to open the jars and smell it. And wow, the smells bring me back to my childhood and cook offs. Such a delightful afternoon.

Name: The Spice & Tea Exchange
Author: Liz
Review: Really cute store located on the 2nd floor of Pier 39 offering a wide variety of teas, spices, salts, sugars, and custom blends. Also has a small selection of gifts and candles! Really friendly and kind store associate, and really great tea that they can make on-site.

Name: The Spice & Tea Exchange
Author: Rhino Home Care
Review: It’s a cute place to shop for gift and to make your home smell wonderful with scented candles. The service was excellent, the boba tea was just right. They have several types of teas, I asked the young man to surprise me with a classic tea. He knew was he was doing! Highly recommend this place for a quiet time away from the crowded places.

Name: The Spice & Tea Exchange
Author: Eric Williamson
Review: Two words to describe my experience at The Spice And Tea Exchange.  Simply Marvelous!    Lauren helped  me navigate through the many options to choose from.  She was awesome.  When I asked her for suggestions, she was smart and ask what are my likes first.  From that information, she gave wonderful suggestions.  I loved that everything was well organized and passing all the wonderful aromas of the teas was such a delightful experience.  I also purchased a candle called the San Francisco Sunshine.   It smelled wonderful.   Whenever I'm in San Francisco again. I'm definitely stopping by there:)

Name: The Spice & Tea Exchange
Author: Markee Andrade
Review: I always get the creme brulee and some fancy sugar. This place is absolutely lovely, and I'm glad they have places like this to get quality tea. I make an iced creme brulee yummm

Name: Castro Coffee Company
Author: RITZ Carlton
Review:
Here's a review for the Castro Coffee Company:

---

I recently visited Castro Coffee Company and tried their hazelnut lattes. I have to say, they were absolutely delicious! The flavor was rich and perfectly balanced, with just the right amount of sweetness and a delightful hazelnut aroma. The coffee was smooth and creamy, making it a truly enjoyable experience. Highly recommend this place for anyone looking for a great coffee spot. Can't wait to come back!

---

Name: Castro Coffee Company
Author: Richard Shulaw
Review: Great independent coffee shop. Large selection of pastries and coffee. Friendly staff reasonable price and great location for people watching on Castro street.

Name: Castro Coffee Company
Author: Lagavulin08
Review: Excellent selection of coffee from an independently owned small business. Wife, and I stumbled upon this place looking to grab a quick coffee before exploring Castro. The owner, an older gentleman was very helpful in recommending us the beans based on our palate preference. The beans are fresh and great quality. The empanadas were homely and he even gave us espresso on the house (which was well done). Writing this review while enjoying my coffee brewed with Hawaiian Kona Coffee beans we got from here over the weekend.

Name: Castro Coffee Company
Author: Emilie Paulsen
Review: Accidentally stumbled upon this coffee place and I’m so happy I did. The service is amazing and their coffee selection is really impressive. I got an ice latte and I think it was the best I’ve ever had. My dad is a huge coffee enthusiast and he also absolutely loved it.

Name: Castro Coffee Company
Author: Coffee Man
Review: Huge selection of coffee with good service. I wanted a nice strong smooth coffee as we were exploring this area before heading to the park. Walked away with what is proving to be a lovely coffee for my aeropress on our RV road trip. Thanks both.

Name: Westlake Coffee Shop
Address: 1901 Junipero Serra Blvd st, D St, Daly City
Author: Taura Jackson
Review: Food was horrible first off I got cold instant potatoes. The server was nice to get me more. Overall the food had no flavor and my salmon was very dry. Had to add lot of pepper salt and hot sauce

Name: Westlake Coffee Shop
Address: 1901 Junipero Serra Blvd st, D St, Daly City
Author: Nick Kapp
Review: Ok I'm a little old and I remember when the coffee shop was in Westlake mall. The waitresses were older grandmas and had on like a white waitress uniform.  I've been wanting to go to the new place for a while and just have not made it.  It is still a classic coffee shop, service is quick and the food is good.  I had scrambled eggs and links.  I'll bring my 85 year old dad back.  The place was very neet.  While conversational I just feel something was missing it may be on my part.  If you go you will enjoy it.  Sit at the counter and talk to the waitress about soccer.

Name: Westlake Coffee Shop
Address: 1901 Junipero Serra Blvd st, D St, Daly City
Author: Sal Torres
Review:
This is hands-down one of thee best diners you’re gonna find anywhere in the entire bay area. The owner serves you with a smile and great conversation. Their menu is robust serving breakfast lunch and dinner. If you’re lucky to be served by her daughter, she is an absolute dynamo, with a terrific smile, and makes you feel you’re eating at a Michelin star restaurant.

If you’re in the market for good food at an affordable price that’s also family friendly, stop by Westlake coffee shop in Daly city. If nothing else you’ll walk away feeling that you’ve been fed well by a wonderful family who’s warmth and kindness will improve your day and you’ll enjoy the peacefulness that this wonderful little restaurant offers.

Name: Westlake Coffee Shop
Address: 1901 Junipero Serra Blvd st, D St, Daly City
Author: Salvadorian
Review: Nice Café with a small menu, very friendly service from a nice lady. I had the Ham and Eggs sandwich with American Cheese, eColeslaw on the side. I order the Donut Holes, the server ask if I wanted the three donut piece warm and I said yes, sadly the product tasted a bit old. The sandwich was a piece of sliced deli ham with scrambled eggs on a white toasted bread. Clean place with a warm lady server.

Name: Westlake Coffee Shop
Address: 1901 Junipero Serra Blvd st, D St, Daly City
Author: Alzila
Review: I never knew this place was even here. Past by many times without even noticing this spot. Then my girlfriend told me about it and wanted some breakfast. Clean seating as you walk in and along side with nice service. Classic bar seating available, you will find something you like on their not so broad menu.

Name: Delah Coffee
Author: Luana Helsinger
Review: I just had the pleasure of swapping my usual Starbucks run for a visit to Delah Coffee and what a delightful change it was! There’s something special about supporting a local spot, and Delah really hits the mark with its cozy, friendly vibe. The staff were incredible—so patient and eager to walk me through their coffee menu, which made me feel right at home. I skipped on the food this time, though the tempting display of snacks—from savory to sweet—definitely caught my eye. The place has plenty of comfy seats, and, it was easy to find a (paid) parking spot right on the street. Highly recommend Delah Coffee if you’re looking to mix up your coffee routine with a touch of local charm!

Name: Delah Coffee
Author: Ann Koppuzha
Review: Super cute cafe. The pastries looked delicious but I didn't try anything. The rose latte was great too. I would probably come back if I were in the area. The Yemeni chai seemed good too.

Name: Delah Coffee
Author: Bre Lee
Review: Beautiful pastry & coffee. I tried the chicken bread thing as it didn’t have dairy as was delicious. Oat milk latte incredible & my bf had the pistachio and regular baklavas and the Turkish coffee which he said were amazing. The staff is so welcoming and warm.

Name: Delah Coffee
Author: Natasha Kanhai
Review: We were introduced to this place by our Lyft driver.  It's a different type of coffee spot and coffee culture.   It's some of the most original coffee historical.  The sweets were beautiful,  some more authentic and delicious than others.   I would totally go back to soak in some more of this coffee culture.  Our favorites were the Yemeni latte and baklava cake.   Please check this gem out!

Name: Delah Coffee
Author: Amal Ben
Review: It’s a nice place with great vibes that remind me of my country. I only tried the strawberry smoothie, but I didn’t get the full experience this time. I really liked the music and the staff, who were very nice. Looking forward to coming back!

Name: Ballast Coffee
Author: A. W.
Review: I always get drinks and pastries at this coffee shop! Their mochi muffins and veggie quiche are really good. Just tried their avocado toast, and I could tell it was made with care and fresh ingredients (lemon juice/arugula). :)

Name: Ballast Coffee
Author: Edison Huang
Review: Found this unassuming coffee spot in the neighborhood. We ordered their ube ballast cappuccino, barako crush (iced), and an ube mochi donut. We were surprised they have a cappuccino option. The drink ended up a bit too sweet for us though. Other than that we love everything about the place.

Name: Ballast Coffee
Author: Chad Santo Tomas
Review:
Lovely neighborhood cafe in West Portal. Really the only good cafe in the area with an awesome and friendly fan base and a lovely owner.
The highlight of this cafe is that it sells Filipino coffee, Barako, which is a lot stronger and robust than arabica that you find at ritual, blue bottle, etc.

They stock pastries from third culture and starter bakery, BUT they've been having pop-ups with other local Filipinx food vendors. It seems to be on a monthly basis,but their IG has updates on pop ups and collabs.

Overall a great and lovely cafe. and YES there is WiFi and indoor and outdoor seating!

Name: Ballast Coffee
Author: jewel
Review: I came to this coffee shop for the first time about half an hour before closing time, while it was relatively full with other customers still. I had fully intended to try and have a coffee beverage only to be told that no espresso drinks were no longer able to be ordered, which narrowed my options list by half.. as an open minded person I didn't mind this, as I quickly attempted to order a cafe au lait immediately after, and yet was denied again because it wasn't available anymore either, as he then followed by saying he couldn't do drip coffees either. The worker suggested I order something else like a plain cold brew, chai, or matcha, yet none of those were what I had in mind at all and this truly disappointed me and the people I had gone with because I had to skip out on ordering anything at all. My best guess is that the worker had already cleaned the machines and simply wasn't willing go out of his way to make an exception or just didn't want to provide me service. Either way, it was ridiculous to not be able to order any of the different beverages i so desired at a COFFEE shop.

Name: Ballast Coffee
Author: Renée CS
Review: Chill place to sit with a coffee and study. The ube latte is exceptional.

Name: Delah Coffee
Author: Daniel Kofman
Review:
This might just be my absolute favorite middle eastern bakery/ coffee shop. They are so friendly, nice, and welcoming; I didn’t want to leave the place!!
Their honey bite is so darn good! I’m coming right back to buy another one!

Name: Delah Coffee
Author: Alison M C
Review: I definitely recommend the baklava $6.95/2 pieces. The beautiful interior is a calm, quiet place to study. Wifi was slow so I wouldn't work on a laptop here. I also did not appreciate the service. I ordered a piece of cake. While ordering I said I don't like desserts with a strong rose water flavor and asked for a recommendation. I wish she would have told me the pistachio milk cake was soaked in rose water. Ultimately my fault for not being familiar, but it would have been nice to say. I couldn't eat the bottom portion. After ordering they never gave me the cake or baklava. I had to go back and ask and wait again. The server placed my baklava pieces in the liquid from the cake. She nearly served the cake in a plastic box, but I insisted "for here please" and asked for a plate. Everything else came in disposable, one-time-use plastic when they have plates right there. It's wasteful. Finally, hard dry mint bits in the refresher drink gave it an unpleasant texture. Why not use fresh mint? If I came back, I would try the coffee and get baklava to take away.

Name: Delah Coffee
Author: Helene
Review: Really good yemeni latte. I think it tastes better hot than iced. You can also order through bigger pots of coffee to share with a group. The bee bite pastry was also quite good, soft bread with cheese in the middle, topped with honey. They also had different cakes and baklavas.

Name: Delah Coffee
Author: Abu Mamooji
Review:
Cozy middle eastern cafe with plenty of drink and small bite options.
Recommend the Yemeni latte.
Will be coming back

Name: Delah Coffee
Author: Rebeca Mejia
Review:
There is no doubt, it is the best coffee shop in all of Oakland, CA.

The time I was there, I went almost daily for my coffee: Rose Delah Latte or Yamine Latte are my favorites and their desserts are delicious.

The atmosphere is extremely pleasant, the decoration is beautiful and the music is always at an appropriate volume.

They are friendly with pets, my kitten was always welcomed.

And I want to highlight the most is that all the staff is very attentive and sweet, always with a smile and the best attitude.

My gratitude and my love to them for being so good to me.

If you are in Oakland, don't miss the opportunity to go to this sensational place.

Thank you so much for everything.

Name: Caffe Trieste
Author: Deniz Gunduz
Review: I visited one of the oldest cafes in an Italian Village Caffe Trieste next to Chinatown in San Francisco, and it was an amazing experience. The atmosphere was incredible, giving off a strong sense of Italian culture that feels authentic and unchanged over the years. I enjoyed a delicious latte and a croissant while observing that most of the clientele were older locals, adding to the charm. It truly felt like stepping into a local gem that has stood the test of time. Highly recommended!

Name: Caffe Trieste
Author: Attosa Tavallai
Review: 4.5 stars! I really enjoy coming to this little gem of a place after I eat across the way at El Farolito. If they had better coffee, they'd get a 5 from me. The service is hit or miss. Either you'll get some super lovely guy in glasses who likes to speak in a fake Italian accent (in an adorable way) or you get a super grumpy hipster kid who doesn't want to be there, pained to muster up a "have a nice day." The cheesecake is TO DIE FOR and the seating and table space is good for a little San Francisco shop. Love to play backgammon and make art here. They've got that much table room!

Name: Caffe Trieste
Author: PPanayotov
Review: The place is one of the iconic places in San Francisco, and the staff, food, and drinks are worth it. It was a joy to visit. P.S.: They only accept cash, but there's an ATM inside the cafe so you can get some cash out.

Name: Caffe Trieste
Author: Chef Tony
Review:
So this place was recommended and I went there. It started off bad, but after I relaxed with a cappuccino and replayed everything,  I went and apologized to the 2 workers.
Now the cappuccino was great, and the cookie was good, too. I was disappointed that they didn't have sandwiches, but they do have some amazing looking pastries. I will go back in a few weeks.
This is just a reminder to those who do read this it's cash only here.

Name: Caffe Trieste
Author: P
Review: Great place to have a real cappuccino! The pastries are delicious without being too sweet. The establishment has lots of indoor and outdoor seating, you will always find locals chatting and having a good time. In addition, they have a bathroom and a little store to get your own coffee beans or mocha pot.

Name: Sama Coffee Shop
Author: J M
Review: Great local spot with a very cool menu. Came here on a Sunday afternoon and the place had a very intimate feel (although I’ve driven by this place while it was packed). Lots of interesting drinks, both gold and cold, and the bites all sounded tasty. We got the mango mint mocktail, biscoff, and even got to try Natalia’s favorite kiwi pineapple drink. All were so good and the service was even better. The pistachio cake was also a nice surprise. I think Natalia and the rest of the stuff really made our first experience amazing and it surely won’t be our last!

Name: Sama Coffee Shop
Author: Sarah Rebello
Review: I came here a few times when it first opened last year, and I would suggest coming here for drinks only. I came back today after a long time and was craving baklava. I paid $35 for 6 little pieces of baklava. It was $5.95 an order which I thought would come with more than one piece but it was price per piece!There was no line & just one person after me. Even though I ordered to go and didn’t want them heated, I still had to wait 7 mins to get them. Seems like operations still need to be improved in terms of being efficient. And their slices of cake are $10…,pretty steep if you ask me, considering you can get a slice of cake at Whole Foods for $7. If you want baklava, you’re better off ordering from Mediterranean Kitchen in Burlingame and getting 3 pieces for $7.95. Once again, if you want middle eastern drinks, you can get those here, and sit & enjoy if you have the time. This review is solely based on prices of the desserts, and operations.

Name: Sama Coffee Shop
Author: LaToya Thompson
Review:
I was on my way to the local Starbucks in San Mateo, when I passed by this local hidden gem of a coffee shop. The decor and the atmosphere here at Sama Coffee are so inviting.
The staff treats you with patience and grace and the drinks and pastries are simply a delight. I ordered the Mango Mingle Hot Espresso and it was topped with a coconut honey almond crumble that smelled like heaven…I also ordered the Kunafeh with Cheese and it takes 20 minutes because they make it to order and it is definitely worth the wait.
I highly recommend Sama Coffee whether it is an early morning treat or an afternoon lift.

Name: Sama Coffee Shop
Author: Krystine B
Review: I loved the decor, but don't visit on a weekend. Service is incredibly slow, 20 minutes for my Turkish coffee, although the mango mingle came out immediately, so my friend had to wait for me to finish or wait and have a cold coffee. Several groups took too many seats, so there were 3 empty tables with no seats. I'm sure a weekday visit would be much better though. We didn't love the coffees, but the ambiance is really nice. The friend who suggested we visit said you only need to go once and I'd have to agree. 3.5/5.

Name: Sama Coffee Shop
Author: Shireen Aliewa
Review:
I visited this coffee shop yesterday and everything deserves 5 stars. It’s a cute shop with a lovely atmosphere. The have a big coffee and tea menu and they also serve desserts such as baklava and cakes. Among my friends and I we tried 4 drinks and a cake and everything was great.
Highly recommend you try this place out!

Name: Another Cafe
Author: Amanda
Review: Ordered a matcha latte and a Kouign-amann. The lady helping me was kind and polite, she offered to warm up the pastry as well which is not always offered, but personally always appreciated! The latte was very mild. Store environment is interesting with good music and there is ample seating for getting some work done.

Name: Another Cafe
Author: Tilia Bell
Review:
Decent cafe for working with lots of tables and outlets. I probably will keep looking for a difference place though, since my whole milk cappuccino was too bubbly and they don't have ceramic mugs. They do have a food menu, but I didn't order food.

They also have paintings by local artists for sale on the walls which is a nice touch.

Name: Another Cafe
Author: Aleksandra Savchik
Review: Very nice Coffee, but oh god, breakfast sandwich was the worst thing I ever had. Egg was tasting like a sponge and it was greenish gray inside. Just terrible

Name: Another Cafe
Author: Björn Grunert
Review: I had Gorgeous bagle with egg, cheddar and bacon. For drink i had an excellent caffe latte.

Name: Another Cafe
Author: Lindsay Rimpel
Review:
Currently at the the cafe as i write this. I love this place. Amazing coffee. The service is top notice and understanding. The atmosphere is cozy and cute. And if you need more privacy, there is the upstairs area where someone can get work and studying done.

The best part is that it stays open pretty late or later than the cafe that is around. My go-to remote place to work at.

Name: Antigua Coffee Shop
Author: Noah Aguilar (VLAD)
Review:
Great little cafe that we discovered y accident, and they made us feel at home.
Enjoyed their fresh juices and smoothies and looking forward to a full breakfast.
Great selection of coffees, food, wine and beers.
Cheers

Name: Antigua Coffee Shop
Author: Ranaya Patrick
Review: Latte was amazing. Good recommendation by the barista. Coffee cake was the right balance of cake and cinnamon topping (just a tad bit dry). Love venturing to new areas and trying unique cafes. The atmosphere and vibe of this place is a win all around.

Name: Antigua Coffee Shop
Author: Sofia Preza
Review:
Loooved it here!! Interior is soo nice and the food was perfect!! Best breakfast we’ve had in San Francisco. I took the breakfast burrito and pancakes. Definitely recommend it! If you want an authentic restaurant with lots of options you’re at the right place. :))
Thanks for this amazing breakfast!

Name: Antigua Coffee Shop
Author: H M
Review: Entrepanes and Antigua is the same owner so he closed Entrepanes but the phone number is answered by Antigua Cafe. The entrepanes sándwich were excellent but the Antigua sandwich are just average

Name: Antigua Coffee Shop
Author: Jeanette G
Review: Antigua is my go to spot for my Mexican “antojitos” (cravings). I’ve ordered in person and via DoorDash a few times and they’re consistent with taste and quality.

Name: Haraz Coffee House
Author: Alexander Blocker
Review:
A delightful cafe with exciting coffee options beyond the American standards. Note that the ginger coffee packs a strong punch! The staff is friendly and knowledgeable; the barista happily answered my questions and shared her recommendations.

While the coffee menu is great, the Biscoff milk cake is fine but unremarkable; some cake layers weren't fully saturated and the flavor is mild.

The light-filled interior is a very pleasant place to dine in, with rich wooden furnishings, glass walls great for people-watching, and interesting coffee history facts on the walls. And the staff is great. I would definitely go back.

Name: Haraz Coffee House
Author: Abby Shen
Review:
Haraz is quickly becoming my favorite coffee shop in SF! The coffee here is unique and excellent—it’s not your typical coffee found everywhere in the city. The spices are delicious. Can’t wait to try everything on their menu!

The big tables, spaciousness, and pleasant atmosphere are great for working, studying, reading, and socializing alike.

Name: Haraz Coffee House
Author: Qiao Kuang
Review: Love this place! Great drinks and great vibes. I've had quite a few times the cardamon-based lattes, though the pistachio latte is a tad bit too sweet for me but I've had pretty much every drink on the menu and everything has been great. Really enjoy this alternative to all the bars as a late-night spot.

Name: Haraz Coffee House
Author: mahesh s
Review:
They have clean restrooms inside for their customers. They also serve milk chai for free (ONLY if you buy something from their menu first) that you can go back and re-fill countless times.

I sat inside to work on a project on my laptop for over an hour.

The Baklava took me to heaven (not literally) it was delicious and distracted me from focusing on my work! A must-try. The Turkish coffee is the main thing here.

I also had the samosa (veggie) and it wasn't that great. I am from India so am biased towards the samosas we get in India and Indian restaurants which tend to be thicker with heavier stuffing. The samosas here were..."different" and not to my liking. Maybe that's how Turkish samosas are and I need to get used to them.

This is a great place to go to if you are looking for a coffee shop vibe where you can sit down and work on your laptop for a while. I also saw several people relaxing and chatting amongst friends/co-workers/dates here so a good spot for any of those activities as well.

Name: Haraz Coffee House
Author: Riegmar “Ricky” Valies
Review:
Looking for some quality coffee? Looking for quality cheesecake? Looking for a nice environment to chill, work or just spend some time with a friend?

Honestly, this place checks all the boxes. I had a visit there tonight and I was satisfied with the quality of the coffee, the cheesecake was such a compliment, and the service was super friendly and informative with getting the right coffee and choosing the right cake. Highly recommended visit!!

Name: Delah Coffee
Author: Ricky Tang
Review: the first two drinks we wanted to order were not available. one of the two pastries we had was not thoroughly heated up. it was wet and cold underneath so it had a freezer smell to it because it was displayed in a cooler. other than that, the two drinks we got were refreshing. the other pastry was really tasty. the service was wonderful. wish they would use less disposable ware, however. the atmosphere was comfortable since the place was roomy and management didn't pack it with too many seats. nice wall murals and beautiful details on the ceiling. check out their bathroom. it is painted gold.

Name: Delah Coffee
Author: Leyda Velarde
Review: This is by far the best coffee house  I've ever been to. The staff are wonderful, and the environment is so welcoming! The hospitality is actually insane. I got the Rose Delah Late  and the Spinach Savory Pie, and both were the best that I've ever had! Authentic Arabic Coffee.

Name: Delah Coffee
Author: Atif Khan
Review: First time trying and was recommended the mangarabian smoothie and it was easily the best drink I’ve ever tasted! Highly recommend it to anyone coming in. Fresh mango and quality taste. You can tell they emphasize details with the interior of the store and the beautiful, Arab style cups. The employees were extremely helpful as well and super nice. Best service I’ve had!

Name: Delah Coffee
Author: Lana MM
Review:
Beautiful cafe near Cal Berkeley campus. After riding our bikes for a few hours. We just wanted an afternoon coffee and pastry. We loved the Yemen Chai and coffee and Bee bite pastry here! The Yemen Chai called Adani? ..we actually drank this earlier the same morning at Farmers Market near Lake Merritt and loved it there too. No coincidences!! I realized that day I need this Yemen Chai in my life daily..I think it's my new favorite hot beverage. I love the flavor, unique spice & it's not as sweet as Indian Chai (I always prefer less sweet)
But the Bee bites pastry.. it has a cheese inside and lightness to it..but hunee...the honey on this pastry..was the best honey I've ever tasted..they said the honey is from Yemen as well..so amazing!! Definitely returning!! 💙 🙋🏻‍♀️💙
I will check out all their locations besides Berkeley they said they have one in Oakland and 2 in SF. ☺️

Name: Delah Coffee
Author: Amanda Zhang
Review: loved the baklava, honeycomb bread, and iced yemeni latte. also cutest kittens bean and beanie 🫶🫶🫶🫶🫶🫶🫶😻

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: kamel ezzili
Review: I have a profound affection for Peet's coffee; it effortlessly reigns as the most robust cup of brew I have ever encountered at any chain café. This cherished beverage became a staple during my time working in the city.

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Carrie Ramirez
Review:
My boyfriend and I live right next to the Starbucks at Fairmont but have stopped going to Starbucks due to us boycotting them. My boyfriend and I work at specialty coffee shops where we make a lot of espresso focused drinks; because of this we mostly buy frappes or drip coffee whenever we need a fix. The frappes here are better than their competitors, tasting more like coffee than just sugar which we enjoy. It's a great post lunch dessert we get for ourselves as we read or do work together. I got a caramel latte macchiato, and we were both pleasantly surprised by the tree! It was very sweet to get, made by a taller guy with slightly shaggy straight hair.

What really makes it are the staff here. There is a community of people that exists of regulars that come in. The staff are respectful and none judgmental of our orders (which, is really nice since I know our drinks are harder to make). They remember us and they're all usually very sweet and helpful.

My only complaint is that there is a slightly older gentleman who works there, I think he might be a shiftlead or manager? I rarely see him. He was nice, however, he yelled the drinks super loudly. Like drill sergeant level yell. Which I understand, especially when guests don't pick up their drinks but it disrupted the vibe of the space a bit.

Otherwise this place continues to be a safe place for my partner and I to be able to come and relax.

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Hermi Argonza
Review: Ordered cappucino...very disappointing....it's better to brew my own coffee at home and spend 50 cents to a dollar than paying more than 5 dollars and not get the satisfaction of a very good cappuccino.

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Z z
Review: Today grand opening received free 1/2lb beans with promo code, here is good place enjoy coffee with friends👍😉😉

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Eli
Review: I got HORRIBLE food poisoning from their vegan breakfast sandwich. I started throwing up almost immediately afterwards. How do you even mess premade food up that badly?? The food is just mid in general.

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Julie Durkin
Review:
Breakfast bun!!!

Mouth watering, delicious. Service was great. If I could order and get them shipped to the UK I would do.

I had 3 in one day.

Definitely recommend

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Ilre Censore
Review: It should not be legal to have a place with so many tables and no toilet. I was directed both by the usher and by a sign to the filthy public toilet outside.

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Roberto Tsuri Jim
Review: Great coffee! Excelent place! So many options to eat! The Boudin is not cheap and recomend the outside clam chowder!

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Monty Wright
Review: Coffee is so good, they also have really good food and cool animal shaped baked bread!

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Min
Review: Good coffee ,but clam chowder taste like plastic

Name: Piccino Coffee Bar
Author: Mitchell Hanson
Review: Cute local neighborhood spot for coffee with both doors wide open upon my arrival. Mixes of natural woods, homemade ceramic cups/containers, fresh quick grabngo meals. Got an iced mocha and gluten free carrot cake. The mocha is as I think modest in size relative to the price but and ideally have liked it sweeter/more chocolate flavor BUT if you’re looking for rich, authentic espresso flavors this was a good place. The gluten free carrot cake was extremely soft with a crumbly top. I think it was slightly expensive for the price but otherwise remarkable flavor and texture.

Name: Piccino Coffee Bar
Author: Sho Yoshimura
Review: Tiny cafe serving great coffee. No seats inside but there's a single bench out front. Super happy and boisterous staff.

Name: Piccino Coffee Bar
Author: Chris Jex
Review: Great small coffee shop and bakery. Not really a place for a sit down coffee although spots outside in front. The coffee is the star of the show here, baristas are pros. Highly recommend stopping by if you’re in the dog patch

Name: Piccino Coffee Bar
Author: Hazel
Review:
Food was good 😊, the service was a little busy somewhat grumpy/ impatient on that day we were there, but it's Sunday brunch so we tried to understand.

The atmosphere is amazing. I love the area and the restaurant itself.

The food was great, but may not be worth the price. It wasn't extraordinary or at least something more complex that I would say, no can't make that at home. Taste is exceptional, but maybe pricing OR serving size needs some check?

Name: Piccino Coffee Bar
Author: Sia A.
Review: Have tried this place multiple times mostly during weekends. The staff has rotated but the unfriendliness and messing up of orders hasn’t. It is very irritating. No apologies, no smiles, nothing. They announce orders by the name of the order not any numbers or names so it gets confusing. I don’t see a reason to come back or recommend this place, to be honest.

Name: Biscoff Coffee Corner
Author: Alejandro De La Torre
Review: Nice hot coffee right on the entrance to pier 39. Small place, get coffee or hot chocolate to go. Biscuits are the highlight for me. Sweet flavoured and they give you a small one with every drink. You can also buy a variety of biscuits there.

Name: Biscoff Coffee Corner
Author: Amanda
Review: Such a fun little shop! I’m a big fan of these cookies so I had to try the Biscoff latte. It’s a whole dessert in of itself since they incorporate the Biscoff cookie butter straight into the latte. (If you order it iced, you’ll probably get chunks of cookie butter up your straw, so be aware if you’re sensitive to food textures!) Overall still a really fun novelty dessert item and the store offers all sorts of Biscoff related treats. Definitely a fun stop to make while at the pier.

Name: Biscoff Coffee Corner
Author: Greg Eakins
Review:
Get yer Biscoff flavored stuff here. I think the best thing was the Biscoff coffee - I haven't seen that before. You also get a cookie with every order, which is a welcome treat.

Prices are a little high, but not out of line for what you'd expect from a touristy area.

Name: Biscoff Coffee Corner
Author: Amanda
Review: Yes! Go here! I don’t even like coffee and regret not getting a larger biscoff latte. It was. So. Good! It’s definitely sweet so if that’s not your thing, I don’t get that. We also got some extra goodies to try for later. I can’t wait!

Name: Biscoff Coffee Corner
Author: Jamie Janazian-Polizzi
Review: They have great coffee, in addition to every type of Biscoff known to mankind. We didn't try the bakery items, but they looked fantastic

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Leonard Suguitan (Len)
Review:
The Peet's store lacks energy.  The cashier never said a word to the customer in front of me, and myself.  They never smiled either.  The lack of enthusiasm impacted my experience.  The entrance area doesn't smell pleasant - almost like a urine smell.  The seating area didn't feel clean, so I chose to sit on the bench outside instead.  Outside, there's a trash can, so I didn't want to sit there either.

I'd say the store needs a thorough cleaning, and needs some air freshener.  Also, it needs some life in the store.  Doesn't feel cozy, and not inviting.

It's unfortunate, because I like coffee, but the experience wasn't good.  Would I go back here?  Probably not.

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: jude foster-mcderby
Review: They were so rude and wouldn’t even look me in the eye when I asked for a fork. They had no patience and were very aggressive towards the costumers

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Robert Dolores
Review: My favorite coffee chain.this area, pending special events or rush hour, isn't too busy most days. Pending when you go, it is very quick to get your order

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Rajah Mahsohn Omega
Review: Open early, diy cream and sugar, outlets, Wi-Fi, open restrooms. Why can’t Starbucks do this in SF?

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Véronique
Review: This location has updated hours and they work till 8PM. Baristas are awesome! coffee vs coffee cake - all was delicious! service is friendly and welcoming

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Nicolás L.
Review: With no hesitation one of my favorite coffee franchises in Shanghai/ Beijing. The quality of the coffee is amazing and even when at times it might be crowded, it is still a good place to go and drink a huge Flat White, one of my favs of the menu.

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: shannon birchell
Review: Very good coffee with vegan breakfast options. Bothe the burrito and bagel were very good. Almond and oat milk alternatives.  Better than Starbucks!

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Orkun K.
Review:
The coffee shop's practices are concerning. For a large Americano, which should contain 3 shots, they use two double shots but position the cup to waste one shot down the drain. This is incredibly wasteful.

The cleanliness of the establishment is subpar, and there's a noticeable homeless presence outside. When I ordered a large Americano, I observed them make two double shots, discarding one shot in the process. When questioned, the barista claimed I needed to pay extra for an additional shot, which doesn't make sense given their method.

I've witnessed this wasteful practice twice now. The barista mentioned working there for 4 years, which makes me wonder about the cumulative waste of coffee beans over that time. Additionally, the server's attitude was less than professional

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Matthew Rodriguez
Review:
I ordered a 32 oz ice coffee with 2 add shots, the girl making the drinks called my name and I was like oh dang cause it was hot, told her it was supposed to ice coffee (politely cause I worked food)  miss mam with the black ponytail silently spinning around to slam shot cup on the machine, then makes it as a 16 oz.
I even was like "I'm sorry" as if it was my fault she gave me not what I ordered.
The guy that rang me up was nice and the place itself looked safe, but if I lived here I would never come back based on the attitude I was given from something that was not that serious.

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Chris Saw
Review: Let’s just say I expected more, you can always judge a coffee place by their drip, and this was burnt while the medium was out

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Anthony Lorbach
Review: It was nice to see so many people in quiet contemplation.   As a bonus, they were open later than other coffee shops in the neighborhood. I especially enjoyed the music they were playing.

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Steven s
Review: I enjoy the coffee drinks at Peets, and the desserts are always decent. While I'm not an expert, I've always found the taste really outstanding, and preparation top notch. The staff is always, always friendly, and they keep the location clean and neat. There's four plugs if you want to use your computer, but wifi has always been available.

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: David Ventimiglia
Review: 2024-04-05 this place has gone downhill. Constant staff turnover means there's almost always a well-meaning kid in training behind the register, but come on. $10 for a medium mocha that tastes like hot water? Get out of here with this. Go down the street to Royal Ground. It's better and cheaper.

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Ron Allen
Review: I like the coffee here, better than most. Nicer service too.

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Honey Frauline
Review: Visited not expecting anything. Got a cold brew as it was a hot summer day and to my surprise, it was lovely! Got some of their beans and really love the coffee here!

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: salvador Lopez
Review: they wouldn't let me use the bathroom in peace even tho i was in the for less than 3 minutes. i just bought the coffee for the bathroom. There were people on line for the bathroom without being customers

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Consuelo Martinez
Review: If I could give a negative star rating it would be -5 the customer service here was beyong horrendous. Over $5 for a cup of cofee that they messed up x3 times ended up throwing it away in their trash and walking out. Only because listening to the employee making the cofee talk about how great Donald Trumpnis made me literally sick.

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Eduardo Hernandez
Review: I am very pleased with this place, I ordered it a caramel frappe and I was treated like a king which is exactly what I expected for my place like pit‘s coffee and tea. Definitely coming back for more since this place very close to my place.

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: J
Review: There is one dude who works here who has a major attitude. He made a cappuchino for me but he made a small. All I said was "oh I think that was supposed to be a medium". He didn't say a word. Just glared at me and fling the cup away. Made another one and then stared at me as he half tossed it down in front of me without saying a word like I just ruined his day when it's his own fault.

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Worried Consumer
Review: It's always the male baristas that have an attitude, minus the older gentleman with a mustache. He is the only polite male employee I have had the luck of encountering at the register and at the bar. I asked a simple question upon grabbing my drink and was met with an aggressive and hostile response from the younger barista.

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Maya
Review: While I was waiting for a large order of 8 items a woman was at the counter. She was very rude to the staff while ordering and picking up (because of misunderstanding how the menu and substitutions work) and I was sad to see that. Staff was efficient, kind, and knowledgeable.

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: capricorn11
Review:
Windee ( hope i'm spelling that right) is a delight and really embodies what customer service should be like but sadly in this day and age is a rare thing.  She was friendly and professional.
I don't have a rating for the Atmosphere really becuase my order was to go however the store looked clean and there were a few
people working on their laptops.

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Ghidossi Paul
Review:
Peets are the CLOSEST cafe's to HOW IT USED TO BE before the covd circus🤣
It's great that they let you choose how much oat milk or regular fat milk you want in your coffee. And what more could I ask for,... they actually still have HONEY! And just an added plus... their coffee is NOT THE NEW SOUR LIGHT ROASTS that they are trying to convince us are actually drinkable! Espresso coffee beans need to be roasted pretty much like what they do in Italy. If you roast them too light then they will remain sour....yuk!
So Pete will always be my go to Cafe... Just like 20 years ago 💪

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Z z
Review: Very good service Coffee and help. But today the latte temperature too low,  also they charge "Surcharge Healthy SF" with 4% when a lot of restaurants or coffee shops don't charge anymore. I am Unsatisfied with this interaction. Within previous transactions I was never charged for such payment.  ☕🍵🥐

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: J De Santiago
Review: I do seasonal work around the neighborhood, and I always get a poorly made drink. I love Peet's Coffee elsewhere. I usually get a chocolate & caramel javiva swirl. It's basically a frappe. The issue is that there's usually something wrong with it at this establishment. My last and final time it was not properly blended. Right off the back I could tell it was off. I will attach some pictures so you can see it's more of an ice coffee with with crushed ice over a frappe. I would request a new one but I'm just over it. Previously when I have complained they get rude and loud. In my opinion, it's a way of making sure it seems like I'm just some poor brown person complaining (clientele is mainly affluent White people). I love Peet's Coffee, but I will never return to this particular establishment. It's just hopeless.

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: steakfries
Review:
ALEX IS COOL!! not on fire lol, but we talked about his pins and he was super friendly!!
coffee was good too.

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Chris “Vectrexer” Romero
Review: Great for a Peet's Coffee location. Accurate and tasty coffee order. Nice and happy employees. It was a good experience in downtown Burlingame.

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Ed Moghis
Review: This is a great place to hang out while your vehicle is being serviced. The staff are super and the coffe & pastry is excellent.

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Laurie Galvin
Review: I love this place and the employees are such a great group of people.  They all pitch in and if you are a regular as I am (Monday, Wednesday& Friday every week) they have your order ready for you when you arrive!!!

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Chaw Khine
Review:
Clean place
Reasonable price
Tasty Drink
I ordered White chocolate mocha and it was good.☕☕☕

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Inga
Review: The cashier was great that rang me up and got my order right . I tipped him. The barista male was very unprofessional. He doesn’t look like he enjoys his job and does a rush job. He made my drinks wrong and left out the sugar tree vanilla and it was an easy fix but I heard him say I thought you pumped the sugar free vanilla into the cup to the gentleman who took my order and I thought to myself  but it’s still the baristas responsibility to know all ingredients are for sure put into the drinks not assume it is once it’s in his hands to make the drink. I felt that’s not the way you handle addressing an issue by passing the blame back to the other employee and especially where I can hear it at a customer . It makes it weird and uncomfortable for the customer. Also if I felt he did a good job to where I tipped him because his attention to getting my order right speaking of the man who took my order . Also the barista seemed more into talking to the young lady that was there and that’s probably why he made my drinks wrong and forgot specific ingredients. Also Don’t embarrass your associates like that . That was something he could have told the employee out of the customer’s presence because I felt I had to make the air lighter for the employee and so shouldn’t have to feel like that and I don’t want weird experiences starting my day when I’m just trying to get coffee to start my day. I’m just saying it’s unprofessional. I am a business woman and my attention to details in customer service is close .

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Ahmad Lutfi
Review:
Good food friendly staff and welcoming environment, anyone can study, for long hours,non-stop 🤗
Had everything vegan burger with hot warm mint tea
Then  some cinnamon rolls, for dessert but the burrito was tasty I couldn't forget the pinto beans protein mixed with all the veggies

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Cynthia E
Review:
Great Peet's.   Both inside and outside seating.

Friendly, helpful service.

In Downtown San Mateo - kitty corner with B Street Books - great little book shop.

Pick up a book at B Street and then relax with a coffee or tea at Peet's!

Double door entrance - I don't know if there is a button available to push if you use mobility aid and need help with door.

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Wee Peng
Review:
Staffs here are not friendly, ordered my drink from a guy at counter called Justin, I didn’t want the plastic cover for my hot tea, he stared straight to my eyes talked loudly “I don’t want you to burn yourself!” I told him that I’m environmentally concious.

Another staff that I asked for toilet password wasn’t that friendly too.

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: L Moala
Review: The best setup of a Peet’s store. Good location for study. Just parking can be tough or $.

Name: Martha & Brothers Coffee Company
Author: Mohammed Aboobacker
Review: Neighbourhood coffee shop in Noe Valley with great selection of coffees and cakes. Staff is courteous and helpful. Great place to hangout with friends in Noe

Name: Martha & Brothers Coffee Company
Author: Spencer Browning
Review: the staff here are super friendly and the coffee is great! i also really love their lavender oat latte. located in a great spot right across the street from the neighborhood square where there is a weekly farmers market and lots of outdoor seating.

Name: Martha & Brothers Coffee Company
Author: Dana K
Review:
Very good coffee and the staff are really sweet.
They have some specialty coffees. My flat vanilla oat was smooth.
They had a few vegan and gluten free options in the bakery but I needed up getting a carrot cake.
This is not near my route but if I’m in this area again defiantly getting some coffee here ❤️

Name: Martha & Brothers Coffee Company
Author: S D
Review: First time checking out Noe valley! Happened up on this cafe with no regrets! Super awesome :)

Name: Martha & Brothers Coffee Company
Author: 김서진
Review: I like their bakery! Looks delicious and one lady who works there was so kind! I ordered hot americano. It was good.

Name: The Social Study
Author: Adroit
Review:
I wanted to like this place but especially for a venue only open from 1-5pm for laptop use, the experience as a whole just fell below my expectations.

When I placed my order I waited over 15 minutes for something that was likely just scooped out from a pan, heated up, and came with a side of cream. This Strawberry Bread pudding tasted of mostly bread, lacked butter, possibly salt, made a barely noticeable custard, and the whipped topping served adjacent to it made it palatable considering I gassed $10 for it. Its more so as if they just forgot about me I dunno. The way the entire place is decked out in "Laptops prohibited after 5pm", the fact that all users were being shuttled out, the entire experience souring my liking of the joint instead of discouraging laptop use or better giving people a more relaxed cutoff, it felt extremely strict. Almost as a bad as having a strict parent taking away your device to go to bed.

My appetite spoiled at the price/quality of my bread pudding and the atmosphere so I took off at that 5pm cutoff.

Name: The Social Study
Author: Andrew H.
Review:
I was so disappointed because this place has been on my list for so long. :( Thought this would be a great place to study because it’s quite literally in the name but they don’t allow laptops after 5pm.

Pros
- super cute environment
- honey latte was so yummy
- fair pricing, latte was $5.75

Cons
- they couldn’t do hot beverages even though they’re on the menu?
- not super friendly service, I had to ask to pay and they didn’t say thank you ( I did though :))
- no sign on the door that laptops aren’t allowed after 5pm. There was a tiny sign on the table, but was blocked until some people moved. The place was probably about 20% full so who cares?
- it smelled like cigarettes inside🚬
- ceiling had tiles and dust falling off of it

Name: The Social Study
Author: felicia
Review: I wanted to like this place for various reasons: location, drinks, bites, music vibe, aesthetics and, of course, Prince on the wall! 💜 After today’s visit, I don’t know. The overall vibe today was, “why is this Black lady in here?” All while playing the music of mostly Black entertainers. I don’t understand. Yes, I was the only Black patron. 🤷🏾‍♀️

Name: The Social Study
Author: Brian Osweiler
Review: We stopped in for a drink on a Sunday afternoon.  Great spot, nice atmosphere and very reasonable drink prices.  The food looked good, but we were short on time.  Definitely worth a stop if you’re in the area.

Name: The Social Study
Author: Аlisa Paludeti
Review: So I was strolling around Japantown and stumbled upon this cafe. Since I needed a caffeine fix, I decided to give it a try and ordered a cappuccino. The vibe of the place was awesome and the ambiance was on point. Although the cafe was pretty packed on a Saturday afternoon, I managed to grab a cute little table for myself. They also have bigger tables for groups if you need them. I loved the vintage decor on the walls - books, skateboards, and vinyls - it was a feast for the eyes! It was so chill just hanging out there and people-watching. Definitely planning to come back with a friend next time.

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Cody Watters
Review:
TL;DR - bad cups.

Here’s the thing about Peet’s coffee - it’s great. Underlying, the beans are wonderful, the atmosphere is great, and the environment is adequate. Will I buy the beans at the store? Sure. Do I love the freshly brewed Peet’s coffee at my office? Definitely. Will I grab a quick cup of coffee to go? Absolutely not.

Why?

The cups are awful - theres a seam on the lip of the cup that either does not have the proper adhesive or is just too damn big so the coffee creeps up out of the cup and forms a ring under the lid, then spills down the cup.

I get coffee all over my hands when I go here, I’ve had coffee leak onto my pants and onto my car seat, and it ruins the moment.

It’s wonderful coffee but please please fix the lids.

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Marcus Durano
Review:
It's disappointing to admit that Peet's has consistently let me down when it comes to my favorite drink, a Frappe. Although I've visited various locations across the city, this particular one is where I often come to do remote work. The order details are not complex or cryptic. Why is it that my order never matches what's on the label, especially when I always request extra chocolate drizzle? Picture this: you invite a friend over and kindly ask them to take off their shoes at the door. Despite regular visits, your friend keeps forgetting to do so. It can be quite frustrating and irritating, right? The fact that I've taken time out of my life to write this review is surprising to me.  But God willing, in all that is holy in the name of our lord and savior Jesus Christ. I HOPE they can get their act and skills together for a simple task. * Cue the Karan Scene* 'PUT SOME GOD DAMN EXTRA CHOCOLATE DRIZZLE ON MY ORDER!'

PS: They always run out of certain  consumables like cups and whip cream. Poor management of inventory.

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Rashad Ujaimi
Review: Really well made coffee, liked the pastry 🥐 very much, nice atmosphere too.

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Charles Levy
Review:
Service was extremely slow on a Sunday morning, took forever to get the chocolate croissant, I actually had to go back and ask where is my order,   After waiting..
Also they should get a better supplier for the baked goods..
Sad, as I really love the coffee..

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Victor Veiga
Review:
As a local I’ve been coming for years, great community of regulars
Great spot, to grab coffee and read/catch up on screen time, mid century modern vibes decor with greenery wood accents
Friendly staff but drink turnaround time can be really slow in light of heavy take out order flow
Everyone’s welcome here, so expect frequent homeless visits
Overall great community, inclusive and diverse spot, truly highlights the uniqueness and character of San Francisco

Name: Alem's Coffee
Author: Junior Luna
Review: Had lunch around 12PM on a Friday. Waited less than 10 minutes for my food and it was delicious. Ther3 was lots of available seating inside and outside. The Tibsy / Tsebhi sga was delicious.

Name: Alem's Coffee
Author: Natalie Kaucic
Review: This place is pure magic. I’ve been coming to Alem’s for a few years and finally tried branched out from the Ful to try the Fata. Needless to say it was incredibly and I should have branched out sooner! I don’t understand how food can taste so good. The folks behind the counter are the best and always earnestly want to know if you liked it. Come here after the farmers market on Sunday! You won’t regret it!

Name: Alem's Coffee
Author: Ton T
Review: This small coffee shop carries Eritrean dishes in addition to coffee, sandwiches and pastries. Adjacent to the DMV, the cafe is slightly off the popular Temescal shopping area. It’s clear that the folks here care about the quality and authenticity of their dishes. You should not expect silverware but plastic utensils are provided if requested. The cafe is small but the indoor/outdoor seating atmosphere was great. If you’re as wimpy as I am, you can also tell them to dial down the spiciness of the dish when ordering. Weekend crowds attest to their popularity.

Name: Alem's Coffee
Author: Harry Bernholz
Review: I left California a few months ago and Alem’s is probably the spot I miss the most. Walking here for delicious coffee or tibs/ful on Sundays was often the highlight of my week. Sweet people, great music. Hope to be back soon

Name: Alem's Coffee
Author: Alexander Boulgakov
Review:
Fantastic breakfast!!

We ordered the shihan ful and tibsy, and both were excellent. Very rich flavors, very fresh.

Good, strong coffee.

The owners were very kind, and the environment was very cozy. A very welcoming and lively atmosphere.

This is a must try place for breakfast.

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Ali
Review: It's crazy how one Peets can be so much better than another. This Peets, unfortunately, doesn't make the good list. It's super small and always busy. They always need to brew a batch of coffee and hardly ever have your kind ready to go. It only has a few good employees. The others almost always get the order wrong and change wrong, and don't care when you point out the mistake. Nothing compared to the Peets in my neighborhood.

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: DR_ MANHATTAN
Review: Good vibes and great tasting coffee. Here is another place to visit and try there products. Keep up the great work... Yours Truly, DR_MANHATTAN.

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Clau Guzman
Review: I come in early in the morning to pick up a delicious triple shot latte with almond milk. The staff is always kind and attentive. Also, I’ve been loving the cinnamon coffee cake.

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Ali
Review: The employee's Allways get orders wrong and don't apologize for mistakes. Good coffee horrible service.

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: S Duan
Review:
I tried the oat milk cold brew latte, honestly, I wasn’t able to taste any coffee. I would not buy again for almost $6.

This store itself is very busy and is a little messy. Specifically, they just sweep the trash/dirt to hide them underneath their table for sugar/milk. The restroom could be cleaner.

The store is run by very young staffs, and they seem to be very busy chatting among themselves.

Name: Martha & Bros. Coffee Co.
Author: Amal Ben
Review: Really enjoyed my visit here! The coffee looks amazing, and their tiramisu is delicious. The staff is super friendly, making the experience even better. Definitely worth a visit!

Name: Martha & Bros. Coffee Co.
Author: Tee Ponsukcharoen
Review: A true local, affordable, and good coffee shop is a place you see people from all walks of life come to enjoy the coffee before heading to work, playing sudoku, or talk about life. This is the place. The coffee and the bakery are good with a wide range of selections. What stands out the most for me is the communal vibe of this place. Would love to spend more time there and try more pastries.

Name: Martha & Bros. Coffee Co.
Author: Susana Chhetri
Review: This place reminds me of how beautiful my country and general Latin vibes are, the coffee, food and music , arriba MI Gente, love it.

Name: Martha & Bros. Coffee Co.
Author: Alan Hellawell
Review: Super-friendly barista. Beans were not very fresh or high quality, judging from my cappuccino.

Name: Martha & Bros. Coffee Co.
Author: Adrian Lunsong
Review:
(Detailed review below)
Food - ⭐⭐⭐⭐
Accessibility - ⭐⭐
Ambiance - ⭐⭐⭐⭐
Cost - ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
Service - ⭐⭐⭐⭐

Food - Great selection of cakes which drew me here! Some good options for cold sandwiches and they also do some hot bagels. Good coffee too served either hot or cold. Food and drinks are served in takeaway cups/bags, plates are offered for sandwiches etc.

Accessibility - Doors are propped opened for easy access, no steps (everything is on ground floor), standard restrooms (not accessible friendly), sitting areas are quite cram so wheelchair users will need to take seats along the walking area.

Ambiance - Light and easy; big windows letting in loads of natural light (great for people watching too), casual and relaxed atmosphere. Clean and tidy. Some nice homely decor. Warm and inviting :-)

Cost - Roughly between $2.50 and $4.50 for coffee (type and size dependent), cakes are the same too. Pretty good value for money.

Tips - No table service, so just head straight to the counter and order your food/drinks. Have a nosy around though as there are cakes before and after the order point so I missed seeing some options. Free Wi-Fi available. And good speed.

Service - Quick and fast. Staff are friendly and smiling!

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee
Author: Qh S
Review:
The store is located in a residential area and does get very busy during the weekend. It's a great place to hang out with friends or simply work from there.

The coffee tastes excellent, and they also have a great selection of bakeries. We ordered the bay breakfast sandwich and also added avocado and turkey with it. It tasted amazing. Highly recommended!!

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee
Author: Angela Q. Yang
Review: I like the vibe here, drink usually served fast even with lots of orders waiting. The only thing is that the hot drink’s temperature is relatively lower than other Blue Bottle locations. For example, the latte I usually ordered at other locations, I need to order extra hot here. Even extra hot is not hot enough, the foam on the top is a bit cold when served… I wonder if it’s the way they used the machine?

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee
Author: Cynthia De Las Salas
Review: No sign on the doors, just the blue bottle symbol so you wonder, “how good can the coffee be?” Well as an avid coffee drinker I can tell you it’ll be good to the last drop. The atmosphere is just what you think a coffee shop should have: pleasant conversations, people together, people alone, people on their computers and even people with their dogs! In a hurry? No worries! Just call and pay ahead and walk in, grab your freshly brewed coffee and walk out, just like that! Hope you #enjoy it as much as we did and “why, yes,” stop by and thank me later ☺️❤️☕️

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee
Author: Nneile Nkholise
Review:
Blue Bottle Coffee on Fillmore street is in the top 5 Blue Bottle Coffee shops I’ve been to (I bet the cafes in Palo Alto, Mission Bay & South Bay would want to know if they’ve made the top 5 list).

The most beautiful thing about a coffee shop is the community that spontaneously gets built over a cup of coffee & I believe that is something so special that this coffee shop has been able to do, at the location it’s at.

What I love most about Blue Bottle Coffee is how they have somehow stuck strong to their tradition of coffee engineering. A lot of larger chain coffee shops easily lose their core mission, in Chase of revenue. BBCoffee has stuck strong to brewing great coffee.

What I love most about all the BBCoffee locations is how they have simple interiors using plain colors like white, a dash of blue and plain table & chairs. That makes their spaces quite big and lit up, which has some positive effect on people & their mental comfort.
The simplicity of their spaces + intriguing notes of their coffees, makes the place a must visit.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee
Author: Nirvana 1111
Review: The Coffee here wasn't up to standard like the other blue bottle locations I have been to. Order a Mocha couldn't taste the chocolate or the coffee and the milk was too hot. Definitely not worth the 7.50+tax they charge for it.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee
Address: 201 S B St Suite A, San Mateo
Author: Judy Margaret Chen
Review:
A good coffee shop with good coffee and nice atmosphere to take a break and chit chat.

I would give the food 5 star if the barista didn’t mix up my order. I asked for iced caffe latte. The one who took my order nodded but what I received was hot latte. It was not crowded yet I waited 10 minutes to get my coffee. This made me give up asking for my coffee to be remade. Sorry, I didn’t want to stand there waiting forever.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee
Address: 201 S B St Suite A, San Mateo
Author: Brendan
Review:
Upscale/High End coffee shop conveniently located in downtown San Mateo. Plenty of indoor seating, immaculately clean, wonderful drinks and friendly staff! It can be a little pricey but I like to think of this place like a Starbucks but much better with higher quality coffee and care put into their drinks. In order to conserve the environment, they now default to using oat milk in their drinks by default, but this makes everything much better.

Highly recommended if you’re looking to enjoy a coffee in a clean and spacious environment with friendly people and good coffee.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee
Address: 201 S B St Suite A, San Mateo
Author: Jasmine Hart
Review: Food and coffee are great (although still a bit overpriced in my opinion). My biggest problem with this place is the lack of outlets. There isn’t a single outlet in the entire shop. Also the wifi is incredibly slow and unsecured.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee
Address: 201 S B St Suite A, San Mateo
Author: Jo V
Review:
The coffee is really good, the best in San Mateo. They recently opened their indoor seating…but there NO PLUGS,  come on…can’t even charge my phone if need be. If people working in your coffee shop is an issue, just add less plugs or limit wifi use to an hour or say no laptops?
What is the thinking behind not adding plugs to your seating area.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee
Address: 201 S B St Suite A, San Mateo
Author: Abdallah ghali
Review: I stopped this morning around 6ish am to garp my coffee. This is the second time I have been treated poorly by one of the baristas. This White girl with braided ponytail was so rude. Not patient, rushing me . Handed my the pastry in a bizarre way. Not sure what her issue is? I hope not because of my color or race.specially she were nice to other white customer.Did not felt welcome or comfortable at all.

Name: Green Beans Coffee
Address: International Airport San Francisco, Peninsula Ave, San Francisco
Author: Info Source
Review:
This place serves Burmese cold food (wraps, salad, sandwiches, etc.) besides coffee, smoothies and baked items.  Tried Sousa wrap which is pretty decent.  Wish they could warm it gently for better Sam usa taste.  Guess it’s a cold wrap.

Would have given better rating if the staff is more welcoming.

Name: Green Beans Coffee
Address: International Airport San Francisco, Peninsula Ave, San Francisco
Author: Miriam Dalley
Review: The staff were nice enough but sadly the coffee was naasty. They don't do flat whites so I ordered a cappuccino but the milk was completely oversteamed. They kindly swapped it out for a drip coffee at my request but even the drip coffee tasted stale. Food wise the pastries looked nice but the 'Double Berry' muffin I had was unfortunately pretty tasteless and contained approximately 2 berries (see picture) so perhaps the name is literal?

Name: Green Beans Coffee
Address: International Airport San Francisco, Peninsula Ave, San Francisco
Author: Lion Sharp
Review:
The staff was very nice but unfortunately the food was absolutely disgusting. Tried croissants, burgers, and brownies, and all felt like they were 1 week old...
Avoid, even if there is no other choice.

Name: Green Beans Coffee
Address: International Airport San Francisco, Peninsula Ave, San Francisco
Author: puneet puri
Review: Awesome staff. They have a secret chocolate milk shake if you want for kids. They were friendly enough to even give a whipped cream in a cup for our younger one.

Name: Green Beans Coffee
Address: International Airport San Francisco, Peninsula Ave, San Francisco
Author: Chris Saw
Review:
Located at gate G on departures level, there must be other locations because this one here is beside the airport travel agency and has a white lighted sign. Decent coffee.
Welcome to San Francisco

Name: Cafe Réveille
Author: Emily Chen
Review:
One of my favorite spots in Duboce! The food is pretty good - my favorite is the kale / mushroom bowl with chicken for lunch and their chicken pasta for dinner.

Great outdoors space. Friendly staff. WiFi only available during weekdays. It’s usually hard to find a spot to sit so I don’t usually plan to work or eat there unless I get there super early

Name: Cafe Réveille
Author: Bobby Miklaušić
Review: Wanted a healthy protein filled bowl. Got a healthy protein filled bowl. Great produce. Nice sharp dressing on the veggies. Delicious slightly spicy sauce on the poached eggs. Solid. And it's on really pretty, quiet street with a lovely outdoor dining situation.

Name: Cafe Réveille
Author: Gemma Boothroyd
Review:
Cute, buzzy spot with plenty of patio area to enjoy your coffee in the sun! Super friendly service, and high-quality espresso too.

Plenty of folks on their laptops - but no outlets here, as a forewarning!

Name: Cafe Réveille
Author: Michele Losse
Review: We went there twice for breakfast, as we were staying in the area. The first time, we ordered a white coffee and a latte. The girl taking the orders asked us what kind of coffee we wanted, they had a choice of two, I ended up with 2 coffees! When I queried it and asked for a refund for the extra, they couldn't do it, possibly because it's a UK card. The girl in charge couldn't do it either and as she was busy, said she would do it later.
