It would have been nice if she'd asked for some personal details to confirm this, I still don't know if the refund went through or if they even tried! The croissants were OK, not the best I ever had but not bad. The next day I had the coconut bowl which was weird, it was mostly frozen coconut, with a very light sprinkle of granola, a very light sprinkle of cocoa nibs and fresh fruit! Never had ice cream for breakfast before! At 15 dollars I thought it was well overpriced for what I got. My husband enjoyed his eggs and potatoes.

Name: Cafe Réveille
Author: Emma Dolcini
Review: We got an iced americano and a oat milk macha latte. The thing I love most about this cafe is that it’s open until 7:30pm. It’s busy in the morning and in the evening. Perfect place to come and do some work or relax after dinner.

Name: Enter The Cafe
Author: ADLbh
Review:
Great coffee shop in Chinatown. They have a lot of options for drinks and snacks.
Amazing avocado egg toast!

Name: Enter The Cafe
Author: P R
Review: Got a great breakfast here. I ordered the avocado toast with egg and fruits. Looked amazing and tasted very delicious. Perfect place to start the day! Very lovely and nice service, too.

Name: Enter The Cafe
Author: carol velasco
Review: I've found my new Favorite place to eat lunch, relax after lunch, patiently wait for friend, etc etc. Can order thru electronic kiosk or nice, helpful, young lady. ..So far I've tried 2 egg sandwiches & Purple Rice Yogurt-AWESOME-hear it Boosts Energy. I love that it's a relaxing corner café w wide windows to hear the world, but not be part of it.

Name: Enter The Cafe
Author: HaNg
Review: Great service. Really nice people and great food to match.

Name: Enter The Cafe
Author: Nico Gorbal
Review: The boba milk tea was alright, not the best not the worst. It’s basically a black tea+milk+boba. The stuff seemed pretty busy, took 15 min to get my tea.

Name: Foghorn Coffee
Address: 900 North Point St Suite E204B, San Francisco
Author: Junior Marín
Review: The chocolate + croissant experience was absolutely amazing I can’t even describe it, my soul returned to my body, the barista was a girl, did a good job.

Name: Foghorn Coffee
Address: 900 North Point St Suite E204B, San Francisco
Author: Sierra Warren
Review: Ordered a flat white. Coffee was bold and flavorful. Milk could have been frothed a little more for texture and heat. Great location by Ghirardelli

Name: Foghorn Coffee
Address: 900 North Point St Suite E204B, San Francisco
Author: Abbie Lu
Review: Much better coffee options then inside Ghirardelli. They had more milk alternatives and sugar free flavor. It was not the best but decent and a much better option for the area. I recommend it over the coffee inside. The barista is more skilled as well.

Name: Foghorn Coffee
Address: 900 North Point St Suite E204B, San Francisco
Author: Max Heegard
Review: Good coffee but the barista has got to be the rudest service person I’ve had the displeasure of ordering from in recent memory.  It’s a shame you are forced to tip before your coffee is served because I wouldn’t have tipped this guy after the fact.  I don’t want to elaborate he was just extremely cold and rude.  If you don’t like your job, that’s ok, find something else.

Name: Foghorn Coffee
Address: 900 North Point St Suite E204B, San Francisco
Author: Nelda Fernandez
Review: We started walking to Pier 39 early in the morning, and this was one of the only places open for coffee. I ordered a latte, and it was probably the one of the best I've had. I'm actually really surprised to see the ratings are not higher than what they are. And the location is perfect. You can stroll around the square then head over to Pier 39.

Name: Contender Coffee
Author: Patricia Pan
Review:
Such a gem!! Love the layout and love how friendly Jessica is.

Also, every Tuesday from 7am to 1pm, the food is 50% for Taste Test Tuesday. All baked goods, hot foods, even the overnight oats are 50% off but smoothies and other drinks are still full price.

Definitely want to come back and try new things!!

I had the Alfredo sandwich, which is on piazza blanco (mix between ciabatta and focaccia) which was incredibly delicious.

The bialy and overnight oats and the lemon poppyseed muffin tops are on my shortlist for next time!

Name: Contender Coffee
Author: Summer Johnson
Review: I stopped by after work and was pleasantly surprised :-). I passed this place a day before going to a different coffeeshop and decided to come back and check it out. The menu is pretty hefty with all of the customary coffee drinks, but they also have smoothies with protein offerings. They hold boxing 🥊 classes here, hence the name of the shop. Usually places that multitask like this let the coffee fall by the wayside, but the barista recommended a drink he put on the menu that day, a peanut butter cup latte, and it was really good! Hope he keeps it on the menu. Anyways, this place had a nice friendly vibe and staff was kind. The shop is open and airy and I will definitely be a regular.

Name: Contender Coffee
Author: Nini “Nini” Salinas
Review: I keep forgetting about this gem in the neighborhood and how convenient that there is also a boxing gym within this place also! Great coffee. Great staff. People People must try it and come in! All the natural sunlight that peeks through their windows in the AM is so amazing. Love the ambiance! You get amazing coffee and a work out 💪🏽 if you choose

Name: Contender Coffee
Author: SF Shopper
Review: Great coffee and super friendly staff. They have a boxing gym here that I’m considering trying out. It’s clean and overall welcoming here. Doesn’t hurt that the coffee is good.

Name: Contender Coffee
Author: Julia Schaefer
Review: The coffee at Contender is delicious! So excited to see that they serve Thanksgiving coffee, the pioneer of Artisan roasted coffee. Delicious cappuccino, smoothies and very sweet staff. Next time, I'm going to sign up for a boxing class, too! Highly recommend :)

Name: CoffeeShop
Author: Zhuo Z
Review:
This is quite an amazing coffee place. They have coffee beans all around the world and all freshly roasted. Love that Thai breakfast cold latte drink. The sweetness of the banana with the touch of spices is so perfectly balanced.

They use melted Belgian Chocolate for Mocha!

Their cruffin with raspberry jam is amazing. The cruffin comes with a crust that crumbles and melts in the mouth.

Also love the muffin shaped pastry with an egg inside (I think the name is devil inside?). Very tasty but a tad bit too much baking soda.

Name: CoffeeShop
Author: Vicky Vuong
Review:
The coffee shop is a bit hard to find, probably because its name is literally coffeeshop, even Google is confused. However, when you find it, it is a cute little neighborhood cafe that serves your standard espresso drinks. Coffee was tasty.

They also serve pastries, those were fresh. I got a croissant which was really nice.

There is some limited seating outside, but overall the cafe is quite small.

Would recommend.

Name: CoffeeShop
Author: Falling Action Films, Inc.
Review: Parking is difficult, but if you get a chance well worth the effort. Had my Oat milk latte and it was delicious. Also had an almond croissant and it was very fresh and tasty.

Name: CoffeeShop
Author: Mikhail Sidorov
Review: Great coffee. I love the fact that they didn’t make a big deal when I asked for ceramic plate and mug. So rare nowadays! Definitely recommend.

Name: CoffeeShop
Author: Katherine Chen
Review: Dirty Matcha (espresso + matcha) is great! Love it😍 Pastries also look good and would also like to try some next time!

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee
Author: Hecvi V
Review: Blue Bottle is one of my favorite coffee shops in San Francisco. I always order the NOLA with oat milk, either hot or cold depending on the weather, and it’s delicious. The service is always great. The Ferry Building location is my favorite because it stays open later.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee
Author: Lavinia Pedrollo
Review:
The guys were so funny and nice! They made a special cappuccino for me (see the funny pic) and made my day!
Coffee is a bit expensive for the cup size, but hey! Coffee is really good. :))

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee
Author: Bab Bab
Review: The origin place of Blue bottle cafe. It was aquite small cafe. With long history, there is heritage of San Francisco. In fact, there are Blue Bottle cafes all over the city, but if you like Blue Bottle, it's worth visiting at least once. In the evening, if you walk around the pier behind the cafe with coffee, the night view across the sea is wonderful. You can drink coffee and take a look around. It's a great place with a story.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee
Author: G Santana
Review: Good cappuccino but the chocolate croissant not so great….

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee
Author: Gosia Ozga
Review: Great coffee choices while you wait for a ferry! I was a bit bored and walked around and saw this coffee place! It was so nice and simple! As a matcha lover I got one for myself… I took it to go! The coffee art was so cute! They really make a good matcha there!

Name: CoffeeShop
Author: Eileen Lau
Review: Small specialty coffee shop which has some unique drinks, merchandise, and pastries. There are a few tables and chairs outside. My friend got a matcha drink and I got an iced Americano. My Americano was on the acidic side, and quite strong. Prices are on the high end

Name: CoffeeShop
Author: Samantha Lau
Review: small, simple coffee shop as the name suggests. they have some pastries and the service is quick and friendly.

Name: CoffeeShop
Author: Alan Hellawell
Review: Very pleasantly quirky venue and barista. Fantastically rich 8 oz cappuccino. I also enjoyed the cookie.

Name: CoffeeShop
Author: Donny Pusawong
Review:
On Sunday mornings, I try to go out in the mornings and support local coffee shops.  I use my Nespresso machine on most days now!   I usually order just a espresso, a coffee when I'm outside.  I ordered the hot chocolate, Latte, and a espresso!It was too early for iced coffee w/coffee ice cubes!  Bulletproof coffee or Sherpa sounds too rich in fat for me!  Not bad espresso and a good latte. Great neighborhood spot.
Cute small shop and friendly and hard working batista!  Easy parking in front on a Sunday morning.

Name: CoffeeShop
Author: Daniela Nuñez Zurita
Review: Lovely little neighborhood coffee shop. They have great coffee and tea options as well as amazing pastries. Their baristas are very friendly and super helpful. Great spot to grab a coffee while sitting outside and enjoying a sunny Mission day.

Name: Coffee Bar Montgomery
Author: Kay Bee
Review: I warning eh area and needed a latte. While there are many cafes in the area, I do love their coffee. Service was quick and they have outdoor seating if you want.

Name: Coffee Bar Montgomery
Author: Farshad P
Review:
The coffee here is very good, you can tell everything is measured and carefully made. You can also taste it in the final product too. I’ve been getting the iced latte (rotating between their house made caramel or vanilla syrups) for over a year and it’s consistently been great each time and never varies.

It’s one of the best coffee in FiDi for sure!

Name: Coffee Bar Montgomery
Author: Ted Eicher
Review: To start the coffee beans are $27 per pound. That is a big price for mediocre coffee. Bought a half pound of Neapolitan beans, featuring a medium dark roast. Flavor notes say dark chocolate,  hazelnut and caramel. Couldn't detect any of those flavor notes even remotely. Made the coffee at home using a Baratza Encore grinder and a Bodum French Press. Really lacking any richness or body. Tasting it the one word that came to mind was ordinary. Contacted the owner BEFORE trying the beans about their return policy. She explained to me it could be possible on a case by case basis, but would not accept a return just because you don't like the flavor profile. There in lies the problem, to me this coffee doesn't have a flavor profile. Coffee Bar uses Mr. Espresso beans which are fire roasted. Philz Coffee is way better. Caffe Trieste is 50 times better. So now I'm stuck with a half pound of beans I paid over $13 for and I don't even want to drink it. I do not recommend the Coffee Bar on Montgomery Street

Name: Coffee Bar Montgomery
Author: Leslie Wu
Review:
4 stars only because the hours are wrong and we’ve gone multiple times only to be burned by it being closed. It’s almost always closed when we go, and we are craving a coffee fix so we have to turn to the nearby blue bottle, so I’ve decided to add the new hours here for others. Although it is no longer summer, these hours seem to be the correct ones, hopefully things don’t change again 🤔

Otherwise, it’s great coffee and I’d highly recommend!!

Name: Coffee Bar Montgomery
Author: Thomas L
Review: My go-to coffee shop whenever I work from the office. I usually order a cappuccino, and sometimes I'll add an order of cold brew to drink the next day lol. The coffee is consistently good. Note: if you order your cold brew without ice, there's an extra charge of $1. Staff is friendly. I wish they'd bring back the punch cards :)

Name: Fresh Brew Coffee
Author: Austin (Amache)
Review: Food was great, had a ham egg and cheese croissant. Friend had a turkey egg and cheese bagel and said it was great as well. Drinks were very good as well, iced Chai latte and a hot caramel latte. Price is beyond fair and the service was great. Will definitely be back.

Name: Fresh Brew Coffee
Author: Akiko In SD
Review:
Delicious Vietnamese sandwiches here. Try the falafel avocado banh mi - it's amazing! My friend loved the pork one too.
Limited seating inside and out. We usually grab takeout and enjoy our sandwiches up on California Street by the cathedral. Great food with a view!

Name: Fresh Brew Coffee
Author: Yuen Yee Bianca Cheng
Review: Dropped by to get a pork sandwich and a cheese veggie bagel, for drinks we get a latte and a smoothie. Everything tastes so well and the stuff came out super quick. The place is full of people and you could see the letters and notes from previous customers. The price is very reasonable, around $12 for the bagel and the smoothie, $11 for the sandwich and the latte. Definitely recommend it to people living nearby.

Name: Fresh Brew Coffee
Author: David Basler
Review: Great local find in the neighborhood! We shared a couple Vietnamese sandwiches (the veggie Banh Mi was fantastic!) and a coffee drink then headed back out on our way to explore the city. Definitely a locals spot—two different customers came in and said “I’ll take the usual” 😉

Name: Fresh Brew Coffee
Author: Kira Sano
Review: Great quick service, nice indoor and outdoor seating, great coffee, amazing Banh Mi with pate (soooo good!) and great location. I got a cheap coffee and sandwich for great price.

Name: The Coffee Movement
Author: priyanka pola
Review: Absolutely love this place. It's best coffee experience. I tried the cocoa cappuccino. The staff was very welcoming even during a busy hour. The place is the turn point for Powell-Hyde and Powell-Mason. There is little space outside the Cafe to sit and enjoy the coffee and view. I am going to go there again to try the coffee flight.

Name: The Coffee Movement
Author: E Bo
Review: The coffee flight was amazing, and excellent service. There is no shade, no umbrellas, chairs, or inside seating. If it were raining or cold, or I didn't want full sun, I would have to go elsewhere since I like to relax and enjoy my coffee. With such wonderful coffee, a few chairs and umbrellas would be nice.

Name: The Coffee Movement
Author: Jaleh Moyse
Review: We had a slight wait to get our coffees, but it was worth it. I opted for the oat milk latte, which was a solid choice—smooth, creamy, and well-priced. The employees were also very polite, making the experience even better.

Name: The Coffee Movement
Author: Vicky C.
Review:
Got the Cocoa cappuccino and matcha latte with honey lavender syrup around early May.
I like that they have seasonal menu.
The place is really small and not indoor to stand or sit. As someone who isn’t huge about coffee, I would only stop by if you are nearby and no lines.

Name: The Coffee Movement
Author: Nichole Alex Ibarra Fernandez
Review: Amazing coffee, super friendly baristas. Tried the tasting flight then came back the next day to get the filter coffee. I love trying specialty coffee shops and this place is really one of the best. They also have a good selection of coffee beans, I got a bag from DAK. The barista was really helpful about brewing options and suggestions.

Name: Timeless Coffee
Author: Bow K. Techataveekijkul
Review: Stopped by a vegan cafe in Oakland this morning, and yes, everything was *truly* vegan! The cherry cheesecake was a gem—no cheese, of course. I had a matcha strawberry soy drink, and it was absolutely delicious. The cozy vibe of the place just made it even better!

Name: Timeless Coffee
Author: P. M.
Review: Timeless Cafe has a sleek, contemporary vibe with excellent coffee and ok pastries. The atmosphere is lively, making it a great spot for a casual meet-up or a quick break.

Name: Timeless Coffee
Author: Claire Hart
Review:
A Vegan Gem in Oakland

Timeless Coffee in Oakland is a true gem for anyone who appreciates quality vegan food and drinks. Their hot chocolate is nothing short of incredible, and their house-roasted coffee consistently delivers an exceptional flavor that keeps me coming back. I’m always excited to try their specialty drinks, like the pistachio latte or the refreshing iced strawberry matcha, which are always crafted to perfection.

The atmosphere at Timeless is laid-back and inviting, with comfortable seating both inside and outside. It’s the kind of place where you can easily lose track of time, whether you’re catching up with friends or simply enjoying a quiet moment on your own.

What truly sets Timeless apart is their staff. I visit weekly, and no matter how long I take to decide between their amazing options, they’re always patient, kind, and friendly. Their consistency in providing excellent service is something I deeply appreciate.

As a vegan, Timeless is a sanctuary. Knowing that everything on the menu is 100% vegan gives me complete peace of mind, allowing me to enjoy their offerings without hesitation. They’re constantly refreshing their menu with new specialty drinks and mouthwatering sweets. Their cherry cheesecake is my personal favorite—an absolute must-try!

Timeless Coffee isn’t just a coffee shop; it’s a community space that nails the balance between a cool, relaxed vibe and seriously good food and drinks. Whether you’re vegan or not, this place is definitely worth a visit.

Name: Timeless Coffee
Author: Justin Xiao
Review: All in all a great place that I would love to come back to. They roast their own coffee and they have a roaster in the store. The cakes are all vegan and they all look very delicious. We had the tiramisu it and we loved it. The coffee was also  so very good and one person in my party said it was some of the best coffee they've tasted in a while. The bathroom is super clean in the back. Staff very friendly and efficient. There's also nice outdoor seating in front of the cafe. The indoor has very nice atmosphere for hanging out, catching up my friends

Name: Timeless Coffee
Author: R “RR”
Review: What an incredible place. Everything they do is on another level. Whether you are vegan (their foods and coffees are Vega ) or not, I think their coffees and pastries are some of the tastiest in the Bay Area.

Name: Central Coffee Tea & Spice
Author: Erica W
Review:
Found this place this morning on my dog walk… cute spot and the space has a lot of potential.

Amazing latte… highly recommend! Order the 12 oz because the 16 oz is not filled.

Name: Central Coffee Tea & Spice
Author: B Warr
Review: If younneed to work or study, they have no access to public wifi even after purchasing food and drink

Name: Central Coffee Tea & Spice
Author: Luke Robinson
Review:
Went here 3 of our 4 mornings in SF. Great coffee and bagels.
Thanks Ali!!

Name: Central Coffee Tea & Spice
Author: Alma Payne
Review: A cute hole in the wall cafe. Literally covered in green leafy plants  from the street view. It is a cute and cozy place. The girl who assisted me was so helpful and patient. She referred a white chocolate temptation ice coffee. Trying to watch my plastic, she was kind enough to put my iced coffee in a glass cup for me. I will definitely be coming back soon to try more.

Name: Central Coffee Tea & Spice
Author: Marguerite Bradley
Review:
Love this place. Still has the original coffee shop vibe, if you are looking for the minimal sterile tech break room coffee vibe, this isn’t your place. Some of the best coffee in Sf at some of the best prices. Every kind of milk alternative you could ask for. I’ve never been disappointed with the food. Some of these comments below seem like they are rating a breakfast restaurant, it’s a coffee shop... you know, you order coffee, grab something on the side, read a book, do some writing, drawing or work on something. Maybe you even grab and go.

Anyways, so glad I live in walking distance and myself and my dog can enjoy our treats on the seats outside. Stay golden. You guys are a gem.

Name: modern coffee
Author: Lauren Murphy
Review: I got a vanilla matcha, pretty tasty. Cafe opens up into an atrium which was a bright and quiet place to work, super perfect for what I needed. The wifi worked well (I didn't realize, but was still here after they closed and the wifi was working) and bathrooms were clean.

Name: modern coffee
Author: McKinley Peña
Review: one of the best coffee shops in the neighborhood. regularly come in to get a burnt cinnamon latte with oat milk and it NEVER disappoints. friendly staff, great coffee, and a nice environment. 10 out of 10 recommend

Name: modern coffee
Author: ELI GONZALEZ
Review: Hands down one of the best coffees I’ve had! Real artistry in coffee. I liked that they had some unique and creative drinks. For example I ordered the Masala Chai Latte with a shot of expresso. My fried. Ordered the blackstrap molasses latte. Both tasted fabulous!! Also the design and furniture looked really cool. Definitely coming back to this place.

Name: modern coffee
Author: Melvin Johnson
Review: Got a very tasty Cortado. The inside is very nice but there is a very cool indoor courtyard attached to the coffeeshop that is very nice to pull your laptop out in to work. Lots of natural light.

Name: modern coffee
Author: Alyssa Serrano
Review: Burnt cinnamon latte is amazing! Really nice shop. I enjoyed the pretzel.

Name: Union Street Coffee Roastery
Author: Nikolay Dubina
Review: outstanding. I travelled here from Singapore in 2022, it was excellent. now in 2024 I go here from Hong Kong, this place is excellent even still. truly outstanding cafe. best in San Francisco.

Name: Union Street Coffee Roastery
Author: 365 virgo
Review: Great little coffee shop. Has a wide have a wide variety of different coffees and flavors .if I do say so myself it's better than Starbucks. Even their pastries are fantastic . The staff are helpful and very knowledgeable and willing to answer any questions you might have. and believe it or not it's even in a good price range.a must go if you're ever on Union Street.

Name: Union Street Coffee Roastery
Author: Laval P. Olaya
Review: Their service was excellent, prices are a little higher than their competitors but they do have a wide variety of coffee. The pastries were very delicious expecially the lemon bar. And the cost of pastries were pretty reasonable

Name: Union Street Coffee Roastery
Author: Jay
Review: Reasonably decent.  A relatively quiet place to rest from site-seeing with open seating.  Quiche was quite good, cheesy and fluffy with flakey, buttery crust. Espresso was about "average" (a good alternative to Starbucks).

Name: Union Street Coffee Roastery
Author: Michelle Agudelo
Review: Great oat cappuccino served with a smile! Lovely to sit in the shade in the afternoon and get some work done.

Name: Sextant Coffee Roasters
Author: Lauren
Review:
As a Melbourne local I have very high expectations for coffee, and coming into America, I was definitely nervous! When I Googled coffee shops close to my hotel, Sextant came up and I was not disappointed.
On my way out for a drive I stopped by for a cold brew and Morning Bun which were both delicious (thank god).
A really nice selection of kombuchas and other cold drinks as well.
Cute warehouse-y location and was nice and quiet given 9am on a Sunday!

Name: Sextant Coffee Roasters
Author: SB Duggirala
Review:
Really great coffee!

I loved the balance flavors of coffee in this place. Coffee is perfectly creamy with right amount of bitterness. Tried their latte, cappuccino and mushroom croissant. Loved them all.
If you are in the neighborhood, you need to try this place for sure!

Ambience is excellent with indoor and outdoor seating. Friendly staff.

Name: Sextant Coffee Roasters
Author: alejandra corbella
Review:
Solid coffee with great gluten free pastries!

I recently hosted an event and I bought a large container of coffee. Their service was super fast and they had it ready in 15 min.

They have a variety of Asian flavored desserts as well as the usually croissants and cookies. They also have a few gluten free options, I recommend the banana walnut cookie

Name: Sextant Coffee Roasters
Author: Gary Cole
Review: Solid caffe latte with just the right combination of espresso notes, milk and crema. I love places that roast their own beans and enjoyed viewing the roaster on site while breathing in the rich aromas of roasted coffee beans. Service was average though the barista did share he was a highly satisfied regular patron before taking the job. That’s always a good sign. What makes this establishment special is a secluded courtyard in the back that allows one to bask in the sun while enjoying private space - right in the heart of busy, industrial SoMa district. Chart a course to Sextant and your sails will be filled.

Name: Sextant Coffee Roasters
Author: David Barwick
Review: Highly rated for a reason. Great coffee and great service. We really enjoyed the organic food options. Indoor space is very nice with lots of room to sit and work. The patio outside is very nice as well.

Name: Cafecito
Author: Taeko Ichioka
Review: Tiny little hole in the wall cafe run by the sweetest staff. The tamales are INCREDIBLE. Right amount of spice, tender meat and so moist. I also had their breakfast sandwiches. MASSIVE! For SF, best bang for your buck. I stayed in the neighbourhood for 4 nights, went there twice - if I didn’t have to catch an early flight, probably would’ve been 3 times. Neighbourhood is sketch but…downtown. Everywhere is sketch

Name: Cafecito
Author: Sascha S.
Review:
We had breakfast in this small neighborhood place. My girlfriend likes her coffee strong, and we were definitely in the right place for that.
We both had the veggie quesadilla which was very delicious.
Don't sleep on the salsa!
Friendly people, good coffee, and reasonable prices made this a 5* experience for us

Name: Cafecito
Author: Nočnica Fee
Review: A great meal from the sweetest people. My breakfast burrito was hot, crispy and deliciously flavored. My latte smooth and rich. And again: kind personable staff. A little oasis.

Name: Cafecito
Author: Marco Gomes
Review: excellent sandwich and mango smoothie. i'm definitely coming back for more food.

Name: Cafecito
Author: Atasha Jordan
Review: I loved my breakfast from this restaurant. I got the breakfast burrito and asked for it spicy. The portion was huge and there was so much flavor in every bite. I also enjoyed the Mexican chocolate. The nice spiced hot chocolate was a refreshing yet indulgent drink.

Name: Haus Coffee
Author: Miranda
Review: lovely backyard and indoor seating. the owners are very nice and dedicated to the coffee making. didn’t feel like it was a lot of marketing with the in person experience :) the latte was good.

Name: Haus Coffee
Author: Grace Lee
Review: They offer single origin pour over coffee, clearly priced at $5 on the menu. We chose two exotic pour overs to enjoy. Freshly brewed coffee tastes the best and it has a noticeable acidity. All-wood interior decor along with the spacious and bright seating, made it chill and relaxing. There is also an outdoor seating at the back.

Name: Haus Coffee
Author: PJ Pasricha
Review:
I recently visited Haus and was impressed by the peaceful and quiet ambiance of the place. It's the perfect environment for getting work done, with plenty of seating and a beautiful outdoor patio that's definitely worth checking out.

The black coffee at Haus is absolutely wonderful, with a rich and robust flavor that's sure to satisfy any coffee lover. I highly recommend it!

Service is also very nice and the staff is very kind.

Overall, Haus is a great place to come for a productive work or study session. The atmosphere is peaceful and quiet, the coffee is excellent, and the ample seating and beautiful outdoor patio make it a great place to spend a few hours getting things done.

Name: Haus Coffee
Author: Chloe Venn
Review: great for a peaceful conversation or getting work done. i had the cappucino and americano with the columbia espresso, the indonesia java pour over is delicious too. the barista works totally alone (respect) - and is super chill and friendly. coming back soon 🤍

Name: Haus Coffee
Author: SB Duggirala
Review:
Beautiful place with great coffee!

Tried their cappuccino - well-balanced and robust flavor.
They have ample indoor and outdoor seating.
They are pet-friendly, so you can enjoy your coffee with your dog.
Definitely recommend this place for a great coffee!

Name: Ritual Coffee Roasters
Author: O K
Review: Great coffee! Not too many options for flavor but they have a unique variety. Ample seating and nice ambiance. Great seating spot by the window to people watch.

Name: Ritual Coffee Roasters
Author: Vaishnavi Gupta
Review:
Iced lattes are overly milky no matter how little milk I ask for. Below is a picture of a tiny 4 oz cup that funnily is somehow still the same too-milky light brown shade. This was supposed to have a standard double shot in it but it doesn't seem like it does?

It'd be nice if they could accommodate for an option between straight espresso and super milky.

Name: Ritual Coffee Roasters
Author: Salvadorian
Review: Nice clean coffee roaster store to chill and work on your computer, there is seating outside surrounded by the eye catcher half hull of a boat resting on the side structure at Valencia street. When I visited on an early evening there was ample space to seat inside.

Name: Ritual Coffee Roasters
Author: Bryce Schaefer
Review: Ritual is hands down one of my favorite coffee spots in the bay. Their coffee is always phenomenal. The cafe is a pleasant spot with natural light and a lot of seating. They've got a good amount of merch available for purchase as well. Check this place out!

Name: Ritual Coffee Roasters
Author: Samantha Pantoja
Review:
Not pet friendly :( I was only allowed to buy coffee and food to go and had to wait outside, I was only allowed to carry my friend’s dog with me on my arms to enter the  café.

Coffee was ok.

Name: Réveille Coffee Co.
Author: Darshan Gajara
Review:
Delicious coffee & breakfast. The avocado toast was a little hard to cut, but it tasted good nevertheless.

The only inconvenient part was the designer chairs. They looked good but didn't have a backrest and were quite uncomfortable.

Name: Réveille Coffee Co.
Author: Kristin P Ho
Review: I can’t speak to the coffee but the food is good - really enjoyed the breakfast bowl and crunchy bowl (lunch) with added eggs. More flavorful than it looks.

Name: Réveille Coffee Co.
Author: Charmie Kapoor
Review:
I've tried avocado toast in about 20 different places in SF and this would be in the top 3.

Good coffee, good toast.

They gave us a take out even though we mentioned dining in.

Name: Réveille Coffee Co.
Author: Marina
Review: You have to try their Acai bowl, which has GF granola and fruits. Their matcha chia pudding is really good too. Their coffee is a no-brainer.

Name: Réveille Coffee Co.
Author: Ieva Dekaminavičiūtė
Review:
There is one guy cooking in the kitchen and kudos to him! I have been here a few times and in food and coffee we can trust.

Perfectly poached egg, well seasoned, elevated simple perfection!

Name: Moka Coffee
Author: Slex
Review: Super fast iced mocha latte, great ambiance, decent amount of seating, and loved all the books! I was eying up that almond poppy seed lemon muffin but I just wasn’t hungry enough to try it 😅 staff was very sweet and lightning fast ❤️

Name: Moka Coffee
Author: Pamela L
Review: It's a cozy place to grab coffee, muffin, breakfast food or sandwiches. It has about 8 tables of 2 and outdoor chairs and mini tables. Service is good and friendly. Food is average. If you're in the neighborhood, give it a try. It's the only coffee place that in the block.

Name: Moka Coffee
Author: Katie Ahmann
Review: Quaint little coffee shop with a relaxing atmosphere. Great service, calm music. Lots of outdoor seating. Straight forward menu with tasty bagels. Great quality coffee. Definitely a nice weekday spot to sit and read a book.

Name: Moka Coffee
Author: Lucia Trevino
Review: Really cute spot to read, do work, or hang out and chat! The food was delicious and the service was friendly and welcoming! Definitely worth it for people looking to get a bang for your buck! We will be returning!

Name: Moka Coffee
Author: Jerry L Javier
Review:
The chai latte was very sweet, which I enjoyed but I had been expecting the more spiced taste. I haven’t tried their coffee yet.

Nice staff and a cozy inside with seating. They also have outdoor seating that’s covered.

Name: The Coffee Movement
Author: Peter Farejowicz
Review:
The Coffee Movement has been on my wish list for a while to visit as I’ve heard good things. This spot is one of their two SF locations and far enough from downtown to have plenty of nearby parking.

Inside the shop is super modern and aesthetic, the baristas get through orders quickly and the prices aren’t too bad either.

On this visit I went with their signature cocoa cappuccino which tasted great. The cocoa component is just a dusting on top and not really part of the drink but the cappuccino itself was super smooth, balanced, and delicious. I only wish more came with the cup as it was a fairly small serving.

Name: The Coffee Movement
Author: Sylwia K
Review:
The coffee was really excellent. It might be the best in the city? We tried the tasting flight with 3 different filter coffees (dark, medium, light) and the specialty pour over. It was a great way to taste the full range they had on offer.

The pour over was spot on. The light roast had some much aroma to it! I really enjoyed the medium roast as well.

The orange pistachio muffin was very unassuming looking but moist and delicious.

The line was out the door but moved quickly and we had no problem grabbing a seat. The only minor complaint was that the place was very loud.

Name: The Coffee Movement
Author: Edison Huang
Review: Easily my second favorite coffee spot in the city but only because their Chinatown location holds a special place in my heart. I do love how this location has more space and seating for everyone. Same great drinks and same great vibes from the employees. Highly recommend.

Name: The Coffee Movement
Author: DJ DIDONNA
Review:
Best coffee in the city. More importantly, they walk the fine line between being super serious about their craft (curating amazing and weird roasters) while not coming off as pretentious.
Come for the coffee sampler, come back for the rotating bean selections from all over the place.

Name: The Coffee Movement
Author: Wade Hannah Aaron
Review: One of the best coffee I have had so far in San Fran. Prodigal on drip was delicious. The espresso was great too! speciality shop and worth the wait.

Name: Coffee Roastery
Author: Wally
Review: First of all the roasters has a great vibe to it. Clean interior and clean bathroom. The staff was friendly and considerate with us and the guests we saw while there. They make sure we're attended to and that we enjoy our coffee. The chocolate cafe latte was rich and creamy. The New York crumble cake was moist and warm, it was delicious!!

Name: Coffee Roastery
Author: Natalie Do
Review: Family owned and so lovely in here! The prices are phenomenal for what you get. I loved my breakfast sandwich! The eggs were so fluffy! I had the ice Vietnamese coffee and it was made to perfection! The staff was very personal too! You can tell they value their customers and they seem to know all their regulars ☕️

Name: Coffee Roastery
Author: Qh S
Review:
Coffee roastery with great vibe. The coffee was delicious, and they had a lot of bakery options as well.

We ordered two burritos, and they are all very tasty.

The store is very spacious, but it gives a mama&papa store feeling. Good for hanging out with friends or just working from there. Service is very friendly also.

Name: Coffee Roastery
Author: Araceli G.
Review:
Lovely location with a great interior, I gave it 2 stars bc the food was microwaved and the coffee was made incorrectly. Most of the food prepackaged and putting prepackaged food with adhesive stickers on is a horrible mistake. Even Starbucks removes the wrapping before warming food. Trying to unwrap the food was disgusting, the adhesive was melted on. Totally not worth the price for the mediocre service.
There is no parking for this location and bathrooms are not allowed for customers.

Name: Coffee Roastery
Author: Kerri Bermudez
Review: Lovely locally owned coffee shop with good strong bold flavored coffee and wonderful friendly owners. We tried a couple of other places and this was our favorite. Great place to stop for a cup in the morning.

Name: Ritual Coffee Roasters
Author: Brian Cho
Review: Great place for coffee in near city hall.  The staffs are very friendly and I enjoy the outdoor atmosphere.  It is kind of small though.

Name: Ritual Coffee Roasters
Author: Happy
Review: This coffee isn’t cheap. It’s $5 for a drip. In LA I can get incredible organic flavorful drip for $3.25 & a free refill. Why no free refill? & if your $7 pour over is tasteless. Very awkward to return… so you’re stuck for $7. Customer service varies a lot. Many times I feel disappointed.

Name: Ritual Coffee Roasters
Author: Miranda
Review: good coffee but horrible almond croissant - super stale and dry, felt like leftover from 2 days ago. the items are overpriced even for sf standards. the area outside of it is supposed to be nice but in reality there are crazy people loitering in front of the store. not the best environment to sit down and sip your coffee.

Name: Ritual Coffee Roasters
Author: Pilar Morais
Review: Try and avoid the weekends and have no wait time to enjoy a San Francisco classic. Great coffee in a great little spot. They truly know what is a long black which is a nice surprise. They normally have tasters of the chocolate which pretty much entices you to buy a bar. You can walk across the way and enjoy the coffee on a nice patch of grass.

Name: Ritual Coffee Roasters
Author: Dhruv Patel
Review: Tiny, shed-size coffee shop at Patricia’s Green. Plenty of outdoor seating. The Brazilian single-origin they had on drip was acidic and a somewhat earthy with a dry aftertaste. Raspberry was the prominent flavor. It was served piping hot so be careful! The baristas were super friendly too. I wasn’t blown away by the coffee, but this shop is worth a shot if that’s the flavor profile you like.

Name: 3 Bees Coffee
Author: John Hill
Review:
Fantastic latte prepared and served up by the owner. Loved the laid back vibe of the place. Life slowed down when I went in. I was not rushed to order, the owner was not rushed in preparing my coffee, and I took time to indulge in every amazing sip amongst other laid back patrons.

Also, great downtown location. I was visiting, but I wish I lived in San Mateo so I could frequent this place regularly. If you find yourself there don't miss it. If you are commuting through, jump off the train and walk over. Definitely worth it!

Name: 3 Bees Coffee
Author: Cecilia bode
Review: Such a cute local caffe! I’ve gone in and ordered DoorDash from them before and was never disappointed! I always look forwards to going in when I have time in the morning to chat over coffee with my girls, or to grab a cup on my way to work! Don’t go to Starbucks when this is just a couple streets away!!!

Name: 3 Bees Coffee
Author: Nate Ulsh
Review:
Came for an iced mocha in the morning and enjoyed every sip of it. Staff was amicable and quick service. The al fresco (outdoor seating) is a great touch and adds to the vibe (B street recently closed for pedestrian mall activity).

The ambiance is fantastic inside and out. Paid street parking nearby.

Name: 3 Bees Coffee
Author: Aishwarya nandapurkar
Review: Good coffee and great service. Had their Mocha and caramel machhiato. Both were decent. They have a good variety of bakery items too. Cute street side coffee shop!

Name: 3 Bees Coffee
Author: Donny Pusawong
Review: Cozy mom and pop coffee shop in San Mateo.  Ordered a shot of espresso and a delicious latte.  They also sell lots of breakfast items and other goodies.  Reasonable prices and great service.  Outdoor seating if it's not too cold for you.

Name: Timeless Coffee
Author: Edison Huang
Review: We don't really seek out vegan coffee; oat milk just isn't our thing... They do offer a few different selections like almond and soy milk though! That being said, the cappuccino wasn't bad. It's a little pricey but what isn't in the Bay Area these days lol.

Name: Timeless Coffee
Author: Jesse Nichols
Review: I never order oat milk (I'm not even vegetarian), but their oat milk cappuccino was stellar. They roast their own beans in an excellent third wave style. Plus their desserts were great. I had a raspberry cheesecake that had a gluten free crust and it was sooooo good. Loved this place!

Name: Timeless Coffee
Author: Rachael Kemper
Review:
I visited Timeless Coffee in the Jack London District of Oakland today and left incredibly disappointed. After ordering and paying for my coffee, I was informed that they are a vegan shop and don’t offer any dairy options like half-and-half, milk, or cream. The only alternatives were almond milk (which I’m allergic to) and oat milk (which upsets my stomach).

This lack of options was a huge letdown, especially since I needed a good iced coffee before an important meeting. Unfortunately, I had to leave empty-handed because I can't drink coffee black. To make matters worse, there were no signs or notifications about this policy anywhere in the shop. I was hoping to shop local vs. just going to Starbucks...

I understand that businesses have their own philosophies, but some clear communication about the lack of traditional coffee add-ins would have been appreciated. I won’t be returning, and I hope they consider adding more inclusive options or at least making their policy more transparent to avoid frustrating future customers.

Name: Timeless Coffee
Author: Lizzy
Review: Excellent food and coffee as always. I love to stop by here before meeting up with people in the area and bring a bunch of great vegan pastries and treats. Loved by omnivores and vegans alike.

Name: Timeless Coffee
Author: Stanley Ipkuss
Review:
I found out about this spot because some independent baker in SF sent all of their followers to cyber bully this business’s Instagram page.  Before that I had no clue there was an all-vegan bakery & coffee shop in Oakland; had to come check it out! Been here a couple times, the workers are nice & welcoming, the spot is pretty chill, nice location.  I really enjoyed the drip coffee! Definitely some of the best I’ve had in this area, however this spot is more expensive than the others.  It’s located inside of a new pop-up gentri-apartment (like literally built last year 😒) so maybe the rent there is high? I tend to dislike plant-based businesses that charge a lot, just seems kind of unfair on a socioeconomic level but hey, it’s 2022 Oakland, what do you expect… My favorite item here is the butter croissant, taste amazing & the texture is spot on! Only lame thing is it sells out before 10am so you have to be an early bird vegan to enjoy it; I’ve only had the honor of trying it once.  Wish they made more of this product or had it readily available for vegans that wake up past 9am, we do exist. Also tried the key lime pie; phenomenal! 🔥 Will make this a 5 star review if I can get a croissant at 11am.

EDIT: 5 stars! Turns out the croissant availability there is random.

Name: MY Coffee Roastery
Address: 2080 Martin Luther King Jr Way, Berkeley
Author: Patrick Min
Review: The orange blossom latte proved to be a delightful concoction, seamlessly blending the richness of a creamy latte with the refreshing kick of an espresso shot, complemented by the subtle infusion of orange flavor. The service provided by the barista was entirely satisfactory, and the establishment's ambiance exuded a distinctive charm, setting it apart from the typical modern and trendy coffee shops.

Name: MY Coffee Roastery
Address: 2080 Martin Luther King Jr Way, Berkeley
Author: Luke Schmidt
Review: Their drip coffee didn't drip on me. It must be difficult for drip coffee to sit still in a cup. It was made to drip. I order drip coffee in hopes that when it gets to me, it's dripping days are over, but maybe that's naive. Once a drip coffee, always a drip coffee. That's why whenever my drip coffee starts dripping on my finger when I'm driving, I try not to get upset. I try to remember that I wouldn't have this drip coffee in the first place if it weren't for its dripping ways. Luckily, the drip coffee I got from here didn't give me any unwanted drips. Would definitely come here again.

Name: MY Coffee Roastery
Address: 2080 Martin Luther King Jr Way, Berkeley
Author: Jeff Gowan
Review:
This is a cute little shop with really good coffee. I've enjoyed my visits here. I was a bit surprised when I realized the beans I bought to bring home were only 8 ounces, for $22. That's on me for assuming I was getting a 12oz bag, which is still a bit much.
That said, their service and coffee have been very good when I've enjoyed them on site.

Name: MY Coffee Roastery
Address: 2080 Martin Luther King Jr Way, Berkeley
Author: mahima agarwal
Review: Orange blossom coffee is the best thing here 🙌  Flavor is more prominent in the cold one so liked it better. Cozy cafe and baristas are super nice and friendly. This place gets little crowded with a long queue so finding a place to sit can be difficult sometimes. Parking is also not easy to find in the neighborhood, there are superstore parkings nearby you can park for shorter durations there.

Name: MY Coffee Roastery
Address: 2080 Martin Luther King Jr Way, Berkeley
Author: A Snow
Review: Love this little coffee shop! They were all so kind and the coffee was so good. We got a vanilla latte and cold brew.

Name: Bicycle Coffee
Author: Mahsa Rouhani
Review: The customer service was top-notch. The staff were incredibly knowledgeable and the lady with the purple hair was super nice and friendly. I've tried countless coffees in the Bay Area, but their Espresso Blend is hands-down one of the best I've ever tasted. The quality is exceptional, and the flavor is rich and satisfying. I've already bought a bag to enjoy at home, and I can't wait to come back for more. If you're looking for premium coffee beans to make your own espresso at home, their Espresso Blend is a must-try.

Name: Bicycle Coffee
Author: Oleg Vakhitov
Review: Bicycle Coffee is one of my favorite coffee shops - it has a simple interior that is combined with a roasting shop. You can see for yourself how the magic of roasting coffee happens. Good baristas who know how to make delicious coffee work here. And Espresso coffee beans will allow you to make great coffee in your kitchen in your espresso machine.

Name: Bicycle Coffee
Author: cece art
Review:
The cashier wrote a profanity on my drink and I don’t feel safe drinking this.
This is the second time this cashier has done something sketchy to my food. Unprompted.

Name: Bicycle Coffee
Author: Matthew MacCormack
Review:
My go-to coffee shop in the Jack London area. Great espresso drinks, cold brew, and pastries. Big fan of the vanilla oat milk latte and the ham & gruyere croissant. The new location is nice and modern, with plenty of seating. I love the menu board with visualizations of each drink option.

Recently had a great customer service experience as well. I'd bought a pound of ground espresso to use at home. When I got home, I found that it had been ground on the wrong setting. I returned to see if I could get it re-ground; not only did they give me a new pound (correctly ground) for free, but they also let me keep the old pound and gave me a free cold brew for my troubles. Shout out Jackie for the great customer service!

Name: Bicycle Coffee
Author: Crystal
Review:
12/2021 UPDATE 🚴🏽‍♀️
Still my go to coffee shop. I don’t need points or rewards to compromise where I go for coffee. I come so frequent I could work here. I’ve purchased multiple times, from coffee, beans, and merchandise. Even come on the weekends when I can. The owners, staff, customers all know quality.

For a month at my new job I always thought this was a bicycle shop until I walked over and saw it was a coffee shop that delivers via bicycle! How rad is that?

The perks: EVERYTHING

Service = A1
User friendly menu = words with pictures, fool proof
Milk options = no added cost!
Coffee = delicious

Happy camper, right here.

Name: Caffe Central
Author: Jemellee Manaoat
Review:
There’s a variety of breakfast items and snacks. They also have different types of sandwiches. When we came in we just wanted coffee and snacks. So we picked up a couple different cookies. I got the Sweet Sydney’s chocolate chip and Ginger Snap and cafe centrals own chocolate chip cookie. They were soft and chewy like how I like it. The coffee was nice and strong.

Service was fast and friendly. You could sit in and enjoy your treats or you can just order to go.

Ambiance, it’s a cafe: I like their light fixtures. So, there’s a counter where you order and a few tables. Nothing fancy. If you’re in the city and need a quick grab and go come here.

Name: Caffe Central
Author: Playmaker88x
Review: This place rocks! My wife and I came in here for breakfast around 9:30am on a Wednesday and it was very busy. We waited in line about 5-6 minutes to order at the counter then paid and took our seat. The coffees came to our table first, and then our food. Very efficient and timely service. We had a breakfast croissant, iced mocha, central latte, and 2 well shot shots, called “gut check”. All items were fresh and absolutely delicious! Great place in San Fran

Name: Caffe Central
Author: J T
Review:
This small but mighty cafe has many terrific breakfast options with lots of types of hot and cold coffees. I highly recommend the Lox Bagel. It's big, crunchy, and loaded with smoked salmon, avocado, and more. It also comes with a side of fresh fruit. The Gibraltar coffee was nice and strong. And the chocolate hazelnut croissant and cheese Danish were delicious.

There's a restroom at the end of the seating area. You can sit indoor or outdoor, but it was packed when I got there. I ordered to-go, and the coffee and food came out in just a few minutes.

It's not far from Union Square, so having this good breakfast with the beautiful weather outside made it a very enjoyable morning.

Name: Caffe Central
Author: Ivan Baradi
Review:
I decided to come here to eat since I’m hungry. I’m quite surprised that they have a good location despite the one at Westfield Valley Fair Mall since that was just a food vendor at a food court. I like how colorful the menu is.

I honestly thought that this is just a regular cafe, but it’s more than that. It’s the way that they prepared the food as if it’s a five-star restaurant. Food is really good and also like how they display the food from the display bars.

It’s definitely one of the go to places if you want to stop by and eat if you’re hungry. Highly recommended to try out this place!

Name: Caffe Central
Author: Isaiah Jackson
Review:
Really great, hearty breakfast and healthy choices. Omelettes, sandwiches, toasts, pancakes, etc. - there is something for everyone. They have juices and smoothies too. Or if you need baked goods, they have a great selection as well.

The line is long but service is incredible. They have a great thing going here.

Name: Matching Half
Author: Blake Amedeo
Review:
Matching Half is a classic SF coffeehouse with layers of history, excellent service, and delicious drinks & food.

Definitely stop by for a good vibe—it’s more than a shop, it’s a community space.

Name: Matching Half
Author: Bree Reeves
Review:
Great coffee shop. Every time I’m in SF for work I make sure to stop by.
The barista(s) made a point to make conversation with their regulars and even knew their drink orders by heart. Great atmosphere.

Name: Matching Half
Author: Shreya Dixit
Review: This is a lovely cafe! The lattes have cute designs and the baristas are super warm, friendly and patient! I love sitting here and working! This place has both outlets and wifi! But most of all, I feel cozy and happy when I’m in the space.

Name: Matching Half
Author: Matthew Ye
Review:
I've been here a few times now since I live nearby, and each time the baristas have felt curt and a bit arrogant - I guess it must be a job requirement.

The coffee takes forever to come out and is average at best.

The shop has ample indoor and outdoor seating and is well-decorated but I'd rather take my business elsewhere.

Name: Matching Half
Author: Jenny Wang
Review: The vibes and aesthetics of this cafe makes me so happy. Ordered a breakfast egg sandwich to go. It was delicious. There’s lovely outdoor seating and people on laptops inside. Favorite cafe in the neighborhood! And closest cafe with matcha on the menu.

Name: U Cha
Author: Dharma M
Review: Found this while we were at Berkeley. It is at the corner of the street. Parking could be a challenge. Overall, the drinks were good. Boba is good. Service is a bit slow.

Name: U Cha
Author: Cordelia Frykman
Review:
The staff was nice but the boba was flat and bland. I only came here once because I was so disappointed. I got a brown sugar milk tea that basically tasted like sweetened milk, no rich brown sugar flavor or bitter tea notes. The boba pearls were forgettable.
Don’t recommend especially with the number of other boba places nearby.

Name: U Cha
Author: Elaine Wang
Review: Got the Bomberman 50%, I usually get 25% at milk tea places but saw someone recommend 50. I think next time I could be ok with 25. If you like milk you will like this drink.

Name: U Cha
Author: Photos Are Forever
Review: We dropped our new boba and she gave us a brand new one and was very nice about it. The boba is also delicious!

Name: U Cha
Author: Max Song
Review: Decided to go here on a nice summer walk. I was plesently surprised that this store was not your average boba place. Great customer service, I asked for a recommendation they recommend the rose peach tea which was amazing. My friends got the Okinawa tea and passion fruit green tea which were also amazing. Love this place, will definitely be back for more.

Name: Gong Cha
Author: Kerwin McKenzie
Review: They were good. But the kiosk takes away from customer interaction. There's no talking between the server and the customers, except to call your number. Atmosphere was much better when they didn't have the kiosk.

Name: Gong Cha
Author: Daning Lu
Review: ignored my take out order and let three orders cut in before me. Wont come back anymore. I cannot believe I even tipped 20%.

Name: Gong Cha
Author: Natasha Kanhai
Review: Pretty cute place, bright and clean.  Big beautiful kiosks to oder from, but I assume you can order at the counter as well.  The employees making the drinks had their hands full though.   Lots of variety from Boba,  fruit mixes and tea with other additions.  My first time here but other family members have been. We had matcha with pudding, 1 in a cute bottle.   We came for the bottle!  Isn't s/he cute!

Name: Gong Cha
Author: David Brotman
Review: Most of the other milk tea restaurants close to Union Square had already closed for the evening and this place just happened to be across the street from the hotel I was staying at. The space inside Gong Cha is really small so some people were waiting outside and some inside. I ordered a simple milk tea with boba from one of the touchscreen kiosks and waited next to a staircase where I had a good view of the staff preparing the orders. I waited, and waited and waited some more as the staff called out the orders ahead of me. I watched as the female staff poured out the milk mixtures from nearly empty jugs. After waiting nearly a half hour, she finally got to my order. The milk tea tasted ok but not worth waiting a half hour for.

Name: Gong Cha
Author: FrauAM M
Review: Try pumpkin milk tea for first time. Nice!

Name: Plentea
Author: Nina Timofeyeva
Review: Cool "plant" milk tea offerings. Variety of flavors of drinks made with quality ingredients. Teas have deep flavor and boba is fresh. They offer drinks in glass containers which you can re-use when you bring them back to the shop.

Name: Plentea
Author: LeiLani Bertram
Review: Fantastic! The service was wonderful these ladies are so nice and polite.  Drinks are fresh and hit the spot.  There is room to sit and sip or of course to just take on the go as well.

Name: Plentea
Author: Edison Huang
Review:
One of my go to's whenever I'm in the area! Highly recommend their grape fruit tea with sea salt crema (blueberry is my second choice)! The tea flavors here are better than the other boba shops. I still opt for 0% sugar, but y'all may want to go a little bit higher.

Sustainability is one of their core values. They offer glass bottles at the same price. You can get 10% off your drink if you reuse it!

Name: Plentea
Author: James
Review:
Hands-down, one of the best places to get milk tea in San Frnacisco, especailly for their sustainability.

All their teas come in reusable glass bottles that you can keep and compared to other ilk tea places, the prices are about the same if not sometimes cheaper.

You can order at the small tablet/kiosk or with one of the workers.

There is also a lovely dining space upstairs and a few chairs downstairs.

Only downside is that they use plastic straws.

Name: Plentea
Author: ayush kalani
Review:
Ideally, I'd give it a 2.5/5.

Plentea has a charming atmosphere. The shop itself is small but inviting, with terrariums lining the walls, adding a touch of nature. They offer a wide variety of drinks, from classic milk teas to fresh fruit options and even smoothies. You can also choose between getting your drink in a reusable glass bottle or a standard plastic cup, which is a nice eco-friendly touch.

Unfortunately, my experience with the actual drink fell a bit short. I opted for the Thai tea, a classic boba flavor. While the presentation was nice, the taste was a little disappointing. The tea itself was quite bitter, and there was a slightly chalky aftertaste.

Overall, Plentea is a cute spot with good intentions.  However, based on my experience with Thai tea, the flavor execution needs some work. If you're looking for a trendy boba shop with a wide variety of options, Plentea might be worth a try. But if you're a boba purist seeking the perfect cup, you might want to keep exploring.

Name: WanPo Tea Shop
Author: Julie Taeko
Review: Excellent drinks but slightly pricier. Great ambiance and fun little store with tons of anime and tea aroma. Small and no place to sit but cute. I had the brown sugar boba milk.

Name: WanPo Tea Shop
Author: Johnny Chan
Review: One of my top go to spots for milk tea, sugar cane, mango pomelo, and their jelly toppings in the Bay. Quality tea and owner provided great service. The drinks are consistent and similar to what I had in Taiwan. I’d recommend to anyone looking for Taiwanese quality tea

Name: WanPo Tea Shop
Author: miketea
Review: Only Wanpo around. Come here many many many times. Always good quality! I usually get tea with 30% sugar level, because it's the best taste and favor compared to others. Once a while I will drink the milk tea.

Name: WanPo Tea Shop
Author: Nico De Osso
Review: it was so delicious, cute little pop in shop with authentic flavors! I got the brown sugar and matcha tea! Would love to try something else next time!

Name: WanPo Tea Shop
Author: Steph Wong
Review:
Recently discovered WanPo was in my area. I was randomly browsing online for a new boba spot that I haven't tried and this one popped up.  I was pretty excited to see that it was close to me and decided to head over.

There is a kiosk that you can order from on the side, but there's also the register where you can order from as well. The menu is pretty extensive so I would definitely recommend looking up what you want to order before you get there. Ordering is quick and painless you just use the kiosk and pay by card.

The decor inside is super cute. There's tons of figurines and posters of different anime. Inside is not the biggest, so if they get a bunch of orders you are able to wait outside. My drink was ready in less than 5 minutes.

I got matcha with boba and definitely liked the flavor.  The milk was not too overpowering and you could definitely taste the matcha flavor. The boba itself was not too chewy.

There is a parking lot, so you should have no trouble finding spaces.

Name: Boba Guys Fillmore
Author: Rose Garcia
Review: So good! I always drop by boba guys when I'm nearby. They have the best strawberry matcha. It does get busy here, though (but the wait is worth it).

Name: Boba Guys Fillmore
Author: S T
Review: They must have the worst cups. The lid simply doesn't stay on and we have had 2 incidents of lid popping out. It also seems like the stuff don't read special instructions like no ice, less sweet and extra topping. We have bought 10+ drinks from here and half of the drinks were made wrong. Last time we were here the bobas were undercooked and inedible, very disappointing.

Name: Boba Guys Fillmore
Author: Hannah R
Review: We had a great experience at this Boba Guys location! We arrived right when they opened and had to wait a little bit to order boba because it wasn’t ready, but the employees were so nice. Iris was especially helpful in making us feel welcome. Thanks guys for a great experience!

Name: Boba Guys Fillmore
Author: Christine
Review: This review is for the Boba Guys Catering.  We hired Boba Guys to provide a "boba bar" for two hours during a wedding celebration, and the experience was very positive.  The communication with the catering team was prompt; the delivery and presentation of the drinks were also excellent.  The drinks themselves were, of course, delicious, as always.  The guests were delighted to have an afternoon refreshment with the boba.  We would definitely hire them again in the future if the need arises.  Highly recommend!

Name: Boba Guys Fillmore
Author: Young Kim
Review: Small boba place, ordered lychee olong tea and was refreshing but little too sweet for my taste. The taste of sesame cookie was unique and actually it was delicious! Nice quiet atmosphere and friendly staff. Quick service!

Name: Mr. Green Bubble | Oakland
Author: James
Review:
This Mr. Green Bubble location offers exquisite service and has a lovely interior with plenty of dining tables. You can order up front or at one of the Snackpass kiosks.

They offer specialty drinks as well as good milk tea (although I don't recommend their Earl Grey, which is one of two decaf options).

Prices are fair and quality is superb.

Name: Mr. Green Bubble | Oakland
Author: Miles Rowley
Review: Mr. Green Bubble is my go-to boba place! The milk teas and food are delicious. I will definitely be coming here again very soon!!

Name: Mr. Green Bubble | Oakland
Author: J L
Review:
Ordered for delivery.  We wanted to celebrate the end of the school week and the beginning of the Presidents' holiday week with a nice treat for the kids.  We ordered from this new place (to us) and truly enjoyed the meal.  We got some popcorn chicken,  deep fried tofu,  egg Puff pancake,  Thai iced tea with boba, popping boba, and lychee bits, and lastly an oreo milk tea drink.  The tofu was really good and well seasoned and fried.  The popcorn chicken was well seasoned and fried too and the size of the nuggets were good for a nice mouthful of flavorful chicken.  The Thai iced tea with all the add-ons was tasty but too sweet, but my son loved it.  The oreo milk tea was a bit bland and not sweet at all. The egg Puff pancake was tasty  and had heart shaped puffs. I didn't get a chance to take a photo of the egg Puff cuz everyone ate it all before I could take a shot at it.  🥲😒😆😅

Overall, this place is pretty good and next time I'm in the neighborhood I may just stop by and try out the food fresh and not delivered.

Name: Mr. Green Bubble | Oakland
Author: Andres M
Review:
At first glance, you might believe it's a typical boba drink pastry shop. Small space, simple design, green letter hinting of a potential vegan-esque feel and approach.

I took a look at the menu. And it was quite surprising at the variety of snacks and drinks they offered . I was in the mood for popcorn chicken , and to my surprise they had some of the best popcorn chicken within walking distance of where I reside.

There a popcorn chicken was exceptional and they have a variety of options in terms of the way you want it cooked and things you might want to add or avoid. Chicken fully cooked.Do stress if you want your popcorn chicken extra crispy as that is the only thing I would have improved

I also ordered the lemonade and strawberry lemonade and the packaging they come in ( meaning the cup) is environmentally friendly and also makes it easy to share amongst each other in your group. The drink could use less sugar as it was a bit over sweet.

The online ordering process was quick and simple, and how's your order track live with updates and approximate time for you to pick up your order.  Parking is limited to the main street. Indoor lounge holds approximately 3 to 4 tables. No matter your budget there's always a great value for you with great food waiting on the other end.

The place was clean will it was warm and inviting, the customer service was quick, friendly and helpful.

I overlooked this place all the time while walking down Piedmont. It just became one of my go-to places

Name: Mr. Green Bubble | Oakland
Author: Meichi Chan
Review:
I love their tofu and mix mushroom.the best ever.
Dark roast oolong tea, pink lady, grapefruit paradise and fruity tea are my go to drinks.

Seating area nice and cozy, good to hangout with friends.

Name: Brew Cha
Author: Alice Garner
Review: Really great experience here! Super fast. Ordered the mango passion fruit slushy, love that you can mix and match flavors! They have games and a nice couch and it’s in a super convenient location! Nice friendly staff. REALLY YUMMY BOBA! Would 100% go back again.

Name: Brew Cha
Author: cravesinthebay
Review: Brewcha SF 💌 one of the best freshly brewed boba tea spot in SF 😍! Their drinks were super fresh & tasty. I recommend getting the Black Sugar Creme Brulee Latte ❣️ which I felt in love with!

Name: Brew Cha
Author: Vee Dinh
Review:
Discovered a milk tea spot with light flavored drinks. Follow me!

Highlights of the menu:
1. Tiramisu milk tea
2. Lavender matcha latte
3. Panda milk tea
4. Roasted oolong tea with cheese cream top

Name: Brew Cha
Author: Flor Fernandez
Review: FRESH, organic, has quite a few milk options (even lactose free) and a large selection of drinks!  I loved that they used real taro for their drink. Friendly staff. Worth it!

Name: Brew Cha
Author: xo_julienna
Review: It’s my first time going there. My brother in law frequently goes to Brew Cha for boba drinks. See photos I took that gives you a full picture. My favorite drink is hot black sesame latte and it is so delicious!

Name: Teaspoon San Mateo
Author: Sharadha Ramaswamy
Review: Our friends in Bay area suggested this place and it’s absolutely worth visiting. This place has the best mango bubble tea. It was so refreshing and felt like summer on a tall cup. The caramel bubble tea was average when compared to mango flavour. The service was quick and the ambience was decent. I would recommend the silky mango bubble tea here.

Name: Teaspoon San Mateo
Author: Murillo Pontual
Review: Great boba tea at San Mateo. Staff is very polite and the store is clean. I highly recommend it.

Name: Teaspoon San Mateo
Author: Ahmad Lutfi
Review:
Tried their mango on fire smoothie:

First you've got the sweetness of mango, until you find the kick of red spice, but it's yet finished until you find salt, that's the last kicker !, it made my tongue dance from excitement!

Name: Teaspoon San Mateo
Author: Fire Hazard
Review: This is an ok spot to grab a quick boba. The drinks are a lot sweeter than what I normally would expect from other boba places so you might want to ask them to reduce the sugar. The biggest draw back was the straw. It is not plastic and I understand why they would want to help the environment by finding an alternative. What they are currently using is not the answer though. Both my wife and I went through more than one straw while finishing our drinks and if you aren’t really careful they will crack. If you are going to use a plastic cup with a plastic lid you might as well use a plastic straw. Also they no longer serve shaved ice at this location.

Name: Teaspoon San Mateo
Author: Venu Javarappa
Review:
Superb bakery. Sublime cakes. Fresh bread. Fresh hong kong milk tea.

Sublime egg tarts. Ahh, the pork buns.

Wonderful service. Clean store. Brightly lit. Great to sit and enjoy a cafe atmosphere with fresh smells and a trendy vibe.

Right in downtown san mateo with pretty easy parking. Great addition to downtown san mateo.

Name: Milk Tea Lab
Address: 630 El Camino Real, South San Francisco
Author: Baby Panda
Review:
I wish I could give an even higher rating! I 100% recommend going here and only here for your boba! Not only are the drinks super delicious but also high quality, amazing choices and great prices! We refuse to go anywhere else for our boba and come at least once a week! 💜 My kids and family love this location because the owner and the employees are so sweet and friendly! They are the BEST!!

Remember to tip them, everyone works hard to make your drinks and it's well deserved! 🥹💖

PS, the drinks here are Thai tea with regular boba (tapioca pearls), mango slushies with mango popping boba and the big with mango stars and leachie jelly and a Green tea with lychee jelly and Crystal boba (aka cool boba) and I'm not sure what else but its my new favorite!
Strawberry raspberry is okay ..
Honeydew is good but get it 50% sweet only 😅
I'll add more pictures!

I also love their turo milk tea, Thai tea, honey roasted tea and matcha green tea milk with tiny boba! Matcha with the tiny boba is my #1 favorite!!

Name: Milk Tea Lab
Address: 630 El Camino Real, South San Francisco
Author: Emy Suhaimi
Review:
This is my absolute favorite boba place in all of the Bay. While I tend to stick to my personal favorites, (Wintermelon Milk Tea & Jasmine Milk Tea) I truly believe that their selection of drinks will always be of high quality. The menu options aren’t as widespread as other milk tea lab locations, so that is something to keep note of but still a very good selection to choose from. It is rare that I take time to rate a restaurant/business but I can’t not share my opinion on this store!!! It is my favorite boba place and I’m always looking forward to my next boba drink from here

+ the owner of this location is always so sweet, personable, friendly and attentive.

Name: Milk Tea Lab
Address: 630 El Camino Real, South San Francisco
Author: M Antonio C
Review:
This location is nice with pleasant staff.  We were in the area, and so this place is our  go-to location when south of the City.

We ordered 4 drinks and had no issues with quality or service.   We like it here.

Name: Milk Tea Lab
Address: 630 El Camino Real, South San Francisco
Author: 00 SVN
Review: Love this place. Honestly have worked my way through the menu! Mango lovers frozen drink is a go to! They are generous with their toppings & the prices are super fair for these huge boba teas. Smoothies to tea to tonics, all very satisfying. The green tea isn’t too strong on the tea flavor. Go here at least twice a week. Amazing efficient, fast, and friendly staff! Super clean place. Joanne is great! (I don’t recommend the Thai tea is my only negative.)  More flavors in store than the online menu too. Definitely recommend!

Name: Milk Tea Lab
Address: 630 El Camino Real, South San Francisco
Author: Tasnim Ara Islam
Review: They have a variety of choices for drinks. I ordered a regular mango milk tea. The drink was perfectly made and tasted rellay great. Another interesting thing of this shop is the their interior design. Very unique. And the price is absolutely reasonable.

Name: iTea
Author: Kim Chan
Review:
I can't recall any other boba places in SF having tofuhua as a topping so I usually get a drink from here when I'm in the area.

Writing this review to remind myself to get my drinks at 25% sweetness.

FYI any card payments for orders under $15 get charged $0.50 extra

Name: iTea
Author: Adriana Perez
Review:
After finishing a delightful dinner at a nearby restaurant, we decided to satisfy our sweet tooth with some fun drinks for dessert and it was an excellent decision!

The variety of flavors and toppings was impressive, catering to both traditional and adventurous palates. I opted for 100% sweetness, to my watermelon, sago, and it was refreshing.

There was no seating, it was definitely a grab and go Location .

Name: iTea
Author: Michael
Review: Straight-forward no frills bubble tea shop with a lot of variety and good taste. They don’t even have any seating, just a small space to order when you walk in. They seem well staffed, so the orders come out pretty fast. They have good prices, lower than some of the other bbt shops around but they do have a credit card transaction fee though!

Name: iTea
Author: Mithun Manivannan
Review: The boba was good, we went on a really hot day so it was packed. They had many people in line and got orders confused a couple times. I think they were under staffed but they handled the situation fine. My classic boba was good, and their tapioca pearls were soft.

Name: iTea
Author: A Fashionable Condition
Review: The only reason I am shorting this place a star is because they did not have the Thai tea I ordered but I think that's because we came in so late so probably not their fault. otherwise one of the many, many holes in the wall in the sunset District that you should stick your head into if you have the chance and are looking for some boba, milk tea or Shave Ice. Very friendly service  and  one of the places  open  late  on  Irving past 19th Avenue. Another one of the places I always suspect are laundering operations  because  they "prefer* cash  and the machines are down that take cards  a little too often  but I can assure you they are not the only place  where this  is  on the Reg. And it always looks like it's chronically staffed by someone's cousin's friend. get there early if you want the Thai iced tea! ♥️♥️♥️♥️

Name: Qualitea (San Francisco Location)
Author: Alexis Aschenbrenner
Review:
Rose petal tea is so good here! Also a beautiful pink color 🩷 complete with actual rose petals!
I also love the caffeine free sparklers and the jasmine green tea. Hands down my favorite boba in the city!

Name: Qualitea (San Francisco Location)
Author: Zamir Mamytov
Review:
I never thought I'd find myself writing a review for a milk tea shop, as most are pretty standard. However, this place is an absolute gem!

From the moment you walk in, you're greeted with warmth and hospitality. The staff is incredibly friendly and eager to assist you. As a nice touch, they offer sample milk teas upon entry, and even have treats for our furry friends! The drinks themselves are phenomenal—fresh, clean, and bursting with flavor.

The ambiance is equally delightful, with real flowers adorning the center table and warm, wooden walls that create a cozy atmosphere. It's the kind of place that makes me nostalgic for my time living in SF!

I tried the matcha blossom, and it exceeded all expectations. It's not only delicious but also visually stunning. If you're ever indecisive about which drink to choose, I highly recommend trying this one.

Overall, the service is exceptional, and the vibes are fantastic. It's definitely worth a visit!

Name: Qualitea (San Francisco Location)
Author: Sean
Review:
Between 4 pm and 8 pm they have happy hour it seems, so with boba and tax their house milk tea is exactly $5.00. They give a lot of tea and boba too; I got quite full part way. The owner is very nice

The closest bathroom which is in the laundromat next door does not have a sink.

Name: Qualitea (San Francisco Location)
Author: Becky Lin
Review: Super fast, nice customer service who explained the drink as he gave it to me and really tasty, will be back!! (Got the rose milk tea and it was perfect sweetness at 30% and not soapy)

Name: Qualitea (San Francisco Location)
Author: Seanan Fong
Review: My favorite boba spot in the city. The bobas are always super fresh, warm from being freshly cooked. And they always accommodate my no sugar no ice order. Also I find the folks are super friendly, and the vibe is chill with board games and a mixed crowd of folks.

Name: Teaspoon
Author: felicie diaz
Review:
ABSOLUTE BANGER SO SO GOOD!!
Got the rose milk tea and the caramel cream boba with Oreo and it was divine so good.
Will be coming back
New obsession

Name: Teaspoon
Author: Julie Taeko
Review: I tried the watermelon juice that was absolutely delicious, and customized to less sweet. And it had delicious little bits of crunchy seeds at the bottom. My friend got one of the snow ice desserts which was also quite delicious. And the service was fantastic!

Name: Teaspoon
Author: Sher Zhang
Review:
Favorite bubble tea in the area!
I’ve visited 5 times already in the last month… my boba cravings are adequately satisfied here :)

Creamy Jasmine and and grasshopper are my two top drinks so far, depending on if I’m in a milk tea or fruity drink mood.

Liquid gold is a solid drink if you like the roasted flavour, and caramel cream is decadent and sweet (although I wish there was a milk tea version, as this one only contains milk)

Name: Teaspoon
Author: Mitchell Robinson
Review: Everything is good, drinks, baked goods, teas, service, everything. The music could be cranked down just a little bit though. It’s quite loud in my opinion. But good place good stuff, nonetheless.

Name: Teaspoon
Author: Edison Huang
Review:
The kiosk may need to be updated. We ordered the lychee on lychee drink which has lychee jelly included.. the kiosk also mentioned that... but the next option is to add topping so we chose no topping because we don't want to add any additional topping.. well the drink ended up having no topping whatsoever. Pretty much paid for a full drink only to lose out on the topping.
