The pear-adise drink isn't bad though. I recommend not going 0% sweet on that one.

Name: Sharetea
Author: Tommy Nguyen
Review: Not my cup of tea. Drink looks boring. Drink are too sweet even though “No Sugar” was chosen. No fruit in the drink even though it’s call fruit tea.

Name: Sharetea
Author: Matt B
Review:
Half decent boba tea spot in the lobby of the Metreon building. A fine place to grab a drink before or after a movie, but certainly nothing to write home about.

½ sweetness was the right amount of sweetness.

Name: Sharetea
Author: Sean Nguyen
Review:
I love going to the city to watch Wrestling and I have been seen @shareteametreon on my Instagram feed and I wanted to check it out.  I got iced blended coffee with creama and ice cream. It was so sweet and amazing. The ice cream on top is so elevating. I love how the fresh fruit tasted in the Peach tea with kiwi fruit. I love a good mojito and the lime Mojito did not disappoint. It was sweet and tangy, which was perfect for me. The last drink I got was the Matcha red bean milk tea with mini pearls and it was so good. I usually enjoy red beans in Vietnamese Che but I been getting it in Boba and it so good. I can understand why this drink is so popular! All in all everything was great and I would be coming back to try more drinks from  @shareteametreon .

📍ShareTea in the Metreon in San Francisco, CA.

Name: Sharetea
Author: A A
Review: This is a 3.5 star rating. More sweet has the same sweetness as extremely sweet, and less sweet. The Happy Family Large, came to $6.00 with tax, tip, and mandate, and was a sour combination of big pearls, small pearls, hard lychee cubes, soggy red beans, and.....the kitchen sink. The actual milk tea was as bland, and forgettable as the local Evening News. Yet, there was, and always seems to be a line, due to the perfect location of being on the way to AMC movie theater, at the food court. There's so many better bubble tea stores out there, I would skip Sharetea. $$$ out of $$$$$. No dedicated bathroom. Garage parking nearby. I would not recommend Sharetea.

Name: Sharetea
Author: Benny Avalos
Review:
I wish that pricess were cheaper since half the labor is automated. I hope the operation cost trickle down to higher hourly rates for workers.

They were really lovely and nice

Name: Beach Boba
Author: Danielle M
Review: The service was pretty good but overall I dont understand how this place is in business. Worst boba I have ever had the brown sugar boba balls were a crunchy texture and my drink was disgusting.

Name: Beach Boba
Author: Tiffany powell
Review: What a great little shop. The milk tea with brown sugar and traditional boba was so good. The service was great and they were so friendly. They need to build one up in Idaho we have none of these there and I would love one lol. Thanks for the great drinks.

Name: Beach Boba
Author: P
Review: This small boba place is great. Every time i come here, I end up coming out with a smile in my face. They have a lot of different options to choose from. The place is clean and staff is really friendly and helpful with any questions. I love the fact that they have free metal straws , and they give discounts if you use them. I will be coming more to this place and would definitely recommend it.

Name: Beach Boba
Author: Jesua Sarmiento
Review: very nice place, and nice man. love the selection of homemade boba and the bursting boba too.

Name: Beach Boba
Author: Paola Gutierrez
Review: The owner is very friendly and accommodating. He even gave me extras for my drink. This is the only boba shop that has a gazillion boba topping choices. I was surprised by how wide their variety is, and you won’t be disappointed with the taste. Definitely will go back!

Name: T4
Author: MissfitFL
Review: My taro smoothie with pudding was not any kind of sweet, full of ice chunks but it was a warm day, so it'll melt. This basil popcorn chicken was definitely delicious.

Name: T4
Author: Serena Ren
Review: Their foods is better than other places for me. I love their snacks and drinks so so much, especially their popcorn chicken, their tofu,  their calamari, more and more

Name: T4
Author: Zachary Borja
Review:
Let's start with the good, the taro smoothie is legit and great.

But the food, this Curry is disgusting. The meat is basically fatty leftovers from someone else's lunch. The smell was great but the taste and consistency was just revolting, like burned rubber.

This was all $12 btw.

Name: T4
Author: Sadie Sausage
Review: I cant believe other peeps rated this low. This is the best milktea i tried so dar coming here in America. Roasted oolong with cream cheese by the way. And their takoyaki too. It has little hint of crunch in it. Best ever! This will be my go-to now.

Name: T4
Author: liyah
Review: This tastes like toothpaste. Every boba place nearby is infinitely better.

Name: Inner Richmond- Little Sweet
Author: Ian Kidder
Review: Got the lemon with green tea, aloe, and agar and really liked it. 25% sugar was as expected and not too sweet. I wish the green tea had a slightly stronger flavor, but otherwise enjoyed it and the price was decent. Favorite spot near me so far

Name: Inner Richmond- Little Sweet
Author: Pratik Naik
Review:
About the Boba Tea Place:
This boba tea place is situated in the lively Inner Richmond area and boasts ample parking options, making it a convenient destination. The only quirk I noticed was their choice of songs, which didn't quite match my personal taste but seemed to appeal to others.

About the Drinks:
During my visit, I ordered a matcha milk tea, and it turned out to be a good choice. I opted for boba and brown sugar agar, both of which complemented the drink well. The brown sugar boba was a particularly enjoyable addition. One notable feature is that the drinks are non-dairy by default, but customization is available to suit individual preferences.

Overall, the boba tea place offers a pleasing experience, with a variety of options and customization available. For its quality beverages and convenient location, a four-star rating seems fitting.

Name: Inner Richmond- Little Sweet
Author: RJ
Review:
Absolutely loved this place for milk teas with boba and agar 🧋 We tried the creamy thai green milk and brown sugar milk tea. I tried Agar for the first time and loved it. Both the drinks were perfect as customized although a little pricey. The drinks are made with non dairy products by default but you can customize it. Definitely recommend this place for anyone in the area.

You have to place an order through their system/monitor in store or online and the drinks are made for you. There is little to no interaction with the staff. Service can be quick based on how crowded it is.

Name: Inner Richmond- Little Sweet
Author: Lingling
Review: Randomly picked this boba shop, and I really liked the watermelon strawberry w/ salt cream here—it tasted good, especially with the fresh fruits. The mix of salty and sweet was just right. I chose 25% sweetness, and it was a bit sweet for me. If you're not into sweet stuff, maybe go for a lower sweetness level or you might find it too sugary. But overall, it's a tasty treat with options for different tastes.

Name: Inner Richmond- Little Sweet
Author: Lilizhu Lee
Review:
I have always loved communication in any kind of way! And I think this is one of them :)) I usually ordered Iced Coffee from Little Sweet on my day off but I scrolled through the menu list today and I could not find it, so I left the note! And the staff kindly took her time to give me some response! So, I just want to shout out for this small little service that created my smile this morning!!! Thank you so much for making me good coffee every time and communicating with me. Your great service means so much :))

Updated my review here!!!

Anna and Angelina are the best!!! They both make a very good coffee every single time!!!! I love the old recipe for iced coffee and I feel the same way until now :) Shout out to both of you!!!! I am working at Philz Coffee but I love coming here on my off day because it is also nearby and love having sight glass coffee taking a little break from Philz a little!! And they both have made me impressed every time!!! Thank you

Name: Happy Lemon | Berkeley
Author: Sunny H
Review: The formula of classic milk tea is different from all other Happy Lemons that I've been to, and it taste weird. I don't know if it's the sugar or the tea that's different, but it's not how they make it in other franchise location.

Name: Happy Lemon | Berkeley
Author: aarzoo karki
Review: They are extraordinary, impressive, one of a kind in messing up people's orders. I don't know how they manage to do that every single time. That's a skill. I have ordered boba five times here and been introduced to creative ways to screw up orders every single time. Once they got my order right, but gave me a wrong straw (Made for juice.. Too small for boba). Messing up once or twice might be a fluke, but messing up every single time, that's sheer negligence. No hard feelings, but please do better. Thank you!

Name: Happy Lemon | Berkeley
Author: Samir Patel
Review: Barista Ina is a hidden gem at Berkeley’s most underrated boba spot. Always positive, fast, and makes drinks that taste great!

Name: Happy Lemon | Berkeley
Author: Mishu Pham
Review: I’ve gotten the same thing (matcha/mango smoothie) at various happy lemon locations but here it was off. It had little flavor and was disappointing considering it’s basically $8 if you get a topping. The shop itself was also run down compared to other locations I’ve been too.

Name: Happy Lemon | Berkeley
Author: jade
Review: i've been here 3 times now, and my usual is the strawberry shortcake. the first two times were perfect, but i went yesterday at around noon to get proven wrong. girl has the meanest look ever and barely interacted with me as if it was my fault she was working here. my drink didn't really taste of strawberry and was really icy instead of having its usual smooth consistency. maybe she was new, but i doubt it if she was left out alone to handle register and orders. if i come back and see her making drinks ever again i'm walking out asap

Name: Feng Cha Teahouse - Berkeley
Author: J L
Review:
I was excited to try a new snack place, and we odes a few things to try. Ordered for delivery popcorn chicken,  fried tofu,  curly fries,  summer melon drink, Thai iced tea, dirty Boba, regular classic Boba drink.

Let's start with the drinks.

1. Summer melon was okay but wasn't sweet enough. There were bits of watermelon floating in the drink and maybe some bitsof lemon.  Unfortunately, the watermelon wasn't ripe, and it tasted like they used the white part of the watermelon rind. 😕 The Boba tasted just okay,  it had enough chew but didn't have that honey sweet taste to it like other places make it.

2. Dirty Boba was mediocre with hardly any brown sugar taste. It tastes like just a regular milk tea drink.

3. Thai ice tea was okay.

4. Classic Boba was just okay... nothing really to write about other than it was a bit bland.

Now let's talk about the food...

1. Popcorn chicken was beyond disappointing... it would have made the chicken that it came from very ashamed and disgusted. On the outside it looks good,  but once you pop it in your mouth it feels like you are eating a wad of cardboard.  Not only was it dry, but it was hardly seasoned. I had to put garlic salt on it to make it taste ok, but then I had to dip it in a sauce to hide the dryness of the meat.  The other disappointing thing was that the majority of the pieces of chicken were tiny.  It looked like someone used the tiny dregs of meat and balls of flour from the bottom of the flour bin to cook  and sell.

2. Deep fried tofu was another disappointing snack that tasted as bland as a white piece of paper.  Someone must have had a bag day or was too busy to pick up the seasoning shaker to flavor the tofu.  Also, they must be saving cooking gas because the oil they used was not up to temp to properly fry the tofu.  You can tell because the outside coating of the tofu absorbed all the oil and didn't properly crisp the outside.  Yes I know it was delivered and it couldn't be crisp by the time it got to me but I've had experience buying fresh deep fried tofu from Oakland Chinatown and driving it all the way home five cities away and the outside of the tofu was still hard.

3. Curly fries... I don't normally order this but my daughter likes them.  The curly fries were okay.. seasoning was fine.  I think the fries were the best thing in my whole order with the drinks coming in second.

Overall,  I wouldn't go back unless I totally forgot about this place and accidentally visited or ordered from them again.  😅

Name: Feng Cha Teahouse - Berkeley
Author: Rachel Kim
Review: Please close your hot drinks or tape them bc they spilled all over me and burnt my hands. Customer service was not friendly either. I asked her for a cup holder because I had three drinks and she threw the plastic bag on the counter and I had to put down all my drinks to put it in. Restrooms aren’t working either.

Name: Feng Cha Teahouse - Berkeley
Author: Brendan Sullivan
Review: Lighting fast service at lunch time, and the boba and tea were delicious. Place is spacious and good for a lot of people so they can handle crowds pretty well it seems. Staff was pleasant and upbeat.

Name: Feng Cha Teahouse - Berkeley
Author: Leo Lee
Review: The chicken is really good! I've been craving this for so many years and I've finally got the chance to try it. The sweet Chilli flavor for the chicken is really good and crunchy.

Name: Feng Cha Teahouse - Berkeley
Author: Faye
Review: Great location, but hard to find parking space nearby, so be prepared for looking for parking. otherwise, dessert and drinks are all great to try! This new location comes with a totally modern interior design and clean style, which I really do like it. The cake I would like to recommend matcha and strawberry.  All fruit drinks are awesome fresh,  also dirty milk tea is the signature in here too.

Name: TP TEA Berkeley
Author: Safira CA
Review: This is one of the best boba place in Berkeley, it’s very close to the Southgate/sather gate of UC Berkeley. This place offer great variety of boba, some of them are nice, but some of them have weird taste lol. Whenever I visit this place, I always try the new variant, that’s why I ended up with the weird taste ones 😅 but yeah overall I like the boba, if you want a safe choice, just pick the original one. The price is a bit higher than other boba places. And yeah, They have 3-4 tables for seating area.

Name: TP TEA Berkeley
Author: Sid Shah
Review: I love the milk teas from here, they're very strong, giving the boba a little more depth than simply sugar milk. This is a personal preference though, as many of my friends dislike the strength. I do also think that 100% sweet isn't too sweet, and I normally do 75%-80%.

Name: TP TEA Berkeley
Author: Andrew Parker
Review: All of the drinks we got were delicious! The fries were good too! The boba texture was perfect. Friendly and knowledgeable staff as well. Some people with us had questions about ingredients due to allergies, and the staff made some good suggestions on what they might like.

Name: TP TEA Berkeley
Author: Mahik Kaur
Review: I never really liked boba, but TP TEA DELIVERED. I usually try new flavours every time I go, and they haven’t disappointed. Also, I feel like the amount of sugar you request can really make or break the taste of the drink, and service quality can vary depending on demand. Overall, really enjoy this place

Name: TP TEA Berkeley
Author: Amish Gupta
Review: Having tried all the boba spots near campus, TP Tea is objectively the best boba in terms of price, quality of tea, and preparation. They make sure all their drinks follow a strict standard and it reflects in the taste

Name: Tiger Tea & Juice
Author: Julie Taeko
Review: Very good quality drinks! I’ve tried at least four different drinks now, and absolutely loved every single one. The service is excellent. They are incredibly friendly and nice. It’s also a very clean store with a very clean bathroom. Love it. New favorite place in Burlingame/Millbrae.

Name: Tiger Tea & Juice
Author: JP G
Review: The boba brown sugar with cheese foam was pretty good. I was quite thirsty so I downed it pretty fast, but the flavor was pretty good, though I think I was expecting more nuance of flavour in the brown sugar syrup. The cheese foam topping did have that nice nuance though. The default amount of boba isn't all that much be advised to order extra boba if you really like boba. The ordering is done through a tablet which allows you to see the pictures of the drinks if you need that in order to decide. I think you can probably also order from a person. The interior was nice and minimalist with a fair amount of small tables and one large table tucked into an alcove. It looked like there were plenty of electrical outlets if you want to do work there. I do kind of find it sad that so many businesses have very little interaction with people anymore. When your order is done you will get a text that it is done. I guess that this is helpful for efficiency and that is really nice for some people. I would say that the wait was pretty long though, so I don't know if this is actually making anything more efficient. Overall I enjoyed it and recommend this business though I think if there was more of a human touch I'd love it more.

Name: Tiger Tea & Juice
Author: L C.
Review:
The peach oolong milk tea was very disappointing. There was no taste and the tea tasted like water. The worst part is that I ordered regular ice, and there was literally only 2 pieces of ice and the rest was lukewarm light artificial peach flavored water. Made me doubt my taste buds.
The strawberry matcha in comparison was drinkable. But the matcha flavor was lacking.
The actual boba balls were fine, did not wow us but was at least edible.
Overall do not recommend. For the price, 0& is a much better choice and actually uses real fruit instead of powders and jam.

Name: Tiger Tea & Juice
Author: Andrew Jaru
Review: Hands down one of my top 3 favorite milk tea places in the Bay Area. Their potted plant option sounds gimmicky, but it adds a nice dimension of flavor to whatever you get it on. The tea itself is clean tasting and very smooth. The jellies are soft and have a nice texture. Inside is clean and well designed. Overall, a great place for drinks.

Name: Tiger Tea & Juice
Author: Wendel Tse
Review: The teas are great, but even better, the chill crew won't bother you if you decide to relax in the clean dining room with a friend, chat, and sip your beverages slowly.

Name: Quickly
Address: 1212 El Camino Real A, San Bruno
Author: Aayushi Trivedi
Review:
Honey milk tea w/ mini boba & mango popping boba (left), Rose milk tea w/ mini boba (right) and sweet potato fries. Loved both drinks!

This has been my go to place for boba drinks but for the fried snack options, I wish they were less greasy.

Highly recommend the additional topping of mango popping boba for a fun little kick to the drink. They also have the option for a strawberry popping boba.

Name: Quickly
Address: 1212 El Camino Real A, San Bruno
Author: Sebastian M.
Review: I normally order black mango tea with boba from here or of fruit variety. This location is my go to boba spot when in the area, very quick on producing orders, although a couple of times it did taste watered down, sometimes even forgetting to add my flavor "popping" additions. Has 4 tables with benches for seating, plenty of parking around the area.

Name: Quickly
Address: 1212 El Camino Real A, San Bruno
Author: Nora Salah
Review:
My daughter bought a slushy with boba from here yesterday and she found a BUG in her drink, well it was in her mouth after drinking it 🤮🤮
This place used to be clean but now it's disgusting! I advice everyone to get their boba fix at other places.

Name: Quickly
Address: 1212 El Camino Real A, San Bruno
Author: Ryan Jech
Review: God awful! Just got food poisoning from a mango smoothie today. Literally just started vomiting later tonight

Name: Quickly
Address: 1212 El Camino Real A, San Bruno
Author: ROMMEL RIVERA
Review: Great place to buy boba drinks.  Also have a big selections for snacks . Popcorn chicken is one of the best items on they're menu but for get the fries. Everything now is automated for your convenience.

Name: Tpumps
Author: DGL
Review: Super friendly staff, great teas, and a wide variety of options makes for a fun experience any time I am in town and have a chance to swing by. On my most recent visit, I brought two colleagues who had never been (one being new to bubble tea altogether), and they really enjoyed sampling some of the teas and building their drinks to their liking.

Name: Tpumps
Author: Ben A
Review: Trendy spot frequented by young adults and teens. Its a place you can grab some flavor (pumps) for green or black tea. Try the green tea with Mango and Pomegranate.

Name: Tpumps
Author: Priyanka Chavan
Review: Boba is so fresh! There are a LOT of options for flavors. We love their boba, and is popular amongst locals here.

Name: Tpumps
Author: Andrew Ling
Review: Located at downtown Burlingame. Very busy. Teas  favored by lots of young folks.

Name: Tpumps
Author: Kristie Drawe
Review:
Downtown Burlingame and parking near by. Lines can be long but once you order the drink is ready pretty quick.

I ordered the first item on the popular menu, rose vanilla green milk tea with bubble and light sweetness. Wow. It was really sweet(tasted a little more than what I would expect normal Sweetness from another place). I also picked up a daily special (premade drinks) from the fridge by the checkout desk. That one was normal Sweetness but it was too sweet. We dumped some tea out to add water to it.

$6 for both drinks and Amex ok.

Name: Sharetea
Author: Austin Zhuang
Review:
My experience at Sharetea in Berkeley was nothing short of phenomenal, particularly due to their creative collaboration with ATEEZ fans for a cupsleeve event. As an ardent fan of the K-pop group, I was thrilled to attend a gathering that celebrated their music and brought fans together. Sharetea went the extra mile by infusing the cafe with K-pop-themed decorations, playing ATEEZ's hits, and offering exclusive merchandise. The ambiance was electric; you could feel the excitement and camaraderie among fans. The cupsleeve event was like a mini-festival, complete with photo opportunities and social interactions that made it a memorable day.

What impressed me the most was how seamlessly Sharetea managed to balance the bustling event with their usual level of quality service and professionalism. Despite the crowd, the staff remained attentive and patient, ensuring that everyone felt welcome and well-served. The drinks were consistently delicious, showcasing the quality that Sharetea is known for. Their bubbly enthusiasm and warm interactions added a personal touch, making each person feel like a valued guest, whether they were there for the event or just to grab a quick drink. It's this elevated level of service that distinguishes Sharetea from other establishments.

In addition, the logistical execution of the events was impeccable. Sharetea seamlessly integrated the ATEEZ-themed activities without disrupting the regular flow of business. This coordination not only kept the fans happy but also ensured that the other customers were not inconvenienced. They managed to create an inclusive environment where everyone could enjoy their time without feeling overwhelmed. Overall, Sharetea's ability to host such a specialized event while maintaining their high standards of service speaks volumes about their dedication and versatility. This experience solidified my admiration for Sharetea, making it my go-to spot in Berkeley.

Name: Sharetea
Author: Ginger L
Review:
It’s usually pretty busy in here. I’ve been here a few times (including boba day where they had a bogo deal) and they’re pretty fast so I’ve never really had any wait time issues. Their boba is consistently chewy and I find that they actually read the “less ice” detail I add to the my orders. Today I saw the lady sift out the ice before sealing my drink since I asked for 50% ice

I also like that they have an honest review policy and they respond genuinely to most reviews on Google maps and try to make things right.

Decor is simple, not necessarily a place I’d study at but I’m really only here to get boba quickly

Name: Sharetea
Author: ZAYED ALBAZIAN
Review: Senara, Jocelyn, Marisol were awesome! Had my drink out within a minute and were super nice and helpful. I got the Taro milk tea and some soy nuggets that were delicious. Great place to come relax, hangout and enjoy a snack and a drink.

Name: Sharetea
Author: kavya parikh
Review: the boba was good and so was the store but i found a hair in my cup hence the low stars

Name: Sharetea
Author: Prasanna Laxmi
Review: Ordered hot ginger milk tea but it was not hot at all. Just about room temperature. We asked the staff to make it hot and they said there are not equipped to make it more hotter. I suggest to remove the hot option or rename it to warm

Name: Teaspoon Berkeley
Author: nesticoh
Review:
I ordered the strawberry colada smoothie with boba which tends to have the boba stuck at the bottom. This led to me shaking it to mix it up like most other boba places but the lid came loose and the drink spilled everywhere. The customer service was the best.

They offered to remake the drink and gave me plenty of things to help clean myself up as well as make the situation better as a whole. Best drinks in boba as well by far. Will continue coming here for the rest of my time here at Berkeley!

Name: Teaspoon Berkeley
Author: c w
Review: shop is cute, drink is okay, I got the dirty ube and the coffee tasted way stronger cause I didn’t taste the coconut/ube actually. also drinks are super expensive but shop is cute!

Name: Teaspoon Berkeley
Author: Laura VanVliet
Review: The staff here are so nice and the drinks are so creamy and sweet and with good variety. I tend to get drinks here as a treat for a bad day and even though I order online the staff is incredibly kind and still quick with the order.

Name: Teaspoon Berkeley
Author: Gavin Sagastume
Review:
Ameilia served and made my drink and she was a baddie!!! she was so iconic and the drink she made was so good!!! so much yum i came back multiple times for her to make me my drink. I love you amelia!! so happy you’re in the boba industry
for your career it wouldn’t be the same without you. ♥️😻

#Amelia

Name: Teaspoon Berkeley
Author: Helena Su
Review:
Order 25% sweet and the drink is sugar water. Bad quality and basically undrinkable.
Not too mention it’s extremely overpriced! A basic boba milktea near $9. Does it look like people get their money for free?

Name: Tibear
Author: Jefferson Anugerah Utomo
Review: I heard that this was one of the best Milk Teas in the Berkeley area! And it's great. They have this subtle mochi topping that's unique. Definitely recommendes for a treat once in a while!

Name: Tibear
Author: Donald Thornton
Review: Ordered doordash several times, I really like their bubble milk tea. It has a subtle tea taste.  The QQ mochi milk tea and Taro mochi milk tea are also tasty. Will try more. （Updates～Taro Tornado tastes so good. Of course i added boba! *Mango yogurt tastes so good! 09.21.24)

Name: Tibear
Author: Janina Rudzinski
Review:
She’s so sweet! This Phoenix Oolong Boba Tea is perfect and delicious. I ordered it hot for something soothing, and it’s amazing.
Thank you so much

Name: Tibear
Author: Wenran Lu
Review: I ordered a mango slushie and it tastes super plain. The ingredients don't taste fresh to me. Very disappointed for $8.5 on such a drink.

Name: Tibear
Author: Paul-Elie Nauleau
Review:
The mochi milk is quite a different experience!
The boba was great and there aren’t a lot of more normal milk tea options
It was good overall, but just not quite the 5 stars!
The inside is tiny and no seating, so be ready to take it to go

Name: ZERO& San Francisco Centre
Author: Andy Ng
Review:
Zero& seemed to have great reviews and I can see why - the drinks are great, they offer non-dairy milk options, and you really get some high quality tea options here.

This spot is located on the bottom floor of the SF Centre. It took me a while to find it since the maps inside are atrocious, but I was able to find it in due time.

I ordered a Jasmine Green Milk Tea with Oat Milk and it was perfect. Default settings were less ice and less sugar, making it easy to order. Again, I can't stress how nice it is to be offered non-dairy milk options.

All orders are taken online so there is little to no interaction with the staff until after your order has been prepared. I found this a bit perplexing as I was trying to navigate the kiosks and would encounter an error trying to get back to the main screen from someone's previous order. A small thing overall, but something to take into account.

Name: ZERO& San Francisco Centre
Author: Shirley Cen
Review: Ordered this for pickup.  The price is the same, but the portions are shrinking.  The cup on the left is ridiculously low.  It wasn't even leaking.  I need to check the bag before I leave next time.

Name: ZERO& San Francisco Centre
Author: Jill DeCandio
Review: Grabbed lunch here at the location at the Westfield San Francisco Centere.  Unfortunately I didn’t realize ordering was automated and stood there at the counter for quite some time wondering why the employees were in the back looking at me and not coming to take my order.  There wasn’t any sign or instructions indicating ordering was automated.  Never having eaten here I didn’t know.  And non of the employees provided any help or direction.  Once I figured things out my lunch was handed to me.  I got the ham and cheese croissant.  Unfortunately it was cold.  It would have been so much better if it were hot.  Im guessing it was hot once when it was first made but it must have just been sitting there awhile.  Disappointing.  Live and learn.

Name: ZERO& San Francisco Centre
Author: Dyanna Volek
Review: Go-to boba spot in the Westfield mall. They make it easy to order by putting your number on a screen when it's ready. I love the black sesame - so delicious and such a cool black color - with boba. It's a perfect treat to shop around the mall with.

Name: ZERO& San Francisco Centre
Author: Alice Hou
Review: Tried the creme brulee boba milk tea here. Boba was great, as always! Unique flavor, chewy pearls. Convenient spot in Westfield.

Name: Tpumps
Author: Matt B
Review:
These folk make an interesting drink which is somewhere between a smoothie and boba milk tea. I got a pomegranate black tea with boba and it was an interesting mixture of textures and tastes, but I was quickly into it. I’d definitely come back again. Their default milk is dairy-free.

Drinks were also relatively cheap, for SF prices. On Tuesdays, you can get a large for the price of a medium.

The store is a bit drab and dark. It takes minimalism a degree or two too far, but they sometimes have free tea samples!

Name: Tpumps
Author: Jennifer Pejo
Review: When you visit San Francisco you got to try boba iced tea. This place if a local favorite. They have two sizes and I recommend no more than 3 flavors.

Name: Tpumps
Author: Ava Quinn
Review: Fully customizable boba offers thousands of unique flavor combinations. You can pick your own 3 flavors, sweetness level, and add-ons. Definitely a great place to stop by for bubble tea.

Name: Tpumps
Author: Wendy Mena
Review: I’ve been coming here for years and love their Tea but I wish they would spend a little extra to make their syrups a little higher quality maybe add a few more things to freshen up the shop but I love this place ❤️

Name: Tpumps
Author: keith k
Review:
I visited here on a Tuesday night— (Free Upgrades all day on Tuesday!) TPumps seem to be always busy, and the employees here work very hard and are efficient.

I was impressed to see the vast selection of different flavor combinations and availability on the menu board. However, I noticed that TPumps use Torani Syrup to flavor their teas. I personally think by adding this artificial syrup to the teas, it really diminishes the overall quality of the drink.

I appreciate when Boba and Tea shops use purees, juices, and authentic ingredients in their craftsmanship; versus just using a standard artificial sugar syrup.

The Boba itself was good! The texture was nice and chewy and the flavor had just the right amount of sweetness.

Name: BB Tea
Author: P
Review: I had really high hopes when I came here and I was disappointed.I would give this place a 2.5 starts. There were 2 girls in the place and they were kind of rude. One of them was using her phone all the time that I was there. After I ordered one of them was doing everything. I ordered soufflé pancakes and it was supposed to take 12-15 minutes but i waited for at least 25 minutes! in addition the girl that was making my pancakes wasn't wearing gloves(that was the same girl in the cash register). Finally, I got the pancake and it was completely overcooked. With the cream on top, you wouldn't notice and it looked beautiful but I wanted to taste the correct way. It wasn't horrible but I didn't get what I paid for. I don't know if the place and service is like this all the time or if I was lucky but I will be coming back because I want to try the drinks.

Name: BB Tea
Author: Brandy Baker
Review: This is almost 5 stars for me, but not quite. I love the self order kiosk and the text when ready ordering system - it makes the process a lot smoother than other boba shops in the area. We ordered four drinks but the flavor wasn’t quite there on all of them. The drinks were good, and we enjoyed them! We will be back and hopefully we just had drinks on a bit of an off day.

Name: BB Tea
Author: Kiya J
Review: Tried BB Tea's Ube Mont Blanc cake at FoodieLand and I had to get it again because it's one of the best desserts i've had in years ! Too yummy !! Also got a Creme Brulee and Lavender Milk Tea, also super delicious.

Name: BB Tea
Author: Sisi Chen
Review: We got brown sugar milk tea with boba and salted egg cookies. Milk tea was a 3, not enough tea taste. 25% sweet is the least amount of sugar and it was definitely still on the sweeter side. The boba is a 5, perfectly chewy and sweet. The cookies were the highlight for me. So nutty, creamy and love the salty sweet taste. Will come back for more sweet treats!

Name: BB Tea
Author: Viveat T
Review:
Creme brulee milk tea is above average. My boyfriend tried the creme brulee crepe and it was so good, filled with creamy brulee, fresh strawberry, ice cream, and a super crunchy sugar top, definitely worth $9, don't be set back by the price!
They accidentally forgot to add boba to my drink but gave me a cup of boba on the side. Glad I caught that before walking out! It's really no big deal but they were very nice about it.

Name: 8-Twelve Oriental Market
Author: Valery Castro
Review: Every thing is perfect 🤩 the food,the place, especially there boba's and every one is so nice in there.

Name: 8-Twelve Oriental Market
Author: Aaron Curie
Review: Best boba place in the Bay, but don't tell any body! Hands-down cheapest price, freshest warm plentiful serving of boba for no extra charge, large sizes, and fast service, and games you can play for free too. No public restroom because we can't have nice things in the Bay but the drinks service and prices are unbeatable!!! 500 Stars!

Name: 8-Twelve Oriental Market
Author: Mya Mo
Review: Let’s start with right away the owner doesn’t want to work. He was in a horrible mood when taking my order. Toppings are extra and I asked for strawberries and bananas and got 1 piece of each. It was hard and burnt and asked to get another one. I was watching from a farther distance and he throw it away then took it out the trash and put the strawberry and banana inside again. That’s disgusting very unprofessional, especially since I have been coming since they open. That’s messed up, he robably thought I wasn’t looking.

Name: 8-Twelve Oriental Market
Author: Brittiney Zachery
Review: Horrible service. I went here with my son. He asked the man behind the counter a question only to be met with an attitude. The store was empty and he only asked him one question about the menu that the man couldn’t be bothered to answer. Bought a drink because my son really wanted one and it wasn’t even good.

Name: 8-Twelve Oriental Market
Author: denica rodriguez
Review: The place is pretty cool, more like a hangout spot for the younger crowd. The creps were ok but not great. The boba tea was pretty good, you get to serve your own toppings before they add the tea. We had fun playing with the super sized jenga.

Name: Heeretea
Author: Anke Hao
Review: The boba was okay and the indomie was very much fancy instant noodles, but the place is very cute and very suited for studying or working! There's plenty of tables and very cute decor. Will try some more snacks off their menu when I go a second time; they seem to have pretty interesting choices.

Name: Heeretea
Author: Joyce Lam
Review:
Cute little boba shop. The menu has a few uncommon items. We tried the fresh sugar cane juice with coconut popping boba. Never had coconut popping boba before. They are very fun and very coconutty. But a bit too sweet.

They have Hong Kong snacks too.

Name: Heeretea
Author: Carey
Review:
Best boba in San Mateo! The owner was highly recommended Mango coconut pudding and Fresh taco highland barley milk tea, I was very happy what I had.
They used fresh fruit and good quality of ingredients to make the drink really special and tasty, I couldn’t help myself to order the same thing again even though I was full.
Friendly service, cute decoration, easy to order, reasonable prices, great location.
Definitely I would come back again!

Name: Heeretea
Author: Love Dove
Review:
Ordered for pick up and this was my first time to this tea shop. Cute little whole in the wall type of shop. They are actually open late which is super convenient and you actually have to park and walk down past the barricade on B street to get to the shop. Friendly staff when I walked in to pick up my order. The vibes are nice in there and they also have a self serve machine.

They have some cute decorations and the cups are super cool, original and authentic. Their tea is freaking DELICIOUS! Will call this place my spot when ever I'm here in San Mateo. 😋

Name: Heeretea
Author: Kimberly Iwamoto
Review:
A very cute tea shop with many cat portraits inside.

Ordered Sakura snow top oolong , on top has dried strawberries pieces.

Tea is not not strong enough.
Price is reasonable $6.00

Name: Bobaville
Author: Jam
Review: when its made right its really good, the past three orders my extra toppings have been missing. specifically the salted cream. its my favorite part of it so when its missing theres no point of drinking it :(

Name: Bobaville
Author: Liz Donut
Review:
I got the Hong Kong Milk Tea with 50% sweetness to try. It was bland, like watered down tea.
The boba was surprisingly good. It surprised me because I wasn't used to seeing light-brown boba!

Name: Bobaville
Author: Sara Tong
Review: Love this boba shop! My favorite drinks are probably the mango summer tea, the mango matcha, and the roasted oolong salted crema! I brought my own jar a couple times and they were fine with it! They also make their own tapioca balls!

Name: Bobaville
Author: Hien P
Review: We occasionally stop here for boba when we are near it!  Boba is homemade and it's smaller than usual bobas , they are also very different in squishiness than usual bobas too! It's super good and you definitely need to try it out to know. There is always no line when we go to order but I think it is because they are so quick with making their boba people literally jump in and out! Worth going back to trying all their different drinks!

Name: Bobaville
Author: Shirley C
Review:
You come here for their homemade tapioca pearls.

I got the Mango Summer Tea - unfortunately the drink itself just wasn't for me.  It touts "mango puree", but I'm not sure the drink had any kind of puree or bits.. It tasted more like straight up mango juice instead of a tea + mango. Maybe I'm just used to my favorite mango teas being more tea like with mango instead of the other way around.

My husband got the coffee milk tea and he really like that.. so maybe I just got the wrong drink.

Their homemade boba IS good, BUT more expensive ($0.75), and less in amount than you typically get at other shops that also do homemade boba.

Bobaville is a bit out of the way for me, so I don't think I'd specifically make a trip to come here.. Maybe I'd try it again if I happen to be in the area.

Name: Societea Tea House & Eatery
Author: Laura Michaelis
Review:
I was a little nervous at first because I am not very open when it comes to my diet. Let me tell you, I was extremely happy.

We had a party of six with two separate orders, I had ordered. The Pork Belly sandwich with the Furi-Furi Sprouts. My husband had the Thai Chicken over rice. The dishes were amazing!! I am craving the sandwich now.

The Thai Milk Tea was spot on and the Honeydew Tea was like they blended the melon and stuck a straw in it, it was that good.

The waffle fries were on point, the popcorn chicken was okay, The burgers were good, but the jalapenos were not spicy (according to my friend).

As we walked out, we discovered that you could actually check out a board game and play a game in the cafe while enjoying your meal. Might keep the kids off their phones.

Name: Societea Tea House & Eatery
Author: B Na
Review: Happy to have found a place close to the airport to have lunch with a friend while waiting out a layover. We ordered the lemongrass crispy tofu sandwich and I had a matcha brown sugar bubble tea. Both were great and we played some board games to pass the time. There is a large seating area in the back for group gatherings.

Name: Societea Tea House & Eatery
Author: Jennifer Ho
Review: Cute little place.  Craved banh mi and found them through Yelp.  Spacious room in the back to work and eat.  Can’t take work calls here 😉 background noise.  Plenty of parking in the back with easy QR code to scan and pay $1 for 2 hours.

Name: Societea Tea House & Eatery
Author: Esther C.
Review:
It is a casual cafe with enough seating space to sit and play games with friends or study on your own (which was the primary reason I came here and appreciated their set up) but unfortunately within the last month as I’ve started to come in more often and have brought friends, we’ve encountered coming into the store to music that is so loud you cannot hear one another speak, and 4-6 employees gathered at the register laughing loudly, which wouldn’t be a problem had my friends and I been greeted upon our entry.

As for the food, their drinks are fun and some are tasty but the boba is too stiff and not chewy at all. I find that their Jasmine tea is consistently over steeped but black tea based drinks are solid. Their food is okay.

If you do not mind a loud atmosphere, this can definitely be the place for you but if you’re seeking a place to study or get work done, this might not be the spot for you.

Name: Societea Tea House & Eatery
Author: Cindy L
Review:
Fav spot in the peninsula for popcorn chicken and milk tea! The workers are super friendly and drinks come out fast.

My go to is the strawberry matcha latte and Thai green tea. It’s the perfect balance of creamy yet refreshing matcha flavor and the honey boba is perfect lightly chewy texture and not too sweet.

Name: Tea Hut
Author: Mekah Kaur
Review: This is a cute place, the atmosphere is chill. There is a screen where you can self-order which makes my life easier since I have modifications and take time. I got jasmine milk tea with honey boba with 50% less sweetness, it was delicious! I definitely giving this place a try.

Name: Tea Hut
Author: Milton Chui
Review:
Jasmine Milk Tea, regular sweet, crystal and regular boba...Crystal boba was clumped together.
Order was very fast. They have a self-serve kiosk with tap pay.

Name: Tea Hut
Author: Catarina Zhang He
Review:
Used to be my go-to place when I’d be in the area.
The past month or two I’ve gone in 3 times and every visit has been worse than the previous one.
My most recent visit I ordered a Mango Coco drink (my go-to) and this time it came with grapefruit (!!!) inside, and a few pieces of my non-chosen topping. I called and the employee said it has always come with grapefruit. There’s a reason I ordered Mango Coco and not other mango drinks, I didn’t want any citrus & I’ve ordered a Mango Coco before.
The previous visit I saw two employees on their phone in plain sight by the back, while there was an ordering line, I wanted to order at the counter and no one came to assist me. A third employee exited from the back and made drinks.

Name: Tea Hut
Author: Mei Fu Lee
Review: I go to the post office next to tea hut pretty often. I was waiting for teaspoon to open here for such a long time, but it finally opened as tea hut. There aren't that boba shops in this area, other than up towards 19th and ocean.  Usually there's a long line and I heard that service can get slow here. The one time there was no line, I decided to try it. Inside is pretty small with no sitting space. They have a pretty extensive menu with a lot of variety of teas. I ordered the lemon lime green for my friend. She likes her drinks to be not as sweet and said that the drink was perfect. They put in pretty big slices of lemon and lime in the drink. I also tried their oolong milk tea with boba. Their boba was really well made and the milk tea tasted great. They also have other types of oolong teas that seem fun to try. I would love to come again, but it does get busy quickly.

Name: Tea Hut
Author: Jasmine Lo
Review: the worst boba shop ever. I paid for extra boba for osmanthus rice milk tea and they didn't even give me a single taro ball or boba. do not buy at this place. the milk tea also tasted bad.

Name: Teaspoon Corte Madera
Address: 132 Corte Madera Town Center, Corte Madera
Author: Jean Tsou
Review: Drink tastes way too sweet. I will suggest 0 sugar level if you order any fruite puree related drinks. Nice decor inside and friendly service.

Name: Teaspoon Corte Madera
Address: 132 Corte Madera Town Center, Corte Madera
Author: Millie Obregon
Review:
If you’re looking to get some yummy fresh boba tea then choose Teaspoon! I was impressed for my first time here.

Drinks: The boba teas were fresh and light. They both tasted just right, not too sweet, and with great flavors. We tried the taro milk tea and the dirty ube and we would highly recommend both!

Atmosphere: It was a charming little place with indoor and outdoor seating available. The place wasn’t too crowded as well and staff were friendly and welcoming.

Price: Normal price for these quality drinks! You get what you pay for.

Parking: Parking is found in the parking lot area outside of the many shops/restaurants. You don’t have to pay for it either.

Name: Teaspoon Corte Madera
Address: 132 Corte Madera Town Center, Corte Madera
Author: Jeremy Yee
Review: We catered Teaspoon for our wedding at StoneTree Estate in Novato and it was a hit! All of our guests loved the drinks and they were gone in a matter of minutes! Jennifer was a delight to work with, she answered all of our questions, and she provided great guidance in terms of how many and which drinks she recommends. Her communication was also very prompt. We would definitely recommend Teaspoon as a boba vendor for your event! They are also one of our regular go-to spots for boba!

Name: Teaspoon Corte Madera
Address: 132 Corte Madera Town Center, Corte Madera
Author: Johnny & Mora
Review:
We got catering through Teaspoon for a party of 150 people and everything from the initial request to the email exchanges to the pick up went so smoothly and everything was professionally and excellently executed.

We ordered 4 tea travelers of Liquid Gold, 3 Creamy Jasmine, and 3 Assam Black tea - all 25% sweet. Each traveller came with a topping so we chose 5 honey boba, 3 egg pudding, and 2 strawberry heart jellies. We got our own ice on the side and they gave us everything else including straws, cups, and lids.

The owner even gave us tips on how to serve the items so that it would be easy for guests and the proper way of storing everything (no putting boba in the fridge!). We ended up getting drink dispensers and pouring it all into them but the travelers were so high quality, that I would suggest skipping that step (it really was only for the aesthetic but the travelers are cute too). We also put some cute stickers on the cups to let people differentiate their drinks and to go along with the theme ($5 for like 100 stickers on Amazon).

We even had some extra tea and toppings and we served then the next day at church and it allowed for another 30 people to be happily satisfied. The whole thing was a hit and we will definitely be doing this again when we have a special occasion to celebrate. Or even for some low-key get-togethers since it really is a good deal!! It probably came up to $1.50 a guest since, with ice, people had like 2 rounds each. They all had a blast. Thank you so much!!

Name: Teaspoon Corte Madera
Address: 132 Corte Madera Town Center, Corte Madera
Author: Kim Stephens
Review: The boba was great as per usual. However, I had a bad experience with a server. I struggled to take my card out of my wallet and it took a minute or two and the girl working the register, Sofi, aggressively asked, “So are you gonna pay?!” Not only that but she gave me and my colleague dirty looks. I was baffled by the disrespect and grabbed my drinks and left.

Name: My Cup of Tea
Author: Gregg Tavares
Review: My Cup of Tea is like the unofficial community center for the local youth. Lots of groups playing boardgames, studying, working. I've stumbled into in store events like a k-pop fan goods event among other things. Also, the garlic noodles here are what made me fall in love with garlic noodles and the standard by which I judge the rest. The garlic noodles at My Cup of Tea have a very comfort food vibe. Soft, thick, and chewy and the sweet pickled shredded carrots are the perfect condiment. Free wifi and right next to an M line stop or 4 mins from a K line stop it's a great spot

Name: My Cup of Tea
Author: Elsie Militar
Review: I'm glad to have found a boba place that was open late. It was full of college students (I assume) at 10pm on a Monday. The fresh spring rolls were just okay but our drinks were good. I would come back and try other food. It was pretty loud in there from people playing board games. It also looked like a popular spot for people with laptops.

Name: My Cup of Tea
Author: Jeff Ho
Review:
Opened until 12am, My Cup of Tea is convenient for those of us that takes naps and wake up late. The ladies that work here are fast. I only had to wait 15 minutes for my food after ordering at the counter. The chicken garlic noodles $13 are buttery and tasted good. I would have liked more garlic. This was still good because it wasn't salty. It could have benefited from chopped pickled green onions. The grilled pork vermicelli noodles $13 was fine. Both dishes are served in small take out containers.

Minor stuff:
- They don't give you a bag or napkins.
- There are dorky, loud, male SFSU students playing their board games.
-  Homeless man sitting with his big plastic of clothes in the dining room. It kind of felt like a homeless shelter at 10:30pm.

Tip: wifi password is the restaurant's name without the spaces.

Name: My Cup of Tea
Author: Mei Fu Lee
Review:
UPDATE: I've been going here for a long time now and have noticed how much it has changed and how popular this boba shop has gotten now. I still appreciate the amount of space this place has and how clean it is too. Their food is really good! I was honestly quite skeptical of how their noodle soup would taste, but it's actually really tasty! I also typically order their pork banh mi. Usually the sandwiches are very stuffed, but I have gotten a few skimped out sandwiches before. For drinks, their oolong milk tea with boba is my go to drink. Their mango green tea is also really good. Their drink sizes are pretty big and the large should definitely be shared. Their boba quality is consistent and has really improved over time. Their prices have gone a bit higher, but I think it's worth it for the quantity and quality.

I highly recommend ordering by phone because it can get quite busy!

I've come here a few times. The sign is a little misleading, as it isn't really My Cup of Tea, but actually Cool Tea Bar. I liked that this place is open late, until midnight. It's really big inside, with lots of tables and free wifi, so I thought it would be an ideal place to study. I came around 9PM on a Tuesday and it was SO CROWDED. People were really loud and it was pretty hard to study. I assume a lot of students were here hanging out here since it's summer. Even after ordering, I noticed a lot of people still came inside.

I ordered the pork banh mi and a mango slush. For $6, the banh mi was pretty cheap (definitely cheaper than dinosaurs up the street). However, it took 20 minutes to make. Thankfully, they didn't make the drink until the food was done, so the drink wasn't all melted. I got the drink 50% sweet, but it still seemed pretty sweet. The regular size is actually really big and the large size is definitely meant to be shared. The sandwich was pretty tasty and decently sized. The other banh mi sandwiches they have are $8  though. I stayed for about an hour, before I just left since it was just so loud.

While not ideal to study, as it was really crowded and loud, but I can appreciate the drinks are really big and taste pretty good. This place can be a great place to hang out, especially since they also have board games available. I would definitely get the pork banh mi again. They also have other food like popcorn chicken and fries.

Name: My Cup of Tea
Author: Kevin L
Review:
We had popcorn chicken, pho, and mango 101 drink. Everything tasted good and would order again.

Place had a few games on hand and there is free wifi.

Name: 1011 Sip TEA SF
Author: Matt B
Review: Fairly innocuous storefront, and a snoopy themed interior. They make a pretty good boba milk tea, and use non-dairy milk as their default.

Name: 1011 Sip TEA SF
Author: Jem Le
Review: They have an interesting selection of flavored teas and many can be served hot. Their drink is on the sweeter side even though I had mine at 25%. The purple yam milk tea I had came with boba included already. Will try their cold drinks next time I visit.

Name: 1011 Sip TEA SF
Author: LLL
Review: I love their gum 🍑 peach!!!!!!! I tried both drinks from the beauty section and it was really good for the Apple one, cucumber not my type! But for some store willing to use some high food cost ingredients, I would definitely come back again. Love the decorations too! Highly recommended this location and will definitely come back again!

Name: 1011 Sip TEA SF
Author: Ketty Nakamura
Review: The cutest Snoopy themed tea shop ever!  Compared to Oahu we don’t have very much Chinese flavors for tea and toppings so it was refreshing to try all these different drinks.  My favorites were the Apple Red Dates and Lavender Goji Berry.  The Grapefruit Lychee was yummy too!  The Cucumber Kumquat is very strong in its cucumber flavor so unless you absolutely love cucumbers in combination with kumquat I don’t recommend it.  Otherwise, I’ll definitely be back when I’m in SF!

Name: 1011 Sip TEA SF
Author: Sisi Chen
Review: Got their most popular Taiwanese milk tea with tapioca. They cannot do 0% sweet on this drink so got 25%. The drink was tasty with a bit of coffee flavor to it. The boba was chewy and sweet. Will try their oolong next. Hopefully they can do 0% sweetness for other drinks.

Name: Qualitea (Pinole Location)
Author: Jordan B
Review: A nice little boba cha shop, good location, the option to order with the employees or order on a touchpad near the menu area to the left of the door. The menu has a good amount of choices and some unique drinks as well. The staff was friendly and accommodating. I ordered the "Thai Milk Tea" with soy milk, crystal boba (agar agar) and Honey Boba 0%sweet and 50%ice 🧋

Name: Qualitea (Pinole Location)
Author: Adrian Lopez
Review: Super underrated place to get Boba, really slept on! Good prices too, compared to the Sharetea in Hercules. Staff is super friendly, drinks are good, and the place is really clean. There is also self order iPads if you’d like. Thanks Qualitea!

Name: Qualitea (Pinole Location)
Author: Akiko Nakamura
Review: Such delicious boba tea!! The staff was incredibly helpful—we brought our kids and they helped us figure out what would be appropriate drinks to give them (no caffeine, no sugar level but added sweetness from the fruit). Great prices, too!

Name: Qualitea (Pinole Location)
Author: Karen Hansen
Review: I highly recommend Qualitea! Their customer service is impeccable and their drinks are incredible. If you’re looking for a good boba spot, I’d definitely check it out. Their pink sparkle drink is delicious! 😋

Name: Qualitea (Pinole Location)
Author: Nai Saevang
Review: Great boba spot with delicious drinks and friendly staff.

Name: Raretea Alameda
Author: Shani Laureta
Review: Normally Id rate 4 stars, but for some reason, the chicken was EXTREMELY salty. Dumped 2 boxes of chicken straight down the trash because it was literally inedible. The takoyaki and drinks in general were delicious otherwise.

Name: Raretea Alameda
Author: bunbun
Review: i’m sorry but it was so gross and the boba was soggy? i ordered regular sweetness but it legit tasted like milk (yes i did mix/shake cup before drinking) they r usually good when i go so idk what happened

Name: Raretea Alameda
Author: BAD TUMMY
Review: So good best place ever if you need a good drink the prices are good and the service are a 10/10 will come back again for a another!!

Name: Raretea Alameda
Author: Hui Aut
Review: We ordered rose milk tea. It almost all milk of the whole cup and only little rose. It tastes less.

Name: Raretea Alameda
Author: Mariela Testa
Review: The thai tea latte that I bought today tasted so bad and sour. The drink has these weird looking white dots that make me guess that the milk went bad. Why no one is checking the expiration dates of these things?!

Name: oddcha San Rafael, CA
Author: Claire
Review: Super cute spot with great boba and friendly staff. I’ve had the brown sugar milk tea and the mango sticky rice inspired drink which is amazing and so unique and fun! Easy to order on the kiosk and quick service.

Name: oddcha San Rafael, CA
Author: Kyla Lam
Review: I got “less sugar” option, matcha w boba and it was freaking delicious !!!! There are so many options of jelly, boba and toppings here. I can’t wait to come back and try the others.

Name: oddcha San Rafael, CA
Author: Christina Ng
Review:
Ordered in the car so it was ready when we drove through San Rafael because I'm always searching for new boba places to try!

We both ordered less sweet brown sugar bobas. I added crystal boba to mine.  One of our favorite drinks. I usually order brown sugar boba or classic milk tea. Drinks were good and would come by again if in the area.  Less sweet is perfect for me. Not too much sugar but enough the tea isn't bitter.

Drinks were made super fast so they were sitting a bit while we were stuck in traffic and they kept them in the fridge. Thank you!

Name: oddcha San Rafael, CA
Author: Melissa Kamowski
Review: Super delicious and easy to order on their kiosk. They have several options including vegan and dairy free options. Will be getting again!!

Name: oddcha San Rafael, CA
Author: Hilary Go
Review: Stopped by this spot by chance and it was amazing!! The mango tango drink which is basically mango sticky rice is so creative, made with real fruits and sticky rice and authentic tasting. I love that you can also adjust the sweetness level of drinks - will definitely be back!

Name: Yifang Taiwan Fruit Tea
Author: Jean Tsou
Review: Large interior area with seating. They only have one kiosk to take order with card payment. Countertop only take payment with cash. Lots of fresh fruity drink choices here which they are famous for. Taro latte with no sugar but still really sweet. They use real taro not powder here which taste great! Good place for a boba drink with fresh choice.

Name: Yifang Taiwan Fruit Tea
Author: Liza Vlasenko (LizArt)
Review: Nice atmosphere and quick service. The drinks are really good and unique, but some are not for everyone because the drinks are very unique to the culture, since it’s a Taiwanese Boba tea cafe!

Name: Yifang Taiwan Fruit Tea
Author: Jessica Monteiro
Review: I go out of my way usually to come and get Boba here cause I enjoy it so much, but today I had the worst service ever. (7/5 @ 7:20pm)  The two boys refused to let me use my rewards by insisting I use the self checkout. When I showed them the rewards didn't work on the self checkout station and that the their sign said that rewards are only on the cashier's station, they argued instead of helping or showing me how it was possible to do it on my own, despite being the only customers in the store. I've been here multiple times and have never had this problem and even been explicitly told to use my rewards I have to order on the cashier's station. My husband and I gave up and left due to them arguing in the name of being lazy rather than making the effort to help. As we left, they also mocked and laughed at us. Super disappointed , won't be coming back so quickly.

Name: Yifang Taiwan Fruit Tea
Author: Olivia Hara
Review: The tea is so yummy! We got Almond Jelly Pineapple Mountain Tea w/sago and a Matcha Latte. The ordering system is a kiosk. If the cashier and the kiosk were taking orders it would have been a more efficient system.

Name: Yifang Taiwan Fruit Tea
Author: Rany M. Truong
Review:
Atmosphere: such a cute spot with friendly staff. You can order at the kiosk or at the cashier.

Service: staffs are friendly and helpful. I got my drink in less then 5 mins

Brown boba milk latte: boba comes out warm and I got lite sweetness (that’s what is recommended on the menu)

I def will be back. Best milk tea spot near me besides from happy lemon but they don’t have warm boba.

Name: i-Tea San Leandro
Author: Adriana Perez
Review:
Visiting this spot occasionally for boba is a delight; there's always a crowd, a diverse menu, and we tried the Okinawa milk tea and crème brûlée Boba milk tea. With so many tempting choices, I'm eager to explore more options next time!

They reduced seating by closing off most of their establishment, and it’s a quick
In and out experience.

Name: i-Tea San Leandro
Author: Anwesha Ghoshal
Review:
We went to i-Tea for their famous winter melon tea with tapioca (boba). I enjoyed every bit of this drink. I will visit them again for sure.

They have a huge seating area for customers and have a variety of food options.

Name: i-Tea San Leandro
Author: Megan Reilly
Review: Best milk teas and Boba! C-1 50% SUGAR my go to! I had the garlic noodles, fantastic! Long line most times and few places to sit but brilliant products!

Name: i-Tea San Leandro
Author: Jonathan quan
Review: Good service, friendly environment. The food is okay but pricey. Make sure you know when the rush hours are or you will be in line for while.

Name: i-Tea San Leandro
Author: Tala
Review:
I really love their foods and drinks. The best are these: M8, M7, Black Tea Oreo, Matcha with Redbeans. The best to go foods are popcorn chicken and takoyaki. I'm just very disappointed with the customer service. So learn from my experience, order ahead either from call or online and PAY RIGHT AWAY! Or else it's nonsense to make the order because you have to still fall in line even your order is just sitting right next to the cashier.
Having a prepaid pick up counter is useless! Very frustrating. Also, I ordered m8 they gave m7. So let them repeat your order and also like any other department stores, they charge 10 cents for the plastic bag.

Name: Milk Tea Lab
Author: Fae LeDawson
Review: Great boba and I appreciate the cash discount!  The sweetness is lower than usual, so if you typically order 50% sweet, get 75% here.  Both the plain milk tea and carbonated fruit teas were excellent!

Name: Milk Tea Lab
Author: Hamilton Hayes
Review: Best Dang Gone Place To Afford Milk 🥛 Tea 🍵  In San Francisco & A Few But Great Varieties to choose from. My Personal Favorite Is The Alien Tonic 👽.  Mmmm Taste just like Sprite. In Fact it is Sprite. But a with a Sweet & Bursting Kick Of Refreshing Taste 😋

Name: Milk Tea Lab
Author: Brienne Lee Brown
Review:
Tasty drinks and friendly people!
I ordered the Mango Sago which is creamy tropical goodness (seasonal), and my kiddo ordered the SpongeBob. You can adjust the sugar/sweetness levels and non/dairy choice for the beverage so it works out great for kids!
My daughter was offered a candy (after she checked with me 😀), as an extra little bonus and those are the kind little things that set the service at a level above!

Name: Milk Tea Lab
Author: Derek Wong
Review:
Best boba in San Francisco. I ordered the Tiger Milk Tea and it was spot on!

The worker Stephen made my drink just right. Highly recommend to place if you are trying boba for the first time.

Name: Milk Tea Lab
Author: perla Coot
Review: The service is so good the employees are so so nice and friendly I would go again the drinks are so good and sweet and I love I can customise my drink if your in the area definitely come get boba here it’s super cheap

Name: Purple Kow
Author: Courtney
Review: We got an oolong milk tea.  The tea flavor wasn’t really there and I thought it tasted diluted.  The tapioca pearls were a little too hard for my liking however they did give us a generous scoop in our drink.  Sitting down at the shop, it was crumby and sticky.  This place used to be good but it’s gone downhill.

Name: Purple Kow
Author: Jennifer Cooksey
Review: I got the matcha with oat milk and 80% sweetness. It had a floral flavor that I was not expecting, but was good.

Name: Purple Kow
Author: Max Podell
Review: Great boba and huge portions, but the seating area was pretty dirty and uninviting. I would still go back, but I would get my drink to-go.

Name: Purple Kow
Author: Christina Ng
Review:
Tried the toffee QQ half sweet with oat milk. Everything was good.  The cups are huge and don’t fit in a cup holder. Might be hard for little hands to hang on to.

Seating available inside.  Parking could be challenging.

We waited 5 minutes, there was no one in-front of us.

Straws look like compostable, hoping it doesn’t get soggy before I finish. I like to take my time and enjoy it all afternoon.

Name: Purple Kow
Author: Aliya Nadeeva
Review:
We didn’t like it here.
The almond milk boba tasted really artificial and was too sweet, although we ordered regular. Didn’t even finish it.
The garlic fries gave me a heartburn and didn’t taste good. For a big bucket we got a small container of the sauce and they charge 50 cents for an extra one.
We didn’t feel welcomed here at all.

Name: Tea Hut
Author: chilito 456
Review:
I ordered my tea at 7 pm; I don't know who was on shift, but honestly, they are giving the place a bad reputation. I paid more than 30 dollars for a tasteless tea, with no flavor at all.

Edit: Well, the order cost $24.87 exactly (I thought it cost more due to another issue) and it was between 6 and 7 PM that it was ordered. I ordered two Oreo Milk Tea and one Osmanthus Rice Milk Tea. I had tried the teas before but this time the ingredients were not mixed and overall they didn't seem like the ones I had tried and liked.

Name: Tea Hut
Author: Laura Qi
Review: I used to think this was one of the better bubble teas in San Francisco, but it has let me down more and more. First, the jasmine milk tea no longer has the fragrance of tea and has become increasingly watery. Second, the Kyoho grape flavor is not Kyoho grape at all; in fact, it's not even grape. I've ordered four or five times, giving it chance after chance, and I can confidently say it always has an off taste. I'm deeply disappointed.

Name: Tea Hut
Author: Richard Shulaw
Review: Beautiful National forest lots of activities available for the family

Name: Tea Hut
Author: Faye
Review: New products are awesome! The pink lychee drink you must order once in here, and you will love the flavor. Dragon fruit drink also is a new one, since they use fresh dragon fruit, the flavor is incredible​.

Name: Tea Hut
Author: Kalen Gutierrez
Review:
If I'm being honest, I found out about this place on TikTok, and from looking at the drink with mochi, and the dragon fruit, we decided to try it out, and what more perfect timing that we spend our last day in California in San Francisco!

At first, I thought that we would be waiting forever in line to order our drinks, but it was a relief to see that the line was actually for the bakery right next door. It was still quite busy at Tea Hut, but that didn't stop anything from not being amazing! I was not expecting the drinks to be made from real, all natural fruits! It's actually pretty hard to find good places that are innovative AND use real, fresh fruit. It's even hard to find a good place that just uses real, fresh fruit!

I feel like everyone that was working had really good chemistry, and they all seemed experienced because I kept seeing them whipping out drink after drink! It was honestly pretty busy and more and more people kept coming and coming, but they still whipped out the drinks quickly and perfectly! They were also pretty nice as well.

I was happy to see that the seven drinks which we ordered were not only perfectly made, but also tasted delicious and were aesthetically pleasing! By the way, all the drinks weren't for me, but I did get two drinks, and one of them was the Mango Dreamer (with Boba), and the other was Cheese Dragonfruit (with Boba as well)! From my family I heard the drinks were super tasty, and one of my cousins finished it quickly and they NEVER finish any of their drinks, so I thought it must've been good. I personally liked the mango drink more than the dragonfruit one because it was more refreshing and I'm a big fan of mango drinks! The dragonfruit drink was tasty, but I wish it was colder, but overall it was really good and tasted just like dragonfruit! Same with the mango, it tasted just like mango and you could taste the tea that was added. The boba was also very tasty and very soft and lucious!

Overall, it was a pretty fun experience and opportunity to try this place out. It was awesome spending our final day of vacation in the bay area and San Francisco. We started in Vegas and stayed for a month, went to San Diego for a few days, then Sacramento, and now it's coming to an end, and I feel like I ended it on a high note by trying Tea Hut!

Name: Boba & Beyond
Address: 3550 San Pablo Dam Rd a2, San Pablo
Author: Serena Reynolds
Review:
What a cute little place! Lots of geeky knick knacks to look at while you wait for your boba. They were super willing to customize my drink with the sugar levels and they also give a discount if you pay cash!

Overall nice place and nice people ᕦ⁠ʕ⁠ ⁠•⁠ᴥ⁠•⁠ʔ⁠ᕤ

Name: Boba & Beyond
Address: 3550 San Pablo Dam Rd a2, San Pablo
Author: Amanda Villalobos
Review: So happy that we live so close to this boba shop! Their boba is some of the best I have ever had. It is a little chewy on the outside, but soft on the inside. The drinks itself are tasty and the guys behind the counter are super friendly! It is heavily decorated in Pop Figures, so it is fun to look around while you wait for your item to be ready.

Name: Boba & Beyond
Address: 3550 San Pablo Dam Rd a2, San Pablo
Author: Ferdelle Lauren
Review: Hidden gem with delicious boba with the most toppings options i’ve ever seen on a menu! We also ordered the pandan waffle and it was served super hot, soft with a little crisp and extra tasty, like a yummy bowl of cereal. Boba was served quickly. Lots of fun things to look at too. It was like visiting a cousin’s place. Comfortable, low key, and you get taken care of. Thank you.

Name: Boba & Beyond
Address: 3550 San Pablo Dam Rd a2, San Pablo
Author: Noi L
Review: I got the Taro milk tea bobba. I like that they have quality milk 🥛 options & lactose free in addition to vegan milks. The topping to choose from bobba’s to assorts of jelly’s were not soggy & enjoy to chew bite after bite! Size of cups are generous for the price. This new gem place in El Sobrante will definitely be my to go place after my lunch or dinner for treat! The place is also clean & parking is easy access. Hope this help for those looking for a great bobba tea to enjoy!🥤💜

Name: Boba & Beyond
Address: 3550 San Pablo Dam Rd a2, San Pablo
Author: Bill Reichman
Review:
We had a very good milk teas with boba.  The boba was nice, fresh, and still warm.  It has a Pokemon theme for the decor, including card sales, which is quite different from some of the earlier pictures.  (The kids thought it was great.)

Note they accept cards, but give a discount for cash.

Name: Kiss My Boba
Author: Pranit Ravuri
Review:
Recommendations: Get the small or the large, the medium is the same as the small. Trust the staff with recommended sweetness, it is very balanced. Burnt brown sugar is solid, salted cheese foam not so much.

Pretty typical boba place for locals to hit up and get their tea fix. Can’t go wrong with coming here. There’s a great variety for everyone and some house specials that are worth adding to the “Have Tried” and “Nostalgia” foodie lists.

Don’t bother with the Taro, it is just the pre packaged powder and not fresh.

Service is excellent. Very responsive and engaging. Order was taken, prepped and in my hand in 10 minutes.

Name: Kiss My Boba
Author: Dylan G
Review: Interesting place, definitely a quirky entrepreneurial spirit at work in this one.  Tunes were blasting out of a giant LED-lit Dj speaker when I walked in, there was a stuffed animal arcade game in the corner that only accepted dollar bills, and I think the owner was outside taking a surfboard off of his Toyota.  The place was spotless.  I shared a Tongan Taro with milk tea, which was really good and I even went for the “Big Daddy” size…it was a lot, kind of like the Boba version of 7-11’s Big Gulp. Whimsical place with pretty good Boba.

Name: Kiss My Boba
Author: Emily
Review: ITS DISGUSTING. SAVE YOUR MONEY. Go literally anywhere else. I got the burnt brown sugar milk tea with boba and the drink itself was mid at best; it kind of gave me a stomachache. THE BOBA WAS HORRID. It wasn’t even tapioca I presume? That or it was extremely overcooked, hard, and difficult to chew. Absolutely disgusting, photo of the “boba” for reference it might be hard to tell the texture from the picture. It also barely came with any (in the picture I left the majority of what they gave), so even if it was actual tapioca, it didn’t have nothing.

Name: Kiss My Boba
Author: Aayushi Trivedi
Review: Tried Thai Milk Tea w/ boba & coffee jelly and Matcha Milk Tea w boba & mango popping boba. This place is definitely getting added to one of my few go-to spots for boba. The drinks were perfectly rich without them being too creamy or sweet. Bonus ⭐️ for the mango popping boba!!!

Name: Kiss My Boba
Author: Mafrey S
Review: Affordable price !!!! I love it so much ! Will definitely be back again. The staff was friendly too

Name: Happy Lemon Burlingame
Author: Theo Wiggin
Review: Super nice and helpful staff, the drinks were ready so fast! The tea and boba are pretty good, but the menu is slightly limited. The interior of this place is not the cutest, but there's lots of chairs. Very good location, right in the downtown.

Name: Happy Lemon Burlingame
Author: Julie Taeko
Review: The customer service was fantastic. We tried the Yakult and grapefruit drink at 25% sweetness and it was still a bit sweet but very interesting. We also tried the most popular drink of black oolong tea with cream cheese and that was the perfect amount of sweetness and bitterness.

Name: Happy Lemon Burlingame
Author: Abdul Rehman
Review:
Great place to grab a quick drink. They have a lot of variety. All the toppings are great. Wouldn’t suggest eating any snacks but depends on your preferences if you like em you like em 🤷🏻‍♂️ I didn’t.
But drinks overall, seating area and service was all on point!

Name: Happy Lemon Burlingame
Author: V Reddy
Review:
Another Boba drink place,
Parking on meter.
Service is good and fast.
Drinks are good but not the best.
Try mango green tea.
Whole theme is very good with stickers, advertisements, decoration with happy lemon

Name: Happy Lemon Burlingame
Author: College Restaurants
Review:
Shirley was great and accommodating!
She made my drink exactly how I like it where other places won’t even bother. Thank you Shirley!

Name: Inner Sunset - Little Sweet
Author: Simon Tan
Review: The mango / strawberry sago was just okay - kind of too sweet (ironically), with no option to customize using the digital ordering kiosk. The bubble waffle was also underwhelming.

Name: Inner Sunset - Little Sweet
Author: Matt B
Review: Very quick service bubble tea place, with some overly chewy boba and decent milk tea.

Name: Inner Sunset - Little Sweet
Author: Mel Nguyen
Review: We walked to Little Sweet from Golden Gate Park. This place has the best Winter Melon Gourd Milk Tea in SF. I would come back just to have it again. If you order a split cup they give you the drink in two half-filled cups. The drink was a good amount of creamy and tea. There's merch available if you want to represent. There's a broken glass area near the pick up that I hope they fix if it's legitimately broken. You'll be called/texted when your drink is available but until then they ask that you wait outside until your order is ready.

Name: Inner Sunset - Little Sweet
Author: Lyndon Zamora
Review: This place is great! Open during the pandemic and you can mobile order.  I had the Iron Buddha and it was strong. I felt this was a great value of a boba drink. The only thing I didn't like was the paper straws. Paper changes the taste of the drink.

Name: Inner Sunset - Little Sweet
Author: LLL
Review: Fabulous place with very friendly customer service. First time here today and found out they have a lots of drink I like. The lady named Cici helped me with my order. Super friendly customer service and helped me figure out what dink I would like the best. Got a orea shack and mango drink, and some other milk tea drinks, they all really fresh and delicious. Personal I recommend their fruit tea and chocolate egg puff. Highly recommended this location and will definitely come back again!

Name: Tpumps
Address: 985 E Hillsdale Boulevard, Metro Center Blvd, Foster City
Author: Meng Pang
Review: Very good milk tea and tea. Love it and there is free sample to try. Nice service. But no seat inside.

Name: Tpumps
Address: 985 E Hillsdale Boulevard, Metro Center Blvd, Foster City
Author: James
Review:
If you're here for a premium milk tea experience, you've come to the wrong place. But if you're looking for a cheap, sweet drink, consider Tpumps as they're known for using syrups and flavored teas.
