This Strawberry Bread pudding tasted of mostly bread, lacked butter, possibly salt, made a barely noticeable custard, and the whipped topping served adjacent to it made it palatable considering I gassed $10 for it. Its more so as if they just forgot about me I dunno. The way the entire place is decked out in "Laptops prohibited after 5pm", the fact that all users were being shuttled out, the entire experience souring my liking of the joint instead of discouraging laptop use or better giving people a more relaxed cutoff, it felt extremely strict. Almost as a bad as having a strict parent taking away your device to go to bed.

My appetite spoiled at the price/quality of my bread pudding and the atmosphere so I took off at that 5pm cutoff.

Name: The Social Study
Author: Andrew H.
Review:
I was so disappointed because this place has been on my list for so long. :( Thought this would be a great place to study because it’s quite literally in the name but they don’t allow laptops after 5pm.

Pros
- super cute environment
- honey latte was so yummy
- fair pricing, latte was $5.75

Cons
- they couldn’t do hot beverages even though they’re on the menu?
- not super friendly service, I had to ask to pay and they didn’t say thank you ( I did though :))
- no sign on the door that laptops aren’t allowed after 5pm. There was a tiny sign on the table, but was blocked until some people moved. The place was probably about 20% full so who cares?
- it smelled like cigarettes inside🚬
- ceiling had tiles and dust falling off of it

Name: The Social Study
Author: felicia
Review: I wanted to like this place for various reasons: location, drinks, bites, music vibe, aesthetics and, of course, Prince on the wall! 💜 After today’s visit, I don’t know. The overall vibe today was, “why is this Black lady in here?” All while playing the music of mostly Black entertainers. I don’t understand. Yes, I was the only Black patron. 🤷🏾‍♀️

Name: The Social Study
Author: Brian Osweiler
Review: We stopped in for a drink on a Sunday afternoon.  Great spot, nice atmosphere and very reasonable drink prices.  The food looked good, but we were short on time.  Definitely worth a stop if you’re in the area.

Name: The Social Study
Author: Аlisa Paludeti
Review: So I was strolling around Japantown and stumbled upon this cafe. Since I needed a caffeine fix, I decided to give it a try and ordered a cappuccino. The vibe of the place was awesome and the ambiance was on point. Although the cafe was pretty packed on a Saturday afternoon, I managed to grab a cute little table for myself. They also have bigger tables for groups if you need them. I loved the vintage decor on the walls - books, skateboards, and vinyls - it was a feast for the eyes! It was so chill just hanging out there and people-watching. Definitely planning to come back with a friend next time.

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Cody Watters
Review:
TL;DR - bad cups.

Here’s the thing about Peet’s coffee - it’s great. Underlying, the beans are wonderful, the atmosphere is great, and the environment is adequate. Will I buy the beans at the store? Sure. Do I love the freshly brewed Peet’s coffee at my office? Definitely. Will I grab a quick cup of coffee to go? Absolutely not.

Why?

The cups are awful - theres a seam on the lip of the cup that either does not have the proper adhesive or is just too damn big so the coffee creeps up out of the cup and forms a ring under the lid, then spills down the cup.

I get coffee all over my hands when I go here, I’ve had coffee leak onto my pants and onto my car seat, and it ruins the moment.

It’s wonderful coffee but please please fix the lids.

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Marcus Durano
Review:
It's disappointing to admit that Peet's has consistently let me down when it comes to my favorite drink, a Frappe. Although I've visited various locations across the city, this particular one is where I often come to do remote work. The order details are not complex or cryptic. Why is it that my order never matches what's on the label, especially when I always request extra chocolate drizzle? Picture this: you invite a friend over and kindly ask them to take off their shoes at the door. Despite regular visits, your friend keeps forgetting to do so. It can be quite frustrating and irritating, right? The fact that I've taken time out of my life to write this review is surprising to me.  But God willing, in all that is holy in the name of our lord and savior Jesus Christ. I HOPE they can get their act and skills together for a simple task. * Cue the Karan Scene* 'PUT SOME GOD DAMN EXTRA CHOCOLATE DRIZZLE ON MY ORDER!'

PS: They always run out of certain  consumables like cups and whip cream. Poor management of inventory.

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Rashad Ujaimi
Review: Really well made coffee, liked the pastry 🥐 very much, nice atmosphere too.

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Charles Levy
Review:
Service was extremely slow on a Sunday morning, took forever to get the chocolate croissant, I actually had to go back and ask where is my order,   After waiting..
Also they should get a better supplier for the baked goods..
Sad, as I really love the coffee..

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Victor Veiga
Review:
As a local I’ve been coming for years, great community of regulars
Great spot, to grab coffee and read/catch up on screen time, mid century modern vibes decor with greenery wood accents
Friendly staff but drink turnaround time can be really slow in light of heavy take out order flow
Everyone’s welcome here, so expect frequent homeless visits
Overall great community, inclusive and diverse spot, truly highlights the uniqueness and character of San Francisco

Name: Alem's Coffee
Author: Junior Luna
Review: Had lunch around 12PM on a Friday. Waited less than 10 minutes for my food and it was delicious. Ther3 was lots of available seating inside and outside. The Tibsy / Tsebhi sga was delicious.

Name: Alem's Coffee
Author: Natalie Kaucic
Review: This place is pure magic. I’ve been coming to Alem’s for a few years and finally tried branched out from the Ful to try the Fata. Needless to say it was incredibly and I should have branched out sooner! I don’t understand how food can taste so good. The folks behind the counter are the best and always earnestly want to know if you liked it. Come here after the farmers market on Sunday! You won’t regret it!

Name: Alem's Coffee
Author: Ton T
Review: This small coffee shop carries Eritrean dishes in addition to coffee, sandwiches and pastries. Adjacent to the DMV, the cafe is slightly off the popular Temescal shopping area. It’s clear that the folks here care about the quality and authenticity of their dishes. You should not expect silverware but plastic utensils are provided if requested. The cafe is small but the indoor/outdoor seating atmosphere was great. If you’re as wimpy as I am, you can also tell them to dial down the spiciness of the dish when ordering. Weekend crowds attest to their popularity.

Name: Alem's Coffee
Author: Harry Bernholz
Review: I left California a few months ago and Alem’s is probably the spot I miss the most. Walking here for delicious coffee or tibs/ful on Sundays was often the highlight of my week. Sweet people, great music. Hope to be back soon

Name: Alem's Coffee
Author: Alexander Boulgakov
Review:
Fantastic breakfast!!

We ordered the shihan ful and tibsy, and both were excellent. Very rich flavors, very fresh.

Good, strong coffee.

The owners were very kind, and the environment was very cozy. A very welcoming and lively atmosphere.

This is a must try place for breakfast.

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Ali
Review: It's crazy how one Peets can be so much better than another. This Peets, unfortunately, doesn't make the good list. It's super small and always busy. They always need to brew a batch of coffee and hardly ever have your kind ready to go. It only has a few good employees. The others almost always get the order wrong and change wrong, and don't care when you point out the mistake. Nothing compared to the Peets in my neighborhood.

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: DR_ MANHATTAN
Review: Good vibes and great tasting coffee. Here is another place to visit and try there products. Keep up the great work... Yours Truly, DR_MANHATTAN.

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Clau Guzman
Review: I come in early in the morning to pick up a delicious triple shot latte with almond milk. The staff is always kind and attentive. Also, I’ve been loving the cinnamon coffee cake.

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Ali
Review: The employee's Allways get orders wrong and don't apologize for mistakes. Good coffee horrible service.

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: S Duan
Review:
I tried the oat milk cold brew latte, honestly, I wasn’t able to taste any coffee. I would not buy again for almost $6.

This store itself is very busy and is a little messy. Specifically, they just sweep the trash/dirt to hide them underneath their table for sugar/milk. The restroom could be cleaner.

The store is run by very young staffs, and they seem to be very busy chatting among themselves.

Name: Martha & Bros. Coffee Co.
Author: Amal Ben
Review: Really enjoyed my visit here! The coffee looks amazing, and their tiramisu is delicious. The staff is super friendly, making the experience even better. Definitely worth a visit!

Name: Martha & Bros. Coffee Co.
Author: Tee Ponsukcharoen
Review: A true local, affordable, and good coffee shop is a place you see people from all walks of life come to enjoy the coffee before heading to work, playing sudoku, or talk about life. This is the place. The coffee and the bakery are good with a wide range of selections. What stands out the most for me is the communal vibe of this place. Would love to spend more time there and try more pastries.

Name: Martha & Bros. Coffee Co.
Author: Susana Chhetri
Review: This place reminds me of how beautiful my country and general Latin vibes are, the coffee, food and music , arriba MI Gente, love it.

Name: Martha & Bros. Coffee Co.
Author: Alan Hellawell
Review: Super-friendly barista. Beans were not very fresh or high quality, judging from my cappuccino.

Name: Martha & Bros. Coffee Co.
Author: Adrian Lunsong
Review:
(Detailed review below)
Food - ⭐⭐⭐⭐
Accessibility - ⭐⭐
Ambiance - ⭐⭐⭐⭐
Cost - ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
Service - ⭐⭐⭐⭐

Food - Great selection of cakes which drew me here! Some good options for cold sandwiches and they also do some hot bagels. Good coffee too served either hot or cold. Food and drinks are served in takeaway cups/bags, plates are offered for sandwiches etc.

Accessibility - Doors are propped opened for easy access, no steps (everything is on ground floor), standard restrooms (not accessible friendly), sitting areas are quite cram so wheelchair users will need to take seats along the walking area.

Ambiance - Light and easy; big windows letting in loads of natural light (great for people watching too), casual and relaxed atmosphere. Clean and tidy. Some nice homely decor. Warm and inviting :-)

Cost - Roughly between $2.50 and $4.50 for coffee (type and size dependent), cakes are the same too. Pretty good value for money.

Tips - No table service, so just head straight to the counter and order your food/drinks. Have a nosy around though as there are cakes before and after the order point so I missed seeing some options. Free Wi-Fi available. And good speed.

Service - Quick and fast. Staff are friendly and smiling!

Name: Cafe Réveille
Author: Emily Chen
Review:
One of my favorite spots in Duboce! The food is pretty good - my favorite is the kale / mushroom bowl with chicken for lunch and their chicken pasta for dinner.

Great outdoors space. Friendly staff. WiFi only available during weekdays. It’s usually hard to find a spot to sit so I don’t usually plan to work or eat there unless I get there super early

Name: Cafe Réveille
Author: Bobby Miklaušić
Review: Wanted a healthy protein filled bowl. Got a healthy protein filled bowl. Great produce. Nice sharp dressing on the veggies. Delicious slightly spicy sauce on the poached eggs. Solid. And it's on really pretty, quiet street with a lovely outdoor dining situation.

Name: Cafe Réveille
Author: Gemma Boothroyd
Review:
Cute, buzzy spot with plenty of patio area to enjoy your coffee in the sun! Super friendly service, and high-quality espresso too.

Plenty of folks on their laptops - but no outlets here, as a forewarning!

Name: Cafe Réveille
Author: Michele Losse
Review: We went there twice for breakfast, as we were staying in the area. The first time, we ordered a white coffee and a latte. The girl taking the orders asked us what kind of coffee we wanted, they had a choice of two, I ended up with 2 coffees! When I queried it and asked for a refund for the extra, they couldn't do it, possibly because it's a UK card. The girl in charge couldn't do it either and as she was busy, said she would do it later. It would have been nice if she'd asked for some personal details to confirm this, I still don't know if the refund went through or if they even tried! The croissants were OK, not the best I ever had but not bad. The next day I had the coconut bowl which was weird, it was mostly frozen coconut, with a very light sprinkle of granola, a very light sprinkle of cocoa nibs and fresh fruit! Never had ice cream for breakfast before! At 15 dollars I thought it was well overpriced for what I got. My husband enjoyed his eggs and potatoes.

Name: Cafe Réveille
Author: Emma Dolcini
Review: We got an iced americano and a oat milk macha latte. The thing I love most about this cafe is that it’s open until 7:30pm. It’s busy in the morning and in the evening. Perfect place to come and do some work or relax after dinner.

Name: Enter The Cafe
Author: ADLbh
Review:
Great coffee shop in Chinatown. They have a lot of options for drinks and snacks.
Amazing avocado egg toast!

Name: Enter The Cafe
Author: P R
Review: Got a great breakfast here. I ordered the avocado toast with egg and fruits. Looked amazing and tasted very delicious. Perfect place to start the day! Very lovely and nice service, too.

Name: Enter The Cafe
Author: carol velasco
Review: I've found my new Favorite place to eat lunch, relax after lunch, patiently wait for friend, etc etc. Can order thru electronic kiosk or nice, helpful, young lady. ..So far I've tried 2 egg sandwiches & Purple Rice Yogurt-AWESOME-hear it Boosts Energy. I love that it's a relaxing corner café w wide windows to hear the world, but not be part of it.

Name: Enter The Cafe
Author: HaNg
Review: Great service. Really nice people and great food to match.

Name: Enter The Cafe
Author: Nico Gorbal
Review: The boba milk tea was alright, not the best not the worst. It’s basically a black tea+milk+boba. The stuff seemed pretty busy, took 15 min to get my tea.

Name: Foghorn Coffee
Address: 900 North Point St Suite E204B, San Francisco
Author: Junior Marín
Review: The chocolate + croissant experience was absolutely amazing I can’t even describe it, my soul returned to my body, the barista was a girl, did a good job.

Name: Foghorn Coffee
Address: 900 North Point St Suite E204B, San Francisco
Author: Sierra Warren
Review: Ordered a flat white. Coffee was bold and flavorful. Milk could have been frothed a little more for texture and heat. Great location by Ghirardelli

Name: Foghorn Coffee
Address: 900 North Point St Suite E204B, San Francisco
Author: Abbie Lu
Review: Much better coffee options then inside Ghirardelli. They had more milk alternatives and sugar free flavor. It was not the best but decent and a much better option for the area. I recommend it over the coffee inside. The barista is more skilled as well.

Name: Foghorn Coffee
Address: 900 North Point St Suite E204B, San Francisco
Author: Max Heegard
Review: Good coffee but the barista has got to be the rudest service person I’ve had the displeasure of ordering from in recent memory.  It’s a shame you are forced to tip before your coffee is served because I wouldn’t have tipped this guy after the fact.  I don’t want to elaborate he was just extremely cold and rude.  If you don’t like your job, that’s ok, find something else.

Name: Foghorn Coffee
Address: 900 North Point St Suite E204B, San Francisco
Author: Nelda Fernandez
Review: We started walking to Pier 39 early in the morning, and this was one of the only places open for coffee. I ordered a latte, and it was probably the one of the best I've had. I'm actually really surprised to see the ratings are not higher than what they are. And the location is perfect. You can stroll around the square then head over to Pier 39.

Name: Contender Coffee
Author: Patricia Pan
Review:
Such a gem!! Love the layout and love how friendly Jessica is.

Also, every Tuesday from 7am to 1pm, the food is 50% for Taste Test Tuesday. All baked goods, hot foods, even the overnight oats are 50% off but smoothies and other drinks are still full price.

Definitely want to come back and try new things!!

I had the Alfredo sandwich, which is on piazza blanco (mix between ciabatta and focaccia) which was incredibly delicious.

The bialy and overnight oats and the lemon poppyseed muffin tops are on my shortlist for next time!

Name: Contender Coffee
Author: Summer Johnson
Review: I stopped by after work and was pleasantly surprised :-). I passed this place a day before going to a different coffeeshop and decided to come back and check it out. The menu is pretty hefty with all of the customary coffee drinks, but they also have smoothies with protein offerings. They hold boxing 🥊 classes here, hence the name of the shop. Usually places that multitask like this let the coffee fall by the wayside, but the barista recommended a drink he put on the menu that day, a peanut butter cup latte, and it was really good! Hope he keeps it on the menu. Anyways, this place had a nice friendly vibe and staff was kind. The shop is open and airy and I will definitely be a regular.

Name: Contender Coffee
Author: Nini “Nini” Salinas
Review: I keep forgetting about this gem in the neighborhood and how convenient that there is also a boxing gym within this place also! Great coffee. Great staff. People People must try it and come in! All the natural sunlight that peeks through their windows in the AM is so amazing. Love the ambiance! You get amazing coffee and a work out 💪🏽 if you choose

Name: Contender Coffee
Author: SF Shopper
Review: Great coffee and super friendly staff. They have a boxing gym here that I’m considering trying out. It’s clean and overall welcoming here. Doesn’t hurt that the coffee is good.

Name: Contender Coffee
Author: Julia Schaefer
Review: The coffee at Contender is delicious! So excited to see that they serve Thanksgiving coffee, the pioneer of Artisan roasted coffee. Delicious cappuccino, smoothies and very sweet staff. Next time, I'm going to sign up for a boxing class, too! Highly recommend :)

Name: CoffeeShop
Author: Zhuo Z
Review:
This is quite an amazing coffee place. They have coffee beans all around the world and all freshly roasted. Love that Thai breakfast cold latte drink. The sweetness of the banana with the touch of spices is so perfectly balanced.

They use melted Belgian Chocolate for Mocha!

Their cruffin with raspberry jam is amazing. The cruffin comes with a crust that crumbles and melts in the mouth.

Also love the muffin shaped pastry with an egg inside (I think the name is devil inside?). Very tasty but a tad bit too much baking soda.

Name: CoffeeShop
Author: Vicky Vuong
Review:
The coffee shop is a bit hard to find, probably because its name is literally coffeeshop, even Google is confused. However, when you find it, it is a cute little neighborhood cafe that serves your standard espresso drinks. Coffee was tasty.

They also serve pastries, those were fresh. I got a croissant which was really nice.

There is some limited seating outside, but overall the cafe is quite small.

Would recommend.

Name: CoffeeShop
Author: Falling Action Films, Inc.
Review: Parking is difficult, but if you get a chance well worth the effort. Had my Oat milk latte and it was delicious. Also had an almond croissant and it was very fresh and tasty.

Name: CoffeeShop
Author: Mikhail Sidorov
Review: Great coffee. I love the fact that they didn’t make a big deal when I asked for ceramic plate and mug. So rare nowadays! Definitely recommend.

Name: CoffeeShop
Author: Katherine Chen
Review: Dirty Matcha (espresso + matcha) is great! Love it😍 Pastries also look good and would also like to try some next time!

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee
Author: Hecvi V
Review: Blue Bottle is one of my favorite coffee shops in San Francisco. I always order the NOLA with oat milk, either hot or cold depending on the weather, and it’s delicious. The service is always great. The Ferry Building location is my favorite because it stays open later.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee
Author: Lavinia Pedrollo
Review:
The guys were so funny and nice! They made a special cappuccino for me (see the funny pic) and made my day!
Coffee is a bit expensive for the cup size, but hey! Coffee is really good. :))

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee
Author: Bab Bab
Review: The origin place of Blue bottle cafe. It was aquite small cafe. With long history, there is heritage of San Francisco. In fact, there are Blue Bottle cafes all over the city, but if you like Blue Bottle, it's worth visiting at least once. In the evening, if you walk around the pier behind the cafe with coffee, the night view across the sea is wonderful. You can drink coffee and take a look around. It's a great place with a story.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee
Author: G Santana
Review: Good cappuccino but the chocolate croissant not so great….

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee
Author: Gosia Ozga
Review: Great coffee choices while you wait for a ferry! I was a bit bored and walked around and saw this coffee place! It was so nice and simple! As a matcha lover I got one for myself… I took it to go! The coffee art was so cute! They really make a good matcha there!

Name: CoffeeShop
Author: Eileen Lau
Review: Small specialty coffee shop which has some unique drinks, merchandise, and pastries. There are a few tables and chairs outside. My friend got a matcha drink and I got an iced Americano. My Americano was on the acidic side, and quite strong. Prices are on the high end

Name: CoffeeShop
Author: Samantha Lau
Review: small, simple coffee shop as the name suggests. they have some pastries and the service is quick and friendly.

Name: CoffeeShop
Author: Alan Hellawell
Review: Very pleasantly quirky venue and barista. Fantastically rich 8 oz cappuccino. I also enjoyed the cookie.

Name: CoffeeShop
Author: Donny Pusawong
Review:
On Sunday mornings, I try to go out in the mornings and support local coffee shops.  I use my Nespresso machine on most days now!   I usually order just a espresso, a coffee when I'm outside.  I ordered the hot chocolate, Latte, and a espresso!It was too early for iced coffee w/coffee ice cubes!  Bulletproof coffee or Sherpa sounds too rich in fat for me!  Not bad espresso and a good latte. Great neighborhood spot.
Cute small shop and friendly and hard working batista!  Easy parking in front on a Sunday morning.

Name: CoffeeShop
Author: Daniela Nuñez Zurita
Review: Lovely little neighborhood coffee shop. They have great coffee and tea options as well as amazing pastries. Their baristas are very friendly and super helpful. Great spot to grab a coffee while sitting outside and enjoying a sunny Mission day.

Name: Coffee Bar Montgomery
Author: Kay Bee
Review: I warning eh area and needed a latte. While there are many cafes in the area, I do love their coffee. Service was quick and they have outdoor seating if you want.

Name: Coffee Bar Montgomery
Author: Farshad P
Review:
The coffee here is very good, you can tell everything is measured and carefully made. You can also taste it in the final product too. I’ve been getting the iced latte (rotating between their house made caramel or vanilla syrups) for over a year and it’s consistently been great each time and never varies.

It’s one of the best coffee in FiDi for sure!

Name: Coffee Bar Montgomery
Author: Ted Eicher
Review: To start the coffee beans are $27 per pound. That is a big price for mediocre coffee. Bought a half pound of Neapolitan beans, featuring a medium dark roast. Flavor notes say dark chocolate,  hazelnut and caramel. Couldn't detect any of those flavor notes even remotely. Made the coffee at home using a Baratza Encore grinder and a Bodum French Press. Really lacking any richness or body. Tasting it the one word that came to mind was ordinary. Contacted the owner BEFORE trying the beans about their return policy. She explained to me it could be possible on a case by case basis, but would not accept a return just because you don't like the flavor profile. There in lies the problem, to me this coffee doesn't have a flavor profile. Coffee Bar uses Mr. Espresso beans which are fire roasted. Philz Coffee is way better. Caffe Trieste is 50 times better. So now I'm stuck with a half pound of beans I paid over $13 for and I don't even want to drink it. I do not recommend the Coffee Bar on Montgomery Street

Name: Coffee Bar Montgomery
Author: Leslie Wu
Review:
4 stars only because the hours are wrong and we’ve gone multiple times only to be burned by it being closed. It’s almost always closed when we go, and we are craving a coffee fix so we have to turn to the nearby blue bottle, so I’ve decided to add the new hours here for others. Although it is no longer summer, these hours seem to be the correct ones, hopefully things don’t change again 🤔

Otherwise, it’s great coffee and I’d highly recommend!!

Name: Coffee Bar Montgomery
Author: Thomas L
Review: My go-to coffee shop whenever I work from the office. I usually order a cappuccino, and sometimes I'll add an order of cold brew to drink the next day lol. The coffee is consistently good. Note: if you order your cold brew without ice, there's an extra charge of $1. Staff is friendly. I wish they'd bring back the punch cards :)

Name: Fresh Brew Coffee
Author: Austin (Amache)
Review: Food was great, had a ham egg and cheese croissant. Friend had a turkey egg and cheese bagel and said it was great as well. Drinks were very good as well, iced Chai latte and a hot caramel latte. Price is beyond fair and the service was great. Will definitely be back.

Name: Fresh Brew Coffee
Author: Akiko In SD
Review:
Delicious Vietnamese sandwiches here. Try the falafel avocado banh mi - it's amazing! My friend loved the pork one too.
Limited seating inside and out. We usually grab takeout and enjoy our sandwiches up on California Street by the cathedral. Great food with a view!

Name: Fresh Brew Coffee
Author: Yuen Yee Bianca Cheng
Review: Dropped by to get a pork sandwich and a cheese veggie bagel, for drinks we get a latte and a smoothie. Everything tastes so well and the stuff came out super quick. The place is full of people and you could see the letters and notes from previous customers. The price is very reasonable, around $12 for the bagel and the smoothie, $11 for the sandwich and the latte. Definitely recommend it to people living nearby.

Name: Fresh Brew Coffee
Author: David Basler
Review: Great local find in the neighborhood! We shared a couple Vietnamese sandwiches (the veggie Banh Mi was fantastic!) and a coffee drink then headed back out on our way to explore the city. Definitely a locals spot—two different customers came in and said “I’ll take the usual” 😉

Name: Fresh Brew Coffee
Author: Kira Sano
Review: Great quick service, nice indoor and outdoor seating, great coffee, amazing Banh Mi with pate (soooo good!) and great location. I got a cheap coffee and sandwich for great price.

Name: Bicycle Coffee
Author: Mahsa Rouhani
Review: The customer service was top-notch. The staff were incredibly knowledgeable and the lady with the purple hair was super nice and friendly. I've tried countless coffees in the Bay Area, but their Espresso Blend is hands-down one of the best I've ever tasted. The quality is exceptional, and the flavor is rich and satisfying. I've already bought a bag to enjoy at home, and I can't wait to come back for more. If you're looking for premium coffee beans to make your own espresso at home, their Espresso Blend is a must-try.

Name: Bicycle Coffee
Author: Oleg Vakhitov
Review: Bicycle Coffee is one of my favorite coffee shops - it has a simple interior that is combined with a roasting shop. You can see for yourself how the magic of roasting coffee happens. Good baristas who know how to make delicious coffee work here. And Espresso coffee beans will allow you to make great coffee in your kitchen in your espresso machine.

Name: Bicycle Coffee
Author: cece art
Review:
The cashier wrote a profanity on my drink and I don’t feel safe drinking this.
This is the second time this cashier has done something sketchy to my food. Unprompted.

Name: Bicycle Coffee
Author: Matthew MacCormack
Review:
My go-to coffee shop in the Jack London area. Great espresso drinks, cold brew, and pastries. Big fan of the vanilla oat milk latte and the ham & gruyere croissant. The new location is nice and modern, with plenty of seating. I love the menu board with visualizations of each drink option.

Recently had a great customer service experience as well. I'd bought a pound of ground espresso to use at home. When I got home, I found that it had been ground on the wrong setting. I returned to see if I could get it re-ground; not only did they give me a new pound (correctly ground) for free, but they also let me keep the old pound and gave me a free cold brew for my troubles. Shout out Jackie for the great customer service!

Name: Bicycle Coffee
Author: Crystal
Review:
12/2021 UPDATE 🚴🏽‍♀️
Still my go to coffee shop. I don’t need points or rewards to compromise where I go for coffee. I come so frequent I could work here. I’ve purchased multiple times, from coffee, beans, and merchandise. Even come on the weekends when I can. The owners, staff, customers all know quality.

For a month at my new job I always thought this was a bicycle shop until I walked over and saw it was a coffee shop that delivers via bicycle! How rad is that?

The perks: EVERYTHING

Service = A1
User friendly menu = words with pictures, fool proof
Milk options = no added cost!
Coffee = delicious

Happy camper, right here.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee
Author: Austin Zhuang
Review:
This Blue Bottle location was and is a prime example of why I will be careful about going back to this location. It’s located in a prime location in Downtown Berkeley which is quite convenient from the BART location, so I figured to go here and get my work done.

Here was my experience thus far at the Berkeley location:

(1) The first thing that irked me big time was the fact that they didn’t have outlets… let alone blocked outlets that you don’t allow guests to use. No wonder that’s why there was such less foot traffic than all other local coffee shops in our area…
The atmosphere of the coffee shop was absolutely unwelcoming too. Chairs were just the right size but tables were too close — I didn’t have enough personal space to get my work done and it was annoying having to tend to protecting my things because of the fact that the staff wasn’t going to do anything to let these homeless people stay or leave.

(2) The service was just very unwelcoming - it felt rushed and I had to force myself to smile through the ordering process of things. I was constantly looked down at the baristas like I was being surveillanced, and to be honest, it made me feel really uncomfortable.

(3) Food wise: the iced NOLA with whole milk was actually a delicacy. It wasn’t bad and the equilibrium between coffee and milk was right just there.

Overall, i don’t know if I’ll be back honestly.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee
Author: Mina Khan
Review: very good coffee ! clean and cozy. not enough outlets if you want to sit there and work on laptop tho

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee
Author: R L (Froyo)
Review: A nice place to chill and chat with friends. Apparently their coffee now goes with oat milk by default, which works perfectly for me.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee
Author: Adrian Moise
Review:
☕️🌞 Berkeley Bliss: Sipping Sunshine at Blue Bottle Coffee 🌇🥛

My recent visit to Blue Bottle Coffee in Berkeley, California, was a delightful rendezvous with caffeinated goodness. But before we dive into the details, did you know that Berkeley's charm often lies in its corner cafes, inviting you to soak in the Californian sun? 🌞

Now, let's spill the beans on our Blue Bottle experience:
Aesthetic Ambiance:
🏞️ Corner Perfection: Nestled in a good old corner location, the café was a picturesque escape.
🪟 A Flood of Natural Light: The abundant windows let the Californian sunshine pour in, creating an inviting atmosphere.
Coffee Chronicles:
🥛 Oat Milk Extravaganza: For the coffee connoisseurs who favour oat milk, this place has your back.
📡 Wi-Fi Wonders: Open Wi-Fi was a boon, and the landing page greeted us with tempting discounts. Perfect for those who want to pair their coffee with a bit of work or leisure.
🌐 The Need for Speed: On the flip side, the Wi-Fi speed left something to be desired, clocking in at 4.3Mbps for download and 0.3Mbps for upload. Streaming might be a tad sluggish here.
🎶 Jazzy Vibes: An upbeat, jazzy soundtrack played in the background, creating a pleasant ambiance without drowning out conversation.

In conclusion, my time at Blue Bottle Coffee in Berkeley was a sunny affair. With an excellent location, a cascade of natural light, and oat milk for your java, it's a charming spot to sip and savour. Just don't forget to bring your own honey and cinnamon if you like to sweeten the deal🍯🍂.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee
Author: Terri Wong
Review:
My go to place for drip coffee filters (best engineered!) and gifts for coffee nerd friends (including me😊)

Add a croissant or cookie to sweeten the gift 🎁

The space: open and inviting, good for friends catch up or business talk.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee
Author: Sakura Momoko
Review: I really love the matcha latte here! It has a very fluffy and rich texture, much better than the ones I’ve had in London so far. The only downside is the WiFi seems super slow, and there are no plugs for any digital devices.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee
Author: Charleen Firlus
Review: The lattes are decent but they don't offer decaf here. Service does a good job at attempting to best meet your needs with their limited menu. Prices are cheaper than not but coffee shops generally in SF also don't stay open late, even on the weekend.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee
Author: R L (Froyo)
Review:
For some reason I feel like the latte tastes less good than other blue bottle stores in the area. The wait time is also longer, this place is also more crowded and messier. Pastries and matcha latte are great as usual.

Update: so I visited this store two weeks later and was super disappointed. First, when I told the staff that I wanted a scone but I saw a fly on it (it was literally lying on the scone), and she just carelessly replied “oh, sorry” and didn’t do anything about it. Then I ordered a matcha latte which was also carelessly prepared and there’s no latte art or anything (excuse me why would I pay $7 for something like that?). After I finished and headed out, the scone with the fly was still there. I’m never gonna come back.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee
Author: Tatyana Fernandes
Review: Very tasty waffle with pearl sugar mmm and avocado toasts also really nice ( soft and crunchy bread). Quite vibe good for work or meeting

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee
Author: Nathan Kam
Review: What's not to love about Blue Bottle? Great coffee, excellent pastries and sandwiches and friendly service. Love the atmosphere here too, and if you have a dog, there's some complimentary treats for them in the back area. 🤙🏼🤙🏼🤙🏼

Name: The Coffee Movement
Author: priyanka pola
Review: Absolutely love this place. It's best coffee experience. I tried the cocoa cappuccino. The staff was very welcoming even during a busy hour. The place is the turn point for Powell-Hyde and Powell-Mason. There is little space outside the Cafe to sit and enjoy the coffee and view. I am going to go there again to try the coffee flight.

Name: The Coffee Movement
Author: E Bo
Review: The coffee flight was amazing, and excellent service. There is no shade, no umbrellas, chairs, or inside seating. If it were raining or cold, or I didn't want full sun, I would have to go elsewhere since I like to relax and enjoy my coffee. With such wonderful coffee, a few chairs and umbrellas would be nice.

Name: The Coffee Movement
Author: Jaleh Moyse
Review: We had a slight wait to get our coffees, but it was worth it. I opted for the oat milk latte, which was a solid choice—smooth, creamy, and well-priced. The employees were also very polite, making the experience even better.

Name: The Coffee Movement
Author: Vicky C.
Review:
Got the Cocoa cappuccino and matcha latte with honey lavender syrup around early May.
I like that they have seasonal menu.
The place is really small and not indoor to stand or sit. As someone who isn’t huge about coffee, I would only stop by if you are nearby and no lines.

Name: The Coffee Movement
Author: Nichole Alex Ibarra Fernandez
Review: Amazing coffee, super friendly baristas. Tried the tasting flight then came back the next day to get the filter coffee. I love trying specialty coffee shops and this place is really one of the best. They also have a good selection of coffee beans, I got a bag from DAK. The barista was really helpful about brewing options and suggestions.

Name: Cafe Bean
Author: Harue Seki
Review: Great little coffee shop. Good coffee, very nice owner. You can tell a lot about a person by how they treat their very old and blind doggie. This little guy was so well loved. 10/10 small business will come again.

Name: Cafe Bean
Author: Mandy Middlewood
Review: I used to come here pretty often for breakfast during my university days. Best damn oatmeal and yogurt bowls. Owner always fun and welcoming. Glad the place is still here! Leaving a review in honor of all the times spent here ❤️

Name: Cafe Bean
Author: Jeff
Review: Awesome cozy and homey cafe, we ended up stumbling across it and we are so glad we did. We ended up going in for coffee and food. Food was amazing the veggie pesto melt was really good and the drink selection and coffee was also amazing as well. Not sure if the man who took our order was the owner but he was very welcoming and very kind as well. Will definitely come back whenever we are back!

Name: Cafe Bean
Author: Matthew MacLaurin
Review: Chester is a tentpole of the community here in Sutter village. This funky cozy corner shop is so welcoming and homey it’s like your cool uncles place with comics. The bagel with eggs, cheese, and fresh sauteed spinach and mushrooms is unmatched in the city for freshness and quality. The coffee is old school dark roast brewed strong - hard to find these days. Stay a while and you’ll catch the daily vibe of life here. Amazing.

Name: Cafe Bean
Author: Alex Shafer
Review: Displaced Joe's customer looking for a new regular cafe in the neighborhood - this place would be an okay replacement except it seems like the owners are using half the dining room as a storage locker. There were 4 other customers when I stopped in and the place felt full because of all the storage.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee
Author: Seungho Lee
Review:
I recently visited Blue Bottle Coffee at 705 Market St and found it to be just okay. The ambiance is modern and clean, which is nice, but the overall experience didn’t quite live up to the hype. The coffee was decent, but not particularly impressive compared to other specialty coffee spots in the city.

The staff was friendly, and the service was efficient, which I appreciated. However, the coffee itself lacked the robust flavor and depth I was expecting from a place with such a strong reputation. It’s a convenient location if you’re in the area and need a caffeine fix, but I wouldn’t go out of my way to visit again.

Overall, an average experience – good, but not great.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee
Author: Ursula S.
Review: Stopping by Blue Bottle on my way to work is my favorite way to start the day. The drinks are always excellent and the staff truly elevates the experience, with Raul and Damon standing out for their exceptional service and darling latte art. I always leave feeling energized and satisfied, and I highly recommend this location to anyone looking for a top-notch coffee experience!

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee
Author: Ben Park
Review: Great coffee. Enjoyed their NOLA shakerato. A bit disappointed with their affogato though. I remember it being better. Still tasty, but wasn’t blown away like I did years ago when I first tried it.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee
Author: Jesper Rasmussen
Review: Seems like a bit over hyped place.  The decor is modern with a Scandinavian feel, but seems like a place people just come to be seen.  There is almost no place to sit down.  The service was okay, a little slow, and the size of the portions does not quite match the price.  The quality of the coffee is average, not the best coffee in town, but far from the worst.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee
Author: A M
Review:
They mistook the order.
I asked if they have low fat milk and they said yes so I ordered latte with low fat milk without ice, but they were using whole milk.
Also, I asked not to change the amount of milk to cover the space of ice(I ordered iced latte without ice) but they pour milk to the maximum volume of the cup.
The store wasn’t that clean unfortunately.
It’s unfortunate that cafe in the US is not clean even in Blue Bottle Coffee.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee
Author: Ads
Review:
I was told that the blue bottle chain is the OG, godfather and almighty mecha of SFO coffee. Wow, this joint did not disappoint! Flawless blend, phenomenal flavor and incredible vibes and tones, unbelievable and one of the best experiences I've had, period.
I love this chain, it's truly super yummy coffee and has humble grass roots lifeblood which I can really get behind. I'd happily chug the cold brew cans all day, every day and unbelievably addicted.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee
Author: Tzruya Calvão Chebach
Review: Perfectly spacious and fun urban spot, great coffee, lovely blueberry cake, super friendly staff, opens early, a great and relaxed spot for a couple of hours of work on your computer

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee
Author: R L (Froyo)
Review: Came here to buy their instant coffee and ordered a latte and a hot Nola. Honestly the Nola tastes better with ice. Staff here are super friendly.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee
Author: Sergey Tiraspolsky
Review: Nice location. Cappuccino was good. It was a bit warm as if A/C wasn't working.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee
Author: Steve Murdock
Review:
Packed house, but the staff is very efficient. Most ppl get coffee to go, so seating is available if you want to chill out.

The coffee and food is amazing also, but most ppl already know this about blue bottle.

Name: Philz Coffee
Author: Ben Park
Review: Used to love drinking Philz Iced Mint Mojito when I lived in San Francisco. Visiting the city close to a decade later, it’s still pretty amazing. Really enjoyed this delightful drink. Unfortunately, this location is located fairly close to some dangerous blocks within the city.

Name: Philz Coffee
Author: keith k
Review:
I really appreciated the staff and vibe of this Philz located near the Civic Center. The cafe was pretty full and there was always a line; the staff here did a really good job welcoming in customers and giving each customer awesome service at the register and at the hand-off.

I will definitely be back! I ordered a dancing water iced coffee and it did not disappoint!

Name: Philz Coffee
Author: Eddie Valadez
Review: I am not sure where my initial review of Philz on Golden Gate is located but it's worth a second review. The location on Golden Gate is near my favorite sandwich spot (Saigon Sandwich). This location is always busy I think because of the Federal building. This location is fast, busy, and energetic. The staff members are all very friendly and customer service oriented. I love coffee shops at this capacity and vibrancy.

Name: Philz Coffee
Author: Leo Hidalgo
Review: The coffee was fantastic, but the cinnamon roll and the burrito were just ok! Great service and atmosphere. I even bought a bag of coffee to go!

Name: Philz Coffee
Author: Irene Jang
Review: I came into this café not expecting much. But I ordered the rose coffee and wow. The floral taste was very strong but it was really GOOD that I thought maybe I should have ordered a medium and not a small. I’m looking forward to trying all the flavors there! The store is decorated beautifully and it’s nice and cozy. The store is very spacious and also includes working outlets and wifi! There are plenty of seats for the customers and is nicely air conditioned. The workers were all so very kind :) The only bad thing I would point out is that the bathroom is extremely dirty but other than that, this will be my go-to coffee spot!

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee
Author: Barath T
Review: Wonderful taste for coffee, unique taste and smell for ones that are very used to starbucks, a much better alternative to regulars., doesn't have a proper place to dine in but there are a couple of benches outside., its in a very smaller street so park on the near by roadside (have decent paid parking spots).

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee
Author: Foo T
Review:
Their first brick and mortar location!
They are becoming famous in Japan among coffee enthusiasts (I’m not one of them) and I went there so that I have something to talk about.  Didn’t know much about them but their coffee was delicious!!
I had winter blend, my friend got Nola or spiced nola.  Both were surprisingly good, no wonder they are popular!

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee
Author: Nicole Nazario
Review: Very cute hidden location. Barista did a good job :)

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee
Author: Norma Jean Actress (KillersoftheFlowerMoon)
Review: Amazing coffee! The cops go here so you know it’s good! Went there today. And oh, look at their featured flower arrangement when you visit, today’s was stunning! I ordered a Cappucino with Almond Milk - it was stellar.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee
Author: matteo vianello
Review: This kiosk on Linden has been our go-to coffee place in the neighborhood since it opened. Has the quality of the coffee gotten worse since the acquisition by Nestle? Perhaps. Has that affected the vibe in this alleyway? Not really. The baristas come and go, but the place remains a fixture of the neighborhood experience.

Name: Cafe Suspiro
Author: David Barwick
Review:
Organic food options!!

Love the vibe at Suspiro with vintage records and tapes! Definitely worth a trip. Lots of o80’s memories in there. Great tasting coffee with some tasty snacks thrown in for good measure. They had a decently diverse selection of foods and snacks that even included an organic salad.

Definitely check Suspiro out!

Name: Cafe Suspiro
Author: Brad Weisman
Review: Exceptionally cool little place with great pulls and great vibes. Raul has great energy and has clearly put a lot of love into this combo coffee shop + record store!

Name: Cafe Suspiro
Author: Sarah Nelson
Review: I was helped by someone I assume was the owner and they couldn't have been nicer! Love the vibe of this tiny shop and will definitely return. A SoMa gem!

Name: Cafe Suspiro
Author: Muhammad Rizky Harun
Review: Good Coffee. There also lots of classic records for sale

Name: Cafe Suspiro
Author: Madonna Crouse
Review: The barista makes an excellent espresso and the service is excellent! I bought a really good book while I waited and sat on their sidewalk seating while I enjoyed my new book & espresso. This is the kind of local business that makes San Francisco great!

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: john “Optionworld 2000” smith
Review: The Peet's Coffee on Van Ness Ave is quite, safety and relaxing. Good customer, next to the bookstore, and other restaurants. Relaxation to drink coffee and reading book, customer are mostly middle ages(elderly) in safety, and several restaurants to eat by taking on many bus. Couple and elderly occupied inside/outside at the bookstore.But the workers might be not be friendly in their short conversation. Anyway, good and fast service. Any hours, always more than 4 people entering the store. Few seats, but more relaxing without sometimes no music.

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Shawn P. M.
Review:
Ordered a large drip coffee, which I wish was a bit hotter.  Service was quick and friendly.

Unlike some other nearby coffee shops, I could pour my own milk / cream, rather having to ask a staff member to do that.

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Shelby Wildeman
Review:
The service was good. They seem to be understaffed here but that is probably management trying to make a bigger profit.
The two people at the counter were working extremely hard and quick and still had time for a smile. They were extremely friendly and professional.

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Miles Sergeant
Review: The Waffle Breakfast Sandwich is wonderful  with maple syrup. K8 hi79i

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Rae Marie
Review: Always a friendly smile and great coffee

Name: K's Coffee House
Author: Meghan Zulian
Review: Sorry but this was not it. Everything bagel was super salty. Avocado was smeared all over the wrapper and not ripe. I had to chew it and it wasn’t spread onto sandwich so just kept falling out onto me. Coffee also wasn’t great and cashier added wayyyyyy too much milk and didn’t ask how much I wanted. I won’t be going back.

Name: K's Coffee House
Author: Joey Bishop
Review: Excellent customer service, friendly wait staff and delicious pastries and coffee! It’s a must for lethargic students who need to get energized for the day! (P.S. try the Rugelach, it helps Ukrainian families and is really good!)

Name: K's Coffee House
Author: Navjot Kaur
Review: I recently moved to Berkeley and found this cafe by chance. I enjoy their coffee, smoothies.and wraps. Food is always fresh and very delicious. If you are looking for good food at fair price definitely worth trying.

Name: K's Coffee House
Author: Navjeet Chahal
Review: I have been here many times and had their Capachino and bagels. It's very good quality coffee. They use Italian made coffee machine so coffee comes our really good. They have good customer service. Definitely give it a try.

Name: K's Coffee House
Author: Kulwarn Singh
Review: Very nice place ! Cool students and Teachers visit here most of the time! I like recommend this place for your waiting time ! I will visit this place whenever I visit my client here in Berkeley 💖👌❤️👀🖖

Name: 1951 Coffee Company
Author: Gary Cole
Review: Good coffee in clean, upscale interior space with cafe style outdoor tables for enjoying a sunny day. Cappuccino and cortado were good though lacking intense aromas, thick crema and strong arabica notes I’ve come to expect from top tier coffee shops. Chocolate croissant was dry - likely a day old pastry given it was Memorial Day.  This establishment roasts its own beans which I appreciate. My son previously purchased beans here from which we made good coffee at home, however, did not buy on today’s visit. The draw of this establishment appears to be its financial  support of Bay Area refugees and large, quiet inside space which many patrons were enjoying while working on their laptops. There is also a sitting area with soft chairs in a quiet corner. If milder coffee is your preference - my palate prefers strong Italian espresso flavors - 1951 Coffee Company could be a good choice.

Name: 1951 Coffee Company
Author: Amanda
Review: I can tell I'm in these California prices like 11 for my dirty chai. I can't really afford these prices but I don't live here so maybe you can. Good flavors though. Inside was way too busy for me but out back has patio seating.

Name: 1951 Coffee Company
Author: Lauren Haley
Review:
This cafe is a great place to study because of its wonderful atmosphere. Drinks are also pretty good, as are pastries.

However, what truly makes Cafe 1951 stand out is its incredible employees! They are extremely kind and friendly, making this a great cafe experience. Service is also quick.

Name: 1951 Coffee Company
Author: Bryce McLean
Review: Magnificent coffee and delectable croissants. Staff are lovely; professional & skilled. Courtyard area out back in the sunshine is recommended, unless you want to hang with the students inside.

Name: 1951 Coffee Company
Author: yilin ye

Name: Coffee Time
Author: B V
Review:
I’m a coffee lover willing to try new coffees wherever I travel especially in support of mom and pop places. This little spot was convenient.

Unfortunately, I can’t give them my 5 stars because the plain iced coffee and latte were lackluster at best. But that’s only my opinion so take it with “a grain of salt,”if you will. They were so sweet and the place was continuously busy. The locals love it!

The little shop with outdoor seating is located in an industrial area near the marina. Breakfast entree photos looked yummy and people have given the coffee many kudos so I say give them a try!

Name: Coffee Time
Author: James A
Review: Small quaint building with some seating outside. Great for coffee to go and can get some food and go down to nearby marina. Nice staff and prices are low. Better quality than the chain coffee houses and much better food.

Name: Coffee Time
Author: Lei'Orum Travel and Entertainment LLC
Review: Small place, but the service and the breakfast burritos are amazing 👏

Name: Coffee Time
Author: Jonah Ewell
Review: Excellent place to get breakfast or sandwich and a coffee. Small, efficient, friendly, delicious, and well priced! As of December 2023 a sausage egg and cheese croissant is still just $5.45, when donut shops are charging up to $7/8 for the same thing.

Name: Coffee Time
Author: Rachel Carey
Review: Small town coffee coffee shop. Good breakfast burritos, crispy toasted on the outside. Great hot chocolate & she was able to make a slightly sweet white mocha that my boyfriend loved.

Name: Matching Half
Author: Blake Amedeo
Review:
Matching Half is a classic SF coffeehouse with layers of history, excellent service, and delicious drinks & food.

Definitely stop by for a good vibe—it’s more than a shop, it’s a community space.

Name: Matching Half
Author: Bree Reeves
Review:
Great coffee shop. Every time I’m in SF for work I make sure to stop by.
The barista(s) made a point to make conversation with their regulars and even knew their drink orders by heart. Great atmosphere.

Name: Matching Half
Author: Shreya Dixit
Review: This is a lovely cafe! The lattes have cute designs and the baristas are super warm, friendly and patient! I love sitting here and working! This place has both outlets and wifi! But most of all, I feel cozy and happy when I’m in the space.

Name: Matching Half
Author: Matthew Ye
Review:
I've been here a few times now since I live nearby, and each time the baristas have felt curt and a bit arrogant - I guess it must be a job requirement.

The coffee takes forever to come out and is average at best.

The shop has ample indoor and outdoor seating and is well-decorated but I'd rather take my business elsewhere.

Name: Matching Half
Author: Jenny Wang
Review: The vibes and aesthetics of this cafe makes me so happy. Ordered a breakfast egg sandwich to go. It was delicious. There’s lovely outdoor seating and people on laptops inside. Favorite cafe in the neighborhood! And closest cafe with matcha on the menu.

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Andrei Victor
Review: Love this branch. The outdoor seats are nicely placed that looks private and the morning sun is not directly at you. Great place to take breakfast/break/snack before going to the DMV across the street.

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Neal
Review:
Standard Peet's Coffee location.

This coffee shop is located near the DMV and has plenty of parking nearby. There's also a grocery store with a door that leads into here. There is no indoor seating, but there are a few tables and benches outdoors. I don't think there's a restroom, but I could be wrong. When ordering an iced drink, remember to ask for light ice if you want more to drink. The prices have increased, but they're average for the city.

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Sophie
Review: Have been doing a lot of cooking recently. Thought I’d come to Pete’s to see what the REAL pros are up to. Let’s just say Ina Garten needs to take a page out of their book 🤣🤣🤣 (sorry Ina I didn’t mean it 😟🤣). Zoe from the register I am in love with you please come see my short film on Thursday 8 pm if you want

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Sarah Odeh
Review: Delicious coffee. The service is fast and friendly, and the cappuccino's are quite tasty. They now have oat milk for those of us who can't do dairy. The store is very clean. Check it out.

Name: Peet's Coffee
Author: Bruce Young
Review: I just come here for my coffee on the 15minute break. Spent my whole 15 minute break listening to the lady in the green mask chat up the people in front of me. Needless to say I left without buying my coffee

Name: Tierra Mia Coffee
Author: Alison M C
Review: Pretty café. Small pepino mint lemonade was expensive (over $4), artificial tasting, and too sweet. One single pastry at around 4 pm felt like not enough to choose from. I like the idea of Latin American coffee shop but I wish it felt less corporate. Echo-ey inside, not a good place to take a call.

Name: Tierra Mia Coffee
Author: A.T. Green
Review:
Non-local, here visiting a friend in the bay area and stopped by on a Sunday morning.  Quite possibly the worst tasting chai tea latte I have ever had in my life. Not sure about the other drinks but I would never order this drink again should I return.

Pro: spacious, ample seating, friendly employees

Cons: limited outlets, only tables and hard back chairs (no sofas, lounge furniture, etc),

Name: Tierra Mia Coffee
Author: Brandon Cable
Review:
As a non-local staying in the area for the weekend, I decided to give this spot a try. I ordered ahead, walked in, grabbed my coffee, and left. While the staff wasn't overly friendly, that was perfectly fine since I was all business. The Pistachio Cold Brew was exceptional—smooth, flavorful, and just the right amount of sweetness.

This cute little spot, as other reviews have pointed out, has a charming ambiance that I would love to experience more if I lived in the area. I would definitely come back and explore more of their menu. Highly recommend for a quick and quality coffee run!

Name: Tierra Mia Coffee
Author: liamplant !
Review: Very nicely located and cute coffee shop with a beautiful plant-decorated interior and ample seating. They have vegan milk options (oat milk and almond milk) and I enjoyed a peppermint mocha that was nicely not too sweet and not too peppermint-y. The music was also very nice, lots of guitar and spanish music.

Name: Tierra Mia Coffee
Author: Fatima Wright
Review: This is a beautifully designed Cafe that’s very welcoming! The Horchata latte and almond croissant that I had was absolutely delicious! Only reason why it’s a four-star is because their directional signs aren’t very clear and the bathroom smells really bad!

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee
Author: Trevor Mah
Review: I finally stopped by the trendy Blue Bottle Coffee to see what all the buzz was about. While I’ve heard some locations can get pretty busy, this one was surprisingly chill in the mid-afternoon. I ordered the Hayes Valley Espresso but turned it into a simple Americano, and it was perfect for a quick afternoon pick-me-up—fresh, piping hot, and flavorful. The atmosphere inside is modern and trendy, with plenty of space to relax or get some work done. They also have a great selection of their own merch and beans if you want to take some home. I’ll definitely be back next time I need a caffeine fix!

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee
Author: gtb5
Review: First Blue Bottle experience brings great memories from this SF location. Exceptional coffee and pastry we paid a few visits there during the trip.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee
Author: K W
Review:
Good place for a morning cup of coffee. I ordered a latte which was nice and creamy.

Love the iconic blue bottle logo. Space is beautiful with plentiful seating. Also there is good merch selection at this location (I got a reusable silicon eco cup which I’m very excited to use)

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee
Author: H Dave
Review: Blue Bottle Coffee in San Francisco is a haven for coffee connoisseurs. Their commitment to sourcing the finest beans and meticulous roasting process results in a cup of coffee that is nothing short of extraordinary. The inviting atmosphere, whether you're enjoying a quiet moment with a latte or engaging in lively conversation over pour-over, makes it a cherished spot for locals and visitors alike. The knowledgeable baristas are always on hand to guide you through their diverse selection, ensuring you find the perfect brew to suit your taste.

Name: Blue Bottle Coffee
Author: R L (Froyo)
Review: Their (matcha) latte and coffee are consistently of high quality. Pastries are also delicious!

Name: Haus Coffee
Author: Miranda
Review: lovely backyard and indoor seating. the owners are very nice and dedicated to the coffee making. didn’t feel like it was a lot of marketing with the in person experience :) the latte was good.

Name: Haus Coffee
Author: Grace Lee
Review: They offer single origin pour over coffee, clearly priced at $5 on the menu. We chose two exotic pour overs to enjoy. Freshly brewed coffee tastes the best and it has a noticeable acidity. All-wood interior decor along with the spacious and bright seating, made it chill and relaxing. There is also an outdoor seating at the back.

Name: Haus Coffee
Author: PJ Pasricha
Review:
I recently visited Haus and was impressed by the peaceful and quiet ambiance of the place. It's the perfect environment for getting work done, with plenty of seating and a beautiful outdoor patio that's definitely worth checking out.

The black coffee at Haus is absolutely wonderful, with a rich and robust flavor that's sure to satisfy any coffee lover. I highly recommend it!

Service is also very nice and the staff is very kind.

Overall, Haus is a great place to come for a productive work or study session. The atmosphere is peaceful and quiet, the coffee is excellent, and the ample seating and beautiful outdoor patio make it a great place to spend a few hours getting things done.

Name: Haus Coffee
Author: Chloe Venn
Review: great for a peaceful conversation or getting work done. i had the cappucino and americano with the columbia espresso, the indonesia java pour over is delicious too. the barista works totally alone (respect) - and is super chill and friendly. coming back soon 🤍

Name: Haus Coffee
Author: SB Duggirala
Review:
Beautiful place with great coffee!

Tried their cappuccino - well-balanced and robust flavor.
They have ample indoor and outdoor seating.
They are pet-friendly, so you can enjoy your coffee with your dog.
Definitely recommend this place for a great coffee!

Name: Cafe Francisco
Author: Juan Carlos Rivera
Review:
I enjoyed my brief stay in this lovely Cafe.  The Owner is very friendly and it's nice to see him engage with locals and tourists. Really hard working and I liked the sandwich I had , "The San Fran", you must try it! It's about 6-8 minutes walk from Pier 39, I chose to come here to be away from the busy and touristy area. Definitely worth it, easy to locate, just by the corner, few seats outside, you won't miss it! I'll definitely come back
-JC from Italy/UK

Name: Cafe Francisco
Author: Shannon Lynch Dickinson
Review: I've been a fan of Cafe Francisco for over a decade and have never once been disappointed! Due to a number of factors (primarily the pandemic), I hadn't been back since summer 2019. Imagine my delight when the vibe, food, and friendly staff were just as fabulous as ever. Just slightly off the beaten path, between North Beach and Fisherman's Wharf, locals and tourists alike are treated like family here. The Francisco bagel with an iced latte is and always will be my fave, but I'm going to try the avocado toast next time!

Name: Cafe Francisco
Author: Logan Rosselli
Review: Simply delicious. Great coffee and the Francisco Bagel is the reason for my annual trip to SF. I can’t recommend this spot enough.

Name: Cafe Francisco
Author: Rachel Wilcox
Review:
Absolutely love Cafe Francisco! What a North Beach gem, simple food with fresh ingredients!

I always get an iced coffee and the francisco bagel that has schmear, tomatoes, red onion, fresh basil, and what really makes it great is the olive oil and capers. Owner is very friendly and the cafe is a staple to the North Beach community! Dog friendly spot too :)

Name: Cafe Francisco
Author: Collin Street
Review: I don't usually leave reviews, or take pictures of my food, but I had to capture the essence of my experience. I really wanted to like this place. I paid $32 for a frittata and an iced latte, including tip. The frittata came recommended by the owner himself, and was bland, included 3 slices of avocado, a pathetic strand of red onion, 1 slice of tomato, packaged butter and smuckers jelly and a pack of tapatio (???), served on a paper plate on wax paper which ripped, with plastic utensils - for $19. I left it as shown and had oatmeal at home. 2 stars only for the atmosphere.

Name: M One Cafe Daan
Author: M Mmm
Review: Love the service and atmosphere here. Foods is reasonable price and the coffee tasted fragrant and nice. Would come back here again when I visit Taipei. Both the omelette dishes was our fave followed by the french fries and bread.

Name: M One Cafe Daan
Author: Jonathan Wang
Review: An excellent place to have a weekend brunch in the heart of Taipei: food is great, and the free-refill coffee is a big plus!

Name: M One Cafe Daan
Author: Michael Liu
Review:
1. Hash brown, everyone comes for the hash brown!

This place was recommended by two of my friends at the same week same time, so I was curious to find out how good the restaurant is.
Overall my wife and I came with high expectation, it didn’t quite exceed the taste and flavor we are looking for, but we both agree it is a more western Taiwanese that you won’t easily find in Taiwan. Maybe will come back in the future to try other dishes, but with the price at fairly 400/person wanting to feel fill there are many other options around the area.
Beautifully decorated, great atmosphere to gather with friends, mostly packed with youngsters.

Name: M One Cafe Daan
Author: D. Land
Review:
Great brunch place and relaxing atmosphere!

Strongly recommended their Hash Brown, French Fries and French Toast

Name: M One Cafe Daan
Author: Zachary Hubbell
Review:
Meal: Breakfast
Day Visited: Sunday
Budget: NT$ 500
Closest Station: Zhangxiao Fuxi
Reservation Needed?: Yes
Payment: Cash; Credit
Kid Friendly?: Yes
English Menu?: Yes

We often skip breakfast, but I’m so happy that we didn’t this morning! If you love the first meal of the day, M One Cafe is a must visit for weekend (or weekday) brunch.

Our omelet set was generous and included the famous hash browns. The omelet was light and tasty and was accompanied by French Toast and yogurt. Perfect!

My patty melt rivaled some of the better sandwiches I’ve had, and now - after years of living in Japan - miss dearly. It was also served with a scrumptious hash brown.

Highly recommended. Get a reservation for the weekend! This place is popular!

Name: Heritage Bakery & Cafe
Author: Pei Ni L.
Review:
Food is pretty good but price point is a little high.

Stellar recommendation has got to be the guava cheesecake! Must try! Coffee’s also awesome!

They have an elevator in the restaurant so take that if you have accessibility problems.

For our order of tacos, they had provided gloves in our cutlery box. However, this was not communicated to us. It’s a sincere gesture for the convenience of customers but it will be good to introduce it to us 😊

Name: Heritage Bakery & Cafe
Author: Anne S.
Review: Guava Cheesecake is a must try! I fell in love with this cake 😍 Breakfast burrito and Cinnamon roll were awesome too! Their atmosphere and interior design is so fancy and pretty. I’ll come this cafe every time I visit Taipei!

Name: Heritage Bakery & Cafe
Author: Irene Navarro
Review: If you are craving a tasty-yet-affordable European-like breakfast, I can very much recommend this place. Their coffee was the best I had in Taipei (despite going to specialty coffee shops in the city) and the breakfast burrito made my last day in Taipei. Service is on point, fast and friendly. I did not try any cake but all of them looked delicious. The café itself is beautifully decorated and every table has a bottle of water for you to enjoy, that's always a bonus! 🙌🏼

Name: Heritage Bakery & Cafe
Author: Riki Sharma
Review:
Heritage Bakery & Cafe in Taipei serves up beautiful surroundings and delicious treats that are sure to please. The atmosphere is minimalist and modern, with ample natural light and artistic touch, making it a great place to focus, study, or relax with friends.

During my visit, I had the chance to try the chocolate cookie, cranberry cookie, and the guava delight cake, which is one of their specialties. The baked goods were delicious, but what really struck me was how sweet they were. The cookies and cake were noticeably sweeter than most other cake and cookie bakeries. This may be a pro or a con depending on individual preferences, but it's worth noting if you prefer treats that are less sweet. Along with our treats, we ordered a chilled earl grey milk tea, which was refreshing and the perfect complement to our sweet goodies.

The only downside to Heritage Bakery & Cafe is the cost. It is on the pricier side, with our order of three desserts and two drinks coming to $620 Taiwanese dollars. However, given the beautiful surroundings, great service, and delicious treats, the prices are still acceptable and affordable compared to Western standards. Plus, if you opt for takeout, you can enjoy a discount which makes it a viable budget option.

The staff are proficient in English and are happy to assist you with anything you need. This made ordering easy and hassle-free, despite being in a foreign country.

Overall, Heritage Bakery & Cafe in Taipei offers a unique experience with its beautiful ambiance and delicious sweet treats. If you have a sweet tooth, you will certainly enjoy the cookies and cake here. The cost may be slightly on the expensive side, but the quality of food, service, and surroundings make it a worthwhile visit.

Name: Heritage Bakery & Cafe
Author: Hen Ezra
Review:
Great food! The chicken pesto fucaccia was tasty!

But I think the prices are wayyyy to high!
Coffee, cake and sandwich (and they are all small yes?) for 670 dollars? Crazy expensive 💸💸
You definitely pay for the instagram photo from this place.

They take 10% for cleaning fee (????) I’m sorry but isn’t this their job?? Ridiculous

Name: CuppaCuppa Cafe
Address: No. 12號 (中間, Lane 559, Section 4, Zhongxiao E Rd
Author: Roy Chan
Review: Very nicely brewed coffee and nice vibe. There was a small bench outside for a nice sit for the coffee as well.

Name: CuppaCuppa Cafe
Address: No. 12號 (中間, Lane 559, Section 4, Zhongxiao E Rd
Author: Kalani Scarrott
Review: great coffee, great service, cool little spot ! very stoked with my cuppa

Name: CuppaCuppa Cafe
Address: No. 12號 (中間, Lane 559, Section 4, Zhongxiao E Rd
Author: will c
Review: Purely a to-go place. Ordered latte. Perfect temperature and taste. Price is very reasonable. Highly recommended.

Name: CuppaCuppa Cafe
Address: No. 12號 (中間, Lane 559, Section 4, Zhongxiao E Rd
Author: Dipesh Birdy
Review: Good coffee, knowledgable staff and at a great price point. No compromise on quality of beans; defiantly worth a visit. It’s a great little coffee shop nestled in Xinyi. If you’re after legit coffee and are in Xinyi or around the Songshan Cultural park it’s  definitely worth a stop. Also check out the monstera latte art!!!

Name: CuppaCuppa Cafe
Address: No. 12號 (中間, Lane 559, Section 4, Zhongxiao E Rd
Author: Basil Chua
Review: Afternoon cuppa is a must have. Chose the dark roast flat white…. Heavenly smooth. ❤️❤️‍🩹

Name: Libo Cafe
Address: No. 12, Lane 42, Section 2, Zhongshan N Rd
Author: Amy Nguyen
Review: If you’re looking for the casual place for chitchat, working for a bit then Libo is suitable for you. It could be very noisy sometimes so don’t expect a quiet environment here. Cafe was okay, tea was fine. Staffs were friendly, toilet was clean. They offer linepay.

Name: Libo Cafe
Address: No. 12, Lane 42, Section 2, Zhongshan N Rd
Author: 巴跃康
Review: Simple cafe with a chill vibe and setup, which is great for an afternoon alone or with friends. The staff are warm and friendly, fare is affordable, and the dessert I had was wonderful.

Name: Libo Cafe
Address: No. 12, Lane 42, Section 2, Zhongshan N Rd
Author: George Chang
Review: Accepts Line Pay, great service and pretty good coffee. Quiet and nice environment

Name: Libo Cafe
Address: No. 12, Lane 42, Section 2, Zhongshan N Rd
Author: Pras Siva
Review: Cute little cafe with good coffee. Quite busy with local students studying or meeting up for a bite.

Name: Libo Cafe
Address: No. 12, Lane 42, Section 2, Zhongshan N Rd
Author: Ayako “Aÿ” Yasuda
Review: We spent here for a hour after opening. Most picked to go coffee, but it will be much fun with latte art at comfortable atmosphere. They open at 9 am, earlier than the other individual coffee shop around this area. 👍

Name: CAFE Start
Author: Spencer Tan
Review:
Small coffee stand near the station that opens early. Popped by for a coffee in the morning and was pleasantly surprised- the medium dark roast was delicious and the owner was very nice. My Chinese is a little rusty but the owner was patient and I had a good time chatting with him. He even gave some suggestions for good food around the area.

Highly recommend this place if you’re around the area!

Name: CAFE Start
Author: Fannie Wu
Review:
Great coffee with fair price. Recommend Latte
The owner is cute by the way😆😆

Name: CAFE Start
Author: Jordy Leung
Review: You pay 80 bucks but can taste 150+ bucks latte in this area.. definitely great one for quick coffee

Name: CAFE Start
Author: Ling Tam
Review: Good latte, very friendly owner, a couple stools to sit on. Recommended!

Name: CAFE Start
Author: Chaoxing Alexander
Review: Very nice coffee

Name: CAFE ACME mini｜Taipei 101
Author: Mike Liu
Review: Passed by the area and saw this coffee shop. Decided to try it especially since they had a tie guan yin basque cheesecake. I got that which is a small cheesecake suitable for sharing with 4 people. The cheesecake was just okay and could not pick up any tea flavor. I also got a honeycomb latte as they no longer had brown sugar winter melon latte. The espresso was slightly burnt and overall just okay. Nothing special.

Name: CAFE ACME mini｜Taipei 101
Author: Chanyanat S.
Review: I occasionally stop by Cafe Acme for coffee before work. Their Homey Comb Oat Milk Latte is one of the best I've had.

Name: CAFE ACME mini｜Taipei 101
Author: Grace Ban
Review: Really liked the pink guava smoothie (with 30% sugar). Also really liked the chia pudding. Seeing some of the local reviews here that said the “pudding” name is misleading but it’s literally a direct translation from its English name. Hope that clears it up for some people. Also nice to be able to get an authentic pain chocolat here. Will try a donut next time.

Name: CAFE ACME mini｜Taipei 101
Author: Shih-fei Jinn
Review:
A bit pricey but tasty though. I  would like it better if the slice of bread was thicker.
They should serve the coffee first when it's ready instead they wait until everything is ready.But the foam on the latte has collapsed then.

Name: CAFE ACME mini｜Taipei 101
Author: Justin Wang
Review: Good mocha latte and croissant, but croissant is pricey. It's also crowdy during weekend hours, and a bit smelly when sitting outside.

Name: CHIT CHAT Cafe 南京店
Author: Amy Nguyen
Review: The foods were all great. Decent place to enjoy late brunch or afternoon tea. Menu prices are quite high. Clean and bright toilet. However the staff was so rush to push us out right when our table time is up, didn’t give us some buffer times although there’s no group waiting outside. Well, hope this will be improved then.

Name: CHIT CHAT Cafe 南京店
Author: Warren Ong
Review: A delightful cafe located along the main road within walking distance from Nanjing-Sanmin metro station. Definitely one of the best cafes in town offering quality dishes, coffee and tea, and desserts. There’s ample seating areas and the ambience is cost, warm and comfortable. Staff are friendly and very helpful too. Highly recommended!

Name: CHIT CHAT Cafe 南京店
Author: Yating Jhang
Review:
A calm place where to enjoy good food & tea.
Japanese mocha latte (sugar free) was nice, full aroma of mocha tea.
Afternoon tea set for two persons was just fine in its taste and size of the offer and perfect to have it with friends in the afternoon after a small lunch.

Name: CHIT CHAT Cafe 南京店
Author: Ting Huang
Review:
Super nice and cozy interior! The design and atmosphere is very detail oriented and aesthetically pleasing. The owner offered a Madeleine on the house in return for a 5 star review!

However, I’m not doing this
for the Madeleine. Chitchat is a newly opened cafe under the pandemic since March 2021. They deserve all the support for keeping their business running!

All the staff are so friendly and hospitable. Even though everyone wears a mask, but you can sure tell they’re all serving with a big heart!

I ordered a dripped iced coffee and a iced honey latte for only 180NTD. They’re now offering buy 1 drink get one drink free.

I feel very safe dining in at Chitchat cafe!

Name: CHIT CHAT Cafe 南京店
Author: gwen chee
Review: Had the salmon and avocado brunch woth osmanthus coffee. The food is nice.

Name: YOLO's Cafe
Author: Serdar G
Review: Good coffee, but they have to stop serving espresso in a mug!😀

Name: YOLO's Cafe
Author: Jessica CC
Review: Either coffee or tea makes you day utterly. Nice service with wifi & laptop power supply is highly recommended if 30-60 minutes work for settling. Enjoy!

Name: YOLO's Cafe
Author: Rik De Busser
Review: A non-pretentious coffee place with very decent cheap coffee and consistently good service. Not fancy, but trendy. Have been here multiple times and will come again.

Name: YOLO's Cafe
Author: Justin Wang
Review: Friendly service.  A good spot to get quck snack, simple sandwich, and coffee.

Name: YOLO's Cafe
Author: thanyaphan limkangwanmongkol
Review: Nice little coffee shop with toasted, bagels and sandwiches good food and location the shop is open wide so you get the nice feeling of the out side atmosphere with the cooling control from the. A/C

Name: YOLO's Cafe
Author: Soo Ee
Review: Good selection of lattes. Offers tea and drop coffee. Chill ambience and barista speaks English. Does not serve coffee shots but I didn’t regret doing a double shot espresso. Great tasting. Zebra mocha 斑马摩卡 deliciously fragrant.

Name: YOLO's Cafe
Author: Eva Beekers
Review: Friendly and stylish atmosphere with great service - a safe bet for a relaxed and tasty breakfast!

Name: YOLO's Cafe
Author: Joseph Teng
Review: Great coffee and tea selection. Family and kids friendly atmosphere. My favorite go to place either to hang out with friends or to put in some work with my laptop.
