Review: The atmosphere is great. Ordered the Okinawa brown sugar coffee and triple cheesecake. Slightly overpriced but still enjoyed the chill and slow tea time here.

Name: Haowu Spirit Cafe
Author: Charles Remboldt
Review: Wow, great food. Really enjoyed the overall experience. It was a challenge to order due to the restaurants use of a QR code to download an app and then submitting your desired food. Not real user friendly, but I managed, and I’m an old (almost) Baby Boomer 🤣. I’ll definitely come again.

Name: Haowu Spirit Cafe
Author: 施素珊
Review:
Pros: Lovely space with natural lighting, just the right amount of soft, soothing background music. The proprietress was very welcoming and clearly working hard. I genuinely wish her all the best.

Cons: Wait staff appears to be completely apathetic and slow. I waited close to 15 minutes for a single slice of cheesecake to appear. Despite fulfilling its promise to be very rich and cheesy, this may have been the worst cheesecake I've ever had.

I just can't recommend this place to anyone at the moment. Maybe in 6 months or so it will be worth another try. Clearly the proprietress has high ambitions and deserves some support.

Name: (Flowers Taiwan) R9 Café
Author: Pi Er
Review:
The relaxed ambience and wooden cottage of the cafe gives us a heart warming vibes.

Bread that's served with the soup was e best among all the food.

Sad to say, that's about all.

Hawaiian pizza taste more like bread with frozen shrimps, canned pineapple and cucumber. Yes, cucumber on a Hawaiian pizza.

Sandwich doesn't have the wow factor to let you have the craving to have a second bite

Fries and shrimps also uses the same frozen shrimps and it would be better if they had fried the garlic until it's crispy

Tiramisu doesn't taste like one. More like chocolate cake

NO CARD PAYMENT. THEY ONLY ACCEPT CASH!!!

Feel kind of wasted as we've left so much food due to it's taste

Name: (Flowers Taiwan) R9 Café
Author: Min Yeo
Review: nice little cafe at the corner, the exterior is very arty hence it draw my attention . interior are pretty as well. Food are average as i ordered simple food like sandwich

Name: (Flowers Taiwan) R9 Café
Author: Greg Kuebler
Review: Food was generally good. Pizza was probably best.   Cuisine portion kinda on. smaller side.  They use disposable plastic cups for water which fall over easily.  Funny to watch almost every nearby table accidentally spill water on the tables.  Not sure why they use plastic.  Does not accept credit cards

Name: (Flowers Taiwan) R9 Café
Author: Kayo Chang Black
Review:
It's a cute cafe but crowded. The service wasn't great. The staff failed to tell me that the pork knuckle would take 30-40 minutes to cook. I would not have gotten it if I had known it would take that long.

My friend's fried chicken and eggs & toast (with peanut butter and apples, I think)  didn't look great. My pork knuckles tasted pretty good but it wasn't worth the wait. The drinks were mediocre.

Name: (Flowers Taiwan) R9 Café
Author: Chris (I am D1N)
Review:
I came to this cafe given it has a 4.2 rating and decided not to walk back to Lido which looks much better.
I only wanted a coffee and a light dessert but they only serve big portion waffles and toast which I only ordered a cup or hot latte.
Didn’t know the server has no clue what oat milk is and I went for whatever milk they serve. Took them 25 min (since the order was placed) and the cup of latte was a joke. I can’t taste any coffee and the whatever latte art was blurred.
If you wanna take photos, only need to take the outside of the building and there would be better choices for coffee in the neighborhood.

Name: Once Again Cafe(再來咖啡）
Author: Kelsey F
Review: Very nice cafe to relax in with cute decorations and a clean bathroom. All of the employees were friendly and I really appreciated how the menu also had English. Both the hot sea salt caramel latte and hot brown sugar latte were the perfect sweetness and temperature. The iced honey citron tea was refreshing. Everything I tried was delicious, and I especially liked the seasoned potatoes that came with the sandwich. I’d definitely go back next time I’m in Taipei!

Name: Once Again Cafe(再來咖啡）
Author: Sean L
Review:
I came here a few times while visiting from the US. The menu includes English and the staff was very helpful and friendly.

Favorite drinks -  latte,  brown sugar latte and the iced black lemon.

Favorite food -  “Transition Life Style” which comes with a salad, scrambled eggs, home-made yogurt and a piece of juicy and tender chicken.

Favorite dessert - Basque Cheesecake

Everything was very yummy and especially nice on a rainy day

Name: Once Again Cafe(再來咖啡）
Author: Therese Chua
Review: I ordered the latte and my cousin ordered the lemon honey fizz coffee thingie. We also shared a slice of lemon cake, which was delicious! One of the staff (maybe the owner?) even gave us a complimentary glass of apple juice after we had finished our drinks!

Name: Once Again Cafe(再來咖啡）
Author: PACA C
Review:
Many options of coffee ☕️ and dessert
Dine in and outside seat available
Suit for couple and who wanna study in here.

🌟8-10 tables
🌟air conditioning
🌟WiFi provides
🌟charging available
🌟toilet

Name: Once Again Cafe(再來咖啡）
Author: 張以全
Review: Great food at 8:00. Good coffee with price that worth it. Somewhat more expensive compare to those grab and go around Taipei, but you stay and enjoy Adele in this nice cozy place.

Name: Modism Café 摩德年代 士林店
Author: Hayley
Review: Hidden jem!!!! The menu is very very special and this is the best coffee ever!!! I ordered elderflower liqueur cafe latte and it gave a hint of sweetness,florally, sweet, and the aromatic smell of grounded coffee. I also ordered almond liquor caffe latte and it's very good too with a hint of almond nutty flavor. Recommend everyone to come here! The atmosphere is good, relaxing and calm. A good place to get away from the hustle and bustle of the city.

Name: Modism Café 摩德年代 士林店
Author: Frosty Ace
Review:
Came here right after hiking at Qingtiangang Grassland to chill and have cup of coffee along with some small bites.

It's very well located and easily accessible being right beside the Shilin MRT Station.

All of the drinks (coffees, teas, mocha) and cakes (Tarts and pound cakes) that we ordered were superbly delicious and very aesthetically presented 😋

The atmosphere/ambiance in here was pretty laid-back, calming and quiet.

This would definitely be a perfect place to be to relax and escape from the hustle and bustle of the city for a brief moment!

Would recommend to drop by here if you're in the Shilin Area! ☺️

Name: Modism Café 摩德年代 士林店
Author: Mich Lin
Review: A really nice and quiet environment. I got the Earl Grey chocolate tart and the two flavor was very obvious, they didn’t use gelatin so it melted quicker but that was perfect. I also got an honey au lait which was so much better than I expected. If the prices are very reasonable and the atmosphere is perfect for studying.

Name: Modism Café 摩德年代 士林店
Author: Helen Lam (Helen Lam)
Review:
A hidden gem! I mean literally, a bit tricky to find, but im glad I found it! Their tarts are fresh, drinks are on the small portion side, but luckily they have plenty of options.

Love their decorations! not too many seats tho, better reserve a table before going, I had a wonderful chatty night here!

Name: Modism Café 摩德年代 士林店
Author: R L
Review: Great little cafe tucked away from the hustle and bustle of Shilin. The hot chocolate is probably one of the best hot chocolates I’ve had. The cake was nice and tasty but the tart was the star of the show. Highly recommend going there for the passion fruit tart. An added bonus is they accept card payments!

Name: Do you have coffee neo cafe
Author: kopiandmilktea
Review:
This place is all about vibes 😍 the shop is pretty cool with neon lights and cool seating (there was a table where you can sit on a platform without shoes, but the space is rather small). We visited at around 1pm & the shop's pretty empty at first but customers started flowing in after 130pm. We ordered army stew for one, peanut butter bacon pizza, burnt chestnut latte & brown sugar cinnamon latte (the price is pretty steep).

The army stew's broth was NOT army stew but tasted like shin ramyum soup base (it's just shin maggie mee with pork meat slices, fish cake skewer and some other stuff) BUT the pumpkin mash side dish was interesting.

The peanut butter bacon pizza tasted interesting but we got sick of the taste quite quickly (meaning it's a flat taste & not multi layered).

We couldn't really taste the chestnut in the burnt chestnut latte so it tasted like a normal cup of latte to us.

The brown sugar cinnamon latte was not bad & not that sweet so we liked it better.

Overall it's an aesthetics cafe with average tasting food 🙃

Name: Do you have coffee neo cafe
Author: William Lü
Review:
Tbh, I wasn’t sure what to expect. Their recent reviews seem to be pretty bad. Since today was the holidays, we didn’t have much choices so we decided to check this place out.

The decor and the ambience are cozy, gezellig, and cute. The service wasn’t bad as I thought it would but probably because there were only two of us. The staff were friendly when I told them I needed help because I couldn’t read chinese.

My friend ordered the German sausage pizza while I got the Thai basil pork with rice. Both dishes were delicious. The rice dish was spicy but it really brought out the flavor. I personally don’t really care for pizzas in Taiwan. They’re all too greasy and American/Japanese influenced (too much cheese). However, my Taiwanese friend loved it.

The drinks are yummy too. We got the iced black tea and iced brown sugar latte. They are absolutely delish.

Name: Do you have coffee neo cafe
Author: Wong Eunice
Review:
Love the nicely prepared and presented food & beverages.
Cozy vibe, friendly and helpful staff.
Maybe a bit weird to say - but i love their washroom which promotes adoption (=^ェ^=)

Name: Do you have coffee neo cafe
Author: 潘怡謙
Review: First of all, as a diner it's a bit crowded because about one third of the of the place is packed with the accessories it sells. Secondly, even if you did order a meal, it forces to order a beverage no matter how much you already have to pay for the food. The food is quite good, yet the amount of the food is barely enough to diminish the feeling of hunger. It's still quite a nice place to chat and have a little snack. But if you're looking for a place to enjoy a feast with over eight people, I wouldn't recommend it.

Name: Do you have coffee neo cafe
Author: Fabian V
Review: Didn't try the food, but coffee is good and it has kind of a Berlin-y alternative vibe (coincidentally, there's an old sign with German text). The aircon tends to be set to "freezing cold", so you might want to make sure to get a seat on the balcony to warm up.

Name: TankQ Cafe & Bar
Author: Crystal S.
Review:
Long story short..
Taste was not bad. Service was alright. Interesting theme. But I got FOOD POISONING. 🤮

I am not sure why the 4000+ review. I was there for an early dinner, and there was only one table of two when I got there. There were more people coming, but I would imagine a restaurant with 4000+ reviews would be packed. But anyway...

FOOD
The taste itself was not bad. The portions were good, too. The three things I have a problem with are
1) There is no egg in their brunch menu? OK, I get that may be the restaurant's preference, but I was hoping to have an egg if I ordered from a breakfast/brunch menu.
2) They served their food in a storage box? I get that it's their theme to have drinks in those Disney/Pixar themed mug and food in their TankQ box. But, toy truck and non food storage box with felt? I questioned their sanitary. Those are not food graded containers either. This leads to my third problem
3) I got food poisoning that night and made me very sick the entire day the following day with series of vomiting and diarrhea. I ran to the bathroom multiple times that night, and it was very painful.

SERVICE
It was not the best I had, but I would say not bad. The person I spoke on the phone with got a bit irritated when I confirmed some questions with her. I did not want to look up other restaurants, so I still went. The service at the restaurant was not bad. They answered my questions patiently. It is CASH ONLY, tho.

ATMOSPHERE
The restaurant is decorated with Disney/Pixar themed items. You can pick your own mug if you order a drink. They also have key chain and stuff that you can make your own with additional price. I did not experience these parts, so I will not comment more.

Overall, it was alright. The food poisoning was the main issue, which I am not sure if it was the food or the serving containers. I hope this is only an one off event, but I will not take another chance.

Name: TankQ Cafe & Bar
Author: Yeu-Ting Ko (Ting-Ting)
Review: Such a unique and cute place! With iconic cartoon, nintendo and sanrio characters where you get to choose your mugs to be served! 😍 It is quite popular so I recommend booking and the food comes in cute box containers but don't be fooled.. Its actually a big portion 🤯 I was so full to my stomach with their yummy risotto 😋

Name: TankQ Cafe & Bar
Author: Alix M
Review:
If you love Disney/basically any animated characters, this will probably be worth a visit. It's certainly gimmicky and tailored to the social media crowd, but I love an Instagram trap sometimes.
There is paraphernalia everywhere and several photo spots. You can also make your own keychain for a reasonable fee, which is a great way to pass the time waiting for your food.
You get to choose your character cup for your drink, but the choices for hot drinks are slightly more limited.
Also, as I wanted to fully embrace the IG trap, it would have been nice if they had a vegetarian option for the suitcase meal. There was only one vegetarian option on the whole menu and it was risotto served in a shipping container box--it was cute and tasted pretty good but it's nice to have a least one other option on the menu.

Name: TankQ Cafe & Bar
Author: Chnn_yue
Review: I think it's a bit expensive, two drinks and a service fee of NT $370. The environment is very good. After ordering, the staff will take you to choose the style of the cup. The service attitude is very good, and the taste of the drink is also very good.

Name: TankQ Cafe & Bar
Author: Amanda
Review: the food is good and we can take really nice Instagram photos here . PLS rmb to make reservations before coming. I came on Sunday without reservation and it was super packed .I like how u can choose a own cup for the drinks and we can DIY keychain after eating . everything was so cute and I would really recommend my friends to come!!

Name: Tea House
Author: Matthew Layton
Review: This is a cute little little food hut on the grounds of the Chiang Kai-Shek memorial complex. It has both light bites as well as more substantial offerings. The proprietor is very friendly, the prices reasonable, and the air conditioning a welcome respite from the hot Taiwan weather!

Name: Tea House
Author: Clay Trippet
Review: Hot day.. Bought two vanilla soft-serve cones.  They were so bad, we had to throw them away next door.  Worst ice-cream on the planet.

Name: Tea House
Author: Rob Mcdowell (Rob)
Review: Fast and delicious snack while seeing the sites of the area!

Name: Tea House
Author: Evgeni Nonchev
Review: Good spare for a little chill out

Name: Tea House
Author: 王子康
Review: The house is clean, bright, cozy 👍👍👍👍👍, meals are yummy! Good service 🥳🥳

Name: Yao Yue Teahouse
Author: Alexis Busso
Review: Lovely experience in this beautiful tea house that is open 24 hours. The place is hugging, offering plenty of indoor and outdoor seatings. We came in the afternoon on a weekday and it was not busy at all. Would love to come here in the evening during sunset. There are several buses or you can walk there from the gandola station.

Name: Yao Yue Teahouse
Author: Barbara Lee
Review: We ordered chicken soup, but unfortunately, we weren't provided with spoons and bowls. Despite politely requesting them multiple times, our requests were not promptly addressed. Eventually, when the spoons and bowls were brought to our table by a male waiter, it was done so in an aggressive manner,  the waiter rudely slammed the spoons and bowls onto our table.

Name: Yao Yue Teahouse
Author: J Y
Review: Nestled in the side of the mountain this place is open 24 hours to serve tea and food.  The air is clear up here, a nice relaxing environment. You brew your own tea.

Name: Yao Yue Teahouse
Author: Earn S.
Review:
There’re many teashops you can visit in Maokong but this is my favorite. While you can choose some other tea shops to see the day/night view of Taipei 101, here you will be able to put yourself in a cozy wooden teahouse and will just see only mountain view, which is actually suitable for drinking tea vibe. You will feel like you’re not in Taipei city anymore.

Also, the place is open 24 hours, so if you wanna have a late night snack and hot tea, you can also call uber to come up here anytime. (In the morning you can take cable car and walk around 15 minutes).

For the tea, you will have to pay around 90-110 ntd per person as an additional. Then you will be able to pick a tea bag and make tea by yourself.

For the food, I recommend you to have some big meal first and just come here for snacks or dim sum because the main dish is in a big portion and quite expensive. (But if you come with many people, I think it’s worth it to have a big meal here).

Name: Yao Yue Teahouse
Author: Chung Mandy
Review: Food is just ok but the service is terrible. They only provide one small bowl for each person. No small plate. The waiter looked annoyed if you ask them for extra utensils. Also, the flies keep coming during our meal.

Name: Xuan Zi Ju Tea House
Author: Angelica
Review: The tea master (5th gen) is very passionate and enthusiastic about tea. He showed us photos and videos about what happens during tea competitions. We had a tour guide who translated everything which helped a great deal. My favorite is the Honey Scented Black Tea which is a tea that naturally developed its sweetness due to grasshopper bites. I ended up buying a lot here but they definitely don’t pressure you into buying anything which was nice. Had a great time with the tea ceremony and learned a lot.

Name: Xuan Zi Ju Tea House
Author: Adam Dipert
Review: This is a special gem in the middle of a quiet town. The tasting and lesson is THE BEST! Definitely try the Baozhong tea. It's a speciality in the area.

Name: Xuan Zi Ju Tea House
Author: Silvia de Donato
Review: Lovely experience in this little tea house. The owner is very competent and passionate about tea. He does not speak English but uses Google Translate, while his son is a better English-speaker. It was super interesting, and they offer amazing teas. I would highly recommend to stop by, it is really worth it!

Name: Xuan Zi Ju Tea House
Author: CJ
Review:
This is a tea shop that does tea tastings. We went here with a guide who translated the owner’s explanation. Not only was he kind and knowledgeable, the tea he brewed (Bhaozong Tea, Honey Black, and Alishan High Mountain Oolong) were incredible and each different and unique in their own way.

Not only can you experience proper tea brewing in an open air shop, you can also purchase fresh teas and tea-making tools there.

A little gem in Pinglin that I highly recommend.

Name: Xuan Zi Ju Tea House
Author: Gelisa Joseph
Review: Amazing tea tasting and friendly staff! Highly recommend this spot and doing a Tea tour from Taipei if you a tourist!

Name: Wistaria Tea House
Address: No. 1號, Lane 16, Section 3, Xinsheng S Rd
Author: Edwin Wilson
Review:
Money can’t buy happiness but it doesn’t hurt if you’re looking for serenity… and tea. I’m historically more of a coffee guy but I’m thinking of getting into tea. It’s more contemplative.

It’s definitely worth visiting this tea house if only for the experience. The staff are very helpful and sat down to show me how to brew the tea. I unfortunately had to do some homework here because I’m a cog in the education machine, but this would be an excellent place to meet a friend or—dare I say—a date.

Name: Wistaria Tea House
Address: No. 1號, Lane 16, Section 3, Xinsheng S Rd
Author: Nancy
Review: When they get busy, they won't be able to spend a lot of time talking with you about tea, but the atmosphere is very nice and tranquil. I can't speak for the regular food, but the snacks that you can get with tea are good. Recommend getting here early on a weekday so there's no wait. Service was friendly, and there is an English menu for those who need it. You are required to spend 400NTD per person, which is reasonable. This is a nice place to relax with some tea in the middle of Taipei.

Name: Wistaria Tea House
Address: No. 1號, Lane 16, Section 3, Xinsheng S Rd
Author: Sabrinna
Review: It looks like a very unassuming place from the outside. But once you enter, it’s a cute little traditional tea shop. As I was dining alone, and they were out of places in the main dining area, I was allowed to take up a table in the tatami room. This room felt like a place out of time. A throwback to traditional Japan. All the staff were very patient with me as I told them I didn’t know how to prepare my own tea. The staff who helped me was very patient and answered all my basic questions. If you’re looking for some respite from the cold or from the bustle of city life, this is the ideal spot for it. (your experience is also dependent on the civic mindedness of the other patrons, if they are loud, it detracts from the atmosphere)

Name: Wistaria Tea House
Address: No. 1號, Lane 16, Section 3, Xinsheng S Rd
Author: Jiraporn Trakonthanasoontorn
Review:
Tea was good but the price is so high and cost per head is at least 400 TWD. Staffs can speak English but some of them didn’t explain much. Tea demonstration was perfect. The guy that serviced us was really nice.

Ps. They no longer serve food.

Name: Wistaria Tea House
Address: No. 1號, Lane 16, Section 3, Xinsheng S Rd
Author: Tatiana Ramos
Review: I learned about the tea ceremony and it was magical. If you are in Taipei, it's the perfect place to relax and enjoy yourself with your loved ones. The perfect peaceful atmosphere while tasting how the tea flavor in its different types changes as it is shared in the typical handmade tea pots.  Absolutely beautiful.

Name: Jiufen Teahouse
Author: Alwin Goh
Review: Though the prices are on the higher side, the tea experience at this teahouse in Jiufen was truly fantastic. The staff were incredibly knowledgeable about the different tea leaves, and they took the time to recommend a tea that suited my taste—and it was actually one of the more affordable options! A wonderful experience for tea lovers looking for authenticity and quality.

Name: Jiufen Teahouse
Author: Roland Czabány
Review: If you like tea and your are even close to Taiwan, then this place is a perfect destination. The view is amazing and has a greaz selection of local high quality tea. It has a museum as well where you can see tea sets and various tools connected to tea. The service and atmosphere is excellent. It is a memorable experience.

Name: Jiufen Teahouse
Author: R G
Review: This was my favorite tea house in Juifen. It is the oldest and the atmosphere no matter where you sit is beautiful. It’s been three weeks since my visit and I’m still dreaming about it. This tea house is for tea what a Tuscan Villa would be for wine. A pot of tea buys you three hours of relax time and while pricey it’s well worth it for the experience. This is especially true because the tea you select you get to keep . Whatever you don’t drink, they package up the remaining tea leaves in a sealed package for you. You also get a free postcard of various art from the region which is a plus. They have multiple levels with pottery and wares and studios where they make the little sculptures and bowls and cups. People come around regularly to refill your tea kettle or tend to the cozy fire in the middle of your table. For weary travelers they do have a restroom with a sit down toilet and in case it’s occupied there is a gorgeous koi pond to stare at as you wait. The bites are also delicious. I highly recommend the pumpkin seeds and the red turtle cake.

Name: Jiufen Teahouse
Author: Evgenia Martynova
Review:
Truly, a highlight of my visit to Juifen. The building looks nice from the outside but inside it’s just gorgeous: beautiful colors and a nice combination of wood, metal and pottery. The design combines traditional elements with more playful like cute cat statues. After you’re done with the tea ceremony it worth exploring the downstairs area where they show some tea sets and a pottery collection. I recommend sitting in the gazebo, there you have both a view and air conditioning:)
To taste tea you need to buy 30g and the staff can help with choice. Then they bring you a teapot and tea set and show how to brew the tea. After that you can brew it yourself and you can stay there up to 3 hours. Better to come as a couple or small group to split the costs and enjoy it together but I was on my own and I think this experience still was worth every dollar! Needless to say that both tea I picked and matcha cheesecake were delicious!

Name: Jiufen Teahouse
Author: Craig Buller
Review: Beautiful rustic feel. Extensive tea menu. The process for pouring and settling the tea is explained well. You get to keep the tea leaves that are not used, which makes the entire experience great value. Keep in mind the tea leaves are good for at least 6 pours of a dainty cup.

Name: 三口半茶館 Saguhan Tea House
Author: Lye
Review:
Great place to try out tea in a quiet and zen environment. Opt for the bar seats to interact and watch how they prepare tea. The staff’s passion for tea is evident and prices are reasonable - you pay a base and add on for additional tea flavors.

Would recommend.

Name: 三口半茶館 Saguhan Tea House
Author: Appuru -
Review:
Spotted this gem while finishing up our day travel.
Started off with just selected tea on our side, chatted with the people there and I learned a lot from them on tea brewing with different pot and on the different kind of tea mixture can be done with tea leaves

The owner also shared some cold brew tea (which is not on the menu) with us.

Good place for people who loves tea and enjoy the quietness

Name: 三口半茶館 Saguhan Tea House
Author: Victor
Review: Took a break in this tea house and it was very relaxing ! The staff was nice and caring, the tea was great and it was really quiet, we enjoyed it a lot.

Name: 三口半茶館 Saguhan Tea House
Author: Vincent Lim
Review: A very good experience that takes you through the journey of tea, complemented by amazing and friendly service! Definitely check it out if you appreciate or want to develop an appreciation for tea.

Name: 三口半茶館 Saguhan Tea House
Author: Justin Wei Nan
Review: I had a wonderful experience at this cosy tea spot! It's the perfect place to unwind with a cup of tea. They offer a great selection, and the ambiance is just so relaxing. If you're in the area, definitely stop by for a tea break!

Name: Three Leafs 三葉茶食
Author: Adi Rainbow
Review:
The place has a relaxing atmosphere where you can be undisturbed with your tea, food, and friends. We felt the staff were incredibly friendly and caring which made us very feel comfortable.

The food was very delicious which we felt was worth the price for what you get. If you’re looking for something inexpensive to eat, then it’s worth checking out the snacks and desserts.

We’ll go back for sure to try more of their cold brew teas since we really enjoyed its distinctive taste - so we’re keen to try the others.

Name: Three Leafs 三葉茶食
Author: Morgan Sheeran
Review:
Awesome tea shop in Taipei. There is a pretty wide variety of teas to choose from and the space itself is very relaxing and open. Tea quality is superb and the owner is very passionate about highlighting Taiwanese teas and local farms.

Definitely recommend checking it out!

Name: Three Leafs 三葉茶食
Author: mia tseng
Review:
I had a great time here. Enjoyed the different flavors of all the different teas, it’s really interesting and let us know more about Taiwan’s awesome tea- oolong tea!!!!!

Great space, great owner, great tea! I look forward to visiting you again. ☺️☺️☺️

Name: Three Leafs 三葉茶食
Author: 陳老师Anisah
Review:
We're looking for a nice café for a quick meal and to stretch our tired legs after a long day of walking at the Daan Flower Market.

We ordered pasta - I had  truffle and wild mushroom - it's really good.  Pasta was al-dente, the dish smells scrumptious, I wasn't really hungry before but I ended up finishing the generous serving.  Could do with a wee bit more actual wild mushrooms.

Overall I've enjoyed my meal.
The lady chef was there to take our orders and made sure we're ok.

Enjoyed the tea too.
Will come back for their desserts .

Name: Three Leafs 三葉茶食
Author: Jenny Cheung
Review: artisanal modern tea shop! it’s a social enterprise that contributes to community and improve life of farmer. Very meaningful story.

Name: South Street Delight Tea House
Author: Isabelle L
Review: A wide selection of aromatic teas, including Taiwanese tea, Japanese tea, fruit teas, and others. They also provide tea leaves samples so you can smell and judge each tea before ordering. The environment is warm and vintage—perfect for a relaxing afternoon or to snap a picture. Highly recommend!

Name: South Street Delight Tea House
Author: Julia Sternberg
Review: Delightful experience! The staff was very helpful in picking out a tea and the atmosphere is lovely, very calming. Both of our teas were great, would recommend!

Name: South Street Delight Tea House
Author: Karina Kisselite
Review: Tea house where you can choose your tea based on the description as well as smell. You can also get refills, plus some snacks accompany the tea. Light music and characteristic decor make this tea house a good option for a break. It’s on a second floor of a tea shop.

Name: South Street Delight Tea House
Author: YiHsuan Tseng
Review:
It’s a lovely tea house full of a reminiscent atmosphere. They got various tea collections including those without caffeine. The tea leaves are put into different glass jar so that we can smell their flavors first before deciding which to try.

The desserts are elegant and in traditional Taiwanese style. We are quite satisfied with their sweetness (not too sweet).

The location could be a bit difficult to find. The first floor is a gift shop where displays many interesting cultural and creative commodities derived in Taiwan. Definitely recommend this house for a try!

Name: South Street Delight Tea House
Author: Daniel Santoso
Review:
If you want to try taiwanese tea house experience, you can come here to try. the price is not so pricy for a tea drinking experience. the minimum order is 280nt/person. for 280nt, you will get 1 variant of tea with several traditional desserts and free refill of your hot water for your tea.
when you come, they will give you a menu book that explain each tea that they sell. They will give you the tea leaf samples so you can smell its fragance.
the room is well air conditioned and the music suits your experience. it is located on the 2nd floor of a classic building. so you need to go inside a souvenir shop on the first floor to find a stair to this shop.

Name: Qingtian Tea House
Author: Veteravel
Review:
Tranquility, serenity… shhh don’t talk so loud…
After you sit down, automatically you will switch on your quiet mode and experiencing tea at its tasty form of life. A beautiful place in Taipei when you are tired and wants to have some relaxation to your taste, nostrils, metal, mind and sight!

Name: Qingtian Tea House
Author: Kate Z
Review: Amazing tea experience! The place is beautiful and the staff is very kind and helpful, they explained the tea making process really well! Loved it 😇🍵

Name: Qingtian Tea House
Author: Craig Buller
Review: This is a lovely, Japanese style tea house hiding in a narrow back street. The tea menu is extensive, with a nice range of sweet snacks. The service is exceptional.

Name: Qingtian Tea House
Author: Julien van der waal
Review:
Authentic Taiwanese tea house & experience! This place is the real deal and it is definitely worth going here to learn more about traditional Taiwanese tea culture.

The place itself is stationed within a traditional Taiwanese house, is super relaxing and the staff are very pleasant and calm while also explaining the culture. It is rather expensive, especially if you go solo or with only 2 people. But this is 100% worth it as the experience is priceless.

From the delicious tea & snacks (can especially recommend the pineapple pie) to the amazing ambience created by the calmness & the beautiful decor. You should definitely go here to experience tea on another level.

Name: Qingtian Tea House
Author: Trần Hòa
Review: Visited this place on a rainy afternoon and the atmosphere goes together with tea service totally made my day. I would highly recommend this place when you just need a “hide” from the crowded life

Name: CNSalon
Author: Beth Godley
Review: This tea house was a great find on a rainy day. The tea blends are creative and delicious. Rey, our server, was attentive and patient describing the different tea blends. The tea room was filled with plants, glass, and wool (yes, wool!) items which made for a cozy eclectic atmosphere.

Name: CNSalon
Author: Angela Shih
Review: Lots of plants but mosquitoes too! Average tea, average food . Presentations are good, but very noisy …. I was surprised the place is much more noisy than Starbucks!! Would not recommend.

Name: CNSalon
Author: 邱邱
Review:
This is a family on vacation
Specially arranged place for pilgrimage to the north

Follow CN Flower with friends for a long time
Deeply attracted by high quality
Everywhere you look when you step into the store is full of surprises
Every shot is artistic🌹

A set meal worth more than 400 yuan per person
Choose your favorite tea from the first sniff
You know it’s a high-end herbal tea
Scented tea comes with the utensils delivered
and refreshments with stunning displays
Here both body and mind are healed
The charm of slowing down and chatting with sisters ✨

Full of good taste
Antiques paired with fashionable style
It is also decorated with professional and domineering greenery!
Invite quality relatives and friends to visit
This would be a great first choice
Looking forward to visiting again

Name: CNSalon
Author: YUKI LIU
Review: The beautiful space full of greenery in the city, the store is very comfortable, with thriving plants, comfortable environment furnishings, and elegant aroma. I tasted the delicious tea with full aroma. It is elegant at first, and the sweet aroma that emerges in the aftertaste is amazing. Cute forest-style snacks, worth a visit to spend a pleasant afternoon with friends

Name: CNSalon
Author: Ming-Ruen Wu
Review: The environment in the store is very elegant, full of vanilla aroma, and makes people feel relaxed while sitting there. Ray, one of the service staff in the store, was very patient and professional in introducing herbal tea, which is highly recommended.

Name: Stop By Tea House
Author: Anh C
Review: Lovely shop on a quiet side street in Yong Kang. Cluttered in the right way. Nice teas and great cakes! The Tea Man (most likely the owner) took us to his Tea Narnia in the basement. He feels like an anime character and not real, but one of the cutest people you'll meet.

Name: Stop By Tea House
Author: K “Momo80” K
Review: Quite a hidden at a side street rustic tea house. Many choices of tea and dessert available. Purely for tea and relaxation. It was a pity that tea was not served at the basement area with the winding water way system. That was the main reason for our visit. We didn’t realize there were restrictions. That area is mostly only open for events which can seat  up to 20 people. Price is on request, I don’t want to say it just in case there may be fluctuations. But the owner was kind enough to show us the beautiful designed tea below the store.

Name: Stop By Tea House
Author: Blair Stinson
Review:
Really nice tea house to stop by and relax for a bit. Wife and I had an oolang and white tea which were both fantastic. The small bites we got, dumplings and glutenous cake, were perfect. The owner was super helpful and cute.

I think a tourist politely asked the see the pretty basement event area so he took a group of patrons down to see it. It's pretty neat but far away from the main store, so it makes sense why it's for events only. It's a shame there's some bad reviews from entitled tourists demanding to see the basement when it's not part of the normal tea shop.

Overall highly recommend, the owner was so kind and helpful I will absolutely come back when I'm in Taipei again.

Name: Stop By Tea House
Author: E Tan
Review:
The basement was designed by Mr Shen 沈僥宜 先生. He was an ex-architect and is very passionate about the design of his underground tea experience.

Very casual and relaxed atmosphere at the teahouse thanks to the friendly and hospitable owner.

Name: Stop By Tea House
Author: Joyce Ching
Review: This was such a lovely and cosy place :) A quiet and chill teahouse off the bustling streets of Yongkang. The owner and his customers were very friendly with one other and were very open to share their knowledge about tea. Spent a good afternoon reading with the tea and Chinese snacks. Really loved the welcoming atmosphere here

Name: Thirst quenching company
Address: No. 65, Alley 22, Lane 150, Section 5, Xinyi Rd
Author: Eri Cahyono
Review: Accidentally found this store after hiking in Xiangshan. I asked the staff for the best seller menu, and he recommended Aiyu. It’s traditional Taiwan tea based drink. Then, I chose Pineapple Aiyu, it’s very delicious! Combination of redressing drink with jelly and pineapple with soft texture. One day when I travel to Taiwan again I want to try it again. Anw, they don’t provide chairs or table.

Name: Thirst quenching company
Address: No. 65, Alley 22, Lane 150, Section 5, Xinyi Rd
Author: Pepi To
Review: Perfect after hiking the elephant trail. Tastes good especially with the jelly and the cheese roll. The CHEESE ROLL is the bomb. You really must try it. Friendly owners, great english speakers, answered our questions. All in all amazing place!

Name: Thirst quenching company
Address: No. 65, Alley 22, Lane 150, Section 5, Xinyi Rd
Author: Wen Huang
Review: Location, location, location… as the old saying goes. Well placed drink stall with less common selection of flavors (ie not another bubble tea store) specializing in 愛玉 jelly. Well worth a stop before and after your hike up to 象山

Name: Thirst quenching company
Address: No. 65, Alley 22, Lane 150, Section 5, Xinyi Rd
Author: Toshi Joehren
Review: Incredible Aiyu jelly and drinks at a cheap price. Great location to relax after hiking.

Name: Thirst quenching company
Address: No. 65, Alley 22, Lane 150, Section 5, Xinyi Rd
Author: Xuan Thong
Review: Aiyu jelly drink was indeed a thirst quencher, but only for about 2 mins before the drink settled at room temperature in the summer heat. Flavour and texture of the aiyu jelly itself were average and staff were impatient.

Name: Jassid Tea House 畝士茶堂
Author: Marian Ma
Review: Wonderful experience here. As a tourist speaking limited Mandarin, I was nervous about interacting with native speakers in local stores. But the sales associate gentleman at Jassid was so very kind in explaining things, and he even offered me a sample of the Ruby black tea which was wonderful. I ended up purchasing Ruby black tea and oolong tea! Highly recommend if looking to buy high quality tea leaves or a spot to just sit and sip.

Name: Jassid Tea House 畝士茶堂
Author: Amy
Review: The worker was very nice so I will still give 5 stars but the cheesecake I got was frozen solid. The black oolong tea was very nice.

Name: Jassid Tea House 畝士茶堂
Author: Henry
Review: Beautiful little spot, they have a pretty extensive collection of tea to try, and purchase, staff were friendly. There was another large party that was there when we arrived and unfortunately staffs' attention primarily only went to them which made our party of 2 and another guest being mostly ignored for things like having our tea order taken, waited about 15 minutes after sitting down which made us feel like they forgot about us. I purchased some tea from their collection, 150g of dong ding tea. Have not tried it yet but have opened it and it looks quite good. Am excited to try it out.

Name: Jassid Tea House 畝士茶堂
Author: Malou Maxine
Review: Great teahouse with a very kind owner. Good atmosphere and delicious tea

Name: Jassid Tea House 畝士茶堂
Author: Mark Yeo
Review:
An amazingly cosy place for high quality local tea.

The knowledge level on the teas they serve is high, service is warm and friendly.

A great place to slow down and enjoy the subtleties of the delicate drink that is tea.

Name: Morning Tea House
Author: James Sheu
Review: Lady boss is super friendly, taught the family how to make tea and appreciate them… the scenery is awesome bundled with the fresh air, we could stay here the whole day!!!

Name: Morning Tea House
Author: Kailey Watson
Review: A wonderful place to relax and enjoy the view while drinking tea. I made my own tea, and the staff was very friendly and helpful and communicated very well in English.  For the price, I would probably have stayed longer and had another tea if my schedule had allowed it. I plan on returning next time I’m in Taipei!

Name: Morning Tea House
Author: Hao En
Review:
Nice place to have tea. The tea house sells tea and allow you to enjoy tea by the mountain.

What to expect:
1. The shop sells boxes of tea leaves (you can bring your own)
2. The shop charges by number of people having tea there (fees are for the tea sets)
3. They sell snacks to company the tea, you can choose to bring your own too

Name: Morning Tea House
Author: Wouter Vlas
Review: Really wonderful authentic tea house. The owners are really nice and take the time to show you how to brew your tea. Prices are also really good. Best is when you can sit downstairs among their tea plantation overlooking Taipei.

Name: Morning Tea House
Author: F Paul
Review:
Nice tea place with tea drinking place next to a tea farm. The lady here can speak some English.

You need to buy the tea from the shop first. They'll ask if you want to drink it there. Dont worry, they don't mean you need to finiah the whole big box of tea if you bought a big box. Then go and find a seat either inside the shop area or the outdoor seatings across the street. The staff will fins you and show you how to brew the tea that you bought.

Name: Artist Teahouse
Author: Ash P
Review: I had the pleasure of visiting this beautiful teahouse in March. We felt lucky to find this place which was like an oasis in the midst of heavily crowded Jiufen. We have been to few other famous teahouses in Jiufen - by far Artist teahouse was the best. Our waitress was lovely and very thoroughly explained the menu and tea brewing process. Tea was good, they packed us our leftover tea. Their housemade delicacies (Oolong cheesecake, cookies) were also great. Serene, extraordinary views - perfect to capture the landscape. We could stay here for hours if we had more time! We will return when back in Jiufen.

Name: Artist Teahouse
Author: Jaki Teo
Review:
Definitely not the cheapest tea experience around but arguably the most enjoyable. Artist Teahouse is owned by the same people as Jioufen Teahouse but set in a much less crowded area.

We loved the way the coal for the hot water kettle was set up and it was absolutely perfect for sitting outdoors on a cold day. The servers are knowledgeable about tea and speak good English, Mandarin, and Japanese. The owner is an artist (hence the name of the teahouse) and you use a tea set that he designed!

The tea infused cheesecake was also incredible and a very nice accompaniment to hot tea. Highly recommend for people who actually want to drink tea instead of just taking selfies! You can take the rest of the tea leaves to go if you cannot finish brewing them (quite impossible to drink it all.)

NOTE: Guests can drink tea for a limit of up to 3 hours. There is a surcharge of 100NT per extra person sharing a pot of tea. They don’t have a meal service any more, but there are many savoury and sweet snacks.

Name: Artist Teahouse
Author: Mango Bourdain
Review:
5 stars! I was looking for an A-mei alternative because I didn't want to wait in the long que. Then I found this teahouse nearby and it was such a lovely and relaxing experience!

The shop lady was very friendly and can speak English, Japanese and Chinese!

I HIGHLY recommend the oolong tea cheesecake! It may have been my favorite cheesecake I've ever had so far!

Name: Artist Teahouse
Author: J
Review: Beautiful space away from the crowded alleys- we booked two different tea sets and were shown by a lovely lady the optimal brewing methods. The rest was up to our own memory. Loved our time and experience here.

Name: Artist Teahouse
Author: James Tan
Review: Once you enter the teahouse, head downstairs via the stairs to find the seatings. Find a table either indoors or outdoors and choose the tea. The staff will teach you the steps on how to brew your own tea first. Very interesting experience!

Name: MUSHIN NOSHIN 無心之心
Address: 1樓, No. 20號, Lane 233, Section 1, Dunhua S Rd
Author: Kate Z
Review: The concept is really interesting and cool, the space is very modern zen and the drinks are pretty good 😊

Name: MUSHIN NOSHIN 無心之心
Address: 1樓, No. 20號, Lane 233, Section 1, Dunhua S Rd
Author: Tiffany Wong
Review:
Love the vibe here
So calming and soothing with good drinks and deco
A must visit if you are around the area for quite a unique experience
A rare find i would say

Name: MUSHIN NOSHIN 無心之心
Address: 1樓, No. 20號, Lane 233, Section 1, Dunhua S Rd
Author: Grace Ban
Review: Such a pretty new tea shop/bar concept that’s focused on helping you find your zen. From the Turrell inspired lighting in the front area to the high ceiling and soft, cushiony seating in the back area, this spot is designed with relaxation in mind. We enjoyed our dessert and teas and also really liked the designs on their menu cards. Hope to revisit soon to try out their cocktails in the evening.

Name: MUSHIN NOSHIN 無心之心
Address: 1樓, No. 20號, Lane 233, Section 1, Dunhua S Rd
Author: Hong Chen
Review: A gud place to stay away from the hustle n bustle. Decor is Zen wiz modern. In luv wiz the fame furniture n lovely flower arrangements.

Name: MUSHIN NOSHIN 無心之心
Address: 1樓, No. 20號, Lane 233, Section 1, Dunhua S Rd
Author: Dũng Nguyễn
Review: Would recommend, the atmosphere is perfect for relaxing and hanging out with friends after work.

Name: Wangtea Lab ｜ 當代茶吧 Modern Tea Bar
Address: No. 24號, Lane 64, Section 2, Chongqing N Rd
Author: Patrick Smith
Review: The place is really cool and aesthetic. The tea is great. I had a teaffogato which was different but was very good.  Alot of flavours of tea to choose from. Definitely recommend to try the cakes

Name: Wangtea Lab ｜ 當代茶吧 Modern Tea Bar
Address: No. 24號, Lane 64, Section 2, Chongqing N Rd
Author: Ben Ben
Review: Went when it just opened in the morning. Nice place to relax over Taiwanese tea brewed in either classic or innovative ways. Friendly service. Had two different types of tea: one cold nitro-brewed, the other hot pour-over. Both were excellent.

Name: Wangtea Lab ｜ 當代茶吧 Modern Tea Bar
Address: No. 24號, Lane 64, Section 2, Chongqing N Rd
Author: Y L
Review:
I was amazed by WANGTEA LAB when I went there for the first time I liked there even better when I went there for the second time. This is definitely one of the best places you can find in Taipei for a cozy afternoon alone or hanging out with friends. The quality of their tea and dessert is impeccable with reasonable prices. I tried three kinds of tea and four kinds of dessert during the two visits and all of them are awesome.

First of all, the space can definitely accommodate more tables, but they didn’t put too many tables in the space so that everyone can enjoy their personal space feeling relaxed staying there.

Second, the tea is so good and smooth that you can feel the calmness and comfort. Needless to say, the deserts are delicious beyond words. My favorite is the single one in the picture.

The atmosphere and interior design are just comfortable. On top of everything they offer really good service. If you can only stay in Taipei for a couple of days, this place is definitely my top three recommendations for you.

Name: Wangtea Lab ｜ 當代茶吧 Modern Tea Bar
Address: No. 24號, Lane 64, Section 2, Chongqing N Rd
Author: Natan P
Review: A very cool tea house with a modern twist on tea - we tried the nitro tea which was really excellent, it gives the tea a smooth and cool taste and texture. The owner is really nice, he talked to us about his mission of making natural and healthy tea cool and accessible to the young generation. The menu also has carbonated tea, tea beer, pour-over tea and teapresso.

Name: Wangtea Lab ｜ 當代茶吧 Modern Tea Bar
Address: No. 24號, Lane 64, Section 2, Chongqing N Rd
Author: Andrew Wang
Review: Had the Sunmai Chichong on tap - a delightful and refreshing rendition of the tea beer; will be back for more in-store tea next time. A wonderful ambiance too for relax/work.

Name: Incense Tea House
Author: Vlada Pakhomova
Review:
We've been visiting Taiwan for two weeks in September 2024 and stepped in this tea shop, it was an amazing experience!
We met Aaron, and he was so friendly and helpful, we tried a lot of different types of Oolong, and it was such a great experience. Thank you Aaron!
Probably one of the most pleasant memories of Taiwan will be a visit to this tea shop. 💚💚💚

Name: Incense Tea House
Author: yui yu
Review:
found this place while browsing the google maps. turns out the rating does not lie.
I met Aaron, the owner. he was very nice to recommend the tea that suits me and also spend some time chatting about life while serving tra for me. the fragrant smell of tea smells like mixed with flower.
ends up buying 1 bag tea and a set of tea cups and pot.
He is also generous enough to give tea sealer and 1 more can of tea.

One of the highlights of my first trip to Taiwan. Meeting new people who are generous enough to share their life lessons and view always are considered a present for me.

Hope your business goes well, Aaron.

Name: Incense Tea House
Author: Marcus Siang
Review: It's probably one of the best tea houses/tea shops in Taipei, Taiwan. Very friendly host and shop owner gave us multiple tea to try, plus we get to learn about tea history. Will definitely come back to this place to get more tea.

Name: Incense Tea House
Author: Megh Marciniszyn
Review: A friend spotted this store while out and about and we decided to go back to check it out. The owner is truly kind and so incredibly knowledgeable about all of the pieces and teas in his shop. What I thought would be a quick 10 minute shop ended up being nearly 30-45 minutes of trying teas, learning the history of different pots, and just overall great conversation. With a range of prices between tea leaves and sets, I can't recommend this store enough. Plus international shipping through his website. I wish I could give more stars.

Name: Incense Tea House
Author: H P
Review: We stumbled on this tea shop because I was looking for a tea set. We had looked at several stores and could not fine one we really liked. This store has a wide variety at different price points! The shop owner was very kind and explained to us how different pots and cups affect the taste of the tea. He also served us samples of Alishan high mountain oolong tea and pu'er tea, which were delicious! He told us that he can ship tea to the US as well.

Name: Xiaoyin Tea Temple Dongmen
Address: No. 12-1號, Lane 143, Section 1, Hangzhou S Rd
Author: EightNine (EightNine)
Review:
We had a wonderful experience in this cozy and beautifully decorated tea house! There are few seats but we were able to walk in during mid-afternoon on a weekday.

The staff recommended two oolong teas that complimented each other nicely, as well as some sweet nibbles to go with the tea.

The ambience was relaxing and the space was well curated.

Name: Xiaoyin Tea Temple Dongmen
Address: No. 12-1號, Lane 143, Section 1, Hangzhou S Rd
Author: GIRL Godzilla
Review:
Nice food, especially the spicy dumplings!!
The aroma is wonderful 🥟
The environment is very relaxing
Perfect for drinking  tea 🍵

Limited seats, Remember to reserve a table.

Name: Xiaoyin Tea Temple Dongmen
Address: No. 12-1號, Lane 143, Section 1, Hangzhou S Rd
Author: Meng Shyong Cheng
Review: Excellent hideaway spot to take a break from your walks in Taipei. Nested within a residential area just a block away from the main road, it's a cosy teahouse with a warm atmosphere but the prices are slightly on the steep side. Worth a drop by if you are looking a quick respite.

Name: Xiaoyin Tea Temple Dongmen
Address: No. 12-1號, Lane 143, Section 1, Hangzhou S Rd
Author: Anny Wang
Review:
Cozy place to chat with friends and drink some tea
It’s not worth the value for money but the vibe is really great. You will only come here once, trust me. A memorable one is fine I think.

Name: Xiaoyin Tea Temple Dongmen
Address: No. 12-1號, Lane 143, Section 1, Hangzhou S Rd
Author: Richard Huang
Review: Small traditional tea shop. Limited seats, but cozy atmosphere. Recommend the mochi.

Name: 穆葉茶房MuYèh Tea Room(ReservationOnly)
Author: Debbie Yeh
Review:
We took the Taiwanese Tea Class in English and it was really great and educational! The content and instruction was so well organized and effective - we've been to various tea tours and ceremonies but it never really clicked until it was explained to us in this course. Having all of the teas laid out to taste was a wonderful part of the experience too.

The ambiance in the tea room is very calm and well-decorated. Zoe the owner is truly passionate about tea and it shows in her space and programs. We will definitely come back and bring our friends.

Name: 穆葉茶房MuYèh Tea Room(ReservationOnly)
Author: 簡莉芩
Review:
Surrounded by the bustling Yongkang business district, in a cozy teahouse that offers tranquility amidst the bustle, settle your body and mind into this world of tea banquets!

In this space, the tea master will hold tea art-related courses from time to time. You can also make an appointment via IG private message to taste tea here!

It’s so nice to choose a favorite book for yourself, choose a tea you want to drink, and spend a quiet and peaceful time❤️

Name: 穆葉茶房MuYèh Tea Room(ReservationOnly)
Author: hyeongmin lee
Review:
This is a 3-story tea house located in a quiet alley. The interior using natural wood and light blue creates a comfortable atmosphere.

I was able to enjoy a variety of teas, and one of the great pleasures was seeing the tea utensils suited to each type of tea. The owner spoke really good English, so there was no problem communicating. I don’t think there will be any problem for those who are traveling to enjoy it too! If you are looking for a tea house to stop by during your trip, I recommend it!!

Name: 穆葉茶房MuYèh Tea Room(ReservationOnly)
Author: 王新宜（阿新）
Review:
The space is comfortable! The lady boss is very kind and easy to talk to~

The proprietress has a lot of knowledge about tea. During the tea tasting, she carefully explained the fermentation and roasting degree of the tea, and how to distinguish the quality of the tea.

The description of the taste is very detailed, carefully explaining whether the aroma and taste are consistent or not, and the connection with fermentation.

Spent a pleasant and wonderful night of tea tasting here~

Name: 穆葉茶房MuYèh Tea Room(ReservationOnly)
Author: 佐々木奈採
Review: A hidden teahouse located in a corner of Yongkang Street. The female owner is fluent in both Japanese and English. You can hear a lot about tea, and it's the perfect place to experience the Taiwanese atmosphere. I think you will be able to know the true taste of Taiwanese tea♪

Name: 柳隅茶舍 LiuYu Teahouse （Located in Underground Mall)
Address: Section 1, Xiyuan Rd, 145號10 Basement 1st Floor 地下一樓B1-10
Author: Emi Hockett
Review:
Going to different tea houses is my main hobby in Taiwan and this is by FAR my favorite. Gin speaks English, French, and Chinese, and she truly loves tea and meeting with people from all over the world.

Her tea tasting/education sessions are so much more reasonably priced than any other location I’ve been to, but you can also go to enjoy tea without reserving a session.

I recommend the oriental beauty tea, and the guifei. I also always purchase some dried fruit as well. Everything in that tea house is of the highest quality!

If you’re looking for souvenirs to bring home, definitely pick up some loose leaf or tea bags here. I cant say enough positive words about Gin and her business! If you get into longshan temple station, you can walk through the underground mall to the tea house without needing to go outside!

Name: 柳隅茶舍 LiuYu Teahouse （Located in Underground Mall)
Address: Section 1, Xiyuan Rd, 145號10 Basement 1st Floor 地下一樓B1-10
Author: Wenyi Guan
Review: Cooling place to visit after Longshan temple. Staff are helpful and friendly. Have 2 lovebirds to keep kids entertained

Name: 柳隅茶舍 LiuYu Teahouse （Located in Underground Mall)
Address: Section 1, Xiyuan Rd, 145號10 Basement 1st Floor 地下一樓B1-10
Author: TaiwanWalker
Review: This tea house doesn’t look like those fancy ones at other districts in Taipei, but their choices of tea and the prices are good and reasonable, free dried fruits and snacks would be given to eat with tea, a good place to spend an afternoon.

Name: 柳隅茶舍 LiuYu Teahouse （Located in Underground Mall)
Address: Section 1, Xiyuan Rd, 145號10 Basement 1st Floor 地下一樓B1-10
Author: Lucas Espósito
Review:
Located within the station, opposite to Longshan temple.

The owner speaks perfect English and is very knowledgeable. She helped me to pick a tea among the wide variety she offers.

Dried fruits are included as a snack along with the tea.

Super recommended to anyone who is into tea or just wants to try a teahouse for the first time!

Name: 柳隅茶舍 LiuYu Teahouse （Located in Underground Mall)
Address: Section 1, Xiyuan Rd, 145號10 Basement 1st Floor 地下一樓B1-10
Author: Craig Buller
Review: LIU Yu Teahouse serves traditional Taiwanese teas. Friendly staff will talk you through their tea menu, discuss the flavours you enjoy and make recommendations. They will then show you how to make the perfect cup of tea. You also get some little snacks that compliment the tea flavour. Highly recommended.

Name: tea room SERENDIP
Author: Linda Hsieh
Review: So glad to know this tea house- they sell authentic Ceylon tea. The owner of the tea house is very knowledgeable and the service is excellent! They also host tea tasting events to enjoy Ceylon tea in various ways and will introduce you the history of the tea

Name: tea room SERENDIP
Author: Mekko
Review:
One of my favorite places in Taipei. The owners are extremely knowledgeable and welcoming (in Chinese and English!). Within a couple visits, we became friends, and I not only learned about tea from them, but had many good times bringing friends, meeting people, and trying teas (and styles of serving teas) from around the world.

Especially if you're a foreigner interested in tea, this is an amazing place to come. The owners have lived abroad, are very welcoming, and enjoy connecting people. I rarely point this out as I speak Chinese fluently, but they truly know how to welcome all kinds of people into their store and make them feel happy to be there. So glad I accidentally stumbled here on a hot afternoon first date!

Name: tea room SERENDIP
Author: Benjamin Schultz
Review: What an interesting great idea, to bring to Taiwan high quality black tea/ from other countries including Ceylon and Nepal. The owner has an extensive knowledge on this subject. Highly recommend if you want to try something new.

Name: tea room SERENDIP
Author: A G
Review: Cool place for tea time … knowledgeable boss

Name: tea room SERENDIP
Author: 澎大海（同學你什麼時候搬走）
Review:
Excellent service and unforgettable tea flavors
Love your service and handmade dessert!!!!!!!!!

Name: 蒔現 Tea House
Author: Drawing Illustration
Review: We came in on a rainy night and the Tea shop owner was super friendly and made us feel at home. Inside was a warm atmosphere with a lovely artist vibe, the interior design and the decor was comfy and nice. The traditional tea selection very great and on the menu there were even some very creative tea cocktails. We had a delicious floral tea and another earl grey with whisky and a milk cap. Very tasty.

Name: 蒔現 Tea House
Author: Sayaka Sensui
Review: Nice atmosphere and chill. Taiwanese tea taste awesome ;)

Name: 蒔現 Tea House
Author: Asiri
Review:
Fantastic place owned by adorable women.
Highly recommend for Tea lover

Name: 蒔現 Tea House
Author: Yuki Takeuchi
Review: Best place to have a tea in Taipei ✨✨

Name: 蒔現 Tea House
Author: Lenneth Chen
Review: I like the atmosphere of this old house, so I ordered tea and wine, which are delicious. After the milk cap is stirred, it has another flavor, which is very smooth. Friends who were traveling with us ordered tea and also praised it highly.

Name: Day Eat Tea House & Co.
Author: Kevin Barker
Review: Came here for breakfast and enjoyed it quite well! The restaurant is a bit further down off the main path in a quiet area. The breakfast was a nice mix of veggies, bread, fruit, and eggs! The sausage was a little weird, but not bad. Would suggest for breakfast if you're in the area.

Name: Day Eat Tea House & Co.
Author: paulneo
Review: First time at this cozy cafe as it was raining. It was recommended by our home-stay boss. Their caramel macchiato is good. Pork curry is nice. Wish the waffle is softer.

Name: Day Eat Tea House & Co.
Author: CKoon H
Review:
The pork curry was surprisingly yummy, the best food I had in Jiufen! The meat was soft and tender, I could eat without chewing.

The owner is friendly and thoughtful.

The seaweed soup was served in a cup, which made it easier to drink.

While waiting for food, there is a cute cat for entertainment.

I would recommend this shop to anyone visiting Jiufen to stop by and try the best pork curry in Taiwan.

Name: Day Eat Tea House & Co.
Author: Steng S
Review: We had a very good experience dining here. We could tell lots of efforts were put into the preparation of the food. We love the pork curry and the bagel. Fruit salad was very fresh and the taiwanese fruit tea is very refreshing

Name: Day Eat Tea House & Co.
Author: Dr. Rona S.
Review: Highly recommended! Food is excellent and very good service!!! Prices are affordable…tea is amazing…must try! The chef and the staff is very kind,friendly and helpful

Name: 貓空金盆茶園 Gold Basin Tea Garden
Author: Ben Ben
Review: Friendly lady owner brewed all the different types of tea (wenshan baozhong/pouchong, tie guanyin/kwanyin and wulong/oolong) available for me to taste. Patiently she explained the difference in processing each type, resulting in unique aroma and taste profiles. She took me to the uppermost floor to view how tea leaves are dried and roasted. She explained the leaves were harvested from own plantation/farm and hers is 4th generation family business. While she and her husband are still active, her son will be taking over the business. As all the teas I tasted were fresh and good, I bought all 5 types (one wenshan baozhong/pouchong, 2 types of tie guanyin/kwanyin,  2 types of wulong/oolong).

Name: 貓空金盆茶園 Gold Basin Tea Garden
Author: Beverly Fung
Review: Both owners are warm and nice! We entered because we wanted to buy the tea and still they offered to let us try it first. They performed the traditional tea serving. You can see the tea was high quality and they have many recognitions displayed. Also they provided extra gift packages. Auntie and Uncle guided us to the bus so we dont have to walk back. The bus station is right in front of the store.

Name: 貓空金盆茶園 Gold Basin Tea Garden
Author: J M Rossy
Review: Cute little shop, very friendly people. Great tea of course

Name: 貓空金盆茶園 Gold Basin Tea Garden
Author: Jeni Sis (Jenisis)
Review: Very friendly and experienced tea masters. They grow and make their own Tea and it tastes Great!!!

Name: 貓空金盆茶園 Gold Basin Tea Garden
Author: Tiffany
Review: Amazing!!!!! You should give it a try

Name: Sunyang Tea House
Author: m n
Review: The Maokong area harvests teas, which can be enjoyed at the tea cafe. I loved the local tea - especially the lighter colored Oolong.  A minimum order of 150 NTD per person.

Name: Sunyang Tea House
Author: krō waque
Review:
4th and 5th

Obsessed over their ginger tea chicken after first tasting it 3 years back, I visited Taiwan in January 2024 and June 2024 to no avail, as no hot dishes were served on both occasions. The grandmother remained sweet while giving me the bad news with a smile, so I settled on the fruit tea fusion and tea cheesecake (frankly, just passable tastewise) most recently.

2nd and 3rd

Food and tea remain excellent in taste.

Unassuming yet delicious!

You must never come here if you are in a rush as service takes longer than usual, but the quality of food makes the wait more than worth it.

Sun in summer  is scorching but the mountain breeze alleviates the heat factor.

1st

Ginger chicken at Sunyang was a revelation, becoming my favorite dish in Taiwan,  a must-try over and over.

I sorely missed their chili pepper stirfry.

Impeccable service, but never quick.

Awesome view.

Name: Sunyang Tea House
Author: Magdalene Lim
Review: The milk tea was very cold and delicious for a hot day. Food wise, we found it rather oily for our tastes and did not enjoy it as much as we had hoped, especially for the price paid. Additionally, they only accept cash. However, it is near the train station and has great views.

Name: Sunyang Tea House
Author: Nomadian King
Review:
Only come here for their tea selection, nothing else.

The food is abysmal. The cabbage was just literally boiled, and the egg pancake was still raw and drenched in oil. The pork was like eating jerky instead of stir-fry.

The only good thing is the outside veranda with a great view and their tea selection.

Name: Sunyang Tea House
Author: Malou Camara
Review: Spent some time here to enjoy the view, the breeze and some good tea and snacks.  Nice to be surrounded by greenery and see local sights that are so accessible from Taipei.  Highly recommend this activity!

Name: Xiaoman Tea Experience
Author: atsuhiro shima
Review:
You can have a great time.
A cozy space, delicious tea, and kind, friendly staff - everything you need for a wonderful time.

I would like to come back and try some new tea.

Name: Xiaoman Tea Experience
Author: Xaviera Ho
Review:
Setting the location at 小慢 since it is the same address, but the shop is @wan__taipei , a new open dessert bowl shop in town.

I tried two desserts out of three.

RED is a flan with mixed spiced boba on top. I love the boba with this earthy herbal touch, but the flan wasn’t as compatible.

YELLOW on the other hand was the hero. Frozen mango and passion fruit mixture on soft tofu tamed my tropical soul properly.

#food #dessert #sweet #bowl #flan #boba #douhua #tofu #taipei #taiwan #weekender #summer

Name: Xiaoman Tea Experience
Author: Erling W.
Review: Wonderful place for a quiet afternoon tea ceremony in the comparably relaxed Taiwanese style which is nothing as rigid as the formal Japanese tea ceremony. Best enjoyed with friends for a “tea and chill” Sunday vibe. Large selection of Taiwan specialty teas directly sourced in Taiwan’s tea mountains. Try the "White Tea" (白茶). Very knowledgeable owner.   You can also book a special Taiwan tea experience ceremony. Small but exquisite desert side dishes, I especially liked the green bean ice cream.

Name: Xiaoman Tea Experience
Author: YanZhen Lin (Peggy)
Review: The space is very well curated. The teas were well selected from the shop. Very elegant space for a quiet tea afternoon with books or conversation.

Name: Xiaoman Tea Experience
Author: Maria Jeong
Review: I had a headache while traveling, and I could stay comfortable while drinking tea. The taste of tea, which varies subtly with the temperature of water, was also a new experience.

Name: Maokong Tea House
Author: Momo
Review: I tried the ice cream with tapioca, it was really really good. You can taste the tea right away, and the tapioca was perfect. Not to sweet, good balance of flavor. The cookie also was not to bad. I also buyed some tea there (if you forgot, at the gondola station of the Taipei zoo, they also sell some of the same tea, for the same price!). Anyway the location is perfect right in front at the station, 10/10.

Name: Maokong Tea House
Author: Lin Huihui Maya
Review: The tea and cheesecake was yummy. The service was not so bad. But the cafe is small, hence it gets really crowded and uncleared tables due to insufficient turnover time.

Name: Maokong Tea House
Author: Josh Leard
Review: Dude... This place is amazing. The tea is soooo good! We got the kumquat citron tea, fruit tea, and the local brew. My gosh it was good, favorite part of the day so far! I would definitely come back.

Name: Maokong Tea House
Author: david chin
Review: The first highlight after alighting from the gondolas on 13th Oct is to try the oolong tea ice cream here. The staff is friendly and the environment is warm. As my ice cream had melted while placed on hold at counter before I need to use the toilet urgently, the staff kindly replaced it free of charge after I got back from the toilet.

Name: Maokong Tea House
Author: Xiao Xuan
Review:
Staff was friendly and kind to explain the variety of Teas. Environment was nice, air conditioning provided, and plenty seats available.

Oolong tea ice cream was slightly expensive, but the tea flavour was not very strong, it was mild only. The cookie is very cute, and according to the staff, it is freshly baked daily on site.

Would recommend, but would not try again.

Name: Yi Bei Tea
Address: No. 54之1號 之 號, Yongkang St
Author: SH Kim
Review: Lovely place for tea lover. You can taste hiquality taiwan tea and chinese tea. Host will welcome you with warm tea.

Name: Yi Bei Tea
Address: No. 54之1號 之 號, Yongkang St
Author: 손미옥
Review: The atmosphere is great and you can see and purchase various things related to cars.

Name: Yi Bei Tea
Address: No. 54之1號 之 號, Yongkang St
Author: 韓奕
Review:
A leisure space for professional artworks, collections, and Pu'er tea.

There are many special numbers to drink, which are worth pondering.

Name: Yi Bei Tea
Address: No. 54之1號 之 號, Yongkang St
Author: 廖淑愉

Name: Yi Bei Tea
Address: No. 54之1號 之 號, Yongkang St
Author: 劉小伶

Name: Ruth Fang Art & Culture Teahouse
Author: MNML journey
Review: Really lovely traditional experience with tons of history. The owner is proud to share his story and about making teas. The food is very high quality. The ambience is beautiful.

Name: Ruth Fang Art & Culture Teahouse
Author: Tony Fu
Review: Beautiful tea house, full of art pieces, feels like a scene from a Ghibli animation. The lamb hot pot (take a while to prep, but you can phone the restaurant to order before you arrive) and fish dishes are very tasty. Very hospitable owner, too.

Name: Ruth Fang Art & Culture Teahouse
Author: Quentin Pan
Review:
This tea house doesn't feel like a restaurant; instead, the coziness feels like home.

The shop owner is an artist, and many of the decorations were made by himself. I really enjoy the vintage style, and would definitely recommend this place for people who wish to sit down and chat with old friends.

We ordered braised pork with potatoes, sliced pork with garlic sauce, and stewed fish (each with rice, boiled vegetable, and soup). The food here seemed quite healthy. And they're the homely dishes.

There is a park near this tea house. Having a stroll there after meal is relaxing.

Name: Ruth Fang Art & Culture Teahouse
Author: Yuen Yu Ping
Review: Aesthetic atmosphere, delicious fish and tea

Name: Ruth Fang Art & Culture Teahouse
Author: KY
Review: Interesting tea house, fish meal tasteful and the owner, master painter, mr WANG is an interesting host

Name: Teapook 茶舖客
Author: Yunjung Cho
Review: They explained the tea well and both the traditional tea and milk tea they recommended were delicious :) I was very touched when they said "Welcome to Taiwan" while serving me. It was hot in the middle of the day, but I took a calm and satisfying tea time and came out. I recommend it. The sign doesn't say teapook, but it's on the first floor as shown on Google Maps. (H was in it, but I can’t remember the exact name.)

Name: Teapook 茶舖客
Author: tiwb
Review:
The environment is great and the tea is deliciouswww
It’s a very high-quality store in Tamkang area and very suitable for gatherings.

Name: Teapook 茶舖客
Author: antireligion 11
Review: The tea is very particular and the desserts are exquisite

Name: 老叢茶圃
Author: 法克友
Review: Life is art, and art is a model of life. Good tea, good pot, good cup, good life, good products with heart is the real marketing!

Name: 老叢茶圃
Author: 吳佩玉
Review:
It is a high-quality merchant of contemporary ceramic artists producing exquisite tea sets, tea mats, installation art, and tea products.
Worth visiting.

Name: 老叢茶圃
Author: 陳奕仲
Review: The proprietress is kind and generous, and the gift box is very tasteful, making it a decent gift and affordable for your own use.

Name: 老叢茶圃
Author: Chen Mervyn
Review: I passed by accidentally and found that there was such a quiet corner at the door. It was really great.

Name: 老叢茶圃
Author: 恩恩
Review:
The black tea is great. The boss introduced it carefully.
Tea sets are great authors

Name: FUSENSEN
Author: Ada Chang
Review: The boss is very friendly and knows how to make tea. He patiently listens to your needs and finds the right tea. He highly recommends it as a gift, for personal use or for business use.

Name: FUSENSEN
Author: Kay Wen
Review: The boss is very kind, professional and good at making tea. The product packaging is high-quality, practical and can be customized. The tea is really delicious! !

Name: FUSENSEN
Author: 釋體輪佛教學系學士班2017
Review: The young boss has friendly service, superb tea skills, and affordable tea prices.

Name: FUSENSEN
Author: 박지혜
Review: The store owner is very friendly and makes delicious tea. After writing this, it seems like a really cliché comment, but… It's real. I was a little scared when I went in because there were few reviews, but I liked it so much that I am leaving a review. Many Koreans, please come!!

Name: FUSENSEN
Author: Wü
Review: Whether you need wedding gifts or want to drink tea, this is a good choice😊

Name: Jinjiang Tea House
Author: Frank Young
Review: Fantastic hakka food. Great low brow ambience. Small place and crowded with limited seats. Must have the taiwanese water lily. The green veggies photo. The chicken with their kumquat sauce. And the shrimp paste spring roll. Pork oil rice is interesting but can give it a pass.

Name: Jinjiang Tea House
Author: Andre
Review: Great authentic experience. The salted pork was delicious. Shrimp rolls were not what I was expecting but delicious none the less and the tofu with peanut I think was amazing. Try the smoked plum juice!

Name: Jinjiang Tea House
Author: Ivo Niet
Review: Never disappointed when eating here. Although the food is greasy by default, it's full of flavor and extremely well prepared. The restaurant has a very charming, homey atmosphere. Service is attentive and friendly. This time we tried the ostrich which is a little chewy by nature but if you've never tried it, this would be a good place to start. The mushrooms with the salted eggs were on point taste and texture wise and the tofu was outstanding, would order both dishes again in an instant. Try their 擂茶 Lei Cha, a type of mashed tea rich in protein and fiber. While not too pricey, the menu is a bit on the expensive side, otherwise I'd definitely dine here more often!

Name: Jinjiang Tea House
Author: toni neo
Review: 2 of us went there for lunch. Lady boss was very hospitable. Helped us to choose 5 dishes from their extensive menu. Everything dish is home cooked flavour and so good!! A must visit if u r in Taipei. It’s just 3 stations from Ximending. Will definitely come back again!

Name: Jinjiang Tea House
Author: Abhinav Jadon
Review: Noticed this place walking around the house, later checked the ratings and reviews and decided would definitely check this out. The food was amazing, the setting cozy. That’s what fulfilling meals are made of.

Name: 埕畔茶坊 CHENGPION Tea House（建議先來電預約 Reservation Please）
Author: Betty Wong
Review:
For a real tea enthusiast and also newbie like me, I absolutely love this shop. The shop itself is beautiful and modern. The owner is extremely knowledgeable and walked me through the different teas, teaching me about the types, the leaves, the best way to make it, the type of water to use, etc. The teas are so high quality and the price is so reasonable for it. I wanted to mail a tea to a friend overseas, and she even offered to mail it for me.

I can't wait to come back. I highly recommend everyone to come here for a true, immersive tea experience!

Name: 埕畔茶坊 CHENGPION Tea House（建議先來電預約 Reservation Please）
Author: Suddenly S
Review: I recommend every one (interested in Tea or not) to go to this shop. The owner is very courteous, intelligent, respectful and will share his incredible knowledge of Tea with you. He has 12 years of experience in this business and has provided me with a customer service I have never experienced elsewhere. Be sure to plan at least 1 hour for your visit as you will be guided to choose the tea that suits you best. As the owner said - it’s not about price or high altitude as many people think, it’s about what tea tastes best to you. I absolutely agree with this statement and had a wonderful time here. I recommend everyone that plans on buying tea in Taiwan to buy it here. I will definitely come back the next time I visit Taiwan.

Name: 埕畔茶坊 CHENGPION Tea House（建議先來電預約 Reservation Please）
Author: Emma Louise Wallis
Review: My friends and I had a wonderful experience with Arno at Chengpion Tea House. Arno was kind enough to speak English with us (a group of 4 foreigners) and his English was excellent. He gave us his full attention for two whole hours and let us try a wide variety of teas, explaining everything about each tea that we tried - and for free! Arno was very friendly and made us feel very welcome and relaxed. We all came away from this experience extremely happy and touched by his kindness. We very happily bought some of our favorite teas from him, and we will definitely come back again with other friends. Thank you, Arno!

Name: 埕畔茶坊 CHENGPION Tea House（建議先來電預約 Reservation Please）
Author: Steven Huang
Review: I rarely leave reviews but I want to say I really love this place. The owner is very knowledgeable and the tea is very good. Will definitely come back next time.

Name: 埕畔茶坊 CHENGPION Tea House（建議先來電預約 Reservation Please）
Author: ブトン
Review: Boss Arno was very knowledgeable and passionate on tea especially ơn delivery the best taiwan tea to customers, bringing the best experience to customers.
