The entire tea tasting session was relaxing and we enjoyed chatting with Arno. His selection of tea was amazing (he sources for his own tea for every tea season) and I had a difficulty choosing which tea to buy.

Name: 水澐靝茶舖xMuMu
Author: Madeline Matthys
Review: We had an absolutely amazing time here! The owner was so attentive, explained the history of teas and brought over many other teas for us to try, with explanations of the tasting notes and origins. He also helped us figure out the itinerary for the rest of our trip. Such a wonderful afternoon here - definitely would come back and recommend to friends! Thank you!

Name: 水澐靝茶舖xMuMu
Author: cheng jessica
Review: It's a warm and cozy place for chilling. Will def. visit again.

Name: 水澐靝茶舖xMuMu
Author: Imane EL ARRASSI
Review: Nice place for the tea time!

Name: 水澐靝茶舖xMuMu
Author: Jenny K
Review:
A very special and attentive tea shop. The drinks are delicious. We particularly love their hot apple cinnamon tea. It is rich in fruity aroma and has a mellow tea taste. It is very delicious. They also have mille-feuille cake🍰. I personally think it is better than Taiwan’s. The Lady M’s is delicious😋, but unfortunately it’s sold out if I go to buy it tonight🥺.

*I didn’t order chocolate layer cake today, so I ordered tiramisu instead. After trying it, I found it tasted ordinary and a little disappointed. Fortunately, there was also hot apple grapefruit-flavored cinnamon tea.

Name: 水澐靝茶舖xMuMu
Author: tony Li
Review:
Tianmu Hidden Edition Tea Shop is comparable to Tianmu's famous landmark Tianmu 7 Section famous 🌟Buck. Personally, I feel that going to 🌟Barker to check in and take photos is outdated. Come to this Tianmu Hidden Edition Internet Beauty Lady Afternoon Tea to check in and take photos, and the interior decoration Beautiful design, it looks like a lot of money was spent, very careful decoration design, unique style, visual inspection only cost about 3 million less to design such a comfortable and beautiful environment, five-star recommendation 👍How to say 5⃣️ 🌟Recommendation? Every corner of this store is a photo spot for internet celebrities and beauty. It looks great no matter how you take it. Everyone’s internet beauty wall is everywhere🤣There is nothing to say about the tea and snacks inside. I just don’t know how to drink tea. Tea lovers will know that tea is a drink brewed from good tea leaves! It is impossible to brew the cheap tea from chain stores with the quality of their tea. The Pu'er tea has a strong and mellow aroma, and the aftertaste of the front, middle and back. Regardless of whether you drink tea or not, in the comfortable environment of this store, I believe that everyone will You can feel it all. Good tea allows you to open up the Rendu and Dumai. The high-temperature tea leaves, taste carefully, the tea flavor and tea aroma are really delicious. Needless to say, the tiramis in the store is sweet but not greasy. It’s really paired with their Pu’er pearl milk tea🧋Take it carefully and enjoy it slowly. You’ll really be immersed in the scene and feel like you’re a noble lady🫣🫣🫣
Good store highly recommended. Everyone can come and taste good tea, live comfortably and leisurely like a noble lady, and no longer have to go to the old-fashioned 🌟8g check-in and take photos. After visiting once, you will know that this shop will make everyone take endless photos... Recommend 👍👍👍Wandering food critic.

Name: Longya Tea House
Address: No. 24之2號, Lane 40, Section 3, Zhinan Rd
Author: Joellene Buccat
Review: I highly recommend visiting this beautiful, hidden tea house in MaoKong! After our gondola ride, we took the mini bus and walked a short distance to the tea house entrance. As we walked down a short path, we felt transported to a magical garden situated near a stream. We were the only guests and had such a peaceful experience compared to the nearby crowded tea houses (around 11am). The family who owns the place were so welcoming even though we don’t speak Mandarin. The woman who works there was able to communicate to my wife in Cantonese. Order the tea noodles and tieguanyin tea which is grown there.

Name: Longya Tea House
Address: No. 24之2號, Lane 40, Section 3, Zhinan Rd
Author: Kate Z
Review: Love the vibe of this place, felt like in a rain forrest 😊 amazing tea 🍵 and overall great place to relax. Also the cat was cute 😁

Name: Longya Tea House
Address: No. 24之2號, Lane 40, Section 3, Zhinan Rd
Author: Lisa Laeven
Review: Beautiful tea house with large garden next to a small creek. Calm and relaxing atmosphere away from the crowds. Owner is very friendly and helpful, reasonable prices for tea too. Will definitely go back!

Name: Longya Tea House
Address: No. 24之2號, Lane 40, Section 3, Zhinan Rd
Author: David Merryman
Review:
A must visit when in Maokong!  The Longya Tea House is a family run business which gives it that local Maokong charm! The entire family is very personable and bring a hospital energy as they make you feel welcome; almost as if you’re part of the family.

If you are an English speaker, ask for Eric. His English is near perfect as he fluently spoke about his job as an Engineer and his upcoming trip to the United States for work.  Not only is Eric well traveled and educated, but he still dedicates time to help his parents run their family tea plantation, tea house, and restaurant!

Eric and I sat together and chatted for a few hours, and yet it only felt like minutes. The Longya Tea House provided us an atmosphere nestled alongside a flowing creek. I couldn’t help but smile as Eric and I talked while the sounds of waterfalls echoed around us.

I will be back to Longya Tea House, and I recommend you stop by yourself for an extraordinary experience!

Name: Longya Tea House
Address: No. 24之2號, Lane 40, Section 3, Zhinan Rd
Author: Shane H. Lin
Review: Really nice place with high quality (but affordable) tea. The owner family (including niu-niu the cat) are very approachable. We had a great time enjoying tea and chatting with the family. Highly recommend and will definitely pay another visit!

Name: 洛可可茶苑 Rococo Tea House
Author: 許志強
Review: a great variety of cuisine can be chosen,  good taste and great experience

Name: 洛可可茶苑 Rococo Tea House
Author: Janet C Lee
Review: excellent.

Name: 洛可可茶苑 Rococo Tea House
Author: chia i hou

Name: 洛可可茶苑 Rococo Tea House
Author: 鄭秋雯
Review: Good

Name: 洛可可茶苑 Rococo Tea House
Author: Luo Fion
Review: Food is delicious🤩

Name: French Windows British Teahouse 琺蘭綺瑥朵英式茶餐館
Address: No. 51-1, Alley 22, Lane 553, Section 4, Zhongxiao E Rd
Author: Ricky Ngai
Review:
Not sure if my experience is super unique. That night we have dinner. The staff are friendly and familiar with the manual. Two things at least for the dinner:
1. long waiting time, 3 hours to deliver 3 dish
2. average pasta taste, as u see in the photo I upload, this kind of sausage pasta with average taste I can cook a better one at home. To be honest I don’t believe my eye when I see it as I try to see where is the hidden camera of the TV joke show.

The quality of food is completely not align with the price. At least it’s on the perspective from a tourist from Hong Kong

Name: French Windows British Teahouse 琺蘭綺瑥朵英式茶餐館
Address: No. 51-1, Alley 22, Lane 553, Section 4, Zhongxiao E Rd
Author: Christina Cheng
Review: Hawke, the one employee working and cooking here when we went, was really attentive and considerate. He was working really hard while also providing good service. The food was exceptional and the spaghetti was cooked perfectly al dente, like the traditional dish in Italy. The desserts are all fresh and warm with unique dishes full of flavor. The tea here is the main event with all types you can choose and smell. Overall, my experience at this restaurant was amazing and I will definitely be coming back.

Name: French Windows British Teahouse 琺蘭綺瑥朵英式茶餐館
Address: No. 51-1, Alley 22, Lane 553, Section 4, Zhongxiao E Rd
Author: Y Lau
Review:
The food is incredible, highly recommend the "grey pesto"
The waiter explains every dish in detail. It feels like you are in 5-star hotel.

Name: French Windows British Teahouse 琺蘭綺瑥朵英式茶餐館
Address: No. 51-1, Alley 22, Lane 553, Section 4, Zhongxiao E Rd
Author: Michelle Chan
Review:
From the moment I walked in, I was greeted with a warm and friendly smile from the staff. The atmosphere was elegant yet inviting, and the decor was tastefully done.

The menu offered a variety of options, and each dish was beautifully presented. The food itself was absolutely delicious, bursting with flavor and cooked to perfection.

But what truly made this dining experience exceptional was the attentive service. The staff was knowledgeable about the menu and offered helpful recommendations, and they made sure that our every need was met throughout the meal.

Overall, I highly recommend this restaurant to anyone looking for an outstanding dining experience

Name: French Windows British Teahouse 琺蘭綺瑥朵英式茶餐館
Address: No. 51-1, Alley 22, Lane 553, Section 4, Zhongxiao E Rd
Author: William Liu
Review:
Our host Hawke is exceedingly diligent, delivering great service despite being apparently drained.

Overall, the food is delicately-placed and well-decorated. The service also received a high compliment from my dad, which is exceptionally rare.

Name: Gaoli Zhao Ming Tea
Author: Baby Lion
Review:
Very good service and good tea.
I love it !!!!!

Name: Gaoli Zhao Ming Tea
Author: Qiaoru Han
Review: Good service and explanation of the staff. The quality of tea leaves is great too!

Name: Gaoli Zhao Ming Tea
Author: kelvin 4806
Review: Nice service and really welcomed,friendly.

Name: Gaoli Zhao Ming Tea
Author: 배유진 YUJIN B
Review: Very kind and the tea is very delicious ;D

Name: Gaoli Zhao Ming Tea
Author: ES KIM
Review: So kind and nice tea.

Name: 小隱茶庵 信義店
Address: Alley 14, Lane 150, Section 5, Xinyi Rd, 8號1樓
Author: Anjani Ganapathy
Review: We loved sitting here for a cozy, peaceful afternoon on a rainy and chilly day in Taipei! The tea was excellent and you can re-steep as much as you want. Would definitely come back and recommend to anyone in the area who wants to try local tea!

Name: 小隱茶庵 信義店
Address: Alley 14, Lane 150, Section 5, Xinyi Rd, 8號1樓
Author: TaiwanWalker
Review: A calm and quiet tea room with a lot of tea choices, a good place to enjoy tea and catch up with friends. We wanted to eat ming bean cakes and glutinous rice balls skewers but they’re sold out the day we went, the food was a bit disappointing to us.

Name: 小隱茶庵 信義店
Address: Alley 14, Lane 150, Section 5, Xinyi Rd, 8號1樓
Author: Sandy Chiang
Review: Great food and friendly service! A quiet place to enjoy the tranquility in the city.

Name: 小隱茶庵 信義店
Address: Alley 14, Lane 150, Section 5, Xinyi Rd, 8號1樓
Author: Lily T
Review: Loved the atmosphere and the decoration in the tea house. Food ,desserts and tea are very delicious and dedicated. Will definitely come by again. Recommend coming and chill with friends or family. Only one thing is not sure why they require the minimum order is $380 per person if we already ordered a lunch-set $250..

Name: 小隱茶庵 信義店
Address: Alley 14, Lane 150, Section 5, Xinyi Rd, 8號1樓
Author: 전지현
Review:
traditional desserts are wonderful.
oolong tea is extraordinariliy nice.
i was touched.
(+also kitty was so kind

Name: Uncle Well Tea
Author: Sergey Glushkin
Review: My girlfriend and I visited this tea house, and we were very pleased! The bill came to 640 TWD, which is quite reasonable for the experience. The view from the tea house is stunning — with a green mountain and a city panorama creating a cozy and harmonious atmosphere. The staff were friendly and attentive, helping us choose and explaining everything. And the tea was simply amazing — very delicious and fragrant! We definitely recommend this place for a relaxing and enjoyable visit.

Name: Uncle Well Tea
Author: I CY
Review:
This place is a real gem. We stumbled upon this place after descending from Maokong station. Upon arriving we felt very welcome.

The minimalistic concrete aesthetics gives this place the vibe! Also, the ceramics and tea + food presentation is A1 🥹✨.

The wintermelon iced tea is such a refreshing drink after a bit of hiking.

Name: Uncle Well Tea
Author: Lilykate B
Review:
So first of all I haven’t been to exactly this place, but I’ve met Uncle Well at the Taipei Tea Promotion Center 2 km down the street.
He’s really nice and produces some amazing tea. Unfortunately I didn’t have time to go to his tea house, but I’d recommend it anyway since his tea is one of the best I’ve tasted in Asia.

Good luck good Sir!

Name: Uncle Well Tea
Author: Heiko RUDOLPH
Review: Tea making equipment everywhere, you can drink the tea, talk chat hang out. Owner talked with us, made tea for us. He spent his life in tea and knows a lot about tea. Makes it the old style way, slowly by himself. ORGANIC, very nice flavour. Small plantation, can only buy tea at the door.  Groups come for a tea experience, info and sampling tea.

Name: Uncle Well Tea
Author: Tommy T.
Review: Great tea experience. Compliment with their snacks, the tea experience is wonderful. Saw Uncle Well himself, giving a tutorial on tea.

Name: Zhuang ZI Tea House
Author: R P
Review: I came to the place but no store on ground floor.. Found a office door on 9 floor but place was closed at 12.52 afternoon 🤷‍♂️ not sure if this shop still exists.. Definitely nothing like a shop on the pictures here

Name: Zhuang ZI Tea House
Author: 김지원
Review:
best oolong tea ever!
I stopped by the store because it’s just near the hotel I stayed and bought the cheapest one for just spend the rest of the money
but it tastes amazing
& it’s really worth the money than other expensive ones like Wangdechaun

Name: Zhuang ZI Tea House
Author: 이동현
Review: This tea house might be the best tea house in Taipei. I had the best cup of tea in my life. Thank you again.

Name: Zhuang ZI Tea House
Author: Yi-Fang Huang
Review: Well packaged great quality tea

Name: Zhuang ZI Tea House
Author: 黃曜晟
Review: Good！！！

Name: Youshan Tea Visit Qingtian Club
Author: ys k1
Review:
I had the most amazing tea experience with the world's kindest tea professional, Yuria!
She asked me my favorite kinds of tea, and gave me some recommendations.
Then she brewed tea for me, which was so professional and graceful, and I could learn how to brew tea from her. It was amazing to enjoy the scent and flavor of tea. This is the second time that I visit Taiwan and I have been in love with Youshan Tea since my first visit.
Also the price is affordable for me. Whoever wants to experience tea should visit this place!

Name: Youshan Tea Visit Qingtian Club
Author: Phil L
Review:
Awesome tea shop!
Impeccable service and really great tea.
We bought many different varieties after trying many of them.
Highly recommend to those that want great tea to drink or gift

Name: Youshan Tea Visit Qingtian Club
Author: Jerald Kng
Review: Good place to get authentic Taiwan tea, the lady staff was very hospitable, served us tea to try, and recommended different tea based on our personal preferences

Name: Youshan Tea Visit Qingtian Club
Author: Chocolate Mint
Review:
The employees are very friendly, warm, kind and helpful. They can speak english very well and will patiently guide you to figure out which tea is the most suitable to your taste!
The teas are amazing 🤩 I would recommend to go there to try out their tea!

Name: Youshan Tea Visit Qingtian Club
Author: I v y Y
Review: I was looking for gifts to bring back to the States and I found this tea shop. It was small but cozy. The hostess was super friendly. She introduced different choices of tea as I was looking for something more on the lighter and sweeter side. She also let me tasted some of them before I made up my mind. I left the shop happily as I didn't only buy teas for my friends, but also bought some for myself!

Name: 九份茶行 山海坊1983 Shanhai Tea House 1983
Author: Dane Lee
Review: The bus tour guide introduced it to me, and I bought it like I was fascinated by the tea that I could taste on the spot in a cool space. I bought Alisan oolong tea (black tea) and Oriental Beauty tea, and the price was cheaper than I thought, and the tea was fragrant the way I made it in Korea. Tea in Taiwan Recommended to buy!

Name: 九份茶行 山海坊1983 Shanhai Tea House 1983
Author: Ahn혜준
Review: It is the most luxurious and excellent quality among Taiwanese oolong teas. Normally, the more the tea is brewed, the bitterer it becomes, but this one has a deep flavor, so I highly recommend it as a gift for a loved one! I would also like to thank the guide who introduced me to a good place♡

Name: 九份茶行 山海坊1983 Shanhai Tea House 1983
Author: Alex Jo
Review: Oolong tea specialty store. Visited at the recommendation of the guide during the Let Me Here Yespokji tour. I visited because I heard it was run by a native Taiwanese owner, and it was nice to be able to taste it and then buy it. I bought two for 2000NT. I also received a teacup as a free gift.

Name: 九份茶行 山海坊1983 Shanhai Tea House 1983
Author: YOUNGJAE LEE
Review: Not only did the tea taste great, but the atmosphere was really good, and the staff were friendly, so I was happy with my purchase^^ I gave it as a gift to people around me, and they loved the tea and said it was so delicious.

Name: 九份茶行 山海坊1983 Shanhai Tea House 1983
Author: Minhui Yu
Review:
I was passing by at noon and saw a young girl wearing a vest coming out of the store with a black trash can and neatly dumping it into the grass nearby!
No matter what kind of garbage is being dumped, this behavior is really bad!

Name: 巡茶大人 Mr. Teawanted
Author: 腦麻兒
Review: The aroma of the tea is very strong, and the flavor is also very sufficient. It is much better than some inferior teas. After brewing it several times, the taste is much better than the king of tea. If you walk two tea pounds, you can definitely tell which one is the tea patrolling master. of.

Name: 巡茶大人 Mr. Teawanted
Author: SD
Review: The first time I came here to drink it, I immediately bought a gift box for my family. The aroma is so unique, so sweet and fragrant.

Name: ASW TEA HOUSE
Author: Ms.G0772
Review:
July 31, 2024.  Arrived at 5:00pm and was told that they close at 6:00pm.  One of the servers wearing a mask told us that the open table in the middle and the bar stools were not available.  We stood by the door to wait for a table to open. Luckily, another server came and sat us down in the middle table. The other server was short tempered and you can tell she didn’t want to serve us. Thankfully, the short haired server was super nice and friendly.

Despite the initial disappointment, the place is still worth coming to. The tea’s were top notch and the scones were delicious. I’ll definitely return but hopefully the service will be improved.

Name: ASW TEA HOUSE
Author: Grace Chang
Review:
The hubby and I were wandering around and found this little gem! We came here on a whim on a Sunday evening around 9:30pm. This place is so cute and adorable! 😊.

I got the imperial palace cocktail and my husband got the GAO LIANG 4pc shot. I loved my drink, however, my husband didn’t- he thought it was too strong, though, he was able to taste the SLIGHT tea aroma :)

The service is also amazing, didn’t disappoint at all. Thank you!

Name: ASW TEA HOUSE
Author: Yohana Stephanie
Review:
A sudden visit to here. I was passing by this store & looked like this is a need to be visited place.

We need to go upstairs. The interior is elegant & classic. The lighting is not too bright and it’ comfortable for enjoying an afternoon tea.

There are not many seats. But, they provide seat for working/ studying (with the cable port).

I ordered a set menu & it was beautiful.

Enjoy the time here~

Name: ASW TEA HOUSE
Author: F H
Review: Overall atmosphere of the shop was great as well as the service. We decided to try the 4-piece shot set with tea-infused kaoliang, all of which left very unique aftertastes. Definitely worth to try if you drink kaoliang as some of it is quite strong. We also ordered some tea and scones which were pretty tasty as well. It was a weekday when we went so the environment was quieter for those looking to enjoy a peaceful afternoon.

Name: ASW TEA HOUSE
Author: Keng Fai
Review:
Wide selection of non-alcoholic drinks, tea, cocktails, local liquor and whiskey.

They serve small bites and cakes which were tasty and go well with the drinks.

Atmosphere was very good and relaxing. Suitable for long chats and catchups.

Service staff was knowledgeable, patient and extremely friendly.

Down side was that it's a little expensive.

Overall positive experience.

Name: 無事生活 活版印刷小茶館 Letterpress Tea House
Author: Mikk Tamme
Review:
Letterpress is an amazing small teahouse in Taipei. tucked away in a smaller street under a residential building.

It is ran by three sisters. Each of them has their speciality. Flower arrangements, tea, music, letterpress typing.

The tea is great and it is lovely and calm environment. Small place. At least one of the sisters speaks fair english also.

I come back here every time and bring friends. Totally love this place.

Name: 無事生活 活版印刷小茶館 Letterpress Tea House
Author: Eric
Review: Good traditional tea, quiet location, calm atmosphere, beautiful design and friendly owners. Started by three sisters who studied tea from an old master in hualien. Downstairs is a beautiful exhibition room with work from a local artist who lives in the mountains (only until end of June '16). If you're looking for the old feel of a traditional teahouse, this is a little treasure you'll want to check out, but be sure to reserve seats because the place is really small (only three tables)!

Name: 無事生活 活版印刷小茶館 Letterpress Tea House
Author: Fred Jin
Review: Wonderful place in an otherwise quiet area. They have interesting tea as well as a wide selection of pottery tea sets for sale. The printing press they exhibit tells an interesting story of life before computers. Well worth a visit!

Name: 無事生活 活版印刷小茶館 Letterpress Tea House
Author: Rosemary Bower
Review: This is a very special place run beautifully by a family. Combining tea ceremony, flower arranging, a beautiful art exhibition space and paper printing. Relax with a perfectly made cup of tea in an exquisite tea setting. Friendly and gentle owners.

Name: 無事生活 活版印刷小茶館 Letterpress Tea House
Author: Nashiki Toki
Review: Having good experience and good time in here.

Name: Chun Shui Tang Taipei Station Branch
Author: Porex Porex
Review:
Highly recommended for tourists to eat local Taiwanese food and drinks!
After deciding what to eat on the menu, you will need to pay for the food first on the front desk before eating just like fast food restaurants.
Handmade thin noodles with sesame oil are very good and cheap as well in my opinion. The pink guava lemon premium jasmine tea tastes more like a smoothie than a tea but it’s still amazing to try out.
In addition, spicy wontons with chilli oil are sweet rather than sour and spicy. The speed of preparation for dishes here was also efficient even though there were a large number of people eating.
To sum up, there are a lot more delicacies you can try to enjoy here and I would give this meal a 9.5 out of 10.

Name: Chun Shui Tang Taipei Station Branch
Author: Nicole Tan
Review:
Service is good.

There were 3 groups waiting when I arrived on a weekday around 8pm. There is a QR code to join the queue. Was seated in less than 15 mins.

Ordered the recommended siew mai. The taste is different from the Cantonese dim sum I'm used to. I thought it was rather average.

The braised beancurd had an interesting taste profile made up of five spices, mala, basil, coriander, spring onions, etc. I like the texture of the beancurd.

The bubble tea is their signature and a must-try. I noticed many locals having the drinks in the larger size. I like the taste - light, smooth, chewy, not too creamy, or too sweet.

Name: Chun Shui Tang Taipei Station Branch
Author: Tiffany Dy
Review: Our experience was so so... Quiet hassle if you are a foreign traveler who likes to try this restaurant and doesn't have a Line account. You need a Line account to be able to get a queue to dine in. The receptionist didn't even help us or accommodate for the booking of our dine in. I have high expectations for the beef noodles and it was just ok. Milk tea is good though, not too sweet and a good taste of the tea blend.

Name: Chun Shui Tang Taipei Station Branch
Author: k
Review:
This place is known for being the inventor of the beloved bubble tea. For takeout, you walk straight into the restaurant to the register. There was a 15% discount for ordering a medium. They take card/tap in addition to cash.

- Medium Bubble Tea (30% sugar, less ice): 161 NT after discount; 3/5 stars. I liked the smaller boba, but there wasn't much flavor to them, and the milk tea tasted average.
- Medium Puer Latte with Rose Salt: 187 NT after discount; 2/5 stars according to my friend.

Name: Chun Shui Tang Taipei Station Branch
Author: Kanto Toyoda
Review:
I had the beef noodles for the first time and had a great experience.
Not only the noodles but the pickles that came with the noodles tasted really nice when you put it into the soup.

Name: SENTO Kampo Tea
Author: WoodenRoller
Review:
This atmospheric teahouse is just off the main road and houses quite a theatrical tea experience. The interior combines zen garden in the dining space with a village hut made of rushes to cover the kitchen and serving space.

The lighting is soft with low humming background music, this is the perfect place to escape the city and sip tea quietly. The menu has a good range of tea, both hot and cold. The items have layers of flavour and texture, each of them unique and complex. They also serve tea-based desserts and a deliciously creamy pudding. Everything is presented in a beautiful Sino-Nippon style while incorporating some more colourful modern elements.

The staff are very friendly and so welcoming, please be sure to visit and try out a few items, you'll definitely want to come back for more.

Name: SENTO Kampo Tea
Author: Carie Lee
Review:
Was quite looking forward to coming here.. Unfortunately they had very average drinks and toast was just like a usual bread. Milk tea was too sweet and there is a minimum spend of one drink per person.. the food and drinks were not worth its price.

The seating area is just one huge table shared with others and the lighting casts a shadow that makes it hard to do anything like relaxing to read or write, so go there only if you want to catch up with friends

Name: SENTO Kampo Tea
Author: Renee Bates
Review: Lovely atmospheric contemporary tea house with innovative cocktail-like drinks

Name: SENTO Kampo Tea
Author: 浪人萊恩
Review:
Super nice staff and black tea.
Hyped.

Name: SENTO Kampo Tea
Author: Eddie Wong
Review: perfect to take a rest

Name: A-Mei Teahouse
Author: Jack Jaegar
Review:
We were lucky we didn’t have to wait at all and got a seat on the terrace! (Monday around lunch time)

Easily the best lotus tea I’ve ever had; the view probably made it taste even better. My friend got the tea ceremony which was really fun to experience too and the snacks were really great.

Name: A-Mei Teahouse
Author: Nattarika Srivisad (Filmsoloxseries)
Review:
Here you go for the most famous tea house, referencing in the Spirited Away movie. It's quite crowded for many people taking photos in front of the tea house, especially at night.
I went there alone for the Oolong tea set of 300NTD (a bit overpriced if you ask me, but its the landmark so it is what it is). They will teach you quick session on how to boil the tea correctly (which i just nod and say i understand but fail to follow the method). If you are lucky, you will have the bice table with view, but if you are already assigned to a table, then you see the better one, you can't move 😢 Overall, i love the tea ♡.

Name: A-Mei Teahouse
Author: Michael H
Review:
Amazing view from the balcony. This place is awesome, you have to go through the tunnel to get here then enter and voila, a gorgeous overlook of the bay and tea service.

The food is good too, pork rice, squids, shrimp, vegetables. They have board games and other items you can play while enjoying the gorgeous view, eating food, and drinking tea. What else is there to want?

Name: A-Mei Teahouse
Author: Jia Wei
Review: Very nice and chill place for some tea. The employee will briefly explain the proper way to enjoy the tea and they’re also very fluent in Japanese. Do support them even though is a bit on the pricey side but it is all for the experience

Name: A-Mei Teahouse
Author: Fadzlina ZS
Review:
We were welcomed by not-so-friendly staff, probably because of too much crowd.

The tea set which includes crackers/mochi - taste somehow average

But, the view from this teahouse especially during sunset is spectacular.

We got a free gift from the owner(a postcard) , which i find it very thoughtful and definetly something i think about when i came back from travelling. Might consider coming to this teahouse again for the view and the experience (experience where i felt like i travelled to an ancient times and immerse in the cultural heritage of Jiufen)

Name: Maokong Songyuan Teahouse
Address: No. 33-2 號, Lane 38, Section 3, Zhinan Rd
Author: Alexander Y. (Alex68)
Review:
Fresh vegetables straight from the garden. Great taste. Highest view in Maokong.
The place is not big, 5-6 tables which makes it more manageable. Space with big windows and natural views. Can be transformed into air-conditioning space when weather is reaaly hot.
Menu offered in Chinese and English. You can order full meal, or just a tea.
Signature dishes: 3-cups chicken and green tea oil chicken.

Name: Maokong Songyuan Teahouse
Address: No. 33-2 號, Lane 38, Section 3, Zhinan Rd
Author: Dominic Tam
Review: Great view but very expensive.  Have minimum cover charge per person.  Average service.

Name: Maokong Songyuan Teahouse
Address: No. 33-2 號, Lane 38, Section 3, Zhinan Rd
Author: KojiKojiKaren (こじこじかれん)
Review:
A tea shop located about 10 to 15 minutes from the Ropeway Nekoku Station.

It's run by a very nice couple.
This is a homely and reasonably priced shop.
This time I had planned to pay in cash from the beginning, so I haven't checked, but I think it's possible to pay by card or QR code.

The entrance is a little difficult to find.
The large ``Shoen Chabo'' sign is the landmark, but you have to walk up narrow stairs and a gravel driveway to get to the store.

Although the building is old, it is comfortable and has air conditioning inside.
In addition to tea, you can also enjoy meals such as vegetable soup, stir-fried dishes, fried rice made with oil extracted from tea, and fried rice made with roasted tea leaves.

This time, I ordered the local tea recommended by my husband, ``Masamune Tetsukannon Tea'', ``Tea Oil Fried Rice'', and ``Tea Leaf Fried Rice''. (There is a Japanese translation of the menu, but Japanese cannot be understood)
My wife made some tea for me, and I took the leftover tea leaves as a souvenir.

Although it was a very hot day, the tea was so delicious that I ended up drinking several cups, and the ``Tea Oil Fried Rice'' was so delicious that the aroma of the tea filled my mouth.

Smoking is generally prohibited inside the store, but there are ashtrays, so you can smoke outside.

Name: Maokong Songyuan Teahouse
Address: No. 33-2 號, Lane 38, Section 3, Zhinan Rd
Author: Dindin Huang
Review: This store is located in a higher position, with an unobvious signboard. The view is blocked by the roof of the store in front. The store is independently run by a couple. The taste is not bad, the attitude is warm and friendly, and the price is reasonable. There are about four tables in the store, with 20 seats. Seats, not many. Fried tofu and pineapple shrimp balls are both delicious!

Name: Maokong Songyuan Teahouse
Address: No. 33-2 號, Lane 38, Section 3, Zhinan Rd
Author: Faye Wang
Review: The boss is very nice, and the dishes are freshly picked organic vegetables. The dishes are delicious! The view is great! The menu is in Japanese and English, which is very convenient for foreigners.

Name: Yingyue Teahouse
Author: simone riedi
Review:
Good food, Great Service and Good Atmosphere!
The guest was particularly nice, offering lots of advice, even through the language barrier, and even free food items!

Name: Yingyue Teahouse
Author: franc ch
Review: Super expensive for such common dishes, and unimaginable tea & water charges? More ridiculous was the toilet that’s still old fashioned unfitting to folks who have hard time bending..

Name: Yingyue Teahouse
Author: Justin Davis
Review: I would give 10 stars if I could!  The gentlemen went out of the way to make us extra comfortable.  We were hot and sweaty after walking and he gave us extra drinks, walked us out with umbrellas when it was raining, and gave us drinks to go.  He wouldn’t accept a tip even! Fabulous tea, perfect for a rainy day and beautiful views!

Name: Yingyue Teahouse
Author: lee yong xian
Review:
The service man is very kind and takes an extra mile to recommend the best for us.
Even helped us get a driver down the mountain.

Name: Yingyue Teahouse
Author: Michael Zhu
Review: Unexpexted tasty and affordable!

Name: Boil Tea House
Address: No. 20之1號, Lane 40, Section 3, Zhinan Rd
Author: Richard Chen
Review: AMAZING atmosphere and the view is gorgeous. Most importantly, the service is top-notch and the quality of tea/food... it's as if God is pouring tea for you. 10/10 highly recommend!

Name: Boil Tea House
Address: No. 20之1號, Lane 40, Section 3, Zhinan Rd
Author: 愛可
Review: It's great. The space here is very nice, not noisy, and not cold. It's suitable for bringing girls. It's just that one side is a little steep, so girls should be careful when wearing high heels.

Name: Boil Tea House
Address: No. 20之1號, Lane 40, Section 3, Zhinan Rd
Author: 無極
Review:
The half-packed space provides a little bit of privacy.
It's a pity that you can't enjoy the mountain view of Maokong while making tea, but having a parking lot is a real plus.
You must use mineral water to boil hot water in a quick boiler to make tea.
The owner and his wife are very friendly.
Places that are easily accessible by public transportation are suitable for venturing.

Name: Boil Tea House
Address: No. 20之1號, Lane 40, Section 3, Zhinan Rd
Author: 蔡依婷
Review:
The whole environment is pretty good
Each box can seat many people
Although everyone is a little too happy
But it’s really nice to be able to chat and make tea freely
Although there are not many types of meals
But they are all delicious. Highly recommended.

Name: Boil Tea House
Address: No. 20之1號, Lane 40, Section 3, Zhinan Rd
Author: Hannah Lïn
Review:
Senchawon is so cool ꉂ ೭(˵¯̴͒ꇴ¯̴͒˵)౨”
✨The text contains a beautiful starry sky🌃 and also has a menu✨

🔸Jinxuan Tea $350
My favorite tea base when I was a student was Jinxuan tea
The fragrance of tea is so refreshing that you won’t be able to fall asleep after drinking it.
The tea is not too thick and is suitable for both adults and children.

🔸Dumplings (10 pieces) $110
🔸McChicken Nuggets (9 pieces) $90

Compared with Yaoyue Tea House
The items at Senchain are indeed much smaller and the prices are relatively uneconomical.
The single biggest advantage is
The parking lot is so vast that parking is not a problem
If there is really a problem
It may be challenging to go up the mountain😅

The dining environment is mosquito-free and very comfortable
There are chairs and cushions, so your butt won’t feel uncomfortable.
Each area is a private box, so you don’t have to worry about being disturbed.

🔹Tea fee $100/person

Senchawon

Sunday 13:00-22:00
Address👉 No. 20-1, Lane 40, Section 3, Zhanzhi Road, Wenshan District, Taipei City 116
Phone 👉(02)2939-2288

Name: Redwood Tea House
Author: J.David Hwang
Review: Came over here to enjoy the delicious meal with sunset & evening views of Taipei.  All the dishes were prepared with fresh ingredients & cooked perfectly.   There is no other better way to enjoy a delicious meal with fabulous sunset & evening views.  Strongly suggest anyone who would like to enjoy the magnificent sunset & evening Taipei views with great delicious meal to come here to try.

Name: Redwood Tea House
Author: Yusi
Review:
Amazing experience hanging out here with friends. Everting is good; remember to learn to how to make tea from the staff here.
View is nice during the day but gets more interesting during the evening.
Food here is also great. Everything is tasty.

Name: Redwood Tea House
Author: David Tuval
Review: Cafe at street level - excellent coffee from a proper espresso machine, and nice deserts like cheese cake with tea leaves and rice pudding. (Their restaurant is downstairs below street level).

Name: Redwood Tea House
Author: Andrew
Review: This place has an amazing view, make sure you go down the side of the building to the lower floor for open balcony view. The food were delicious overall, make sure to ask for 茶油雞.

Name: Redwood Tea House
Author: Julie Chen
Review: Amazing food! I loved the dishes we had, especially the noodles in tea oil and juicy bamboo shoots. One of the best meals I had in taipei.

Name: 九份好韻茶葉
Author: ATran
Review:
Very calm and away from the crowd.

Order from the bottom, there should be a menu in English. Comes with tea pastry and tea plums that are absolutely delicious.

Amazing view of the mountain in an AC Room. Literally cost 1/10th of the other tea houses.

Bonus point: clean private bathrooms.

Name: 九份好韻茶葉
Author: Mei Yi Yeap
Review: Very friendly staff who explains tea to the uninitiated. The tea they sell here are mostly grown themselves in Taiwan. They have the regular high elevation tea as well as those mixed with fruits and berries. Tried both and are very very enjoyable. They have an upstairs seating with a view of the town, with calming music that's perfect for enjoying your tea. We ordered a set that came with some snacks -- the pickled stuff may not be to everyone's taste.

Name: 九份好韻茶葉
Author: Eunbi Kim
Review:
This place was very comfortable with the beautiful view and many seats available at the time we visited(noon, sunday). The iced dongfangmeiren tea and snacks were good, and the price was resonable.
You can order your tea on the first floor and enjoy it on the upper floors. They will bring it for you.
2024/1/14

Name: 九份好韻茶葉
Author: Suny Kim
Review: Best place to enjoy an cozy and relaxing cup of tea. 100% recommend, please avoid the tourists traps like Amei tea house. Very reasonably priced, beautiful aesthetics. Harder to find as it’s on the 3rd floor of the tea shop. Order at the bottom and they will bring the tea sets up.

Name: 九份好韻茶葉
Author: Steph Couvrette
Review: Very nice teahouse and the tea service is at a good price. Order downstairs and walk up to the 2nd or 3rd floor to have your tea with a view.

Name: 九份暗街茶房
Author: Cassia Attard
Review:
Really amazing tea house experience!!! One really nice man told us about the tea he grows and then did the whole ritual. He’s very kind. The tea was delicious. We had a green oolong tea.
We sat there for over an hour and drank so much tea. It was $500 (Taiwan) for 2 people. Really worth it.

Name: 九份暗街茶房
Author: Praveen Kaur
Review:
This cute little tea house is run by an elderly couple. Master Yang is truly passionate about his tea. They were so hospitable. We sat in the little outdoor garden where Master Yang shared his life stories with us over tea that he harvested.
Nice little place for parents with kids.

Name: 九份暗街茶房
Author: Anchen Ko
Review:
老闆楊先生楊太太非常親切，非常照顧我們。真是一個很棒的地方。
We stayed at the bed and breakfast above the tea restaurant. The owners are kind and  treated us very nicely. Excellent place to spend a night! Really happy we came here :)

Name: 九份暗街茶房
Author: Philipp Wulff
Review: Very nice and chilled tea house. Friendly owners. Not as crowded as the Main Street at all :). The owner showed us how to properly prepare the tea, because we did not know.

Name: 九份暗街茶房
Author: Jiri Suchan
Review: Nice atmosphere with a few cats chilling around.

Name: Yu Zai Fan Shu Tea House
Author: Rosalind Chaw
Review: Came here for their local dishes cooked with fresh ingredients. This was recommended by the Owl Grandma for lunch and tea. We ordered pig intestines with duck blood stew, local bitter gourd omelette, and mapo tofu. All are prepared to perfection. Hospitable service staff. The dining environment is perfect with antiques and wooden furniture.

Name: Yu Zai Fan Shu Tea House
Author: brijesh patel
Review: Beautiful and Decorated Tea House. You can enjoy getting into Cave before reaching to the Tea house. It is has a beautiful views of Jufin. I enjoyed the traditional way of Tea serving. Actually its a Tea ceremony. They taught us well how to prepare and do it. We sit for 2 hours and it was awsome experience.

Name: Yu Zai Fan Shu Tea House
Author: Jeffrey C
Review:
Highly recommended. Such a special tea house. The walk in through the cave entrance was so intriguing. Then you enter a beautifully decorated tea house. As you proceed further in, you get tables with million dollar views.
And then there the food. So unique and so delicious. Order the tea and watch them show you how to brew a perfect pot of tea.

Name: Yu Zai Fan Shu Tea House
Author: Chris SG
Review: This tea house is absolutely breathtaking!! It shares the same beautiful view with ah mei tea house and offers homely food with excellent tea as well. I was so glad to find it. The shop owner (店长) is so kind and even showed me around the tea house, which is incredibly decked with thoughtful pieces of furniture that look so well put together. The entrance to the hidden tea house is also decorated like a secret cave along the stairs up and past the ah Mei tea house, and it was so fun 🤩 to walk through the “cave” and have the tea house and its gardens appear before you at the end of the cave . 20/10 must visit if you are in jiufen!

Name: Yu Zai Fan Shu Tea House
Author: Michelle Mitchell
Review:
My second visit to 九份, but the first time visit to Yu Zai Fan Shu. A hidden gem.  The view from the terrace was breathtaking, and the food was awesomely delicious. We ate so much at lunch yesterday... we skipped dinner... that was how delicious the dishes were.
I had to save its location in my favorite so I will be able to find it next time!!!

Name: Mountain Tea House
Author: Griffith Ho
Review: 6/22, first time here. If you want a amazing view with good foods, I think Mountain Tea House is the one for you. Honestly, all the food we have is good but I especially recommend you their Fried Pork Ribs in Typhoon Shelter Style which impressed me. It’s no exaggeration to say that it’s the best tea restaurant in Maokong.

Name: Mountain Tea House
Author: krō waque
Review:
Another gem of a restaurant in the Maokong area.

Chicken with tea oil stirfry is still a personal favorite. It amazes me how this dish is never prepared the same way, and how the taste varies from one restaurant to another, but still delivers that comforting familiar deliciousness I crave when in Taiwan.

Equally impressive are the two elderly couple manning the dining hall and the youthful ladies operating the kitchen.

The heavy downpour was a blessing during our visit as it made us appreciate more how comforting the dishes at this restaurant are. (At the same time, it was unfortunate to people who got stuck atop the Maokong gondolas.)

Name: Mountain Tea House
Author: F
Review: Delicious food. Friendly service. And best of all, really nice night view. Would come again!

Name: Mountain Tea House
Author: Diana Suhendra
Review: I loved the atmosphere here! The food looked so colorful and the food was very very tasty! The service was very nice too and they also gave our kids home made popcorn! We could enjoy the food while watching the view of Taipei city night lights and the atmosphere was nice too so you don't need the AC because the breeze was very nice too.

Name: Mountain Tea House
Author: Chanida R.
Review: I am very glad to select this restaurant when I visit Maokong Gondola. It’s really good taste, good service, nice atmosphere and very reasonable price. I ordered 4 dishes for 2 person only 800 TWD 😁😁😁

Name: Cheng's Tea House
Author: N/A
Review: N/A

Name: Tea House
Author: Matthew Layton
Review: This is a cute little little food hut on the grounds of the Chiang Kai-Shek memorial complex. It has both light bites as well as more substantial offerings. The proprietor is very friendly, the prices reasonable, and the air conditioning a welcome respite from the hot Taiwan weather!

Name: Tea House
Author: Clay Trippet
Review: Hot day.. Bought two vanilla soft-serve cones.  They were so bad, we had to throw them away next door.  Worst ice-cream on the planet.

Name: Tea House
Author: Rob Mcdowell (Rob)
Review: Fast and delicious snack while seeing the sites of the area!

Name: Tea House
Author: Evgeni Nonchev
Review: Good spare for a little chill out

Name: Tea House
Author: 王子康
Review: The house is clean, bright, cozy 👍👍👍👍👍, meals are yummy! Good service 🥳🥳

Name: Ten Ren's Tea Xinyi Branch
Author: Christian McKinley
Review: The taro green tea latte was one of my favourite milk teas I had whilst in Taiwan - recommend!

Name: Ten Ren's Tea Xinyi Branch
Author: Claudia P
Review: Ordered a King oolong tea with grass jelly. So refreshing and tasty. Highly recommend!

Name: Ten Ren's Tea Xinyi Branch
Author: Alan Chien
Review: This is one of the bigger branch that actually serve you tea as samples.  The quality of tea is great, with okay prices.  They also explain the quality of the tea you are buying, along with beautiful tea sets to go with your purchases.

Name: Ten Ren's Tea Xinyi Branch
Author: Joseph Lin
Review: This store sells some of my favourite Taiwanese oolong teas.  The top quality Taiwan Alishan Oolong tea is one of the best in the market.

Name: Ten Ren's Tea Xinyi Branch
Author: Jerald Dine
Review: My favorite tea shop/maker in Taipei 😍. I love the quality of the tea. And I think this is the best option for those health conscious people. I always buy their green tea. 👌 And They've got the softest tapioca balls too.

Name: 興華名茶 SHING HWA TEA SHOP
Author: Helen Hsu, Psy.D.
Review:
We’ve been following this family of tea specialists and roasters for 20 yrs. Every winter when we return to Taipei I re-stock our tea supply, but gifts & sit down for some lovely tea tasting. It’s clean & beautiful inside the shop.
Conveniently near MRT and lots of great places to eat.
We honestly enjoy all their teas, my favorites are high mountain oolong. Loose leaf is best of course but the tea bags are really convenient for the office or travel.

Name: 興華名茶 SHING HWA TEA SHOP
Author: Jonathan Friden
Review:
Taiwan is known as producing the best oolong tea in the world. Shing Hua produces the best oolong tea in Taiwan. The conclusion is simple. I have tried tea from the most famous shops in all of Taiwan. There is not even a close second place. Shing Hua’s tea is far superior to any tea you will find anywhere else. There is no reason to visit other tea shops unless it is to prove my point as you will easily notice the difference in quality.

The owners have their own tea farm on Li Shan. They use only organic fertilizer. They hand pick their tea. They dry, ferment, roast, and package their tea themselves. From start to finish, only Shing Hua’s hands are involved in their tea production. Their quality control is exceptional. They ensure each tea variety is produced to exact standards resulting in the best product possible. There are many years of technical experience involved in the production of their tea and it shows with each sip. Shing Hua’s tea is truly crafted by masters.

While you are here you can purchase some of the high quality teaware available at the shop that will bring out the best of your tea. You can also have a tea brewing lesson with the friendly tea master who works at the shop. He can teach you how to properly brew this superior tea. By purchasing tea here you will have the Rolls Royce of teas. You should do yourself a favor and learn how to get the most out of it.

Name: 興華名茶 SHING HWA TEA SHOP
Author: Danser
Review:
This is my first Five-Star Review ever.  (Oh, wow, second one.  I just reviewed my reviews. Sorry for the error.  7/2022)

Three years ago, a friend of mine, who knows that I am a devoted aficionado of Chinese Tea, had an opportunity to visit Taiwan.  While there, she was introduced to the Shing Hwa Tea shop and bought some tea leaves to bring back to Maryland and give me.  I was impressed.

But I have been buying Chinese tea leaves for over 30 years now, in Taiwan, in Hong Kong and online.  I know how easily the quality of the tea can vary from season to season and supplier to supplier.  Here, at Shing Hwa Tea, I find myself a satisfied return customer after these three years and expect to continue to be so.

The leaves have been of consistently high quality.  They are whole, tightly rolled, clean and very low in stick quantity (see the photo, "Tea Leaves Dry.")  Upon steeping, they unfurl to reveal mostly complete leaves, some tops and good color.  The texture of the used leaves is slightly elastic; i.e., they have not been over-roasted (see "Used Leaves."  This by the way, is another of my all-time favorite Oolong teas.)

Shipping charges might be a little high, but my orders consistently arrive within a week, far faster than orders I have placed from importers located in Canada, on the West Coast of the U.S., and even here in Maryland.  I also did some comparison shopping and concluded that, gram per gram the price for, let's say Da Yu Lin Tea (another outstanding leaf), was approximately HALF of what I had been paying elsewhere (don't tell them that...um).  The overall expense is about level with purchasing teas of lesser quality elsewhere on-line.

I hesitate to post this review, since It may stimulate more purchasing from Shing Hwa which, in turn, may result in a run on the stock and cause them to be sold out when I next need tea.  But responsiveness, friendliness and excellence ought, I suppose, be acknowledged.  Besides, I just made a large purchase that arrived in 5 days and some of which I am sipping now  ;-)

Name: 興華名茶 SHING HWA TEA SHOP
Author: Brian Lee
Review: Very cool experience! The tea master was very kind in explaining each tea type and allowed us to try them before purchase! Definitely recommend a visit!

Name: 興華名茶 SHING HWA TEA SHOP
Author: Johnny Wang
Review:
Fantastic tea shop focusing on organic, natural (no additives) Taiwanese high mountain teas! They offer free tastings, a wealth of knowledge, and more importantly, very, very enjoyable teas, at a variety of price points.

Don't come in looking for weird fake stuff like strawberry black teas, but do come here if you're looking for high quality local teas for yourself or as gifts. Teas come in both cheaper bulk packaging, as well as more expensive gift packaging, and the store also has an online shop offering international shipping!

Name: Coffee and Tea 天母精品咖啡
Address: 號 48 攤位, No. 100, Shidong Rd
Author: Julia Chen
Review:
You can either have your coffee to-go or there's a few stools for sit-ins. Had a great Yirgacheffe coffee, will defs visit again :)

#士東市場 #單品咖啡 #日曬耶加雪菲

Name: Coffee and Tea 天母精品咖啡
Address: 號 48 攤位, No. 100, Shidong Rd
Author: Per van der Horst
Review: Definitely the best single origin espresso, macchiato and cappuccino in Taipei. My favorites are Sidamo and Yirgacheffe unwashed.

Name: Coffee and Tea 天母精品咖啡
Address: 號 48 攤位, No. 100, Shidong Rd
Author: Syrena C.
Review: good good

Name: Coffee and Tea 天母精品咖啡
Address: 號 48 攤位, No. 100, Shidong Rd
Author: Yao
Review:
I ordered a single-origin latte at your store at 9 o'clock this morning. The barista who made the Italian style didn't seem to have enough experience; the light roasted beans, the milk was over 90 degrees, and the milk foam was too thick and not dense. I hope that the store can pay more attention to the workplace education part, rather than letting novices directly serve cups, which affects the customer's perception.
I'm also a barista and I hope you guys can keep up the good work.

Name: Coffee and Tea 天母精品咖啡
Address: 號 48 攤位, No. 100, Shidong Rd
Author: ching2 ching2
Review:
Specialty 70

Black, too sour.

Tasty ☀️☀️☀️
Visit again☀️

Name: IN （飲） CAFE （Coffee / Tea / Light Food )
Address: Lane 77, Section 2, Zhongshan N Rd, 11號一樓
Author: Amy Nguyen
Review: A bit too much milk in the latte, you may need to ask the boss for adjusting. It tasted good overall 😃

Name: IN （飲） CAFE （Coffee / Tea / Light Food )
Address: Lane 77, Section 2, Zhongshan N Rd, 11號一樓
Author: Bill Po-Kai TUNG
Review: Both the location and coffee were great, but when you're enjoying the coffee, the place might be filled with the smell of cigarettes because there are no separations between indoor seats and outdoor ones.

Name: IN （飲） CAFE （Coffee / Tea / Light Food )
Address: Lane 77, Section 2, Zhongshan N Rd, 11號一樓
Author: Rebecca Yeo
Review: Simple freshly made delicious thick toast. Speedy good service as well.

Name: IN （飲） CAFE （Coffee / Tea / Light Food )
Address: Lane 77, Section 2, Zhongshan N Rd, 11號一樓
Author: Derek Hayter
Review: Excellent location. The boss is extremely helpful, kind, attentive and nice. Although the food is alright, the drinks are great.

Name: IN （飲） CAFE （Coffee / Tea / Light Food )
Address: Lane 77, Section 2, Zhongshan N Rd, 11號一樓
Author: Marvin Ruano
Review: Great food! Nice atmosphere! Cool place!

Name: Mountainside Taiwan Tea-gifts
Author: Hazel Christie Valle
Review: Had such an amazing experience at Mountainside. A friend recommended the place and we did the tea tasting experience. The owner was very knowledgeable about everything. He explained the brewing process, cultural ties, the types of tea, etc. These are legit high quality tea!

Name: Mountainside Taiwan Tea-gifts
Author: Reginald James Lorico
Review:
I'm dividing my review into two sections:

SHOP VISIT

I visited Mountainside on the last day of 2023. A good friend recommended it since I mentioned that I was planning to restock on tea supplies while on a quick visit to Taiwan.

Right off the bat, I felt warmly welcomed by the owner. He led me through an immersive experience of Taiwanese tea, and tried out Milky Oolong (Jin Xuan), Li Shan, Gaba, and an award-winning batch of Oriental Beauty. I absolutely loved the look, aroma, and taste of every cup. I learned a lot throughout the session and could say that it got me hooked. I would even love to visit some of the reputable estates where the tea originates in the near future.

Taiwanese tea is among the best in the world so I highly recommend anyone who is curious about it or seeks to learn more to come visit and bring home different varieties. As mentioned by others, I also think that it’s a plus that the owner speaks multiple languages really well (English, Chinese, Russian). Any visitor can expect a proper and detailed explanation about the tea they are considering which I think is very helpful for locals, expats, and tourists alike.

With the dedicated attention of owner, I did not even notice the time I spent at the shop. Aside from tea, we got to discuss many things such as life in Taiwan, surfing, my hometown, among others. I’m sure that any visitor can expect the same level of attention and dedication when they pay a visit.

POST-SHOPPING EXPERIENCE

It's a little over a month since I bought different kinds of tea from the shop. I’m of the opinion that tea is even better when shared and I’m pleased to report that after several servings at home and sharing with our local barista and my boss, I can attest that the quality of all the tea that I bought is superb. Since tea is not commonly consumed like coffee in my hometown, as with most localities in the Philippines for that matter, I am glad to have been able to share Taiwanese tea of such quality as it significantly helps in influencing more people to embrace drinking tea more regularly.

Looking back, I wish I got to buy more of Pouchong (Baozhong), Milky Oolong, Gaba, and other high-mountain tea options. I am also considering to choose among the beautiful gift box options to give to my friends that I recently introduced Taiwanese tea to. I’m already looking forward to my next visit and purchase.

Name: Mountainside Taiwan Tea-gifts
Author: Raymond Dong
Review: Very unique & unsurpassed collections of Taiwanese tea. Welcoming vibe for chilling in a rainy day. Beautiful design and packaging for tea gift boxes. Definitely a place to visit if you love high quality Taiwanese tea.

Name: Mountainside Taiwan Tea-gifts
Author: 楊書瑋
Review:
Very cool place near Dongmen MRT.

The owner treated us some nice tea.
We bought Honey Scented Black Tea and GABA Tea. GABA tea I really liked.

We also bought Taiwanese bamboo gift box.

Name: Mountainside Taiwan Tea-gifts
Author: suetwan lo
Review: The most unique tea shop I have ever tried, The best choice for Taiwanese souvenirs. It’s not only the tea taste particularly refreshing, but also the staff are also very kind and friendly.

Name: Three Leafs 三葉茶食
Author: Adi Rainbow
Review:
The place has a relaxing atmosphere where you can be undisturbed with your tea, food, and friends. We felt the staff were incredibly friendly and caring which made us very feel comfortable.

The food was very delicious which we felt was worth the price for what you get. If you’re looking for something inexpensive to eat, then it’s worth checking out the snacks and desserts.

We’ll go back for sure to try more of their cold brew teas since we really enjoyed its distinctive taste - so we’re keen to try the others.

Name: Three Leafs 三葉茶食
Author: Morgan Sheeran
Review:
Awesome tea shop in Taipei. There is a pretty wide variety of teas to choose from and the space itself is very relaxing and open. Tea quality is superb and the owner is very passionate about highlighting Taiwanese teas and local farms.

Definitely recommend checking it out!

Name: Three Leafs 三葉茶食
Author: mia tseng
Review:
I had a great time here. Enjoyed the different flavors of all the different teas, it’s really interesting and let us know more about Taiwan’s awesome tea- oolong tea!!!!!

Great space, great owner, great tea! I look forward to visiting you again. ☺️☺️☺️

Name: Three Leafs 三葉茶食
Author: 陳老师Anisah
Review:
We're looking for a nice café for a quick meal and to stretch our tired legs after a long day of walking at the Daan Flower Market.

We ordered pasta - I had  truffle and wild mushroom - it's really good.  Pasta was al-dente, the dish smells scrumptious, I wasn't really hungry before but I ended up finishing the generous serving.  Could do with a wee bit more actual wild mushrooms.

Overall I've enjoyed my meal.
The lady chef was there to take our orders and made sure we're ok.

Enjoyed the tea too.
Will come back for their desserts .

Name: Three Leafs 三葉茶食
Author: Jenny Cheung
Review: artisanal modern tea shop! it’s a social enterprise that contributes to community and improve life of farmer. Very meaningful story.

Name: TWG Tea at SOGO Zhongxiao Taipei
Address: Section 4, Zhongxiao E Rd, 45號b2 樓
Author: 李珮琪
Review: Nice service, I love their macarons. It can be the best gift for your friends too. Only 60 ntd per piece. The location is in B2 at SOGO

Name: TWG Tea at SOGO Zhongxiao Taipei
Address: Section 4, Zhongxiao E Rd, 45號b2 樓
Author: poyi liu
Review:
I like their assorted macarons, they weren’t too sweet and paired really well with tea.
The service wasn’t very good though.

Service: 2/5
Food: 4/5

Name: TWG Tea at SOGO Zhongxiao Taipei
Address: Section 4, Zhongxiao E Rd, 45號b2 樓
Author: Min ling Lin
Review: The packaging is exquisite and high-quality for gift giving. The macarons are delicious, sweet and delicious. They go best with tea and coffee! The service is good and the counter is secret!

Name: TWG Tea at SOGO Zhongxiao Taipei
Address: Section 4, Zhongxiao E Rd, 45號b2 樓
Author: 徐銀秀
Review: The waiter has a great attitude and introduces the products carefully. There are many choices of macaron flavors. It is suitable for giving as a gift and for personal use. This store is worth recommending. The tea moon cake gift box is so exquisite.

Name: TWG Tea at SOGO Zhongxiao Taipei
Address: Section 4, Zhongxiao E Rd, 45號b2 樓
Author: 閻月燁
Review: I asked for three of each flavor other than chocolate and mint. I checked when I got home and found that the two flavors were missing 1. Tiramisu gave me 5 🥲

Name: Lin Mao Sen Tea Co.
Author: F
Review: I came here after reading all the good reviews and that the staff are good at telling you about the teas and that you can try some teas before purchasing. The man that helped me didn’t allow me to try anything when I asked and pointed to the other customers seated at the big table doing tastings. He proceeded to rush me and made me feel like I was in his way even though I was buying things. It looked like the whole store was focused on the large group. From the conversations I heard, it sounded like they were going to be making a big purchase to bring to China. I still ended buying a few bags of tea but I regret it because after that I went to the tea shop just a few steps away and the service was phenomenal.

Name: Lin Mao Sen Tea Co.
Author: Nancy
Review: This is the place to go to get Gaoshan Oolong (High Mountain Oolong) and tea sets. There is one employee who speaks decent English and very good Japanese. You can ask to try one flavor of tea. The smallest increment of purchase is 150g. Then it goes up by 150g up to 600g. The prices listed on the barrels are for 600g. They can vacuum seal the tea for you in house and package them with bubble wrap for easy packing into luggage without worry of damage.

Name: Lin Mao Sen Tea Co.
Author: Charlie P.
Review: Amazing selection. Great service. Cool store in general. Might be on the pricey side but worth it!

Name: Lin Mao Sen Tea Co.
Author: Matt L
Review:
Tea: ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Ambience: ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Service: ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Value: ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

Actually a very nice teashop, with all the tea stored in metal barrels with the prices on top. It might look expensive but each price is per 600g, with the minimum purchase of 150g of any tea.

They allow you to only try (a small amount) of one tea. The staff seem to speak a variety of languages. They have some teas they claim that other places don't have, like real Tieguanyin. I'm not tea expert so can't vouch for the validity of these statements!

Tea comes in plastic bags, so if you're looking for tea as a gift, you might want to purchase from elsewhere (although they do sell presentation tins here).

Name: Lin Mao Sen Tea Co.
Author: M Tran
Review: Great tea tasting experience! The owner speaks English and has a very knowledgeable on teas. He even provided us with instructions on how to properly brew the teas. We bought our favorite teas.

Name: TP TEA
Author: Mu Keng Kao
Review: Nice service and will visit again

Name: TP TEA
Author: johnnycool
Review: Fantastic as always! Love it!

Name: TP TEA
Author: 梁海豚
Review: Good service is indeed worth visiting again, even if there are small imperfections, the store manager always does his best to provide the best service💪👍 The strong male store clerk received him kindly and politely, and the drinks are delicious. I like the new taste of chicken cakes😋

Name: TP TEA
Author: Anna Chien
Review:
▲Emerald Pearl Latte: At least the sugar tastes good.
▲Ceylon roof lemon black tea: It tastes good, but the slight sugar is too sweet.

Name: TP TEA
Author: Nancy Chao (北投人南西)
Review:
Today I would like to recommend their chicken cakes
The tea tastes very fragrant🍵The custard is not sweet and it will burn your mouth when eaten hot, but it is very happy🤩
Last time I had a lot of pearls, this time the portion was smaller but still not bad🙌🏻
The shape of the lid and cup is very cute~~~~

Name: Oolong Tea Project - Da'an
Address: No. 39, Lane 107, Section 1, Fuxing S Rd
Author: warat niamsa-ing
Review: Delicious oolong tea

Name: 有記名茶 WANGTEA ｜ 茶廠 Refinery Tea Factory
Address: No. 26號, Lane 64, Section 2, Chongqing N Rd
Author: Kiat Yingda
Review: Bought some tea leaves here, my first time at a tea house. Lady staff was very knowledgeable and keen in explaining. When I said I wanted something to drink daily, she recommended something more budget friendly and did not hard sell the more expensive leaves. Also went next door for a tea-beer after that. Very relaxing environment on a hot summer day!

Name: 有記名茶 WANGTEA ｜ 茶廠 Refinery Tea Factory
Address: No. 26號, Lane 64, Section 2, Chongqing N Rd
Author: Troels Steenholdt Heiredal
Review: What a beautiful and insightful experience. Jason was a calm and incredible guide, blowing our minds as he educated us on the tea production processes. Unfolding the history of tea culture, while also elaborating on distinct brewing techniques and the newest trends and experimentations.

Name: 有記名茶 WANGTEA ｜ 茶廠 Refinery Tea Factory
Address: No. 26號, Lane 64, Section 2, Chongqing N Rd
Author: Wayne Mowery
Review:
Wang Tea is doing things with tea that were previously unimaginable. Spectacularly delicious.

I recommend the Nitro draft tea.

Name: 有記名茶 WANGTEA ｜ 茶廠 Refinery Tea Factory
Address: No. 26號, Lane 64, Section 2, Chongqing N Rd
Author: JC L
Review:
Very friendly staff who explained to me the history of the place and the store which brings the whole tea shopping experience to the next level! They're the only one out of the 200 tea shops around this area that survived from long ago. The tea tastes good as well. They use the same type of oolong tea but fermented and baked them in different ways, which makes for the different variant of teas. I like the 'wen Shan bao Zhong' tea the most, as it gives a light and refreshing spring like taste.
Also got to tour the factory which is just behind the shop but too bad no production was running.

Name: 有記名茶 WANGTEA ｜ 茶廠 Refinery Tea Factory
Address: No. 26號, Lane 64, Section 2, Chongqing N Rd
Author: Panthea Lee
Review: We loved our visit here, learning about the process of selecting, picking, fermenting, roasting, and blending all sorts of tea. We were lucky enough time spend time with Jason, who was incredibly kind and patient with all our many questions, and we're loving the high quality tea that we bought from them. Such a treat to learn about the history of this long-running family business, and also to visit Wangtea Lab next door and seeing how they're refreshing tea culture for modern times. So appreciate the passion and vision of this multigenerational crew!

Name: Ming San Tea /明山茶集MSTC
Address: No. 3號, Lane 70, Section 2, Chongqing N Rd
Author: Simon K.
Review: Nice family-owned tea shop with very tasty, high-quality tea. The tea tasting we had, was absolutely perfect!! We learned so much about the taiwanese tea history and business. They also speak perfect english. Thank you, Ming San Tea, for that nice experience!

Name: Ming San Tea /明山茶集MSTC
Address: No. 3號, Lane 70, Section 2, Chongqing N Rd
Author: Chua Chua
Review: Great place to learn how to taste and blend your own tea. Also found out about how tea competitions work. Brought home my own pack of mixed tea.

Name: Ming San Tea /明山茶集MSTC
Address: No. 3號, Lane 70, Section 2, Chongqing N Rd
Author: Michael Crocker
Review: Great experience learning about Taiwan teas! Our host for the tea class was nice and knowledgeable, and the teas we tasted were all very good.

Name: Ming San Tea /明山茶集MSTC
Address: No. 3號, Lane 70, Section 2, Chongqing N Rd
Author: Kevin Chang
Review: The tea tasting lesson was great and Yvonne, our host, was both informative and kind. Highly recommend for anyone who loves tea or is curious about it!

Name: Ming San Tea /明山茶集MSTC
Address: No. 3號, Lane 70, Section 2, Chongqing N Rd
Author: Prasan Shirantha
Review: Good and very competitive Tea Suppliers

Name: 三口半茶館 Saguhan Tea House
Author: Lye
Review:
Great place to try out tea in a quiet and zen environment. Opt for the bar seats to interact and watch how they prepare tea. The staff’s passion for tea is evident and prices are reasonable - you pay a base and add on for additional tea flavors.

Would recommend.

Name: 三口半茶館 Saguhan Tea House
Author: Appuru -
Review:
Spotted this gem while finishing up our day travel.
Started off with just selected tea on our side, chatted with the people there and I learned a lot from them on tea brewing with different pot and on the different kind of tea mixture can be done with tea leaves

The owner also shared some cold brew tea (which is not on the menu) with us.

Good place for people who loves tea and enjoy the quietness

Name: 三口半茶館 Saguhan Tea House
Author: Victor
Review: Took a break in this tea house and it was very relaxing ! The staff was nice and caring, the tea was great and it was really quiet, we enjoyed it a lot.

Name: 三口半茶館 Saguhan Tea House
Author: Vincent Lim
Review: A very good experience that takes you through the journey of tea, complemented by amazing and friendly service! Definitely check it out if you appreciate or want to develop an appreciation for tea.

Name: 三口半茶館 Saguhan Tea House
Author: Justin Wei Nan
Review: I had a wonderful experience at this cosy tea spot! It's the perfect place to unwind with a cup of tea. They offer a great selection, and the ambiance is just so relaxing. If you're in the area, definitely stop by for a tea break!

Name: Ten Ren's Tea
Author: Daniel Skye Pollard
Review: Friendly staff, with delicious tea. Specialties often change to match the season.

Name: Ten Ren's Tea
Author: Genie T
Review: Good tea selection and prices. Delicious tea latte :)

Name: Ten Ren's Tea
Author: Au Pi Hai澳屁孩
Review: Good quality of tea and pearls

Name: Ten Ren's Tea
Author: 蘇玲玲
Review: I bought two cups of matcha ice cream smoothie today. The lid was missing and I pushed the ice cream down, so the original amount of smoothie became less. The cup was only a medium cup, and the portion was smaller than before. One cup still costs 90 yuan. , changing the price and increasing the price. This is very dishonest. I asked the clerk what was going on, but the clerk insisted that the previous portion was extra for you, so do we want to thank you? ! Don’t treat consumers like fools. I will never shop at such a dishonest store again! !

Name: Ten Ren's Tea
Author: 吳怡蕙
Review:
So delicious😍😍😍
Must order 913 milk tea + pearls every time~
Although it is a chain brand, this one is the best🥹

Name: Ten Ren's Tea
Author: Frans Oilinki
Review: Better than average takeaway teas (a bit pricey, though, compared to if you brew your tea by yourself, 哈哈). Also good place to eat (price quality ratio good). In this shop you can also buy tea leaves, and sometimes they have nice discounts. If you have not found your favorite tea shop, at least you know what you get if you buy your tea leaves from here.

Name: Ten Ren's Tea
Author: Li Fang Wang
Review: The server maintains good service explaining their products to me even at busy hours when there're many customers  in the shop

Name: Ten Ren's Tea
Author: audrey l
Review: They always get the amount right on my half honey green tea. Good staff. I also like their promotional coupon events.

Name: Ten Ren's Tea
Author: Huyen Bui
Review: good taste.

Name: Ten Ren's Tea
Author: rai rainn
Review: Oh my god, the boss and his wife are so nice. I closed the store during the typhoon and found that I had forgotten my mobile phone. The remote control was out of power and could not be turned on. I went to the store along the way and saw Daiso with the light on. I approached and closed it. I asked before. The owner of Tenren Tea who bought it wholesale didn’t say anything. After removing the screws, there happened to be a battery. I wanted to give them more but they didn’t charge me anything. I was so happy. I had to buy some tea. It’s great to have you. Bless you all. Peace and joy💖💖

Name: Oolong Tea Project
Author: Chomticha S.
Review: The best Milk tea ever! It is at Ximending night market. picked up only, no seats. "Cheese milk foam spring oolong tea" is one of the best ones! That's a good price, just 50 TWD / or around 55 Baht. (this quality and tasty in Thailand you will pay more than 150 baht) very impressive.

Name: Oolong Tea Project
Author: Flo G
Review:
This bustling bubble tea chain is in Wanhua close by Long Shan Temple vicinity.

Saw the good reviews and streaming customers, tried a cream cheese foam oolong roasted tea with bubble.

Overall an above average drink although we would prefer a stronger oolong tea flavour.

Prices are very reasonable.

Staff are courteous and helpful & advised that those with # indicated are signatures.

Recommend to try!

Name: Oolong Tea Project
Author: Max T Tran
Review: So far this is the best tea shop here in Taipei that we have tried. I ordered a Lemon Oolong tea. I recommend to do half sugar and less ice.

Name: Oolong Tea Project
Author: Esmonde Tan
Review: Randomly chanced upon this goated bubble tea store and was pleasantly blown away. It turned out to be the best oolong tea I had in Taipei. The tea was roasted well and the coffee jelly complemented the drink well too. Was pleasantly surprised that they have many other stores too.

Name: Oolong Tea Project
Author: Raisha W
Review:
One of the best Milk Tea in town!
Too sweet, so, order the 30% or 50%.
Ordered the dark oolong milk tea.
I personally love oolong tea, we can taste the tea!

Name: Tenren Tea Zhongxiao Second Store
Author: Fabio K
Review:
We went there at 9m and didn’t know they were closing already.
But the lady was really sweet and friendly brewing tea for us to taste

Name: Tenren Tea Zhongxiao Second Store
Author: Gareth Moose
Review: Highly recommend the Dark Oolong Milk Tea!

Name: Tenren Tea Zhongxiao Second Store
Author: Gemma Kim
Review: We tasted a variety of oolong teas in the back of the shop. the staff (we call him "the elderly tea master") was very helpful and informative. he introduced us to their whole collection. we bought a tea set, some tea from their classic collection and few from their new limited arrivals.

Name: Tenren Tea Zhongxiao Second Store
Author: Ivy Ngu
Review: Service was great, staff was helpful

Name: Tenren Tea Zhongxiao Second Store
Author: Marc Pilmann
Review: Tasted tea for over an hour and they really took time to help us choose from their great variety of oolong teas. Definitely recommended.

Name: Morning Tea House
Author: James Sheu
Review: Lady boss is super friendly, taught the family how to make tea and appreciate them… the scenery is awesome bundled with the fresh air, we could stay here the whole day!!!

Name: Morning Tea House
Author: Kailey Watson
Review: A wonderful place to relax and enjoy the view while drinking tea. I made my own tea, and the staff was very friendly and helpful and communicated very well in English.  For the price, I would probably have stayed longer and had another tea if my schedule had allowed it. I plan on returning next time I’m in Taipei!

Name: Morning Tea House
Author: Hao En
Review:
Nice place to have tea. The tea house sells tea and allow you to enjoy tea by the mountain.

What to expect:
1. The shop sells boxes of tea leaves (you can bring your own)
2. The shop charges by number of people having tea there (fees are for the tea sets)
3. They sell snacks to company the tea, you can choose to bring your own too

Name: Morning Tea House
Author: Wouter Vlas
Review: Really wonderful authentic tea house. The owners are really nice and take the time to show you how to brew your tea. Prices are also really good. Best is when you can sit downstairs among their tea plantation overlooking Taipei.

Name: Morning Tea House
Author: F Paul
Review:
Nice tea place with tea drinking place next to a tea farm. The lady here can speak some English.

You need to buy the tea from the shop first. They'll ask if you want to drink it there. Dont worry, they don't mean you need to finiah the whole big box of tea if you bought a big box. Then go and find a seat either inside the shop area or the outdoor seatings across the street. The staff will fins you and show you how to brew the tea that you bought.

Name: 茶敬茶 Tea to Tea
Author: Alicia Wong
Review: Very quiet and quaint tea spot in Yong Kang Street. They have different milk teas but I was tempted to taste their freshly brewed pure teas. I ordered Lishan Pure tea and it was very good. The tea is extremely smooth and easy to drink with light floral notes. It takes about 7-8min for them to brew the tea and they will also give you the leaves used to brew your tea in a to-go container for a second brew at home. If you are a big fan of tea and is looking for a non-sugary option to clean your palette I highly recommend you try this place out.

Name: 茶敬茶 Tea to Tea
Author: Pham Xuan Phu
Review: Had my very first Taiwanese milk tea experience here and it was totally worth it. I could easily tell they used high quality dong ding oolong tea from its fresh taste, light roasted flavour and calming aroma. Even though I opted for regular sugar level, the sweetness was moderate and blended well with the richness of fresh milk. The design of the shop was simple yet elegant, making the overall visit a relaxing and enjoyable one.

Name: 茶敬茶 Tea to Tea
Author: Sophia Shin
Review:
Great milk teas! We realized we hadn't had much yet, and stopped by to get some milk tea - so glad we did!

My partner got the milk tea (made with milk, not creamer like the classic milk tea here) with Dong Ding Oolong at regular sugar and ice with peals. He said it was great, especially after a long walk. Raving review if I've ever heard any from him. :) The tea has a distinctive oolong flavor - I wouldn't go in thinking that the pearls are going to be life-changing since the tea is really the star of the show. Makes sense since they're a tea shop. :)

I got the Milk meets cream with black tea, 70% sugar, and 30% ice. The creaminess and level of sweetness was a perfect complement to the black tea. I loved it and would definitely get it again - my partner said the same, too! (Although he did initially say mine was even better than his lol)

I'd 100% recommend visiting if you're in the area. I would probably also want to check out their other tea options. They didn't have the alcoholic teas available when we visited, but maybe someone else can try it when they are available. :)

Note: There are 3 tables with 2-3 chairs each outside, so just be aware of the limited seating.

Name: 茶敬茶 Tea to Tea
Author: Jeroemy
Review: A quiet & quaint store, enjoyed their milk tea and matcha tea with pearl

Name: 茶敬茶 Tea to Tea
Author: Mings
Review:
😀/🥹

The oriental beauty tea was very pretty and lightly floral. Brewed well, and they even give you a container for a second brew at home. Very very thoughtful

Name: Gaoli Zhao Ming Tea
Author: Baby Lion
Review:
Very good service and good tea.
I love it !!!!!

Name: Gaoli Zhao Ming Tea
Author: Qiaoru Han
Review: Good service and explanation of the staff. The quality of tea leaves is great too!

Name: Gaoli Zhao Ming Tea
Author: kelvin 4806
Review: Nice service and really welcomed,friendly.

Name: Gaoli Zhao Ming Tea
Author: 배유진 YUJIN B
Review: Very kind and the tea is very delicious ;D

Name: Gaoli Zhao Ming Tea
Author: ES KIM
Review: So kind and nice tea.

Name: 玉琪咖啡茶飲Coffee & Bubble Tea-北投市場.樂活名攤
Address: Huanggang Rd, 33號428攤（網球場旁D棟中繼市場
Author: Molly
Review: Good quality and fair price drinks.

Name: 玉琪咖啡茶飲Coffee & Bubble Tea-北投市場.樂活名攤
Address: Huanggang Rd, 33號428攤（網球場旁D棟中繼市場
Author: 劉懶蟲
