Review: Cute

Name: L'Adresse Guéliz, Café & Restaurant
Address: Atar، 1 Villa, Av. Mohammed V, Marrakesh
Author: Mohamed Karim
Review:
The menu offered a wide variety of delicious coffee and tea options, as well as an array of pastries and light bites.

I tried their specialty cappuccino and red velvet cake it was hands down one of the best I have ever had.

The staff was friendly and attentive, always making sure I was comfortable and had everything I needed.

And I have asked for cheesecake and most of the cakes on the menu was not available, they sent one of their staff with all other type of cakes they have and you could choose from her selection.

Name: L'Adresse Guéliz, Café & Restaurant
Address: Atar، 1 Villa, Av. Mohammed V, Marrakesh
Author: Denisa Staffenova
Review: The atmosphere was great, and food was amazing!! Our waiter was Yasin and he looked after us very well. The food arrived quick and was really tasty! The bread in the basket was still hot when it arrived to the table which is great! The prices were reasonable, I would even say that it’s cheap for the quality. Thank you for the experience and we will be returning

Name: L'Adresse Guéliz, Café & Restaurant
Address: Atar، 1 Villa, Av. Mohammed V, Marrakesh
Author: FAtim zehra Barmaki
Review:
A beautiful place with a calm breeze , a wonderful kind staff good food the perfect place to work in or have dinner with family in the best erea in Marrakesh thanks to l’adresse for a lovely evening experience thank you to the waiter that served us MOURAD he was lovely kind made our experience even better with his good energy .

I would definitely recommend guys to come visit u will love it 👌🏽❤️❤️❤️

Name: L'Adresse Guéliz, Café & Restaurant
Address: Atar، 1 Villa, Av. Mohammed V, Marrakesh
Author: Custódio Nunes
Review:
What a fantastic dinner!
The food and the dessert were absolutely delicious! All the staff and especially
Mohammed were very kind and professional.

Name: L'Adresse Guéliz, Café & Restaurant
Address: Atar، 1 Villa, Av. Mohammed V, Marrakesh
Author: Ben Aungkon
Review: Very good food with outstanding service. I really enjoyed my time here, enjoying the sun and some good food. I would definitely come back! The quality and the quantity of the food is a lot!

Name: أتاي كافي
Address: 62 rue amsafah, Rte Sidi Abdelaziz, Marrakech
Author: Justice Rosegreen
Review:
Good location, beautiful views from the rooftop. Staff are attentive and friendly from start to end.
The food was nice and I enjoyed the assortment of Moroccan bruschetta.
It is expensive as it’s central but! It was delicious

Name: أتاي كافي
Address: 62 rue amsafah, Rte Sidi Abdelaziz, Marrakech
Author: Tom Groves
Review: Nice spot for dinner. Burger was great! Nice rooftop. We went quite late and was very quiet. Chicken tagine was also very good and crisp. Decent value also

Name: أتاي كافي
Address: 62 rue amsafah, Rte Sidi Abdelaziz, Marrakech
Author: Luca Peranzoni
Review:
⁉️ BRIEFLY ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Lovely cafe where you can also lunch if you want to. They have some of the most iconic Moroccan staple foods such as tagine and hummus.

👎 A bit pricey 💵 you might easily end up paying 16€ or more and for Moroccan standards is a lot.

👎 Chicken tagine while flavourful the chicken meat is a bit dry.

👍 Lovely and comely service.

👍 Nice view of the medina from the terrace.

Name: أتاي كافي
Address: 62 rue amsafah, Rte Sidi Abdelaziz, Marrakech
Author: Caterina Bassano
Review: Fantastic chicken tagine! It was quiet when we visited but it was nice, service was friendly and quick, and prices were very reasonable compared to other rooftop Restaurants in the medina. We also had a lovely fruit juice and a bice Moroccan dessert with yogurt and argan oil.

Name: أتاي كافي
Address: 62 rue amsafah, Rte Sidi Abdelaziz, Marrakech
Author: Tabitha Stir
Review:
The food was mega delicious, but portions too small. Make sure you order two dishes.
Good job to the chef ❤️

Name: Monquotidien
Author: Elisabeth Myers
Review: Always pleasant shopping in here. The ladies behind the counter are nice as well as patient and offered a taste of some cookies before I bought them. The apple shortbread tart was very nice.

Name: Monquotidien
Author: Marina Barchuk
Review:
Very nice pastery shop, you can find so many deferent dessert + so much more .
Definitely stop by .
Highly  recommend 👌 👍

Name: Monquotidien
Author: Akatsuki Yona
Review:
I visited Mon Quotidien with a friend, craving something sweet to help stabilize my blood sugar. The pastries were absolutely delicious However, the service we received did not match the quality of the food.

When we asked to have our order split so that we could each pay for our own items, the cashier responded with a sigh and a dismissive attitude, insisting that it wasn’t possible. His lack of professionalism was noticeable, as he didn’t make eye contact or offer any kind of alternative solution. His cold and indifferent demeanor made what should have been an enjoyable experience feel uncomfortable. Unfortunately, I wasn’t feeling well enough to press the issue further, so we ended up paying together.

While I cannot fault the quality of the food, the poor customer service left a negative impression. It’s a shame that such a wonderful bakery is let down by staff who do not reflect the same level of care that goes into the products.

Name: Monquotidien
Author: Shauna Miller
Review: I had dinner here 2 nights ago and it was really good. The food was delicious and the staff were very friendly an helpful. The cakes were fantastic I could have eaten more.

Name: Monquotidien
Author: dounya damir
Review:
I had a delightful breakfast
The ambiance was serene, the service was excellent, and I truly appreciated the calm atmosphere. Additionally, I want to express my gratitude to @lahoussinefor their outstanding assistance. Thank you for a wonderful experience

Name: Armanl cafe resturant
Author: N/A
Review: N/A

Name: Complexe Rihab
Author: Tyra S.
Review:
Peaceful spot to sit, relax and take a break. Service is excellent! Food is fresh and delicious! And free WiFi but it's expensive and they have no air conditioner.
They have a park next to it where kids can run and play, and also have some seats there.

Name: Complexe Rihab
Author: Amine Lakraa
Review:
Very nice place that's have all what you gonna need
Place to pray
Green area and beautiful coffee shop

Name: Complexe Rihab
Author: Mohan Uttamchandani
Review: Fresh food and good ambiance

Name: Complexe Rihab
Author: janet logan
Review: excellent coffee. nice stagg

Name: Complexe Rihab
Author: Zakariae ALAMI
Review: not bad

Name: Café LOBO
Author: Moroccan Wonderer
Review: The food is acceptable, service is good, but it was way better whan i was here in 2019, the atmosphere is familial

Name: Café LOBO
Author: Shahm Jaffer
Review: Food was good, but made us wait over an hour for crepes. Went and asked several times for our crepes (which we ordered at the same time as our main dishes) and they only gave us the crepes when we got fed up and asked for the bill. Come here if you like sitting around wasting your time being disrespected.

Name: Café LOBO
Author: EL HADDAJ YASSINE
Review:
Very nice location, a nice place to chill.
Drinks are fresh

Name: Café LOBO
Author: Marouan Chagour
Review: Good calm and good service great Mm Amal + rajae

Name: Café LOBO
Author: zahra saidi
Review: Nice place, great service and good location.

Name: The Saloon
Address: les jardain D"oranges، Mag N2 Imm c AV gemmassa، mhamid, Marrakesh
Author: Monssif
Review: My favorite place for more than 3 years if I m not mistaken, i rather make my coffee at home if the place is close for some reasons, I simply like everything about this place, big thanks to Yassin the manager, Emad,Hoda and Chaima. I will definitely miss this place when I travel.

Name: The Saloon
Address: les jardain D"oranges، Mag N2 Imm c AV gemmassa، mhamid, Marrakesh
Author: christofer red
Review: Today was my second time I decide to go and pay a visit to that beautifully designed resturant Saloon, and I m truly truly disappointed from the waitress who is called Houda she was so rude and no professional, I even wondered who is the guest and who is the person who worked in that place bcs of her disrespectful manners and poor mentality, however it's really unfortunate to stop going to such beautiful place that I used to enjoy going to for almost it s opening bcs of such things that I really appreciate in hospitality. Look after your staff dear managers, and supervisors.

Name: The Saloon
Address: les jardain D"oranges، Mag N2 Imm c AV gemmassa، mhamid, Marrakesh
Author: saad Jaafari
Review: since 2020 till now the saloon was and still one of my favorite places where I enjoy my free time. nice design , great service / management , friendly staff and beautiful atmosphere . I highly recommend it ( 10 out of 10 )

Name: The Saloon
Address: les jardain D"oranges، Mag N2 Imm c AV gemmassa، mhamid, Marrakesh
Author: Said Hatouchi
Review:
What a unique place / cafe on the area,
The design and symplicity is just high, totally clean, and stuff are friendly,
I went here with friends and Absolutely going back,
Thank you Saloon for having me here, I just loved it,
Hard to find a place like this at least on the area of Mhamid.

Name: The Saloon
Address: les jardain D"oranges، Mag N2 Imm c AV gemmassa، mhamid, Marrakesh
Author: Moroccan Wonderer
Review: Best coffeeshop snack in mhamid, the space is big, service is so friendly, food is alright

Name: Cafe Las Torres De Majorelle
Address: JXQW+M97, Av. Yacoub El Mansour, Marrakech
Author: Victoria
Review:
Eaten here twice so far, food was lovely
Good place for a quick bite to eat or for dinner. Located on the main crossroads. Very busy road but the traffic is entertaining watching the cars bikes and people trying to cross the road.
I had the carbonra and my partner had the chicken kebabs. Both were amazing definitely recommend. The latte was delicious and the mint tea after food was one of the best I've had in marrakesh.
We had a pizza the day before which was also delicious 😋 Good selection on the menu, reasonably priced and friendly staff.

Name: Cafe Las Torres De Majorelle
Address: JXQW+M97, Av. Yacoub El Mansour, Marrakech
Author: Ablay Gabdullin
Review: The waiters are really nice, however the food wasnt the best. I would improve the quality of food. At the end, they give complimentary moroccan tea, which is nice

Name: Cafe Las Torres De Majorelle
Address: JXQW+M97, Av. Yacoub El Mansour, Marrakech
Author: Davinder S
Review: If you are looking to have a meal or coffee on local prices near to YSL museum then must go to this place. Only short walk away from the museum where you can find variety of food with fraction of the price. As its just on the corner of the street will give you nice view of local city.

Name: Cafe Las Torres De Majorelle
Address: JXQW+M97, Av. Yacoub El Mansour, Marrakech
Author: Yolanda
Review:
This is definitely one of the places I would return to.
Near YSL museum and the Majorelle garden, this cafe/restaurant has very good food (tasty and generous portions) at very affordable prices.
During my stay I had some difficulty finding places that inspired me with confidence to have meals (I admit it's because I'm picky with food) and this one met all the requirements.
The pizzas are big (of course, it depends on your appetite, but I can say that a pizza is good for two people), the pastas are served in good portions, the natural juices are very good (we asked for avocado, mango and cucumber juice) and we were even offered a tea at the end.
If you need a place to eat, this is it!

Name: Cafe Las Torres De Majorelle
Address: JXQW+M97, Av. Yacoub El Mansour, Marrakech
Author: Richard Law
Review: Stopped by for a late lunch, the pastilla poulet (chicken) ordered was great with a lovely presentation - however the coffee I asked for never arrived, and only when the bill came did I realise they charge 10dh for a small bottle of water on the table which was outrageous (only then did it dawn on me that's why no one actually opened their bottle on the table) - would have otherwise given it better rating

Name: Cafe Pizzeria
Author: Chasing Lemons
Review: Excellent place for morning breakfast. Filled with locals and great prices.

Name: KRUSTI MARRAKECH
Author: Sia el
Review: My husband and I visited this coffee place for breakfast, and we were thoroughly impressed. Despite arriving a bit late for breakfast, the staff were incredibly friendly and accommodating. The mssmen was the most delicious I've ever had, and the eggs with khliia were equally delightful. The atmosphere was quiet and perfect for working, with super friendly staff ensuring a pleasant experience. Even though our bill was under 100 DHS, they allowed us to pay with a card, which was very convenient. Highly recommended!

Name: KRUSTI MARRAKECH
Author: Enguerrand Racinoux
Review: I used to go there when I had class in the nearby high school. The place is close to the school and offers a wide variety of activities for young teenagers to enjoy. gaming PC, Xbox, switch, etc.. all can be used for a small fee for hours of fun during your lunch break!

Name: KRUSTI MARRAKECH
Author: Peter Manshoven
Review: A few years ago Krusti was the place to be and Hotspot of the Victor Hugo zone. Today it is still ok for a coffee,and a meeting as you will find always space. However it is time that the owner gives the place a nice makeover

Name: KRUSTI MARRAKECH
Author: Anouar Bzn
Review: Wonderful place to visit if you want to be comfortable

Name: KRUSTI MARRAKECH
Author: gurdeep dhillon
Review: A nice local place to work or relax with great coffee and breakfast. Friendly staff

Name: Café Hexagone
Author: ZAKARIA ATEL
Review: The waiters literally don't care, they take your orders and make you wait for hours, and if you question it, they just ignore you. I don't recommend it at all, not family friendly, and diffently not for kids.

Name: Café Hexagone
Author: Salty B
Review: Service zero. mo3amala zero. 9hwa zero.

Name: Café Hexagone
Author: AYOUB YOUSRI
Review: Good cafe good service

Name: Café Hexagone
Author: Khalid Essayf
Review: Cafe

Name: Café Hexagone
Author: Abdelkarim Hakam
Review: Slt

Name: cafe snack EL Ouaaid
Author: Prof Svt استاذ العلوم
Review: Oh God, make it easy

Name: Garrison Coffee
Address: N, E5 Magasin, complexe ibn tachfin, 14 Bd Prince Moulay Abdellah, Marrakech
Author: Amine El merzouki
Review:
An excellent spot for people who enjoy vaping and seek a relaxing environment, accompanied by a very exceptional service.
I really recommend GarrisonCoffee

Name: Garrison Coffee
Address: N, E5 Magasin, complexe ibn tachfin, 14 Bd Prince Moulay Abdellah, Marrakech
Author: Mohamed Moualim
Review: A unique vibe

Name: Garrison Coffee
Address: N, E5 Magasin, complexe ibn tachfin, 14 Bd Prince Moulay Abdellah, Marrakech
Author: Mery AD

Name: Garrison Coffee
Address: N, E5 Magasin, complexe ibn tachfin, 14 Bd Prince Moulay Abdellah, Marrakech
Author: Ikrame JAA

Name: Garrison Coffee
Address: N, E5 Magasin, complexe ibn tachfin, 14 Bd Prince Moulay Abdellah, Marrakech
Author: Khalil Mahrache

Name: MOROCCAN TEAHOUSE RESTAURANT - 1112 MARRAKECH
Address: N° 12 Place de La Kissariat Ben Youssef, Marrakesh
Author: Hayes Roberts
Review:
Incredibly beautiful restaurant / tea house.   Tea was like nothing I’ve ever had before and pastries were delicious.  After tea we received a full detailed tour and learned about tea, Moroccan culture, history and more.  And got a rooftop view of the city, all from a very knowledgeable employee who we wandered into.

I would highly recommend this place.   We thought we were just getting tea and instead had an amazing experience.

Name: MOROCCAN TEAHOUSE RESTAURANT - 1112 MARRAKECH
Address: N° 12 Place de La Kissariat Ben Youssef, Marrakesh
Author: Adam Bensalah
Review: From the teas to the pastries, and even the hospitality, everything is on point and part of a learning experience. The cozy ambiance perfectly complements the rich flavors of the offerings, but what truly sets this teashop apart is the mini museum inside. It’s a hidden gem that provides a deeper understanding of Marrakech’s cultural and historical heritage. I left not only refreshed but also enriched with knowledge about my beautiful city. A must-visit for both tea lovers and curious travelers!

Name: MOROCCAN TEAHOUSE RESTAURANT - 1112 MARRAKECH
Address: N° 12 Place de La Kissariat Ben Youssef, Marrakesh
Author: Joe Austin
Review:
We had a lovely time drinking Moroccan mint tea and coffee being served by Mohammed who was very hospitable and gave us a lesson on how the teas are made and the history of the tea house.

Would highly recommend sitting in here with a Casablanca mint tea.

Thank you so much to Mohammed and the rest of the staff for the best tea experience we’ve ever had 😌

Name: MOROCCAN TEAHOUSE RESTAURANT - 1112 MARRAKECH
Address: N° 12 Place de La Kissariat Ben Youssef, Marrakesh
Author: Dine Time
Review:
Wow what a beautiful place! We hade some tea and pastries here and must say it was the best tea we’ve tried since arriving in Morocco. We even boutique some to take home.

The staff was equally as good and when visiting here you’ll get a tour around this beautiful Riad. Definitely a must see in Marrakech.

We had to go back one more time! And I can now recommend their breakfast as well.

Name: MOROCCAN TEAHOUSE RESTAURANT - 1112 MARRAKECH
Address: N° 12 Place de La Kissariat Ben Youssef, Marrakesh
Author: thedrummer2k
Review: Beautiful spot we stumbled across, serving authentic Moroccan tea and food. Muhammad was very helpful, taking time to explain the assortments of teas and pastries available. Well worth a visit before the influencers find out about it.

Name: MY Kechmara
Author: Mee “Lala” Yi
Review: We went in for breakfast, and it was the right place!  Fresh OJ, good coffee, and full plate of omelet with salad that cost a total of 140 Mad for two people.  I like the interior of the place. Highly recommend to tourists.

Name: MY Kechmara
Author: Michaelle G. Bonaccorso
Review: We ordered for my wife and I, a Vegan Salad and their vegetarian Gyozas for 220 dhs ( incl. delivery), please see this is what we got an oily cucumber tomato & old greens, a 10 dhs salade in truth, that tastes old and horrible. Disgusting frankly. And the Gyozas were filled with carrots and uncooked mushrooms … this was the most terrible money spent on our entire trip. Shame on your kitchen and chefs for sending this non sense. We threw it in the trash by the way! The food is just terrible. Delivery costed only 30 dhs.

Name: MY Kechmara
Author: Jessica B
Review: Super understated! Accidentally happened upon this place and decided to sit in the upper deck. Beautifully decorated, great atmosphere. Full bar and delicious tapas and dessert. Would highly recommend this place!

Name: MY Kechmara
Author: Hank Itkin
Review: The vegetarian items we ordered were excellent.  Unfortunately, they were out of the vegan burger, and the cooling system wasn't working at all on a 100 F day.  Nice, off the beaten track place, though!

Name: MY Kechmara
Author: Joe L.
Review: Cute little relaxing local restaurant with a relaxing, peaceful rooftop filled with greenery. Nice respite from the hot weather.

Name: Café Square
Address: N، 92 Av. Prince Moulay Rachid, Marrakesh
Author: Julia S
Review: Chicken was really raw, bread dry and the service was really slow. There was a loud noise from the kitchen and the place was restless. I do not recommend.

Name: Café Square
Address: N، 92 Av. Prince Moulay Rachid, Marrakesh
Author: Andrea Robles
Review: The chicken sandwich we ordered was really bad, the mayo tasted like it was 100 years old. A small water bottle was 12 DH and it wasn’t even cold. The service was non-existent. I do not recommend.

Name: Café Square
Address: N، 92 Av. Prince Moulay Rachid, Marrakesh
Author: Erika Espinel
Review:
We decided to dine in this place due to it's position and the good reviews given. We also got stopped by a guy who explained us the menu and told us we could order lamb too, that we were looking for.

Service was bad and rude, and we were told that there was no lamb in their menu. I tried to explain her I was told differently by the other guy both in English and French but shy said she didn't understand.

The worst part was at the cash desk where we asked to pay by card. They told us it was not possible which was unexpected so we took a little. When I came back to pay I the man was really rude by slamming the change on the desk without any word.

We ordered Tanjia marrakechia, Tajin de poulet avec citrons et olives and their Cous Cous. Food was good overall  though cous cous was really dry and without any sauce given. Prices were good.

Name: Café Square
Address: N، 92 Av. Prince Moulay Rachid, Marrakesh
Author: Mohamed LAMARI
Review: Honestly the food was delicious and great also I can't forget to thank Badr he was so nice with us and all the time smiling and help us with everything 😊 highly recommend it

Name: Café Square
Address: N، 92 Av. Prince Moulay Rachid, Marrakesh
Author: Abdelkarim Afrad
Review: The food was fine. Kind service with good price

Review: Beautiful spot we stumbled across, serving authentic Moroccan tea and food. Muhammad was very helpful, taking time to explain the assortments of teas and pastries available. Well worth a visit before the influencers find out about it.

Name: Bamal Tea
Author: N/A
Review: N/A

Name: Le Menzeh
Author: Coral Cotto
Review: Very nice place for afternoon coffee.

Name: Le Menzeh
Author: Charly Echevin
Review:
We didn't stay in the hotel but visited the gardens and had tea at Le Menzeh cafe.
The gardens are very beautiful and can be visited for free even if you are not a hotel guest.
The mint tea was good and the setting beautiful. 70 Dh for tea per person.

Name: Le Menzeh
Author: Be Heritage
Review: Thank you MR ASSAD for the elegance of your service, I was with my clients they really appreciated the setting and the service as well as the Moroccan tea

Name: café snack hamdinou
Author: EL MEHDI HAFIDI
Review: I loved the area. The tea was fresh. The atmosphere was windy and welcoming. The waiter and manager were nice to us. Recommended

Name: café snack hamdinou
Author: the best kabir
Review: Such a beautiful restaurant

Name: café snack hamdinou
Author: rabi3 el khattaby
Review: God

Name: café snack hamdinou
Author: Klaus Schuster
Review: Beautiful sun terrace, lots of space and a large TV room.

Name: café snack hamdinou
Author: Zohra Moulim
Review: Nice place. Perfect service. Very good coffee

Name: Chez Hicham
Author: Mariona Peiretó
Review:
This place is really cozy. The juices are super fresh and smoothies as well. You can also go there for crepes. We went there everyday for a healthy refreshment as our Riad was just around the corner.

The atmosphere is also very nice. There’s space to sit inside and outside and the ambience and decor is really vacay like. Plus the guy who’s serving is super quiet but very good vibe! Very caring and with a lot of attention to detail. I’d recommend it for a sweet craving or healthy snack/drink stop!

Name: Chez Hicham
Author: Catherine Hughes
Review: A lovely spot to enjoy while you’re in Marrakech. Delicious fresh fruit juices and more at this relaxing local cafe. The owner is warm and welcoming,  making it a perfect spot to take a break and watch daily life unfold in the Old Town.

Name: Chez Hicham
Author: Daniela
Review:
We had the lemon juice, the orange, and the pineapple (all individually) it was so good!! Fresh and refreshing. He makes them fresh.
We also had some crepes, and they were delicious!
We were only disappointed to find this place on our last day.
Thank you!

Name: Chez Hicham
Author: Millie Jarvis
Review:
This is a crêpe and juice stand. We had both a crêpe and juice. The juices were made fresh upon ordering and tasted amazing. We ordered one strawberry and one mango juice we visited later on our trip to to order some crepes at night. They were made fresh and tasted amazing and are very good further price that you pay.

The service was quick, and the server was very friendly and cheerful. Thank you

Name: Chez Hicham
Author: frxendly
Review:
BIG CRÊPE SMALL PRICE ⭐️
* For only 8€ - SO LESS THAN 10€ - you can get two Crêpes + two freshly squeezed juices 🥤
* Furthermore, those were perhaps the best crepes that I’ve ever tried NO CAP 🧢 (outside of France)
* Extremely kind owner and cute cat 🐈

This place is WORTH A VISIT when you visit Marrakesh ✨

Name: TCHABA "a cup of time"
Author: Himmad Khan
Review: I have seen a few travel sites so to come here for tea but it's literally a booth in the mall. I'm sure it's a good place to buy tea but not a place to sit down for tea

Name: TCHABA "a cup of time"
Author: Kadim ELAROUSSI
Review: The area is also good, the products but the service average

Name: TCHABA "a cup of time"
Author: Driss
Review: Poor service. I used to eat this tge before, but no longer remembering the type I used before, I wanted to taste it to make the right choice. The salesperson asked me to come by the following week. Hmm

Name: TCHABA "a cup of time"
Author: ABDELJEBBAR HARTIT
Review: The quality of their teas and herbal teas is incredible

Name: TCHABA "a cup of time"
Author: Dar Khmissa Riad & Spa
Review: Magnificent selection of teas

Name: HENNA LOUAYA
Address: Chez Riad Louaya, 24 Derb Halfaoui, Marrakech
Author: Joanne Hong
Review:
Fast and amazing service!

I was 10 minutes late for an appointment I made via email but they still greeted me warmly with complimentary tea. The riad was kind of tucked away from the main street (the sign won’t say Henna Louaya) so watch out for that.

The courtyard in the Riad is beautiful!. I had to stay there ~30 minutes after the treatment and just sitting in the waiting area was relaxing in itself. Once I arrived they gave me a book of designs I can choose from. Some folks with me chose their design before arriving.

We were initially worried because the henna was giving us a slight cooling and tingly sensation. The henna seemed genuine (reviews, colour was brown). It was confusing because it couldn’t have given all of us allergic reactions. Thankfully the sensation went away after few minutes. We were told not to take off the henna / wash hands for 3 hours. After 8 hours ish I scraped them off. I’ve attached photos before and after 2 days.

The whole thing cost 120dh. They only take cash but also can take euros if you have any.

Overall satisfied with the service!

Name: HENNA LOUAYA
Address: Chez Riad Louaya, 24 Derb Halfaoui, Marrakech
Author: Miona Kikanovic
Review: Henna Louaya provides an exceptional henna art experience. Their professionalism shines through, delivering stunning henna designs. Riad's hospitality adds to the ambiance, offering mint tea and water upon arrival. The plethora of henna design options ensures everyone finds their perfect match. The environment exudes cleanliness and a serene energy, perfect for a relaxing session. For top-notch henna art, look no further than Henna Louaya.

Name: HENNA LOUAYA
Address: Chez Riad Louaya, 24 Derb Halfaoui, Marrakech
Author: Ahsan Khan
Review: We looked for reviews of the top Henna artists in Marrakesh. We were suggested Henna Louaya, and it definitely lived up to the recommendation. We was greeted with mint tea and given various henna designs to choose from. The henna quality was excellent, and the riad environment was delightful. Excellent value for money. Highly recommend it.

Name: HENNA LOUAYA
Address: Chez Riad Louaya, 24 Derb Halfaoui, Marrakech
Author: Crystal C
Review: You can find this place using walking directions with google. And you should visit if you want henna. The Henna is made with natural ingredients and was done by a women who’s been learning henna since her teens. She was so sweet and the atmosphere was calm. You also get mint tea and water. I really enjoy my time and the prices online are fairly accurate to what you’ll pay but the final price is based on the design itself.

Name: HENNA LOUAYA
Address: Chez Riad Louaya, 24 Derb Halfaoui, Marrakech
Author: Bianca Sims
Review: Hidden away at the end of an alley in a Riad. A lovely group of ladies are waiting to do the most beautiful Henna for you. I was welcomed and served tea while having my Henna done. For the one I chose in my photo it was €20. Price ranges from €5+. Come here for your Henna, do not go to J’ma Elfna square!

Name: Lmkaoui's house
Author: N/A
Review: N/A

Name: Cafe tadziwin
Address: PVM8+6FJ، إقليم تارودانت منطقة, Ctre Commune Ahl Tifnoute Assarag
Author: N/A
Review: N/A

Name: Villa Majorelle
Author: Phoebe Thomas
Review: Went to this cafe on 11th October 2024 .Most rudest service provided by this waiter . He was rude to my partner when we asked change of table . We have to get our own menu . He only took our order . Got our receipt mixed up . Hence I decided not to tip him and he followed me and tried to embarrass me in front of the other customers for not tipping him . I did have to speak back to him . Don’t tip unless he offers you a service . The other staff members were friendly and greeted with a smile . Food was okay . But the whole experience was let down my how we were treated by the WAITER .

Name: Villa Majorelle
Author: Juan Joseph
Review: The food took more than 30 minutes to arrive after placing the order. While the food was decent, it was very overpriced for a cafe. The service was slow and a tall waiter wearing a hat demanded a tip while we were paying via card. He was rude and was insisting on tipping him even when we said we didn’t have any cash available. He went on to say that he saw money in our wallet and kept muttering behind us while we walked out of the restaurant afterwards !!! Extremely rude and unprofessional behaviour!!

Name: Villa Majorelle
Author: C B
Review:
Would not recommend at all!!!
We ordered bottled drinks, which took almost 30 minutes to arrive. On top of that, the waiter had the audacity to demand a very high tip despite the poor service. Overall, a disappointing experience and felt like a scam.

Name: Villa Majorelle
Author: C K
Review:
It’s a pretty place.
Staffs are very nice but they are just so so so busy… no time for ordering and no time for check 🫠 …management’s problem

Name: Villa Majorelle
Author: Anna Moser
Review:
Very long waiting time, it took us 2 hours to have lunch. Food is good, although they didn’t have the dessert we asked.
Since it’s clearly a touristic place everything is overpriced.
However, if you’re not in a rush it’s a beautiful place for lunch after visiting museum.

Name: Salon de Thé
Author: Jun
Review:
Got scammed, should avoid.
Order a couple glasses of beer, the beer was not on the menu, but we thought it wouldnt  be too expensive. When the bill came, it was actually 70 freaking Dirham per glass. Double or perhaps triple the local price. Other dishes taste fine, but small portioned.
Ask before order, otherwise avoid at all cost..

Name: Salon de Thé
Author: Cécile Prin
Review: We stop here by chance, to eat and drink tea. We ask what the pastries are, unable to get an answer from the waiter. To all the questions we ask he answers with a “yes”. We then suggest going down to choose, he says no and offers to bring a tray so that we can choose. Summary of the shopping: the Berber sandwich is not served, ''because it's too late'', the requested coke is replaced by a pepsi, we waited a long time for three skewers, and the chef's surprise: we have to pay for the entire tray of pastries because we “touched” it. In short, a lousy technique to put us against the wall, no commercial gesture made, and a server who shone with his bad faith. Run away

Name: Salon de Thé
Author: EL MAJDOUBI Mimoun
Review: The best place you and your family can go to

Name: Dibo
Author: Comrade Zach
Review:
The service is great, they have next to everything you'd want, and all the employees are very nice and fun to chat with.
I often look forward to going there just for the small talk.

Name: Dibo
Author: Koom Martial Arts Retreat Center Marrakech
Review: Clean and attached to a meat seller and a beautiful mosq.

Name: Dibo
Author: Rachid Ait Barhich
Review: Nice store very helpful stuff

Name: Dibo
Author: Naoufal Immobilier
Review: Bosn services

Name: Dibo
Author: Mustapha AIT ALLA (Mufasa)
Review: Clean and well deserved

Name: J HOUSE SPECIALITY COFFEE
Address: Résidence assila 146 immeuble F magasin 17 Mohamed Marrakech, Marrakech
Author: Nab Lamdani
Review: Small spot specially inside. They have AC inside for hot weather, the service is good and quick,

Name: J HOUSE SPECIALITY COFFEE
Address: Résidence assila 146 immeuble F magasin 17 Mohamed Marrakech, Marrakech
Author: Rihab Houmadi
Review: Great coffee, the place is spacious, conveniently close to the airport, and offers free parking. Prices are reasonable, but I hope they add brunch options soon.

Name: J HOUSE SPECIALITY COFFEE
Address: Résidence assila 146 immeuble F magasin 17 Mohamed Marrakech, Marrakech
Author: Ben Dane
Review: I’m afraid the recent comments are right, staff is very nice and helpful but the coffee is in no way third wave/specialty coffee.. “cappuccino” came in a glass with loads of cocoa powder. If you claim soft opening, please adjust your online appearance accordingly.

Name: J HOUSE SPECIALITY COFFEE
Address: Résidence assila 146 immeuble F magasin 17 Mohamed Marrakech, Marrakech
Author: Seth Colchester
Review: This place is not what it says it is, pictures are not real, Instagram is fake, wasted my time and money going there for a nothing special coffee, Awful!

Name: J HOUSE SPECIALITY COFFEE
Address: Résidence assila 146 immeuble F magasin 17 Mohamed Marrakech, Marrakech
Author: Walaa's Colorways
Review: This place is not what it appears to be, it’s nothing like the pictures, it was a scam. Don’t go if you are looking for a specialty coffee

Name: Café Oxo
Author: zanga losseni kone
Review: Cool

Name: Besties Bakery
Author: khaoula guedad
Review:
Amazing service, Nice food and the places is all pink like a barbie house, I loved it 😍  it was one of the best places i have visited ♥️♥️.
The name of the person who served us is Amin he was nice, gentel, smiling all the time and very helpful 💖 .
After that the manager his name is Soufiane he was also amazing, humble and very helpfull 💖💖💖 I will visit there again and again for sure . Because of their service I am doing my birthday there soon  . Thank you all for this experience 🙏.

Name: Besties Bakery
Author: Hajar Hamadi
Review: I'm giving this 2 stars for the friendly service. I was disappointed with my red velvet cookie. It was a bit overcooked to my liking. I probably wouldn't go back anytime soon.

Name: Besties Bakery
Author: VOYAGEZ AU MAROC
Review: Besties Bakery in Marrakech is truly a gem! The bakery offers a delightful range of delicious treats that are sure to satisfy any sweet tooth.

Name: Besties Bakery
Author: Zafizuddin Zainuddin
Review: I had the pleasure of coming here a couple of days ago. The food was great and the service was amazing. I would like to commend Amine because he catered to our every need. As a Singaporean who can’t speak the Moroccan language, he did very well in conveying his words in English. Kudos! Highly recommended.

Name: Besties Bakery
Author: Jihane Barehmi
Review:
Quality of the cakes has to be improved. I took 4 different cakes was impressed with none of them. They did not taste fresh at all
The price charged exceeds the expectation of the quality delivered
Cupcake Oreo that did not taste like Oreo it more seemed like génoise. The red velvet brownie too heavy and no taste , the red velvet cake was more brown than red
The mango cupcake was okay taste wise
Definitely review the quality

Name: عبد الجليل ليمامي
Author: N/A
Review: N/A

Name: Café jawahir House
Author: Islam Ben omar
Review: It's wonderful

Name: Café NzaHa Place
Author: Ilyes Bkhanee
Review:
Full experience in this location
You will found all the activities you want
Camel ride, quad, buggy,
And if you want with this activity a lunch or dinner just call the number here
I know the place 100%
I gave the place 5⭐ to everything food and service also the atmosphere
All this things inside the desert of agafay

Name: Café NzaHa Place
Author: Sanae Douich
Review: Pleasant time spent with family. Come there with your eyes closed

Name: Café NzaHa Place
Author: Stéphanie (Noen)
Review:
Wonderful place
Extraordinary view.
Always so welcoming.

Name: Fassila
Author: Agata Nowak
Review: I had some cookies to go, they were ok. Coffee definitely wasn’t „the best in the souk”. Ive read before good reviews about friendly staff.. unfortunately the woman working there wasn’t one of them.

Name: Fassila
Author: Sarah Spinelli
Review: Fabulous mint tea and great vibe. Loved stopping into this little spot for a break from medina shopping.

Name: Fassila
Author: Melik Babur
Review: There were no galette de sarrasin and they were not bothered to prepare. Had chicken&cheese sandwich instead which was horribly hard to chew. Try to avoid.

Name: Fassila
Author: paolo salvetti
Review: Best coffee and tea in Marrakesh. Faboulous crepes. Amazing service. Recommended!

Name: Fassila
Author: Ewi D
Review: The best! Love Mimi the kitty too.

Name: Coffee House
Author: N/A
Review: N/A

Name: Secret House
Author: Meriem GHOUZALI
Review: Good coffee nice stuff and comfortable place to prepare ur exams and hang out with friends

Name: Secret House
Author: MOUJAHID Abdelali
Review: Nice staff.

Name: Secret House
Author: Ahmed Ayso
Review: I like it

Name: Secret House
Author: choukri said
Review: Well it's beautiful

Name: Secret House
Author: Med El habib
Review: Not bad

Name: Bacha Coffee
Address: Dar el Bacha, Rte Sidi Abdelaziz, Marrakech
Author: H Nakamura
Review:
Highly recommended!! Really beautiful place and a really perfect service! It was impressive!
We went for coffee and desert after lunch, it was a great idea!
They have way too many variety of coffee type, we got lost though… better to ask advice or do your research in advance, unless you are an expert and know what you want

Name: Bacha Coffee
Address: Dar el Bacha, Rte Sidi Abdelaziz, Marrakech
Author: Denise Wang
Review:
Beautiful space for a coffee or a meal. As other people have mentioned, there is a wait (we waited about an hour), however it gives you an opportunity to explore the beautiful museum. If you have some free time, I’d recommend going, however between the wait and the meal, you’ll need to allocate about three hours.

To get into the museum (you need to pay entry to get to the cafe), it’s 60DH per person (cash only). The cafe itself takes credit card.

Name: Bacha Coffee
Address: Dar el Bacha, Rte Sidi Abdelaziz, Marrakech
Author: Liezel San Andres
Review: The waiting time at the restaurant was about an hour and a half, with service being incredibly slow—taking 30 minutes just to receive the menu, another 30 to 45 minutes for the food to arrive, and nearly an hour to finally get the bill to think we only ordered two coffees and two slices of cake—yet the marble cake was so hard it was almost inedible, though the ‘Pastry of the Week’ cappuccino was decent. The ambiance was pleasant, but the service was truly awful and price was so expensive. On the bright side, they offer a wide range of coffee options, which I appreciated, and the iced coffee was enjoyable. I also bought coffee beans from the shop, which turned out to be the highlight of the experience—they were gorgeous when brewed at home in my coffee machine. Unfortunately, that was the only truly positive aspect of the visit. It is an experience but I would never dine again.

Name: Bacha Coffee
Address: Dar el Bacha, Rte Sidi Abdelaziz, Marrakech
Author: Eli Haugen
Review:
The coffee, food, and service were so good that I visited three times during my week in Marrakech. The wait time is absolutely worth it if you pay the little bit extra to explore the museum and make friends with the other people waiting (shout out to the lovely lady from Holland ♥️). I loved learning about the history of the building and of the coffee shop as well.

Don’t miss trying the homemade cream and fresh vanilla shavings in your coffee - after you enjoy it black (as they helpfully suggest). I’m a particular fan of the Yemeni coffee, and my friend enjoyed the Jamaican Blue Mountain coffee.

Name: Bacha Coffee
Address: Dar el Bacha, Rte Sidi Abdelaziz, Marrakech
Author: Julia Hope
Review: Arrived around 10:40 and seated at 12:30- I recommend going straight to the cafe from the museum so they can get your name down on the list then you have time to explore- well worth the wait! Loved all the coffee choices and the pastries were amazing- we got the cheesecake and tiramisu!

Name: Gourmet Market by La Table du Marché - Carre Eden
Author: Del Boy
Review:
A Hidden Gem in Marrakech – Gourmet Market is a Must-Visit!

On my first night in Morocco, I stumbled upon what would become the highlight of my trip: Gourmet Market in Marrakech. This charming little restaurant is more than just a place to eat—it's an experience that made me feel like part of the family from the moment I walked in.

The staff here are absolutely amazing. From the moment I entered, I was greeted with warmth and genuine hospitality that made me feel at home in a foreign city. One staff member in particular, a remarkable young lady, went above and beyond in making my evening special. Her English was impeccable, and her friendly demeanour instantly put me at ease. She is truly a credit to the service floor, embodying the best of Moroccan hospitality.

Now, let’s talk about the food. I decided to try one of their burgers, and it was nothing short of spectacular one of the best I’ve ever tasted. The flavours were perfectly balanced, the ingredients fresh, and the presentation flawless. It’s clear that Gourmet Market takes immense pride in the quality of their dishes.

Despite the late hour when I arrived, the staff made me feel as though I was their first customer of the day, with energy and enthusiasm that never waned. The ambience was cosy and welcoming, with a vibe that felt both intimate and vibrant, perfect for a solo traveller, a small to large family, or a group of friends.

Gourmet Market isn’t just a restaurant; it’s a place where memories are made. If you find yourself in Marrakech, do yourself a favour and visit. The incredible food, the outstanding service, and the unforgettable experience are something I’ll be reminiscing about for years to come. Thank you, Gourmet Market, for turning my first night and every night after in Morocco into an unforgettable experience!

I also would like to thank Rachid for his warm welcome, great service, and amazing send-off. Thank you my friend.

Name: Gourmet Market by La Table du Marché - Carre Eden
Author: Wissal Mojahid
Review: The food and service were absolutely good ! It's rare to find a place where both aspects exceed expectations, I'll definitely be going back for more

Name: Gourmet Market by La Table du Marché - Carre Eden
Author: Eliza Rucinska
Review: Delicious food, great customer service. Especially the waiter girl, she’s very nice.  Whenever I will come back I will definitely pop in again. Thank you for being amazing ♥️🇲🇦

Name: Gourmet Market by La Table du Marché - Carre Eden
Author: Ahmed Alattar
Review:
Was great!

Has really delicous desserts!

But the portion of the drinks was really little!

Name: Gourmet Market by La Table du Marché - Carre Eden
Author: Desert Tour From Marrakech
Review: It's à wounderfull coffee shop for refreshing your mind and have a cool evening 😎 I reccomend

Name: Thirty5ive Marrakech
Author: Oanh
Review: This hip and laid-back modern coffee shop is a great spot to relax. You can unwind downstairs or upstairs with your coffee or smoothie of choice while enjoying cool music from a record player.

Name: Thirty5ive Marrakech
Author: Emily
Review: This cafe is a gem in Marrakech! It’s the best coffee I’ve had in the city. It has a nice minimalist design and the barista is skilled and friendly. I would have happily worked at this cafe had I discovered it sooner. The cinnamon honey matcha latte with soy milk is so good!

Name: Thirty5ive Marrakech
Author: Mònica Noviembre
Review: Great place in terms of ambience and good vibes. Also, high quality coffee. Very recommended!

Name: Thirty5ive Marrakech
Author: Dalida Spenser
Review: Beautiful and bohemian place, quiet luxury with elegant products, to escape classic places, you most visit this minimalistic  designed cafe with well researched music repertoire. Well done !

Name: Thirty5ive Marrakech
Author: Emils U
Review: Wow! What a space. Beautiful vinyl music, amazing specialty coffee and a fantastic pottery space. Far outdid any expectations we had. Fantastic service to add!

Name: CHEZ LALLA ZORA
Author: N/A
Review: N/A

Name: Café Hexagone
Author: ZAKARIA ATEL
Review: The waiters literally don't care, they take your orders and make you wait for hours, and if you question it, they just ignore you. I don't recommend it at all, not family friendly, and diffently not for kids.

Name: Café Hexagone
Author: Salty B
Review: Service zero. mo3amala zero. 9hwa zero.

Name: Café Hexagone
Author: AYOUB YOUSRI
Review: Good cafe good service

Name: Café Hexagone
Author: Khalid Essayf
Review: Cafe

Name: Café Hexagone
Author: Abdelkarim Hakam
Review: Slt

Name: Riad Yima Boutique & Art Gallery
Address: 52 derb Aarjane Rahba lakdima Medina, Marrakech, Marrakesh
Author: Mohit Gulati
Review:
Nice cozy place, just a short walk from the medina center but very peaceful. We came across zero tourists.

Nice indoor and terrace space where we enjoyed the Moroccon tea, served by the generous Haitam.

Name: Riad Yima Boutique & Art Gallery
Address: 52 derb Aarjane Rahba lakdima Medina, Marrakech, Marrakesh
Author: Ella Christella
Review: Interesting photography, clothes, shoes, lamps and teas designed by Hassan Hajjaj, a Moroccan contemporary artist who lives in London. I would have loved to buy one of his large photographs and lamps if I had a car to transport them in. I'm happy to have discovered him. Thank you for offering this space.

Name: Riad Yima Boutique & Art Gallery
Address: 52 derb Aarjane Rahba lakdima Medina, Marrakech, Marrakesh
Author: Nadav David
Review: What a wonderful hidden gem in the Madina! Beautiful and interesting art on three floors and the terrace. Very welcoming and friendly host. Highly recommended!

Name: Riad Yima Boutique & Art Gallery
Address: 52 derb Aarjane Rahba lakdima Medina, Marrakech, Marrakesh
Author: C Charritte
Review:
Really cool spot.

Riad, art gallery and tearoom :)
You can admired Hassan Hajjaj work while enjoying this quiet space close by the spices square.
The pop art vibe would definitely give you a good energy.

It's not easy to find but you just have to follow the sign!

Enjoy!

Name: Riad Yima Boutique & Art Gallery
Address: 52 derb Aarjane Rahba lakdima Medina, Marrakech, Marrakesh
Author: Linda M
Review: It is quite the experience to hunt down the riad to visit the retail outlet of YIMA (Google maps is incorrect. The alleyway entrance is opposite Riad Tahyra) Although the prices are high, the items are unique.

Name: La Mignardise
Author: Victor Reteghi
Review:
Absolutely great experience and lovely food!

This is our first time in Marrakech and we were looking for a place to have breakfast and while walking we have found this one which became our favourite(we been for 6 times in total). The food was amazing, desserts not to mention about, mouth watering!! The place is very clean, the waiters are very polite so the whole experience was brilliant!! Btw, I could pay by card which was also great. I would like to say THANK YOU SO MUCH to Mr Yassine who was the reason why we went there, he is extremely polite and was all the time smiling so we felt happy around him.
I would totally recommend this place!!

Name: La Mignardise
Author: Johanna S.
Review: Very great place! They were so cute with our son and the staff is all very attentive and friendly! They have highchairs and items on the menu which are very kid's friendly- my son loved the mash potatoes! The pastry is delicious and the drinks are great! Definitely recommend!

Name: La Mignardise
Author: Patricija Elizabete
Review: Amazing breakfast selection and very well-balanced pastries and cakes. Loved the attention to the detail for the coconut-mango dessert. Highly recommend their special breakfast plate which leaves you full for the whole day. Service and atmosphere was good too.

Name: La Mignardise
Author: Younes M
Review: My favorite spot in Marrakech. Staff is very friendly and helpful. Food is exquisite and the interior decor complements the experience.

Name: La Mignardise
Author: mhader hajar
Review: The food is good but the service is the worst. They always don't have change and many times I had to not buy at the end because of change.

Name: Palais Darkum - Sofitel Marrakech
Author: Adam Bencheikh
Review:
I’ve been in many places around the world and to be honest the experience i had in palais darkum is so special and memorable.
Lovely team, amazing service and adorable approach to customers…
i went there more than once i even make a cute friendship with all the team
Hi & kisses to all; mehdi, saber ouassif, aziz, yassine and the amazing manager Aicha 😘
“miss You guys, im definitely visiting soon”

Name: Palais Darkum - Sofitel Marrakech
Author: Marie Belle
Review: Memorable experience there ! We loved our stay  and every of its details.. From the hotel incredible scent to its architecture and pool area, excellent service and comfiest bed ! Still dreaming about it, we will come back and recommend without hesitation 👌❤️

Name: Palais Darkum - Sofitel Marrakech
Author: Harouch Harouch
Review: best hotel i'v ever see

Name: Palais Darkum - Sofitel Marrakech
Author: Stéphanie Krepelka
Review: One of the places where I spend the most time during my stays at the Sofitel in Marrakech… accompanied by a mint tea served with chickpea and sesame flour biscuits… or in the evening with a glass of wine… with the sparrows, the sound of their movements in the branches, the wind blowing making the pink and white flowers move, the mosaic tiles, the numerous passages of employees... thank you for creating this enchanting universe of which I never tire! See you soon ! “Madame Stéphanie” :)

Name: Palais Darkum - Sofitel Marrakech
Author: ER. Keny
Review:
Magnificent place, very refined and tastefully decorated. I've been coming here for several years now and it's nice to see this place regularly change its appearance. Going for a drink or eating in peace is really pleasant there. A staff that reflects the place, I would like to take this opportunity to thank Souad who took over from Yacine to serve me. The anecdote is that a bee fell into my juice, of course this happens, my ginger booster was so good that I understand the bee.
I pointed this out to the waitress asking her to remove it for me, when others would have simply removed the bee, Souad was immediately eager to replace it for me. Bravo for your professionalism young girl and your welcome. Your smiles made me feel welcome in this establishment. Thanks to Souad, without forgetting Yacine.
I really recommend this place.

Name: Guest House
Address: JXQV+HMC, Av. Yacoub El Mansour, Marrakech
Author: N/A
Review: N/A

Name: MFF Movenpick - MARRAKECH FINE FOOD
Author: R B
Review: Great place in Marrakech to taste 100% butter pastries. I really liked the eggs Benedict, as well as the Caesar salad. If you want to refresh yourself, the smoothie is excellent, but so is the iced tea. the team takes great care and is very professional. A big thank you to Kadija for her smile and her professionalism. See you soon

Name: MFF Movenpick - MARRAKECH FINE FOOD
Author: Fatima-Zahra Bentaleb
Review: A franchise where quality is always there, no matter the location! Never disappointed, I recommend without hesitation 👍

Name: MFF Sidi Ghanem - MARRAKECH FINE FOOD
Author: Fatima-Zahra Bentaleb
Review:
Quite surprised to see this place hasn’t received more reviews. As with all MFF franchises, the service and food here are of great quality 👍. Their viennoiseries and coffee are a must-try, and with the current heat in Marrakesh, their fresh (sugar-free) smoothies mixed with MFF honey are so refreshing! I love the mango one! Their food is as tasty as it is healthy!
The prices are really good compared to the service and quality food offered.

Name: MFF Sidi Ghanem - MARRAKECH FINE FOOD
Author: catalina cipri
Review: In an up and coming area for artists and creators, not too far from the city center of Marrakech. The quality was incredible and so many unique offerings. The prices are expensive for Morocco but definitely worth it (more like European/American prices)

Name: MFF Sidi Ghanem - MARRAKECH FINE FOOD
Author: Amine Saoudi-Hassani
Review:
A real WOW effect. Products are excellent, from Tea to roasted fresh Specialty Coffee, to pastries and cookies, this new Flagship of the Tchaba (tea)and Zero Robusta (Coffee) by the Baroudi Family (top entrepreneurs) is a MUST visit to indulge yourself in a premium store. A new landmark for Marrakech and Morocco. Definitely becoming addicted to this place and their products. Congrats.
Note: the service is really excellent, the team here is totally customer focused, well trained, friendly, and it is obvious they also love the product. A Blast!

Name: MFF Sidi Ghanem - MARRAKECH FINE FOOD
Author: caludgero
Review: Amazing tea and coffee selection! Good for gifts!

Name: MFF Sidi Ghanem - MARRAKECH FINE FOOD
Author: Be Heritage
Review: The best coffee ever, different type of coffee for sales, nice place and new concept. Keep going

Name: Chez moha
Author: N/A
Review: N/A

Name: Café Des Épices
Author: BCL ADRIAN
Review: This place is very nice to have breakfast. The Moroccan breakfast is extremely nice and the food is amazing. I would highly recommend a visit here for breakfast lunch and/or tea or coffee. They gave seating both upstairs, on the street level, and also roof top. It is warm to eat here as there is no air conditioning but the environment and the food is with it even without air-conditioning. I would recommend to visit here. Bear in mind the small print they do charge 7% service fee additional to food cost.  It is quite expensive here nearly the same prices as you pay in Europe. But the food is worth it.

Name: Café Des Épices
Author: Caterina Bassano
Review: Great Moroccan food in a lovely rooftop space (the middle floor is also super nice). Great views over the Medina. The fish tagine was very tasty, and the hummus was delicious. We didn't book in advance but we went there early so it was quiet. It gets very busy especially around sunset hours, so service was a little slow. Reasonable prices!

Name: Café Des Épices
Author: Marlene Po
Review:
Very charming café situated at the small square where hats and baskets are sold .

But that's about it.

We ordered   Aish Burrito ( small portion for 60 DH ),  Vegetarian Sandwich ( again small portion for 70 DH... AND! VERY BLAND. Had to squeeze lots of lemon on  it.  Bread was hard.   Lentil salad was ho- hum, not much taste either.

In short, don't go here.

Overpriced, tasteless

Name: Café Des Épices
Author: Yousra
Review:
Had lunch here sadly came at a pick time, but still the view was great. Food was nice, morrocan tea.
Good touristic and prices are more expensive than the usual in the city. Friendly staff.

Name: Café Des Épices
Author: Ella Willoughby
Review: Great stop for a nice drink. We sat on the middle floor level as the top was too hot, but all the decor was very sleek and nice. The window looking onto the market was amazing to look at. We only had some fuzzy drinks but the food looked and smelt delicious.

Name: Le Salon Berbère
Author: N/A
Review: N/A

Name: Coffee Houmti ️ قهوة حومتي
Author: allison arandez
Review: Great little cafe, had a really nice cafe latte and sat and watched local life go by

Name: Coffee Houmti ️ قهوة حومتي
Author: Junghoon seo
Review:
My favourite cafe in Marrakech while I was there. Cozy place with a couple seats outside. It is a great resting place while you walk through the markets. Espresso slightly bitter but can be reduced with the sugar. I came here twice and the first time I got the croissant which was very nice. Soft on the inside and brushed with a sweet sauce. Super friendly barista.

It is in the middle of a very busy road so you will have motorbikes going past you. Other than that, worth a visit for sure.

Name: Coffee Houmti ️ قهوة حومتي
Author: AANUIT
Review: Nice place for a quick coffee and croissant. Hicham is very friendly and in for a small talk. The cappuccino is not like the “European” one I guess, but still good. Croissant were fresh.

Name: Coffee Houmti ️ قهوة حومتي
Author: Mia Cunningham
Review: Wow! This little spot is an amazing stop for a coffee & pastry. Very good prices and the quality is amazing. One of our favourite places for coffee in Marrakech. Thank you to the owner for his amazing service and beautiful coffee 🙏🏼❤️

Name: Coffee Houmti ️ قهوة حومتي
Author: Eamonn Coughlan
Review: Had coffee and orange juice. Both incredible. Loved the setup. Simple but elegant and well thought about

Name: Henna Art Cafe
Author: João Almeida
Review:
We found this cozy café-restaurant while walking around the Medina, and to be honest, it's a gem.

They have a small terrace, which, despite not having a stunning view, is still pleasant even on a hot day—thanks to a few fans they have there.

As for the food... it was excellent, from the appetizers to the dessert.

The staff is also friendly and helpful, which really enhances the overall experience.

Keep up the great work! :)

Name: Henna Art Cafe
Author: Nash
Review:
Come for henna, and stay for lunch!! But also, do it the other way around so you don't mess up your henna while you eat the best falafel you'll ever taste!

The henna artists were very quick and very friendly! The owners were there and were helpful and kind as well. The food was delicious and tasted super fresh!

I got henna on both my hands, lunch for two, and a couple little henna cats at the gift shop for around 630 MAD. Don't hesitate coming here it will not break the bank!

Name: Henna Art Cafe
Author: Andy Harris
Review:
Excellent gluten free selection with fantastic food, good menu choice and friendly staff.

Coeliac friendly and the dishes were super tasty.

The ladies doing the henna were also very talented and use authentic henna.

It is definitely a must visit!

Name: Henna Art Cafe
Author: Vikki M
Review:
Cute little place. We dropped in for an early dinner, we had the Moroccan salad and lentil soup - both were beautiful. Very quiet, very pleasant and very reasonable prices. Friendly staff.
We'll be back.

Name: Henna Art Cafe
Author: Wendy Warren
Review: This place is cute and a relaxing rooftop terrace to eat at. Just off the sook, you step into a place where you can get a henna or pop up top to eat. The lentils are great, as are the omelets. I had the mojito one day, and coffee today. Both good. Service is attentive and prompt. The patio is decorated with lovely art and they have artworks for sale. They take credit card with a 3% charge :) We are really enjoying it.

Name: Si Said Restaurant
Author: Abdulrahman Mohamed
Review:
10/10 - We came here today for a brunch for 20 people (which they last minute accommodated almost double!)

I am already looking forward to coming back again

Location & Setting: 😍 10/10

Food: So much variety and everything was so tasty (see pics)

Service: I can’t overstate how kind, accommodating and professional the team were. They really went the extra mile with helping with crying babies to accommodating food allergies so that no one felt left out.

A special thank you to Fatima, Omar, Amin, Said & Toufiq for their hard work and making the meal so special.

If you’re looking for a delicious meal in a lovely environment then you’ve found your spot.

Name: Si Said Restaurant
Author: Sara Galvin
Review: Beautiful food and wonderful service in a lovely setting.

Name: Si Said Restaurant
Author: Sikhei Leung
Review: Restaurant Angsana Si Said offers a delightful fusion of Moroccan & Asian flavors in a romantic courtyard setting. Live music, attentive service, & beautiful plating make for an unforgettable experience. Assorted starters & Green Curry are must-try dishes. A gem in Marrakech that exceeds expectations.

Name: Si Said Restaurant
Author: Andra Sas
Review: Exceptional food and service! We came in for my friend’s birthday dinner and, even though we didn’t book in advance, they made it into a very special evening for her and a great memory for all of us. The lamb tanjia is a must try. Our waiter Fatima was an absolute sweetheart and very accommodating.

Name: Si Said Restaurant
Author: Azra
Review: Just come here, whether it’s to stay or dine, you’re in for a wonderful experience.

Name: Pastry Gato, Marrakech, Morocco
Address: Rue Hassania, 2 pas de la clinique Chifa, et à Côté de l'école du Sud, Marrakech
Author: Pelin
Review: I've never tasted Moroccan sweets this delicious before. They are incredibly addictive and flavorfull. It's made from real ingredients like almonds, honey, and rose oil so they are healthy and also very Tastyyy. Huge thanks to Gato for creating such delightful sweets!!

Name: Pastry Gato, Marrakech, Morocco
Address: Rue Hassania, 2 pas de la clinique Chifa, et à Côté de l'école du Sud, Marrakech
Author: MARK MATTHEWS
Review: Take your time to google and find this place if you love quality Moroccan 🇲🇦 Pastries if you have had them before you will know there not cheep but worth every penny I bought half a kilo about £10 but there excellent 👍

Name: Pastry Gato, Marrakech, Morocco
Address: Rue Hassania, 2 pas de la clinique Chifa, et à Côté de l'école du Sud, Marrakech
Author: Alice Corolleur
Review: The pastries here are incredible! A special mention for the gazelle horns, which are absolutely excellent. Highly recommended for anyone looking to enjoy authentic Moroccan treats!

Name: Pastry Gato, Marrakech, Morocco
Address: Rue Hassania, 2 pas de la clinique Chifa, et à Côté de l'école du Sud, Marrakech
Author: Shaahid I
Review: Traditional Moroccan pastries made by this family business. Awesome taste and presentation. Pleasant service from the owner. One slight complaint is that the coffee served was a Nespresso capsule. A tea house should really have a proper coffee prep method. Absolutely worth a visit or take away.

Name: Pastry Gato, Marrakech, Morocco
Address: Rue Hassania, 2 pas de la clinique Chifa, et à Côté de l'école du Sud, Marrakech
Author: Nadia Bibi Sarwar
Review:
Sellou a dessert, roasted flour mixed with butter, sugar, cinnamon, almonds (or sometimes peanuts), and other ingredients is super yum.
Sfenj is a doughnut sprinkled with sugar or soaked in honey. Every pasty is distinctive and its only enough crust to hold the filing in a bite size.
Don’t forget to try them when you are in Morocco.

#Morocco #Food #SoloTraveler #Speaker #Gato #Patisserie #SehatKarwan #SDG03 #GoodHealthAndWellBeing

Name: Cafe La pampa
Author: ilyass boumzebra
Review:
Very wonderful coffee place for having brunches,breakfasts and coffee/tea/juices/mojitos.
The server is just wonderful and Very friendly makes you feel you re at home already. The service in general is Very professional and adequate.
I recommend this place highly for a chill time with family/friends/even meetings

Name: Cafe La pampa
Author: S4Y immo
Review:
I visited this cafe for the first time which has a very good design, comfort and air conditioning.
It's very cool inside.
On the other hand, I tried the coffee which costs 14dh. With this price we should have had a quality coffee.
Unfortunately the quality of the coffee is very average.
It is clear that the dose was very light.
You don't really feel the good taste of the coffee
It's obvious that this is a new cafe.
The beginning is always good in the projects of Marrakech
I hope they continue in this direction but they need to improve the quality of the coffee.

Name: Cafe La pampa
Author: Myriam Ladjimi
Review: Wide choice, taste and presentation are there. Very pleasant staff. A coffee shop that deserves to be known in Marrakech

Name: Cafe La pampa
Author: tcl tcll
Review: It's been a while since I didn't come but, I noticed a change in the quality of the coffee, the taste has become wonderful

Name: Cafe La pampa
Author: mohammed benbrahim
Review: Well renovated cafe, service and quality products, I recommend.

Name: ROUGE VANILLE
Address: 5 RESIDENCE SARA, Ave Mohamed VI, Marrakesh
Author: Lisa Teiger
Review: delicious french style pastries in a modern setting.  Upscale local neighborhood not far from one of the big malls.  We enjoyed the pastries, a cappuchino and the wifi - great afternoon break.

Name: ROUGE VANILLE
Address: 5 RESIDENCE SARA, Ave Mohamed VI, Marrakesh
Author: Elbariki Toufiq
Review: I JUST LOVE THE PLACE QUIET AND THE OWNER IS VERY COOL . THE COLORS ARE SO BEAUTIFULLY DONE ON THE WALLS AND THE SWEETS 😍😍😍

Name: ROUGE VANILLE
Address: 5 RESIDENCE SARA, Ave Mohamed VI, Marrakesh
Author: Mctar Trade
Review: Best in Marakesh. ...must visit...sweets made with love...

Name: ROUGE VANILLE
Address: 5 RESIDENCE SARA, Ave Mohamed VI, Marrakesh
Author: Meryem Baskoun
Review: The place seems to be closed. I think they are out of business

Name: ROUGE VANILLE
Address: 5 RESIDENCE SARA, Ave Mohamed VI, Marrakesh
Author: Ayoub Jackoup
Review:
very calm pastries
A healthy and affordable breakfast
The team and service are affordable
There is nothing to say thank you
And thank you to Ms. Youssra for following

Name: HESPERIS Coffee Factory
Author: David Hatch
Review:
Delightfully surprised. Did not expect to find such an upscale restaurant in Sidi Ghanem. Prices are a bit high for the area, but good value and we'll worth it. Very few places in all of Marrakech have service as good as here at HESPERIS. I would give the service 6-Stars if I could. Very well trained, polite, and respectful. The artwork is also enjoyable as well. Coffee was well done presented well.
Great choice to try!

Name: HESPERIS Coffee Factory
Author: Abraham Terpstra
Review: What an inspiring place! Hesperis serves specialty coffee that they roast themselves. I had the Ethiopian Giju G1. Floral and alive. Very friendly and helpful staff, we had the opportunity to see the roaster in the basement. I love the place and how they thought of every little detail in the interior (the terrace floors are beautiful).

Name: HESPERIS Coffee Factory
Author: H Ouk
Review:
Was absolutely clean and relaxing place
I like how the staff are smiling in front of anyone.

Name: HESPERIS Coffee Factory
Author: Julia Onorato
Review: Absolutely delicious food, amazing ambiance and the coffee was out of this work. The pastries and sweets were spectacular, and so fresh they must have been handmade that morning. The eggs were also light and fluffy - this was one of those breakfasts where you wish you could eat more because everything was so good. Would definitely recommend to my friends and will be coming back again soon!

Name: HESPERIS Coffee Factory
Author: Emily Byrski
Review: One of the best coffee shops I visited in Marrakech! The coffee and breakfast were delicious, the space was bright and airy, and the staff and owner were so hospitable and friendly! It was truly a joy and I’d recommend this spot to all of my friends! :)

Name: أتاي كافي
Address: 62 rue amsafah, Rte Sidi Abdelaziz, Marrakech
Author: Justice Rosegreen
Review:
Good location, beautiful views from the rooftop. Staff are attentive and friendly from start to end.
The food was nice and I enjoyed the assortment of Moroccan bruschetta.
It is expensive as it’s central but! It was delicious

Name: أتاي كافي
Address: 62 rue amsafah, Rte Sidi Abdelaziz, Marrakech
Author: Tom Groves
Review: Nice spot for dinner. Burger was great! Nice rooftop. We went quite late and was very quiet. Chicken tagine was also very good and crisp. Decent value also

Name: أتاي كافي
Address: 62 rue amsafah, Rte Sidi Abdelaziz, Marrakech
Author: Luca Peranzoni
Review:
⁉️ BRIEFLY ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Lovely cafe where you can also lunch if you want to. They have some of the most iconic Moroccan staple foods such as tagine and hummus.

👎 A bit pricey 💵 you might easily end up paying 16€ or more and for Moroccan standards is a lot.

👎 Chicken tagine while flavourful the chicken meat is a bit dry.

👍 Lovely and comely service.

👍 Nice view of the medina from the terrace.

Name: أتاي كافي
Address: 62 rue amsafah, Rte Sidi Abdelaziz, Marrakech
Author: Caterina Bassano
Review: Fantastic chicken tagine! It was quiet when we visited but it was nice, service was friendly and quick, and prices were very reasonable compared to other rooftop Restaurants in the medina. We also had a lovely fruit juice and a bice Moroccan dessert with yogurt and argan oil.

Name: أتاي كافي
Address: 62 rue amsafah, Rte Sidi Abdelaziz, Marrakech
Author: Tabitha Stir
Review:
The food was mega delicious, but portions too small. Make sure you order two dishes.
Good job to the chef ❤️

Name: Café Babouche medina
Author: Gary and Bianca
Review:
Loved the staff.

We watched the Euro Football and regrettably didn’t get to eat here. The staff were all lovely and engaging. The menus of the day were well priced and the food looked good. Not a salubrious looking place but full of smiles.

Name: Café Babouche medina
Author: Lara NJ
Review:
Best food I tried i Marrakech, unfortunately we found it at our last day. I recommend it, tasty food and nice design.
The only thing that I don’t like is everything costs extra, basic salad costs 30 MAD!

Name: Café Babouche medina
Author: Aleksandra Jarosz
Review: I visited the place in the afternoon and there were free seats.  The kitchen served meals quite slowly, but that's the norm in this city.  The food was very good so that everyone could enjoy it.  There are also vegetarian options, but a vegetarian tajine could have more types of vegetables.  Nevertheless, the very good location and service make it a nice place to visit.

Name: Café Babouche medina
Author: Yousef Shafie Zadeh
Review:
Hidden Gem! Lovely staff that are informative and kind. I felt really comfortable and relaxed here compared to most other places around Marrakech. Food is devine and healthy. Super reasonable pricing with Authentic Moroccan cooking.

Places like this make you want to come back again and again 5 stars 😀

Andd I was taught some Arabic to help me throughout the rest of my trip 👌 e.g. how to say no coriander as my companion is allergic

Name: Café Babouche medina
Author: Ane Miren Arriaga
Review:
Just beautiful! Food is absolutely amazing and the staff very kind and friendly.
They don’t take long to deliver food and they bring you traditional sweets at the end. A must go :)

Name: Café restaurant DAR YEMMA
Address: MX2W+R7W, 228 Assif C Avenue 18 Novembre, Rue Naïm Assif D Marrakech, Marrakech
Author: Raja Benchekroun
Review: Beautiful and welcoming space for family gatherings, work, and business meetings. They offer a variety of food items, drinks, and, of course, Moroccan breakfast for all ages (kids approved 😉) and tastes! The staff is super friendly and helpful. This is my third year coming here! It’s calm even when it’s busy, making it perfect for getting things done for Nomad workers,this is a great spot for you!  Bakery is also a 5- Star.

Name: Café restaurant DAR YEMMA
Address: MX2W+R7W, 228 Assif C Avenue 18 Novembre, Rue Naïm Assif D Marrakech, Marrakech
Author: Moroccan Wonderer
Review: A good experience in the area of daoudiate where there are limited choices to pick from, the brunch choices are simple but the food is tasty and representable, the staff is friendly, it's difficult to sit outisde in this heat wave but inside the atmosphere is good.

Name: Café restaurant DAR YEMMA
Address: MX2W+R7W, 228 Assif C Avenue 18 Novembre, Rue Naïm Assif D Marrakech, Marrakech
Author: Rania NEDRI
Review:
We had the best brunch experience in Marrakech at Dar Yemma! The menu options were diverse, the portions were generous, and the food was absolutely delicious.

The staff went above and beyond to make us feel welcome, and we enjoyed it so much that we returned for a second time. Plus, the prices were very reasonable compared to other brunch spots in the Medina.

This is definitely a must-try when visiting Marrakech!

Name: Café restaurant DAR YEMMA
Address: MX2W+R7W, 228 Assif C Avenue 18 Novembre, Rue Naïm Assif D Marrakech, Marrakech
Author: Abdelhak Bakhiyi
Review: The great place Dar Yemma is so welcoming especially an amazing man who works there Hamza masters his job. Thank you and I will definitely recommend this place.

Name: Café restaurant DAR YEMMA
Address: MX2W+R7W, 228 Assif C Avenue 18 Novembre, Rue Naïm Assif D Marrakech, Marrakech
Author: Nour Eddine
Review: One of my favs in the city, always a good option to chill and eat or drink something delicious.

Name: Les Terrasses Des Arts Marrakech
Author: Ricardo Brifeni
Review:
Lovely terrace on the rooftop, the restaurant even has a little photography exposition that shows you Marrakech from another perspective.
The food was incredible, from the entrance, to the main dish and dessert with a coffee as well.
I also ordered 2 homemade iced tea and each of them was very tasty and unique.
Service was absolutely fantastic. Hind, our waiter, was very kind, respectful and welcoming.
Highly recommend visiting this restaurant, it will be worth your time and your money!

Name: Les Terrasses Des Arts Marrakech
Author: Island Girl
Review: Lovely restaurant located in a quieter part of the Medina.   Excellent service.  Next time I’ll have to do dinner as well to get the nighttime ambiance.

Name: Les Terrasses Des Arts Marrakech
Author: Katelyn Siebert
Review: This is an amazing restaurant in Marrakech! We had the penne pomodoro  and the beef filet. Everything was so good!! The veggies were a little too salty for our taste but everything else was amazing. We ate on their main terrace and then went up to the top terrace for the sunset and the chocolate fondant which was spectacular. This place is a bit spendier than most other Moroccan restaurants but it’s definitely worth the price (and not expensive compared to most EU or US standards). The staff were super nice as well.

Name: Les Terrasses Des Arts Marrakech
Author: Matthew Giles (#Dita-Veloci)
Review:
Talk about a hidden gem. Absolutely fantastic food, service and vibe.

The staff are incredibly friendly. We opted for starters as I wanted to try multiple dishes and they were all amazing!

My missus is gluten free, when they brought out the starter bread and dips I mentioned she was gluten free, the chap disappeared and came back a few minutes later with fresh out the oven gluten free bread!

Absolutely brilliant!

Name: Les Terrasses Des Arts Marrakech
Author: Robert Witlox
Review: Perfect place to be🥰. We were surprised with a starter from the house which was great. The staff is super friendly and kind. The food we ate was very tastfull, also for the eye. After diner we got another great surprise. It was all to good to be through and we even felt a bit embarresed. Just go there... you won't regret it🍀🙏🏻

Name: Blue Ribbon Marrakech
Address: Magasin #12, 96 Rue Mohammed el Beqal, Marrakech
Author: Ishana Biju
Review:
Great place. Great service.  Wished I asked the name of the lady who attended to us.  She was great.

My “not cous cous” salad was great.  Really great meat and refreshing veggies.  The smash potato salad was perfectly textured. The crisp and soft and crunch was 🤌🏼 mwah!!!!!
The bahnmi sandwich was also really good. The pistachio cold drew was a perfect blend of flavours.
My pompom was okay. Refreshing but the only underwhelming thing we had.

Name: Blue Ribbon Marrakech
Address: Magasin #12, 96 Rue Mohammed el Beqal, Marrakech
Author: Michael G
Review:
Hidden Terrace with Farm Fresh Food .
Delightful and serene hidden cafe in bustling Guelz.
The fresh ingredients are sourced from their organic farm. Wonderful!

I enjoyed the slow cooked lamb shoulder with rosemary garlic marinade sandwich. Simply tender, full of flavors and delicious !
It came with a side kale salad with lemon yogurt dressing, very nice.

There is an outstanding bakery with deserts and pastry that will make your day!
An absolute must try!

Name: Blue Ribbon Marrakech
Address: Magasin #12, 96 Rue Mohammed el Beqal, Marrakech
Author: Ahsan Mohiuddin
Review: Nice place to be, quality food , friendly staff and great ambience, bit pricey as compare to what you can get in other similar cafes :) would highly recommend though, the coffee with pistachio milk was out of this world, you must try!!

Name: Blue Ribbon Marrakech
Address: Magasin #12, 96 Rue Mohammed el Beqal, Marrakech
Author: Hidden Marrakech
Review: 🏆TOP TIP: shaded terrace seating with plug sockets make it a perfect spot to eat while you work!  ✨ Blue Ribbon is a delightful hidden spot in Gueliz! Serving fresh juices, salads and sandwiches prepared with ingredients from their own farm. Friendly service and daily fresh muffins and artisan sourdough breads.  Whether you choose to dine on their shaded terrace or grab a takeaway, you're in for a delicious experience!

Name: Blue Ribbon Marrakech
Address: Magasin #12, 96 Rue Mohammed el Beqal, Marrakech
Author: Guillaume KEYROUZ
Review: Wonderful hidden quiet place. The staff was very friendly, cheese bagel with kale was wonderful and granola bowl was fantastic. I recommend it

Name: Chez Bismilah SAND Coffee
Author: Fabian Gysel
Review: Very friendly staff and excellent prices. I paid only 30dh for breakfast, including bread, two soups, tea, and water. There is also a rooftop with a beautiful view of the medina and the nearby Ibn Yussuf Mosque. Everything is small but tastefully decorated, and it was enjoyable to relax here. I would love to come back.

Name: Chez Bismilah SAND Coffee
Author: Ignacio Gajardo
Review: We loved the experience,  a friend that already came to Marrakech recommended these sand coffee. ☕️ it was great, tasty and the service also amazing

Name: Chez Bismilah SAND Coffee
Author: P S
Review: You will not find a friendlier, more hospitable place in Marrakesh. The coffee is excellent and the owner is the most kind-hearted man who will happily talk to you about anything in or outside of the shop. True Bedouin hospitality in the heart of the Medina!

Name: Chez Bismilah SAND Coffee
Author: Leonardo Bertolani
Review: Enchanting oasis of tranquility among the noise of the souks. Mint tea served with delicious desserts and a wonderful view from the terrace at their disposal. Really, great experience!

Name: Chez Bismilah SAND Coffee
Author: Doug Noble
Review:
Wonderful local coffee shop where coffee is made and heated in sand.
The owner is incredibly friendly and takes time to explain why the coffee is heated and made in this way.
There are a few different coffee options to choose from, and he adds a little bit of his secret spice mix to enhance the flavour.
You can enjoy the coffee either on the street or on a private terrace at the top of the shop.
Such a gem!

Name: Beldi & Juicy
Author: Mariusz Skoneczny
Review: Best Mint Tea in town. Lovely place for a little break during a walk in Medina

Name: Beldi & Juicy
Author: Rémi Marsan
Review: Very charming little terrace. Honest cuisine. Perfect for a quick stop.

Name: World Storytelling Cafe - Vegan Cafe
Author: Esme Ann
Review:
