A nice vegan spot with a range of small Moroccan tapas dishes, as well as a couple of mains. We ordered 5 tapas (zaalouk, hummus, falafel, green beans and humble beans), which was just about enough for a light lunch.

The humble beans were our favourite, the green beans and falafel were good and then the other two were just fine. The portions are pretty small! It felt a little bit pricey for the amount of food we got.

Overall, it was alright! Perhaps wouldn’t return, but was sufficient for what we needed.

Name: World Storytelling Cafe - Vegan Cafe
Author: Sophie Knapton
Review: A lovely lunch stop with great vegan options. We enjoyed a selection of starters including the falafel and hummus, a mint iced tea and a peanut butter smoothie! A really great little haven right in the middle of the markets

Name: World Storytelling Cafe - Vegan Cafe
Author: Ben Small
Review:
Fantastic experience in one of the city’s most atmospheric locations. Hidden away between the maze-like streets of the Medina, this architectural wonder stands out among the many places you can get traditional Moroccan food. They have delightful drinks, shakes and tapas-style menus items which are served by the wonderfully hospitable staff.
Break up the Souk shopping with a trip here!

Name: World Storytelling Cafe - Vegan Cafe
Author: Sarah Schäd
Review:
Lovely modern Café. We came here for some lunch and the food was delicious. Small little tapas portions.
Service was friendly and welcoming. Would recommend if in Marrakech.

Name: World Storytelling Cafe - Vegan Cafe
Author: Pascal Becker
Review:
This restaurant which is located centrally in the Medina and inside a beautiful building left us with mixed feelings. It's the only fully vegan restaurant in Marrakech which is why we went.

The food was tasty although only eight tapas are available, no real dishes, no real desserts or mains. Two of the tapas also aren't real tapas (olives and tomato "salad") which further decreases food options. What we had however was tasty!

The atmosphere was boring, no music and definitely no stories! There are some books and incomplete board games.

Service was kind and friendly but very slow. I know it's Morocco but this was a bit excessive. It takes around 15 minutes until somebody comes to take your order even though there are only three tables taken. Another half hour or more goes by before you get the food and drinks aren't brought up beforehand.

The food is nice and prices are ok (20 DH per tapa) but bring time, don't come too hungry and order everything in one go!

Name: Henna Café
Author: Emily Anderson
Review: This was around the corner from our riad. We stopped one morning for coffee/tea/just to check it out. They have an interesting creation story. The service was quick. The food and drinks were okay. I loved looking in the different rooms. The henna room was busy and is on the first floor.  The cafe is up top.

Name: Henna Café
Author: Alice Bell-Trayner
Review: My henna is beautiful!! And I had some delicious houmous too. Lovely friendly staff, and a cute tortoise too!

Name: Henna Café
Author: Kevin Lancashire
Review: We enjoyed a relaxing lunch on the terrace, savoring a small plate of falafel and salad while sipping tea.

Name: Henna Café
Author: Tiziano Biagi
Review:
A really nice local cafe with good food (vegan and vegetarian options available and marked on the menu) for reasonable prices. The falafels were probably the best I ate in Marrakech so far!

They provide support to people in need too, handing out free meals throughout the day. It is also possible to buy a “spare plate” which will allow them to provide 10 meals (it only costs 40MAD).

Definitely worth a visit!

Name: Henna Café
Author: sonali franklin
Review:
Came here for a meal with my husband.

Cute little spot run by a small group of friendly women.

We ordered the falafel, hummus, beans and bread. Was all super delicious.

Our tummies were a little unsettled that evening, not sick - and this was the only real meal we had but otherwise it was absolutely delicious!

Very very reasonable pricing too.

Name: Kremm Café
Author: Susana Zelaya Rivera
Review: Really cute and hidden cafe! The service was amazing. I ordered their toast and butter variety and it came with a lot more than toast. The orange juice was exquisite and fresh. My waiter was very polite and nice. The place is in a hidden alley but other than that it’s beautiful and very serene.

Name: Kremm Café
Author: Marlene Po
Review:
Another " accidental
" find in the medina.  As you enter, it's another world, leaving the hustle and bustle outside.  Very pleasant and elegant central courtyard.

It's for afternoon snack- so Caprese Sandwich ( fresh mozzarella, tomatoes and pesto. Very generous filling .

The Chocolate Fondant was the winner. Thick hot chocolate "cream" oozes out and coupled with vanilla ice cream,  it's heaven.

A nice place to take a break and forget about the busy-ness outside .

Name: Kremm Café
Author: Selena Hu
Review: Beautiful garden with the cafe-  a little hidden off the road, but the nicest waiters and service! We stopped by for a coffee and to just relax for a while after wandering the nearby souks. Very pretty atmosphere and quiet where you cannot hear the street noise at all!

Name: Kremm Café
Author: Sujay PS
Review: Great atmosphere and attentive and kind staff. The tea and food was amazing and clean cozy space right near to all the shops and attractions.

Name: Kremm Café
Author: Umesh Maharjan (wu_mes)
Review:
I had a lovely breakfast at Kremm Cafe recently. Despite not being guests at the hotel, we were warmly welcomed and served perfectly. The staff's hospitality made the experience even more enjoyable.

The breakfast itself was delightful, with every item prepared to perfection. The inviting atmosphere and exceptional service added to the overall charm of the cafe.

Name: MANZAL LAHBAB
Author: Mrs Stephens
Review: This place is truly wonderful, and I highly recommend it. The staff were very friendly and hospitable thank you too Noureddine and Kamel for being so accommodating. I visited with my family, and the accommodations were perfect for everyone; the kids really enjoyed themselves, and my husband and I appreciated having our own space as well. The presence of kittens and chickens was a delightful surprise for my son, who adores animals. I found the atmosphere to be relaxing and private, and the food was fantastic and traditional, which we thoroughly enjoyed. Meals were served at our convenience, and we could request tea drinks snacks whenever we liked. We decided to hire a car for the week, which made it easy to explore the area. Being only about a 20-minute drive to the city allowed us the flexibility to visit other towns as well. This has been the best holiday we've ever had, and we're excited to return in June because we loved it so much! ❤️

Name: MANZAL LAHBAB
Author: Matheus Palmeira
Review:
We started the trip to Marrakech here and for my first time in Morocco this was the perfect place to relax and recharge after all the travel. The Médina can be quite busy and crowded so having a quiet get away from that is definitely a major thing.
Our room was private, quiet and stunning! Service was incredible, our host was gentle and always present bringing us tea and answering questions! The food was divine. There was not a meal I didn’t finish! Chicken with raisins was so incredible and my favorite!
I had the opportunity to meet the owners and they made me feel like part of the family!
I hope to go back there whenever I am in Marrakech!

Name: MANZAL LAHBAB
Author: Belahcene Imad
Review: We had one of our best family holiday ever so far, and regarding it was our first time ever in Morocco it could not get any better than that, the house was great, nice and spacious for the kids with a big swimming pool the location was good, only 20mn drive from Marrakech city centre, the food and drinks was amazing 🤩 and the hosts were exceptional, Noureddine & Kamal are very hospitable people really took care of us and made us feel like home ❤️we can’t wait to see you guys again soon😍

Name: MANZAL LAHBAB
Author: Nur Azrin Binte Abdullah
Review: This place out of the city center is underrated. They are amazing! We reached Marrakesh and stayed in the old Medina, where the barrage of smoke and sounds became unbearable after a few days. We moved to Manzal Lahbab hotel and immediately found tranquility. Very very honest and sweet people working in the hotel. There is nothing too difficult even if you don't have a car (we didn't!). It is only 14 mins by car to the center. Idris, the main staff taking care of us and the place, gave us the number of a taxi driver - Hamid - who takes us everywhere. He comes even if it's a short trip. There's parking for those who  are driving. The place is full of olive trees and plants, so you get to see a mini garden every morning and splash in the pool if you like. The pool is really clean and well-maintained, and many sunbeds available. The room is spacious and very very beautifully decorated. Come and experience this hotel; it will leave you calm after the bustle of Marrakesh.

Name: MANZAL LAHBAB
Author: sylvain marie
Review:
Super nice place and super nice staff especially Idriss who took really good care of us.
It's really a quiet paradise to recover from animated Médina.
Food is super good and all staff is flexible and helpful to our different queries.

Name: Café Guerrab
Address: jemma el fna، 39 Souk Ableuh, Marrakesh
Author: Nikoleta Nižnanská
Review: Really wonderful place, tasty food, beautiful roof terrace with chill atmosphere. We had skewers and tacos and both were delicious. Recommend to try mint tea here.

Name: Café Guerrab
Address: jemma el fna، 39 Souk Ableuh, Marrakesh
Author: Weronika Turowska
Review:
We had amazing time at Café Guerrab. Great service, nice views and the atmosphere on the roof terrace.
Delicious eggplant salad, tajine and desserts. We felt looked after - very homely, family atmosphere. Very genuine Moroccan experience
A must visit in Marrakesh!
Highly recommend :)

Name: Café Guerrab
Address: jemma el fna، 39 Souk Ableuh, Marrakesh
Author: Rayane Spirit
Review: I absolutely loved this place! great atmosphere, gorgeous view. The vibe is immaculate, and the food is amazing, so worth it!

Name: Café Guerrab
Address: jemma el fna، 39 Souk Ableuh, Marrakesh
Author: Akerke Jetygenova
Review: Beautiful place! Everything is so clean, very polite staff. I recommend it 💯. There is Moroccan food and regular food, super 👏🏻 hello, from Kazakhstan

Name: Café Guerrab
Address: jemma el fna، 39 Souk Ableuh, Marrakesh
Author: Emily Brown
Review: Gorgeous rooftop terrace nestled away from the busy souks. We had the corgette salad (SO DELICIOUS!), kebab tahini (delightful), and chicken skewers (yum). Food was served quick and can tell it’s made with love. Our waiter was great and very helpful. Would definitely recommend to a friend - gorgeous flavours and chilled vibes. Reasonably priced.

Name: MFF Noria - MARRAKECH FINE FOOD
Address: Domaine de Noria, Zone Commerciale, Marrakech
Author: GSHK LTD
Review: Nice Cafe

Name: MFF Noria - MARRAKECH FINE FOOD
Address: Domaine de Noria, Zone Commerciale, Marrakech
Author: عبدالرحمن زاهد
Review:
One of the most beautiful specialty coffee cafes
Service😍
Atmosphere😍
Baked goods 😍
Work team 😍
Coffee of all kinds and shapes 🤩
Luxurious and varied gifts 😘😘

Mashallah, may God bless you 💓

Name: MFF Noria - MARRAKECH FINE FOOD
Address: Domaine de Noria, Zone Commerciale, Marrakech
Author: Kamal Hassan
Review:
Stunning
Luxury products
Golden staff
Well done

Name: MFF Noria - MARRAKECH FINE FOOD
Address: Domaine de Noria, Zone Commerciale, Marrakech
Author: Younes Msougar
Review: Excellent experience 👍quality and impeccable service 😊

Name: MFF Noria - MARRAKECH FINE FOOD
Address: Domaine de Noria, Zone Commerciale, Marrakech
Author: fouzia allel
Review: Very good products from savory to sweet

Name: Café et Patisserie Nawal
Author: Mikel Odriozola
Review: Great breakfast place. Try the eggs and pastries.

Name: Café et Patisserie Nawal
Author: Isabelle Steinmann
Review:
Authentic and delicious café!
We had a very nice breakfast over here. Service was very friendly and brought us a delicious typical morrocan breakfast when we were not really sure what to order (due to our language barriere)
Prices were great!

Name: Café et Patisserie Nawal
Author: Faiz
Review: best breakfast I got in Maroc, well priced and everything you could ask for.

Name: Café et Patisserie Nawal
Author: Andrea Cazzaniga
Review: Small place, great avocado smoothie. Coconuts biscuits also top. Definitely worth a try

Name: Café et Patisserie Nawal
Author: Anass Zaoui
Review: Excellent mango juice of incredible freshness, top-notch welcome and service, I recommend 100%

Name: Boston Coffee Restaurant Marrakech
Address: N'15, magasin, complexe El habous, Bd Allal Al Fassi, Marrakesh
Author: Nicole Adkins
Review: This place has a nice atmosphere and good food. The owner and staff are super nice and friendly, the service was excellent. All the food was great, and we highly recommend the yellow fruit smoothie bowl.

Name: Boston Coffee Restaurant Marrakech
Address: N'15, magasin, complexe El habous, Bd Allal Al Fassi, Marrakesh
Author: A Gho
Review: Amazing food! Very yummy! We had the cheese burger and the Nutella banana crepe which were amazing. Our waiter was very kind and friendly. Beautiful design and interior. 😍 We’ll come again next time we’re in marrakesh! ☺️🌺

Name: Boston Coffee Restaurant Marrakech
Address: N'15, magasin, complexe El habous, Bd Allal Al Fassi, Marrakesh
Author: Hajar Taoussi
Review: Great restaurant with a chill atmosphere. The whole experience for me was a 10/10, the Food was delicious with affordable prices. Special thanks to our lovely Server Karima. Also, it's great for studying or finishing up some work

Name: Boston Coffee Restaurant Marrakech
Address: N'15, magasin, complexe El habous, Bd Allal Al Fassi, Marrakesh
Author: Grace Lim
Review: Nice place to eat after visiting Jardin Majorelle. We had a good variety of breakfast and enjoyed it very much. Karima was friendly and helpful!

Name: Boston Coffee Restaurant Marrakech
Address: N'15, magasin, complexe El habous, Bd Allal Al Fassi, Marrakesh
Author: rayan Aichoun
Review: Greeted bt the same lovely karima and served by a very kind gentlemen named Khalid, the food once again was 10/10 the first time was so nice I had to do it twice

Name: Le Studio
Author: Denise Wang
Review: A delicious place to grab a bite after exploring the museum. It gets super busy, so there may be a bit of a wait at times. Only accepts credit cards!

Name: Le Studio
Author: Дарья Блохина
Review: Very nice cafe inside the YSL museum. Small menu but everything was quite nice. On a pricier side

Name: Le Studio
Author: Helen Ho
Review: One of the best museum restaurants I have ever been! Was extremely impressed with the quality of the food served, you can tell that the staff there have so much pride in what they make. The service staff were also brilliant, too! If you want a meal or a light bite, then don't hesitate in coming here.

Name: Le Studio
Author: J. Lepianda
Review: a lovely little cafe in the corner of the YSL museum. i love the ambience & their delicious food.

Name: Le Studio
Author: Lubna
Review: The food was amazing! I normally don’t choose salad but it was so refreshing in this hot weather and the taste was absolutely delicious.

Name: Apothicaire Tuareg
Author: G Osei
Review: Gentle owner who knows his products well, reasonable prices and puts zero pressure on his customers. I was worried about trying to haggle in the spice market after other shop owners had been pushy so this was a welcome change. Can’t recommend more!

Name: Apothicaire Tuareg
Author: Andrew Tompsett
Review: Cool experience. Went without the intent of purchasing anything but bought saffron and a dried mint tea blend. The proprietor was super friendly and chatty. Was willing to let me smell many spices. After making my purchase, I was invited to drink “Toureg” tea, which was excellent.

Name: Apothicaire Tuareg
Author: Joseph Weller
Review:
Amazing shop. Not like the other pushy/marked-up herb shops.

Really know their stuff. Nice staff.

Name: Apothicaire Tuareg
Author: Bea Alberto
Review:
Samir is really nice! He owns the place and makes the spices himself!
Highly recommend. You can really find here unique spices and teas

Name: Apothicaire Tuareg
Author: Antonio Di Stefano
Review: Thanks Abdul, very friendly, informative and seems fair

Name: MOROCCAN TEAHOUSE RESTAURANT - 1112 MARRAKECH
Address: N° 12 Place de La Kissariat Ben Youssef, Marrakesh
Author: Hayes Roberts
Review:
Incredibly beautiful restaurant / tea house.   Tea was like nothing I’ve ever had before and pastries were delicious.  After tea we received a full detailed tour and learned about tea, Moroccan culture, history and more.  And got a rooftop view of the city, all from a very knowledgeable employee who we wandered into.

I would highly recommend this place.   We thought we were just getting tea and instead had an amazing experience.

Name: MOROCCAN TEAHOUSE RESTAURANT - 1112 MARRAKECH
Address: N° 12 Place de La Kissariat Ben Youssef, Marrakesh
Author: Adam Bensalah
Review: From the teas to the pastries, and even the hospitality, everything is on point and part of a learning experience. The cozy ambiance perfectly complements the rich flavors of the offerings, but what truly sets this teashop apart is the mini museum inside. It’s a hidden gem that provides a deeper understanding of Marrakech’s cultural and historical heritage. I left not only refreshed but also enriched with knowledge about my beautiful city. A must-visit for both tea lovers and curious travelers!

Name: MOROCCAN TEAHOUSE RESTAURANT - 1112 MARRAKECH
Address: N° 12 Place de La Kissariat Ben Youssef, Marrakesh
Author: Joe Austin
Review:
We had a lovely time drinking Moroccan mint tea and coffee being served by Mohammed who was very hospitable and gave us a lesson on how the teas are made and the history of the tea house.

Would highly recommend sitting in here with a Casablanca mint tea.

Thank you so much to Mohammed and the rest of the staff for the best tea experience we’ve ever had 😌

Name: MOROCCAN TEAHOUSE RESTAURANT - 1112 MARRAKECH
Address: N° 12 Place de La Kissariat Ben Youssef, Marrakesh
Author: Dine Time
Review:
Wow what a beautiful place! We hade some tea and pastries here and must say it was the best tea we’ve tried since arriving in Morocco. We even boutique some to take home.

The staff was equally as good and when visiting here you’ll get a tour around this beautiful Riad. Definitely a must see in Marrakech.

We had to go back one more time! And I can now recommend their breakfast as well.

Name: MOROCCAN TEAHOUSE RESTAURANT - 1112 MARRAKECH
Address: N° 12 Place de La Kissariat Ben Youssef, Marrakesh
Author: thedrummer2k
Review: Beautiful spot we stumbled across, serving authentic Moroccan tea and food. Muhammad was very helpful, taking time to explain the assortments of teas and pastries available. Well worth a visit before the influencers find out about it.

Name: Le Menzeh
Author: Coral Cotto
Review: Very nice place for afternoon coffee.

Name: Le Menzeh
Author: Charly Echevin
Review:
We didn't stay in the hotel but visited the gardens and had tea at Le Menzeh cafe.
The gardens are very beautiful and can be visited for free even if you are not a hotel guest.
The mint tea was good and the setting beautiful. 70 Dh for tea per person.

Name: Le Menzeh
Author: Be Heritage
Review: Thank you MR ASSAD for the elegance of your service, I was with my clients they really appreciated the setting and the service as well as the Moroccan tea

Name: café la joie
Author: Stefanie
Review: The most delicious local coffee and tea. The owner is so nice. This isn’t just coffee or tea, it’s an experience. English speaking.

Name: café la joie
Author: R
Review:
The BEST cafe with the best cafe owner!
You MUST come here and try the drinks. I had both a tea and coffee and preferred the tea. I had a bit of sugar (I think it tastes best with at least a bit as it brings out more flavour). ☕️🍵
Also, you can get a limited selection of oil perfumes for 10 Dirham here - everywhere else you’ll need to strongly barter to get this price.

Absolutely wonderful 😋😍

Name: café la joie
Author: Tammy T
Review:
Went looking for Turkish style coffee in the Medina and google maps led me to near this cafe. The young man running the business (family business) was so lovely and engaging, wanted to offer the best coffee and best service. He invited me to video and take photos as he made the coffee in the hot sand. Was Turkish coffee with the Moroccan spices added in, very tasty. And not expensive.
He has seats in the laneway which is where I sat so I could watch everything happening around me, small area with seating edge of laneway or a small terrace which I visited and found to be very quiet so would be great if wanted to escape the hustle and bustle for bit.
He also sells spices, herbs, teas, oils etc if you interested but he did not push for me to buy anything just gave me some to smell. He then sat and chatted for little bit recommending to me places to visit around Morocco. Very relaxed and lovely visit to his small cafe, will definitely return next time I in Marrakech.

Name: café la joie
Author: Niclas W.
Review: A delightful little café in Marrakech. The coffee here is the tastiest I've ever had in the city. Their Berber tea is a true delight. The rooftop terrace is cozy and authentic, offering a genuine experience. Do take the time to visit this quaint café, it's well worth it!

Name: café la joie
Author: Sean MacDonald
Review: This is the coolest coffee stand. Najib makes super dense spiced coffee and rotates the pot in hot sand to bring out the crema in the coffee. He also serves delicious berber tea. He is super warm and will teach you about all the Moroccan spices. We returned to his stand every day and ended up meeting a lot of people just from sitting on the chairs in the pathway in front of his shop. Great prices too.

Name: Fatima Oils - Traditional & Innovative Tea, Spices & Natural Oils Shop
Address: J2C7+3V9, Rue Bahia Bab Mellah, Marrakesh
Author: Luís Duarte
Review:
One of the best and most professional herbalist shops in Marrakech.

Professional and welcoming service that stands out from other vendors. The shop is super organized and filled with lovely details made, everything provided by Fatima.

The perfect place to buy traditional tea, spices, oils, among other special things and also to learn about them.

I bought traditional tea and blue majorelle pigment as a souvenir at a fantastic and fair price.

Name: Fatima Oils - Traditional & Innovative Tea, Spices & Natural Oils Shop
Address: J2C7+3V9, Rue Bahia Bab Mellah, Marrakesh
Author: Tas Des
Review:
Wide variety.
Gud English
Not pushy.
Fair prices.
Helpful & Sweet
Will definitely recommend

Name: Fatima Oils - Traditional & Innovative Tea, Spices & Natural Oils Shop
Address: J2C7+3V9, Rue Bahia Bab Mellah, Marrakesh
Author: Deborah Hanlon
Review: Great shop with great customer care!

Name: Fatima Oils - Traditional & Innovative Tea, Spices & Natural Oils Shop
Address: J2C7+3V9, Rue Bahia Bab Mellah, Marrakesh
Author: Giulia Visentin
Review: I highly recommend this place to stock up on spices, soaps, cosmetics, oils, herbal teas, etc. Fair prices, friendly and helpful staff. New point of reference for all the times we return to Marrakech!

Name: Fatima Oils - Traditional & Innovative Tea, Spices & Natural Oils Shop
Address: J2C7+3V9, Rue Bahia Bab Mellah, Marrakesh
Author: Lucile Cano
Review:
Great experience! She made us smell many of her products and introduced us to cosmetics! It was very informative!
Very pleasant, funny and dynamic! Thank you for everything and look forward to it!

Name: TCHABA "a cup of time"
Author: Himmad Khan
Review: I have seen a few travel sites so to come here for tea but it's literally a booth in the mall. I'm sure it's a good place to buy tea but not a place to sit down for tea

Name: TCHABA "a cup of time"
Author: Kadim ELAROUSSI
Review: The area is also good, the products but the service average

Name: TCHABA "a cup of time"
Author: Driss
Review: Poor service. I used to eat this tge before, but no longer remembering the type I used before, I wanted to taste it to make the right choice. The salesperson asked me to come by the following week. Hmm

Name: TCHABA "a cup of time"
Author: ABDELJEBBAR HARTIT
Review: The quality of their teas and herbal teas is incredible

Name: TCHABA "a cup of time"
Author: Dar Khmissa Riad & Spa
Review: Magnificent selection of teas

Name: Fassila
Author: Agata Nowak
Review: I had some cookies to go, they were ok. Coffee definitely wasn’t „the best in the souk”. Ive read before good reviews about friendly staff.. unfortunately the woman working there wasn’t one of them.

Name: Fassila
Author: Sarah Spinelli
Review: Fabulous mint tea and great vibe. Loved stopping into this little spot for a break from medina shopping.

Name: Fassila
Author: Melik Babur
Review: There were no galette de sarrasin and they were not bothered to prepare. Had chicken&cheese sandwich instead which was horribly hard to chew. Try to avoid.

Name: Fassila
Author: paolo salvetti
Review: Best coffee and tea in Marrakesh. Faboulous crepes. Amazing service. Recommended!

Name: Fassila
Author: Ewi D
Review: The best! Love Mimi the kitty too.

Name: Pronto Caffè
Address: J2F8+62M, Rue Riad Zitoun el Kdim, Marrakesh
Author: Ronald Prucha
Review: Great little coffee ☕️ cafe in the middle of all the great shops. Great tasting espresso ☕️ quick and easy for a little pick me up while shopping.

Name: Pronto Caffè
Address: J2F8+62M, Rue Riad Zitoun el Kdim, Marrakesh
Author: rosleigh roberts
Review: Great little coffee shop with really good quality coffee. It was a nice surprise in a busy medina where tea is the choice of the day. Had a lovely flat white reminded me of home.

Name: Pronto Caffè
Address: J2F8+62M, Rue Riad Zitoun el Kdim, Marrakesh
Author: Perry Buchan
Review: This is the best coffee!  Get the espresso with spice!  Seriously, they have their own spice blend which works so well with the coffee. Very friendly service.

Name: Pronto Caffè
Address: J2F8+62M, Rue Riad Zitoun el Kdim, Marrakesh
Author: mari
Review: the owner was lovely and the coffee was very yummy, good price!

Name: Pronto Caffè
Address: J2F8+62M, Rue Riad Zitoun el Kdim, Marrakesh
Author: Anthony Robinson
Review: Great little cafe that serves excellent tea. Host is very welcoming

Name: café snack hamdinou
Author: EL MEHDI HAFIDI
Review: I loved the area. The tea was fresh. The atmosphere was windy and welcoming. The waiter and manager were nice to us. Recommended

Name: café snack hamdinou
Author: the best kabir
Review: Such a beautiful restaurant

Name: café snack hamdinou
Author: rabi3 el khattaby
Review: God

Name: café snack hamdinou
Author: Klaus Schuster
Review: Beautiful sun terrace, lots of space and a large TV room.

Name: café snack hamdinou
Author: Zohra Moulim
Review: Nice place. Perfect service. Very good coffee

Name: WRIGHT THÉS D'EXCEPTION
Author: Oussama Imariren
Review: Really nice place where you can get all the teas you can imagine. it is a bit expensive but I am pretty sure it has its own tea lovers and its own niche, the lady working there was very helpful and accommodating

Name: WRIGHT THÉS D'EXCEPTION
Author: Jamila Abubakar
Review: I came and the shop closed, what’s the new address

Name: WRIGHT THÉS D'EXCEPTION
Author: Peter Manshoven
Review: A moment of relaxation in a tea room with colonial decor that makes you dream of faraway places. An excellent choice of tea accompanied by good advice. Beautiful pleasant terrace for eating on site. Highly recommended for all tea lovers.

Name: Khamssa
Author: Marta Raczkowska
Review: One of the best teas from Morocco. I love it.

Name: Khamssa
Author: domi arnaud
Review: A very beautiful store with very successful product packaging!

Name: Khamssa
Author: zhor chanaoui
Review: excellent

Name: Bamal Tea
Author: N/A
Review: N/A

Name: Nina bazzar
Author: N/A
Review: N/A

Name: BL TEA
Author: Emily Hyunjoo Park
Review:
Definitely not authentic taste, cheese tastes different(like nacho cheese but in bad way, not real cheese; shouldn’t complain too much knowing we are in Morocco). Bubble tea’s tapioca pearls were hard when chewing.
However, the server was very kind. Loved her kindness!

Name: BL TEA
Author: Anisha K
Review: lovely hospitality, service and food! looks very beautiful and yummy. would definitely recommend!

Name: BL TEA
Author: Hajar Hamadi
Review: 2 stars for the friendly service. The bubble tea was not good, too sweet and A tad tasteless. The food was a tad pricey . I was an underwhelming experience and a little disappointing.

Name: BL TEA
Author: fadwa ait el hadj
Review: honestly the drink wasn't what I expected , it was super sweet and the bubbles melted instantly in my mouth and their taste was not strong I didn't like it and there's only one size for the drink, but the waiter was nice and the atmosphere just okey.

Name: BL TEA
Author: Andreia Gomes
Review:
🔝 lovely, cosy, nice food and very kind staff 5✨
Found a fresh space and ‘normal’ food in the Marrakesh caos 😊

Name: Chez Hicham
Author: Mariona Peiretó
Review:
This place is really cozy. The juices are super fresh and smoothies as well. You can also go there for crepes. We went there everyday for a healthy refreshment as our Riad was just around the corner.

The atmosphere is also very nice. There’s space to sit inside and outside and the ambience and decor is really vacay like. Plus the guy who’s serving is super quiet but very good vibe! Very caring and with a lot of attention to detail. I’d recommend it for a sweet craving or healthy snack/drink stop!

Name: Chez Hicham
Author: Catherine Hughes
Review: A lovely spot to enjoy while you’re in Marrakech. Delicious fresh fruit juices and more at this relaxing local cafe. The owner is warm and welcoming,  making it a perfect spot to take a break and watch daily life unfold in the Old Town.

Name: Chez Hicham
Author: Daniela
Review:
We had the lemon juice, the orange, and the pineapple (all individually) it was so good!! Fresh and refreshing. He makes them fresh.
We also had some crepes, and they were delicious!
We were only disappointed to find this place on our last day.
Thank you!

Name: Chez Hicham
Author: Millie Jarvis
Review:
This is a crêpe and juice stand. We had both a crêpe and juice. The juices were made fresh upon ordering and tasted amazing. We ordered one strawberry and one mango juice we visited later on our trip to to order some crepes at night. They were made fresh and tasted amazing and are very good further price that you pay.

The service was quick, and the server was very friendly and cheerful. Thank you

Name: Chez Hicham
Author: frxendly
Review:
BIG CRÊPE SMALL PRICE ⭐️
* For only 8€ - SO LESS THAN 10€ - you can get two Crêpes + two freshly squeezed juices 🥤
* Furthermore, those were perhaps the best crepes that I’ve ever tried NO CAP 🧢 (outside of France)
* Extremely kind owner and cute cat 🐈

This place is WORTH A VISIT when you visit Marrakesh ✨

Name: Café Menzeh
Author: Ritvik Jaru
Review:
Vegetarian - ordered veggie panini(grilled cheese, cooked zucchini, cooked peppers) mint tea and sparking water.
So delicious, refreshing, ambiance was totally chill.

Name: Café Menzeh
Author: Isabelly Cairo
Review:
Good view of the Jardin Secret from the top.
It was good to drink a good tea appreciating the nice view.

Name: Café Menzeh
Author: Pedro Aguiar costa Pedro costa
Review: we were seated for 20 minutes to be served while there were only about 5 tables occupied. very rude employees. an employee called adil super frowning is there and after we called a few times he ignored it on purpose

Name: Café Menzeh
Author: Mim's Sdk
Review:
Visited in October, the garden is magnificent (fairly quiet despite the tourists).
The new server is great 👍🏼

I had a perfect carrot/orange juice to regain strength after visits under the sun.
We have a 33cl bottle of water with the coffee (France should take an example).

Besides, noss noss means: half coffee and half milk.

Name: Café Menzeh
Author: olga konstantinidou
Review:
The cafe is actually inside the garden, for which you have to pay an entrance fee!

We never knew!

Name: KEIKEN - JAPANESE GREEN TEA
Author: Ouadghiri My Hafid
Review: A contemporary, welcoming place with lots of charm and good quality products.

Name: Cafe tadziwin
Address: PVM8+6FJ، إقليم تارودانت منطقة, Ctre Commune Ahl Tifnoute Assarag
Author: N/A
Review: N/A

Name: Suuuuui
Author: Musawar Saleem
Review: Very lovely staff excellent customer service and 5 star mint tea( morrocon).

Name: Suuuuui
Author: Adam 2
Review: Very disappointing experience at this cafe. The service was unacceptable, with servers completely lacking in courtesy and professionalism. Their unforgivable behavior ruined our visit. Absolutely avoid. 👎🏻👎🏻

Name: Suuuuui
Author: Adios amigos
Review: I am extremely disappointed with my experience at this cafe. The service was simply unacceptable. The waiters showed unforgivable behavior and a total lack of politeness. I absolutely do not recommend this establishment.

Name: Suuuuui
Author: NMJH gaming
Review: Unacceptable service with unforgivable and unpolite behavior from the servers.

Name: Deming coffee
Author: Greg Edwards
Review: This was my go-to morning coffee spot while in Marrakech. Friendly service, great coffee and super affordable.

Name: Abdul's spice, oil & herb shop
Author: Rav Gem
Review:
Authentic, high quality and fairly priced spices, teas, oils and more from the friendly and multilingual shopkeeper, Abdul.

My girlfriend and I stumbled across Abdul's shop (just a couple doors down from Riad Dar Amanza, situated on Derb Ennakhla street). He is from the Berber community and is a warm and welcoming man that speaks fluent Arabic, French and English.

He introduced us to different spices and teas and unlike shops in the larger more touristic souks, he did not apply a single modicum of pressure to buy anything. Instead, he will talk you through the spices, show you oils and teas, and if you so wish, you can make a purchase.

If you are looking to see an authentic shop with high quality produce sold at a fair price by a family ran business, this is the place for you!!

Enjoy this hidden gem :)

P.s. if you need a visual prompt for identifying Abdul, I uploaded a picture of him next to his sacks of spices and teas.

Name: Abdul's spice, oil & herb shop
Author: Lu Nar
Review: Great guy! Got berber tea for way cheaper than the main markets

Name: Bubble Tea Time Marrakech
Address: En face du lycée Victor Hugo, Marrakech
Author: Sugako e
Review: Part of bubble tea is to have something  that cools you down. The drinks are neither slushy or oftered with ice. Just a very bitter drink served at room temperature.  Price are so high it's easy to understand  why there is no line and all the tables empty.

Name: Bubble Tea Time Marrakech
Address: En face du lycée Victor Hugo, Marrakech
Author: Redouane Khrifi
Review: I tried this kind of bubble tea for the first time during Gitex Africa. It is by far better than the ones that use tapioca balls. These are real bubbles that burst when you bite them. The hostess they had during Gitex was really nice and helpful, she provided excellent service during the 3 days of the event.

Name: Bubble Tea Time Marrakech
Address: En face du lycée Victor Hugo, Marrakech
Author: Анастасия Цибульская
Review:
I was order 2 bubble drinks , and they make di fast dilivery to my home at 00:00 😍
It’s so delicious and welcome service ! Thank uou !

Name: Bubble Tea Time Marrakech
Address: En face du lycée Victor Hugo, Marrakech
Author: Hamid Boulahna
Review: I highly recommend bubble tea time for People Who looking for a refreshing  and tasty bubble tea experience . With great drinks, quality ingredient and a reaaaaaaaally friendly staff , it's a must visit for any bubble lover😍😍😍🤩

Name: Bubble Tea Time Marrakech
Address: En face du lycée Victor Hugo, Marrakech
Author: Salma Cherradi
Review:
The staff was friendly the place was so nice and clean
The bubble tea was delicious and sweet

Name: kardamom Marrakech
Address: 26 bis, rue de la koutoubia, Quartier fhal zefriti, Marrakesh
Author: Inbal KT
Review: Beautiful store, with high quality perfums, fresh spices, tea, café and much more... I was looking for gifts and found there everything I was looking for and more :)

Name: kardamom Marrakech
Address: 26 bis, rue de la koutoubia, Quartier fhal zefriti, Marrakesh
Author: Σπύρος Γαλίφος
Review: Over charged... Touristic trap

Name: kardamom Marrakech
Address: 26 bis, rue de la koutoubia, Quartier fhal zefriti, Marrakesh
Author: Estelle Gabaut
Review:
Radiant patroness. Extra cosmetic products. THANKS . 🧿🧿🧿🪬
I bought almonds (cinnamon/anise/coconut coated) the cosmetics shown in photos and a magnificent straw hat. I will go back there !!!

Name: kardamom Marrakech
Address: 26 bis, rue de la koutoubia, Quartier fhal zefriti, Marrakesh
Author: Nathalie Lhomme
Review: Fatima is very welcoming, smiling, caring and gives very good advice. Great little shop full of different teas, spices, perfumes, beauty treatments, candies (I fell in love with the cinnamon almonds). Not at all a tourist trap, she talks about her products with pleasure and passion. We hope to return to Marrakech soon to stock up on little treasures from home again.

Name: kardamom Marrakech
Address: 26 bis, rue de la koutoubia, Quartier fhal zefriti, Marrakesh
Author: Vilina VOUKSTA
Review: Magnificent boutique that we discovered by chance during a walk in Marrakech. The manager is adorable and has decorated her shop with great taste. All products are of high quality! We had a very pleasant time! During our next stay, we will definitely return! Thanks again! Good luck ❤️

Name: Dibo
Author: Comrade Zach
Review:
The service is great, they have next to everything you'd want, and all the employees are very nice and fun to chat with.
I often look forward to going there just for the small talk.

Name: Dibo
Author: Koom Martial Arts Retreat Center Marrakech
Review: Clean and attached to a meat seller and a beautiful mosq.

Name: Dibo
Author: Rachid Ait Barhich
Review: Nice store very helpful stuff

Name: Dibo
Author: Naoufal Immobilier
Review: Bosn services

Name: Dibo
Author: Mustapha AIT ALLA (Mufasa)
Review: Clean and well deserved

Name: Bel Dej
Author: ALi Ouadihi
Review: Good service from the manager himself and delicious food ❤️

Name: Bel Dej
Author: Taha Traiki
Review:
A friendly café

This sleekly designed café offers good breakfasts every morning. The warm welcome from the owner and the choice between tea, pressed juice and coffee to start the day well are attractive.

My choice fell on a beldi breakfast, made with creamy semolina and an omelette flavored with oregano, all accompanied by a delicious mint tea. A real taste success!

Both affordable and delicious, the dishes make you want to continue the comforting discussions around a creamy espresso. Excellent value for money for snacks with friends and family. To recommend without hesitation!

Name: Bel Dej
Author: Jihane Khounfi
Review: A warm welcome, excellent service with a very varied menu and very, very good breakfasts at low prices. I recommend without hesitation.

Name: Bel Dej
Author: saad labdaoui
Review: I had the pleasure of discovering this café in Marrakech, and I must say that I was totally won over. The warm and friendly atmosphere immediately charmed me, and the service was simply impeccable. But what really impressed me was the exceptional quality of the breakfasts offered, undoubtedly offering the best value for money in the city. A must-visit address for those looking for an authentic culinary experience in Marrakech.

Name: Bel Dej
Author: Reda Zid
Review: Excellent coffee, great atmosphere, I recommend it 👍

Name: M4nature
Author: Latifa Ryahi
Review: High quality products. I wish you every success in your journey.

Name: M4nature
Author: ELAF CONSULT
Review: Very good quality

Name: Moustafa’s Street Food
Author: Alan Alpenfelt
Review:
This is a very very simple street food place but Moustafa’s lentil soup with carrots is AMAZINGLY good, better than any restaurants I’ve been to.
Definitely check it out if you are in the area and enjoy the sugary mint tea. You sit down and watch the passers by, feeling time stopping for a moment.
Moustafa kindly offered me a spoon but…
I always love to eat with my hands to have that connection with the food and smell the taint of spices on my fingers after having washed my hands.
I was full up and happy for the day, ready to delve into bargaining!
(I wrote “coffee shop” because google doesn’t give the street food option. You’ll find mint instead).

Name: Moustafa’s Street Food
Author: Tommaso Giacopini
Review:
Come here if you want a real Moroccan experience! Street food at its best 👌
Moustafa is humble, generous and kind and his food is bery authentic and a real pleasure for the palate. Great food at local prices. Definitely worth grabbing a bite here!

Name: Riad Yima Boutique & Art Gallery
Address: 52 derb Aarjane Rahba lakdima Medina, Marrakech, Marrakesh
Author: Mohit Gulati
Review:
Nice cozy place, just a short walk from the medina center but very peaceful. We came across zero tourists.

Nice indoor and terrace space where we enjoyed the Moroccon tea, served by the generous Haitam.

Name: Riad Yima Boutique & Art Gallery
Address: 52 derb Aarjane Rahba lakdima Medina, Marrakech, Marrakesh
Author: Ella Christella
Review: Interesting photography, clothes, shoes, lamps and teas designed by Hassan Hajjaj, a Moroccan contemporary artist who lives in London. I would have loved to buy one of his large photographs and lamps if I had a car to transport them in. I'm happy to have discovered him. Thank you for offering this space.

Name: Riad Yima Boutique & Art Gallery
Address: 52 derb Aarjane Rahba lakdima Medina, Marrakech, Marrakesh
Author: Nadav David
Review: What a wonderful hidden gem in the Madina! Beautiful and interesting art on three floors and the terrace. Very welcoming and friendly host. Highly recommended!

Name: Riad Yima Boutique & Art Gallery
Address: 52 derb Aarjane Rahba lakdima Medina, Marrakech, Marrakesh
Author: C Charritte
Review:
Really cool spot.

Riad, art gallery and tearoom :)
You can admired Hassan Hajjaj work while enjoying this quiet space close by the spices square.
The pop art vibe would definitely give you a good energy.

It's not easy to find but you just have to follow the sign!

Enjoy!

Name: Riad Yima Boutique & Art Gallery
Address: 52 derb Aarjane Rahba lakdima Medina, Marrakech, Marrakesh
Author: Linda M
Review: It is quite the experience to hunt down the riad to visit the retail outlet of YIMA (Google maps is incorrect. The alleyway entrance is opposite Riad Tahyra) Although the prices are high, the items are unique.

Name: Stop Coffee
Author: Zakaria Ouaada
Review: Best espresso/coffee in town! This is a genuine family run business built for coffee lovers. Don’t be fooled by it small space, This is not like all those tourist traps in Marrakech that serves you black water and call it coffee. This is authentic italian style coffee shop where you will enjoy your coffee to the last sip.

Name: Stop Coffee
Author: smaisma sc
Review: There is no price list

Name: Villa Majorelle
Author: Phoebe Thomas
Review: Went to this cafe on 11th October 2024 .Most rudest service provided by this waiter . He was rude to my partner when we asked change of table . We have to get our own menu . He only took our order . Got our receipt mixed up . Hence I decided not to tip him and he followed me and tried to embarrass me in front of the other customers for not tipping him . I did have to speak back to him . Don’t tip unless he offers you a service . The other staff members were friendly and greeted with a smile . Food was okay . But the whole experience was let down my how we were treated by the WAITER .

Name: Villa Majorelle
Author: Juan Joseph
Review: The food took more than 30 minutes to arrive after placing the order. While the food was decent, it was very overpriced for a cafe. The service was slow and a tall waiter wearing a hat demanded a tip while we were paying via card. He was rude and was insisting on tipping him even when we said we didn’t have any cash available. He went on to say that he saw money in our wallet and kept muttering behind us while we walked out of the restaurant afterwards !!! Extremely rude and unprofessional behaviour!!

Name: Villa Majorelle
Author: C B
Review:
Would not recommend at all!!!
We ordered bottled drinks, which took almost 30 minutes to arrive. On top of that, the waiter had the audacity to demand a very high tip despite the poor service. Overall, a disappointing experience and felt like a scam.

Name: Villa Majorelle
Author: C K
Review:
It’s a pretty place.
Staffs are very nice but they are just so so so busy… no time for ordering and no time for check 🫠 …management’s problem

Name: Villa Majorelle
Author: Anna Moser
Review:
Very long waiting time, it took us 2 hours to have lunch. Food is good, although they didn’t have the dessert we asked.
Since it’s clearly a touristic place everything is overpriced.
However, if you’re not in a rush it’s a beautiful place for lunch after visiting museum.

Name: Terra mia café
Author: Gabi Alves Photography
Review:
The best cafe in Marrakech!!

The food is so tasty and the customer service is amazing!!! During my trip, I went to Terra Mia more the 4 times

Name: Terra mia café
Author: Urwin Monsanto
Review:
This place is amazing, it has a very nice clean and modern look. feel that u are in Morocco, breakfast, lunch or even for a nice hot drink with a slice of pastries that tastes like heaven. The plates are well presented and the food is fresh snd home made and delicious💯!!!! The service is perfect, great staff, good communication, always friendly!
The owner is always there with a big smile and is super friendly, super social really love this guy! I definitely recommend it to friends and family if they wanna have a place to relax and eat. For me the best place in Marrakech

Name: Terra mia café
Author: Hanan B
Review: I love this spot. Always consistent excellence in service, yummy healthy variety of food, drinks, pastry and viennoiserie. Today in particular I was greeted by Nadia who had a huge smile on her face and I needed assistance with my tables arrangement and she ran to assist all while keeping a positive attitude and was super helpful. It really makes a difference. The music is beautiful adding to the lovely atmosphere and just enough to enjoy without being loud to ruin conversations. The decor everything about this place is always tip top. I feel like leaving a review every time I visit because everyone is a great experience. I hope it keeps being at this level because I love cafés that care about details and keep me coming back.

Name: Terra mia café
Author: Anthony Acosta
Review: As someone who lives in Brussels with some fantastic French boulangerie this place is doing it right! My French friends who live in the area also agree. The melted chocolate croissant is like a lucious pain chocolat

Name: Terra mia café
Author: Rupert Holden
Review: Very delicious pastry, the kind you would expect in an upmarket French patisserie, and the best I’ve had in Marrakech after years of visiting from France. The chocolate tart and entremets noisette were highlights! A relaxed and carefully decorated place with a shaded terrace to take a coffee (including lactose free options !)

Name: Coffee Mbehja
Author: Laurent Demeneix
Review:
Really small coffee pit stop but best value found in Marrakech and of good quality.
Became my morning routine and loved their almond espresso.
To note: no milk alternative available. Only 🐮 🐄
And no table, just drink it at the counter or on the go.

Name: Coffee Mbehja
Author: Steven Silverstein
Review: Coffee Mbehja makes truly remarkable espresso!  Best that I've had in many years, including shops all through Italy, Spain, Portugal, and the USA.  Smooth, very full bodied, and perfect crema.  This small friendly family owned shop is located just inside the Sidi Mimoune Gate.  Their coffee might be the reason I relocate permanently to Marrakesh :)

Name: Coffee Mbehja
Author: J. Devos
Review:
Cheap as can be (7dh), great quality, always kind, lots of care into each order.

Small but great coffees.

Name: Coffee Mbehja
Author: Małgorzata Poniatowska
Review: Very nice place, much more better than any coffee shop in city center.

Name: Coffee Mbehja
Author: Sam C
Review: Amazing espresso with great flavours and friendly service!

Name: Le Menara Cafe
Author: Matt VanderPloeg
Review: Veg pastilla, falafel sandwich and chicken sandwich all delicious. Inexpensive especially for the area. With regards to the other reviews the washroom had soap and paper. Slow service for the chicken sandwich, took 20 mins so 4 star.

Name: Le Menara Cafe
Author: Jester
Review:
shortly; like it.

food a part of trying interesting and not same boring other coffees here.

mean: menu short but super. service 1a, mean top. chatting with kitchen, service girl and "boss of service".

outside or in front never quite or boring. it's action for eyes and ears.
price really to accept

thx.

Name: Le Menara Cafe
Author: Vasanth Nag K V
Review: Opposite to Bahia palace, a calm place in the corner. The owner is super nice. We had breakfast. Eggs, bread, coffee and juice, price is very affordable compared to other places here. I was surprised that this place has such less reviews given the quality and affordability of the menu. Do visit here for a quick breakfast when you visit Bahia palace in Marrakesh! Spent around 9 euros for 2 people.

Name: Le Menara Cafe
Author: Thlockman
Review: Great food, prices not had for touristy location.  The couscous in particular was a highlight

Name: Le Menara Cafe
Author: Isabelly Cairo
Review:
The omelet naturale was delicious, homemade and honest price.
A good place to eat beside of the Bahia Palace.

Name: HENNA LOUAYA
Address: Chez Riad Louaya, 24 Derb Halfaoui, Marrakech
Author: Joanne Hong
Review:
Fast and amazing service!

I was 10 minutes late for an appointment I made via email but they still greeted me warmly with complimentary tea. The riad was kind of tucked away from the main street (the sign won’t say Henna Louaya) so watch out for that.

The courtyard in the Riad is beautiful!. I had to stay there ~30 minutes after the treatment and just sitting in the waiting area was relaxing in itself. Once I arrived they gave me a book of designs I can choose from. Some folks with me chose their design before arriving.

We were initially worried because the henna was giving us a slight cooling and tingly sensation. The henna seemed genuine (reviews, colour was brown). It was confusing because it couldn’t have given all of us allergic reactions. Thankfully the sensation went away after few minutes. We were told not to take off the henna / wash hands for 3 hours. After 8 hours ish I scraped them off. I’ve attached photos before and after 2 days.

The whole thing cost 120dh. They only take cash but also can take euros if you have any.

Overall satisfied with the service!

Name: HENNA LOUAYA
Address: Chez Riad Louaya, 24 Derb Halfaoui, Marrakech
Author: Miona Kikanovic
Review: Henna Louaya provides an exceptional henna art experience. Their professionalism shines through, delivering stunning henna designs. Riad's hospitality adds to the ambiance, offering mint tea and water upon arrival. The plethora of henna design options ensures everyone finds their perfect match. The environment exudes cleanliness and a serene energy, perfect for a relaxing session. For top-notch henna art, look no further than Henna Louaya.

Name: HENNA LOUAYA
Address: Chez Riad Louaya, 24 Derb Halfaoui, Marrakech
Author: Ahsan Khan
Review: We looked for reviews of the top Henna artists in Marrakesh. We were suggested Henna Louaya, and it definitely lived up to the recommendation. We was greeted with mint tea and given various henna designs to choose from. The henna quality was excellent, and the riad environment was delightful. Excellent value for money. Highly recommend it.

Name: HENNA LOUAYA
Address: Chez Riad Louaya, 24 Derb Halfaoui, Marrakech
Author: Crystal C
Review: You can find this place using walking directions with google. And you should visit if you want henna. The Henna is made with natural ingredients and was done by a women who’s been learning henna since her teens. She was so sweet and the atmosphere was calm. You also get mint tea and water. I really enjoy my time and the prices online are fairly accurate to what you’ll pay but the final price is based on the design itself.

Name: HENNA LOUAYA
Address: Chez Riad Louaya, 24 Derb Halfaoui, Marrakech
Author: Bianca Sims
Review: Hidden away at the end of an alley in a Riad. A lovely group of ladies are waiting to do the most beautiful Henna for you. I was welcomed and served tea while having my Henna done. For the one I chose in my photo it was €20. Price ranges from €5+. Come here for your Henna, do not go to J’ma Elfna square!

Name: Le boulevard l 1er
Author: michelle lamalmi
Review: Only had coffee.   Love love loved our waiter.  Friendly but professional and attentive.

Name: Le boulevard l 1er
Author: Walid Sennoun
Review: Fantastic atmosphere gentle and respectful staf amazing coffee and tea

Name: Le boulevard l 1er
Author: hicham hicham
Review: Super 🤩

Name: Le boulevard l 1er
Author: Kaukabi
Review:
Clean and well located cafe.
Waitresses a little sluggish and not too responsive.
I recommend their excellent COUSCOUS every Friday lunchtime for 4.5 euros per person.
A real treat.
A must see

Name: Le boulevard l 1er
Author: Elodie Dalli
Review:
Good setting, attentive waiter, affordable price.
The juices are very good (orange, strawberry, orange carrots)
The downside was the pasta which had an aftertaste probably due to the sauce (seafood pasta, turkey dish and minced meat dish)

Name: Le Jardin De Mohktar
Author: Alper Babayoglu
Review:
Nice and calm place. It is suitable for children.
Tea is extraordinary delicious and the fruit juice is extremely tasty. Small but definitely you must take into your list.

Name: Le Jardin De Mohktar
Author: Ayoub M
Review: An exceptional little farm, managed by lovely people! A magical place. Thank you if Mokhtar 🙂

Name: Le Jardin De Mohktar
Author: docteur meubles
Review:
Always a great welcome from Mokhtar and his team.
The herbal infusions, the tagines, the Friday couscous... everything is delicious. There is even a small market on site with fruit and vegetables from the garden.
We really enjoyed being able to play pétanque in Marrakech.

Name: Le Jardin De Mohktar
Author: SOUAD MAAD
Review: A nice family evening. The place deserves to be restored.

Name: Le Jardin De Mohktar
Author: Daniel Bonet
Review: An unusual place, he grows these vegetables himself using the ancestral method. We ate an assortment of vegetables followed by a predominantly vegetable tagine, each more succulent than the other.

Name: STAYLIA
Author: Cameron Mills
Review: I got a flat white from here. The barista was very friendly, and the coffee was very good!

Name: Beldi & Juicy
Author: Mariusz Skoneczny
Review: Best Mint Tea in town. Lovely place for a little break during a walk in Medina

Name: Beldi & Juicy
Author: Rémi Marsan
Review: Very charming little terrace. Honest cuisine. Perfect for a quick stop.

Name: Ste Imperiale Des Thes Et Infusions Sa
Address: 89 Bis Lot Azli, Quartier Industriel, Marrakech
Author: N/A
Review: N/A

Name: Bacha Coffee
Address: Dar el Bacha, Rte Sidi Abdelaziz, Marrakech
Author: H Nakamura
Review:
Highly recommended!! Really beautiful place and a really perfect service! It was impressive!
We went for coffee and desert after lunch, it was a great idea!
They have way too many variety of coffee type, we got lost though… better to ask advice or do your research in advance, unless you are an expert and know what you want

Name: Bacha Coffee
Address: Dar el Bacha, Rte Sidi Abdelaziz, Marrakech
Author: Denise Wang
Review:
Beautiful space for a coffee or a meal. As other people have mentioned, there is a wait (we waited about an hour), however it gives you an opportunity to explore the beautiful museum. If you have some free time, I’d recommend going, however between the wait and the meal, you’ll need to allocate about three hours.

To get into the museum (you need to pay entry to get to the cafe), it’s 60DH per person (cash only). The cafe itself takes credit card.

Name: Bacha Coffee
Address: Dar el Bacha, Rte Sidi Abdelaziz, Marrakech
Author: Liezel San Andres
Review: The waiting time at the restaurant was about an hour and a half, with service being incredibly slow—taking 30 minutes just to receive the menu, another 30 to 45 minutes for the food to arrive, and nearly an hour to finally get the bill to think we only ordered two coffees and two slices of cake—yet the marble cake was so hard it was almost inedible, though the ‘Pastry of the Week’ cappuccino was decent. The ambiance was pleasant, but the service was truly awful and price was so expensive. On the bright side, they offer a wide range of coffee options, which I appreciated, and the iced coffee was enjoyable. I also bought coffee beans from the shop, which turned out to be the highlight of the experience—they were gorgeous when brewed at home in my coffee machine. Unfortunately, that was the only truly positive aspect of the visit. It is an experience but I would never dine again.

Name: Bacha Coffee
Address: Dar el Bacha, Rte Sidi Abdelaziz, Marrakech
Author: Eli Haugen
Review:
The coffee, food, and service were so good that I visited three times during my week in Marrakech. The wait time is absolutely worth it if you pay the little bit extra to explore the museum and make friends with the other people waiting (shout out to the lovely lady from Holland ♥️). I loved learning about the history of the building and of the coffee shop as well.

Don’t miss trying the homemade cream and fresh vanilla shavings in your coffee - after you enjoy it black (as they helpfully suggest). I’m a particular fan of the Yemeni coffee, and my friend enjoyed the Jamaican Blue Mountain coffee.

Name: Bacha Coffee
Address: Dar el Bacha, Rte Sidi Abdelaziz, Marrakech
Author: Julia Hope
Review: Arrived around 10:40 and seated at 12:30- I recommend going straight to the cafe from the museum so they can get your name down on the list then you have time to explore- well worth the wait! Loved all the coffee choices and the pastries were amazing- we got the cheesecake and tiramisu!

Name: Kosy Kawa
Author: Oleg Shestakov
Review: This place is great for anyone looking for specialty coffee in Marrakesh. It has a beautiful ambiance, featuring local art, and offers a good selection of coffee beans for filter brewing. The barista knows what he's doing and can provide helpful recommendations for other specialty coffee spots around Marrakesh. It's truly an oasis of specialty coffee in a desert of 'regular' coffee.

Name: Kosy Kawa
Author: José Reis Santos
Review:
A truly coffee place. The options, flavors and combos are crafted carefully by Muhammad, our host.
One of the best cappuccinos I had. Globally. Very very nice place, with several cosy rooms and super friendly staff. Amazing view on the rooftop over the square and part of the city.
Perfect for a short stop while strolling the city.

Important note: I managed to pay with Revolut.
Important note 2: several milk options.

Name: Kosy Kawa
Author: smiddy550
Review: Great wee spot to enjoy a latte coffee overlooking the square which is not like the other crazy square. A coffee for 25 dirhams and enjoy the lovely clean square where shopkeepers are nice to their cats and feed them. All very friendly. Very clean toilets upstairs. Miriam our waitress is lovely. Speaks great English. Friendly and engaging. Highly recommend. Geoff

Name: Kosy Kawa
Author: Dave Conroy
Review: Broadly third wave coffee shop stopover when in between the palaces and seeking a break. Located in the corner of the square. Very pleasant people offering great service. The upstairs is a bonus if you like to watch the world go by.

Name: Kosy Kawa
Author: David Murphy
Review:
A oasis in a desert.
AMAZING coffee served in a modern and delicious style. The choice of brew method- machine, V60, Aeropress, Cold Brew. A choice of beans from all the over the world. The knowledge of Mohammed about how to extract the best from the beans made for a fantastic coffee.
If you are in Marrakesh and you enjoy outstanding coffee then this is the place.

Name: Thirty5ive Marrakech
Author: Oanh
Review: This hip and laid-back modern coffee shop is a great spot to relax. You can unwind downstairs or upstairs with your coffee or smoothie of choice while enjoying cool music from a record player.

Name: Thirty5ive Marrakech
Author: Emily
Review: This cafe is a gem in Marrakech! It’s the best coffee I’ve had in the city. It has a nice minimalist design and the barista is skilled and friendly. I would have happily worked at this cafe had I discovered it sooner. The cinnamon honey matcha latte with soy milk is so good!

Name: Thirty5ive Marrakech
Author: Mònica Noviembre
Review: Great place in terms of ambience and good vibes. Also, high quality coffee. Very recommended!

Name: Thirty5ive Marrakech
Author: Dalida Spenser
Review: Beautiful and bohemian place, quiet luxury with elegant products, to escape classic places, you most visit this minimalistic  designed cafe with well researched music repertoire. Well done !

Name: Thirty5ive Marrakech
Author: Emils U
Review: Wow! What a space. Beautiful vinyl music, amazing specialty coffee and a fantastic pottery space. Far outdid any expectations we had. Fantastic service to add!

Name: Café Hexagone
Author: ZAKARIA ATEL
Review: The waiters literally don't care, they take your orders and make you wait for hours, and if you question it, they just ignore you. I don't recommend it at all, not family friendly, and diffently not for kids.

Name: Café Hexagone
Author: Salty B
Review: Service zero. mo3amala zero. 9hwa zero.

Name: Café Hexagone
Author: AYOUB YOUSRI
Review: Good cafe good service

Name: Café Hexagone
Author: Khalid Essayf
Review: Cafe

Name: Café Hexagone
Author: Abdelkarim Hakam
Review: Slt

Name: Le dernier chameau
Address: route lagza, Collège Mohamed V 126 derb, zemmouri route lagza, zemmouri, 126 Derb Abdelhamid, Marrakech
Author: Riccardo Capuano
Review: The place is warm and the host is very nice! Unfortunately we waited almost an hour for a simple breakfast. The tea was good. The crepes with honey and cheese were okay, tu cakes were not so good.

Name: Le dernier chameau
Address: route lagza, Collège Mohamed V 126 derb, zemmouri route lagza, zemmouri, 126 Derb Abdelhamid, Marrakech
Author: Daniel Richardson
Review: Great coffee and patisserie cafe, located right near College Mohamed 5 which is a pick up point for most tours in Marrakech. I would recommend stopping here if you arrive early for your tour.

Name: Le dernier chameau
Address: route lagza, Collège Mohamed V 126 derb, zemmouri route lagza, zemmouri, 126 Derb Abdelhamid, Marrakech
Author: Lois King
Review: Beautiful location staff are willing to go above and beyond.Traditional Moroccan breakfast is amazing!

Name: Le dernier chameau
Address: route lagza, Collège Mohamed V 126 derb, zemmouri route lagza, zemmouri, 126 Derb Abdelhamid, Marrakech
Author: Ross Hudson
Review: Very nice traditional breakfast and very reasonably priced. 🙂

Name: Le dernier chameau
Address: route lagza, Collège Mohamed V 126 derb, zemmouri route lagza, zemmouri, 126 Derb Abdelhamid, Marrakech
Author: Kristin Kaimer
Review: Really good intensive coffee from Nespresso capsules. Perfect serving, clean and cool place. High quality service. 75dirham for 2 coffees and cake :)

Name: Oliva mar cafe
Author: Arun
Review:
The mrrakchi and gourmet were super tasty.
reasonably priced, good service.
Obviously, I don't mind the noise of the traffic if the food is yum yum!! I would recommend trying their Moroccan recipes + tea and orange juice.

Name: Oliva mar cafe
Author: Brian C
Review: Amazing service and food! Service is amazing and food is super fresh. Great value for cost! Look at my breakfast mmmmmmm.

Name: HESPERIS Coffee Factory
Author: David Hatch
Review:
Delightfully surprised. Did not expect to find such an upscale restaurant in Sidi Ghanem. Prices are a bit high for the area, but good value and we'll worth it. Very few places in all of Marrakech have service as good as here at HESPERIS. I would give the service 6-Stars if I could. Very well trained, polite, and respectful. The artwork is also enjoyable as well. Coffee was well done presented well.
Great choice to try!

Name: HESPERIS Coffee Factory
Author: Abraham Terpstra
Review: What an inspiring place! Hesperis serves specialty coffee that they roast themselves. I had the Ethiopian Giju G1. Floral and alive. Very friendly and helpful staff, we had the opportunity to see the roaster in the basement. I love the place and how they thought of every little detail in the interior (the terrace floors are beautiful).

Name: HESPERIS Coffee Factory
Author: H Ouk
Review:
Was absolutely clean and relaxing place
I like how the staff are smiling in front of anyone.

Name: HESPERIS Coffee Factory
Author: Julia Onorato
Review: Absolutely delicious food, amazing ambiance and the coffee was out of this work. The pastries and sweets were spectacular, and so fresh they must have been handmade that morning. The eggs were also light and fluffy - this was one of those breakfasts where you wish you could eat more because everything was so good. Would definitely recommend to my friends and will be coming back again soon!

Name: HESPERIS Coffee Factory
Author: Emily Byrski
Review: One of the best coffee shops I visited in Marrakech! The coffee and breakfast were delicious, the space was bright and airy, and the staff and owner were so hospitable and friendly! It was truly a joy and I’d recommend this spot to all of my friends! :)

Name: Pastry Gato, Marrakech, Morocco
Address: Rue Hassania, 2 pas de la clinique Chifa, et à Côté de l'école du Sud, Marrakech
Author: Pelin
Review: I've never tasted Moroccan sweets this delicious before. They are incredibly addictive and flavorfull. It's made from real ingredients like almonds, honey, and rose oil so they are healthy and also very Tastyyy. Huge thanks to Gato for creating such delightful sweets!!

Name: Pastry Gato, Marrakech, Morocco
Address: Rue Hassania, 2 pas de la clinique Chifa, et à Côté de l'école du Sud, Marrakech
Author: MARK MATTHEWS
Review: Take your time to google and find this place if you love quality Moroccan 🇲🇦 Pastries if you have had them before you will know there not cheep but worth every penny I bought half a kilo about £10 but there excellent 👍

Name: Pastry Gato, Marrakech, Morocco
Address: Rue Hassania, 2 pas de la clinique Chifa, et à Côté de l'école du Sud, Marrakech
Author: Alice Corolleur
Review: The pastries here are incredible! A special mention for the gazelle horns, which are absolutely excellent. Highly recommended for anyone looking to enjoy authentic Moroccan treats!

Name: Pastry Gato, Marrakech, Morocco
Address: Rue Hassania, 2 pas de la clinique Chifa, et à Côté de l'école du Sud, Marrakech
Author: Shaahid I
Review: Traditional Moroccan pastries made by this family business. Awesome taste and presentation. Pleasant service from the owner. One slight complaint is that the coffee served was a Nespresso capsule. A tea house should really have a proper coffee prep method. Absolutely worth a visit or take away.

Name: Pastry Gato, Marrakech, Morocco
Address: Rue Hassania, 2 pas de la clinique Chifa, et à Côté de l'école du Sud, Marrakech
Author: Nadia Bibi Sarwar
Review:
Sellou a dessert, roasted flour mixed with butter, sugar, cinnamon, almonds (or sometimes peanuts), and other ingredients is super yum.
Sfenj is a doughnut sprinkled with sugar or soaked in honey. Every pasty is distinctive and its only enough crust to hold the filing in a bite size.
Don’t forget to try them when you are in Morocco.

#Morocco #Food #SoloTraveler #Speaker #Gato #Patisserie #SehatKarwan #SDG03 #GoodHealthAndWellBeing

Name: 101 CAFFE'
Author: Hidden Marrakech
Review:
🏆 Good coffee, delightful sitting on their terrace …and they also sell beans!

101 Caffe is a perfect stop when in Gueliz. Situated on the back streets behind Carre Eden, this local cafe has beans from around the world and an outdoor terrace where you can watch the world pass by as you sip!

Name: 101 CAFFE'
Author: Nizar Hakiki
Review: Best coffeeshop in town, I highly recommend it.

Name: 101 CAFFE'
Author: U C
Review: Worst coffee. She had no idea how to make a coffee at all. I don’t think, that all the other reviews are real.

Name: 101 CAFFE'
Author: Jim
Review: Nice little place to have a nice coffee. Very polite and helpful.

Name: 101 CAFFE'
Author: Kastyel Sinleth
Review: Great ambience, and the two gentlemen working there are very friendly and make you feel at ease. I've been there multiple times for my favorite coffee.

Name: Besties Bakery
Author: khaoula guedad
Review:
Amazing service, Nice food and the places is all pink like a barbie house, I loved it 😍  it was one of the best places i have visited ♥️♥️.
The name of the person who served us is Amin he was nice, gentel, smiling all the time and very helpful 💖 .
After that the manager his name is Soufiane he was also amazing, humble and very helpfull 💖💖💖 I will visit there again and again for sure . Because of their service I am doing my birthday there soon  . Thank you all for this experience 🙏.

Name: Besties Bakery
Author: Hajar Hamadi
Review: I'm giving this 2 stars for the friendly service. I was disappointed with my red velvet cookie. It was a bit overcooked to my liking. I probably wouldn't go back anytime soon.

Name: Besties Bakery
Author: VOYAGEZ AU MAROC
Review: Besties Bakery in Marrakech is truly a gem! The bakery offers a delightful range of delicious treats that are sure to satisfy any sweet tooth.

Name: Besties Bakery
Author: Zafizuddin Zainuddin
Review: I had the pleasure of coming here a couple of days ago. The food was great and the service was amazing. I would like to commend Amine because he catered to our every need. As a Singaporean who can’t speak the Moroccan language, he did very well in conveying his words in English. Kudos! Highly recommended.

Name: Besties Bakery
Author: Jihane Barehmi
Review:
Quality of the cakes has to be improved. I took 4 different cakes was impressed with none of them. They did not taste fresh at all
The price charged exceeds the expectation of the quality delivered
Cupcake Oreo that did not taste like Oreo it more seemed like génoise. The red velvet brownie too heavy and no taste , the red velvet cake was more brown than red
The mango cupcake was okay taste wise
Definitely review the quality

Name: Salon de Thé
Author: Jun
Review:
Got scammed, should avoid.
Order a couple glasses of beer, the beer was not on the menu, but we thought it wouldnt  be too expensive. When the bill came, it was actually 70 freaking Dirham per glass. Double or perhaps triple the local price. Other dishes taste fine, but small portioned.
Ask before order, otherwise avoid at all cost..

Name: Salon de Thé
Author: Cécile Prin
Review: We stop here by chance, to eat and drink tea. We ask what the pastries are, unable to get an answer from the waiter. To all the questions we ask he answers with a “yes”. We then suggest going down to choose, he says no and offers to bring a tray so that we can choose. Summary of the shopping: the Berber sandwich is not served, ''because it's too late'', the requested coke is replaced by a pepsi, we waited a long time for three skewers, and the chef's surprise: we have to pay for the entire tray of pastries because we “touched” it. In short, a lousy technique to put us against the wall, no commercial gesture made, and a server who shone with his bad faith. Run away

Name: Salon de Thé
Author: EL MAJDOUBI Mimoun
Review: The best place you and your family can go to

Name: kartell kollektiv.
Author: Karl Roberts
Review:
Excellent coffee, great staff.

Iced latte and cortado were great.
Ethiopian pour was delicious.

Chill vibe, here.

5*

Name: kartell kollektiv.
Author: Lumi
Review:
This place is amazing! It’s such an artsy and bohemian atmosphere where one immediately feels at ease. Everyone there is so lovely and the drinks and baked goods are so homey and delicious.
Definitely recommend!

Name: kartell kollektiv.
Author: Manale Bourarach
Review:
If I could rate this gem spot 6/5 , but here we go, now you know how precious and tasty it is to pass by kartell kollektiv. once in Marrakech!

Don’t miss its coffee and drinks made with delicacy, the events and atmosphere organized with dedication. Rather you are searching for a spot to work at, or to meet friends or just to harvest love in a cup and cookie between the cats… but mostly don’t miss it for its people! ❤️

Name: kartell kollektiv.
Author: Wesley Go
Review: The worst filter coffee I have had in a while. I asked the barista for a fruity blend, he gave me the floral one. But it turns out to be very bitter, pungent with an unpleasant aftertaste, probably because of sheer over-extraction.

Name: kartell kollektiv.
Author: Cornelia Hänchen
Review: It’s my favourite coffee in Marrakesh. The food is great, the coffee tastes wonderfully and the atmosphere is lovely.

Name: Café et Patisserie Nawal
Author: Mikel Odriozola
Review: Great breakfast place. Try the eggs and pastries.

Name: Café et Patisserie Nawal
Author: Isabelle Steinmann
Review:
Authentic and delicious café!
We had a very nice breakfast over here. Service was very friendly and brought us a delicious typical morrocan breakfast when we were not really sure what to order (due to our language barriere)
Prices were great!

Name: Café et Patisserie Nawal
Author: Faiz
Review: best breakfast I got in Maroc, well priced and everything you could ask for.

Name: Café et Patisserie Nawal
Author: Andrea Cazzaniga
Review: Small place, great avocado smoothie. Coconuts biscuits also top. Definitely worth a try

Name: Café et Patisserie Nawal
Author: Anass Zaoui
Review: Excellent mango juice of incredible freshness, top-notch welcome and service, I recommend 100%

Name: Cafe Michlifen
Author: Mouksit Jihad
Review: Good location, and nice service. The cafe was ok. I ordered Nus Nus and came with a small bottle of water.

Name: Cafe Michlifen
Author: Ismail Doubabe
Review: It is good coffee, it has a nice view

Name: Cafe Michlifen
Author: FAICAL BENINDALSI
Review: Cool

Name: Cafe Michlifen
Author: Taoufiq Leuleu
Review: Excellent cafe

Name: café bin alwesal
Address: JX24+9H4 sr, Av. Al Orouba, Marrakesh
Author: Mohamed Amine EL BADAOUI

Name: Cafè Sidi Asserf
Author: Anna Świergolik
Review: Cheap coffe and tea, just waiting time too long. Served by children. Resturant sent by carpets. Looks very nice. View at the very busy road.

Name: Cafè Sidi Asserf
Author: Tadeáš Varaus
Review: nice locality, food was good, but the porcion was small and we were waiting for a long time

Name: Cafè Sidi Asserf
Author: Ronald van der bijl
Review: A little Berber restaurant with great local atmosphere just outside the tourist trap are. A real recommendation to enjoy the real Marrakech experience.

Name: Cafè Sidi Asserf
Author: Colin Rhiner
Review:
Food was great! but not the location and service.
Plate was delayed, table dusty, dishes not available... but food was tasty

Name: Cafè Sidi Asserf
Author: Charles Eldridge
Review: Delicious food, friendly service, best moroccan salad I’ve had.

Name: Henna Café
Author: Emily Anderson
Review: This was around the corner from our riad. We stopped one morning for coffee/tea/just to check it out. They have an interesting creation story. The service was quick. The food and drinks were okay. I loved looking in the different rooms. The henna room was busy and is on the first floor.  The cafe is up top.

Name: Henna Café
Author: Alice Bell-Trayner
Review: My henna is beautiful!! And I had some delicious houmous too. Lovely friendly staff, and a cute tortoise too!

Name: Henna Café
Author: Kevin Lancashire
Review: We enjoyed a relaxing lunch on the terrace, savoring a small plate of falafel and salad while sipping tea.

Name: Henna Café
Author: Tiziano Biagi
Review:
A really nice local cafe with good food (vegan and vegetarian options available and marked on the menu) for reasonable prices. The falafels were probably the best I ate in Marrakech so far!

They provide support to people in need too, handing out free meals throughout the day. It is also possible to buy a “spare plate” which will allow them to provide 10 meals (it only costs 40MAD).

Definitely worth a visit!

Name: Henna Café
Author: sonali franklin
Review:
Came here for a meal with my husband.

Cute little spot run by a small group of friendly women.

We ordered the falafel, hummus, beans and bread. Was all super delicious.

Our tummies were a little unsettled that evening, not sick - and this was the only real meal we had but otherwise it was absolutely delicious!

Very very reasonable pricing too.

Name: maruzen tea morocco s.a.r.l
Author: Christine Wong
Review:
Avoid like the plague. No professionalism. Huge scammers. They stole $1300 CAD / 9000+ MAD from us.

Never sent our 10KG of matcha we ordered on September 9th, 2024. Back and forth messages, all excuses. If you want to be safe, do not work with Maruzen in Morocco, or NASA Corporation in Japan and a woman named Gaelle or Younes.

They try to make you sign double contracts to pay them money before they even send you the first refund. Don’t be fooled, never sign these contracts.

Name: maruzen tea morocco s.a.r.l
Author: Christine Taylor
Review:
This company is a scam. They took $1300 Canadian (More than 9000 MAD) one month ago on September 9th and I never received my order of matcha or my money back.

Gaelle, who I am in contact with told me to send an international transfer to NASA Corporation in Japan and then they would begin the order. I sent the money and since it’s been a back and forth convo about how I now need to send the money to Maruzen in Morocco, when this was the first option I offered. They have trouble sending my $1300 back to my bank and says it doesn’t work for them.

They said the 10KG of matcha I paid for is ready but they refuse to send it.

If this is a company she works for as the supplier and manufacturer — they should be able to transfer the money I already paid within their own company. It’s not my problem. They told me to pay them, I did. I lost $1300!?

BIG SCAMMERS and a HUGE disappointment. AVOID THESE COMPANIES.

Name: Cafe Medina
Author: Rachel Cabral
Review: The coffee is good and cheap, but unfortunately, the location is dodgy. It's across the main Square Jemaa el-Fna and where my sister had her mobile phone stolen.

Name: Cafe Medina
Author: Sarah Schäd
Review:
Go and visit this small hole in the wall coffee shop! The guy is an absolute smiling legend and so friendly!

Great hot drinks at good prices! Thank you!

Name: Cafe Medina
Author: Justine Christen
Review: lovely coffeeshop! perfect for the coffee to go! best taste and very friendly barista 😍 highly recommend
