Name: Cafe Medina
Author: Adam Wieclawski
Review: Amazing and friendly service. Good coffee in the medina neighbourhood. We would come every day during our stay in Marrakesh.

Name: Cafe Medina
Author: David Gray
Review: Great coffee made fresh to go, good price, will be visiting again! Thanks

Name: Boughaz Coffee
Author: Lhajibi Ayoub
Review: One of the best coffees I have ever had in Marrakesh. The service was great and the atmosphere was beautiful. Keep going guys

Name: Boughaz Coffee
Author: gis spatial
Review: Excellent coffee. Nice little place with good vibes and friendly service. They do takeaway coffee and you can buy some ground coffee for home for good price (no haggling needed)

Name: Boughaz Coffee
Author: Macy “Macy”
Review: This coffee shop is awesome!!! We came here everyday for coffee! The coffee is so good! The staff are incredibly friendly. It was our first day in Morocco and the barista helped teach us a bit of Moroccan Arabic which was so kind. A really nice place to drink coffee and relax! Would recommend this shop and I would go back in a heartbeat!

Name: Boughaz Coffee
Author: Gleb Polovnykov
Review: Very good , classic cappuccino. You can wach nest of birts while drinking coffee

Name: Boughaz Coffee
Author: Ms. Nita
Review:
Super nice tea and lovely service 🤍🤍
I had such a good time in this place :)
A man so lovely and helpful
Thanks for made my day

Name: Pronto Caffè
Address: J2F8+62M, Rue Riad Zitoun el Kdim, Marrakesh
Author: Ronald Prucha
Review: Great little coffee ☕️ cafe in the middle of all the great shops. Great tasting espresso ☕️ quick and easy for a little pick me up while shopping.

Name: Pronto Caffè
Address: J2F8+62M, Rue Riad Zitoun el Kdim, Marrakesh
Author: rosleigh roberts
Review: Great little coffee shop with really good quality coffee. It was a nice surprise in a busy medina where tea is the choice of the day. Had a lovely flat white reminded me of home.

Name: Pronto Caffè
Address: J2F8+62M, Rue Riad Zitoun el Kdim, Marrakesh
Author: Perry Buchan
Review: This is the best coffee!  Get the espresso with spice!  Seriously, they have their own spice blend which works so well with the coffee. Very friendly service.

Name: Pronto Caffè
Address: J2F8+62M, Rue Riad Zitoun el Kdim, Marrakesh
Author: mari
Review: the owner was lovely and the coffee was very yummy, good price!

Name: Pronto Caffè
Address: J2F8+62M, Rue Riad Zitoun el Kdim, Marrakesh
Author: Anthony Robinson
Review: Great little cafe that serves excellent tea. Host is very welcoming

Name: J HOUSE SPECIALITY COFFEE
Address: Résidence assila 146 immeuble F magasin 17 Mohamed Marrakech, Marrakech
Author: Nab Lamdani
Review: Small spot specially inside. They have AC inside for hot weather, the service is good and quick,

Name: J HOUSE SPECIALITY COFFEE
Address: Résidence assila 146 immeuble F magasin 17 Mohamed Marrakech, Marrakech
Author: Rihab Houmadi
Review: Great coffee, the place is spacious, conveniently close to the airport, and offers free parking. Prices are reasonable, but I hope they add brunch options soon.

Name: J HOUSE SPECIALITY COFFEE
Address: Résidence assila 146 immeuble F magasin 17 Mohamed Marrakech, Marrakech
Author: Ben Dane
Review: I’m afraid the recent comments are right, staff is very nice and helpful but the coffee is in no way third wave/specialty coffee.. “cappuccino” came in a glass with loads of cocoa powder. If you claim soft opening, please adjust your online appearance accordingly.

Name: J HOUSE SPECIALITY COFFEE
Address: Résidence assila 146 immeuble F magasin 17 Mohamed Marrakech, Marrakech
Author: Seth Colchester
Review: This place is not what it says it is, pictures are not real, Instagram is fake, wasted my time and money going there for a nothing special coffee, Awful!

Name: J HOUSE SPECIALITY COFFEE
Address: Résidence assila 146 immeuble F magasin 17 Mohamed Marrakech, Marrakech
Author: Walaa's Colorways
Review: This place is not what it appears to be, it’s nothing like the pictures, it was a scam. Don’t go if you are looking for a specialty coffee

Name: café la joie
Author: Stefanie
Review: The most delicious local coffee and tea. The owner is so nice. This isn’t just coffee or tea, it’s an experience. English speaking.

Name: café la joie
Author: R
Review:
The BEST cafe with the best cafe owner!
You MUST come here and try the drinks. I had both a tea and coffee and preferred the tea. I had a bit of sugar (I think it tastes best with at least a bit as it brings out more flavour). ☕️🍵
Also, you can get a limited selection of oil perfumes for 10 Dirham here - everywhere else you’ll need to strongly barter to get this price.

Absolutely wonderful 😋😍

Name: café la joie
Author: Tammy T
Review:
Went looking for Turkish style coffee in the Medina and google maps led me to near this cafe. The young man running the business (family business) was so lovely and engaging, wanted to offer the best coffee and best service. He invited me to video and take photos as he made the coffee in the hot sand. Was Turkish coffee with the Moroccan spices added in, very tasty. And not expensive.
He has seats in the laneway which is where I sat so I could watch everything happening around me, small area with seating edge of laneway or a small terrace which I visited and found to be very quiet so would be great if wanted to escape the hustle and bustle for bit.
He also sells spices, herbs, teas, oils etc if you interested but he did not push for me to buy anything just gave me some to smell. He then sat and chatted for little bit recommending to me places to visit around Morocco. Very relaxed and lovely visit to his small cafe, will definitely return next time I in Marrakech.

Name: café la joie
Author: Niclas W.
Review: A delightful little café in Marrakech. The coffee here is the tastiest I've ever had in the city. Their Berber tea is a true delight. The rooftop terrace is cozy and authentic, offering a genuine experience. Do take the time to visit this quaint café, it's well worth it!

Name: café la joie
Author: Sean MacDonald
Review: This is the coolest coffee stand. Najib makes super dense spiced coffee and rotates the pot in hot sand to bring out the crema in the coffee. He also serves delicious berber tea. He is super warm and will teach you about all the Moroccan spices. We returned to his stand every day and ended up meeting a lot of people just from sitting on the chairs in the pathway in front of his shop. Great prices too.

Name: Deming coffee
Address: JXJR+V48 Grand Poste, Av. Hassan II, Marrakech
Author: Abdel H
Review: Deming Coffee is a hidden gem for coffee enthusiasts. Their expertly brewed beans result in a rich and satisfying cup every time. The welcoming atmosphere and friendly service make it the go-to spot in Deming. Don't miss out on their delectable pastries – a perfect complement to your java!

Name: Deming coffee
Address: JXJR+V48 Grand Poste, Av. Hassan II, Marrakech
Author: elabbassi abdel
Review: Best street coffeeshop in marrakech. I highly recommend "the magic cup" he makes, it deserves a taste

Name: Deming coffee
Address: JXJR+V48 Grand Poste, Av. Hassan II, Marrakech
Author: Mohamed Ouyizme
Review: Support small businesses 🙌

Name: Deming coffee
Address: JXJR+V48 Grand Poste, Av. Hassan II, Marrakech
Author: Mohamed El Morghini
Review: Great coffee in disguise 🥸

Name: Deming coffee
Address: JXJR+V48 Grand Poste, Av. Hassan II, Marrakech
Author: ZOUHAIR Tadghi
Review: Great coffee despite the simple project. I advise everyone to try it

Name: Mister Espresso
Author: Joanna Louise
Review:
What a lovely coffee place with a wonderful owner (who can speak German).

I stopped by and had an espresso-choco and it was a very good coffee. Good coffee machine used and a variety of beans. Just a shame that there is no milk alternative,  but we discussed this with the owner and explained that a lot of Europeans now move away from dairy.

Owner was super friendly and explained how he started off the coffee shop and his background. Other staff were also friendly.

Name: Mister Espresso
Author: IgorW
Review: A very friendly team. It's apparent that they understand coffee. You can drink on-site, take coffee to go, or buy freshly ground coffee beans to suit your needs at home. There are many different types of beans to pick from, and the crew will assist you in selecting the one that best meets your needs. Very competitive pricing. The espresso is actually very good. They offer their unique blend, which is the result of combining several types of beans. I really suggest this location.

Name: Mister Espresso
Author: Yosaf SLM
Review: Really recommended for anyone who wants to have quality coffee and a comfortable place to sit

Name: Mister Espresso
Author: Shauna Eckstadt
Review: Best coffee I’ve had yet in Morocco! Smooth & strong.

Name: Mister Espresso
Author: jaouad chqef
Review: Nice place to taste delicious coffee with good price I like it 👌

Name: Boème coffee
Address: RDC N°650 Lot Al Manar 3, Marrakech
Author: OUDEBJI ABID
Review: For those seeking a calm and serene space to study or read, and for those who need a quiet spot to get some work done, this themed book cafe with its diverse selection of books, including a generous assortment of manga, offers the perfect ambiance.

Name: Boème coffee
Address: RDC N°650 Lot Al Manar 3, Marrakech
Author: LIGHTWIN YT
Review: Very good

Name: Boème coffee
Address: RDC N°650 Lot Al Manar 3, Marrakech
Author: Mounir Alfou
Review: I am monuier

Name: Boème coffee
Address: RDC N°650 Lot Al Manar 3, Marrakech
Author: Abderrahman Bouataoui

Name: Boème coffee
Address: RDC N°650 Lot Al Manar 3, Marrakech
Author: FAROUK BLALOU

Name: caffé andalous
Author: Bartek Swiecicki
Review:
Hawaii soft drink 14 MAD
Watched football WC U-17
Nice service

Name: caffé andalous
Author: fouad Tutos
Review:
very clean and the staff is very helpful
one of the best coffee shop in Marrakech

Name: caffé andalous
Author: Abdelrhani Aallem
Review: The place is good and the coffee is excellent

Name: caffé andalous
Author: Youssef DABAH
Review: Good coffee quality, excellent positioning

Name: caffé andalous
Author: Ahmed Najib El Bissati
Review: Alright

Name: Stop Coffee
Author: Zakaria Ouaada
Review: Best espresso/coffee in town! This is a genuine family run business built for coffee lovers. Don’t be fooled by it small space, This is not like all those tourist traps in Marrakech that serves you black water and call it coffee. This is authentic italian style coffee shop where you will enjoy your coffee to the last sip.

Name: Stop Coffee
Author: smaisma sc
Review: There is no price list

Name: Mood Coffee
Author: Abdelaali Hariri
Review: For those who want to watch

Name: Mood Coffee
Author: chri dark
Review: bi1

Name: CTM Coffee shop
Author: Samir KHODDAR
Review: Gone are the days when CTM used to be a premium transportation company with the highest service, i even paid premium just so i can charge my phone and use the wifi (as advertised), turned out half of the USB posts are not working and the wifi is anything but a wifi

Name: CTM Coffee shop
Author: Erik Kostka
Review: We got taxi from Marrakesh city center to CTM for 50 dirhams. I am not sure, if it is a perfect price, but at our Riat, they told us 150 dorhams for their taxi, so definetely try to haggle.

Name: CTM Coffee shop
Author: Joselito Berbenas
Review:

Review: Coffee at the level 🥰

Name: Fassila
Author: Agata Nowak
Review: I had some cookies to go, they were ok. Coffee definitely wasn’t „the best in the souk”. Ive read before good reviews about friendly staff.. unfortunately the woman working there wasn’t one of them.

Name: Fassila
Author: Sarah Spinelli
Review: Fabulous mint tea and great vibe. Loved stopping into this little spot for a break from medina shopping.

Name: Fassila
Author: Melik Babur
Review: There were no galette de sarrasin and they were not bothered to prepare. Had chicken&cheese sandwich instead which was horribly hard to chew. Try to avoid.

Name: Fassila
Author: paolo salvetti
Review: Best coffee and tea in Marrakesh. Faboulous crepes. Amazing service. Recommended!

Name: Fassila

Review: The best! Love Mimi the kitty too.

Name: With Coffee
Author: N/A
Review: N/A

Name: Caribou Coffee - Gare De Marrakech
Author: yangchen lhamo
Review: Just opened today, tried their iced Spanish latte , the taste is good, the service is excellent! Can’t ask for more ! Finally another decent cafe for me to enjoy my time here!

Name: Caribou Coffee - Gare De Marrakech
Author: mourad
Review: Great coffee, great service and very welcoming staff 🙏

Name: Caribou Coffee - Gare De Marrakech
Author: Nouri
Review: The coffee is well prepared, you smell real Arabica, the service is friendly and the staff is very kind, helpful and smiling.

Name: Caribou Coffee - Gare De Marrakech
Author: Isabelle Gonin Hasnaoui
Review:
The coffee is excellent, the welcome is warm, it’s clean.
To improve: lower the amplitude of the music indoors. On the terrace, parasols and more comfortable seats are welcome.

Name: Caribou Coffee - Gare De Marrakech
Author: Mouad Gouzrou
Review: Very good service and products, as long as it lasts. Special mention for the cookies and cream milkshake👍

Name: STAYLIA COFFE SHOUP
Author: Cole Morton
Review: Abdul is super friendly. His coffee and sweets were delicious. I'll definitely come back! Thank you so much!

Name: STAYLIA COFFE SHOUP
Author: Abdeladim Souidi
Review: Good coffe

Name: Café Latteria
Author: YOUSSEF EL-GHALY
Review: Nice

Name: SAVOR Coffee Shop
Author: Rita M
Review: Lovely new shop that just opened. The sign got us inside with a laugh. Cute vibe, and very sweet young owners with a passion for coffee. The dark chocolate iced mocha is nice and not too sweet or too bitter. The shop owners are so sweet and made us feel very welcome on our first day in marrakesh. Will definitely be back for our daily coffee

Name: SAVOR Coffee Shop
Author: David Oršula
Review: Newly opened very small coffee place. It is just 3 small coffee tables and one bank for sitting, but very cosy and clean.  The espresso tasted just like in Italy, rich, full flavoured, creamy. Free small water comes with the coffee. We've tried two smoothies and they were exceptionally good.

Name: SAVOR Coffee Shop
Author: C. Satriano
Review: Really pretty little coffee shop with an amazing owner. Everything is individually designed, the room, the cards and even the dishes are designed by the owner. It looks very unique and made with passion. We had a delicious coffee and a pleasant conversation with the very kind owner✨

Name: SAVOR Coffee Shop
Author: Lauren Angus
Review: My favourite coffee shop in Marrakesh! The best iced latte and cookies. Service has been amazing, will miss it so much.

Name: SAVOR Coffee Shop
Author: Michael Tan
Review:
A solid choice for coffee in the medina. If you are a bit tired of the traditional Moroccan coffee and instead are craving a flat white or latte, this is the place to go.

The seating is a bit tight as there is just a long bench for 5-6 people, but the room is air conditioned so it was very comfortable.

Name: Bacha Coffee
Address: Dar el Bacha, Rte Sidi Abdelaziz, Marrakech
Author: H Nakamura
Review:
Highly recommended!! Really beautiful place and a really perfect service! It was impressive!
We went for coffee and desert after lunch, it was a great idea!
They have way too many variety of coffee type, we got lost though… better to ask advice or do your research in advance, unless you are an expert and know what you want

Name: Bacha Coffee
Address: Dar el Bacha, Rte Sidi Abdelaziz, Marrakech
Author: Denise Wang
Review:
Beautiful space for a coffee or a meal. As other people have mentioned, there is a wait (we waited about an hour), however it gives you an opportunity to explore the beautiful museum. If you have some free time, I’d recommend going, however between the wait and the meal, you’ll need to allocate about three hours.

To get into the museum (you need to pay entry to get to the cafe), it’s 60DH per person (cash only). The cafe itself takes credit card.

Name: Bacha Coffee
Address: Dar el Bacha, Rte Sidi Abdelaziz, Marrakech
Author: Liezel San Andres
Review: The waiting time at the restaurant was about an hour and a half, with service being incredibly slow—taking 30 minutes just to receive the menu, another 30 to 45 minutes for the food to arrive, and nearly an hour to finally get the bill to think we only ordered two coffees and two slices of cake—yet the marble cake was so hard it was almost inedible, though the ‘Pastry of the Week’ cappuccino was decent. The ambiance was pleasant, but the service was truly awful and price was so expensive. On the bright side, they offer a wide range of coffee options, which I appreciated, and the iced coffee was enjoyable. I also bought coffee beans from the shop, which turned out to be the highlight of the experience—they were gorgeous when brewed at home in my coffee machine. Unfortunately, that was the only truly positive aspect of the visit. It is an experience but I would never dine again.

Name: Bacha Coffee
Address: Dar el Bacha, Rte Sidi Abdelaziz, Marrakech
Author: Eli Haugen
Review:
The coffee, food, and service were so good that I visited three times during my week in Marrakech. The wait time is absolutely worth it if you pay the little bit extra to explore the museum and make friends with the other people waiting (shout out to the lovely lady from Holland ♥️). I loved learning about the history of the building and of the coffee shop as well.

Don’t miss trying the homemade cream and fresh vanilla shavings in your coffee - after you enjoy it black (as they helpfully suggest). I’m a particular fan of the Yemeni coffee, and my friend enjoyed the Jamaican Blue Mountain coffee.

Name: Bacha Coffee
Address: Dar el Bacha, Rte Sidi Abdelaziz, Marrakech
Author: Julia Hope
Review: Arrived around 10:40 and seated at 12:30- I recommend going straight to the cafe from the museum so they can get your name down on the list then you have time to explore- well worth the wait! Loved all the coffee choices and the pastries were amazing- we got the cheesecake and tiramisu!

Name: مقهى فلسطين
Author: Steve Brattig
Review: Good coffee, great staff and friendly people. Every tourist who visits Marrakech has to come in this coffee. We also recommend a fresh orange juice.

Name: مقهى فلسطين
Author: Badr Sabor
Review: Well licated and nice cafe.

Name: مقهى فلسطين
Author: Abderrahman Gaga
Review: I don't recommend it for tourists.

Name: مقهى فلسطين
Author: Saeed Ouzahra
Review: Good service, generally good

Name: مقهى فلسطين
Author: tebbaai mohamed rida
Review: Not good

Name: Starbucks
Author: Ziad Dakir
Review: I recently visited this Starbucks , and it was fantastic! The staff was incredibly friendly, providing customer service that was second to none. My Frappuccino was perfectly crafted and rich in flavor, truly top-notch. The inviting atmosphere made it a great spot to relax before my flight. I highly recommend this to anyone traveling—it’s a must-visit!

Name: Starbucks
Author: C L
Review:
Starbucks has always been my favorite coffee brand. Whichever city I go, I always visit the Starbucks there. HOWEVER, the one in Marrakesh airport really broke my heart.

The staff here was really rude. When me and my friend ready to order, he just sat there and played his phone, seemed not going to order. And when he finally ordered for us, his attitude was like we owed him 1 billion dollars. Really bad experience. If I could give 0 star to this Starbucks in google map, I would do that. And after our observation, we founded out that he only treated us differently, maybe just because we’re Asian.

The staff’s name is Ahmed, I hope next time he could treat others better😉

Name: Starbucks
Author: Chabili Hicham
Review: Very professional attitude and the tastiest coffee I've had in any starbucks, the baristas Anas and Karim were especially kind to accomodate us and patient with our orders

Name: Starbucks
Author: Dominic
Review:
It's a nice Starbucks, the only thing to mention: we received 2 different things than what we ordered. I had the feeling that he understood what I ordered (in English), but apparently that was not the case, and instead of checking he just wrote down whatever he think he heard.

Can happen, but probably good to double-check your order before you pay.

He was friendly, overall good experience

Name: Starbucks
Author: Noureddine EL AZHARI
Review: Highlight the delightful variety of beverages, the cozy ambiance, and the consistently friendly service at Starbucks.

Name: Café Menzeh
Author: Ritvik Jaru
Review:
Vegetarian - ordered veggie panini(grilled cheese, cooked zucchini, cooked peppers) mint tea and sparking water.
So delicious, refreshing, ambiance was totally chill.

Name: Café Menzeh
Author: Isabelly Cairo
Review:
Good view of the Jardin Secret from the top.
It was good to drink a good tea appreciating the nice view.

Name: Café Menzeh
Author: Pedro Aguiar costa Pedro costa
Review: we were seated for 20 minutes to be served while there were only about 5 tables occupied. very rude employees. an employee called adil super frowning is there and after we called a few times he ignored it on purpose

Name: Café Menzeh
Author: Mim's Sdk
Review:
Visited in October, the garden is magnificent (fairly quiet despite the tourists).
The new server is great 👍🏼

I had a perfect carrot/orange juice to regain strength after visits under the sun.
We have a 33cl bottle of water with the coffee (France should take an example).

Besides, noss noss means: half coffee and half milk.

Name: Café Menzeh
Author: olga konstantinidou
Review:
The cafe is actually inside the garden, for which you have to pay an entrance fee!

We never knew!

Name: Café La dose
Author: AYMANE EL MOUMNI
Review: Best service!🔥

Name: Café La dose
Author: Adrien de Lazzer
Review: Perfect

Name: Coffee Mbehja
Author: Laurent Demeneix
Review:
Really small coffee pit stop but best value found in Marrakech and of good quality.
Became my morning routine and loved their almond espresso.
To note: no milk alternative available. Only 🐮 🐄
And no table, just drink it at the counter or on the go.

Name: Coffee Mbehja
Author: Steven Silverstein
Review: Coffee Mbehja makes truly remarkable espresso!  Best that I've had in many years, including shops all through Italy, Spain, Portugal, and the USA.  Smooth, very full bodied, and perfect crema.  This small friendly family owned shop is located just inside the Sidi Mimoune Gate.  Their coffee might be the reason I relocate permanently to Marrakesh :)

Name: Coffee Mbehja
Author: J. Devos
Review:
Cheap as can be (7dh), great quality, always kind, lots of care into each order.

Small but great coffees.

Name: Coffee Mbehja
Author: Małgorzata Poniatowska
Review: Very nice place, much more better than any coffee shop in city center.

Name: Coffee Mbehja
Author: Sam C
Review: Amazing espresso with great flavours and friendly service!

Best coffee place in Marrakech! Small corner shop with typical Maroccan coffee.

You can find also coffee powder for sale at very good price compared to the market.

Very cheerful and friendly owner!

Name: Benbasha Coffee Shop
Author: Johnny Walker_94
Review: Found this hidden place by walking through old town. Awesome coffee with traditional spices. It’s owner is funny and friendly by explaining all the different tastes of coffee. For good espresso/ cappuccino you should go there!

Name: Benbasha Coffee Shop
Author: Egresi Abigel
Review: We had the best coffee here while in Morocco! We came back twice and would have more if the time would have allowed it. Best prices and best coffee! Recommend!

Name: Benbasha Coffee Shop
Author: Luisa V
Review: Very friendly and very good coffee. Thank you a lot.

Review: what an amazing coffee, I come here every single day almost, amazing place, weather, people, service and food, really recommanded if you want a clean and calm coffee. I like it

Review: it's amazing coffee  wonderful pleac

Name: Cs Coffee
Author: The Sports Champions Of Tomorrow Marrakech
Review: I used to love coffee in this place because it is very quiet and beautiful. I go to this place in the month of July and August and the temperature was up to 48. I was comfortable there, because the quality of the coffee and fruit juice drinks.....excellent. The best thing is that this place has 3... Air conditioning devices and I did not feel the temperature and it was cold in a café and I recommend it because it is quiet

Name: Cs Coffee
Author: Lili Hricha
Review: This cafe is one of the best cafes I have visited, and the coffee also tastes wonderful

Name: Cs Coffee
Author: Mohammed Laajaj
Review: A very good café with a good level of coffee quality and 100% high quality service.

Name: Coffee Find yourself
Author: N/A
Review: N/A

Name: sherif coffee
Address: sidi abdelaaziz, Rue Riad Laarous, Marrakesh
Author: Rob Emerton
Review: Definitely the best coffee I had in Morocco! Abdelhamid was the friendliest guy we met, great to chat with and get recommendations. The coffee shop is in a great spot for sitting and watching the chaos of the souks go by - a must visit when in Marrakech!

Name: sherif coffee
Address: sidi abdelaaziz, Rue Riad Laarous, Marrakesh
Author: Will
Review: Awesome coffee- tried the spiced coffee, impressed with genuine barista coffee from a cozy cafe. Super nice owner Abdelhamid, enjoyed chatting and recommend!

Name: sherif coffee
Address: sidi abdelaaziz, Rue Riad Laarous, Marrakesh
Author: Michel Faas
Review: Had a very goo spiced coffee. Great place to grab a coffee, also a great place for a nice conversation, speaking English very well

Name: sherif coffee
Address: sidi abdelaaziz, Rue Riad Laarous, Marrakesh
Author: Tom Howard
Review: Abdel is a lovely man - friendly and plenty of recommendations (suggested Chez Fatima). Delicious espresso and tasty accompanying pastry!

Name: sherif coffee
Address: sidi abdelaaziz, Rue Riad Laarous, Marrakesh
Author: Katie Nowak
Review: Loved this spot! A cute find wandering through the Medina. Hamid was very friendly & helpful. My fiancée tried the spiced coffee & loved it. Unique local flavor & reasonable prices. Definitely recommend!

Name: coffee amaretto
Author: N/A
Review: N/A

Author: Mohamed Khalidi
Review: Very good cafe

Name: Thirty5ive Marrakech
Author: Oanh
Review: This hip and laid-back modern coffee shop is a great spot to relax. You can unwind downstairs or upstairs with your coffee or smoothie of choice while enjoying cool music from a record player.

Name: Thirty5ive Marrakech
Author: Emily
Review: This cafe is a gem in Marrakech! It’s the best coffee I’ve had in the city. It has a nice minimalist design and the barista is skilled and friendly. I would have happily worked at this cafe had I discovered it sooner. The cinnamon honey matcha latte with soy milk is so good!

Name: Thirty5ive Marrakech
Author: Mònica Noviembre
Review: Great place in terms of ambience and good vibes. Also, high quality coffee. Very recommended!

Name: Thirty5ive Marrakech
Author: Dalida Spenser
Review: Beautiful and bohemian place, quiet luxury with elegant products, to escape classic places, you most visit this minimalistic  designed cafe with well researched music repertoire. Well done !

Name: Thirty5ive Marrakech
Author: Emils U
Review: Wow! What a space. Beautiful vinyl music, amazing specialty coffee and a fantastic pottery space. Far outdid any expectations we had. Fantastic service to add!

Name: Café mobile Mountasir
Author: Tommie Kuiper
Review: Very friendly and good quality strong coffee!

Review: Very good place, quiet, green space, nursery, space for family

Name: Kariat abou salma
Author: khalid bouziane
Review: A warm, quiet place that offers delicious meals. The greenery makes it a haven of peace. It has games for children.

Name: Kariat abou salma
Author: Pr Dr Pierre François Encadrant universitaire Maroc
Review: Run by Karim, clean. Children's area. Local and international food.  Good price. I recommend

Name: Le boulevard l 1er
Author: michelle lamalmi
Review: Only had coffee.   Love love loved our waiter.  Friendly but professional and attentive.

Name: Le boulevard l 1er
Author: Walid Sennoun
Review: Fantastic atmosphere gentle and respectful staf amazing coffee and tea

Name: Le boulevard l 1er
Author: hicham hicham
Review: Super 🤩

Name: Le boulevard l 1er
Author: Kaukabi
Review:
Clean and well located cafe.
Waitresses a little sluggish and not too responsive.
I recommend their excellent COUSCOUS every Friday lunchtime for 4.5 euros per person.
A real treat.
A must see

Name: Le boulevard l 1er
Author: Elodie Dalli
Review:
Good setting, attentive waiter, affordable price.
The juices are very good (orange, strawberry, orange carrots)
The downside was the pasta which had an aftertaste probably due to the sauce (seafood pasta, turkey dish and minced meat dish)

Name: Ousmane AbdelKarim
Author: E
Review: BEST COFFEE IN THE MEDINA! It's cheap and delicious—ask for Nos Nos. We discovered this gem with the help of a local, and the shop's pastries are equally delightful and budget-friendly.

Name: Ousmane AbdelKarim
Author: Fabiana A.
Review:
Lovely small place where you can have breakfast. Prices are clearly shown, and they're fair. The place has a good selection of pastries.
Great customer service, too.

Name: Ousmane AbdelKarim
Author: Emily Hand
Review: Best coffee we had in the area, also super cheap, with the coffee being only 5MAD. We went back twice and both people working were super kind and provided exceptional service. Check it out if you'd like cheap coffee or even some pastries!

Name: Sayoja cafe
Author: Day Trips From Marrakech
Review: I had a nice seat there with one of my friends. The place is too quiet and I highly recommend it

Name: Sayoja cafe
Author: bahama othmane
Review: Good place to drink a coffee

Name: Sayoja cafe
Author: EL-Mehdi TANDOFTE
Review: Bad experience

Name: Sayoja cafe
Author: Adil Smour
Review: Spacious

Author: Simo Boudina
Review: Nice

Name: Cafe belle vue
Author: potox spring-flex
Review: Good welcome. Impeccable customer service I love this place and even it touched me LIGHTNING COST

Name: Cafe belle vue
Author: Enisa Mehmedovic
Review: Super nice staff..traditional

Name: Cafe belle vue
Author: Aidali Yassine
Review: GOOD

Name: Moustafa’s Street Food
Author: Alan Alpenfelt
Review:
This is a very very simple street food place but Moustafa’s lentil soup with carrots is AMAZINGLY good, better than any restaurants I’ve been to.
Definitely check it out if you are in the area and enjoy the sugary mint tea. You sit down and watch the passers by, feeling time stopping for a moment.
Moustafa kindly offered me a spoon but…
I always love to eat with my hands to have that connection with the food and smell the taint of spices on my fingers after having washed my hands.
I was full up and happy for the day, ready to delve into bargaining!
(I wrote “coffee shop” because google doesn’t give the street food option. You’ll find mint instead).

Name: Moustafa’s Street Food
Author: Tommaso Giacopini
Review:
Come here if you want a real Moroccan experience! Street food at its best 👌
Moustafa is humble, generous and kind and his food is bery authentic and a real pleasure for the palate. Great food at local prices. Definitely worth grabbing a bite here!

Name: Buvette du Tribunal
Author: John Stapels
Review: Really nice little buvette under a palm tree. Good coffee although a bit expensive.

Name: Buvette du Tribunal
Author: MONK HF
Review:
Charming little interior courtyard
Fresh bread and tasty omelettes
A ray of sunshine in a somewhat gray place

Name: LA TASSE JOYEUSE
Address: 2ème étage, appartement 129, Résidence Albassatime, Av. Guemassa, Marrakesh
Author: Moroccan Wonderer
Review: A good coffee in mhamid, in a good spot, but the space is small a little bit, the breakfasts are good but they could be better and more diversified, service is fast, in the morning you can't sit outside because of the shiny sunlight.

Name: LA TASSE JOYEUSE
Address: 2ème étage, appartement 129, Résidence Albassatime, Av. Guemassa, Marrakesh
Author: ADIL AARAB
Review:
A nice new coffee place with a beautiful design and a diversified menu with a lot to offer. Breakfast menu with multiple choices and even lunch is proposed which is rare in the area. The place is beautiful and new with some modern decoration and fourniture. Highly recommend for a quick cafe or a breakfast or just to watch some football.
I tried the breakfast which I highly recommend it was tasty and nutritious with a price of 40dh, I also tried the carrot juice very tasty and refreshing. I could say the best coffee in the boulevard so far give it a chance

Name: LA TASSE JOYEUSE
Address: 2ème étage, appartement 129, Résidence Albassatime, Av. Guemassa, Marrakesh
Author: Nouamane Iggoute
Review: Nice place and nice service, will be back laterfor sure

Name: LA TASSE JOYEUSE
Address: 2ème étage, appartement 129, Résidence Albassatime, Av. Guemassa, Marrakesh
Author: Abdelhak Abd
Review: bad service

Name: LA TASSE JOYEUSE
Address: 2ème étage, appartement 129, Résidence Albassatime, Av. Guemassa, Marrakesh
Author: Ali Boudda
Review:
Warm place, a very good café restaurant that I highly recommend

Delicious and truly impeccable quality of cuisine with the best quality/price ratio

Name: Xlaze Cafe
Author: Elanaam Tourisme
Review: New coffee shop and they even have the TV to watch a football match live.

Name: Le Menara Cafe
Author: Matt VanderPloeg
Review: Veg pastilla, falafel sandwich and chicken sandwich all delicious. Inexpensive especially for the area. With regards to the other reviews the washroom had soap and paper. Slow service for the chicken sandwich, took 20 mins so 4 star.

Name: Le Menara Cafe
Author: Jester
Review:
shortly; like it.

food a part of trying interesting and not same boring other coffees here.

mean: menu short but super. service 1a, mean top. chatting with kitchen, service girl and "boss of service".

outside or in front never quite or boring. it's action for eyes and ears.
price really to accept

thx.

Name: Le Menara Cafe
Author: Vasanth Nag K V
Review: Opposite to Bahia palace, a calm place in the corner. The owner is super nice. We had breakfast. Eggs, bread, coffee and juice, price is very affordable compared to other places here. I was surprised that this place has such less reviews given the quality and affordability of the menu. Do visit here for a quick breakfast when you visit Bahia palace in Marrakesh! Spent around 9 euros for 2 people.

Name: Le Menara Cafe
Author: Thlockman
Review: Great food, prices not had for touristy location.  The couscous in particular was a highlight

Name: Le Menara Cafe
Author: Isabelly Cairo
Review:
The omelet naturale was delicious, homemade and honest price.
A good place to eat beside of the Bahia Palace.

Name: Café Mazar
Author: WanderLensBiker “WanderLensBiker”
Review:
I recently had the pleasure of visiting Cafe Mazar, a Lebanese café located in Marrakech Airport, and I must say, it was a fantastic experience. The staff were incredibly friendly and welcoming, which made my visit all the more enjoyable. What impressed me the most was their willingness to open early in the morning, allowing travelers like myself to grab a bite to eat even during the wee hours.

One of the standout aspects of Cafe Mazar is its reasonable pricing. In an airport where options can often be limited and expensive, I was pleasantly surprised to find that sandwiches were priced at only 49 Moroccan Dirhams each, making it a budget-friendly choice for a quick meal.

Overall, I highly recommend Cafe Mazar to anyone passing through Marrakech Airport. With its friendly staff, early opening hours, and reasonable prices, it's a gem that stands out among airport dining options

Name: Café Mazar
Author: Lisbeth Stewart
Review:
Delicious food, look in the display cabinet for portion sizes.
Lovely staff who seem to speak French, English & Arabic fluently.
Comfortable seating area with a fence so you can relax feeling your luggage is secure.
Menu (French) available, including a 3 course meal option.

Name: Café Mazar
Author: Abdellatif Merroun
Review: 1 moroccan pancake (رغيفة) small honey for individual use ,small tea bag take away cup and orange juice 91 dirhams. It's just beyond belief. It's a small coffee shop plastic chairs they have not got no butter no cheese to put on your pancake It's just day light robbery as they say.

Name: Café Mazar
Author: Nga Nguyen
Review: I ordered a Moroccan pancake which came with some honey. It was a big pancake however it had no taste whatsoever. Very bland and a bit dry. Tried to make it work with the honey, but the honey tasted weird.

Name: Café Mazar
Author: Stefano P
Review: Great little boxes of food and friendly staff, much tastier than anything inside the airport which is all quite expensive only thing I wouldn’t recommend is the pancake

Name: Kosy Kawa
Author: Oleg Shestakov
Review: This place is great for anyone looking for specialty coffee in Marrakesh. It has a beautiful ambiance, featuring local art, and offers a good selection of coffee beans for filter brewing. The barista knows what he's doing and can provide helpful recommendations for other specialty coffee spots around Marrakesh. It's truly an oasis of specialty coffee in a desert of 'regular' coffee.

Name: Kosy Kawa
Author: José Reis Santos
Review:
A truly coffee place. The options, flavors and combos are crafted carefully by Muhammad, our host.
One of the best cappuccinos I had. Globally. Very very nice place, with several cosy rooms and super friendly staff. Amazing view on the rooftop over the square and part of the city.
Perfect for a short stop while strolling the city.

Important note: I managed to pay with Revolut.
Important note 2: several milk options.

Name: Kosy Kawa
Author: smiddy550
Review: Great wee spot to enjoy a latte coffee overlooking the square which is not like the other crazy square. A coffee for 25 dirhams and enjoy the lovely clean square where shopkeepers are nice to their cats and feed them. All very friendly. Very clean toilets upstairs. Miriam our waitress is lovely. Speaks great English. Friendly and engaging. Highly recommend. Geoff

Name: Kosy Kawa
Author: Dave Conroy
Review: Broadly third wave coffee shop stopover when in between the palaces and seeking a break. Located in the corner of the square. Very pleasant people offering great service. The upstairs is a bonus if you like to watch the world go by.

Name: Kosy Kawa
Author: David Murphy
Review:
A oasis in a desert.
AMAZING coffee served in a modern and delicious style. The choice of brew method- machine, V60, Aeropress, Cold Brew. A choice of beans from all the over the world. The knowledge of Mohammed about how to extract the best from the beans made for a fantastic coffee.
If you are in Marrakesh and you enjoy outstanding coffee then this is the place.

Name: Cafe Rouge
Address: Souq, Marrakech 40000, Morocco، 38 Rue de la Bahia, Marrakesh
Author: FELIPE ROMERO
Review: Lovely view but note great selection of  food. Good service and if u just want a tajin or couscous it's OK... Or tea and coffe

Name: Cafe Rouge
Address: Souq, Marrakech 40000, Morocco، 38 Rue de la Bahia, Marrakesh
Author: Carlos Pizarro del Valle
Review: Nice roof top with nice views of Koutoubia

Name: Cafe Rouge
Address: Souq, Marrakech 40000, Morocco، 38 Rue de la Bahia, Marrakesh
Author: Youssef Sabir
Review: Cozy little place especially with a roof top setup.

Name: Cafe Rouge
Address: Souq, Marrakech 40000, Morocco، 38 Rue de la Bahia, Marrakesh
Author: DesertBrise Travel
Review: amazing with lots of flavour, very impressive for a vegetarian restaurant.

Name: Cafe Rouge
Address: Souq, Marrakech 40000, Morocco، 38 Rue de la Bahia, Marrakesh
Author: chafiq bahra
Review: Good cafe, very sweet tea, order it without sugar.

Name: Estrella blanch
Address: N°B23, GROUPE 14, IMM 4 KODIAT LAABID, N° 9 Rte de Safi, Marrakesh
Author: chris challinger
Review: Nice cafe with good coffee and nice toasted bread stylish and modern they even gave me the glass to take back to the apartment I had the cheapest breakfast and it was good value it come with cereal and some toast with jam the cafe creme is served in a nice mug how I like it so I won't complain there 😋

Name: Estrella blanch
Address: N°B23, GROUPE 14, IMM 4 KODIAT LAABID, N° 9 Rte de Safi, Marrakesh
Author: Lorelei Boutet
Review:
A very welcoming place, hearty and good breakfasts, fresh fruit juices.
Pleasant and kind waiter, very well received.

Name: Estrella blanch
Address: N°B23, GROUPE 14, IMM 4 KODIAT LAABID, N° 9 Rte de Safi, Marrakesh
Author: Nada 80

Name: Estrella blanch
Address: N°B23, GROUPE 14, IMM 4 KODIAT LAABID, N° 9 Rte de Safi, Marrakesh
Author: Fahd Ghazouani

Name: Kesh cup Marrakech
Author: Ellen Hookey
Review: Such a cute little cafe with a lovely atmosphere and stylish aesthetic. Really amazing coffee, perfectly made. There's oat and almond milk available, and the smoothies are delicious too. The owner is so friendly and down for a chat, and is always playing the coolest eclectic music.

Name: Kesh cup Marrakech
Author: Ella Christella
Review: I'm so happy to finally find good coffee latte, 20 DH!! They even offer Alpro almond, soy and oat milk for extra 5 DH. The raspberry smoothie is also great, 30 DH. I managed to get a seat at the window at 15:30. A great time to come. Nice and quiet. Thank you.

Name: Kesh cup Marrakech
Author: Anne Marie J
Review: Great coffee for a great price. 3 cortados and 1 flat white for $8 / 800dh. Cool, cozy vibe. Centrally located. Prompt service - nice for quick pick me up.

Name: Kesh cup Marrakech
Author: S
Review: Amazing coffee, even by Australian standards. Was so nice to stumble across this little gem while in Marrakech after not having a decent coffee in weeks while travelling! They have nut milks and syrups!!

Name: Kesh cup Marrakech
Author: boyan gerretzen
Review: Best place for a coffee in Marrakech during your trip. Take 10/15 minutes to stop running everywhere and stand at the bar/outside to enjoy life and all the chaos of the Medina happening in front of you while drinking your coffee!!

Name: Good vibes Coffee shop
Author: Shwetabh Sameer
Review: Great experience. The shop owner/barista is really friendly. And not to mention, he makes great coffee too!

Name: Good vibes Coffee shop
Author: Younes Sadiki
Review: Very pleasant setting, I had an ICE coffee, the taste of the coffee is incredible....

Name: Good vibes Coffee shop
Author: adoscar Adoscar
Review:
Excellent coffee!
Traditional setting with lots of charm!
Nice surprise
I highly recommend and will return.

Name: Café Des Épices
Author: BCL ADRIAN
Review: This place is very nice to have breakfast. The Moroccan breakfast is extremely nice and the food is amazing. I would highly recommend a visit here for breakfast lunch and/or tea or coffee. They gave seating both upstairs, on the street level, and also roof top. It is warm to eat here as there is no air conditioning but the environment and the food is with it even without air-conditioning. I would recommend to visit here. Bear in mind the small print they do charge 7% service fee additional to food cost.  It is quite expensive here nearly the same prices as you pay in Europe. But the food is worth it.

Name: Café Des Épices
Author: Caterina Bassano
Review: Great Moroccan food in a lovely rooftop space (the middle floor is also super nice). Great views over the Medina. The fish tagine was very tasty, and the hummus was delicious. We didn't book in advance but we went there early so it was quiet. It gets very busy especially around sunset hours, so service was a little slow. Reasonable prices!

Name: Café Des Épices
Author: Marlene Po
Review:
Very charming café situated at the small square where hats and baskets are sold .

But that's about it.

We ordered   Aish Burrito ( small portion for 60 DH ),  Vegetarian Sandwich ( again small portion for 70 DH... AND! VERY BLAND. Had to squeeze lots of lemon on  it.  Bread was hard.   Lentil salad was ho- hum, not much taste either.

In short, don't go here.

Overpriced, tasteless

Name: Café Des Épices
Author: Yousra
Review:
Had lunch here sadly came at a pick time, but still the view was great. Food was nice, morrocan tea.
Good touristic and prices are more expensive than the usual in the city. Friendly staff.

Name: Café Des Épices
Author: Ella Willoughby
Review: Great stop for a nice drink. We sat on the middle floor level as the top was too hot, but all the decor was very sleek and nice. The window looking onto the market was amazing to look at. We only had some fuzzy drinks but the food looked and smelt delicious.

Name: The saloon 2
Author: N/A
Review: N/A

Name: Medina kawa Marrakech
Author: Yusuf Khan
Review: My personal favorite coffee shop in all of Marrakech. The shop itself is small but it has a ton of charm. The coffee and ice cream is also quite good, not as creamy as you’d expect but still great! It’s situated on a semi-busy street so the occasional motorcycle will whizz by you be mindful of that. Otherwise a great experience.

Name: Medina kawa Marrakech
Author: Marky
Review:
Visited here 3 days in a row, they serve some amazing waffles, coffee and cookies as you can see. The female worker is always so happy. Unsure what the other comments are on about Pizza, they don't do Pizza.

The waffles are really good quality, they have a range of sauces. I paid 40 Dirhams for 4 waffles to go today.

Highly recommend and they have WiFi, charging and it's a busy road to sit and watch the world go by

Name: Medina kawa Marrakech
Author: Time To Be You - the Real You
Review: Best place to have cappuccino and fantastic food and the kind and love energy from the girls 🕺👍🔥🥰🙏✨🌟 a place for not to miss if are around 🌟

Name: Medina kawa Marrakech
Author: Borja Garcia
Review: Best coffee in Ben Saleh

Name: Medina kawa Marrakech
Author: Lorraine Grimbley
Review: What a lovely place we stumbled across  your chicken wrap is delicious... and that berry ice cream ... wow... girls you are doing a great job .

Name: Coffee Houmti ️ قهوة حومتي
Author: allison arandez
Review: Great little cafe, had a really nice cafe latte and sat and watched local life go by

Name: Coffee Houmti ️ قهوة حومتي
Author: Junghoon seo
Review:
My favourite cafe in Marrakech while I was there. Cozy place with a couple seats outside. It is a great resting place while you walk through the markets. Espresso slightly bitter but can be reduced with the sugar. I came here twice and the first time I got the croissant which was very nice. Soft on the inside and brushed with a sweet sauce. Super friendly barista.

It is in the middle of a very busy road so you will have motorbikes going past you. Other than that, worth a visit for sure.

Name: Coffee Houmti ️ قهوة حومتي
Author: AANUIT
Review: Nice place for a quick coffee and croissant. Hicham is very friendly and in for a small talk. The cappuccino is not like the “European” one I guess, but still good. Croissant were fresh.

Name: Coffee Houmti ️ قهوة حومتي
Author: Mia Cunningham
Review: Wow! This little spot is an amazing stop for a coffee & pastry. Very good prices and the quality is amazing. One of our favourite places for coffee in Marrakech. Thank you to the owner for his amazing service and beautiful coffee 🙏🏼❤️

Name: Coffee Houmti ️ قهوة حومتي
Author: Eamonn Coughlan
Review: Had coffee and orange juice. Both incredible. Loved the setup. Simple but elegant and well thought about

Name: Bubble Tea Time Marrakech
Address: En face du lycée Victor Hugo, Marrakech
Author: Sugako e
Review: Part of bubble tea is to have something  that cools you down. The drinks are neither slushy or oftered with ice. Just a very bitter drink served at room temperature.  Price are so high it's easy to understand  why there is no line and all the tables empty.

Name: Bubble Tea Time Marrakech
Address: En face du lycée Victor Hugo, Marrakech
Author: Redouane Khrifi
Review: I tried this kind of bubble tea for the first time during Gitex Africa. It is by far better than the ones that use tapioca balls. These are real bubbles that burst when you bite them. The hostess they had during Gitex was really nice and helpful, she provided excellent service during the 3 days of the event.

Name: Bubble Tea Time Marrakech
Address: En face du lycée Victor Hugo, Marrakech
Author: Анастасия Цибульская
Review:
I was order 2 bubble drinks , and they make di fast dilivery to my home at 00:00 😍
It’s so delicious and welcome service ! Thank uou !

Name: Bubble Tea Time Marrakech
Address: En face du lycée Victor Hugo, Marrakech
Author: Hamid Boulahna
Review: I highly recommend bubble tea time for People Who looking for a refreshing  and tasty bubble tea experience . With great drinks, quality ingredient and a reaaaaaaaally friendly staff , it's a must visit for any bubble lover😍😍😍🤩

Name: Bubble Tea Time Marrakech
Address: En face du lycée Victor Hugo, Marrakech
Author: Salma Cherradi
Review:
The staff was friendly the place was so nice and clean
The bubble tea was delicious and sweet

Name: BL TEA
Author: Emily Hyunjoo Park
Review:
Definitely not authentic taste, cheese tastes different(like nacho cheese but in bad way, not real cheese; shouldn’t complain too much knowing we are in Morocco). Bubble tea’s tapioca pearls were hard when chewing.
However, the server was very kind. Loved her kindness!

Name: BL TEA
Author: Anisha K
Review: lovely hospitality, service and food! looks very beautiful and yummy. would definitely recommend!

Name: BL TEA
Author: Hajar Hamadi
Review: 2 stars for the friendly service. The bubble tea was not good, too sweet and A tad tasteless. The food was a tad pricey . I was an underwhelming experience and a little disappointing.

Name: BL TEA
Author: fadwa ait el hadj
Review: honestly the drink wasn't what I expected , it was super sweet and the bubbles melted instantly in my mouth and their taste was not strong I didn't like it and there's only one size for the drink, but the waiter was nice and the atmosphere just okey.

Name: BL TEA
Author: Andreia Gomes
Review:
🔝 lovely, cosy, nice food and very kind staff 5✨
Found a fresh space and ‘normal’ food in the Marrakesh caos 😊

Name: Bubble's pink
Address: Imm Eden Park Magasin N° 2, Angle Rue Tarik Ibn Ziad et, Rue Sourya, Marrakesh
Author: Deara France
Review: This place is THE cutest cafe. I was walking around and the decorations caught my eye. It’s so aesthetically pleasing AND the food is great. The menu is quite expansive so I would like to thank Éloge for helping me out. The mint lemonade & the mini pancakes were great. I do not like Nutella so I had it without it and I loved it. You can tell it was made with  love.

Name: Bubble's pink
Address: Imm Eden Park Magasin N° 2, Angle Rue Tarik Ibn Ziad et, Rue Sourya, Marrakesh
Author: H Mooney
Review: Cute find! Loved the location and atmosphere. Not too pricey. The mini waffles were delish! Clean environment. Service was A+

Name: Bubble's pink
Address: Imm Eden Park Magasin N° 2, Angle Rue Tarik Ibn Ziad et, Rue Sourya, Marrakesh
Author: Ar Rahman
Review: Lovely atmosphere, variety of choices drinks and desserts. Friendly staff members, food rating 10/10. Highly recommended and personally I would definitely visiting there again

Name: Bubble's pink
Address: Imm Eden Park Magasin N° 2, Angle Rue Tarik Ibn Ziad et, Rue Sourya, Marrakesh
Author: May
Review:
Bubble tea here doesn't have an authentic taste. The flavour is so plain and too much sugar at the bottom. I was feeling like I had a milk with extra sugar added. The price is pricey as well.
The decorations and location of the shop are nice and convenient but need to improve on quality of drinks.

Name: Bubble's pink
Address: Imm Eden Park Magasin N° 2, Angle Rue Tarik Ibn Ziad et, Rue Sourya, Marrakesh
Author: Wesley Sparreboom
Review: Amazing place! Everything is perfect, from smoothie till crêpes to the staff. It was a 5 star experience. Hope to come back next time when I'll be in Marrakech

Name: Smash Time
Address: JXWJ+284, Rue Assaqia El Hamra, Marrakech
Author: ALMADIS - Modestik
Review:
Great welcome and professional!
Very fast service.
Quality at unbeatable prices for burger smashes of this quality...

Name: Smash Time
Address: JXWJ+284, Rue Assaqia El Hamra, Marrakech
Author: Aymen Bandeira
Review: It was really good and we were very well received.

Name: Smash Time
Address: JXWJ+284, Rue Assaqia El Hamra, Marrakech
Author: MT IDRISSI

Name: Smash Time
Address: JXWJ+284, Rue Assaqia El Hamra, Marrakech
Author: Paps Lp

Name: Smash Time
Address: JXWJ+284, Rue Assaqia El Hamra, Marrakech
Author: Amina

Name: Made'ina kitchen
Author: Akil Mayet
Review: Amazing place. Authentic bubble tea and crepes

Name: Made'ina kitchen
Author: Yusuf Mayet
Review: The food is great and the service is very good!! I definitely recommend!!

Name: Made'ina kitchen
Author: oumaima cherkaoui
Review: Best cofee in town the food is so tasty too i can only recommend it

Name: Made'ina kitchen
Author: Hope Grace
Review: Warm atmosphere, the staff are very welcoming and the dishes are excellent. I especially recommend the brania sandwich, a delight

Name: Made'ina kitchen
Author: Oscar Lozano
Review: The best Moroccan crepes in Marrakech. Everything we had was delicious, plus there is a good atmosphere and service. Clean and nice place.

Name: Boston Coffee Restaurant Marrakech
Address: N'15, magasin, complexe El habous, Bd Allal Al Fassi, Marrakesh
Author: Nicole Adkins
Review: This place has a nice atmosphere and good food. The owner and staff are super nice and friendly, the service was excellent. All the food was great, and we highly recommend the yellow fruit smoothie bowl.

Name: Boston Coffee Restaurant Marrakech
Address: N'15, magasin, complexe El habous, Bd Allal Al Fassi, Marrakesh
Author: A Gho
Review: Amazing food! Very yummy! We had the cheese burger and the Nutella banana crepe which were amazing. Our waiter was very kind and friendly. Beautiful design and interior. 😍 We’ll come again next time we’re in marrakesh! ☺️🌺

Name: Boston Coffee Restaurant Marrakech
Address: N'15, magasin, complexe El habous, Bd Allal Al Fassi, Marrakesh
Author: Hajar Taoussi
Review: Great restaurant with a chill atmosphere. The whole experience for me was a 10/10, the Food was delicious with affordable prices. Special thanks to our lovely Server Karima. Also, it's great for studying or finishing up some work

Name: Boston Coffee Restaurant Marrakech
Address: N'15, magasin, complexe El habous, Bd Allal Al Fassi, Marrakesh
Author: Grace Lim
Review: Nice place to eat after visiting Jardin Majorelle. We had a good variety of breakfast and enjoyed it very much. Karima was friendly and helpful!

Name: Boston Coffee Restaurant Marrakech
Address: N'15, magasin, complexe El habous, Bd Allal Al Fassi, Marrakesh
Author: rayan Aichoun
Review: Greeted bt the same lovely karima and served by a very kind gentlemen named Khalid, the food once again was 10/10 the first time was so nice I had to do it twice

Name: Waffle Factory
Author: jamal bouhdad
Review: It’s beautiful place in the middle of the big square nice view and they serve good food thank you for everybody and special thanks for Redone,for sure I will back again

Name: Waffle Factory
Author: Travel Zone
Review: I ordered kebab waffle. It’s was ok but could better with nice sauce and spice or pickle. Around everywhere food is expensive. I chose waffle factory because it’s clean and their hygiene standard is better than other street food places. Please visitors watch what you eat. Food poisoning is very common.

Name: Waffle Factory
Author: Kia
Review: Redouane was lovely! & the waffle was insane. Can’t wait to come back!! 🤍

Name: Waffle Factory
Author: Akatsuki Yona
Review: the prices did not align with the quality of the treats. While the ambiance and service were satisfactory, the underwhelming taste and texture of both the ice cream and waffles left me questioning the value of the experience. Considering the HIGH prices, I expected something more delightful.

Name: Waffle Factory
Author: Kelly C
Review: Very friendly staff!! The Apple and caramel waffle is incredible! Coconut Frappe is so fresh and delicious

Name: Café restaurant l'étincelle
Address: oasis RDC, MX9C+XGH Hay izdihar n° 344, Marrakech
Author: jalal sara
Review: Amazing and delicious food and very good service from staff, they work very hard and fast service and kind,

Name: Café restaurant l'étincelle
Address: oasis RDC, MX9C+XGH Hay izdihar n° 344, Marrakech
Author: Naima Hourdane
Review: I had a good time in this place! It's family friendly, the service was amazing and the food was well made and delicious! And the prices were fair! I totally recommend it!

Name: Café restaurant l'étincelle
Address: oasis RDC, MX9C+XGH Hay izdihar n° 344, Marrakech
Author: Colombe 03
Review: Very nice, one of the better restaurant in Marrakech. Great price, everything was fabulous

Name: Café restaurant l'étincelle
Address: oasis RDC, MX9C+XGH Hay izdihar n° 344, Marrakech
Author: Hajar Chichou
Review:
Very nice and family friendly place. The food was very delicious and nicely presented.
The waiters are very pleasant. Perfect experience in this place.

Name: Café restaurant l'étincelle
Address: oasis RDC, MX9C+XGH Hay izdihar n° 344, Marrakech
Author: Blaze Hyne
Review: A modern contemporary, easy going restaurant offering both Moroccan and western food. Great tasting and well presented food. Found early on our trip and came back twice!

Name: Waffle Factory
Address: M Avenue Marrakech, Av. de la Ménara, Marrakesh
Author: Aya Ibrahim
Review: Waffle Factory in Marrakech is a gem! Their friendly service and delicious, perfectly cooked waffles made our visit unforgettable. Highly recommended for anyone seeking a delightful culinary experience.

Name: Waffle Factory
Address: M Avenue Marrakech, Av. de la Ménara, Marrakesh
Author: Ma' Ree
Review: I HIGHLY RECOMMEND!! Food was fresh and delicious!! Service was fast and friendly.  Pictures do no justice.... I will definitely return a few times while here.

Name: Waffle Factory
Address: M Avenue Marrakech, Av. de la Ménara, Marrakesh
Author: NOURHANE HAKEM
Review: The food was delicious. I tried the waffine, and the staff were incredibly friendly. When I asked about the difference between the waffle and the waffine, they explained it to me clearly and kindly.

Name: Waffle Factory
Address: M Avenue Marrakech, Av. de la Ménara, Marrakesh
Author: kawtar farouq
Review: I love the place , very cozy and warm for friends meeting , must visit ❣

Name: Waffle Factory
Address: M Avenue Marrakech, Av. de la Ménara, Marrakesh
Author: Sam Shariff
Review: I had a lovely brunch at Waffle Factory, I was treated exceptionally well by 2 members of the team Rida and Bouchra very friendly showed me how to change machine to English so it was as easier for me to order. They are both an asset to the company. Place was clean and had ac lovely venue lovely team will definitely be back when I return to Morocco.

Name: Le Passage Restaurant
Author: ABDELHAMID SAID
Review:
in ramadan we had a buffet for 4 friends, the place is incredible, especially the servants who are amazing and so helpful, the food was quite good, what i like most is the juices especially the lemon with the great test. we had a lovely evening FTOUR.
Thanks for everything

Name: Le Passage Restaurant
Author: Fadoua Atif
Review:
Not sure if I am right but I never had this small quantity of beef when ordering it somewhere else, I didn’t know it was this small quantity or I would have ordered something else
Ps : this is how they brought it I didn’t eat from it yet

Name: Le Passage Restaurant
Author: Leslie Amuzu-Treve
Review:
Le Passage Restaurant was conveniently located just a short walk from my hotel, making it an easy choice for a meal. The restaurant is well-equipped for football fans, with multiple screens showing the latest matches, which adds to the lively atmosphere.
The service was excellent, with staff members who spoke multiple languages, making communication a breeze. The food was good, offering a variety of dishes that were well-prepared and tasty. I enjoyed my dinner inside, where the atmosphere was calm and relaxing. Afterward, I stepped outside for a refreshing breeze, which was a nice way to end the evening.
Overall, Le Passage is a great spot for a meal, especially if you’re looking for a place to catch a game and enjoy good food in a comfortable setting.

Name: Le Passage Restaurant
Author: Mian Javaid, MD
Review: Good parking. Spacious seating. English speaking staff available. Clean place. Staff was accommodating to my wife who eats gluten free and ensured she was able to enjoy a burger without bun.

Name: Le Passage Restaurant
Author: Adam Eldhshoury
Review: Maybe not just them but almost in every breakfast place in Marrakech, they don't serve good coffee, I tried to explain to them how to Americano and they still made it wrong.

Name: Deming coffee
Address: JXJR+V48 Grand Poste, Av. Hassan II, Marrakech
Author: Abdel H
Review: Deming Coffee is a hidden gem for coffee enthusiasts. Their expertly brewed beans result in a rich and satisfying cup every time. The welcoming atmosphere and friendly service make it the go-to spot in Deming. Don't miss out on their delectable pastries – a perfect complement to your java!

Name: Deming coffee
Address: JXJR+V48 Grand Poste, Av. Hassan II, Marrakech
Author: elabbassi abdel
Review: Best street coffeeshop in marrakech. I highly recommend "the magic cup" he makes, it deserves a taste

Name: Deming coffee
Address: JXJR+V48 Grand Poste, Av. Hassan II, Marrakech
Author: Mohamed Ouyizme
Review: Support small businesses 🙌

Name: Deming coffee
Address: JXJR+V48 Grand Poste, Av. Hassan II, Marrakech
Author: Mohamed El Morghini
Review: Great coffee in disguise 🥸

Name: Deming coffee
Address: JXJR+V48 Grand Poste, Av. Hassan II, Marrakech
Author: ZOUHAIR Tadghi
Review: Great coffee despite the simple project. I advise everyone to try it

Name: Coffee Find yourself
Author: N/A
Review: N/A

Name: Coffee Shop
Author: N/A
Review: N/A

Name: Cafe Medina
Author: Rachel Cabral
Review: The coffee is good and cheap, but unfortunately, the location is dodgy. It's across the main Square Jemaa el-Fna and where my sister had her mobile phone stolen.

Name: Cafe Medina
Author: Sarah Schäd
Review:
Go and visit this small hole in the wall coffee shop! The guy is an absolute smiling legend and so friendly!

Great hot drinks at good prices! Thank you!

Name: Cafe Medina
Author: Justine Christen
Review: lovely coffeeshop! perfect for the coffee to go! best taste and very friendly barista 😍 highly recommend

Name: Cafe Medina
Author: Adam Wieclawski
Review: Amazing and friendly service. Good coffee in the medina neighbourhood. We would come every day during our stay in Marrakesh.

Name: Cafe Medina
Author: David Gray
Review: Great coffee made fresh to go, good price, will be visiting again! Thanks

Name: Sayoja cafe
Author: Day Trips From Marrakech
Review: I had a nice seat there with one of my friends. The place is too quiet and I highly recommend it

Name: Sayoja cafe
Author: bahama othmane
Review: Good place to drink a coffee

Name: Sayoja cafe
Author: EL-Mehdi TANDOFTE
Review: Bad experience

Name: Sayoja cafe
Author: Adil Smour
Review: Spacious

Name: Sayoja cafe
Author: malika tazouanti
Review: Good place to relax

Name: Boughaz Coffee
Author: Lhajibi Ayoub
Review: One of the best coffees I have ever had in Marrakesh. The service was great and the atmosphere was beautiful. Keep going guys

Name: Boughaz Coffee
Author: gis spatial
Review: Excellent coffee. Nice little place with good vibes and friendly service. They do takeaway coffee and you can buy some ground coffee for home for good price (no haggling needed)

Name: Boughaz Coffee
Author: Macy “Macy”
Review: This coffee shop is awesome!!! We came here everyday for coffee! The coffee is so good! The staff are incredibly friendly. It was our first day in Morocco and the barista helped teach us a bit of Moroccan Arabic which was so kind. A really nice place to drink coffee and relax! Would recommend this shop and I would go back in a heartbeat!

Name: Boughaz Coffee
Author: Gleb Polovnykov
Review: Very good , classic cappuccino. You can wach nest of birts while drinking coffee

Name: Boughaz Coffee
Author: Ms. Nita
Review:
Super nice tea and lovely service 🤍🤍
I had such a good time in this place :)
A man so lovely and helpful
Thanks for made my day

Name: Coffee House
Author: N/A
Review: N/A

Name: STAYLIA COFFE SHOUP
Author: Cole Morton
Review: Abdul is super friendly. His coffee and sweets were delicious. I'll definitely come back! Thank you so much!

Name: STAYLIA COFFE SHOUP
Author: Abdeladim Souidi
Review: Good coffe

Name: CAFE BOUALAM
Author: Mohamed Khalidi
Review: Very good cafe

Name: Pronto Caffè
Address: J2F8+62M, Rue Riad Zitoun el Kdim, Marrakesh
Author: Ronald Prucha
Review: Great little coffee ☕️ cafe in the middle of all the great shops. Great tasting espresso ☕️ quick and easy for a little pick me up while shopping.

Name: Pronto Caffè
Address: J2F8+62M, Rue Riad Zitoun el Kdim, Marrakesh
Author: rosleigh roberts
Review: Great little coffee shop with really good quality coffee. It was a nice surprise in a busy medina where tea is the choice of the day. Had a lovely flat white reminded me of home.

Name: Pronto Caffè
Address: J2F8+62M, Rue Riad Zitoun el Kdim, Marrakesh
Author: Perry Buchan
Review: This is the best coffee!  Get the espresso with spice!  Seriously, they have their own spice blend which works so well with the coffee. Very friendly service.

Name: Pronto Caffè
Address: J2F8+62M, Rue Riad Zitoun el Kdim, Marrakesh
Author: mari
Review: the owner was lovely and the coffee was very yummy, good price!

Address: J2F8+62M, Rue Riad Zitoun el Kdim, Marrakesh
Author: Anthony Robinson
Review: Great little cafe that serves excellent tea. Host is very welcoming

Review: Nice

Name: Cafe belle vue
Author: potox spring-flex
Review: Good welcome. Impeccable customer service I love this place and even it touched me LIGHTNING COST

Name: Cafe belle vue
Author: Enisa Mehmedovic
Review: Super nice staff..traditional

Name: Cafe belle vue
Author: Aidali Yassine
Review: GOOD

Name: Mister Espresso
Author: Joanna Louise
Review:
What a lovely coffee place with a wonderful owner (who can speak German).

I stopped by and had an espresso-choco and it was a very good coffee. Good coffee machine used and a variety of beans. Just a shame that there is no milk alternative,  but we discussed this with the owner and explained that a lot of Europeans now move away from dairy.

Owner was super friendly and explained how he started off the coffee shop and his background. Other staff were also friendly.

Name: Mister Espresso
Author: IgorW
Review: A very friendly team. It's apparent that they understand coffee. You can drink on-site, take coffee to go, or buy freshly ground coffee beans to suit your needs at home. There are many different types of beans to pick from, and the crew will assist you in selecting the one that best meets your needs. Very competitive pricing. The espresso is actually very good. They offer their unique blend, which is the result of combining several types of beans. I really suggest this location.

Name: Mister Espresso
Author: Yosaf SLM
Review: Really recommended for anyone who wants to have quality coffee and a comfortable place to sit

Name: Mister Espresso
Author: Shauna Eckstadt
Review: Best coffee I’ve had yet in Morocco! Smooth & strong.

Name: Mister Espresso
Author: jaouad chqef
Review: Nice place to taste delicious coffee with good price I like it 👌

Name: Stop Coffee
Author: Zakaria Ouaada
Review: Best espresso/coffee in town! This is a genuine family run business built for coffee lovers. Don’t be fooled by it small space, This is not like all those tourist traps in Marrakech that serves you black water and call it coffee. This is authentic italian style coffee shop where you will enjoy your coffee to the last sip.

Name: Stop Coffee
Author: smaisma sc
Review: There is no price list

Name: Starbucks
Author: Ziad Dakir
Review: I recently visited this Starbucks , and it was fantastic! The staff was incredibly friendly, providing customer service that was second to none. My Frappuccino was perfectly crafted and rich in flavor, truly top-notch. The inviting atmosphere made it a great spot to relax before my flight. I highly recommend this to anyone traveling—it’s a must-visit!

Name: Starbucks
Author: C L
Review:
Starbucks has always been my favorite coffee brand. Whichever city I go, I always visit the Starbucks there. HOWEVER, the one in Marrakesh airport really broke my heart.

The staff here was really rude. When me and my friend ready to order, he just sat there and played his phone, seemed not going to order. And when he finally ordered for us, his attitude was like we owed him 1 billion dollars. Really bad experience. If I could give 0 star to this Starbucks in google map, I would do that. And after our observation, we founded out that he only treated us differently, maybe just because we’re Asian.

The staff’s name is Ahmed, I hope next time he could treat others better😉

Name: Starbucks
Author: Chabili Hicham
Review: Very professional attitude and the tastiest coffee I've had in any starbucks, the baristas Anas and Karim were especially kind to accomodate us and patient with our orders

Name: Starbucks
Author: Dominic
Review:
It's a nice Starbucks, the only thing to mention: we received 2 different things than what we ordered. I had the feeling that he understood what I ordered (in English), but apparently that was not the case, and instead of checking he just wrote down whatever he think he heard.

Can happen, but probably good to double-check your order before you pay.

He was friendly, overall good experience

Name: Starbucks
Author: Noureddine EL AZHARI
Review: Highlight the delightful variety of beverages, the cozy ambiance, and the consistently friendly service at Starbucks.

Name: CTM Coffee shop
Author: Samir KHODDAR
Review: Gone are the days when CTM used to be a premium transportation company with the highest service, i even paid premium just so i can charge my phone and use the wifi (as advertised), turned out half of the USB posts are not working and the wifi is anything but a wifi

Name: CTM Coffee shop
Author: Erik Kostka
Review: We got taxi from Marrakesh city center to CTM for 50 dirhams. I am not sure, if it is a perfect price, but at our Riat, they told us 150 dorhams for their taxi, so definetely try to haggle.

Name: CTM Coffee shop
Author: Joselito Berbenas
Review:
All good, in time and relax. I will use it again for sure.
Si quieren ventanilla reservar online.

Name: CTM Coffee shop

Review: I wish i could give no star regarding this … the delay is huuge and many many many other things that for sure were completely not good

Name: Bacha Coffee
Address: Dar el Bacha, Rte Sidi Abdelaziz, Marrakech
Author: H Nakamura
Review:
Highly recommended!! Really beautiful place and a really perfect service! It was impressive!
We went for coffee and desert after lunch, it was a great idea!
They have way too many variety of coffee type, we got lost though… better to ask advice or do your research in advance, unless you are an expert and know what you want

Name: Bacha Coffee
Address: Dar el Bacha, Rte Sidi Abdelaziz, Marrakech
Author: Denise Wang
Review:
Beautiful space for a coffee or a meal. As other people have mentioned, there is a wait (we waited about an hour), however it gives you an opportunity to explore the beautiful museum. If you have some free time, I’d recommend going, however between the wait and the meal, you’ll need to allocate about three hours.

To get into the museum (you need to pay entry to get to the cafe), it’s 60DH per person (cash only). The cafe itself takes credit card.

Name: Bacha Coffee
Address: Dar el Bacha, Rte Sidi Abdelaziz, Marrakech
Author: Liezel San Andres
Review: The waiting time at the restaurant was about an hour and a half, with service being incredibly slow—taking 30 minutes just to receive the menu, another 30 to 45 minutes for the food to arrive, and nearly an hour to finally get the bill to think we only ordered two coffees and two slices of cake—yet the marble cake was so hard it was almost inedible, though the ‘Pastry of the Week’ cappuccino was decent. The ambiance was pleasant, but the service was truly awful and price was so expensive. On the bright side, they offer a wide range of coffee options, which I appreciated, and the iced coffee was enjoyable. I also bought coffee beans from the shop, which turned out to be the highlight of the experience—they were gorgeous when brewed at home in my coffee machine. Unfortunately, that was the only truly positive aspect of the visit. It is an experience but I would never dine again.

Name: Bacha Coffee
Address: Dar el Bacha, Rte Sidi Abdelaziz, Marrakech
Author: Eli Haugen
Review:
The coffee, food, and service were so good that I visited three times during my week in Marrakech. The wait time is absolutely worth it if you pay the little bit extra to explore the museum and make friends with the other people waiting (shout out to the lovely lady from Holland ♥️). I loved learning about the history of the building and of the coffee shop as well.

Don’t miss trying the homemade cream and fresh vanilla shavings in your coffee - after you enjoy it black (as they helpfully suggest). I’m a particular fan of the Yemeni coffee, and my friend enjoyed the Jamaican Blue Mountain coffee.

Name: Bacha Coffee
Address: Dar el Bacha, Rte Sidi Abdelaziz, Marrakech
Author: Julia Hope
Review: Arrived around 10:40 and seated at 12:30- I recommend going straight to the cafe from the museum so they can get your name down on the list then you have time to explore- well worth the wait! Loved all the coffee choices and the pastries were amazing- we got the cheesecake and tiramisu!

Name: J HOUSE SPECIALITY COFFEE
Address: Résidence assila 146 immeuble F magasin 17 Mohamed Marrakech, Marrakech
Author: Nab Lamdani
Review: Small spot specially inside. They have AC inside for hot weather, the service is good and quick,

Name: J HOUSE SPECIALITY COFFEE
Address: Résidence assila 146 immeuble F magasin 17 Mohamed Marrakech, Marrakech
Author: Rihab Houmadi
Review: Great coffee, the place is spacious, conveniently close to the airport, and offers free parking. Prices are reasonable, but I hope they add brunch options soon.

Name: J HOUSE SPECIALITY COFFEE
Address: Résidence assila 146 immeuble F magasin 17 Mohamed Marrakech, Marrakech
Author: Ben Dane
Review: I’m afraid the recent comments are right, staff is very nice and helpful but the coffee is in no way third wave/specialty coffee.. “cappuccino” came in a glass with loads of cocoa powder. If you claim soft opening, please adjust your online appearance accordingly.

Name: J HOUSE SPECIALITY COFFEE

Author: Rita M
Review: Lovely new shop that just opened. The sign got us inside with a laugh. Cute vibe, and very sweet young owners with a passion for coffee. The dark chocolate iced mocha is nice and not too sweet or too bitter. The shop owners are so sweet and made us feel very welcome on our first day in marrakesh. Will definitely be back for our daily coffee

Name: SAVOR Coffee Shop
Author: David Oršula
Review: Newly opened very small coffee place. It is just 3 small coffee tables and one bank for sitting, but very cosy and clean.  The espresso tasted just like in Italy, rich, full flavoured, creamy. Free small water comes with the coffee. We've tried two smoothies and they were exceptionally good.

Name: SAVOR Coffee Shop
Author: C. Satriano
Review: Really pretty little coffee shop with an amazing owner. Everything is individually designed, the room, the cards and even the dishes are designed by the owner. It looks very unique and made with passion. We had a delicious coffee and a pleasant conversation with the very kind owner✨

Name: SAVOR Coffee Shop
Author: Lauren Angus
Review: My favourite coffee shop in Marrakesh! The best iced latte and cookies. Service has been amazing, will miss it so much.

Name: SAVOR Coffee Shop
Author: Michael Tan
Review:
A solid choice for coffee in the medina. If you are a bit tired of the traditional Moroccan coffee and instead are craving a flat white or latte, this is the place to go.

The seating is a bit tight as there is just a long bench for 5-6 people, but the room is air conditioned so it was very comfortable.

Review: good

Review: good

Author: B R
Review: The best coffee in Marrakesh. The owner is such a friendly guy. Thank you so much!! We are looking forward to drink your coffee at home in the Netherlands.

Name: Benbasha Coffee Shop
Author: Vittorio Sganzerla
Review:
Best coffee place in Marrakech! Small corner shop with typical Maroccan coffee.

You can find also coffee powder for sale at very good price compared to the market.

Very cheerful and friendly owner!

Name: Benbasha Coffee Shop
Author: Johnny Walker_94
Review: Found this hidden place by walking through old town. Awesome coffee with traditional spices. It’s owner is funny and friendly by explaining all the different tastes of coffee. For good espresso/ cappuccino you should go there!

Name: Benbasha Coffee Shop
Author: Egresi Abigel
Review: We had the best coffee here while in Morocco! We came back twice and would have more if the time would have allowed it. Best prices and best coffee! Recommend!

Name: Good vibes Coffee shop

Traditional setting with lots of charm!
Nice surprise
