Name: café la joie
Author: Stefanie
Review: The most delicious local coffee and tea. The owner is so nice. This isn’t just coffee or tea, it’s an experience. English speaking.

Name: café la joie
Author: R
Review:
The BEST cafe with the best cafe owner!
You MUST come here and try the drinks. I had both a tea and coffee and preferred the tea. I had a bit of sugar (I think it tastes best with at least a bit as it brings out more flavour). ☕️🍵
Also, you can get a limited selection of oil perfumes for 10 Dirham here - everywhere else you’ll need to strongly barter to get this price.

Absolutely wonderful 😋😍

Name: café la joie
Author: Tammy T
Review:
Went looking for Turkish style coffee in the Medina and google maps led me to near this cafe. The young man running the business (family business) was so lovely and engaging, wanted to offer the best coffee and best service. He invited me to video and take photos as he made the coffee in the hot sand. Was Turkish coffee with the Moroccan spices added in, very tasty. And not expensive.
He has seats in the laneway which is where I sat so I could watch everything happening around me, small area with seating edge of laneway or a small terrace which I visited and found to be very quiet so would be great if wanted to escape the hustle and bustle for bit.
He also sells spices, herbs, teas, oils etc if you interested but he did not push for me to buy anything just gave me some to smell. He then sat and chatted for little bit recommending to me places to visit around Morocco. Very relaxed and lovely visit to his small cafe, will definitely return next time I in Marrakech.

Name: café la joie
Author: Niclas W.
Review: A delightful little café in Marrakech. The coffee here is the tastiest I've ever had in the city. Their Berber tea is a true delight. The rooftop terrace is cozy and authentic, offering a genuine experience. Do take the time to visit this quaint café, it's well worth it!

Name: café la joie
Author: Sean MacDonald
Review: This is the coolest coffee stand. Najib makes super dense spiced coffee and rotates the pot in hot sand to bring out the crema in the coffee. He also serves delicious berber tea. He is super warm and will teach you about all the Moroccan spices. We returned to his stand every day and ended up meeting a lot of people just from sitting on the chairs in the pathway in front of his shop. Great prices too.

Name: Boème coffee
Address: RDC N°650 Lot Al Manar 3, Marrakech
Author: OUDEBJI ABID
Review: For those seeking a calm and serene space to study or read, and for those who need a quiet spot to get some work done, this themed book cafe with its diverse selection of books, including a generous assortment of manga, offers the perfect ambiance.

Review: Just opened today, tried their iced Spanish latte , the taste is good, the service is excellent! Can’t ask for more ! Finally another decent cafe for me to enjoy my time here!

Name: Caribou Coffee - Gare De Marrakech
Author: mourad
Review: Great coffee, great service and very welcoming staff 🙏

Name: Caribou Coffee - Gare De Marrakech
Author: Nouri
Review: The coffee is well prepared, you smell real Arabica, the service is friendly and the staff is very kind, helpful and smiling.

Name: Caribou Coffee - Gare De Marrakech
Author: Isabelle Gonin Hasnaoui
Review:
The coffee is excellent, the welcome is warm, it’s clean.
To improve: lower the amplitude of the music indoors. On the terrace, parasols and more comfortable seats are welcome.

Name: Caribou Coffee - Gare De Marrakech

Review: Very good service and products, as long as it lasts. Special mention for the cookies and cream milkshake👍

Name: مقهى فلسطين

Author: Ritvik Jaru
Review:
Vegetarian - ordered veggie panini(grilled cheese, cooked zucchini, cooked peppers) mint tea and sparking water.
So delicious, refreshing, ambiance was totally chill.

Name: Café Menzeh
Author: Isabelly Cairo
Review:
Good view of the Jardin Secret from the top.
It was good to drink a good tea appreciating the nice view.

Name: Café Menzeh
Author: Pedro Aguiar costa Pedro costa
Review: we were seated for 20 minutes to be served while there were only about 5 tables occupied. very rude employees. an employee called adil super frowning is there and after we called a few times he ignored it on purpose

Name: Café Menzeh
Author: Mim's Sdk
Review:
Visited in October, the garden is magnificent (fairly quiet despite the tourists).
The new server is great 👍🏼

I had a perfect carrot/orange juice to regain strength after visits under the sun.
We have a 33cl bottle of water with the coffee (France should take an example).

Besides, noss noss means: half coffee and half milk.

Name: Café Menzeh
Author: olga konstantinidou
Review:
The cafe is actually inside the garden, for which you have to pay an entrance fee!

We never knew!

Name: Coffee Mbehja
Author: Laurent Demeneix
Review:
Really small coffee pit stop but best value found in Marrakech and of good quality.
Became my morning routine and loved their almond espresso.
To note: no milk alternative available. Only 🐮 🐄
And no table, just drink it at the counter or on the go.

Name: Coffee Mbehja
Author: Steven Silverstein
Review: Coffee Mbehja makes truly remarkable espresso!  Best that I've had in many years, including shops all through Italy, Spain, Portugal, and the USA.  Smooth, very full bodied, and perfect crema.  This small friendly family owned shop is located just inside the Sidi Mimoune Gate.  Their coffee might be the reason I relocate permanently to Marrakesh :)

Name: Coffee Mbehja
Author: J. Devos
Review:
Cheap as can be (7dh), great quality, always kind, lots of care into each order.

Small but great coffees.

Name: Coffee Mbehja
Author: Małgorzata Poniatowska
Review: Very nice place, much more better than any coffee shop in city center.

Name: Coffee Mbehja
Author: Sam C
Review: Amazing espresso with great flavours and friendly service!

Name: Cs Coffee
Author: Taleb El Hamdaoui
Review: what an amazing coffee, I come here every single day almost, amazing place, weather, people, service and food, really recommanded if you want a clean and calm coffee. I like it

Name: Cs Coffee
Author: Radouane EL HADDAD
Review: it's amazing coffee  wonderful pleac

Name: Cs Coffee
Author: The Sports Champions Of Tomorrow Marrakech
Review: I used to love coffee in this place because it is very quiet and beautiful. I go to this place in the month of July and August and the temperature was up to 48. I was comfortable there, because the quality of the coffee and fruit juice drinks.....excellent. The best thing is that this place has 3... Air conditioning devices and I did not feel the temperature and it was cold in a café and I recommend it because it is quiet

Name: Cs Coffee
Author: Lili Hricha
Review: This cafe is one of the best cafes I have visited, and the coffee also tastes wonderful

Name: Cs Coffee
Author: Mohammed Laajaj
Review: A very good café with a good level of coffee quality and 100% high quality service.

Name: sherif coffee
Address: sidi abdelaaziz, Rue Riad Laarous, Marrakesh
Author: Rob Emerton
Review: Definitely the best coffee I had in Morocco! Abdelhamid was the friendliest guy we met, great to chat with and get recommendations. The coffee shop is in a great spot for sitting and watching the chaos of the souks go by - a must visit when in Marrakech!

Name: sherif coffee
Address: sidi abdelaaziz, Rue Riad Laarous, Marrakesh
Author: Will
Review: Awesome coffee- tried the spiced coffee, impressed with genuine barista coffee from a cozy cafe. Super nice owner Abdelhamid, enjoyed chatting and recommend!

Name: sherif coffee
Address: sidi abdelaaziz, Rue Riad Laarous, Marrakesh
Author: Michel Faas
Review: Had a very goo spiced coffee. Great place to grab a coffee, also a great place for a nice conversation, speaking English very well

Name: sherif coffee
Address: sidi abdelaaziz, Rue Riad Laarous, Marrakesh
Author: Tom Howard
Review: Abdel is a lovely man - friendly and plenty of recommendations (suggested Chez Fatima). Delicious espresso and tasty accompanying pastry!

Name: sherif coffee
Address: sidi abdelaaziz, Rue Riad Laarous, Marrakesh
Author: Katie Nowak
Review: Loved this spot! A cute find wandering through the Medina. Hamid was very friendly & helpful. My fiancée tried the spiced coffee & loved it. Unique local flavor & reasonable prices. Definitely recommend!

Name: Thirty5ive Marrakech
Author: Oanh
Review: This hip and laid-back modern coffee shop is a great spot to relax. You can unwind downstairs or upstairs with your coffee or smoothie of choice while enjoying cool music from a record player.

Name: Thirty5ive Marrakech
Author: Emily
Review: This cafe is a gem in Marrakech! It’s the best coffee I’ve had in the city. It has a nice minimalist design and the barista is skilled and friendly. I would have happily worked at this cafe had I discovered it sooner. The cinnamon honey matcha latte with soy milk is so good!

Name: Thirty5ive Marrakech
Author: Mònica Noviembre
Review: Great place in terms of ambience and good vibes. Also, high quality coffee. Very recommended!

Name: Thirty5ive Marrakech
Author: Dalida Spenser
Review: Beautiful and bohemian place, quiet luxury with elegant products, to escape classic places, you most visit this minimalistic  designed cafe with well researched music repertoire. Well done !

Name: Thirty5ive Marrakech
Author: Emils U
Review: Wow! What a space. Beautiful vinyl music, amazing specialty coffee and a fantastic pottery space. Far outdid any expectations we had. Fantastic service to add!

Name: Café mobile Mountasir
Author: Tommie Kuiper
Review: Very friendly and good quality strong coffee!

Name: Café mobile Mountasir
Author: Justin
Review: Great local coffee!

Name: Café mobile Mountasir
Author: Patrick Elmensdorp
Review: The real Moroccan experience.

Name: Café mobile oussama
Author: EL Mehdi
Review: great coffee

Name: Café mobile oussama
Author: mehdi rhandour
Review: good service

Name: 101 CAFFE'
Author: Hidden Marrakech
Review:
🏆 Good coffee, delightful sitting on their terrace …and they also sell beans!

101 Caffe is a perfect stop when in Gueliz. Situated on the back streets behind Carre Eden, this local cafe has beans from around the world and an outdoor terrace where you can watch the world pass by as you sip!

Name: 101 CAFFE'
Author: Nizar Hakiki
Review: Best coffeeshop in town, I highly recommend it.

Name: 101 CAFFE'
Author: U C
Review: Worst coffee. She had no idea how to make a coffee at all. I don’t think, that all the other reviews are real.

Name: 101 CAFFE'
Author: Jim
Review: Nice little place to have a nice coffee. Very polite and helpful.

Name: 101 CAFFE'
Author: Kastyel Sinleth
Review: Great ambience, and the two gentlemen working there are very friendly and make you feel at ease. I've been there multiple times for my favorite coffee.

Name: Terra mia café
Author: Gabi Alves Photography
Review:
The best cafe in Marrakech!!

The food is so tasty and the customer service is amazing!!! During my trip, I went to Terra Mia more the 4 times

Name: Terra mia café
Author: Urwin Monsanto
Review:
This place is amazing, it has a very nice clean and modern look. feel that u are in Morocco, breakfast, lunch or even for a nice hot drink with a slice of pastries that tastes like heaven. The plates are well presented and the food is fresh snd home made and delicious💯!!!! The service is perfect, great staff, good communication, always friendly!
The owner is always there with a big smile and is super friendly, super social really love this guy! I definitely recommend it to friends and family if they wanna have a place to relax and eat. For me the best place in Marrakech

Name: Terra mia café
Author: Hanan B
Review: I love this spot. Always consistent excellence in service, yummy healthy variety of food, drinks, pastry and viennoiserie. Today in particular I was greeted by Nadia who had a huge smile on her face and I needed assistance with my tables arrangement and she ran to assist all while keeping a positive attitude and was super helpful. It really makes a difference. The music is beautiful adding to the lovely atmosphere and just enough to enjoy without being loud to ruin conversations. The decor everything about this place is always tip top. I feel like leaving a review every time I visit because everyone is a great experience. I hope it keeps being at this level because I love cafés that care about details and keep me coming back.

Name: Terra mia café
Author: Anthony Acosta
Review: As someone who lives in Brussels with some fantastic French boulangerie this place is doing it right! My French friends who live in the area also agree. The melted chocolate croissant is like a lucious pain chocolat

Name: Terra mia café
Author: Rupert Holden
Review: Very delicious pastry, the kind you would expect in an upmarket French patisserie, and the best I’ve had in Marrakech after years of visiting from France. The chocolate tart and entremets noisette were highlights! A relaxed and carefully decorated place with a shaded terrace to take a coffee (including lactose free options !)

Name: HESPERIS Coffee Factory
Author: David Hatch
Review:
Delightfully surprised. Did not expect to find such an upscale restaurant in Sidi Ghanem. Prices are a bit high for the area, but good value and we'll worth it. Very few places in all of Marrakech have service as good as here at HESPERIS. I would give the service 6-Stars if I could. Very well trained, polite, and respectful. The artwork is also enjoyable as well. Coffee was well done presented well.
Great choice to try!

Name: HESPERIS Coffee Factory
Author: Abraham Terpstra
Review: What an inspiring place! Hesperis serves specialty coffee that they roast themselves. I had the Ethiopian Giju G1. Floral and alive. Very friendly and helpful staff, we had the opportunity to see the roaster in the basement. I love the place and how they thought of every little detail in the interior (the terrace floors are beautiful).

Name: HESPERIS Coffee Factory
Author: H Ouk
Review:
Was absolutely clean and relaxing place
I like how the staff are smiling in front of anyone.

Name: HESPERIS Coffee Factory
Author: Julia Onorato
Review: Absolutely delicious food, amazing ambiance and the coffee was out of this work. The pastries and sweets were spectacular, and so fresh they must have been handmade that morning. The eggs were also light and fluffy - this was one of those breakfasts where you wish you could eat more because everything was so good. Would definitely recommend to my friends and will be coming back again soon!

Name: HESPERIS Coffee Factory
Author: Emily Byrski
Review: One of the best coffee shops I visited in Marrakech! The coffee and breakfast were delicious, the space was bright and airy, and the staff and owner were so hospitable and friendly! It was truly a joy and I’d recommend this spot to all of my friends! :)

Name: Coffee M'Naghma
Author: lexi zhu
Review: Lovely petite cafe in the old Medina neighborhood. Welcoming warm owner, opening till late, reasonable prices and strong freshly made coffee with lots of flavor choices! Recommend. 10/10.

Name: Coffee M'Naghma
Author: Michelle Dziuban
Review: So good, and the only place open before the market starts. Recommend walking the Medina before it opens (calm before the storm), with a cup of coffee from this lovely man

Name: Coffee M'Naghma
Author: Mario Graf
Review: Amazing coffee! You can see and taste how much effort an love the owner puts into making a good product!

Name: Coffee M'Naghma
Author: Aisha Quadrelli
Review: Extremely friendly vendor, when i forgot my money at the hotel he told me i shouldnt worry about it! Coffee is delicious as well, definitely the best in whole marrakech. A lot of choices also tea, caramel latte, coffee with chocolate, rasperry etc. i know he will add plant based milk options as well.

Name: Coffee M'Naghma
Author: Dan Liggins
Review: Grabbing a morning coffee from a more local souk, this place did not let me down. So much foot traffic going past means it is constantly in a bust flow. Slightly slow service, but worth it nonetheless. A friendly good English speaking man served me & did not appear to try & hustle for more money. He stook to the exact ways he treated the locals who ordered before & after me. Quality coffee taste & a varied drink selection to help cater for the tourists who maybe like flavored coffees or even iced drinks.

Name: LA TASSE JOYEUSE
Address: 2ème étage, appartement 129, Résidence Albassatime, Av. Guemassa, Marrakesh
Author: Moroccan Wonderer
Review: A good coffee in mhamid, in a good spot, but the space is small a little bit, the breakfasts are good but they could be better and more diversified, service is fast, in the morning you can't sit outside because of the shiny sunlight.

Name: LA TASSE JOYEUSE
Address: 2ème étage, appartement 129, Résidence Albassatime, Av. Guemassa, Marrakesh
Author: ADIL AARAB
Review:
A nice new coffee place with a beautiful design and a diversified menu with a lot to offer. Breakfast menu with multiple choices and even lunch is proposed which is rare in the area. The place is beautiful and new with some modern decoration and fourniture. Highly recommend for a quick cafe or a breakfast or just to watch some football.
I tried the breakfast which I highly recommend it was tasty and nutritious with a price of 40dh, I also tried the carrot juice very tasty and refreshing. I could say the best coffee in the boulevard so far give it a chance

Name: LA TASSE JOYEUSE
Address: 2ème étage, appartement 129, Résidence Albassatime, Av. Guemassa, Marrakesh
Author: Nouamane Iggoute
Review: Nice place and nice service, will be back laterfor sure

Name: LA TASSE JOYEUSE
Address: 2ème étage, appartement 129, Résidence Albassatime, Av. Guemassa, Marrakesh
Author: Abdelhak Abd
Review: bad service

Name: LA TASSE JOYEUSE
Address: 2ème étage, appartement 129, Résidence Albassatime, Av. Guemassa, Marrakesh
Author: Ali Boudda
Review:
Warm place, a very good café restaurant that I highly recommend

Delicious and truly impeccable quality of cuisine with the best quality/price ratio

Name: Coffee Houmti ️ قهوة حومتي
Author: allison arandez
Review: Great little cafe, had a really nice cafe latte and sat and watched local life go by

Name: Coffee Houmti ️ قهوة حومتي
Author: Junghoon seo
Review:
My favourite cafe in Marrakech while I was there. Cozy place with a couple seats outside. It is a great resting place while you walk through the markets. Espresso slightly bitter but can be reduced with the sugar. I came here twice and the first time I got the croissant which was very nice. Soft on the inside and brushed with a sweet sauce. Super friendly barista.

It is in the middle of a very busy road so you will have motorbikes going past you. Other than that, worth a visit for sure.

Name: Coffee Houmti ️ قهوة حومتي
Author: AANUIT
Review: Nice place for a quick coffee and croissant. Hicham is very friendly and in for a small talk. The cappuccino is not like the “European” one I guess, but still good. Croissant were fresh.

Name: Coffee Houmti ️ قهوة حومتي
Author: Mia Cunningham
Review: Wow! This little spot is an amazing stop for a coffee & pastry. Very good prices and the quality is amazing. One of our favourite places for coffee in Marrakech. Thank you to the owner for his amazing service and beautiful coffee 🙏🏼❤️

Name: Coffee Houmti ️ قهوة حومتي
Author: Eamonn Coughlan
Review: Had coffee and orange juice. Both incredible. Loved the setup. Simple but elegant and well thought about

Name: Magic Coffee
Author: Lea Kosic
Review:
Me and my friends tried the pipusa and it was amazing! Everyone working there was so nice and did their best to make this experience 10/10!
I strongly recommend :)

Name: Magic Coffee
Author: Sam Collier
Review: The spiced coffee was delicious! The ladies were very friendly, offering me tips for exploring and explaining the food to me. Highly recommend!

Name: Magic Coffee
Author: Virangi Manike
Review: We had such a nice experience in magical coffee . Best street foods and hot drinks offered. Specially very clean and tidy place.

Name: Magic Coffee
Author: Lukas Figely
Review: Hostile family with small business gave us chance to experience Marroccos atmosphere- the original spiced coffee in, view and noise of beautiful street, coziness of narrow and colourful room…delightful

Name: Magic Coffee
Author: Liubov Kokoshko
Review: It’s a very good place for the breakfast, lunch or short break. Crepes with honey and cheese were delicious. Spicy coffee was amazing. Don’t forget about ice cream. Hot crepes with cold ice cream are very taste combination)) The service was pleasant. Special thanks to owner's daughter! She is a charming girl and good speaks English))

Name: Café Mazar
Author: WanderLensBiker “WanderLensBiker”
Review:
I recently had the pleasure of visiting Cafe Mazar, a Lebanese café located in Marrakech Airport, and I must say, it was a fantastic experience. The staff were incredibly friendly and welcoming, which made my visit all the more enjoyable. What impressed me the most was their willingness to open early in the morning, allowing travelers like myself to grab a bite to eat even during the wee hours.

One of the standout aspects of Cafe Mazar is its reasonable pricing. In an airport where options can often be limited and expensive, I was pleasantly surprised to find that sandwiches were priced at only 49 Moroccan Dirhams each, making it a budget-friendly choice for a quick meal.

Overall, I highly recommend Cafe Mazar to anyone passing through Marrakech Airport. With its friendly staff, early opening hours, and reasonable prices, it's a gem that stands out among airport dining options

Name: Café Mazar
Author: Lisbeth Stewart
Review:
Delicious food, look in the display cabinet for portion sizes.
Lovely staff who seem to speak French, English & Arabic fluently.
Comfortable seating area with a fence so you can relax feeling your luggage is secure.
Menu (French) available, including a 3 course meal option.

Name: Café Mazar
Author: Abdellatif Merroun
Review: 1 moroccan pancake (رغيفة) small honey for individual use ,small tea bag take away cup and orange juice 91 dirhams. It's just beyond belief. It's a small coffee shop plastic chairs they have not got no butter no cheese to put on your pancake It's just day light robbery as they say.

Name: Café Mazar
Author: Nga Nguyen
Review: I ordered a Moroccan pancake which came with some honey. It was a big pancake however it had no taste whatsoever. Very bland and a bit dry. Tried to make it work with the honey, but the honey tasted weird.

Name: Café Mazar
Author: Stefano P
Review: Great little boxes of food and friendly staff, much tastier than anything inside the airport which is all quite expensive only thing I wouldn’t recommend is the pancake
